V012-N00614.definitions: A forward and downward movement of energy. During pushing hands, this energy enables you to prevent your opponent from raising their arms or legs, and at the same time, allows you to stick to and redirect the opponent’s power.

V012-N00615.label: 挒劲
V012-N00615.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00615.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00615.definitions: Internal energy to push or pluck forward. During pushing hands, this energy allows you to use a spiral motion to split the opponent’s body and destroy the opponent’s balance.

V012-N00616.label: 挒法
V012-N00616.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00616.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00616.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: to separate, to twist or to offset with a spiral motion, often while making immobile part of the body (such as a hand or leg) to split an opponent’s body thereby destroying posture and balance.

V012-N00617.label: 挤劲
V012-N00617.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00617.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00617.definitions: Internal energy that projects forward from its source to prevent your opponent from issuing strength. During pushing hands, this energy radiates along the outside of the forearms and the back of the hands.

V012-N00618.label: 挤法[太极]
V012-N00618.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00618.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00618.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: A pressing or squeezing offset in a direction away from the body, usually done with the back of the hand or outside edge of the forearm.

V012-N00619.label: 捋劲
V012-N00619.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00619.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00619.definitions: The use of force in a sideways direction, such as where we intercept and move with a forward directed attack, simultaneously diverting it slightly to one side and thus to empty space.

V012-N00620.label: 捋法[太极]
V012-N00620.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00620.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00620.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: to apply sideways, circular yielding movement to the opponent’s wrist with one hand and stick to the opponent’s elbow with the other hand.

V012-N00621.label: 掤劲
V012-N00621.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00621.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00621.definitions: Rising or expanding internal energy. With this energy, one feels the body is full of qi in all eight directions. During pushing hands, this energy allows you to create a buffer zone that prevents the first shock of an incoming attack and at the same time, enable you to defend, absorb or redirect the opponent’s power.

V012-N00622.label: 掤法
V012-N00622.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00622.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00622.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: An upward circular movement, forward or backward, yielding or offsetting usually with the arms to disrupt the opponent’s centre of gravity.

V012-N00623.label: 敛臀
V012-N00623.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00623.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00623.definitions: To slightly tuck in the buttocks to facilitate the abdomen and relax the crotch. This helps to keep the body neutral and stable.

V012-N00624.label: 敞裆
V012-N00624.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00624.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00624.definitions: A common error in Taiji practice. An open crotch (>90o) may cause an inability to obtain the internal energy.

V012-N00625.label: 方圆
V012-N00625.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00625.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00625.definitions: The two technical features of Taiji. The “square” refers to the force points, whereas the “round” refers to arc-shaped Taiji movements. The two features are unity of opposites.

V012-N00626.label: 方圆相生
V012-N00626.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00626.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00626.englishNames: Mutual generation of square and round
V012-N00626.definitions: The “square” refers to the hard power point that manifests in four sides and four corners. The “round” refers to the soft, circulating qi. The “square” originates from the “round”, and the “round” contains the “square”. There is “square” within the “round” and vice versa.

V012-N00627.label: 无极
V012-N00627.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00627.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00627.definitions: An ancient philosophical term, referring to the primordial universe prior to the Taiji (supreme ultimate). In Taiji practice, it has three extended meanings: 1) a relatively static state; 2) mental serenity before body moves in Taiji practice; 3) abdomen.

V012-N00628.label: 松紧
V012-N00628.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00628.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00628.definitions: An important feature of Taiji practice. There is a saying that it takes at least 10 years to really understand Taiji. The key part is learning to cultivate internal energy through relaxation and stillness. It is necessary to avoid tension and mental stress before achieving relaxation in Taiji practice.

V012-N00629.label: 松腰敛臀
V012-N00629.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00629.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00629.englishNames: Keep the waist relaxed and buttocks tucked under
V012-N00629.definitions: To relax the waist means to lift the coccyx and sink the points Shensu (BL 23) and Mingmen (DU 4). To tuck buttocks under means to slightly tuck in the buttocks.

V012-N00630.label: 气沉丹田
V012-N00630.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00630.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00630.definitions: To sink qi to Dantian, you need to keep the coccyx upright, sink the chest, relax the shoulders and suspend the crotch, and thus to guide qi down to the abdomen with intent.

V012-N00631.label: 沉肩坠肘
V012-N00631.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00631.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00631.englishNames: Relax the shoulders, sink the elbows
V012-N00631.definitions: Relax and drop down the shoulders, especially the acromion, or the point Jianjing (GB 21); at the same time, slightly flex and drop the elbow.

V012-N00632.label: 泛臀
V012-N00632.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00632.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00632.definitions: To attempt to float the buttocks before issuing internal energy.

V012-N00633.label: 溜臀
V012-N00633.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00633.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00633.definitions: To relax and drop the buttocks when squatting down.

V012-N00634.label: 盘架子
V012-N00634.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00634.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00634.definitions: A series of movements in Taiji practice.

V012-N00635.label: 直腕
V012-N00635.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00635.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00635.definitions: Make the wrist straight and flat.

V012-N00636.label: 神气鼓荡
V012-N00636.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00636.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N00637.label: 立身中正
V012-N00637.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00637.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00637.definitions: To keep the torso perpendicular to the ground and place the bodyweight over the supporting feet. This helps to keep the body stable.

V012-N00638.label: 竖腕
V012-N00638.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00638.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00638.definitions: Keep the wrist and fingers in a vertical position (approximately 90o). However, it is advisable not to use force to bend the wrist.

V012-N00639.label: 粘走
V012-N00639.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00639.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00639.definitions: Two basic skills in the practice of Taiji pushing hands. To “adhere” means to get your opponent to follow you under your control; whereas to “follow” means to obey your opponent’s intention by letting him think he can apply an application, which leads him to use force.

V012-N00640.label: 缩胯
V012-N00640.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00640.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00640.englishNames: Relax and tuck in the hips
V012-N00640.definitions: To relax and, at the same time, tuck in the hips to enable the perineum to become a semicircle.

V012-N00641.label: 肘劲
V012-N00641.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00641.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00641.definitions: Internal energy that projects through the forearm rotation and tip of the elbow. During pushing hands, you can either defend the opponent’s attack with the tip of your elbow. Alternatively, you can use one hand to stick to the opponent’s hand and use flexed elbow of the other arm to spirally press the opponent’s elbow and arm.

V012-N00642.label: 肘法
V012-N00642.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00642.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00642.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: to strike or push with the elbow.

V012-N00643.label: 节节贯串
V012-N00643.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00643.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00643.definitions: This refers to successive movement of nine major joints throughout the body: neck, back, low back, hip, knee, ankle, shoulder, elbow and wrist.

V012-N00644.label: 荡裆
V012-N00644.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00644.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00644.definitions: A common error in Taiji practice. Slack crotch may cause energy to scatter.

V012-N00645.label: 蓄发
V012-N00645.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00645.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00645.definitions: To “store energy” means to relax muscles with mental focus. To “release energy” means to contract muscles and often combine with breathing.

V012-N00646.label: 虚实分清
V012-N00646.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00646.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00646.definitions: In Taiji practice, it is important to distinguish between fullness and emptiness all over the body (i.e. we must know exactly which part of our body is issuing power and which part is receiving it). As for the legs, the “empty” leg is never absolutely weightless, and the “full” leg does not have all the weight placed upon it.

V012-N00647.label: 虚领顶劲
V012-N00647.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00647.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00647.aliases: 提顶； 顶头悬； 贯顶； 悬顶； Head upright, spirit rising
V012-N00647.englishNames: Hold the head as if suspended from above
V012-N00647.definitions: The head is held as if suspended by a thread attached to the crown, at the point Baihui (Du20). This posture aims to avoid tension in the neck. In doing so, do not tilt the head from side to side, or up and down. The whole body is light and nimble when the head is suspended at the crown.

V012-N00648.label: 裹裆
V012-N00648.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00648.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00648.definitions: To adduct the knees intentionally to protect the crotch. This helps to distinguish empty from solid, keep the lower body stable and increase the loading ability of the legs. This can also lengthen the legs.

V012-N00649.label: 身肢放长
V012-N00649.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00649.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00649.definitions: To lengthen the torso through “holding the head as if suspended from above”. To extend the arms through sinking the shoulders and dropping the elbows. To lengthen the legs through relaxing the waist, opening the hips and flexing the knees.

V012-N00650.label: 轻灵
V012-N00650.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00650.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00650.definitions: A distinctive feature of taijiquan. Mindfulness or “intent” guides the body movement and breathing; both breathing and internal qi are controlled by the mindfulness of the will. The “gentle” means to guide movements with intent instead of force. The “flexible” means to move the arms or legs with agility.

V012-N00651.label: 采劲
V012-N00651.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00651.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00651.definitions: Internal energy that grasps or pulls the opponent’s wrist or elbow downwards with the fingertips or palm.

V012-N00652.label: 采法
V012-N00652.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00652.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00652.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: to grasp the opponent’s wrist or elbow downwards with the fingertips or palm.

V012-N00653.label: 靠劲
V012-N00653.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00653.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00653.definitions: Internal energy to squeeze forward with your shoulder, back and hip. During pushing hands, this energy allows you to push the opponent with your shoulder, back or hip.

V012-N00654.label: 靠法
V012-N00654.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00654.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00654.definitions: One of the eight methods in pushing hands: to strike or push with the shoulder, upper back, arm or hip.

V012-N00655.label: 鼓腕
V012-N00655.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00655.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00655.definitions: Pump the wrist to soften and relax the hands.

V012-N00656.label: 齐四梢
V012-N00656.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜太极
V012-N00656.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00656.englishNames: Issue forces from the four extremities
V012-N00656.definitions: Intentionally projecting internal force through the tips of fingers and toes.

V012-N00657.label: 导引气功
V012-N00657.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N00657.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N00658.label: 三关
V012-N00658.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00658.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00658.definitions: The three areas in the ascending path along the Du meridian when internal qi circulates along the pathways of the Du and Ren meridians during practicing the “small heavenly circle” (or “microcosmic orbit”) meditation. They are arduous for internal qi to overcome and therefore called “passes” or “barriers”. Alternatively, they refer to the three steps in internal alchemy (Neidan): refining the essence to qi; refining the qi to spirit; and refining the spirit to emptiness. Some also use three passes to represent the ears, eyes and mouth.

V012-N00659.label: 丹田呼吸
V012-N00659.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00659.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00659.definitions: A beneficial variation of natural breathing with the Dantian area. Unlike usual abdominal breathing, this breathing method does not need mental focus or external force.

V012-N00660.label: 和合四象
V012-N00660.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00660.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00660.englishNames: Harmony of the eyes, ears, nose and tongue
V012-N00660.definitions: A specific state in qigong practice that requires dropping the eyelids, listening to the silence, regulating breathing by focusing the eyes on the tip of the nose and closing the mouth. This state helps to remove distracting thoughts during qigong practice.

V012-N00661.label: 大周天
V012-N00661.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00661.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00661.definitions: Originated in Daoism, it refers to the second stage of internal alchemical process (i.e., to refine qi to spirit). This is performed on the basis of the small heavenly circle exercise and is believed that the large heavenly circle can connect spirit with qi, improve health and attain longevity.

V012-N00662.label: 导引
V012-N00662.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00662.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00662.definitions: To guide qi to harmonize the body and stretch the body to make it soft. It aims to prevent and cure diseases and promote health through stretching exercise combined with breath and internal qi circulation.

V012-N00663.label: 恬淡虚无
V012-N00663.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00663.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00663.definitions: A state of peaceful inner joy and free from ego or desires. This principle on health cultivation was recorded in the Huang Di Nei Jing (Yellow Emperor’s Internal Classic) and highly valued by generations of qigong masters.

V012-N00664.label: 气功
V012-N00664.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜导引气功
V012-N00664.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00664.definitions: An exercise method to regulate the body, breath and mind to achieve body-mind, man-society and man-nature harmonization. It cultivates the essence, qi and spirit internally and sinews, bones and body externally according to ancient Chinese philosophy.

V012-N00665.label: 情志疗法
V012-N00665.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N00665.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00665.englishNames: Mental/emotional therapy; Emotional Therapy; emotional therapy
V012-N00665.definitions: Methods to treat disordered moods and emotions using Chinese medicine theories on emotions.

V012-N00666.label: 五志
V012-N00666.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00666.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00666.definitions: Joy, anger, grief, excess thinking and fear.

V012-N00667.label: 五志过极
V012-N00667.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00667.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00667.definitions: Pathological conditions that result from excessive mental activity impairing the essential qi of five zang organs and/or disturbing qi movement within zang–fu organs.

V012-N00668.label: 五行音乐
V012-N00668.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00668.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00668.definitions: A method to use different qualities of the five musical tones (gong, shang, jiao, zheng and yu), related to the five elements in the treatment of mental disorders.

V012-N00669.label: 喜则气缓
V012-N00669.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00669.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00669.englishNames: Joy causes the qi to slack
V012-N00669.definitions: Excess joy and/or euphoria cause qi to move too slowly and the qi movement becomes sluggish.

V012-N00670.label: 喜胜悲
V012-N00670.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00670.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00670.englishNames: Joy prevails over sadness; Joy Prevailing Over Sorrow
V012-N00670.pinyinNames: xǐ shèng bēi; xi sheng bei
V012-N00670.definitions: An emotion therapy based on the restraining cycle of the five-element theory: joy corresponds to fire; sadness corresponds to metal; fire restrains metal; and so happiness can overcome melancholy and sorrow.

V012-N00671.label: 怒则气上
V012-N00671.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00671.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00671.englishNames: Anger causes the qi to rise
V012-N00671.definitions: Rage and persistent anger cause disorderly ascending of the qi.

V012-N00672.label: 怒胜思
V012-N00672.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00672.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00672.englishNames: Anger prevails over excessive thinking; Anger Prevailing Over Thought
V012-N00672.pinyinNames: nù shèng sī; nu sheng si
V012-N00672.definitions: An emotion therapy based on the restraining cycle of the five-element theory: anger corresponds to wood; thinking corresponds to earth; and wood restrains earth; so provocation to elicit an angry response will relieve anxiety and over-thinking.

V012-N00673.label: 思胜恐
V012-N00673.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00673.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00673.englishNames: Thought Prevailing Over Fear; Thinking prevails over fear
V012-N00673.pinyinNames: si sheng kong; sī shèng kǒng
V012-N00673.definitions: An emotion therapy based on the restraining cycle of the five-element theory: thinking corresponds to earth; fear corresponds to water; earth restrains water; so encouraging mental focus or meditation can help the patient ignore or forget fear.

V012-N00674.label: 恐则气下
V012-N00674.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00674.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00674.englishNames: Fear causes the qi to descend
V012-N00674.definitions: Excess fear or an ongoing state of fearfulness causes qi to strongly descend.

V012-N00675.label: 恐胜喜
V012-N00675.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00675.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00675.englishNames: Fear Prevailing Over Joy; Fear prevails over excess joy
V012-N00675.pinyinNames: kong sheng xi; kǒng shèng xǐ
V012-N00675.definitions: An emotion therapy based on the restraining cycle of the five-element theory: fear corresponds to water; joy corresponds to fire; water restrains fire; so eliciting a fearful response will restrain excess joy and euphoria.

V012-N00676.label: 悲则气消
V012-N00676.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00676.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00676.definitions: Excess sorrow and grief weaken the qi.

V012-N00677.label: 悲胜怒
V012-N00677.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00677.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00677.englishNames: Sadness prevails over anger; Sorrow Prevailing Over Anger
V012-N00677.pinyinNames: bēi shèng nù; bei sheng nu
V012-N00677.definitions: An emotion therapy based on the restraining cycle of the five-element theory: sadness corresponds to metal; anger corresponds to wood; metal restrains wood; so sad feelings and language will subdue or release anger.

V012-N00678.label: 情志相胜法
V012-N00678.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00678.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00678.englishNames: Restraining emotions method; Emotional Restrictive Therapy
V012-N00678.pinyinNames: qíng zhì xiāng shèng fǎ; qing zhi xiang sheng fa
V012-N00678.definitions: A psychotherapeutic method that uses the mutual restraint cycle of five element theory in the treatment of mental-emotional disorders, during which the doctor intentionally arouses certain feelings in the patient that counteract their abnormal emotional state.

V012-N00679.label: 惊则气乱
V012-N00679.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00679.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00679.englishNames: Fright causes the qi to become chaotic
V012-N00679.definitions: Sudden shock and fright disrupt the qi activity and causes qi movements to become confused and disorderly.

V012-N00680.label: 惊者平之法
V012-N00680.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00680.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00680.definitions: A method to treat mental-emotional diseases such as anxiety, panic and post-traumatic stress disorders. Under controlled conditions, by repeated exposure to the circumstance that trigger the patient’s anxiety or traumatic response disorder, their adverse reaction is gradually lessened and normalized.

V012-N00681.label: 意疗法
V012-N00681.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00681.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00681.englishNames: Imagery therapy; mental therapy; mental therapeutics
V012-N00681.pinyinNames: yi liao fa; yì liáo fǎ
V012-N00681.definitions: A method to treat mental disorders by communicating with the patient’s self-image.

V012-N00682.label: 活套法
V012-N00682.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00682.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00682.definitions: Flexible psychotherapeutic methods that apply the mutual promotion or mutual restraint relationship among the five phases to different mental-emotional disorders. This idea was first mentioned in the Su Wen and later promulgated by Zhu Danxi.

V012-N00683.label: 祝由法
V012-N00683.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜情志疗法
V012-N00683.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00683.definitions: A psychotherapeutic treatment protocol that combines analysis of causative factors of mental-emotional disorders with prayer. These analyses and prayer treatments are based on emotion theories and psychological principles in Chinese medicine.

V012-N00684.label: 推拿类
V012-N00684.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N00684.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N00685.label: 一指禅偏锋推法
V012-N00685.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00685.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00685.englishNames: Yi Zhi Chan pushing with the radial side of the thumb
V012-N00685.pinyinNames: yì zhǐ chán piān fēng tuī fǎ
V012-N00685.definitions: A variation of Yi Zhi Chan pushing manipulation using the radial side of the thumb.

V012-N00686.label: 一指禅推法
V012-N00686.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00686.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00686.englishNames: one-finger-pushing manipulation; Yi Zhi Chan pushing
V012-N00686.pinyinNames: yi zhi chan tui fa; yì zhǐ chán tuī fǎ
V012-N00686.definitions: A tuina manipulation to exert constant force on the area to be treated with the tip or the palmar side of the thumb through active swing of the forearm.

V012-N00687.label: 健身推拿
V012-N00687.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00687.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00687.definitions: A tuina method for healthy people or people with suboptimal health to strengthen the body and help with disease prevention and treatment.

V012-N00688.label: 其他推拿术语
V012-N00688.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00688.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00688.pinyinNames: qí tā tuī ná shù yǔ

V012-N00689.label: 击法
V012-N00689.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00689.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00689.definitions: A tuina manipulation to knock the surface of the body with the fist, palms, fingers or mulberry sticks.

V012-N00690.label: 刨推法
V012-N00690.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00690.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00690.definitions: A pushing manipulation with the Hukou area (formed between the thumb and index finger).

V012-N00691.label: 刮法
V012-N00691.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00691.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00691.definitions: A tuina manipulation to scrape with the radial side of the index finger, on the back of the interphalangeal joint of the index finger. Alternatively, scraping can be done with tools such as spoons or coins.

V012-N00692.label: 劈法
V012-N00692.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00692.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00692.definitions: A tuina manipulation to chop the area between the fingers with the ulnar side of the palm.

V012-N00693.label: 勒法
V012-N00693.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00693.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00693.definitions: A tuina manipulation to clamp the affected fingers or toes with the fingers and apply rapid pulling.

V012-N00694.label: 勾法
V012-N00694.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00694.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00694.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the index finger and middle finger together and flex into a hook, stick to the skin with the radial border of the middle phalanx of index finger and then apply continuous pushing and wiping manipulation.

V012-N00695.label: 勾点法
V012-N00695.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00695.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00695.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of hooking and point-pressing manipulations.

V012-N00696.label: 压法
V012-N00696.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00696.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00696.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply continuous, forceful pressure to the surface of the body with the palmar side of the thumbs, palms or elbows.

V012-N00697.label: 叩法
V012-N00697.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00697.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00697.definitions: A tuina manipulation to tap the area to be treated with the ulnar side of the little finger or a hollow fist.

V012-N00698.label: 啄法
V012-N00698.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00698.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00698.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the five fingers together and peck the area to be treated.

V012-N00699.label: 太平车
V012-N00699.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00699.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00699.definitions: An assistant tuina tool comprising an iron bar with five to six pieces of jade or sandalwood made into a chain of beads. The head and tail of the iron bar are folded. The appliance is connected to a short handle and can be held in the hand and the beads work like a rolling wheel. It is often used on the face, bone joints, etc.

V012-N00700.label: 封腰法
V012-N00700.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00700.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00700.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the thumbs and the middle fingers over the lumbar triangles on both sides and apply soft, gentle pressure.

V012-N00701.label: 小儿推拿
V012-N00701.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00701.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00701.definitions: A type of tuina therapy based on the theory of traditional Chinese medicine, using specific manipulations on the meridians and collaterals and specific points on the surface of the body. It helps to prevent against diseases and benefit health through harmonizing Zang–Fu organs, yin, yang, qi and blood, strengthen the body and promote the healthy growth and development of children.

V012-N00702.label: 少林内功
V012-N00702.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00702.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00702.definitions: A main training method in tuina exercise and a key part of Neigong tuina. It is originally a form of martial art to strengthen the body, but now becomes a distinctive method and school of self-exercise plus tuina treatment.

V012-N00703.label: 屈伸法
V012-N00703.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00703.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00703.definitions: A tuina manipulation to slowly and repeatedly flex and extend the joints to stretch the surrounding tissues, and increase the joint range of motion.

V012-N00704.label: 弹法
V012-N00704.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00704.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00704.definitions: A tuina manipulation to flip the area to be treated with fingernails or palmar sides of the fingers.

V012-N00705.label: 弹筋法
V012-N00705.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00705.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00705.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of pinching and lifting manipulations.

V012-N00706.label: 托法
V012-N00706.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00706.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00706.definitions: A tuina manipulation to lift the affected area with one or both hands. It is commonly used for gastroptosis.

V012-N00707.label: 扪法
V012-N00707.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00707.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00707.definitions: A tuina manipulation to rub the palms and gently place (the warm palms) over the area to be treated.

V012-N00708.label: 扫散法
V012-N00708.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00708.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00708.definitions: A tuina manipulation to push and scrub along the Shaoyang meridian in the temporal region using the radial side of the thumb and tips of the other four fingers.

V012-N00709.label: 扳法
V012-N00709.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00709.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00709.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply a sudden thrust force to the affected joint to produce passive rotation, flexion, extension, abduction and adduction.

V012-N00710.label: 抄法
V012-N00710.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00710.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00710.definitions: A tuina manipulation: Place the palms on the lower borders of the subcostal region and extend gradually to the low back, press and knead the soft tissues on both sides of the low back using the palmar sides of the fingers, lift the low back and abdomen and then swing to the left and right gently.

V012-N00711.label: 抓法
V012-N00711.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00711.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00711.definitions: A tuina manipulation to grab and pinch the area to be treated with five fingertips.

V012-N00712.label: 抖法
V012-N00712.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00712.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00712.definitions: A tuina manipulation to grasp the affected limb and apply small-amplitude radial shakings.

V012-N00713.label: 抵法
V012-N00713.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00713.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00713.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply symmetrical pressure to the area to be treated with the fingers or palms.

V012-N00714.label: 抹法
V012-N00714.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00714.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00714.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply top-down, left-right or arch-shaped wiping with the palmar side of the thumbs or the palms.

V012-N00715.label: 押法
V012-N00715.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00715.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00715.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press the area to be treated with the palmar side of the thumbs.

V012-N00716.label: 拂法
V012-N00716.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00716.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00716.definitions: A tuina manipulation to brush or scrub the surface of the body with the fingers.

V012-N00717.label: 拍法
V012-N00717.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00717.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00717.definitions: A tuina manipulation to pat the area to be treated with the palms or fingers.

V012-N00718.label: 拔伸法
V012-N00718.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00718.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00718.definitions: A tuina manipulation to immobilize one end of the affected joint or limb and pull the other end along the longitudinal axis.

V012-N00719.label: 拢法
V012-N00719.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00719.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00719.definitions: A tuina manipulation to clamp and release the skin of the area to be treated with the ulnar aspects of both palms.

V012-N00720.label: 拧法
V012-N00720.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00720.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00720.definitions: 用屈曲食指与拇指，或与屈曲中指，张开如钳状，夹住治疗部位的皮肤，做反复扭捏动作的手法。
V012-N00720.definitionSources: 推拿手法学 -- 周信文 -- 上海中医药大学出版社 -- 1996.11

V012-N00721.label: 拨法
V012-N00721.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00721.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00721.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press the area to be treated with fingers or elbows and then apply perpendicular plucking force to the subcutaneous tissue.

V012-N00722.label: 拳顶法
V012-N00722.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00722.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00722.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply pressure to the area to be treated with a fist.

V012-N00723.label: 拿揉法
V012-N00723.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00723.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00723.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of grasping and kneading manipulations.

V012-N00724.label: 拿法
V012-N00724.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00724.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00724.definitions: A tuina manipulation to pinch and knead the area to be treated with the thumb and the other four fingers.

V012-N00725.label: 按揉法
V012-N00725.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00725.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00725.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of pressing and kneading manipulations.

V012-N00726.label: 按法
V012-N00726.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00726.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00726.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply perpendicular pressure to the surface of the body with the palmar side of the fingers, palms or elbows. It is also one of the common diagnostic methods in tuina practice.

V012-N00727.label: 挤法[推拿]
V012-N00727.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00727.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00727.definitions: A tuina manipulation to squeeze both sides of the area to be treated with one or both hands.

V012-N00728.label: 挪法
V012-N00728.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00728.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00728.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the palm over the area to be treated, grasp and lift the muscle either using the thumb and the other four fingers or exerting a symmetric force from the base of the palm and fingers, hold for a while and then shift forward to repeat the procedure.

V012-N00729.label: 振法
V012-N00729.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00729.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00729.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press acupuncture points or certain body parts with the fingers or palms and apply fast, continuous vibration.

V012-N00730.label: 挽法
V012-N00730.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00730.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00730.definitions: A tuina manipulation to pull or move the limbs of the patient.

V012-N00731.label: 捋法[推拿]
V012-N00731.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00731.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00731.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the palm or fingers over the area to be treated, gradually push from the proximal end to the distal end, and then release the force quickly.

V012-N00732.label: 捏法
V012-N00732.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00732.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00732.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply coordinated pressure to the area to be treated with the thumb and the other four fingers.

V012-N00733.label: 捏脊法
V012-N00733.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00733.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00733.definitions: A tuina manipulation to pinch and lift the skin of the spine symmetrically with the thumb, index finger and middle finger or with the thumb and the other four fingers and release the force immediately. Move forward gradually and repeat the same procedure.

V012-N00734.label: 捩法
V012-N00734.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00734.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00734.definitions: A tuina manipulation to make a passive rotation of the joints.

V012-N00735.label: 捻法
V012-N00735.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00735.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00735.definitions: A tuina manipulation to hold the area to be treated with the thumb and index finger and apply back-and-forth twisting.

V012-N00736.label: 推扳法
V012-N00736.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00736.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00736.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of plucking and pulling manipulations.

V012-N00737.label: 推拿
V012-N00737.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00737.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00737.aliases: 按摩； 按跷； 乔摩； 案扤； Anmo, Anqiao, Qiaomo, Anwu, etc.
V012-N00737.definitions: A Chinese medical therapy to apply manipulation to points or body areas for disease treatment and prevention as well as promoting health. Sometimes, patients are educated to exercise in addition to receiving treatment.

V012-N00738.label: 推拿介质
V012-N00738.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00738.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00738.definitions: Lotions applied to the surface of the body for lubrication or treatment.

V012-N00739.label: 推拿复合手法类
V012-N00739.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00739.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00739.pinyinNames: tuī ná fù hé shǒu fǎ lèi

V012-N00740.label: 推拿手法
V012-N00740.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00740.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00740.definitions: Specific, standardized methods and movements to treat/prevent diseases and benefit health. Tuina manipulations are supposed to be lasting, forceful, even, soft and penetrating to the deeper tissue.

V012-N00741.label: 推拿辅助器具类
V012-N00741.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00741.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00741.pinyinNames: tuī ná fǔ zhù qì jù lèi
V012-N00741.definitions: Supplementary tools to assist in tuina treatment and health care.

V012-N00742.label: 推拿镇痛
V012-N00742.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00742.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00742.definitions: One of the pain control methods in Chinese medicine by applying Tuina to reduce or control pain according to theories of Chinese medicine.

V012-N00743.label: 推揉法
V012-N00743.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00743.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00743.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of Yi Zhi Chan pushing and kneading manipulation.

V012-N00744.label: 推摩法
V012-N00744.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00744.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00744.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of Yi Zhi Chan pushing using the radial side of the thumb and rubbing circularly with the other four fingers.

V012-N00745.label: 推法
V012-N00745.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00745.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00745.definitions: A one-way linear pushing manipulation with the fingers, palms, or elbows.

V012-N00746.label: 掩法
V012-N00746.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00746.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00746.definitions: A tuina manipulation to gently cover the area to be treated with the hand and remain still.

V012-N00747.label: 揉捏法
V012-N00747.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00747.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00747.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of kneading and pinching manipulations.

V012-N00748.label: 揉法
V012-N00748.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00748.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00748.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply perpendicular pressure to the surface of the body with the fingers, palms or elbows. The pressure is supposed to reach subcutaneous tissues. It includes kneading with the finger, kneading with the palm and kneading with the forearm.

V012-N00749.label: 提弹法
V012-N00749.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00749.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00749.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of tendon-flicking and muscle plucking manipulations.

V012-N00750.label: 提法
V012-N00750.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00750.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00750.definitions: A tuina manipulation to lift the post-traumatic sunken bones or joints to normal position using one or both hands or with the help of a rope.

V012-N00751.label: 插法
V012-N00751.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00751.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00751.definitions: A tuina manipulation to insert the fingers into the space between the scapula and thoracic wall. It is specifically used for gastroptosis.

V012-N00752.label: 握法
V012-N00752.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00752.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00752.definitions: A tuina manipulation to clutch and release the limb with the palm.

V012-N00753.label: 搓法
V012-N00753.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00753.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00753.definitions: A tuina manipulation to hold the limb with both hands/palms and apply alternating or back-and-forth twisting.

V012-N00754.label: 摇法
V012-N00754.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00754.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00754.definitions: A tuina manipulation to conduct passive rotation of the affected joint along the axis of motion.

V012-N00755.label: 摩法
V012-N00755.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00755.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00755.definitions: A Tuina manipulation to rub the area to be treated circularly with the fingers or palms.

V012-N00756.label: 摸法
V012-N00756.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00756.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00756.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press the limbs or acupuncture points with fingers to help with diagnosis.

V012-N00757.label: 擦法
V012-N00757.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00757.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00757.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply linear rubbing with the hypothenar, great thenar, palms or fingers.

V012-N00758.label: 易筋经
V012-N00758.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00758.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00758.definitions: A traditional health-building exercise to stretch the body, strengthen the sinews, bones and ligaments and regulate the meridians and zang–fu organs.

V012-N00759.label: 桑枝棒
V012-N00759.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00759.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00759.definitions: An assistant tuina tool made of 12 specific mulberry branches based on certain requirements with a strong effect to unblock and circulate qi and blood. It is often used on the top of the head, chest and back, waist, hip and limbs.

V012-N00760.label: 梳法
V012-N00760.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00760.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00760.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place the palmar sides of the fingers over the area to be treated and apply soft, one-way combing.

V012-N00761.label: 滚法
V012-N00761.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00761.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00761.englishNames: rolling manipulation; Rolling manipulation with the proximal interphalangeal joints
V012-N00761.definitions: A tuina manipulation to clench a fist to allow the distal phalanx bone of the thumb to be inside the fist, stick to the area to be treated with the proximal interphalangeal joints of the index, middle, ring and little fingers and then apply back-and-forth rolling manipulation.

V012-N00762.label: 点法
V012-N00762.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00762.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00762.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply perpendicular pressure to the surface of the body with finger tips, interphalangeal joints or elbows.

V012-N00763.label: 点脊法
V012-N00763.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00763.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00763.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press the bladder meridian on both sides of the spine.

V012-N00764.label: 热敷
V012-N00764.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00764.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00764.definitions: A common external therapy in Chinese medicine to treat diseases through heat stimulation.

V012-N00765.label: 牵抖法
V012-N00765.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00765.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00765.definitions: A compound tuina manipulation composed of pulling-stretching and shaking manipulations.

V012-N00766.label: 理法
V012-N00766.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00766.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00766.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply rhythmic grasping and pinching of the limb.

V012-N00767.label: 碟转法
V012-N00767.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00767.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00767.definitions: A tuina manipulation to apply rotating pressure to the area to be treated with the palms.

V012-N00768.label: 端法
V012-N00768.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00768.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00768.definitions: A tuina manipulation to hold or lift the affected limb with one or both hands.

V012-N00769.label: 缠法
V012-N00769.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00769.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00769.englishNames: Fast Yi Zhi Chan pushing manipulation
V012-N00769.definitions: A fast Yi Zhi Chan pushing manipulation with a frequency of more than 220 times/min.

V012-N00770.label: 美容推拿
V012-N00770.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00770.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00770.definitions: A type of tuina method to achieve healthier skin and slow down the ageing process of the skin.

V012-N00771.label: 背法
V012-N00771.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00771.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00771.definitions: A tuina manipulation to carry the patient on the back and apply traction, shaking, vibration and instant extension to the affected lumbar vertebrae.

V012-N00772.label: 脊柱推拿手法类
V012-N00772.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00772.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00772.pinyinNames: jǐ zhù tuī ná shǒu fǎ lèi
V012-N00772.definitions: Manipulations used on the spine to correct spinal subluxation and regulate the functions of the spine.

V012-N00773.label: 膏摩
V012-N00773.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00773.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00773.definitions: A therapeutic method to apply herbal ointment on the surface of the area to be treated, followed by tuina manipulations.

V012-N00774.label: 贯法
V012-N00774.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00774.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00774.definitions: A tuina manipulation to place one palm over the area to be treated and percuss the dorsum of the hand with a hollow fist made by the other hand.

V012-N00775.label: 跪推法
V012-N00775.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00775.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00775.englishNames: Yi Zhi Chan pushing with the flexed thumb
V012-N00775.definitions: A variation of Yi Zhi Chan pushing manipulation with the radial or dorsal side of the interphalangeal joint of the thumb.

V012-N00776.label: 踩跷法
V012-N00776.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00776.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00776.definitions: A tuina manipulation to step with the feet on certain areas of the body.

V012-N00777.label: 颤腰法
V012-N00777.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00777.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00777.definitions: A tuina manipulation to press the low back coupled with traction and stretching.

V012-N00778.label: 鱼际揉法
V012-N00778.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00778.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00778.definitions: A tuina manipulation to knead and exert force on the area to be treated with the great thenar eminence through rhythmic swing of the forearm.

V012-N00779.label: ？法
V012-N00779.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜推拿类
V012-N00779.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00779.definitions: A tuina manipulation to stick to the area to be treated with the fifth metacarpophalangeal joint and conduct back-and-forth rolling using the hypothenar eminence and the ulnar side of the back of the hand.

V012-N00780.label: 治则
V012-N00780.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N00780.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00780.englishNames: therapeutic principle; Therapeutic Principles; Treatment principles

V012-N00781.label: 从阳引阴
V012-N00781.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00781.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00781.aliases: 阳中求阴； 病在阳而治其阴
V012-N00781.englishNames: Seeking yin from yang; treating yang for yin disorder; treating yin for yang disorder; seeking yin from yang
V012-N00781.pinyinNames: cóng yáng yǐn yīn; cong yang yin yin
V012-N00781.definitions: This is originally a needling method to puncture yang meridians for problems of yin meridians. Later, this method has been developed into a principle based on interdependence between yin and yang: to add some yang-reinforcing medicines to yin-nourishing formulas to assist the generation of yin qi.

V012-N00782.label: 从阴引阳
V012-N00782.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00782.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00782.aliases: 病在阳而治其阴； 阴中求阳
V012-N00782.englishNames: treating yin for yang disorder; treating yang for yin disorder; Seeking yang from yin; seeking yang from yin
V012-N00782.pinyinNames: cong yin yin yang; cóng yīn yǐn yáng
V012-N00782.definitions: This is originally a needling method to puncture yin meridians for problems of yang meridians. Later, this method has been developed into a treatment principle based on interdependence between yin and yang: to add some yin-nourishing medicines to yang-reinforcing formulas to assist the transformation of yang qi.

V012-N00783.label: 先攻后补
V012-N00783.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00783.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00783.englishNames: eliminating pathogenic factors prior to reinforcing healthy qi; Remove pathogenic factors before reinforcing healthy qi; Attacking the Pathogens before Tonifying; reinforcement after elimination
V012-N00783.pinyinNames: xiān gōng hòu bǔ; xian gong hou bu
V012-N00783.definitions: A principle to remove the invading pathogenic factors, followed by reinforcing anti-pathogenic qi. It is indicated for deficiency of healthy qi coupled with acute conditions that require immediate removal of pathogenic factors.

V012-N00784.label: 先补后攻
V012-N00784.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00784.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00784.englishNames: elimination after reinforcement; reinforcing healthy qi prior to eliminating pathogenic factors; Reinforce healthy qi before removing pathogenic factors; Tonifying before Attacking the Pathogens
V012-N00784.pinyinNames: xiān bǔ hòu gōng; xian bu hou gong
V012-N00784.definitions: A principle to reinforce healthy qi, followed by removing the invading pathogenic factors. It is indicated for acute conditions that require immediate removal of pathogneic factors coupled with deficiency of healthy qi.

V012-N00785.label: 先表后里
V012-N00785.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00785.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00785.englishNames: Treat the exterior before the interior; treating the exterior before the interior
V012-N00785.definitions: A principle to treat an exterior pattern before an interior pattern. It applies to a severe exterior pattern but mild interior pattern in concurrent exterior and interior pattern. This principle aims to prevent pathogenic factors in the exterior from entering the interior.

V012-N00786.label: 反治法
V012-N00786.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00786.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00786.definitions: A principle to address false signs or symptoms in extreme conditions such as extreme cold or heat. Under such conditions, it is necessary to use medicines similar in nature to the disease (i.e. follow the false symptoms), for example, to treat false heat with heat or treat false cold with cold.

V012-N00787.label: 同病异治
V012-N00787.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00787.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00787.englishNames: Different treatments for same disease; different treatments for the same disease; different treatment for the same disease; Same Disease Different Treatments
V012-N00787.pinyinNames: tóng bìng yì zhì; tong bing yi zhi
V012-N00787.definitions: A principle to use different treatments for the same disease caused by different etiological factors; or a principle to treat the same disease according to differences in individuality, time, geographic features, disease severity, differentiated patterns, and ongoing struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors.

V012-N00788.label: 因人制宜
V012-N00788.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00788.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00788.englishNames: Treatment in Accordance with Patient's Individuality; treatment in accordance with individual; treatment in accordance with individualized conditions; Treat according to person
V012-N00788.pinyinNames: yin ren zhi yi; yīn rén zhì yí
V012-N00788.definitions: A principle to use an appropriate method and medicine according to individual gender, age and constitution.

V012-N00789.label: 因地制宜
V012-N00789.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00789.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00789.englishNames: Treatment in Accordance with Local Conditions; Treat according to place; treatment in accordance with geographic features; treatment in accordance with place
V012-N00789.pinyinNames: yin di zhi yi; yīn dì zhì yí
V012-N00789.definitions: A principle to use an appropriate method and medicine according to geographic features.

V012-N00790.label: 因时制宜
V012-N00790.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00790.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00790.englishNames: Treatment in Accordance with Seasonal Conditions; Treat according to time; treatment in accordance with seasons; treatment in accordance with time
V012-N00790.pinyinNames: yin shi zhi yi; yīn shí zhì yí
V012-N00790.definitions: A principle to use an appropriate method and medicine according to seasonal climate changes.

V012-N00791.label: 实则泻之
V012-N00791.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00791.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00791.englishNames: Treating Excess Syndrome with Purgation and Reduction; treat excess by purgation; Treat excess with purgation/reduction; treating excess with purgation
V012-N00791.pinyinNames: shi ze xie zhi; shí zé xiè zhī
V012-N00791.definitions: A principle to treat excess conditions with medicines or formulas that are purgative or reducing in nature.

V012-N00792.label: 实者泻其子
V012-N00792.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00792.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00792.englishNames: reducing chuild-element in excess condition; Reduce the son (element) for excess pattern; purging the child-organ for excess syndrome/patterns
V012-N00792.definitions: A principle to treat excess of zang–fu organs based on the five-element theory. For example, since (heart) fire promotes (spleen earth), hyperactivity of heart fire can be treated by reducing the spleen and stomach.

V012-N00793.label: 寒因寒用
V012-N00793.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00793.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00793.englishNames: treating cold with cold; Treat false cold with cold; treating coldness with coldness; Cold Drugs Treating Pseudocold
V012-N00793.pinyinNames: hán yīn hán yòng; han yin han yong
V012-N00793.definitions: A principle to treat false cold (true heat) symptoms with medicines cool or cold in nature.

V012-N00794.label: 寒者热之
V012-N00794.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00794.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00794.englishNames: treat cold with heat; Treat cold with heat; treating cold with heat; treating coldness with heat
V012-N00794.definitions: A principle to treat cold conditions with medicines or formulas that are warm or hot in nature.

V012-N00795.label: 异病同治
V012-N00795.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00795.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00795.aliases: Differ Dis Same Treat； Different Diseases in Same Treatment
V012-N00795.englishNames: Same treatment for different diseases; Treating Different Diseases with Same Method; same treatment for different disease; same treatment for different diseases
V012-N00795.pinyinNames: yì bìng tóng zhì; yi bing tong zhi
V012-N00795.definitions: A principle to use same treatment for different diseases that share the same pathogenesis.

V012-N00796.label: 急则治标
V012-N00796.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00796.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00796.englishNames: treating symptom for urgency; Treat the tip first in acute conditions; treating the tip in acute phases; ji ze zhi biao
V012-N00796.pinyinNames: jí zé zhì biāo; Symptomatic Treatment in Acute Condition
V012-N00796.definitions: A principle to address symptoms in acute conditions, for example, it is important to stop bleeding for heavy blood loss, to arrest diarrhoea for fulminant diarrhoea, alleviate pain for severe, intense pain and promote urination for urine retention.

V012-N00797.label: 扶正固本
V012-N00797.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00797.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00797.aliases: 扶正培本； Strength Resist Consol Constit
V012-N00797.englishNames: Reinforce healthy qi to strengthen the body; reinforcing healthy qi and securing the root; Strengthening Body Resistance to Consolidate Constitution
V012-N00797.pinyinNames: fú zhèng gù běn; fu zheng gu ben
V012-N00797.definitions: A principle to reinforce anti-pathogenic qi to speed up recovery. It is indicated for deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N00798.label: 扶正祛邪
V012-N00798.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00798.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00798.aliases: 扶正达邪； Strength Resist Elimin Pathog
V012-N00798.englishNames: strengthening healthy qi and eliminating pathogen; Strengthening Body Resistance to Eliminate Pathogenic Factors; reinforcing the healthy qi to eliminate the pathogenic factors; Reinforce healthy qi to eliminate pathogenic factors; reinforcing healthy qi and eliminating pathogenic factors; reinforce the healthy qi and eliminate the pathogenic factors
V012-N00798.pinyinNames: fu zheng qu xie; fu zheng 祛xie; fú zhèng qū xié
V012-N00798.definitions: A principle to reinforce anti-pathogenic qi to drive out the invading pathogenic factors. It is indicated for deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi coupled with exposure to pathogenic factors.

V012-N00799.label: 攻补兼施
V012-N00799.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00799.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00799.englishNames: treat with both elimination and reinforcement; Reinforce healthy qi and remove pathogenic factors simultaneously; treating with both elimination and reinforcement; simultaneous eliminination and reinforcement; Reinforcement and Elimination
V012-N00799.pinyinNames: gōng bǔ jiān shī; gong bu jian shi
V012-N00799.definitions: A principle to reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and, at the same time, remove pathogenic factors. It is indicated for excessive pathogenic factors coupled with deficiency of healthy qi.

V012-N00800.label: 标本兼治
V012-N00800.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00800.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00800.aliases: 标本同治； Treat Both Symptom and Radical
V012-N00800.englishNames: treating both the tip and root; Treat both the tip and root; Treating Both Symptoms and Radical
V012-N00800.pinyinNames: biāo běn jiān zhì; biao ben jian zhi
V012-N00800.definitions: A principle to address both symptoms and root cause simultaneously when needed.

V012-N00801.label: 正治法
V012-N00801.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00801.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N00802.label: 热因热用
V012-N00802.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00802.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00802.englishNames: treating heat with heat; treating hotness with hotness; Hot Drugs Treating Pseudoheat; Treat false heat with heat
V012-N00802.pinyinNames: re yin re yong; rè yīn rè yòng
V012-N00802.definitions: A principle to treat false heat (true cold) symptoms with medicines warm or hot in nature.

V012-N00803.label: 热者寒之
V012-N00803.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00803.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00803.englishNames: treating hotness with coldness; treat heat with cold; Treat heat with cold; treating heat with cold
V012-N00803.definitions: A principle to treat heat conditions with medicines or formulas that are cool or cold in nature.

V012-N00804.label: 祛邪扶正
V012-N00804.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00804.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00804.englishNames: Eliminate pathogenic factors and reinforce healthy qi; eliminating pathogenic factors and reinforcing healthy qi
V012-N00804.definitions: A principle to drive out the invading pathogenic factors, supplemented by reinforcing anti-pathogenic qi. It is indicated for excessive pathogenic factors with insufficiency of anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N00805.label: 缓则治本
V012-N00805.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00805.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00805.englishNames: treating the root in remissive phases; Treat the root in remissive stages; treating root cause for non-urgency; Radical Treatment in Chronic Case
V012-N00805.pinyinNames: huǎn zé zhì běn; huan ze zhi ben
V012-N00805.definitions: A principle to regulate and reinforce the body to address the root cause when medical conditions become stable.

V012-N00806.label: 虚则补之
V012-N00806.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00806.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00806.englishNames: treat deficiency by tonification; Treat deficiency with reinforcement; Treating Deficiency by Tonifying; treating deficiency with reinforcement; treating deficiency with tonification
V012-N00806.pinyinNames: xū zé bǔ zhī; xu ze bu zhi
V012-N00806.definitions: A principle to treat deficiency conditions with tonic medicines or formulas.

V012-N00807.label: 虚者补其母
V012-N00807.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00807.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00807.englishNames: Reinforce the mother (element) for deficiency pattern; reinforcing mother-element in deficiency condition; nourishing the mother-organ in dealing for deficiency syndrome/patterns
V012-N00807.definitions: A principle to treat deficiency of zang–fu organs based on the five-element theory. For example, since (spleen) earth promotes (lung) metal, lung deficiency can be treated by reinforcing the spleen and stomach.

V012-N00808.label: 调理阴阳
V012-N00808.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00808.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00808.aliases: 调平阴阳； 调整阴阳
V012-N00808.englishNames: Regulate and balance yin and yang; regulating yin and yang
V012-N00808.definitions: A principle to restore yin-yang equilibrium through medicines, food, emotions or reinforcing/reducing needling techniques.

V012-N00809.label: 通因通用
V012-N00809.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治则
V012-N00809.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00809.englishNames: Purgatives Treating Diarrhea; Treat uncontrolled discharge by unblocking; treating incontinent syndrome with dredging method; treating uncontrolled discharge with purgation; treating the unstopped by unstopping; treating diarrhea with purgatives
V012-N00809.pinyinNames: tong yin tong yong; tōng yīn tōng yòng
V012-N00809.definitions: A principle to treat food stagnation-related vomiting and diarrhoea or uterine bleeding due to blood stasis with purgative or stasis-unblocking medicines or formulas.

V012-N00810.label: 治法
V012-N00810.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N00810.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00810.aliases: 中医治法； Method of Treatment
V012-N00810.englishNames: method of treatment; Treatment methods; Therapeutic Methods

V012-N00811.label: 交通心肾
V012-N00811.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00811.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00811.englishNames: coordinate the heart and kidney; coordinating the heart and kidney; restoring normal coordination between heart and kidney; Coordinate the heart and kidney; restoring coordination between heart and kidney; Restoring Normal Coordination Between Heart and Kidney
V012-N00811.pinyinNames: jiao tong xin shen; jiāo tōng xīn shèn
