V012-N00811.definitions: A treatment method to nourish kidney yin, astringe kidney yang, reduce heart fire and calm the heart mind to submerge yang and coordinate between the heart and kidney. It is indicated for conditions due to disharmony between the heart fire and kidney water.

V012-N00812.label: 以毒攻毒
V012-N00812.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00812.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00812.englishNames: fighting toxin with toxin; predominating over toxin with toxin; Remove toxins with toxins
V012-N00812.pinyinNames: yi dong gong dong; yǐ dú gōng dú
V012-N00812.definitions: A treatment method to remove toxins with poisonous medicines.

V012-N00813.label: 佐金平木
V012-N00813.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00813.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00813.englishNames: Reinforce metal and inhibit wood; supporting metal to suppress wood
V012-N00813.pinyinNames: zuǒ jīn píng mù; zuo jin ping mu
V012-N00813.definitions: A treatment method to disperse the lung (metal) and inhibit the liver (wood). It is indicated for migratory pain in the subcostal region, cough or panting due to hyperactivity of liver yang and failure of lung qi to disperse and descend.

V012-N00814.label: 健脾化湿
V012-N00814.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00814.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00814.aliases: 扶脾化湿； 扶脾祛湿； 化湿和中； 健脾化浊； 健脾利湿； 健脾利水； 健脾祛湿； 健脾渗湿； Supplement the spleen and resolve dampness； 扶脾醒湿
V012-N00814.englishNames: Invigorating Spleen for Eliminating Dampness; Strengthen the spleen and transform dampness; fortifying the spleen to resolve dampness; invigorating spleen for eliminating dampness
V012-N00814.pinyinNames: jian pi hua shi; jiàn pí huà shī
V012-N00814.definitions: A treatment method to tonify spleen qi to transform turbid phlegm. It is indicated for conditions due to spleen deficiency coupled with dampness.

V012-N00815.label: 健脾驱虫
V012-N00815.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00815.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00815.aliases: 补脾驱虫； Fortify the spleen and repel parasitic worms
V012-N00815.englishNames: Strengthen the spleen and repel parasitic worms; fortifying the spleen to expel worms; invigorating spleen for expelling intestinal parasites
V012-N00815.pinyinNames: jiàn pí qū chóng; jian pi qu chong
V012-N00815.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to reinforce the spleen and repel parasitic worms. It is indicated for spleen deficiency coupled with retention of parasitic worms.

V012-N00816.label: 其他治法
V012-N00816.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00816.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N00817.label: 养心安神
V012-N00817.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00817.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00817.aliases: 补心安神； Reinforcing Heart and Tranquilization； Reinforce the heart and calm the mind
V012-N00817.englishNames: Nourish the Heart to Tranquilize; nourishing heart and tranquilizing mind; nourish the heart to tranquilize; nourishing the heart to tranquilize mind; nourishing the heart to tranquilize spirit; Nourish the heart and calm the mind
V012-N00817.pinyinNames: yang xin an shen; yǎng xīn ān shén
V012-N00817.definitions: A treatment method to reinforce the heart and nourish blood. It is indicated for palpitations, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, poor memory and panic due to heart deficiency.

V012-N00818.label: 养血安神
V012-N00818.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00818.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00818.englishNames: nourishing blood and tranquilizing spirit; nourishing blood and tranquilizing mind; Nourishing Blood Tranquilization; nourishing blood for tranquillization; Nourish blood and calm the mind
V012-N00818.pinyinNames: yang xue an shen; yǎng xuè ān shén
V012-N00818.definitions: A treatment method to tonify blood and nourish the heart. It is indicated for palpitations, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, poor memory and panic due to heart blood deficiency.

V012-N00819.label: 养血润燥
V012-N00819.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00819.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00819.aliases: 补血润燥； Nourishing Blood Moisturizing Dryness； Tonify blood and moisten dryness
V012-N00819.englishNames: Nourishing Blood and Moisturizing Dryness; nourishing blood and moistening dryness; nourishing blood to moisten dryness; Nourish blood and moisten dryness
V012-N00819.pinyinNames: yang xue run zao; yǎng xuè rùn zào
V012-N00819.definitions: A treatment method to tonify blood, increase fluids and moisten dryness. It is indicated for blood dryness due to yin deficiency or blood deficiency with wind dryness.

V012-N00820.label: 分消上下
V012-N00820.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00820.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00820.englishNames: separate elimination from the upper and lower; separate elimination from upper and lower; Induce vomiting and promoting bladder/bowel movements; respectively expelling evils from upper and lower
V012-N00820.pinyinNames: fen xiao shang xia; fēn xiāo shàng xià
V012-N00820.definitions: A treatment method to use medicines to induce vomiting, eliminate phlegm and promote bladder or bowel movements to separately eliminate pathogenic factors.

V012-N00821.label: 分消走泄
V012-N00821.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00821.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00821.aliases: Separate Elimination from Upper and Lower
V012-N00821.englishNames: Elimination of Pathogens Through Purgation and Diuresis; separate elimination through urination and defecation; Induce urination and promote bowel movements; elimination of pathogens through purgation and diuresis
V012-N00821.pinyinNames: fen xiao zou xie; fēn xiāo zǒu xiè
V012-N00821.definitions: A treatment method to induce urination and promote bowel movements to eliminate pathogenic factors.

V012-N00822.label: 利咽
V012-N00822.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00822.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00822.englishNames: benefiting the throat; Relieving Sore Throat; soothe the throat; relieving sore throat; Clear the throat; disinhibiting the pharynx
V012-N00822.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and benefit the throat.

V012-N00823.label: 利水消肿
V012-N00823.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00823.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00823.englishNames: inducing diuresis for removing edema; disinhibiting water and dispersing edema; inducing urination and eliminating swelling; Inducing Diuresis for Removing Edema; induce diuresis to alleviate edema; Induce urination and resolve oedema
V012-N00823.pinyinNames: li shui xiao zhong; lì shuǐ xiāo zhǒng
V012-N00823.definitions: A treatment method or formula to induce urination to resolve oedema. It is indicated for oedema.

V012-N00824.label: 利湿化浊
V012-N00824.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00824.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00824.englishNames: draining dampness and resolving turbidity; Drain dampness and transform turbidity
V012-N00824.pinyinNames: li shi hua zhuo; lì shī huà zhuó
V012-N00824.definitions: A treatment method to induce urination to transform turbid dampness. It is indicated for conditions due to dampness.

V012-N00825.label: 利湿开窍
V012-N00825.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00825.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00825.aliases: 化湿开窍； 化湿通窍； 利湿通窍
V012-N00825.englishNames: disinhibiting  dampness and opening orifices; disinhibiting dampness and opening orifices; Drain dampness and open the orifices; Removing Dampness for Resuscitation
V012-N00825.pinyinNames: li shi kai qiao; lì shī kāi qiào
V012-N00825.definitions: A treatment method to transform dampness and open the orifices. It is indicated for turbid phlegm misting the heart mind.

V012-N00826.label: 化湿和中
V012-N00826.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00826.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00826.aliases: 化湿健脾； 化湿醒脾@@@化湿运脾； 化湿醒脾； 化湿运脾； Transform dampness and awaken the spleen； Transform dampness and strengthen the spleen
V012-N00826.englishNames: removing dampness for regulating stomach; resolving dampness to harmonize the middle; resolving dampness to harmonize the middle jiao; Transform dampness and harmonize the middle jiao
V012-N00826.pinyinNames: hua shi he zhong; huà shī hé zhōng
V012-N00826.definitions: A treatment method to transform turbid dampness to strengthen the spleen and harmonize the stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to dampness affecting the spleen and stomach or spleen deficiency coupled with dampness.

V012-N00827.label: 化湿和营
V012-N00827.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00827.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00827.aliases: 燥湿和营； Dry dampness and harmonize the Ying nutrients
V012-N00827.englishNames: resolving dampness and harmonizing the ying-nutrient; resolving dampness and harmonizing the nutrient; Transform dampness and harmonize the Ying nutrients
V012-N00827.pinyinNames: hua shi he ying; huà shī hé yíng
V012-N00827.definitions: A treatment method to transform dampness with aromatic medicines and harmonize between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence. It is indicated for conditions due to dampness affecting the surface of the body.

V012-N00828.label: 化痰开窍
V012-N00828.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00828.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00828.aliases: 祛痰开窍； 涤痰开窍； 豁痰开窍； 豁痰开窍@@@祛痰开窍
V012-N00828.englishNames: resolving phlegm to open orifices; resolve phlegm to open the orifices; dissinating phlegm for resuscitation; resolving phlegm and opening orifices; Transform phlegm and open the orifices
V012-N00828.pinyinNames: hua tan kai qiao; huà tán kāi qiào
V012-N00828.definitions: A treatment method to transform turbid phlegm and open the orifices. It is indicated for phlegm-induced shock or phlegm misting the heart mind.

V012-N00829.label: 化痰散结
V012-N00829.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00829.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00829.aliases: 涤痰破结； 涤痰散结； 化痰破结； 化痰软坚； 豁痰软坚； 祛痰软坚； Transform phlegm and break nodules； Transform phlegm and soften hardness
V012-N00829.englishNames: Dissipating Phlegm and Resolving Masses; resolving phlegm and dissipating masses; dissipating phlegm and resolving masses; Transform phlegm and dissipate nodules; resolving phlegm and dissipating bind
V012-N00829.pinyinNames: hua tan san jie; huà tán sàn jiē
V012-N00829.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to transform phlegm, eliminate masses and soften hardness. It is indicated for phlegm nodules.

V012-N00830.label: 化痰消瘿
V012-N00830.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00830.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00830.englishNames: resolving phlegm and dispersing goiter; Transform phlegm and resolve goitre; dissinating phlegm for eliminating goiter; Dissipating Phlegm for Eliminating Goiter
V012-N00830.pinyinNames: hua tan xiao ying; huà tán xiāo yǐng
V012-N00830.definitions: A treatment method to transform phlegm, resolve goitre and disperse nodules. It is indicated for goitre due to phlegm nodules.

V012-N00831.label: 化痰消食
V012-N00831.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00831.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00831.englishNames: Clearing Heat Phlegm and Resolving Food; Transform phlegm and promote digestion; resolving phlegm and promoting digestion
V012-N00831.pinyinNames: hua tan xiao shi; huà tán xiāo shí
V012-N00831.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to transform phlegm and promote digestion. It is indicated for conditions due to phlegm-food stagnation.

V012-N00832.label: 化瘀养肝
V012-N00832.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00832.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00832.englishNames: resolving stasis and nourishing the liver; Transform stasis and nourish the liver
V012-N00832.pinyinNames: hua yu yang gan; huà yū yǎng gān
V012-N00832.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, tonify blood and nourish the liver. It is indicated for conditions due to liver deficiency with blood stasis.

V012-N00833.label: 化瘀养胃
V012-N00833.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00833.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00833.englishNames: Transform stasis and nourish the stomach; resolving stasis and nourishing the stomach
V012-N00833.pinyinNames: hua yu yang wei; huà yū yǎng wèi
V012-N00833.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, nourish the stomach and harmonize the spleen and stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to stomach deficiency with blood stasis.

V012-N00834.label: 化瘀利水
V012-N00834.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00834.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00834.englishNames: resolving stasis and inducing urination; Transform stasis and drain water retention; resolving stasis and disinhibiting water; Removing Blood Stasis for Diuresis
V012-N00834.pinyinNames: huà yū lì shuǐ; hua yu li shui
V012-N00834.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, resolve water retention and drain dampness. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis and water retention.

V012-N00835.label: 化瘀和胃
V012-N00835.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00835.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00835.englishNames: Removing Blood Stasis for Regulating Stomach; Transform stasis and harmonize the stomach; resolving stasis to harmonize the stomach
V012-N00835.pinyinNames: hua yu he wei; huà yū hé wèi
V012-N00835.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, harmonize the stomach, and soothe the spleen and stomach. It is indicated for stasis obstructing the stomach collaterals.

V012-N00836.label: 化瘀宣肺
V012-N00836.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00836.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00836.englishNames: Removing Blood Stasis for Facilitating Lung; Transform stasis and disperse the lung; resolving stasis to diffuse the lung
V012-N00836.pinyinNames: hua yu xuan fei; huà yū xuān fèi
V012-N00836.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and disperse lung qi. It is indicated for conditions due to stasis obstructing the lung collaterals.

V012-N00837.label: 化瘀宽心
V012-N00837.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00837.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00837.englishNames: resolving stasis to soothe the heart; Transform blood stasis and soothe the heart
V012-N00837.pinyinNames: hua yu kuan xin; huà yū kuān xīn
V012-N00837.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and unblock heart vessels. It is indicated for conditions due to heart blood stasis.

V012-N00838.label: 化瘀宽胸
V012-N00838.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00838.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00838.englishNames: resolving stasis to soothe the chest; Transform stasis and soothe the chest
V012-N00838.pinyinNames: hua yu kuan xiong; huà yū kuān xiōng
V012-N00838.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, and soothe the chest and diaphragm. It is indicated for stasis obstructing the chest, diaphragm or subcostal region.

V012-N00839.label: 化瘀止血
V012-N00839.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00839.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00839.englishNames: Transform stasis and stop bleeding; resolving stasis to stop bleeding; Removing Blood Stasis for Hemostasis
V012-N00839.pinyinNames: huà yū zhǐ xiě; hua yu zhi xue
V012-N00839.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and stop bleeding. It is indicated for bleeding due to blood stasis.

V012-N00840.label: 化瘀消肿
V012-N00840.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00840.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00840.aliases: 化祛瘀消肿； 散瘀消肿； 破瘀消肿； 祛瘀消肿； Dissipate stasis and eliminate swelling； Break stasis and resolve swelling
V012-N00840.englishNames: Removing Blood Stasis for Detumescence; Transform stasis and resolve swelling
V012-N00840.pinyinNames: hua yu xiao zhong; huà yū xiāo zhǒng
V012-N00840.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and resolve swelling. It is indicated for haematoma.

V012-N00841.label: 化瘀清热
V012-N00841.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00841.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00841.englishNames: resolving stasis and clearing heat; Transform stasis and clear heat
V012-N00841.pinyinNames: hua yu qing re; huà yū qīng rè
V012-N00841.definitions: A treatment method to transform stasis and clear heat. It is indicated for blood stasis transforming into heat or heat-induced blood stagnation.

V012-N00842.label: 化瘀理脾
V012-N00842.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00842.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00842.englishNames: Transform stasis and regulate the spleen; resolving stasis to regulate the spleen
V012-N00842.pinyinNames: hua yu li pi; huà yū lǐ pí
V012-N00842.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, strengthen the spleen and harmonize the spleen and stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis affecting the spleen meridian.

V012-N00843.label: 化瘀疏肝
V012-N00843.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00843.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00843.englishNames: resolving stasis to soothe the liver; Transform stasis and soothe the liver
V012-N00843.pinyinNames: hua yu shu gan; huà yū shū gān
V012-N00843.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, soothe the liver and harmonize collaterals. It is indicated for conditions due to liver blood stasis.

V012-N00844.label: 化瘀通脑
V012-N00844.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00844.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00844.englishNames: resolving stasis to unblock the brain; Removing Blood Stasis for Dredging Brain; Transform stasis and unblock brain collaterals.
V012-N00844.pinyinNames: hua yu tong nao; huà yū tōng nǎo
V012-N00844.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and unblock brain collaterals. It is indicated for conditions due to stasis obstructing the brain collaterals.

V012-N00845.label: 升提固涩
V012-N00845.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00845.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00845.aliases: 升清固涩； Ascending and Astringing Method
V012-N00845.englishNames: Ascending and Inducing Astringency; Lift and secure qi; lifting yang qi to secure and astringe; upbearing-lifting and constraining-astringing
V012-N00845.pinyinNames: sheng ti gu se; shēng tí gù sè
V012-N00845.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to supplement, lift and astringe qi. It is indicated for qi failing to secure blood, fluid or essence, etc.

V012-N00846.label: 和中安神
V012-N00846.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00846.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00846.aliases: 和胃安神； Harmonize the stomach and calm the mind
V012-N00846.englishNames: harmonizing the middle to tranquilize spirit; Harmonize the middle jiao and calm the mind; harmonizing the middle and tranquilizing; Regulating Stomach for Tranquilization; harmonizing the middle jiao to tranquilize mind
V012-N00846.pinyinNames: he zhong an shen; hé zhōng ān shén
V012-N00846.definitions: A treatment method to promote digestion, harmonize the stomach and calm the mind. It is indicated for insomnia due to stomach discomfort.

V012-N00847.label: 和络
V012-N00847.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00847.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00847.aliases: 活络； 通络； Unblock meridians； 通经； Dredging Channels and Collaterals； Dredging Collaterals
V012-N00847.englishNames: Harmonize meridians; Activating Collaterals; methods to harmonize meridians; harmonizing collaterals
V012-N00847.definitions: A treatment method to harmonize meridians using acupuncture, tuina, external therapies or herbal formulas. It is indicated for conditions caused by meridian obstruction.

V012-N00848.label: 和营生新
V012-N00848.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00848.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00848.englishNames: Harmonize the Ying nutrients and regenerate new tissues; harmonizing the ying-nutrient to promote regeneration; harmonizing the nutrient to promote regeneration
V012-N00848.pinyinNames: hé yíng shēng xīn; he ying sheng xin
V012-N00848.definitions: A treatment method to harmonize between the Ying nutrients and blood, circulate blood, regenerate new blood and flesh, and speed up wound healing. It is indicated for delayed wound healing due to blood stagnation.

V012-N00849.label: 和血安胎
V012-N00849.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00849.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00849.englishNames: Harmonize blood and quiet fetus; harmonizing blood to calm the fetus; Regulating Blood for Preventing Miscarriage; harmonizing blood to calm fetus
V012-N00849.pinyinNames: he xue an tai; hé xuè ān tāi
V012-N00849.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, regulate qi and quiet the fetus. It is indicated for threatened abortion due to disharmony between qi and blood.

V012-N00850.label: 和解法
V012-N00850.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00850.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00850.englishNames: harmonizing and releasing therapy; methods to harmonize and release; reconciliation method; Methods to harmonize and release; Reconciliation Method; harmonizing method
V012-N00850.pinyinNames: he jie fa; hé jiě fǎ
V012-N00850.definitions: Treatment methods to harmonize and release pathogenic factors affecting Shaoyang or coordinate functions of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N00851.label: 和解表里
V012-N00851.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00851.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00851.aliases: Harmonize and Release the Exterior and Interior； Reconciling Superficies and Interior
V012-N00851.englishNames: harmonizing and releasing the exterior and interior; Mediating Exterior Interior; reconciling superficies and interior; harmonize and release the exterior and interior; Harmonize and release the exterior and interior; harmonizing and releasing the external and internal
V012-N00851.pinyinNames: he jie biao li; hé jiě biǎo lǐ
V012-N00851.definitions: A treatment method to use medicines that are neutral in property to harmonize between the exterior and interior. It is indicated for mild conditions involving both the exterior and interior.

V012-N00852.label: 回阳
V012-N00852.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00852.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00852.aliases: 回阳救急； 救阳； 回阳救逆； 回阳急救； Resuscitate yang for emergency
V012-N00852.englishNames: Restoring Yang; restoring yang[and rescuing patient from collapse]; restoring yang; Resuscitate yang
V012-N00852.definitions: A treatment method to greatly tonify yang qi. It is indicated for conditions due to yang depletion.

V012-N00853.label: 固冲止带
V012-N00853.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00853.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00853.aliases: 固肾涩带； 固肾止带； 固涩止带； Secure the kidney and stop leucorrhoea
V012-N00853.englishNames: strengthening the chong meridian to stop leukorrhea; strengthening thoroughfare vessel to stop leucorrhea; consolidating Chong Vessel for stopping leukorrhagia; secure the thoroughfare vessel and stanch vaginal discharge; Secure the Chong meridian and stop leucorrhoea; Reinforcing Chong Ren for Quenching Leukorrhagia
V012-N00853.pinyinNames: gu chong zhi dai; gù chōng zhǐ dài
V012-N00853.definitions: A treatment method to tonify the kidney and secure Chong and Ren meridians to stop vaginal discharges. It is indicated for vaginal discharges due to kidney deficiency and insecurity of the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N00854.label: 固护阴液
V012-N00854.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00854.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00854.aliases: 敛阴固脱； Astringe yin and prevent collapse
V012-N00854.englishNames: Secure and astringe yin fluids; constraining and protecting yin fluid
V012-N00854.pinyinNames: gu hu yin ye; gù hù yīn yè
V012-N00854.definitions: A treatment method to astringe and nourish yin fluids. It is indicated for exhaustion of yin fluids.

V012-N00855.label: 固摄敛泪
V012-N00855.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00855.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00855.englishNames: Tonify qi and astringe tears; astringing tp stop lacrimation; astringing epiphora by constraining
V012-N00855.pinyinNames: gù shè liǎn lèi; gu she lian lei
V012-N00855.definitions: A treatment method to tonify qi and stop lacrimation. It is indicated for spontaneous flow of tears due to qi deficiency.

V012-N00856.label: 固摄止血
V012-N00856.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00856.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00856.englishNames: stopping bleeding by securing and constraining; astringing for hemostasis; Astringing for Hemostasis; Secure blood and stop bleeding
V012-N00856.pinyinNames: gu she zhi xue; gù shè zhǐ xiě
V012-N00856.definitions: A treatment method to astringe and secure blood to stop bleeding. It is indicated for bleeding due to qi failing to keep blood within the vessels.

V012-N00857.label: 固涩敛乳
V012-N00857.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00857.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00857.aliases: 固涩摄乳； 补气敛乳； 补气摄乳
V012-N00857.englishNames: securing and astringing lactation; Tonify qi and astringe the breast milk; strengthening and astringing to constrain milk; astringing for arresting lactation
V012-N00857.pinyinNames: gu se lian ru; gù sè liǎn rǔ
V012-N00857.definitions: A treatment method to tonify qi and astringe breast milk. It is indicated for spontaneous flow of breast milk due to qi deficiency.

V012-N00858.label: 固涩法
V012-N00858.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00858.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00858.englishNames: astringing method; Astringing Method; method of securing and astringing; Securing and astringent methods; securing and astringing method; securing and astringing therapy
V012-N00858.pinyinNames: gu se fa; gù sè fǎ
V012-N00858.definitions: Treatment methods to secure qi, blood, fluids and essence from consumption

V012-N00859.label: 固经止血
V012-N00859.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00859.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00859.englishNames: Consolidating Channel for Hemostasis; consolidating channel for hemostasis; securing meridians to stop bleeding; Secure menses and stop bleeding
V012-N00859.pinyinNames: gu jing zhi xue; gù jīng zhǐ xiě
V012-N00859.definitions: A treatment method to astringe menses to stop bleeding. It is indicated for heavy menstruation.

V012-N00860.label: 固肾安胎
V012-N00860.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00860.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00860.englishNames: Reinforcing Kidney for Preventing Miscarriage; strengthening the kidney to soothe fetus; Reinforce the kidney and secure the fetus; strengthening the kidney to calm the fetus
V012-N00860.pinyinNames: gu shen an tai; gù shèn ān tāi
V012-N00860.definitions: A treatment method to tonify kidney qi to secure the fetus. It is indicated for threatened abortion due to kidney deficiency.

V012-N00861.label: 固脬止遗
V012-N00861.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00861.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00861.aliases: 补气固脬； 固脬止尿； 固脬止淋； Tonify qi and secure the bladder
V012-N00861.englishNames: strengthening the bladder to stop enuresis; Secure the bladder and stop enuresis; strengthening bladder to stop retention failure
V012-N00861.pinyinNames: gu pao zhi yi; gù pāo zhǐ yí
V012-N00861.definitions: A treatment method to tonify kidney qi to secure the urinary bladder. It is indicated for enuresis or urinary incontinence due to kidney qi insecurity.

V012-N00862.label: 固表止汗
V012-N00862.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00862.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00862.aliases: Hidroschesis Strength Exter； 敛汗固表； 固表敛汗； Astringe sweating and secure the exterior
V012-N00862.englishNames: Consolidating Exterior for Arresting Sweating; Secure the exterior and stop sweating; securing the exteriorto stop sweating; consolidating exterior and stopping sweating; consolidating superficies for arresting sweating; securing the external to stop sweating; secure the exterior to check sweating
V012-N00862.pinyinNames: gu biao zhi han; gù biǎo zhǐ hàn
V012-N00862.definitions: A treatment method to secure the exterior to stop sweating. It is indicated for spontaneous sweating due to insecure Wei-defensive qi.

V012-N00863.label: 固齿
V012-N00863.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00863.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00863.englishNames: strengthening teeth; Strengthening Teeth and Gingivae; strengthening the teeth; Secure the teeth
V012-N00863.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi, secure the teeth and moisten the gums.

V012-N00864.label: 培土制水
V012-N00864.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00864.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00864.englishNames: fortifying earth to control water; banking up earth to control water; Cultivate earth to restrain water
V012-N00864.pinyinNames: pei tu zhi shui; péi tǔ zhì shuǐ
V012-N00864.definitions: A treatment method to tonify the spleen (earth) to restrain overflow of water dampness. It is indicated for oedema or diarrhoea due to spleen deficiency.

V012-N00865.label: 增液通便
V012-N00865.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00865.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00865.aliases: 增水行舟； 增液润肠； 增液通下； 滋阴通下； Increase water to float the boat； 增液润肠@@@增液通下@@@滋阴通下
V012-N00865.englishNames: increasing fluids to promote bowel movements; Increase fluids and promote bowel movements; replenishing humor to promote defecation
V012-N00865.pinyinNames: zeng ye tong bian; zēng yè tōng biàn
V012-N00865.definitions: A treatment method to nourish fluids, moisten the intestines and promote bowel movements. It is indicated for constipation due to large intestine dryness.

V012-N00866.label: 壮水制阳
V012-N00866.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00866.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00866.englishNames: invigorating water to control yang; strengthening governor of water for restraining hyperactivity of yang; Nourish yin fluids to submerge yang
V012-N00866.pinyinNames: zhuang shui zhi yang; zhuàng shuǐ zhì yáng
V012-N00866.definitions: A treatment method to nourish yin fluids to contain hyperactivity of yang qi. It is indicated for hyperactivity of yang due to yin deficiency.

V012-N00867.label: 宁心开窍
V012-N00867.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00867.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00867.aliases: 宁神开窍； Calm the mind and open the orifices
V012-N00867.englishNames: calming heart for resuscitation; tranquilizing the heart to open orifices; tranquilizing the heart and opening orifices; Calm the heart and open the orifices
V012-N00867.pinyinNames: ning xin kai qiao; níng xīn kāi qiào
V012-N00867.definitions: A treatment method to nourish the heart and settle the mind. It is indicated for cardiac shock, shock from heavy blood loss or unconsciousness due to fear/fright damaging the heart mind.

V012-N00868.label: 安神定志
V012-N00868.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00868.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00868.aliases: 安神定悸； 宁心定志； 宁心定悸； Calm the mind and alleviate palpitations
V012-N00868.englishNames: tranquilizing spirit to calm mind; Calm and settle the mind; tranquilizing mind to calm mind; Tanquilizing Spirit to Stablize Mind
V012-N00868.pinyinNames: an shen ding zhi; ān shén dìng zhì
V012-N00868.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors and nourish the heart. It is indicated for severe palpitations, insomnia, poor memory and panic.

V012-N00869.label: 安神止痒
V012-N00869.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00869.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00869.englishNames: tranquilizing spirit to stop itching; Tanquilizing Spirit to Relieving Itching; Calm the mind and relieve itch; tranquilizing mind to stop itching
V012-N00869.pinyinNames: an shen zhi yang; ān shén zhǐ yǎng
V012-N00869.definitions: A treatment method to nourish blood and calm the mind. It is indicated for itching or restlessness due to yin blood failing to nourish the skin.

V012-N00870.label: 安神法
V012-N00870.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00870.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00870.aliases: 安神定志； 安神； Tranquilization
V012-N00870.englishNames: spirit-tranquilizing therapy; tranquillization method; Mind-calming methods; method of tranquilizing mind; Tranquillization Method
V012-N00870.pinyinNames: an shen fa; ān shén fǎ
V012-N00870.definitions: Treatment methods to calm/settle the mind and alleviate palpitations

V012-N00871.label: 宣散湿邪
V012-N00871.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00871.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00871.englishNames: Disperse dampness; diffusing and dissipating pathogenic dampness; Diffusing Dampness Pathogen
V012-N00871.pinyinNames: xuan san shi xie; xuān sàn shī xié
V012-N00871.definitions: A treatment method to disperse turbid dampness with aromatic, pungent medicines. It is indicated for conditions due to dampness binding the exterior.

V012-N00872.label: 宣肺化痰
V012-N00872.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00872.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00872.aliases: 豁痰宣肺； 宣肺祛痰； Transform phlegm and disperse the lung
V012-N00872.englishNames: diffuse the lung to resolve phlegm; Disperse the lung and transform phlegm; diffusing the lung to resolve phlegm; Ventilating Lung Qi for Dissipating Phlegm; ventilating lung qi for dissipating phlegm
V012-N00872.pinyinNames: xuan fei hua tan; xuān fèi huà tán
V012-N00872.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to disperse lung qi and transform phlegm. It is indicated for conditions due to turbid phlegm obstructing the lung.

V012-N00873.label: 宣肺通气
V012-N00873.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00873.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00873.englishNames: ventilating lung qi; Disperse lung qi; diffusing the lung and unblocking qi
V012-N00873.pinyinNames: xuan fei tong qi; xuān fèi tōng qì
V012-N00873.definitions: A treatment method to disperse lung qi. It is indicated for conditions due to pathogenic factors affecting the dispersing of lung qi.

V012-N00874.label: 宣通三焦
V012-N00874.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00874.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00874.aliases: 三焦治法； Disperse and unblock triple energizer
V012-N00874.englishNames: Promoting Sanjiao by Dispersion; Disperse and unblock the three jiao; diffusing and unblocking triple energizer; diffusing and unblocking the threee jiao
V012-N00874.pinyinNames: xuan tong san jiao; xuān tōng sān jiāo
V012-N00874.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi, eliminate pathogenic factors and unblock the three jiao. It is indicated for pathogenic factors impairing the qi flow of the three jiao.

V012-N00875.label: 宽中利湿
V012-N00875.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00875.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00875.englishNames: Soothe the middle jiao and drain dampness; soothing the middle jiao and draining dampness; soothing the middle and disinhibiting dampness
V012-N00875.pinyinNames: kuān zhōng lì shī; kuan zhong li shi
V012-N00875.definitions: A treatment method to transform turbid dampness to strengthen the spleen and harmonize the stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to spleen deficiency coupled with dampness.

V012-N00876.label: 宽胸利膈
V012-N00876.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00876.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00876.englishNames: Relieving Stuffiness of the Chest Diaphragm; soothing the chest and disinhibiting the diaphragm; Open the chest and sooth the diaphragm; soothing the chest and benefiting the diaphragm
V012-N00876.pinyinNames: kuan xiong li ge; kuān xiōng lì gé
V012-N00876.definitions: A treatment method to eliminate pathogenic factors, regulate qi, lift yang, and down-regulate turbidity to soothe the chest and diaphragm. It is indicated for chest stuffiness, distension, fullness and tightness.

V012-N00877.label: 平肝潜阳
V012-N00877.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00877.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00877.englishNames: pacify the liver to subdue yang; Soothe the liver and submerge yang; suppressing hyperactive liver and subsiding yang; soothing the liver and subduing yang; Suppressing Hyperactive Liver and Subsiding Yang
V012-N00877.pinyinNames: píng gān qián yáng; ping gan qian yang
V012-N00877.definitions: A treatment method to use heavy medicinal minerals to soothe the liver and subdue yang. It is indicated for conditions due to hyperactivity of liver yang.

V012-N00878.label: 平肝熄风
V012-N00878.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00878.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00878.aliases: 镇肝熄风； Calm the liver and stop wind； 平熄内风； Calming Endogenous Wind
V012-N00878.englishNames: soothing the liver and extinguishing wind; Soothe the liver and extinguish wind; Calming Liver Wind
V012-N00878.pinyinNames: ping gan xi feng; píng gān xī fēng
V012-N00878.definitions: A treatment method to use formulas that contain heavy medicinal minerals to submerge liver yang and stop wind. It is indicated for conditions due to liver yang transforming into wind or sudden hyperactivity of liver yang.

V012-N00879.label: 平调寒热
V012-N00879.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00879.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00879.aliases: 调和寒热； Mediate cold and heat； Mild Regulating Cold Heat
V012-N00879.englishNames: mildly regulating cold and heat; moderate regulation of cold and heat; mild regulation of cold and heat; Balance and regulate cold and heat; Mildly Regulating Cold and Heat
V012-N00879.pinyinNames: ping diao han re; píng tiáo hán rè; ping tiao han re
V012-N00879.definitions: A treatment method to use neutral-property medicines to clear heat and remove cold. It is indicated for conditions due to disharmony between (yin) cold and (yang) heat.

V012-N00880.label: 开关涌吐
V012-N00880.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00880.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00880.englishNames: resuscitation through vomiting; Induce vomiting to refresh the mind; Causing Vomiting for Resuscitation
V012-N00880.pinyinNames: kai guan yong tu; kāi guān yǒng tù
V012-N00880.definitions: A treatment method to induce vomiting to unblock and resuscitate. It is indicated for exuberant, turbid phlegm disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N00881.label: 开窍法
V012-N00881.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00881.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00881.englishNames: inducing resuscitation method; orifice-opening therapy; Orifice-opening methods; Inducing Resuscitation Method; methods to open orifices
V012-N00881.pinyinNames: kai qiao fa; kāi qiào fǎ
V012-N00881.definitions: Treatment methods to open the orifice and refresh the mind

V012-N00882.label: 开窍通闭
V012-N00882.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00882.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00882.aliases: 通窍开闭； 通关开窍
V012-N00882.englishNames: opening orifices and unblocking block; opening and unblocking orifices; Open the orifices and unblock impediments
V012-N00882.pinyinNames: kai qiao tong bi; kāi qiào tōng bì
V012-N00882.definitions: A treatment method to open the orifices, refresh the mind and unblock impediments. It is indicated for conditions due to pathogenic factors misting the heart mind.

V012-N00883.label: 开音
V012-N00883.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00883.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00883.aliases: 清热开音； 祛痰开音； 祛瘀开音； 润燥开音； 散寒开音； 散结开音； 消肿开音； 行气开音； 宣肺开音； 滋音开音； Facilitating Lung Removing Hoarseness
V012-N00883.englishNames: Sound Producing; restoring voice; Restore the voice; sound producing
V012-N00883.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and restore the voice. It is indicated for hoarseness.

V012-N00884.label: 引火归原
V012-N00884.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00884.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00884.aliases: 导龙入海； Pacifying Kidney Asthenic Fire； 引火归元； Guide dragon to the sea； 引火下行
V012-N00884.englishNames: Conducting Fire Back to Itsorigin; directing fire back to its origin; conducting fire back to itsorigin; Guide fire to its origin; conduct fire back to its origin
V012-N00884.pinyinNames: yin huo gui yuan; yǐn huǒ guī yuán
V012-N00884.definitions: A treatment method to reinforce kidney yang and combine medicines that enter meridians to astringe floating yang qi, i.e. to guide kidney yang into kidney yin. It is indicated for decline of vital fire or floating of deficiency yang.

V012-N00885.label: 强筋壮骨
V012-N00885.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00885.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00885.englishNames: strengthening tendons and bones; strengthening sinews and invigorating bones; Strengthen the sinews and bones; Strengthening Tendons and Bones
V012-N00885.pinyinNames: qiang jin zhuang gu; qiáng jīn zhuàng gǔ
V012-N00885.definitions: A treatment method to strengthen the sinews or bones through exercise or taking medicine. It is indicated for weak sinews/bones due to heredity, trauma or chronic diseases.

V012-N00886.label: 急下存阴
V012-N00886.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00886.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00886.englishNames: Urgent purgation to preserve yin; emergent purgation to preserve yin-fluid
V012-N00886.pinyinNames: jí xià cún yīn; ji xia cun yin
V012-N00886.definitions: A treatment method to conduct urgent purgation to preserve yin fluids. It is indicated for febrile disease with dry heat consuming yin fluids.

V012-N00887.label: 扶正解表
V012-N00887.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00887.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00887.englishNames: Strengthening Body Resistance Relieving Exterior; reinforce the healthy qi and release the exterior; strengthening body resistance for relieving superficies syndrome; 。reinforcing healthy qi and releasing the external; reinforcing healthy qi and releasing the exterior; Reinforce healthy qi and release the exterior
V012-N00887.pinyinNames: fu zheng jie biao; fú zhèng jiě biǎo
V012-N00887.definitions: A treatment method to release the exterior and reinforce the healthy qi. It is indicated for deficiency of qi, blood, yin and yang coupled with external pathogenic factors affecting the exterior.

V012-N00888.label: 提脓拔毒
V012-N00888.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00888.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00888.englishNames: Draw out pus and toxin; Eliminating Pus and Toxicity; eliminating pus and toxicity; draining pus to remove toxin
V012-N00888.pinyinNames: tí nóng bá dú; ti nong ba du; ti nong ba dong
V012-N00888.definitions: A treatment method to drain pus through therapies or formulas to draw out pus and toxin.

V012-N00889.label: 提脓祛腐
V012-N00889.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00889.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00889.aliases: 祛腐排脓； 祛腐提脓； 提脓去腐； 祛腐排脓@@@祛腐提脓
V012-N00889.englishNames: Draw pus and eliminate putridity; draining pus and dispelling putridity
V012-N00889.pinyinNames: tí nóng qū fǔ; ti nong qu fu
V012-N00889.definitions: A treatment method to drain pus and eliminate dead tissues through therapies or formulas to draw pus and remove putridity.

V012-N00890.label: 搜风祛痰开窍
V012-N00890.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00890.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00890.englishNames: Remove wind, eliminate phlegm and open the orifices; eliminating wind and dispelling phlegm to open orifices
V012-N00890.pinyinNames: sōu fēng qū tán kāi qiào; sou feng qu tan kai qiao
V012-N00890.definitions: A treatment method to remove wind, eliminate phlegm and open the orifices. It is indicated for wind phlegm disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N00891.label: 攻下法
V012-N00891.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00891.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00891.aliases: 攻里法； 通下法@@@下法@@@泻下法； Interior purgation methods； 下法； 泻下法； 通下法； 攻下
V012-N00891.englishNames: purgation therapy; Purgative methods; purgation method; Purgation Method
V012-N00891.pinyinNames: gong xia fa; gōng xià fǎ
V012-N00891.definitions: Treatment methods to clear interior heat, retained water or stagnant blood.

V012-N00892.label: 敛疮止痛
V012-N00892.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00892.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00892.englishNames: Astringe sores/ulcers and stop pain; Healing Sore and Relieving Pain; astringing sores to relieve pain; healing sore and relieving pain
V012-N00892.pinyinNames: liǎn chuāng zhǐ tòng; lian chuang zhi tong
V012-N00892.definitions: A treatment method to alleviate pain and speed up ulcerative wound healing.

V012-N00893.label: 敛肺平喘
V012-N00893.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00893.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00893.englishNames: constraining the lung and relieving panting; Astringe the lung and alleviate panting/tachypnoea; Astringing Lung for Relieving Asthma
V012-N00893.pinyinNames: liǎn fèi píng chuǎn; lian fei ping chuan
V012-N00893.definitions: A treatment method to tonify and astringe lung qi to alleviate panting. It is indicated for fast breathing due to lung qi deficiency.

V012-N00894.label: 敛肺止咳
V012-N00894.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00894.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00894.englishNames: Astringing Lung for Relieving Cough; constrain the lung to suppress cough; astringing lung and relieving cough; astringing lung for relieving cough; constraining the lung to stop cough; Astringe the lung and relieve cough
V012-N00894.pinyinNames: lian fei zhi ke; liǎn fèi zhǐ ké
V012-N00894.definitions: A treatment method to tonify and astringe lung qi to stop coughing. It is indicated for cough due to lung qi deficiency.

V012-N00895.label: 散寒化饮
V012-N00895.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00895.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00895.englishNames: Dissipate cold and transform fluid retention; dispelling cold and resolving fluid retention; dissipating cold and resolving retained fluid; dissipating cold to resolve retained fluid
V012-N00895.pinyinNames: sàn hán huà yǐn; san han hua yin
V012-N00895.definitions: A treatment method to dissipate cold and transform water retention using pungent warm medicines. It is indicated for conditions due to internal retention of cold fluid.

V012-N00896.label: 散寒开窍
V012-N00896.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00896.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00896.aliases: 辛温开窍； 辛温通窍； 散寒通窍； 辛温开窍@@@辛温通窍； 散寒开闭； 散寒通闭； Open the orifices with pungent warm
V012-N00896.englishNames: dissipating cold and opening orifices; Dissipate cold and open the orifices
V012-N00896.pinyinNames: san han kai qiao; sàn hán kāi qiào
V012-N00896.definitions: A treatment method to warm yang, dissipate cold and open the orifices with pungent, warm medicines. It is indicated for cold-induced shock or unconsciousness due to cold retention and qi stagnation.

V012-N00897.label: 散寒止痛
V012-N00897.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00897.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00897.englishNames: Dispelling Cold for Relieving Pain; dissipating cold to relieve pain; Dissipate cold and alleviate pain
V012-N00897.pinyinNames: san han zhi tong; sàn hán zhǐ tòng
V012-N00897.definitions: A treatment method to stop pain by dissipating cold. It is indicated for pain due to cold retention.

V012-N00898.label: 散寒除湿
V012-N00898.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00898.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00898.aliases: 散寒祛湿； 散寒燥湿； Dissipate cold and dry dampness
V012-N00898.englishNames: dispelling cold and removing dampness; dissipating cold and eliminating dampness; Dissipate cold and eliminate dampness; Dispelling Cold and Removing Dampness
V012-N00898.pinyinNames: sàn hán chú shī; san han chu shi
V012-N00898.definitions: A treatment method to remove cold and eliminate dampness with pungent, warm medicines. It is indicated for conditions due to cold dampness affecting the body.

V012-N00899.label: 散结
V012-N00899.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00899.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00899.definitions: A treatment method to eliminate phlegm, regulate qi, break stasis and unblock collaterals to soften hardness, disperses nodules and resolve masses. It is indicated for masses, lumps or swelling.

V012-N00900.label: 明目
V012-N00900.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00900.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00900.englishNames: improving eyesight; improving vision; Improving Eyesight; improving the eyesight; improve vision; Brighten the eyes
V012-N00900.definitions: A treatment method to remove wind, clear heat, cool blood, transform stasis, nourish blood, supplement qi, nourish yin, strengthen the spleen and tonify the liver and kidney to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and benefit the eyes. It is indicated for blurred vision.

V012-N00901.label: 杀虫宁神
V012-N00901.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00901.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00901.englishNames: Kill parasitic worms and calm the mind; killing worms and tranquilizing mind; killing worms and tranquilizing spirit
V012-N00901.pinyinNames: shā chóng níng shén; sha chong ning shen
V012-N00901.definitions: A treatment method to kill worms, calm the mind, alleviate pain and stop seizures. It is indicated for pork tapeworm affecting the brain.

V012-N00902.label: 杀虫消疳
V012-N00902.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00902.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00902.englishNames: destroying parasites for curing malnutrition; Kill parasitic worms and alleviate malnutrition; killing worms and releasing malnutrition; Destroying Parasites for Curing Malnutrition
V012-N00902.pinyinNames: shā chóng xiāo gān; sha chong xiao gan
V012-N00902.definitions: A treatment method to kill parasitic worms and improve malnutrition. It is indicated for malnutrition due to retention of parasitic worms.

V012-N00903.label: 止血安胎
V012-N00903.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00903.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00903.englishNames: Hemostasis and Preventing Miscarriage; stopping bleeding and soothing fetus; stopping bleeding and calming fetus; Stop bleeding and secure the fetus
V012-N00903.pinyinNames: zhi xue an tai; zhǐ xiě ān tāi
V012-N00903.definitions: A treatment method to stop bleeding and secure the fetus.

V012-N00904.label: 治五官法
V012-N00904.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00904.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00904.englishNames: therapeutic methods for treating five sense organs; methods to treat five sense organs; Dispelling Five Sense Organs Method; Methods for eyes and ear, nose and throat problems
V012-N00904.pinyinNames: zhi wu guan fa; zhì wǔ guān fǎ
V012-N00904.definitions: Treatment methods for problems of the eyes, noses lips, tongue and ears

V012-N00905.label: 治痈疡法
V012-N00905.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00905.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00905.englishNames: therapies for treating abscess and ulcer; method of treating abscess and ulcer; Method for Treating Sores and Carbuncles; method for treating sores and carbuncles; Sore-/ulcer-resolving methods
V012-N00905.pinyinNames: zhi yong yang fa; zhì yōng yáng fǎ
V012-N00905.definitions: Treatment methods to resolve superficial or suppurative skin conditions

V012-N00906.label: 治风法
V012-N00906.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00906.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00906.englishNames: Removing wind methods; Dispelling Wind Method; dispelling wind method; wind-treating therapy; method of treating wind
V012-N00906.pinyinNames: zhi feng fa; zhì fēng fǎ
V012-N00906.definitions: Treatment methods to dispel external wind or calm down the internal wind

V012-N00907.label: 泻下逐水
V012-N00907.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00907.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00907.aliases: 攻下逐水； 攻逐水饮； 峻下逐水； 逐水； Eliminating Excess Fluids； Purgat Remov Excess Fluid
V012-N00907.englishNames: expel water by purgation; expelling water retention with drastic purgative; purging the lower to expel water; Promote bowel movements and expel water; purging the intestine to expel water; Purgating and Removing Excess Fluid
V012-N00907.pinyinNames: xie xia zhu shui; xiè xià zhú shuǐ
V012-N00907.definitions: A treatment method to use purgative medicines to resolve water retention. It is indicated for conditions due to internal retention of water fluid.

V012-N00908.label: 泻南补北
V012-N00908.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00908.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00908.englishNames: purging the south and nourishing the north; purging the south and tonifying the north; purging south and nourishing north; Reduce the south to reinforce the north
V012-N00908.pinyinNames: xie nan bu bei; xiè nán bǔ běi
V012-N00908.definitions: A treatment method to reduce heart fire and reinforce kidney water. The south here refers to heart fire, whereas the north refers to kidney water.

V012-N00909.label: 泻结行滞
V012-N00909.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00909.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00909.englishNames: purging bind and removing stagnation; purging and removing stagnation; Promote bowel movements and circulate qi
V012-N00909.pinyinNames: xie jie xing zhi; xiè jié xíng zhì
V012-N00909.definitions: A treatment method to combine purgative and qi-circulating medicines. It is indicated for conditions due to constipation due to heat and qi stagnation.

V012-N00910.label: 泻肝清肺
V012-N00910.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00910.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00910.englishNames: Clear heat in the liver and lung; Purging Liver and Clearing Lung; purging the liver and clearing the lung
V012-N00910.pinyinNames: xie gan qing fei; xiè gān qīng fèi
V012-N00910.definitions: A treatment method to clear fire heat in the liver and lung. It is indicated for liver fire attacking the lung or exuberant heat in the liver and lung.

V012-N00911.label: 泻肝清胃
V012-N00911.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00911.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00911.englishNames: purging the liver and clearing the stomach; Purging Liver and Clearing Stomach; Clear heat in the liver and stomach
V012-N00911.pinyinNames: xie gan qing wei; xiè gān qīng wèi
V012-N00911.definitions: A treatment method to clear fire heat in the liver and stomach. It is indicated for liver fire attacking the stomach or exuberant heat in the liver and stomach.

V012-N00912.label: 泻肺逐饮
V012-N00912.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00912.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00912.englishNames: Drain the lung and expel water retention; purging the lung to expel retained fluid; Purging Lung and Eliminating Excess Fluids; purging the lung and expelling retained fluid; eliminating pathogens from lung for expelling fluid retention
V012-N00912.pinyinNames: xiè fèi zhú yǐn; xie fei zhu yin
V012-N00912.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to transform fluid retention and drain the lung. It is indicated for conditions due to fluid retention affecting the lung.

V012-N00913.label: 活血养血
V012-N00913.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00913.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00913.aliases: 祛瘀养血； 化瘀养血； 和血养血； 化瘀养血@@@祛瘀养血； Harmonize and nourish blood； Resolve stasis and nourish blood
V012-N00913.englishNames: Activating and Nourishing Blood; activating blood and nourishing blood; Circulate and nourish blood; activating and nourishing blood
V012-N00913.pinyinNames: huo  xue  yang  xue; huo xue yang xue; huó xuè yǎng xiě
V012-N00913.definitions: A treatment method to circulate and nourish blood. It is indicated for conditions due to blood deficiency coupled with stasis.

V012-N00914.label: 活血化瘀
V012-N00914.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00914.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00914.aliases: 活血祛瘀； 活血散瘀； Circulate blood and eliminate stasis； Circulate blood and dissipate stasis
V012-N00914.englishNames: activate blood and resolve stasis; Circulate blood and transform stasis; activating blood and resolving stasis; promoting blood circulation for removing blood stasis
V012-N00914.pinyinNames: huo xue hua yu; huó xuè huà yū
V012-N00914.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood and transform stasis. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis.

V012-N00915.label: 活血止痛
V012-N00915.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00915.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00915.aliases: 通络止痛； 活血通络止痛； Circulate blood, unblock meridians and alleviate pain
V012-N00915.englishNames: Activating Blood for Relieving Pain; activate blood to relieve pain; activating blood to stop pain; Circulate blood and alleviate pain
V012-N00915.pinyinNames: huo xue zhi tong; huó xuè zhǐ tòng
V012-N00915.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, unblock meridians and alleviate pain. It is indicated for pain due to blood stasis obstructing the meridians.

V012-N00916.label: 活血消积
V012-N00916.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00916.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00916.aliases: 活血消癥； 活血消症
V012-N00916.englishNames: activating blood to resolve accumulation; activating blood to resolve masses; Activating Blood for Resolving Accumulation; Circulate blood and resolve masses
V012-N00916.pinyinNames: huo xue xiao ji; huó xuè xiāo jī
V012-N00916.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and resolve masses. It is indicated for masses due to blood stagnation.

V012-N00917.label: 活血祛风
V012-N00917.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00917.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00917.englishNames: Circulate blood and eliminate wind; activating blood and dispelling wind; Blood Activating Wind Dispelling
V012-N00917.pinyinNames: huo xue qu feng; huó xuè qū fēng
V012-N00917.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, eliminate wind and unblock meridians. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis with wind dryness or blood stasis with wind bi-impediment.

V012-N00918.label: 活血舒筋
V012-N00918.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00918.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00918.englishNames: Activating Blood and Relaxing Muscle Tendon; activating blood and soothing sinews; Circulate blood and relax tendons
V012-N00918.pinyinNames: huo xue shu jin; huó xuè shū jīn
V012-N00918.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis, relax tendons and unblock meridians. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis affecting the tendons and meridians.

V012-N00919.label: 活血行滞
V012-N00919.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N00919.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N00919.aliases: 活血行气； Move blood and circulate qi
V012-N00919.englishNames: activating blood and removing stagnation; Circulate blood and resolve stagnation
V012-N00919.pinyinNames: huo xue xing zhi; huó xuè xíng zhì
V012-N00919.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, resolve stasis, regulate qi and eliminate stagnation. It is indicated for blood stasis due to qi stagnation or qi stagnation due to blood stasis.

V012-N01026.label: 理气消痞
V012-N01026.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01026.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01026.aliases: 行气散结； 行气消痞； 理气散结； Circulate qi and resolve nodules
V012-N01026.englishNames: regulating qi and resolving stuffiness; regulating qi and resolving masses; Regulating Qi Flowing for Relieving Oppression; Regulate qi and resolve masses
V012-N01026.pinyinNames: li qi xiao pi; lǐ qì xiāo pǐ
V012-N01026.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi, unblock stagnation and thus resolve masses/nodules. It is indicated for conditions due to chronic qi stagnation.

V012-N01027.label: 理气行滞
V012-N01027.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01027.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01027.aliases: 理气导滞； Regulate qi and unblock stagnation
V012-N01027.englishNames: regulating qi and moving stagnation; Regulating Qi Flowing for Activating Stagnancy; regulating qiflowing for activating stagnancy; Regulate qi and resolve stagnation
V012-N01027.pinyinNames: li qi xing zhi; lǐ qì xíng zhì
V012-N01027.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi and unblock stagnation. It is indicated for conditions due to qi stagnation.

V012-N01028.label: 理气解表
V012-N01028.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01028.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01028.englishNames: regulating qi and releasing the external; regulating qi and releasing the exterior; Regulating Qi Flowing and Relieving Exterior; Regulate qi and release the exterior
V012-N01028.pinyinNames: li qi jie biao; lǐ qì jiě biǎo
V012-N01028.definitions: A treatment method to release the exterior and regulate qi. It is indicated for pathogenic factors affecting the exterior coupled with qi stagnation.

V012-N01029.label: 理血法
V012-N01029.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01029.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01029.englishNames: Methods to regulate blood; blood-regulating method; blood-regulating therapy; methods to regulate blood; Harmonizing  Blood; regulating blood method
V012-N01029.pinyinNames: li xue fa; lǐ xuè fǎ
V012-N01029.definitions: Treatment methods to regulate blood and restore normal blood circulation

V012-N01030.label: 生气于精
V012-N01030.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01030.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01030.englishNames: generating qi by replenishing essence; Tonify essence to generate qi
V012-N01030.pinyinNames: sheng qi yu jing; shēng qì yú jīng
V012-N01030.definitions: A treatment method to tonify yin essence to transform into yang qi. It is indicated for conditions resulting from yang qi deficiency due to yin essence deficiency.

V012-N01031.label: 疏散风邪
V012-N01031.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01031.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01031.englishNames: dispelling wind pathogens; Dispel external wind; dispersing and dissipating pathogenic wind; Dispelling Wind Pathogens
V012-N01031.pinyinNames: shu san feng xie; shū sàn fēng xié
V012-N01031.definitions: A treatment method to remove wind and release the exterior. It is indicated for conditions due to wind attacking the exterior.

V012-N01032.label: 疏肝利胆
V012-N01032.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01032.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01032.aliases: Dispersing Sagnated Liver Qi for Promoting Bile Flow； Soothing Liver Cholaneresis
V012-N01032.englishNames: soothing the liver and disinhibiting the gallbladder; Soothing Liver and Promoting Bile Secretion; Soothe the liver and promote bile flow; dispersing stagnated liver qi for promoting bile flow
V012-N01032.pinyinNames: shu gan li dan; shū gān lì dǎn
V012-N01032.definitions: A treatment method to soothe liver qi and promote the flow of bile. It is indicated for conditions due to qi stagnation of the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N01033.label: 疏肝和胃
V012-N01033.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01033.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01033.englishNames: Dispersing Stagnated Liver Qi for Regulating Stomach; Soothe the liver and harmonize the stomach; dispersing stagnated liver qi for regulating stomach; soothe the liver and harmonize the stomach; soothing the liver and harmonizing the stomach
V012-N01033.pinyinNames: shu gan he wei; shū gān hé wèi
V012-N01033.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi activity of the liver and stomach to harmonize between the liver and stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to qi stagnation of the liver and stomach or disharmony between the liver and stomach.

V012-N01034.label: 疏肝理气
V012-N01034.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01034.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01034.aliases: 疏肝； 疏肝解郁； Soothing Liver； Soothing Liver Regulating Qi； Soothe the liver and relieve depression
V012-N01034.englishNames: Soothing Liver and Regulating Qi; soothing the liver and regulating qi; soothe the liver and regulate qi; Soothe the liver and regulate qi
V012-N01034.pinyinNames: shu gan li qi; shū gān lǐ qì
V012-N01034.definitions: A treatment method to soothe the liver, regulate qi, unblock stagnation and resolve depression. It is indicated for conditions due to liver qi stagnation.

V012-N01035.label: 疏邪解表
V012-N01035.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01035.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01035.aliases: 疏邪透表； Remove pathogenic factors in the exterior
V012-N01035.englishNames: Dispelling Pathogenic Factor and Relieving Exterior; dispersing pathogenic factors and releasing the external; Remove pathogenic factors and release the exterior; dispersing pathogenic factors and releasing the exterior
V012-N01035.pinyinNames: shu xie jie biao; shū xié jiě biǎo
V012-N01035.definitions: A method to remove pathogenic factors in the exterior. It is indicated for excess exterior pattern.

V012-N01036.label: 疏风透疹
V012-N01036.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01036.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01036.aliases: 解表透疹； Release the exterior and promote skin eruption； 透疹； Dispelling Wind to Promote Eruption； Promoting Eruptions
V012-N01036.englishNames: dispelling wind to promote eruption; dispersing wind and outthrusting rashes; Remove wind and promote skin eruption; Dispelling Wind for Promoting Eruption; dispersing wind and promoting skin eruptions
V012-N01036.pinyinNames: shu feng tou zhen; shū fēng tòu zhěn
V012-N01036.definitions: A treatment method to remove wind and promote skin eruption through formulas or external therapies. It is often indicated for measles or rubella.

V012-N01037.label: 益气固脱
V012-N01037.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01037.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01037.englishNames: Supplement qi and prevent collapse; replenishing qi to prevent collapse; replenishing qi to prevent prostration
V012-N01037.pinyinNames: yi qi gu tuo; yì qì gù tuō
V012-N01037.definitions: A treatment method to prevent life-threatening qi collapse following loss of blood/fluids with qi-supplementing and astringent medicines.

V012-N01038.label: 益气安神
V012-N01038.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01038.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01038.englishNames: benefiting qi for tranquillization; replenishing qi and tranquilizing spirit; Reinforcing Qi for Tranquilization; Supplement qi and calm the mind; replenishing qi and tranquilizing mind
V012-N01038.pinyinNames: yi qi an shen; yì qì ān shén
V012-N01038.definitions: A treatment method to tonify qi, nourish the heart and calm the mind. It is indicated for restlessness due to heart qi deficiency.

V012-N01039.label: 益气滋阴
V012-N01039.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01039.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01039.aliases: 益气养阴； 滋阴益气； 补精益髓； 补肾益髓； Nourish yin and supplement qi； Tonify essence and benefit marrow； Tonify the kidney and benefit marrow； 养阴益气； 气阴双补
V012-N01039.englishNames: benefiting qi and nourishing yin; Supplement qi and nourish yin; Benefiting Qi for Nourishing Yin
V012-N01039.pinyinNames: yì qì zī yīn; yi qi zi yin
V012-N01039.definitions: A treatment method to supplement qi and nourish yin. It is indicated for deficiency of qi and yin.

V012-N01040.label: 益气解表
V012-N01040.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01040.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01040.aliases: Reinforcing Qi Reliev Exter； 补气解表
V012-N01040.englishNames: replenishing qi and releasing the external; benefiting qi for relieving supericies syndrome; Reinforcing Qi and Relieving Exterior; boosting qi and releasing the exterior; Supplement qi and release the exterior
V012-N01040.pinyinNames: yi qi jie biao; yì qì jiě biǎo
V012-N01040.definitions: A treatment method to release the exterior and tonifyqi. It is indicated for exterior pattern coupled with qi deficiency.

V012-N01041.label: 益气通下
V012-N01041.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01041.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01041.aliases: 益气通便； Supplement qi and promote bowel movements
V012-N01041.englishNames: replenishing qi to relax bowels; boosting qi to purge the intestine; Reinforcing Qi for Catharsis; Benefit qi and promote bowel movements
V012-N01041.pinyinNames: yi qi tong xia; yì qì tōng xià
V012-N01041.definitions: A treatment method to supplement qi to promote bowel movements. It is indicated for constipation due to qi deficiency.

V012-N01042.label: 益火消阴
V012-N01042.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01042.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01042.englishNames: replenishing fire to disperse yin; Boosting Source of Fire for Eliminating Abundance of Yin; boosting source of fire for eliminating abundance of yin; Warm yang to dissipate yin cold
V012-N01042.pinyinNames: yi huo xiao yin; yì huǒ xiāo yīn

V012-N01043.label: 益火生土
V012-N01043.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01043.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01043.englishNames: replenishing fire to generate earth; Supplement fire to cultivate earth
V012-N01043.pinyinNames: yi huo sheng tu; yì huǒ shēng tǔ
V012-N01043.definitions: A treatment method to warm and reinforce the vital fire to supplement the transportation and transformation of the spleen (earth). It is indicated for early morning diarrhoea due to decline of the vital fire and spleen yang deficiency.

V012-N01044.label: 益肾宁神
V012-N01044.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01044.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01044.englishNames: Nourishing Kidney to Tranquilize Mind; replenishing the kidney to tranquilize spirit; Benefit the kidney and calm the mind; replenishing the kidney to tranquilize mind
V012-N01044.pinyinNames: yi shen ning shen; yì shèn níng shén
V012-N01044.definitions: A treatment method to tonify kidney essence. It is indicated for palpitations or insomnia due to fear disturbing the mind qi or disharmony between the heart and kidney.

V012-N01045.label: 祛暑利湿

V012-N01045.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01045.aliases: 祛暑化湿； 清暑利湿； 清暑化湿

V012-N01045.definitions: A treatment method to clear summer heat and transform dampness. It is indicated for conditions due to internal retention of summer heat and dampness.

V012-N01046.label: 祛暑法
V012-N01046.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01046.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01046.aliases: 涤暑清热； 解暑； 清暑； 祛暑； 祛暑清热； Relieving Summer Heat； 解暑法

V012-N01046.pinyinNames: qu shu fa; qū shǔ fǎ
V012-N01046.definitions: Treatment methods to remove summer heat

V012-N01047.label: 祛暑清热
V012-N01047.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01047.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01047.aliases: 涤暑清热； 清热祛暑； 清热解暑； 清热解暑@@@清热祛暑
