V012-N01047.pinyinNames: qu shu qing re; qū shǔ qīng rè
V012-N01047.definitions: A treatment method to remove summer heat. It is indicated for conditions due to summer heat.

V012-N01048.label: 祛暑解表
V012-N01048.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01048.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01048.aliases: 解表清暑； 透表清暑； Release the exterior and clear summer heat
V012-N01048.englishNames: Expelling  Summerheat and Relieving Superficies; Eliminate summer heat and release the exterior; dispelling summer-heat and releasing the external; dispelling summer-heat and releasing the exterior
V012-N01048.pinyinNames: qu shu jie biao; qū shǔ jiě biǎo
V012-N01048.definitions: A treatment method to clear summer heat and release the exterior. It is indicated for summer heat attacking the exterior.

V012-N01049.label: 祛湿法
V012-N01049.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01049.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01049.aliases: 化湿； 祛湿； 燥湿； Dissipating Dampness； Dissolving Dampness； Eliminating Dampness
V012-N01049.englishNames: eliminating dampness method; dampness-dispelling therapy; Dampness-eliminating methods; methods to eliminate dampness; Eliminating Dampness Method
V012-N01049.pinyinNames: qu shi fa; qū shī fǎ
V012-N01049.definitions: Methods to disperse and transform dampness with aromatic medicines

V012-N01050.label: 祛湿解表
V012-N01050.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01050.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01050.aliases: 解表祛湿； 疏表化湿； 宣表化湿
V012-N01050.englishNames: eliminating dampness and releasing the exterior; Dissolving Dampness and Relieving Exterior; Eliminate dampness and release the exterior; dispelling dampness and releasing the external
V012-N01050.pinyinNames: qu shi jie biao; qū shī jiě biǎo
V012-N01050.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors in the exterior and resolve dampness with aroma. It is indicated for wind dampness affecting the exterior.

V012-N01051.label: 祛热开窍
V012-N01051.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01051.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01051.englishNames: Eliminate heat and open the orifices
V012-N01051.definitions: A treatment method to clear summer heat, circulate qi and open the orifices. It is indicated for summer heat blocking the flow of qi.

V012-N01052.label: 祛痰化湿开窍
V012-N01052.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01052.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01052.englishNames: dispelling phlegm and resolving dampness to open orifices; Eliminate phlegm, transform dampness and open the orifices
V012-N01052.pinyinNames: qu tan hua shi kai qiao; qū tán huà shī kāi qiào
V012-N01052.definitions: A treatment method to eliminate phlegm, transform dampness and open the orifices. It is indicated for phlegm dampness disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N01053.label: 祛痰化瘀
V012-N01053.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01053.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01053.aliases: 消痰化瘀； 逐瘀化痰
V012-N01053.englishNames: dispelling phlegm and resolving stasis; Eliminate phlegm and transform stasis; Eliminating Phlegm and Removing Blood Stasis
V012-N01053.pinyinNames: qu tan hua yu; qū tán huà yū
V012-N01053.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to eliminate phlegm and transform stasis. It is indicated for conditions due to phlegm combined with stasis.

V012-N01054.label: 祛痰宣痹
V012-N01054.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01054.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01054.englishNames: expelling phlegm and dredging channel blockade; Eliminate phlegm and unblock bi-impediment; dispelling phlegm to diffuse impediment
V012-N01054.pinyinNames: qu tan xuan bi; qū tán xuān bì

V012-N01055.label: 祛痰杀虫
V012-N01055.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01055.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01055.englishNames: Eliminate phlegm and kill parasites; dispelling phlegm and killing worms
V012-N01055.pinyinNames: qu tan sha chong; qū tán shā chóng
V012-N01055.definitions: A treatment method to combine medicines to eliminate phlegm and kill parasites. It is indicated for conditions due to phlegm mixed with parasites.

V012-N01056.label: 祛痰法
V012-N01056.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01056.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01056.englishNames: Expelling Phlegm Method; phlegm-dispelling therapy; method of eliminating phlegm; Resolving phlegm methods
V012-N01056.pinyinNames: qu tan fa; qū tán fǎ
V012-N01056.definitions: Treatment methods to resolve phlegm fluids or nodules in the zang–fu organs, meridians, cutaneous membrane and limbs/joints

V012-N01057.label: 祛痰理气解毒
V012-N01057.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01057.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01057.englishNames: dispelling phlegm, regulating qi and removing toxin; Eliminate phlegm, regulate qi and remove toxins
V012-N01057.pinyinNames: qu tan li qi jie dong; qū tán lǐ qì jiě dú
V012-N01057.definitions: A treatment method to eliminate stasis, regulate qi and remove toxins. It is indicated for toxic phlegm retention.

V012-N01058.label: 祛瘀下胎
V012-N01058.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01058.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01058.aliases: 化瘀催产； 化瘀下胎； 祛瘀催产
V012-N01058.englishNames: Removing Blood Stasis for Parturifacient; dispelling stasis to deliver dead fetus; Transform stasis and induce labour
V012-N01058.pinyinNames: qu yu xia tai; qū yū xià tāi
V012-N01058.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and induce labour. It is indicated for difficult labour due to blood stasis and qi stagnation.

V012-N01059.label: 祛瘀生新
V012-N01059.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01059.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01059.englishNames: dispelling stasis to promote blood production; removing blood stasis for promoting tissue regeneration; dispel stasis to promote regeneration; Eliminate stasis and regenerate blood; 化瘀生新
V012-N01059.pinyinNames: qu yu sheng xin; qū yū shēng xīn
V012-N01059.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and regenerate new blood. It is indicated for blood stasis coupled with blood deficiency.

V012-N01060.label: 祛瘀通络
V012-N01060.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01060.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01060.aliases: 化瘀通络； 行瘀通络

V012-N01060.pinyinNames: qu yu tong luo; qū yū tōng luò
V012-N01060.definitions: A treatment method to circulate blood, transform stasis and unblock meridians. It is indicated for conditions due to blood stasis obstructing the meridians.

V012-N01061.label: 祛腐生肌
V012-N01061.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01061.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01061.aliases: 去腐生肌； Remov Necrosis Promot Granulat； Removing Necrosis Promoting Granulation
V012-N01061.englishNames: Eliminate dead tissues and regenerate new tissues; dispelling putridity to promote tissue regeneration; Removing Necrosis for Promoting Granulation
V012-N01061.pinyinNames: qū fǔ shēng jī; qu fu sheng ji
V012-N01061.definitions: A treatment method to eliminate dead tissues, regenerate flesh and speed up wound healing.

V012-N01062.label: 祛风化痰
V012-N01062.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法

V012-N01098.pinyinNames: jie du xiao zhong; jiě dú xiāo zhǒng; jie dong xiao zhong
V012-N01098.definitions: A treatment method to remove toxins, resolve sores/ulcers and eliminate swelling.

V012-N01099.label: 解表宣肺
V012-N01099.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01099.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01099.englishNames: releasing the external and diffusing the lung; Relieving Exterior and Facilitating Lung; Release the exterior and disperse the lung; releasing the exterior and diffusing the lung
V012-N01099.pinyinNames: jie biao xuan fei; jiě biǎo xuān fèi
V012-N01099.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors in the exterior and disperse lung qi. It is indicated for wind warmth or wind cold attacking the lung.

V012-N01100.label: 解表攻里
V012-N01100.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01100.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01100.aliases: 解表攻下； 发表攻里； 发表攻下； Reliev Exter Purg Inter
V012-N01100.englishNames: Relieving Exterior Purging Interior; releasing the external and purging the internal; Release the exterior and purge the interior; releasing the exterior and attacking the interior
V012-N01100.pinyinNames: jie biao gong li; jiě biǎo gōng lǐ
V012-N01100.definitions: A treatment method to combine purgative and exterior-releasing medicines. It is indicated for excess pattern involving both the exterior and interior.

V012-N01101.label: 解表法
V012-N01101.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01101.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01101.aliases: 发表法； 汗法； 解表； relieving superficies method
V012-N01101.englishNames: relieving superficies method; Relieving Exterior; Exterior-releasing methods; exterior-releasing method; therapy for relieving external syndrome/patterns; releasing exterior method
V012-N01101.pinyinNames: jie biao fa; jiě biǎo fǎ

V012-N01102.label: 解表清里
V012-N01102.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01102.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01102.englishNames: Release the exterior and clear the interior; Relieving Exterior Clearing Interior; releasing the external and clearing the internal; releasing the exterior and clearing the interior
V012-N01102.pinyinNames: jiě biǎo qīng lǐ; jie biao qing li
V012-N01102.definitions: A treatment method to combine exterior-releasing and interior-clearing medicines. It is indicated for exterior–interior heat pattern or exterior cold with interior heat pattern.

V012-N01103.label: 解表温里
V012-N01103.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01103.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01103.aliases: 发汗温里； Reliev Exter Warm Inter； Induce sweating and warm the interior
V012-N01103.englishNames: Relieving Exterior Warming Interior; releasing the exterior and warming the interior; releasing the external and warming the internal; Release the exterior and warm the interior
V012-N01103.pinyinNames: jie biao wen li; jiě biǎo wēn lǐ
V012-N01103.definitions: A treatment method to use medicines to induce sweating, release the exterior, warm the interior and dissipate cold. It is indicated for exterior-interior cold pattern or exterior heat with interior cold pattern.

V012-N01104.label: 解表通经
V012-N01104.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01104.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01104.englishNames: dispersing the external to unblock meridians; releasing the exterior to unblock meridians; Release the exterior and unblock meridians
V012-N01104.pinyinNames: jie biao tong jing; jiě biǎo tōng jīng
V012-N01104.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors in the exterior and unblock meridian qi through formulas, tuina or external therapies. It is indicated for external pathogenic factors impairing the meridian qi.

V012-N01105.label: 解郁安神
V012-N01105.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01105.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01105.englishNames: Resolving Stagnation for Tranquilization; resolving stagnation for tranquilization; releasing stagnation to tranquilize mind; Relieve stagnation and calm the mind; releasing depression to tranquilize spirit
V012-N01105.pinyinNames: jie yu an shen; jiě yù ān shén
V012-N01105.definitions: A treatment method to relieve stagnation, regulate qi and calm the mind. It is indicated for grief affecting the mind due to depression.

V012-N01106.label: 调和气血
V012-N01106.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01106.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01106.aliases: 调理气血； 理气和血； Regulate qi and harmonize blood； Harmonizing Qi Blood
V012-N01106.englishNames: harmonizing qi and blood; regulating and harmonizing qi and blood; Regulate and harmonize qi and blood; Harmonizing Qi and Blood; harmonize qi and blood
V012-N01106.pinyinNames: diao he qi xue; tiáo hé qì xuè; tiao he  qi  xue
V012-N01106.definitions: A treatment method to use qi-regulating and blood-circulating formulas. It is indicated for conditions due to disharmony between qi and blood.

V012-N01107.label: 调和肝脾
V012-N01107.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01107.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01107.aliases: 调理肝脾； Harmonize between the liver and spleen； Harmonizing Liver Spleen
V012-N01107.englishNames: regulating and harmonizing the liver and spleen; Regulate and harmonize the liver and spleen; Harmonizing Liver and Spleen; harmonize the liver and spleen; harmonizing liver andspleen
V012-N01107.pinyinNames: diao he gan pi; tiáo hé gān pí; tiao he gan pi
V012-N01107.definitions: A treatment method to soothe the liver, fortify the spleen and regulate qi activity to harmonize between the liver and spleen. It is indicated or liver qi stagnation with spleen deficiency or liver hyperactivity with spleen deficiency.

V012-N01108.label: 调和营卫
V012-N01108.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01108.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01108.englishNames: harmonizing nutrient and defensive aspects; regulating and harmonizing the nutrient and defense; Harmonizing Yingfen and Weifen; harmonize the nutrient and defense; Regulate and harmonize the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence; harmonizing yingfen and weifen; regulating and harmonizing the ying-nutrient and wei-defense
V012-N01108.pinyinNames: tiao he ying wei; diao he ying wei; tiáo hé yíng wèi
V012-N01108.definitions: A treatment method to coordinate between Ying nutrients and Wei-defence through removing wind and nourishing yin. It is indicated for disorders due to disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence.

V012-N01109.label: 调气和营
V012-N01109.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01109.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01109.englishNames: regulating qi and harmonizing the nutrient; regulating qi and harmonizing the ying-nutrient; Regulating Qi and Ying; Regulate qi and harmonize the Ying nutrients
V012-N01109.pinyinNames: diao qi he ying; tiao qi he ying; tiáo qì hé yíng

V012-N01110.label: 调理冲任
V012-N01110.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01110.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01110.aliases: 调摄冲任； Regulate and nourish the Chong and Ren； 调补冲任； Regulating Chong Ren

V012-N01110.pinyinNames: diao li chong ren; tiáo lǐ chōng rèn; tiao li chong ren
V012-N01110.definitions: A treatment method to use formulas to regulate qi and blood of the Chong and Ren. It is indicated for conditions due to disharmony between the Chong and Ren.

V012-N01111.label: 调理肠胃
V012-N01111.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01111.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01111.aliases: 调和肠胃； Harmonize between the stomach and intestines
V012-N01111.englishNames: coordinating intestines and stomach; Regulate and harmonize the stomach and intestines; regulating the intestine and stomach
V012-N01111.pinyinNames: tiáo lǐ cháng wèi; diao li chang wei
V012-N01111.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi activity of the stomach and intestines to harmonize between the stomach and intestines. It is indicated for conditions due to qi stagnation of the stomach and intestines.

V012-N01112.label: 调理脾胃
V012-N01112.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01112.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01112.aliases: 调和脾胃； Harmonize between the spleen and stomach
V012-N01112.englishNames: regulating the spleen and stomach; Harmonizing Spleen Stomach; Regulate and harmonize the spleen and stomach
V012-N01112.pinyinNames: diao li pi wei; tiao li pi wei; tiáo lǐ pí wèi
V012-N01112.definitions: A treatment method to regulate qi activity of the spleen and stomach to harmonize between the spleen and stomach. It is indicated for conditions due to disharmony between the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01113.label: 软坚散结
V012-N01113.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01113.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01113.aliases: 软坚消结； 散结软坚； 软坚消瘿； 软坚消癥； 软坚消肿； 软坚； Soften Hard Mass Loosen Knot； Softening Hard Masses
V012-N01113.englishNames: softening hardness and dissipating masses; softening and resolving hard mass; softening hardness and dissipating bind; soften hardness and dissipate binds; Soften hardness and dissipate masses; Softening and Resolving Hard Mass
V012-N01113.pinyinNames: ruan jian san jie; ruǎn jiān sàn jiē

V012-N01114.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01114.englishNames: softening hardness and moistening dryness; Softening Hard Masses and Moisturizing Dryness; Soften hardness and moisten dryness

V012-N01115.label: 轻宣润燥
V012-N01115.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01115.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01115.aliases: 轻宣外燥； Disperse qi and moisten external dryness
V012-N01115.englishNames: diffusing and moistening dryness with light herbs; Moisturizing Dryness by Light Diffusion; Moisten by mild dispersing

V012-N01115.definitions: A treatment method to disperse lung qi, increase fluids and moisten dryness. It is indicated for external dryness affecting the exterior.

V012-N01116.label: 辛凉清热
V012-N01116.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01116.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01116.pinyinNames: xin liang qing re; xīn liáng qīng rè

V012-N01117.label: 辛凉解表

V012-N01117.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01117.pinyinNames: xin liang jie biao; xīn liáng jiě biǎo
V012-N01117.definitions: A treatment method to remove wind and clear heat with pungent, cool medicines or formulas. It is indicated for wind heat exterior pattern.

V012-N01118.label: 辛寒清热

V012-N01118.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01118.aliases: 辛寒清气； Clear qi with pungent cold
V012-N01118.englishNames: clearing heat with pungent-cold medicinals; Clear heat with pungent cold; clearing heat with pungent, cold medicinals; Clearing Heat with Pungent Cold
V012-N01118.pinyinNames: xin han qing re; xīn hán qīng rè

V012-N01119.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01119.aliases: 发表散寒； 辛温发汗； Pungent Warm Diaphoresis； 疏风散寒； Promote sweating with pungent-warm； Release the exterior and dissipate cold

V012-N01119.pinyinNames: xin wen jie biao; xīn wēn jiě biǎo

V012-N01120.label: 辟秽化浊
V012-N01120.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01120.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01120.aliases: 芳香化浊； 辟秽泄浊； 芳香泄浊； Transform turbidity with aroma
V012-N01120.englishNames: Repel filth and transform turbidity; repelling foulness and resolving turbidity
V012-N01120.pinyinNames: bi hui hua zhuo; pì huì huà zhuó
V012-N01120.definitions: A treatment method to transform turbidity and repel filth with aromatic medicines. It is indicated for conditions due to filth, turbidity and dampness.

V012-N01121.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01121.englishNames: unblocking meridians to stop itching; Unblock meridians and relieve itch; Dredging Channels and Collaterals for Relieving Itching

V012-N01122.label: 通络下乳
V012-N01122.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01122.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01122.englishNames: unblocking collaterals to promote lactation; Unblock meridians and increase lactation; dredging collateral for promoting lactation; Dredging Collateral for Promoting Lactation

V012-N01123.label: 通耳
V012-N01123.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01123.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01123.aliases: 利耳； Benefit the ears； 利耳窍； 通耳窍； Dredging External Acoustic Meatus
V012-N01123.englishNames: unblocking ears; unblocking the ears; Unblock the ears; Improving Hearing; improving hearing
V012-N01123.pinyinNames: tong dong; tōng ěr; tong er
V012-N01123.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and benefit the ears.

V012-N01124.label: 通鼻
V012-N01124.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01124.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01124.englishNames: relieving stuffy nose; unblocking the nose; relieve the stuffy nose; unblocking nose; Unblock the nose; Clearing the Nasal Passage
V012-N01124.definitions: A treatment method to remove pathogenic factors, reinforce anti-pathogenic qi and benefit the nose.

V012-N01125.label: 重镇安神
V012-N01125.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01125.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01125.aliases: 潜镇安神； 镇惊安神； 镇心安神； Settle Fright and Tranquilize； Tranquilization with Heavy Material； 镇静安神
V012-N01125.englishNames: Tranquillization with Heavy Prescription; tranquillization with heavy prescription; tranquilize by heavy settling; tranquilizing spirit by heavy settling; Calm the mind with heavy medicinal minerals; tranquilizing mind with heavy-weight medicinals
V012-N01125.pinyinNames: zhong zhen an shen; zhòng zhèn ān shén
V012-N01125.definitions: A treatment method to use heavy medicinal minerals to submerge yang and settle the mind. It is indicated for insomnia due to yang hyperactivity.

V012-N01126.label: 金水相生
V012-N01126.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01126.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01126.englishNames: mutual promotion between metal and water; Mutual generation between metal and water
V012-N01126.pinyinNames: jin shui xiang sheng; jīn shuǐ xiāng shēng
V012-N01126.definitions: A treatment method to tonify the kidney and nourish the lung for lung deficiency and kidney deficiency, since the lung (metal) and kidney (water) mutually support physiologically and mutually affect pathologically.

V012-N01127.label: 镇惊安神
V012-N01127.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01127.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01127.englishNames: calming fright and tranquilizing spirit; calming fright and tranquilizing mind; Tranquillize and calm the mind; settle fright and tranquilize
V012-N01127.pinyinNames: zhen jing an shen; zhèn jīng ān shén
V012-N01127.definitions: A treatment method to calm the mind with heavy medicinal minerals. It is indicated for fear/fright disturbing the mind.

V012-N01128.label: 除湿通络
V012-N01128.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01128.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01128.englishNames: eliminating dampness and unblocking collaterals; Remove dampness and unblock collaterals; Eliminating Dampness and Dredging Channels; eliminating dampness and dredging channels
V012-N01128.pinyinNames: chu shi tong luo; chú shī tōng luò
V012-N01128.definitions: A treatment method to transform dampness and unblock collaterals. It is indicated for dampness obstructing the collaterals.

V012-N01129.label: 驱虫法
V012-N01129.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01129.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01129.englishNames: Repelling parasites methods; Expelling Intestinal Parasites Method
V012-N01129.pinyinNames: qū chóng fǎ; qu chong fa
V012-N01129.definitions: Treatment methods to kill or repel intestinal parasites

V012-N01130.label: 驱蛔杀虫
V012-N01130.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜治法
V012-N01130.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01130.englishNames: expelling ascaris and killing worms; Repel roundworms and kill parasites; expelling and killing ascarid
V012-N01130.pinyinNames: qu hui sha chong; qū huí shā chóng
V012-N01130.definitions: A treatment method to repel and kill roundworms. It is indicated for conditions due to intestinal worms.

V012-N01131.label: 药物治疗类
V012-N01131.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N01131.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01132.label: 七情和合
V012-N01132.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01132.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01132.definitions: Compatibility methods of Chinese medicines, including the using of a single medicine, mutual reinforcement, mutual assistance, mutual restraint, mutual suppression, mutual inhibition and mutual opposition.

V012-N01133.label: 七方
V012-N01133.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01133.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01133.definitions: Seven formula types include major formulas, minor formulas, slow-acting formulas, fast-acting formulas, odd-numbered formulas, even-numbered formulas and compound formulas.

V012-N01134.label: 下法
V012-N01134.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01134.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01134.definitions: A method to induce bowel movements to cleanse or eliminate indigested food, dry faeces, cold accumulation, stagnant blood, phlegm, and water retention. It allows for the removal of pathogenic factors out of the body.

V012-N01135.label: 中成药
V012-N01135.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01135.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01135.definitions: Safe, effective and reliable Chinese medical preparations that have been tested by clinical use and manufactured according to standard technical procedures.

V012-N01136.label: 中药
V012-N01136.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01136.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01136.definitions: Medicines to prevent, treat and diagnose diseases under the guidance of Chinese medical theories.

V012-N01137.label: 中药不良反应
V012-N01137.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01137.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01137.pinyinNames: zhōng yào bù liáng fǎn yìng
V012-N01137.definitions: These include side-effects or toxic reactions. Following the standard dosage, side-effects of Chinese medicines are often mild and can resolve spontaneously without treatment. Toxic reactions are often caused by overdose or prolonged use of poisonous materia medica, and can do substantial damage to the organs and functions.

V012-N01138.label: 中药变质
V012-N01138.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01138.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01138.definitions: Physical or chemical changes in Chinese medicines due to inappropriate care in transportation or storage. These changes include mildew, worm-eaten, discolouration, flavour reversion, oiliness or air-slake and can directly affect the medicinal quality, efficacy and safety.

V012-N01139.label: 中药学
V012-N01139.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01139.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01139.englishNames: Theory and practice of Chinese medicines
V012-N01139.definitions: The study of basic theories, origin, production, processing, property, actions, indications and application of Chinese medicines.

V012-N01140.label: 中药性状
V012-N01140.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01140.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01140.definitions: Physical or chemical characteristics of Chinese medicines, including their shape, colour, odour, taste and properties.

V012-N01141.label: 中药性能
V012-N01141.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01141.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01141.definitions: The core part of essential theories on Chinese medicines. These include four properties, five flavours, ascending/floating, descending/sinking, meridian tropism and toxicity.

V012-N01142.label: 中药材
V012-N01142.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01142.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01142.definitions: Raw materials include medicinal plants, animals and minerals that can be prepared into pieces, extracts or Chinese patent medicine.

V012-N01143.label: 中药类
V012-N01143.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01143.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01143.definitions: Knowledge about the concept of Chinese medicines, the basic theories as well as the origin, production, processing, property, actions and indications of medicines

V012-N01144.label: 丸剂
V012-N01144.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01144.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01144.definitions: A solid globular mass made of finely powdered medicines with a suitable excipient or binder. It can be further categorized into honeyed pills, water pills, water-honeyed pills, paste pills, concentration pills, wax-wrapped pills, and mini pills.

V012-N01145.label: 以泻代清
V012-N01145.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01145.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01145.englishNames: Formulas that purge the interior heat
V012-N01145.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and purge the intestine. They are indicated for interior heat. A representative formula is Liang Ge San (Diaphragm-Cooling Powder).

V012-N01146.label: 佐制药
V012-N01146.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01146.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01146.definitions: Ingredient(s) to eliminate or moderate the toxicity and drastic actions of the monarch and minister medicines.

V012-N01147.label: 佐助药
V012-N01147.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01147.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01147.definitions: Ingredient(s) to help facilitate the action of the monarch and minister medicines on main or associated patterns.

V012-N01148.label: 佐药
V012-N01148.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01148.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01148.definitions: Ingredient(s) to help the monarch and minister medicines treat principal or associated patterns; to counterbalance the toxicity or drastic actions of the monarch or minister medicines; or has/have the opposite flavour and property with the monarch medicines. They are further categorized into facilitating assistant medicines, counterbalancing assistant medicines and counteracting assistant medicines.

V012-N01149.label: 使药
V012-N01149.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01149.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01149.definitions: Ingredient(s) to coordinate the rest of the ingredients or direct action to the affected meridians or sites. They are further categorized into coordinating medicines and meridian-directing medicines.

V012-N01150.label: 修治
V012-N01150.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01150.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01150.definitions: The preliminary processing method of Chinese medicines, including purification, crushing and cutting.

V012-N01151.label: 偶方
V012-N01151.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01151.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01151.definitions: Formulas with two or even-numbered ingredients.

V012-N01152.label: 先入后出
V012-N01152.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01152.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01152.englishNames: Formulas that enter first and exit next
V012-N01152.definitions: Formulas that are used for later stage of febrile diseases with retention of pathogenic factors in the Yin phase. A representative formula is Qing Hao Bei Jie Tang (Sweet Wormwood and Turtle Shell Decoction). Bie Jia (Carapax Trionycis) guides Qing Hao (Herba Artemisiae Annuae) to the interior and Qing Hao guides Bie Jia out to the exterior. The combination of the two ingredients nourishes yin and clears internal heat.

V012-N01153.label: 先煎
V012-N01153.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01153.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01153.englishNames: to be decocted first; Decoct first
V012-N01153.definitions: A method for insoluble minerals, rocks or shells that require a long time to decoct. These ingredients need to be ground and decocted 30–60 minutes first before placing into the rest of the decoction. Toxic medicines need to decoct first for 60–120 minutes before placing into the rest of the decoction to reduce their toxicity.

V012-N01154.label: 八法
V012-N01154.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01154.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01154.definitions: The eight methods are categorized according to functions of the formulas. They are sweating , vomiting, draining downward , harmonizing , warming , clearing, reducing and tonifying.

V012-N01155.label: 养心安神药
V012-N01155.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01155.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01155.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour, moist in quality and tonic or neutral in property. They mainly act to nourish the heart and tranquilize the mind. These medicines are indicated for mild or severe palpitations due to malnourishment of heart mind, and insomnia, poor memory and dream-disturbed sleep due to heart yin or heart blood deficiency.

V012-N01156.label: 冲服
V012-N01156.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01156.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01156.definitions: A method to pour the medicine directly into water or other boiling liquid. Valuable medicines need to be ground into fine powder first.

V012-N01157.label: 冷服
V012-N01157.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01157.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01157.definitions: A method to take the decoction when it becomes cold.

V012-N01158.label: 凉开剂
V012-N01158.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01158.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01158.englishNames: Formulas that open the orifices with cold-property ingredients
V012-N01158.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and open the orifices. They are indicated for heat blockage pattern due to toxic heat entering the pericardium in febrile disease.

V012-N01159.label: 凉血止血药
V012-N01159.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01159.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01159.definitions: Medicines that are sweet and bitter in flavour and cold and cool in property. They mainly act to clear pathogenic heat in the blood phase. These medicines are indicated for bleeding due to heat-induced reckless blood flow.

V012-N01160.label: 分服
V012-N01160.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01160.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01160.definitions: A method to take the medicine in divided times.

V012-N01161.label: 分经论治
V012-N01161.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01161.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01161.definitions: These formulas are often used for common cold, headache, and perspiration. A representative formula is Jiu Wei Qiang Huo Tang (Nine Ingredients Notopterygium Decoction).

V012-N01162.label: 切制处理
V012-N01162.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01162.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01162.definitions: A method to cut medicines into pieces, blocks, threads or segments for preparation, drying, storage and weighing.

V012-N01163.label: 刚柔相济
V012-N01163.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01163.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01163.englishNames: Formulas that combine dry/pungent and sweet/moist medicines
V012-N01163.definitions: Formulas that combined dry, pungent and aromatic medicines with soft, sweet and moistening medicines. A representative formula is Huang Tu Tang (Yellow Earth Decoction).

V012-N01164.label: 利水渗湿剂
V012-N01164.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01164.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01164.englishNames: Formulas that promote urination and drain dampness
V012-N01164.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines (sweet or bland in flavour) to drain dampness through urination. They are indicated for water dampness pattern.

V012-N01165.label: 利水渗湿药
V012-N01165.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01165.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01165.definitions: Medicines to unblock the water passage and drain water dampness. They are indicated for internal retention of water dampness.

V012-N01166.label: 剂型
V012-N01166.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01166.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01166.definitions: The form of a prepared medicine designed for medical treatment or prevention.

V012-N01167.label: 化湿药
V012-N01167.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01167.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01167.definitions: Medicines that are aromatic in smell and dry in property. They mainly act to transform dampness and benefit the spleen. These medicines are indicated for dampness affecting the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01168.label: 化痰药
V012-N01168.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01168.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01168.definitions: Medicines to transform or eliminate phlegm. Based on the property and functions, they are further categorized into two types: medicines to warm and transform cold phlegm; and medicines to clear heat and transform heat phlegm.

V012-N01169.label: 化瘀止血药
V012-N01169.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01169.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01169.aliases: 活血止血药； Stasis-transforming haemostatic medicines
V012-N01169.definitions: Medicines to stop bleeding and circulate blood to transform stasis. They act to stop bleeding but do not cause stasis; and circulate blood but do not cause bleeding. These medicines are indicated for haemorrhagic conditions due to stagnant blood or blood failing to circulate within the vessels.

V012-N01170.label: 十九畏
V012-N01170.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01170.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01170.definitions: One of the contraindicated combinations of Chinese medicines. These include: Liu Huang (sulfur) is incompatible with Pu Xiao (crude sodium sulfate), Shui Yin (mercury) with Pi Shuang (arsenic trioxide),Lang Du (Radix Euphorbiae Ebracteolatae) Mi Tuo Seng (litharge), Ba Dou (Semen Crotonis) with Qian Niu (Semen Pharbitidis), Ding Xiang (Flos Caryophylli) with Yu Jin (Radix Curcumae), Ya Xiao (crystalline sodium sulfate) with San Leng (Rhizoma Sparganii), Chuan Wu (Radix Aconiti) and Cao Wu (Radix Aconiti Kuznezoffi) with Xi Jiao (Cornu Rhinoceri), Ren Shen (Radix Ginseng) with Wu Ling Zhi (with Faeces Trogopterorum), and Guan Gui (Cortex Cinnamomi) with Shi Zhi (Halloysitum Rubrum).

V012-N01171.label: 十八反
V012-N01171.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01171.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01171.definitions: One of the contraindicated combinations of Chinese medicines. These include: Gan Cao (Radix Glycyrrhizae) is antagonistic to Da Ji (Radix Euphorbiae Pekinensis), Yuan Hua (Flos Genkwa), Gan Sui (Radix Euphorbiae Kansui) and Hai Zao (Sargassum); Wu Tou (Radix Aconiti) is antagonistic to Bei Mu (Bulbus Fritillariae), Gua Lou (Fructus Trichosanthis), Ban Xia (Rhizoma Pinelliae), Bai Lian (Radix Ampelopsis) and Bai Ji (Rhizoma Bletillae); and Li Lu (Radix Veratri Nigri) is antagonistic to Ren Shen (Radix Ginseng), Sha Shen (Radix Salviae Miltiorrhizae), Dan Shen (Radix Adenophorae), Ku Shen (Radix Sophorae Flavescentis), Xuan Shen (Radix Scrophulariae), Xi Xin (Herba Asari) and Shao Yao (Radix Paeoniae).

V012-N01172.label: 十剂
V012-N01172.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01172.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01172.definitions: A categorization method according to functions of the formula. The 10 types are dispersing formula, unblocking/unclogging formula, tonifying formula, purgative formula, formula consisting of light-weighted medicines, formula consisting of heavy-weighted medicines, lubricative formula, astringent formula, dry formula and moistening formula.

V012-N01173.label: 升浮药
V012-N01173.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01173.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01173.definitions: Medicines to lift yang, release the exterior, induce vomiting, open the orifices, etc. They tend to go upward and outward.

V012-N01174.label: 升降浮沉
V012-N01174.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01174.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01174.definitions: The four cardinal directionalities of medicines: ascending, descending, sinking and floating. These four directions are associated with the weight, property, preparation and combination of medicines.

V012-N01175.label: 单行
V012-N01175.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01175.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01175.definitions: Use a single medicine alone to treat a disorder.

V012-N01176.label: 反佐药
V012-N01176.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01176.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01176.definitions: Ingredient(s) with a property opposite to and a therapeutic effect complementary to the monarch medicine(s).

V012-N01177.label: 古方
V012-N01177.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01177.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01177.definitions: The formulas before the Qing Dynasty (1644–1911).

V012-N01178.label: 另煎
V012-N01178.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01178.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01178.definitions: A method to decoct some medicines separately (2–3 hours), usually precious medicines, to ensure that the ingredients remain active.

V012-N01179.label: 吐法
V012-N01179.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01179.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01179.definitions: A method to induce vomiting to fast remove phlegm, indigested food and poisons from the throat, thoracic diaphragm or stomach.

V012-N01180.label: 君药
V012-N01180.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01180.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01180.definitions: Ingredient(s) that provide a dominant curative action on the main pattern/disorder or primary symptoms.

V012-N01181.label: 和法
V012-N01181.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01181.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01181.definitions: A method to moderate different parts of the body to expel pathogenic factors and regulate functions of the zang–fu organs. It helps to achieve yin-yang or interior–exterior balance by releasing the Shaoyang pattern when the pathogen lies in between the interior and exterior of the body and by harmonizing zang–fu organs.

V012-N01182.label: 和解剂
V012-N01182.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01182.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01182.definitions: Formulas to harmonize and release Shaoyang, coordinate the liver and spleen, and regulate the stomach and intestines. They are used to treat pathogenic factor affecting the Shaoyang, disharmony between the liver and spleen and lack of coordination between the stomach and intestines. These formulas are categorized into three types: formulas to harmonize Shaoyang; formulas to harmonize the liver and spleen; and formulas to harmonize the stomach and intestines.

V012-N01183.label: 和解少阳剂
V012-N01183.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01183.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01183.englishNames: Formulas that harmonize and release Shaoyang
V012-N01183.definitions: Formulas that are indicated for cold damage affecting the Shaoyang (combined exterior and interior pattern).

V012-N01184.label: 咸味
V012-N01184.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01184.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01184.definitions: Medicines with a salty flavour act to purge the intestine, promote bowel movements and soften masses. They are indicated for constipation, goitre, scrofula, phlegm nodules and masses.

V012-N01185.label: 噙化
V012-N01185.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01185.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01185.definitions: A method to melt the medicine in the mouth.

V012-N01186.label: 四气
V012-N01186.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01186.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01186.definitions: The four basic properties of medicines—cold, hot, warmth, and coolness.

V012-N01187.label: 四气五味
V012-N01187.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01187.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01187.englishNames: Four qi and and five flavours
V012-N01187.definitions: The basic properties and flavours of Chinese medicines.

V012-N01188.label: 回阳救逆剂
V012-N01188.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01188.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01188.englishNames: Formulas that resuscitate yang and prevent collapse
V012-N01188.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to warm yang and prevent fainting. They are indicated for approaching collapse of yang qi.

V012-N01189.label: 固崩止带剂
V012-N01189.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01189.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01189.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to stop bleeding and vaginal discharge. They are indicated for metrorrhagia, metrostaxis or profuse vaginal discharge.

V012-N01190.label: 固涩剂
V012-N01190.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01190.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01190.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to astringe or secure qi, blood, essence and body fluids. They are indicated for loss of or abnormal leakage/discharge of qi, blood, essence and body fluids. These formulas are categorized into five types: formulas to secure the exterior and stop sweating; formulas to astringe the lung and stop coughing; formulas to astringe the large intestine and stop diarrhoea; secure the essence gate and stop nocturnal emissions/urinary emissions; and secure Chong and Ren meridians and stop uterine bleeding/leucorrhoea.

V012-N01191.label: 固精缩尿止带药
V012-N01191.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01191.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01191.pinyinNames: gù jīng suō niào zhǐ dài yào
V012-N01191.definitions: Medicines that are sour and puckering in flavour and astringent in property. They mainly act to secure semen, astringe urination and stop vaginal discharge. These medicines are mainly indicated for nocturnal emissions/spermatorrhoea, enuresis/profuse urine, or persistent vaginal discharge.

V012-N01192.label: 固表止汗剂
V012-N01192.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01192.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01192.englishNames: Formulas that secure the exterior and stop sweating
V012-N01192.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to astringe and stop sweating. They are indicated for sweating disorders.

V012-N01193.label: 固表止汗药
V012-N01193.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01193.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01193.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour and neutral in property. They mainly act to secure the exterior and stop sweating and are indicated for spontaneous sweating or night sweats.

V012-N01194.label: 增水行舟
V012-N01194.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01194.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01195.label: 复方
V012-N01195.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01195.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01195.definitions: Formulas consisting of two or more set formulas. They are often used for complex conditions.

V012-N01196.label: 外用药
V012-N01196.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01196.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01196.definitions: Medicines used for topical areas to remove toxins, resolve swelling, drain pus, regenerate tissue, heal wounds, kill insects and stop itching. They are indicated for sores, ulcers, carbuncles, scabies, traumatic injuries, snake bites or problems of the five sense organs. The method includes topical plaster, embrocating, fumigation, blow throat, nose and eye drops.

V012-N01197.label: 大方
V012-N01197.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01197.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01197.definitions: Formulas used for combined patterns upon contraction of excessive pathogenic factors. These include the following five aspects: ① drastic potency; ② many ingredients; ③ large doses; ④ take the large amount in one single dose; ⑤ indicated for major, severe disorders of the lower jiao.

V012-N01198.label: 奇方
V012-N01198.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01198.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01198.definitions: Formulas with one single ingredient or odd-numbered ingredients.

V012-N01199.label: 妊娠用药禁忌
V012-N01199.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01199.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01199.pinyinNames: rèn shēn yòng yào jìn jì
V012-N01199.definitions: Medicines that are absolutely contraindicated to use or used with caution during pregnancy. Absolutely contraindicated medicines are toxic, harsh or abortive. Medicines to circulate blood, move qi, and drain downward, and warm, pungent medicines to warm the interior should be used with caution.

V012-N01200.label: 安神剂
V012-N01200.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01200.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01200.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to calm the mind. Indicated for mental restlessness. These formulas are categorized into two types: formulas to calm the mind with heavy medicines; and formulas to tranquilize the mind by nourishing yin and blood.

V012-N01201.label: 安神药
V012-N01201.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01201.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01201.definitions: Medicines to calm the mind. Based on the property and efficacy, these medicines are further categorized into two types: heavyweight medicines to tranquilize the mind; and medicines to nourish the heart mind.

V012-N01202.label: 宣剂
V012-N01202.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01202.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01202.definitions: Formulas that disperse congestion and relieve stagnation.

V012-N01203.label: 寒下剂
V012-N01203.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01203.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01203.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of cold-property ingredients. They are indicated for interior excessive heat accumulation pattern.

V012-N01204.label: 寒凉药
V012-N01204.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01204.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01204.definitions: Medicines to remove or alleviate heat-induced disorders.

V012-N01205.label: 小方
V012-N01205.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01205.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01205.definitions: Formulas used for one pattern alone upon contraction of mild pathogenic factors. These include the following three aspects: ① mild medical conditions; ② indicated for disorders of the upper jiao and take in small amount in frequent intervals; ③ fewer ingredients

V012-N01206.label: 峻下逐水药
V012-N01206.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01206.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01206.definitions: Medicines that are bitter in flavour and cold in property. These medicines often have a drastic effect and can cause severe diarrhoea and relieve retained water. They are indicated for oedema, pleural effusion, ascites, or panting due to phlegm-fluid retention.

V012-N01207.label: 平性药
V012-N01207.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01207.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01207.definitions: Medicines with no obvious property of cold or heat.

V012-N01208.label: 平旦服
V012-N01208.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01208.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01208.englishNames: administrated before breakfast; Take before breakfast
V012-N01208.pinyinNames: ping dan fu; píng dàn fú
V012-N01208.definitions: To take the medicine in the morning on an empty stomach.

V012-N01209.label: 平熄内风剂
V012-N01209.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01209.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01209.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to soothe the liver and extinguish wind. They are indicated for internal stirring of liver wind.

V012-N01210.label: 平肝息风药
V012-N01210.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01210.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01210.definitions: Medicines to soothe the liver and remove wind. They are indicated for hyperactivity of liver yang and internal stirring of liver wind. Based on their functions and indications, these medicines can be further categorized into two subtypes: medicines to soothe liver yang; and medicines to stop liver wind.

V012-N01211.label: 平降肝阳药
V012-N01211.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01211.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01211.definitions: Medicines that are heavy in weight and cold in property. They mainly act to suppress or submerge yang. They are indicated for dizziness, headache or tinnitus due to hyperactivity of liver yang, and a red face and eyes, restlessness, and bitter mouth due to ascending of liver fire.

V012-N01212.label: 开窍剂
V012-N01212.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01212.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01212.definitions: Formulas containing aromatic medicines. They are indicated for loss of consciousness and blockage of orifices. These formulas are categorized into two types: formulas to clear heat; and formulas to warm the interior and open the orifices.

V012-N01213.label: 开窍药
V012-N01213.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01213.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01213.definitions: Medicines that are aromatic and pungent to open the orifices and refresh the mind. They are indicated for mental unconsciousness in block pattern.

V012-N01214.label: 引经报使
V012-N01214.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01214.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01214.definitions: A property that medicines have special role for certain zang–fu organs or meridians and can guide other medicines to reach the affected meridians and body parts.

V012-N01215.label: 引经药
V012-N01215.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01215.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01215.definitions: Medicines with the ability to guide other ingredients of a formula to access a particular channel/meridian.

V012-N01216.label: 归经
V012-N01216.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01216.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01216.definitions: The ability of medicines to access a channel/meridian. Meridian affinity is based on the theories on zang–fu organs and meridians.

V012-N01217.label: 微丸
V012-N01217.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01217.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01217.definitions: Pills with a diameter of less than 2.5 mm.

V012-N01218.label: 急方
V012-N01218.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01218.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01218.definitions: Formulas indicated for emergency or critical conditions. These include the following four aspects: ① critical conditions that require immediate care; ② fast purging efficacy; ③ drastic potency; ④ directed at symptoms in acute conditions, such as Kai Guan San (Lockjaw-Relieving Powder) or Si Ni Tang (Frigid Extremities Decoction).

V012-N01219.label: 息风止痉药
V012-N01219.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01219.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01219.definitions: Medicines that are cold, cool or neutral in property. They mainly act to soothe the liver, stop wind and resolve spasm/convulsions. They are indicated for dizziness, blurred vision, neck rigidity, tremor of limbs, spasms, epilepsy, convulsions, tetanus or opisthotonus due to internal stirring of liver wind.

V012-N01220.label: 扶正解表剂
V012-N01220.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01220.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01220.englishNames: Formulas that reinforce healthy qi and release the exterior
V012-N01220.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to release the exterior and reinforce the body. They are indicated for exterior pattern in individuals with a weak constitution.

V012-N01221.label: 收敛止血药
V012-N01221.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01221.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01221.definitions: Medicines that are astringent and neutral in property. They mainly act to astringe and stop bleeding. These medicines are widely used for all kinds of bleeding.

V012-N01222.label: 收涩药
V012-N01222.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01222.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01222.definitions: Medicines to astringe and secure qi, blood, essence and body fluids. They are indicated for spontaneous sweating, night sweats, nocturnal emissions, enuresis, persistent leucorrhoea, uterine bleeding, chronic diarrhoea and chronic dysentery. Based on the property, efficacy and indications, astringent medicines are further divided into medicines to secure the exterior and stop sweating, medicines to astringe the lung and intestine and medicines to secure semen, astringe the urine and stop leucorrhoea.

V012-N01223.label: 攻下药
V012-N01223.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01223.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01223.definitions: Medicines that are bitter in flavour and cold in property. They have strong effect in promoting bowel movements and clearing fire/heat. These medicines are indicated for constipation due to excess heat and retention of dry faeces.

V012-N01224.label: 攻补兼施剂
V012-N01224.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01224.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01224.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to purge the intestine and tonify the body. They are indicated for interior excess and deficiency of healthy qi.

V012-N01225.label: 敛肺止咳剂
V012-N01225.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01225.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01225.englishNames: Formulas that astringe the lung and stop coughing
V012-N01225.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to astringe the lung. They are indicated for lung deficiency pattern due to chronic coughing.

V012-N01226.label: 敛肺涩肠药
V012-N01226.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01226.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01226.definitions: Medicines that are sour and puckering in flavour and have astringent properties. They mainly act to astringe the lung and intestine, alleviate coughing/panting, and stop diarrhoea. These medicines are indicated for chronic coughing/panting and persistent diarrhoea/dysentery.

V012-N01227.label: 散剂
V012-N01227.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01227.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01227.definitions: A powder-like preparation made from a mixture of one or more herbs.

V012-N01228.label: 文火
V012-N01228.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01228.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01228.aliases: 慢火； 微火； Slow fire
V012-N01228.definitions: A mild and slow fire to maintain the temperature in decocting Chinese medicine after boiling.

V012-N01229.label: 方剂学
V012-N01229.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01229.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01229.definitions: The study of the origin and evolution of formulas, treatment methods, composition principles, compatibility and clinical applications of Chinese herbal formulas.

V012-N01230.label: 方剂类
V012-N01230.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01230.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01231.label: 时方
V012-N01231.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01231.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01231.definitions: Formulas by physicians after Zhang Zhongjing in the Han Dynasty (220 to 202 BC).

V012-N01232.label: 服药食忌
V012-N01232.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01232.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01232.englishNames: dietary incompatibility; Dietary contraindication during medication
V012-N01232.pinyinNames: fu yao shiji; fú yào shí jì
V012-N01232.definitions: Food that should be avoided while taking medication.

V012-N01233.label: 本草
V012-N01233.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01233.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01233.definitions: An ancient term for Chinese medicine, mainly referring to medicinal plants.

V012-N01234.label: 条剂
V012-N01234.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01234.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01234.definitions: An external dosage form of fine medicinal powder mixed with mulberry paper.

V012-N01235.label: 栓剂
V012-N01235.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01235.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01235.definitions: A solid dosage form made from fine medicinal powder and bases. It is inserted into the rectum/vagina, where it dissolves or melts and exerts local or systemic effects.

V012-N01236.label: 止咳平喘药
V012-N01236.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01236.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01236.definitions: Medicines to stop coughing and alleviate panting. These medicines enter the lung meridian and are indicated for coughing and panting.

V012-N01237.label: 止血剂
V012-N01237.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01237.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01237.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of haemostatic drugs. They are indicated for bleeding pattern.

V012-N01238.label: 止血药
V012-N01238.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01238.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01238.definitions: Medicines to stop bleeding. They are indicated for internal or external bleeding. Based on the property and functions, these medicines are further categorized into four types: medicines to cool blood and stop bleeding, medicines to transform stasis and stop bleeding, medicines to astringe and stop bleeding and medicines to warm meridians and stop bleeding.

V012-N01239.label: 武火
V012-N01239.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01239.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01239.aliases: 急火； 紧火； Intense fire, quick fire
V012-N01239.definitions: A strong and quick fire in decocting Chinese medicine.

V012-N01240.label: 气血两清剂
V012-N01240.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01240.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01240.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat in the qi and blood
V012-N01240.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat, cool blood and remove toxins. They are indicated for pestilence heat in both qi and blood.

V012-N01241.label: 气血双补剂
V012-N01241.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01241.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01241.englishNames: Formulas that tonify qi and blood
V012-N01241.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to tonify qi and blood. They are indicated for deficiency of both qi and blood.

V012-N01242.label: 水丸
V012-N01242.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01242.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01242.definitions: Pills made from fine medicinal powder and uses water (alternatively, alcohol, vinegar, honeyed water or medicinal juice) as excipient.

V012-N01243.label: 水制
V012-N01243.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01243.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01243.definitions: A processing method to use water or other liquid auxiliary materials, including washing, bleaching, soaking, refining with water, etc.

V012-N01244.label: 水火共制
V012-N01244.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01244.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01244.englishNames: Processing with both water and fire
V012-N01244.definitions: A processing method to wrap medicines with wet tissue and place into fire or isolate with oil-absorbing sheets.

V012-N01245.label: 水飞
V012-N01245.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01245.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01245.definitions: A method to obtain extremely fine medicinal powder in water.

V012-N01246.label: 汗法
V012-N01246.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01246.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01246.definitions: A method to induce perspiration, facilitate interaction between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence, and disperse lung qi. It helps to expel six exogenous pathogenic factors through sweating and thus releases the exterior.

V012-N01247.label: 汤剂
V012-N01247.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01247.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01247.definitions: A liquid preparation form by boiling or soaking the ingredients in water, and taken after the sediment is removed.

V012-N01248.label: 汤头
V012-N01248.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01248.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01248.aliases: 汤方； 汤头歌诀； Decoction formulas/ mnemonics
V012-N01248.definitions: Oral herbal formulas to be decocted with water. Wang Ang, a Qing dynasty physician compiled a book named Tang Tou Ge Jue (Decoction in Rhymes) to make it easier for learners to memorize.

V012-N01249.label: 汤液
V012-N01249.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01249.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01249.definitions: A liquid made by decocting prepared pieces of Chinese medicines in water.

V012-N01250.label: 沉降药
V012-N01250.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01250.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01250.definitions: Medicines to purge the intestine, resolve water retention, submerge yang and astringe. They tend to go inward and downward.

V012-N01251.label: 治燥剂
V012-N01251.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01251.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01251.definitions: Formulas that act to dispel external dryness with pungent, light-weight ingredients or eliminate internal dryness with sweet, cold and yin-nourishing ingredients. These formulas are subdivided into two types: formulas to dispel external dryness; and formulas to nourish yin and moisten internal dryness.

V012-N01252.label: 治疡剂
V012-N01252.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01252.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01252.englishNames: Formulas that treat sores/ulcers and abscesses
V012-N01252.definitions: Formulas to remove toxins, resolve swelling, expel toxins, regenerate tissues, and heal the wound.

V012-N01253.label: 治风剂
V012-N01253.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01253.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01253.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of wind-removing or wind-extinguishing medicines. They are indicated for external or internal wind.

V012-N01254.label: 治风化痰剂
V012-N01254.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01254.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01254.englishNames: Formulas that treat wind and transform phlegm
V012-N01254.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to remove/extinguish wind and transform phlegm. They are indicated for wind phlegm pattern.

V012-N01255.label: 泄剂
V012-N01255.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01255.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01255.definitions: Formulas that promote bowel movements and unclog the fu organs.

V012-N01256.label: 泡服
V012-N01256.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01256.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01256.englishNames: Taken after being infused in hot water
V012-N01256.definitions: A method to soak medicines in hot water or decoctions for half an hour and then take the medication after removing the dregs.

V012-N01257.label: 泻下剂
V012-N01257.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01257.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01257.definitions: Formulas to promote bowel movements, remove food stagnation in the stomach and intestines, or resolve water retention. They are indicated for excessive interior pattern. These formulas are categorized into four types: formulas to drain downward with cold-property medicines; formulas to drain downward with warm-property medicines; formulas to drain downward with moistening medicines; and formulas to drain downward with water-expelling medicines.

V012-N01258.label: 泻下药
V012-N01258.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01258.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01258.definitions: Medicines to produce bowel movements. They are indicated for constipation or difficulty passing of stools. Based on the property and actions, these medicines are further categorized into three types: draining purgative medicines, moistening purgative medicines and drastic purgative medicines.

V012-N01259.label: 活血祛瘀剂
V012-N01259.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01259.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01259.englishNames: Formulas that circulate blood and eliminate stasis
V012-N01259.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of blood-circulating medicines. They are indicated for blood stasis pattern.

V012-N01260.label: 活血祛瘀药
V012-N01260.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01260.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01260.definitions: Medicines to circulate blood and resolve stasis. They are indicated for blood stasis. Based on the functions and clinical application, these medicines are further categorized into four types: medicines to circulate blood and stop pain; medicines to circulate blood and resolve stasis; medicines to circulate blood and heal the wound; and medicines to break blood and resolve masses.

V012-N01261.label: 流浸膏
V012-N01261.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01261.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01261.definitions: A semi-liquid medicated preparation containing the active constituents with appropriate solvents.

V012-N01262.label: 浓缩丸
V012-N01262.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01262.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01262.definitions: Pills made from concentrated paste of medicinal decoction coupled with other medicinal powder through drying, grinding and mixing with water, honey or medicinal juice.

V012-N01263.label: 浸膏
V012-N01263.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
