V012-N01263.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01263.definitions: A concentrated medicated preparation obtained by extracting the active constituents with appropriate solvents.

V012-N01264.label: 消导化积剂
V012-N01264.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01264.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01264.englishNames: Formulas that promote digestion and transform masses
V012-N01264.definitions: Formulas to promote digestion. They are indicated for food stagnation from different causes. These formulas are subdivided into two types: formulas to promote digestion to resolve stagnation; and formulas to strengthen the spleen to promote digestion.

V012-N01265.label: 消法
V012-N01265.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01265.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01265.definitions: A method to promote digestion, circulate qi, move blood, transform phlegm, drain water retention and kill parasites.

V012-N01266.label: 消痞化积剂
V012-N01266.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01266.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01266.englishNames: Formulas that disperse and transform masses
V012-N01266.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to circulate qi, transform stasis, soften hardness and eliminate masses. They are indicated for stuffiness, fullness, stagnation or paediatric malnutrition due to food stagnation damaging the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01267.label: 消食导滞剂
V012-N01267.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01267.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01267.englishNames: Formulas that promote digestion and unblock stagnation
V012-N01267.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to promote digestion. They are indicated for poor digestion due to food stagnation.

V012-N01268.label: 消食药
V012-N01268.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01268.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01268.definitions: Medicines to promote digestion and transform food stagnation. They are indicated for disorders due to food stagnation.

V012-N01269.label: 涌吐剂
V012-N01269.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01269.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01269.definitions: Formulas to induce vomiting, expectorate phlegm, undigested food and poison in the stomach. They are indicated for phlegm syncope, food stagnation and food poisoning.

V012-N01270.label: 涌吐药
V012-N01270.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01270.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01270.definitions: Medicines to produce nausea and vomiting. They are indicated for food poisoning, indigestion and phlegm stagnation in the stomach or above the chest.

V012-N01271.label: 润
V012-N01271.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01271.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01271.definitions: A method to moisten and soften medicines with water or other liquids before cutting, including water spraying, washing, soaking, infiltrating, drying, etc.

V012-N01272.label: 润下剂
V012-N01272.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01272.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01272.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to nourish yin and purge the intestine. They are indicated for constipation due to intestinal dryness.

V012-N01273.label: 润下药
V012-N01273.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01273.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01273.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour and oily in quality. They mainly act to moisten the intestine, soften faeces and promote bowel movements. They are indicated for constipation due to exhaustion of fluids, yin deficiency or blood deficiency resulting from ageing, a weak constitution, chronic disease and childbirth.

V012-N01274.label: 润燥化痰剂
V012-N01274.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01274.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01274.englishNames: Formulas that moisten dryness and transform phlegm
V012-N01274.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to moisten the lung and transform phlegm. They are indicated for dry phlegm pattern.

V012-N01275.label: 涩剂
V012-N01275.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01275.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01275.definitions: Formulas that prevent qi, blood, essence and body fluids from exhaustion and collapse.

V012-N01276.label: 涩味
V012-N01276.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01276.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01276.definitions: Medicines with an astringent flavour act to astringe and secure. They are indicated for sweating (due to deficiency), diarrhoea, frequent urination, enuresis, bleeding, etc.

V012-N01277.label: 涩精止遗剂
V012-N01277.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01277.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01277.englishNames: Formulas that astringe the semen and stop enuresis
V012-N01277.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to secure the semen and astringe urination. They are indicated for nocturnal emissions or enuresis.

V012-N01278.label: 涩肠固脱剂
V012-N01278.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01278.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01278.englishNames: Formulas that astringe the intestine and secure collapse
V012-N01278.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to astringe the intestines and secure collapse. They are indicated for faecal incontinence or rectal prolapse.

V012-N01279.label: 淡味
V012-N01279.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01279.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01279.definitions: Medicines with a bland flavour act to drain dampness and promote urination. They are indicated for oedema, dysuria and leg swelling.

V012-N01280.label: 淬
V012-N01280.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01280.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01280.definitions: A method to calcine medicines and place into cold water or liquid excipient quickly for easy crushing and absorption.

V012-N01281.label: 清化热痰药
V012-N01281.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01281.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01281.aliases: Medicines that clear heat and transform heaty phlegm
V012-N01281.definitions: Medicines that are cold or cool in property. They mainly act to clear heat and transform phlegm. These medicines are indicated for disorders due to heat phlegm or dry phlegm.

V012-N01282.label: 清暑益气剂
V012-N01282.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01282.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01282.englishNames: Formulas that clear summer heat and supplement qi
V012-N01282.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear summer heat, supplement qi, and nourish yin. They are indicated for summer heat damaging qi and bodily fluids.

V012-N01283.label: 清气分热剂
V012-N01283.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01283.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01283.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat in the qi phase
V012-N01283.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat. They are indicated for heat in the qi phase.

V012-N01284.label: 清法
V012-N01284.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01284.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01284.definitions: A method to clear heat, reduce fire, remove toxins and cool blood to eliminate pathogenic heat in the interior.

V012-N01285.label: 清炒
V012-N01285.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01285.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01285.definitions: A method to fry without adjuvants. According to the temperature, plain stir-frying is divided into fried yellow, scorch fry and charcoaled fry.

V012-N01286.label: 清热凉血药
V012-N01286.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01286.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01286.definitions: Medicines that are sweet, bitter or salty in flavour and cold in property. They are mainly indicated for excess heat in the Ying nutrients and blood phases. Some medicines can also nourish yin and engender fluids. These medicines can be used for yin deficiency causing damage to fluids.

V012-N01287.label: 清热剂
V012-N01287.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01287.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01287.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of heat-clearing medicines. They act to clear fire/heat, cool blood and remove toxins. These formulas are categorized into six types: formulas to clear heat in the qi phase; formulas that clear heat in the Ying nutrients phase and cool blood; formulas to clear heat in both qi and blood; formulas to clear heat and remove toxins; formulas to clear heat in Zang–fu organs; and formulas to clear deficiency heat.

V012-N01288.label: 清热化痰剂
V012-N01288.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01288.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01288.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat and transform phlegm
V012-N01288.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and transform phlegm. They are indicated for heat phlegm pattern.

V012-N01289.label: 清热泻火药
V012-N01289.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01289.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01289.definitions: Medicines that are bitter or sweet in flavour and cold in nature. They are mainly indicated for excess heat in the qi phase and fire heat in the zang–fu organs, including lung heat, stomach fire, heart fire or liver fire.

V012-N01290.label: 清热燥湿药
V012-N01290.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01290.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01290.definitions: Medicines that are bitter in flavour and cold in property. They mainly act to clear heat and dry dampness and are indicated for damp heat pattern.

V012-N01291.label: 清热祛湿剂
V012-N01291.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01291.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01291.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat and eliminate dampness
V012-N01291.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and resolve dampness. They are indicated for damp heat pattern.

V012-N01292.label: 清热药
V012-N01292.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01292.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01292.definitions: Medicines to clear interior heat. They are indicated for interior heat pattern. Based on the property and actions, these medicines are further categorized into five types: medicines to clear heat/fire, medicines to clear heat and dry dampness, medicines to clear heat and cool blood, and medicines to clear deficiency heat.

V012-N01293.label: 清热解毒剂
V012-N01293.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01293.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01293.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat and remove toxins
V012-N01293.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and remove toxins. They are indicated for pestilence, pathogenic warmth and toxic heat pattern.

V012-N01294.label: 清热解毒药
V012-N01294.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01294.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01294.definitions: Medicines that are bitter in flavour and cold in property. They mainly act to clear heat and remove toxins and are therefore indicated for conditions due to toxic heat. Some medicines can also be used for snake bite or cancer.

V012-N01295.label: 清脏腑热剂
V012-N01295.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01295.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01295.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat in the zang-fu organs
V012-N01295.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear internal heat. They are indicated for interior heat pattern.

V012-N01296.label: 清营凉血剂
V012-N01296.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01296.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01296.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat in the Ying nutrients and blood
V012-N01296.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and cool blood. They are indicated for heat entering the Ying nutrients and blood phases.

V012-N01297.label: 清虚热剂
V012-N01297.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01297.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01297.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear deficiency heat. They are indicated for deficiency heat.

V012-N01298.label: 清虚热药
V012-N01298.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01298.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01298.definitions: Medicine that are cold/cool in property. They mainly act to clear deficiency heat and alleviate bone-steaming sensation. They are indicated for internal heat due to yin deficiency.

V012-N01299.label: 温下剂
V012-N01299.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01299.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01299.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of warm, pungent medicines to warm yang and dissipate cold, coupled with bitter, cold medicines to purge pathogenic factors. They are indicated for excessive interior cold pattern.

V012-N01300.label: 温中祛寒剂
V012-N01300.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01300.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01300.englishNames: Formulas that warm the middle jiao and remove cold
V012-N01300.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to warm the interior and remove cold. They are indicated for deficiency cold of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01301.label: 温化寒痰剂
V012-N01301.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01301.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01301.englishNames: Formulas that warm and transform cold phlegm
V012-N01301.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to warm and transform cold phlegm. They are indicated for cold phlegm pattern.

V012-N01302.label: 温化寒痰药
V012-N01302.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01302.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01302.aliases: Medicines that warm and transform cold phlegm
V012-N01302.definitions: Medicines that are pungent and bitter in flavour and warm and dry in property. They mainly act to warm the lung, eliminate cold, dry dampness and transform phlegm. These medicines are indicated for disorders due to cold phlegm or damp phlegm.

V012-N01303.label: 温化水湿剂
V012-N01303.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01303.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01303.englishNames: Formulas that warm and transform water-dampness
V012-N01303.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to warm yang and transform water and dampness. They are indicated for cold dampness pattern.

V012-N01304.label: 温开剂
V012-N01304.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01304.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01304.englishNames: Formulas that open the orifices with warm-property ingredients
V012-N01304.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to dispel cold and open the orifices. They are indicated for cold blockage pattern: cold, dampness and turbid phlegm blocking the mind or filthy turbidity obstructing the flow of qi.

V012-N01305.label: 温服
V012-N01305.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01305.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01305.definitions: A method to take the decoction when it is neither cold nor hot.

V012-N01306.label: 温法
V012-N01306.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01306.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01306.definitions: A method to warm the interior to eliminate pathogenic cold.

V012-N01307.label: 温热药
V012-N01307.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01307.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01307.definitions: Medicines to remove or alleviate cold-induced disorders.

V012-N01308.label: 温经散寒剂
V012-N01308.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01308.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01308.englishNames: Formulas that warm meridians and dispel cold
V012-N01308.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to warm and unblock meridians and remove cold. They are indicated for joint pain due to cold retaining in meridians.

V012-N01309.label: 温经止血药
V012-N01309.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01309.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01309.definitions: Medicines that are warm/heat in property. They mainly act to warm the interior, dissipate cold, warm meridians and stop bleeding. These medicines are indicated for bleeding due to deficiency cold.

V012-N01310.label: 温里剂
V012-N01310.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01310.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01310.definitions: Formulas to warm the interior, supplement yang, dispel cold, and unblock blood vessels. They are indicated for interior cold pattern. These formulas are categorized into three types: formulas to warm the middle jiao and remove cold; formulas to resuscitate yang to prevent collapse; and formulas to warm meridians and dispel cold.

V012-N01311.label: 温里药
V012-N01311.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01311.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01311.definitions: Medicines to warm the interior and dissipate cold. They are indicated for interior cold pattern.

V012-N01312.label: 湿剂
V012-N01312.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01312.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01312.definitions: Formulas that moisten or nourish the body.

V012-N01313.label: 滋养安神剂
V012-N01313.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01313.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01313.englishNames: Formulas that calm and nourish the mind
V012-N01313.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to nourish and calm the mind. They are indicated for yin blood failing to nourish the heart mind.

V012-N01314.label: 滋阴润燥剂
V012-N01314.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01314.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01314.englishNames: Formulas that nourish yin and moisten dryness
V012-N01314.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines (sweet in flavour and cool in property) to moisten dryness. They are indicated for internal dryness pattern.

V012-N01315.label: 滑剂
V012-N01315.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01315.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01316.label: 漂
V012-N01316.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01316.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01316.definitions: A method to soak medicines in static or running water for a period of time and exchange water regularly to remove the fishy smell, salt or toxic ingredients.

V012-N01317.label: 火制
V012-N01317.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01317.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01317.definitions: A processing method to use heat or fire, including frying, roasting, calcining, etc.

V012-N01318.label: 火郁发之
V012-N01318.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01318.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01318.definitions: Formulas to expel stagnant heat/fire through dispersing, ascending and unblocking using light medicines. Representative formulas are Pu Ji Xiao Du Yin (Universal Relief Toxin-Removing Beverage) and Qing Wei San (Stomach-Clearing Powder).

V012-N01319.label: 灸剂
V012-N01319.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01319.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01319.definitions: External dosage form of moxa wool or moxa sticks to apply to certain body parts or acupuncture points.

V012-N01320.label: 炒
V012-N01320.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01320.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01320.definitions: A method to stir fry medicines in a pot to a certain temperature degree, including plain frying or frying with adjuvants.

V012-N01321.label: 炙
V012-N01321.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01321.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01321.definitions: A method to use liquid adjuvants to stir-fry medicines to change the medicinal property, reinforce the efficacy and reduce side-effects. The excipients include wine, vinegar, salt, ginger, honey or oil.

V012-N01322.label: 炮制
V012-N01322.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01322.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01322.definitions: A sequence of medicinal processing techniques to make materia medica into prepared pieces or different dosage forms. These techniques are adopted according to theories of Chinese medicine and medicinal properties.

V012-N01323.label: 热服
V012-N01323.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01323.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01323.definitions: A method to take the decoction when it is hot.

V012-N01324.label: 煅
V012-N01324.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01324.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01324.definitions: A method to calcine medicines directly or indirectly with a strong fire to fully extract the active ingredients and achieve maximal efficacy.

V012-N01325.label: 煎汤代水
V012-N01325.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01325.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01325.definitions: A method to decoct some ingredients separately and then use the decoction instead of water to decoct the rest of the ingredients. This method has two purposes: 1) to prevent the separately decocted ingredients causing the decoction to appear cloudy or muddy; 2) applies to ingredients that are light in weight, big in dosage and volume, and strong in water absorption.

V012-N01326.label: 煎膏
V012-N01326.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01326.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01326.aliases: 膏滋； 膏方； Oral thick paste
V012-N01326.definitions: A semi-liquid form made from condensed medicinal decoction and processed honey/sugar.

V012-N01327.label: 煎药法
V012-N01327.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01327.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01327.definitions: The decoction of Chinese medicine involves the pot, water, heat control, and decocting method.

V012-N01328.label: 煎药火候
V012-N01328.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01328.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01329.label: 煨
V012-N01329.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01329.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01329.definitions: A method to wrap medicines with water-mixed flour or wet paper, placed in the heat of the ash, or heated with oil-absorbent paper separated from the drug barrier so that the medicine is turned from raw to cooked.

V012-N01330.label: 煮
V012-N01330.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01330.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01330.definitions: A method to heat medicines with water or liquid excipients.

V012-N01331.label: 燀
V012-N01331.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01331.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01331.definitions: A method to blanch in boiling water and take out immediately for drying process.

V012-N01332.label: 燥剂
V012-N01332.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01332.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01333.label: 燥湿化痰剂
V012-N01333.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01333.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01333.englishNames: Formulas that dry dampness and transform phlegm
V012-N01333.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to dry dampness and transform phlegm. They are indicated for damp phlegm pattern.

V012-N01334.label: 燥湿和胃剂
V012-N01334.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01334.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01334.englishNames: Formulas that dry dampness and harmonize the stomach
V012-N01334.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to dry dampness and harmonize the stomach. They are indicated for dampness affecting the spleen and stomach or disharmony of stomach qi.

V012-N01335.label: 理气剂
V012-N01335.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01335.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01335.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to move or down-regulate qi. They are indicated for qi stagnation or adverse ascending of qi. These formulas are categorized into two types: formulas to circulate qi; and formulas to down-regulate qi.

V012-N01336.label: 理气药
V012-N01336.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01336.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01336.definitions: Medicines to regulate qi activity. They are indicated for qi stagnation and adverse flow of qi.

V012-N01337.label: 理血剂
V012-N01337.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01337.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01337.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of blood-regulating medicines. They are indicated for blood stasis or haemorrhage. These formulas are subdivided into two types: formulas to circulate blood and resolve stasis; and formulas to stop bleeding.

V012-N01338.label: 甘味
V012-N01338.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01338.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01338.definitions: Medicines with a sweet flavour act to tonify, harmonize the middle jiao and relieve spasm. They are indicated for deficiency pattern, disharmony between the spleen and stomach, spasm, pain, etc.

V012-N01339.label: 甘寒清热
V012-N01339.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01339.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01339.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat with sweet, cold medicines
V012-N01339.definitions: Formulas that are indicated for exuberant heat damaging bodily fluids. They mainly consist of sweet and cold medicines: the sweet ones to nourish yin and regenerate fluids, and the cold/cool ones to clear heat. A representative formula is Wu Zhi Yin (Five-Juice Beverage).

V012-N01340.label: 甘温除热
V012-N01340.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01340.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01340.englishNames: Formulas that reduce fever with sweet, warm medicines
V012-N01340.definitions: Formulas consisting of sweet, warm medicines. They are indicated for fever due to qi deficiency or yang deficiency. Representative formulas are Xiao Jian Zhong Tang (Minor Middle Jiao-Fortifying Decoction) and Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang (Middle Jiao-Supplementing and Qi-Boosting Decoction).

V012-N01341.label: 疏散外风剂
V012-N01341.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01341.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01341.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of pungent medicines to remove external wind. They are indicated for external wind pattern.

V012-N01342.label: 相使
V012-N01342.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01342.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01342.definitions: Use two or more medicines sharing common efficacies in combination, one being the principal medicine while the others play a subsidiary role to reinforce the action of the principal one.

V012-N01343.label: 相反
V012-N01343.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01343.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01343.definitions: When two medicines are used in combination, their toxic or side-effects can be produced or enhanced.

V012-N01344.label: 相恶
V012-N01344.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01344.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01344.definitions: When two medicines are used in combination, the positive effects of one medicine can be reduced or eliminated by the other.

V012-N01345.label: 相杀
V012-N01345.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01345.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01345.definitions: When two medicines are used in combination, one medicine can reduce or eliminate the toxic or side-effects of the other.

V012-N01346.label: 相畏
V012-N01346.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01346.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01346.definitions: When two medicines are used in combination, the toxic or side-effects of one medicine can be reduced or eliminated by the other.

V012-N01347.label: 相须
V012-N01347.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01347.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01347.definitions: Use two medicines sharing similar properties in combination to reinforce each other’s action.

V012-N01348.label: 硬膏
V012-N01348.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01348.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01348.aliases: 膏药； 薄贴； Medicated plaster； Thin plaster
V012-N01348.definitions: A solid medicated dressing for external application. Often consists of medicines, edible vegetable oil and red lead.

V012-N01349.label: 祛暑利湿剂
V012-N01349.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01349.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01349.englishNames: Formulas that clear summer heat and resolve dampness
V012-N01349.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear summer heat and resolve dampness. They are indicated for summer heat and dampness or downward flow of summer heat and dampness.

V012-N01350.label: 祛暑剂
V012-N01350.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01350.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01350.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear summer heat. They are indicated for summer heat-related problems. These formulas are categorized into four types: formulas to clear summer heat; formulas to clear summer heat and release the exterior; formulas to clear summer heat and resolve dampness; and formulas to clear summer heat and benefit qi.

V012-N01351.label: 祛暑清热剂
V012-N01351.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01351.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01351.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear summer heat. They are indicated for summer heat pattern.

V012-N01352.label: 祛暑解表剂
V012-N01352.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01352.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01352.englishNames: Formulas that clear summer heat and release the exterior
V012-N01352.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear summer heat, transform dampness or release the exterior. They are indicated for exposure to wind heat and summer heat damaging the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01353.label: 祛湿剂
V012-N01353.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01353.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01353.definitions: Formulas that act to resolve water retention and relieve stranguria are indicated for water dampness pattern. These formulas are categorized into five types: formulas to dry dampness and harmonize the stomach; formulas to clear heat and eliminate dampness; formulas to promote urination and drain dampness; formulas to warm and transform cold dampness; and formulas to remove wind and transform dampness.

V012-N01354.label: 祛痰剂
V012-N01354.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01354.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01354.definitions: Formulas that are indicated for phlegm from all causes. These formulas are subdivided into five types: formulas to dry dampness and transform phlegm; formulas to clear heat and transform phlegm; formulas to moisten dryness and transform phlegm; formulas to warm and transform phlegm; and formulas to stop wind and transform phlegm.

V012-N01355.label: 祛风湿药
V012-N01355.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01355.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01355.definitions: Medicines to eliminate wind, resolve dampness and alleviate pain. They are indicated for bi-impediment patterns. Based on the bi-impediment category and medicinal property, these medicines are further categorized into three types: medicines to remove wind and resolve dampness; medicines to remove wind, clear heat and resolve dampness; and medicines to remove wind, resolve dampness and strengthen tendons and bones.

V012-N01356.label: 祛风胜湿剂
V012-N01356.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01356.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01356.englishNames: Formulas that remove wind and transform dampness
V012-N01356.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to remove wind and transform dampness. They are mainly indicated for wind dampness pattern.

V012-N01357.label: 秘方
V012-N01357.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01357.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01358.label: 粉碎处理
V012-N01358.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01358.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01358.definitions: A method to grind medicines by pounding, grinding, filing and crushing.

V012-N01359.label: 糊丸
V012-N01359.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01359.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01359.definitions: Pills made from fine medicinal powder and uses rice paste, flour paste or medicated leaven paste as the excipient.

V012-N01360.label: 纯净处理
V012-N01360.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01360.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01360.definitions: A method to remove dust, impurities and clean medicines by picking, selecting, sieving, scraping and brushing.

V012-N01361.label: 线剂
V012-N01361.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01361.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01361.definitions: A dried external dosage form of soaked and decocted silk or cotton thread.

V012-N01362.label: 经方
V012-N01362.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01362.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01362.definitions: Formulas recorded in medical books before the Han dynasty, represented by Zhang Zhongjing.

V012-N01363.label: 缓方
V012-N01363.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01363.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01363.definitions: Formulas indicated for chronic conditions with a weak constitution. These include the following six aspects: ① many mutually-counterbalanced ingredients; ② use non-toxic ingredients to alleviate symptoms but do not cause damage to the anti-pathogenic qi; ③ use slow-acting ingredients; ⑤ use pills to remove pathogenic factors gradually; ⑥ use demulcent medicines to boost the immune system to help recovery.

V012-N01364.label: 膏剂
V012-N01364.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01364.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01364.definitions: A medicated paste is made from decocted medicines with water or vegetable oil. It can be further categorized into fluid extract, extractum, ointment and plaster.

V012-N01365.label: 臣药
V012-N01365.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01365.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01365.definitions: ① Ingredient(s) to help strengthen the action of the monarch medicines on main pattern or primary symptoms; ②ingredient(s) in a formula that provide a principal curative action for associated conditions or patterns.

V012-N01366.label: 苦味
V012-N01366.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01366.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01366.definitions: Medicines with a bitter flavour act to unblock, purge the intestine, dry dampness, clear fire and benefit yin. They are indicated for fire heat, dampness, adverse flow of qi and fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N01367.label: 苦寒直折
V012-N01367.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01367.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01367.englishNames: Formulas that clear exuberant heat with bitter and cold medicines
V012-N01367.definitions: Formulas that are indicated for exuberant interior heat. These formulas mainly consist of bitter, cold medicines: the bitter ones to clear fire and the cold ones to clear heat. A representative formula is Huang Lian Jie Du Tang (Coptis Toxin-Resolving Decoction).

V012-N01368.label: 茶剂
V012-N01368.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01368.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01368.definitions: Preparations made from the infusion or decoction of herbs or herbal extracts.

V012-N01369.label: 草药
V012-N01369.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01369.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01369.definitions: Regional herbal medicines that may not be recorded in major books on materia medica; however, they are an integral part of Chinese medicine.

V012-N01370.label: 药物偏性
V012-N01370.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01370.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01370.definitions: Peculiar features and actions of medicines.

V012-N01371.label: 蒸
V012-N01371.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01371.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01371.definitions: A method to steam or heat with water.

V012-N01372.label: 蜜丸
V012-N01372.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01372.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01372.definitions: Pills made from fine medicinal powder and uses honey as the excipient.

V012-N01373.label: 蜡丸
V012-N01373.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01373.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01373.definitions: Pills made from fine medicinal powder and uses beeswax as the excipient.

V012-N01374.label: 行气剂
V012-N01374.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01374.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01374.definitions: Formulas that act to unblock qi activities and are often indicated for liver or spleen/stomach qi stagnation.

V012-N01375.label: 补剂
V012-N01375.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01375.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01375.definitions: Formulas that nourish and tonify qi, blood, yin and yang of the five zang organs.

V012-N01376.label: 补气剂
V012-N01376.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01376.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01376.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of qi-tonifying medicines. They are indicated for qi deficiency pattern.

V012-N01377.label: 补气药
V012-N01377.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01377.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01377.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour and warm or neutral in property. They mainly act to tonify qi and are indicated for qi deficiency.

V012-N01378.label: 补法
V012-N01378.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01378.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01378.definitions: A method to reinforce qi, blood, yin and yang of the body. Indicated for deficiency of zang–fu organs, qi, blood, yin and yang.

V012-N01379.label: 补益剂
V012-N01379.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01379.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01379.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of tonic medicines. They are indicated for deficiency of qi, blood and yin and yang. These formulas are further categorized into six types: formulas to tonify qi; formulas to tonify blood; formulas to tonify qi and blood; formulas to nourish yin; formulas to tonify yang; and formulas to tonify yin and yang.

V012-N01380.label: 补虚药
V012-N01380.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01380.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01380.definitions: Medicines to reinforce the body and prevent against diseases. They are indicated for deficiency pattern. Based on the property, functions and indications, these medicines are categorized into medicines to tonify qi, medicines to tonify yang, medicines to tonify blood and medicines to nourish yin.

V012-N01381.label: 补血剂
V012-N01381.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01381.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01381.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of blood-tonifying medicines. They are indicated for blood deficiency pattern.

V012-N01382.label: 补血药
V012-N01382.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01382.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01382.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour and warm in property. They mainly act to tonify blood and are indicated for blood deficiency.

V012-N01383.label: 补阳剂
V012-N01383.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01383.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01384.label: 补阳药
V012-N01384.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01384.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01384.definitions: Medicines that are sweet and pungent in flavour and warm in property. They mainly act to tonify yang qi and are indicated for yang deficiency.

V012-N01385.label: 补阴剂
V012-N01385.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01385.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01385.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of yin-nourishing medicines. They are indicated for yin deficiency pattern.

V012-N01386.label: 补阴药
V012-N01386.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01386.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01386.definitions: Medicines that are sweet in flavour and cold in property. They mainly act to nourish yin fluids and are indicated for yin deficiency (insufficiency of fluids).

V012-N01387.label: 表里双解剂
V012-N01387.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01387.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01387.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior and interior
V012-N01387.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to release the exterior, warm the interior, and reinforce the body. They are indicated for pattern involving both the exterior and interior. These formulas are further categorized into three subtypes: exterior-releasing and interior-clearing formulas; exterior-releasing and interior-warming formulas; and exterior-releasing and interior-attacking formulas.

V012-N01388.label: 解表剂
V012-N01388.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01388.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01388.definitions: Formulas to induce sweating, relieve muscle soreness, and/or promote the eruption of measles. They are indicated for exterior pattern. These formulas are categorized into three types—formulas to release the exterior with pungent, warm medicines, formulas to release the exterior with pungent, cool medicines and formulas to reinforce healthy qi and release the exterior.

V012-N01389.label: 解表攻里剂
V012-N01389.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01389.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01389.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior and purge the interior
V012-N01389.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of exterior-releasing medicines and purgative medicines. They are indicated for retention of exogenous pathogens coupled with interior excess pattern.

V012-N01390.label: 解表清里剂
V012-N01390.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01390.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01390.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior and clear the interior
V012-N01390.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to release the exterior and clear heat. They are indicated for retention of exogenous pathogens coupled with interior heat.

V012-N01391.label: 解表温里剂
V012-N01391.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01391.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01391.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior and warm the interior
V012-N01391.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to release the exterior and warm the interior. They are indicated for retention of exogenous pathogens coupled with interior cold.

V012-N01392.label: 解表药
V012-N01392.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01392.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01392.definitions: Medicines to release the exterior. They are indicated for exterior pattern. Based on the property and actions, these medicines are further categorized into two types: medicines to remove wind and dissipate cold and medicines to remove wind and clear heat.

V012-N01393.label: 证候用药禁忌
V012-N01393.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01393.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01393.pinyinNames: zhèng hòu yòng yào jìn jì
V012-N01393.definitions: Medicines that may aggravate medical conditions should be avoided.

V012-N01394.label: 调和肝脾剂
V012-N01394.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01394.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01394.englishNames: Formulas that regulate and harmonize the liver and spleen
V012-N01394.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to soothe and soften the liver, regulate qi, nourish blood and strengthen the spleen. They are indicated for disharmony between the liver and spleen.

V012-N01395.label: 调和肠胃剂
V012-N01395.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01395.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01395.englishNames: Formulas that regulate and harmonize the stomach and intestines
V012-N01395.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of pungent warm and bitter, cold medicines to disperse and down-regulate qi. They are indicated for disharmony between the stomach and intestines.

V012-N01396.label: 调和药
V012-N01396.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01396.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01396.definitions: The guide ingredient(s) to coordinate the rest of the ingredients in a formula.

V012-N01397.label: 软膏
V012-N01397.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01397.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01397.definitions: A viscous, semi-solid preparation for external application. It is made from fine medicinal powder and ointment bases.

V012-N01398.label: 轻剂
V012-N01398.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01398.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01398.definitions: Formulas that remove pathogenic factors out of the exterior.

V012-N01399.label: 轻宣润燥剂
V012-N01399.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01399.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01399.englishNames: Formulas that moisten dryness with light diffusing medicines
V012-N01399.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of pungent medicines light in weight and dispersing in function. They are indicated for external dryness pattern.

V012-N01400.label: 辅料
V012-N01400.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01400.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01400.definitions: These include liquid materials such as alcohol, vinegar or honey and non-liquid materials such as starch, sugar or salt. The stir-fry method involves medicines and solid materials. These include stir-frying with bran, rice, soil, sand, talcum powder or clamshell powder.

V012-N01401.label: 辛凉解表剂
V012-N01401.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01401.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01401.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior with pungent cool medicines
V012-N01401.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of pungent, cool and dispersing medicines. They are indicated for exterior wind heat pattern.

V012-N01402.label: 辛凉解表药
V012-N01402.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01402.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01402.aliases: 发散风热药； Wind-heat dissipating medicines
V012-N01402.definitions: Medicines that are pungent in flavour and cool in property. They mainly act to remove wind and clear heat and are indicated for wind heat exterior pattern. Some medicines can also clear the head/eyes, benefit the throat, promote skin eruption and stop coughing. These medicines can be used for wind heat-induced red, swollen and painful eyes, red, swollen throat, inhibited skin eruption, and cough due to wind heat.

V012-N01403.label: 辛味
V012-N01403.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01403.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01403.definitions: Medicines with a pungent flavour act to disperse/circulate qi and move blood. They are indicated for exterior pattern, qi stagnation and blood stasis.

V012-N01404.label: 辛开苦降
V012-N01404.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01404.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01404.definitions: The combination of a pungent and bitter flavour allows for dispersal of obstruction and the descent of qi. This includes a single medicine with both flavours or combined pungent and bitter medicines.

V012-N01405.label: 辛温解表剂
V012-N01405.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01405.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01405.englishNames: Formulas that release the exterior with pungent warm medicines
V012-N01405.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of pungent, warm and dispersing medicines. They are indicated for wind cold exterior pattern.

V012-N01406.label: 辛温解表药
V012-N01406.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01406.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01406.aliases: 发散风寒药； Wind-cold dissipating medicines
V012-N01406.definitions: Medicines that are pungent in flavour and warm in property. They mainly act to remove wind and dissipate cold and are indicated for wind cold exterior pattern. Some medicines can also remove wind, alleviate itching, relieve pain, stop coughing, promote urination to resolve oedema, and heal sores/ulcers. These medicines can be used for wind cold-induced itchy rashes, cough, oedema, sores/ulcers, and bi-impediment due to wind and dampness.

V012-N01407.label: 辛甘化阳
V012-N01407.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01407.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01407.englishNames: Pungent and sweet transform into yang
V012-N01407.definitions: The combination of a pungent and sweet flavour engenders a dispersing, outward and yang-assisting function. This includes a single medicine with both flavours or combined pungent and sweet medicines.

V012-N01408.label: 透热转气
V012-N01408.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01408.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01408.englishNames: Formulas that clear heat in the Ying nutrients phase
V012-N01408.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to clear heat and remove toxins, coupled with medicines to clear heat in the qi phase. They are indicated for the early stage of heat entering the Ying nutrients phase in febrile diseases. A representative formula is Qing Ying Tang (Ying Phase Clearing Decoction).

V012-N01409.label: 逐水剂
V012-N01409.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01409.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01409.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of purgative medicines to resolve water retention. They are indicated for interior water retention.

V012-N01410.label: 通剂
V012-N01410.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01410.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01410.definitions: Formulas that move qi and circulate blood.

V012-N01411.label: 道地药材
V012-N01411.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01411.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01411.definitions: High-quality Chinese medicines produced in specific geographical areas.

V012-N01412.label: 配伍
V012-N01412.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01412.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01412.definitions: The combination, according to Chinese medicine theory, of two or more medicines to achieve a pre-determined clinical outcome.

V012-N01413.label: 配伍禁忌
V012-N01413.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01413.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01413.definitions: Combination of some medicines can reduce the curative effect, produce or enhance toxic or side-effects and/or affect the safety of medicines. These combinations should be avoided. Specifically, there are eighteen antagonisms and nineteen incompatibilities.

V012-N01414.label: 配方颗粒
V012-N01414.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01414.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01414.definitions: A new dosage form with standardized specification, dosage and quality manufactured through extracting, separating, condensing, drying, granulating or packaging.

V012-N01415.label: 酒剂
V012-N01415.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01415.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01415.definitions: A liquid form of herbal extracts prepared with distilled liquour.

V012-N01416.label: 酸味
V012-N01416.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01416.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01416.definitions: Medicines with a sour flavour act to astringe and secure. They are indicated for sweating (due to deficiency), diarrhoea, frequent urination, enuresis, bleeding, etc. Some sour-flavoured medicines can generate fluids, calm worms and stop pain.

V012-N01417.label: 酸甘化阴
V012-N01417.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01417.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01417.englishNames: Sour and sweet transform into yin
V012-N01417.definitions: The combination of a sour and sweet flavour engenders a fluid-generating, inward and astringing function. This includes a single medicinal with both flavours or combined sour and sweet medicines.

V012-N01418.label: 重剂
V012-N01418.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01418.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01418.definitions: Formulas that tranquilize and calm the mind.

V012-N01419.label: 重镇安神剂
V012-N01419.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01419.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01419.englishNames: Formulas that calm and settle the mind with heavy medicines
V012-N01419.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines (heavy in weight) to calm the mind. They are indicated for hyperactivity of heart yang and heat disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N01420.label: 重镇安神药
V012-N01420.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01420.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01420.definitions: Medicines (mostly minerals, fossil bones or shells) heavy in weight and downbearing in property. These medicines are indicated for excess mental uneasiness including palpitations, insomnia, convulsions, epilepsy or mania.

V012-N01421.label: 釜底抽薪
V012-N01421.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01421.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01421.englishNames: Formulas that remove firewood from under the cauldron
V012-N01421.definitions: “Take away firewood from under the cauldron” is a metaphorical expression for the method of discharging heat with purgation. These formulas act to purge excess heat. A representative formula is Da Cheng Qi Tang (Major Purgative Decoction).

V012-N01422.label: 锭剂
V012-N01422.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01422.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01422.definitions: A solid form (shaped like a spindle, cylinder or bar) of fine medicinal powder or coupled with adhesive agents.

V012-N01423.label: 阳中求阴
V012-N01423.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01423.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01423.englishNames: Formulas that seek yin within yang
V012-N01423.definitions: A method to combine a small amount of yang-tonifying medicines for kidney yin deficiency. This method enhances the effect of nourishing yin. The representative formula is Zuo Gui Wan (Left-Restoring Pill).

V012-N01424.label: 阴中求阳
V012-N01424.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01424.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01424.englishNames: Formulas that seek yang within yin
V012-N01424.definitions: Formulas to combine a small amount of yin-nourishing medicines for kidney yang deficiency. This method enhances the effect of warming yang. The representative formula is You Gui Wan (Right-Restoring Pill).

V012-N01425.label: 阴阳并补
V012-N01425.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01425.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01425.englishNames: Formulas that tonify both yin and yang
V012-N01425.definitions: Formulas consisting of yin-nourishing and yang-tonifying medicines. They are indicated for deficiency of yin and yang.

V012-N01426.label: 降气剂
V012-N01426.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01426.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01426.definitions: Formulas mainly consisting of medicines to make the qi descend. These formulas are indicated for adverse ascending of qi.

V012-N01427.label: 顿服
V012-N01427.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01427.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01427.definitions: A method to take the decoction all in one single dose.

V012-N01428.label: 频服
V012-N01428.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01428.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01428.definitions: A method to take the decoction in small portions at frequent intervals.

V012-N01429.label: 颗粒剂
V012-N01429.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01429.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01429.definitions: Granulated preparations made by herbal extracts and an excipient or fine medicinal powder.

V012-N01430.label: 饮片
V012-N01430.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01430.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01430.definitions: Prepared herbal pieces that can be used directly in clinical use or preparation production.

V012-N01431.label: 驱虫剂
V012-N01431.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01431.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01431.definitions: Formulas to kill intestinal parasites/worms. They are indicated for intestinal parasites.

V012-N01432.label: 驱虫药
V012-N01432.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01432.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01432.definitions: Medicines to expel or kill parasites. They are indicated for intestinal parasites.

V012-N01433.label: 验方
V012-N01433.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜药物治疗类
V012-N01433.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01433.definitions: Effective formulas that have been demonstrated or confirmed by long clinical practice.

V012-N01434.label: 针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01434.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法
V012-N01434.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01434.englishNames: Acupuncture and moxibustion (including cupping and guasha)

V012-N01435.label: 一源三歧
V012-N01435.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01435.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01435.aliases: The three divergences from a single source
V012-N01435.englishNames: The three branches from one source
V012-N01435.definitions: The Du, Ren and Chong meridians all arise from a common origin: “baozhong” (the lower abdomen where primordial kidney qi gathers), emerge out of the perineum, and then travels along their own pathways.

V012-N01436.label: 七星针
V012-N01436.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01436.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01436.englishNames: seven-star needle; The seven-star needle; sevenstar needle
V012-N01436.pinyinNames: qi xing zhen; qī xīng zhēn
V012-N01436.definitions: A dermal needle that has seven short needles attached to the end of a handle in a cluster. This needle cannot be reused without sterilization.

V012-N01437.label: 三棱针
V012-N01437.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01437.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01437.englishNames: threeedged needle; three-edged needle; The three-edged needle
V012-N01437.pinyinNames: san leng zhen; sān léng zhēn
V012-N01437.definitions: A thick needle with a round handle, a triangular body and a sharp tip similar to a lancet needle. It derives from the Sharp-edged Needle (one of the nine ancient needles).

V012-N01438.label: 上下配穴法
V012-N01438.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01438.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01438.englishNames: superior-inferior point combination; upper-lower points combination; Upper and lower point combination; superior-inferior points combination; superiorinferior points association
V012-N01438.pinyinNames: shang xia pei xue fa; shàng xià pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01438.definitions: A method to combine points on the upper and lower body.

V012-N01439.label: 下合穴
V012-N01439.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01439.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01439.definitions: The points where qi of the six fu organs is infused into the three foot-yang meridians.

V012-N01440.label: 不锈钢针
V012-N01440.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01440.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01440.englishNames: Stainless steel needles; stainless steel needle
V012-N01440.definitions: Acupuncture needles made of stainless steel. It is now commonly used in clinical practice.

V012-N01441.label: 中指同身寸
V012-N01441.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01441.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01441.englishNames: Middle finger measurement; middle finger cun; pro portional unit of middle finger; middle finger body-cun; middle finger body-inch
V012-N01441.pinyinNames: zhōng zhǐ tóng shēn cùn; zhong zhi tong shen cun
V012-N01441.definitions: A method to take the distance between the ends of the two radial creases of the interphalangeal joints of the middle finger as 1 cun when the middle finger is flexed.

V012-N01442.label: 主客原络配穴法
V012-N01442.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01442.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01442.englishNames: Host–guest combination using Yuan-source and Luo-connecting points
V012-N01442.pinyinNames: zhǔ kè yuán luò pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01442.definitions: A point combination method to use the Yuan-primary point of the affected meridian coupled with the Luo-connecting point of its interiorly-exteriorly connected meridian. This method is often employed to treat problems of the zang–fu organs and meridians.

V012-N01443.label: 九刺
V012-N01443.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01443.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01443.englishNames: The nine needling techniques; nine techniques of needling; nine needling (methods); nine needlings
V012-N01443.definitions: A collective term for nine ancient needling techniques, namely, Shu needling, distant needling, meridian needling, collateral needling, intramuscular needling, great drainage needling, skin needling, cauterized needling and contralateral needling.

V012-N01444.label: 九针
V012-N01444.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01444.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01444.englishNames: nine needles; nine kinds of classical needles; nine classical needles; Nine ancient needles
V012-N01444.definitions: A collective term for the nine types of needles described in the Nei Jing (Yellow Emperor’s Internal Classic), namely, shear needle, rounded needle, spoon-like needle, sharp-edged needle, round-sharp needle, sword-like needle, long needle, large needle and filiform needle.

V012-N01445.label: 五刺
V012-N01445.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01445.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01445.englishNames: The five needling techniques; five techniques of needling; five needling techniques; five needling (methods); five needlings
V012-N01445.definitions: A collective term for five ancient needling techniques, namely, half needling, leopard-spot needling, joint needling, Hegu needling, and Shu needling.

V012-N01446.label: 五输穴
V012-N01446.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01446.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01446.definitions: A collective term of the Jing-well, Ying-spring, Shu-stream, Jing-river and He-sea points. They are all located distal to, or at the elbow and knee joints.

V012-N01447.label: 井穴
V012-N01447.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01447.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01447.definitions: The point at which the qi rises is known as the Jing-well. Most Jing-well points are located on the fingers or toes.

V012-N01448.label: 交会穴
V012-N01448.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01448.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01448.definitions: The points intersected by two or more meridians.

V012-N01449.label: 任脉
V012-N01449.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01449.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01449.definitions: The Ren meridian is mainly distributed over the neck, face and along the midline of the chest and abdomen.

V012-N01450.label: 伪针/假针
V012-N01450.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01450.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01450.definitions: A research control method that simulates acupuncture needle insertion without actual insertion, preferably without any penetration that carries physiological response. The tip of a sham needle is blunt to prevent the actual penetration into the body, which effectively trick the patient into thinking they are receiving real acupuncture, such as the “Streitberger” needle.

V012-N01451.label: 俞募配穴法
V012-N01451.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01451.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01451.englishNames: backshu points and frontmu points combination; backshu points and frontmu points association; Shu and Mu point combination; back-shu and front-mu points combination
V012-N01451.pinyinNames: shù mù pèi xué fǎ; yu mu pei xue fa
V012-N01451.definitions: A method to combine the back-Shu and front-Mu points.

V012-N01452.label: 候气
V012-N01452.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01452.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01452.englishNames: awaiting qi; Waiting for qi; waiting for qi arrival
V012-N01452.definitions: A method to leave the needle in place for a while after insertion, without any manipulation.

V012-N01453.label: 偶刺
V012-N01453.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01453.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01453.englishNames: Coupled needling; paired needling; Ou needling
V012-N01453.definitions: A technique to puncture the tenderness spots felt on the front (corresponding to the front-Mu point) and back (corresponding to the back-Shu point).

V012-N01454.label: 傍针刺
V012-N01454.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01454.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01454.englishNames: Bangzhen needling; Accompanied needling; proximate needling
V012-N01454.pinyinNames: bang zhen ci; bàng zhēn cì
V012-N01454.definitions: A technique to insert one needle perpendicularly, coupled with another one lateral to its side. Indicated for tenderness or pain with a fixed location.

V012-N01455.label: 催气
V012-N01455.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01455.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01455.englishNames: hastening qi; Moving qi; promoting arrival of qi
V012-N01455.definitions: A method to manipulate the needle to stimulate the flow of qi.

V012-N01456.label: 八会穴
V012-N01456.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01456.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01456.englishNames: eight influential point; Eight influential points
V012-N01456.definitions: The points that have particular effects in the treatment of disorders relating to the zang, fu, qi, blood, tendon, vessels, bones and marrow.

V012-N01457.label: 八脉交会穴
V012-N01457.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01457.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01457.englishNames: Eight confluent points; eight confluence point
V012-N01457.definitions: The points where qi of the twelve regular meridians meets the eight extraordinary meridians.

V012-N01458.label: 关刺
V012-N01458.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01458.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01458.englishNames: joint needling; Guan needling; Joint needling
V012-N01458.definitions: A technique characterized by puncturing the tendon close to the joint without causing bleeding. It is often used for liver-related problems.

V012-N01459.label: 关阖枢
V012-N01459.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01459.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01459.definitions: Three objects of a door are used figuratively to express the functions of the three yang and three yin meridians. Of the three yang meridians, Taiyang is compared to the bolt, Yangming to the plank and Shaoyang to the hinge. Of the three yin meridians, Taiyin is compared to the bolt, Jueyin to the plank and Shaoyin to the hinge.

V012-N01460.label: 冲脉
V012-N01460.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01460.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01460.definitions: The Chong meridian is mainly distributed over the chest and face, inside the spine and along the first lateral line of the abdomen.

V012-N01461.label: 出针
V012-N01461.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01461.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
