V012-N01461.definitions: To remove a needle when the treatment is done.

V012-N01462.label: 分刺
V012-N01462.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01462.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01462.englishNames: intermuscular needling; Fen needling; Intramuscular needling
V012-N01462.definitions: A technique to puncture directly into the muscle.

V012-N01463.label: 刮痧板
V012-N01463.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01463.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01463.pinyinNames: gua sha ban; guā shā bǎn
V012-N01463.definitions: Guasha tools are often made from buffalo horn, Bian-stone or jade.

V012-N01464.label: 刮痧疗法
V012-N01464.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01464.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01464.pinyinNames: guā shā liáo fǎ; gua sha liao fa
V012-N01464.definitions: A method to scrape the skin repeatedly using a soup spoon or other smooth-edged instruments (such as horned animal bones, water buffalo horn or jade). The skin is often lubricated with massage oil or water.

V012-N01465.label: 刮（柄）法
V012-N01465.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01465.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01465.definitions: A needling manipulation to scrape the handle of the needle with fingernails to promote the flow of qi.

V012-N01466.label: 刺手
V012-N01466.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01466.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01466.englishNames: needling hand; The needling hand; needle-holding hand; needleholding hand
V012-N01466.definitions: The hand to hold and insert a needle.

V012-N01467.label: 刺络拔罐
V012-N01467.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01467.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01467.englishNames: Blood-letting and cupping; pricking and cupping; pricking blood with cupping
V012-N01467.definitions: A method to prick the area to be treated with a three-edged needle or lancet and then suck out blood with a cup.

V012-N01468.label: 刺络法
V012-N01468.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01468.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01468.englishNames: Collateral-pricking method; pricking bloodletting method; collateral pricking
V012-N01468.pinyinNames: cì luò fǎ; ci luo fa
V012-N01468.definitions: A needling technique to prick superficial collaterals using a three-edged or a (single-use, disposable) seven-star needle to let out appropriate amount of blood.

V012-N01469.label: 前后配穴法
V012-N01469.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01469.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01469.aliases: 腹背配穴法、腹背阴阳配穴法； 腹前配穴法； 腹背阴阳配穴法
V012-N01469.englishNames: anterior-posterior points combination; Back and front point combination; anteriorposterior points combination; anteriorposterior points association; anterior-posterior point combination
V012-N01469.pinyinNames: qian hou pei xue fa; qián hòu pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01469.definitions: A method to combine points on the back and front of the body.

V012-N01470.label: 割治
V012-N01470.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01470.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01470.definitions: A therapeutic method to incise the skin to squeeze out a small amount of fat.

V012-N01471.label: 募穴
V012-N01471.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01471.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01471.definitions: The points on the chest and abdomen where qi of the respective zang–fu organs is infused.

V012-N01472.label: 化脓灸
V012-N01472.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01472.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01472.pinyinNames: huà nóng jiǔ; hua nong jiu

V012-N01473.label: 十二刺
V012-N01473.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01473.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01473.englishNames: twelve needling methods; The twelve needling techniques; twelve needlings; twelve techniques of needling
V012-N01473.pinyinNames: shi er ci; shí èr cì; shi dong ci
V012-N01473.definitions: A collective term for twelve ancient needling techniques, namely: coupled needling; successive trigger needling; relaxing needling; triple needling; quintuple needling; straight needling; Shu needling; short needling; superficial needling; yin needling; accompanied needling; and repeated shallow needling.

V012-N01474.label: 十二皮部
V012-N01474.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01474.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01474.definitions: The twelve cutaneous regions are corresponding sites where the qi and blood of the twelve meridians to nourish the skin. They are generally known as the outward manifestations of the twelve meridians and collaterals.

V012-N01475.label: 十二经别
V012-N01475.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01475.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01475.definitions: The twelve divergent meridians branch out from the twelve regular meridians and are deeper in the body. They function to strengthen the connection between internally-externally coupled meridians.

V012-N01476.label: 十二经筋
V012-N01476.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01476.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01476.definitions: The twelve muscle regions are corresponding sites where the qi and blood of the twelve meridians to nourish the muscles and tendons. Their distributions are almost the same as that of the twelve meridians.

V012-N01477.label: 十二经脉
V012-N01477.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01477.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01477.aliases: 十二正经； Twelve regular meridians
V012-N01477.definitions: The twelve meridians (i.e. the twelve regular meridians) are: the lung meridian; the large intestine meridian; the stomach meridian; the spleen meridian; the heart meridian; the small intestine meridian; the bladder meridian; the kidney meridian; the pericardium meridian; the Sanjiao meridian; the gallbladder meridian; and the liver meridian.

V012-N01478.label: 十五络脉
V012-N01478.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01478.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01478.definitions: The fifteen collaterals include the collaterals of the twelve meridians, the collaterals of the Du and Ren meridian as well as the major collateral of the spleen.

V012-N01479.label: 十六络脉
V012-N01479.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01479.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01479.definitions: The sixteen collaterals include the above-mentioned fifteen collaterals plus the major collateral of the stomach.

V012-N01480.label: 半刺
V012-N01480.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01480.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01480.englishNames: Ban needling; Half needling; half needling
V012-N01480.definitions: A technique characterized by a shallow insertion and swift withdrawal. It is often used for lung-related problems.

V012-N01481.label: 单手进针法
V012-N01481.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01481.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01481.englishNames: single-handed needle insertion; Needle insertion with one hand; needle insertion with single hand; needleinserting with single hand
V012-N01481.pinyinNames: dān shǒu jìn zhēn fǎ; dan shou jin zhen fa
V012-N01481.definitions: A method to insert a needle into a point by using the puncturing hand alone.

V012-N01482.label: 原穴
V012-N01482.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01482.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01482.definitions: The points where the Yuan-primordial qi pools.

V012-N01483.label: 原络配穴法
V012-N01483.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01483.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01483.aliases: 主客配穴法、主客原络配穴法
V012-N01483.englishNames: source-connecting point combination; yuansource points and collateralpoints combination; Yuan and Luo point combination; source-connecting points combination
V012-N01483.pinyinNames: yuán luò pèi xué fǎ; yuan luo pei xue fa
V012-N01483.definitions: A method to combine the Yuan-source and Luo-connecting points.

V012-N01484.label: 双手进针法
V012-N01484.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01484.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01484.englishNames: double-handed needle insertion; needleinserting with both hands; Needle insertion with both hands; needle insertion with both hands
V012-N01484.pinyinNames: shuāng shǒu jìn zhēn fǎ; shuang shou jin zhen fa
V012-N01484.definitions: A method to insert a needle into a point by using both the puncturing hand and pressing hand.

V012-N01485.label: 合穴
V012-N01485.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01485.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01485.definitions: The point at which the qi enters inwards is known as the He-sea. The He-sea points are located around the joints of the elbows or knees.

V012-N01486.label: 合谷刺
V012-N01486.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01486.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01486.pinyinNames: he gu ci; hé gǔ cì
V012-N01486.definitions: A technique characterized by puncturing the muscles of the affected region with the needle going obliquely right and left, resembling the claws of a chicken. It is often used for spleen-related problems.

V012-N01487.label: 同名经配穴法
V012-N01487.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01487.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01487.englishNames: combination of points of namesake channels; combinationd of points of namesake meridians; same name meridian points combination; Point combination of the same-name meridians
V012-N01487.pinyinNames: tong ming jing pei xue fa; tóng míng jīng pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01487.definitions: A method to combine points from one hand meridian and one foot meridian that share the same name.

V012-N01488.label: 员利针
V012-N01488.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01488.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01488.pinyinNames: yuan li zhen; yuán lì zhēn
V012-N01488.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a round and sharp needle tip and is often used for abscess, bi-impediment and acute conditions.

V012-N01489.label: 呼吸补泻
V012-N01489.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01489.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01489.englishNames: respiratory supplementation and draining; reinforcingreducing method by respiration; respiratory reinforcement and reduction; Reinforcing and reducing by breathing
V012-N01489.pinyinNames: hu xi bu xie; hū xī bǔ xiè
V012-N01489.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce by inserting or withdrawing the needle when the patient breathes in or out.

V012-N01490.label: 四海
V012-N01490.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01490.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01490.definitions: The four seas include the sea of marrow, sea of qi, sea of water and food, and sea of blood (sea of the twelve meridians).

V012-N01491.label: 回旋灸
V012-N01491.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01491.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01491.englishNames: circling moxibustion; revolving moxibustion; Circling moxibustion
V012-N01491.pinyinNames: hui xuan jiu; huí xuán jiǔ
V012-N01491.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to keep the end of an ignited moxa stick at a fixed distance from the selected area and move it back and forth circularly.

V012-N01492.label: 圆针
V012-N01492.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01492.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01492.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a cylindrical needle body and oval tip. It is often used for point massage in the treatment of muscle problems.

V012-N01493.label: 埋针疗法
V012-N01493.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01493.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01493.aliases: 皮内针疗法； Intradermal needle therapy
V012-N01493.definitions: A therapy to insert an intradermal needle into the skin of the body surface (an acupuncture point) and fix with tape to retain for a period of time.

V012-N01494.label: 壮
V012-N01494.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01494.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01494.definitions: A dosage unit to count the number of moxa cones.

V012-N01495.label: 大泻刺
V012-N01495.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01495.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01495.englishNames: great drainage needling; Daxie needling; Great drainage needling; drainage needling
V012-N01495.pinyinNames: da xie ci; dà xiè cì
V012-N01495.definitions: A technique to incise with a sword-shaped needle to drain pus or eliminate stagnant blood, also known as lancing.

V012-N01496.label: 大针
V012-N01496.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01496.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01496.englishNames: big needle; large needle; Large needle
V012-N01496.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a thick needle body and a slightly round needle tip. It is often used to treat general oedema or abdominal masses.

V012-N01497.label: 天灸
V012-N01497.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01497.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01497.aliases: 发泡灸； 药物发泡法； Scarring moxibustion
V012-N01497.definitions: A method to apply irritants to the selected area to produce congestion and blistering.

V012-N01498.label: 太乙神针
V012-N01498.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01498.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01498.englishNames: Taiyi moxa stick; taivi miraculous moxa roll; Taiyi moxa stick moxibustion
V012-N01498.pinyinNames: tài yǐ shén zhēn; tai yi shen zhen
V012-N01498.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to apply an ignited moxa stick to the selected area. The moxa stick is made of fine powder of Tan Xiang (Lignum Santali Albi), Shan Nai (Rhizoma Kaempferiae), Qiang Huo (Rhizoma et Radix Notopterygii), Gui Zhi (Ramulus Cinnamomi), Mu Xiang (Radix Aucklandiae), Xiong Huang (Realgar), Bai Zhi (Radix Angelicae Dahuricae) and Xi Xin (Radix et Rhizoma Asari).

V012-N01499.label: 头针疗法
V012-N01499.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01499.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01499.aliases: 头皮针、颅针疗法； 头皮针疗法
V012-N01499.pinyinNames: tou zhen liao fa; tóu zhēn liáo fǎ
V012-N01499.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate specific lines on the scalp with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01500.label: 夹持进针法
V012-N01500.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01500.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01500.englishNames: hand-holding needle insertion; Needle-holding insertion with both hands
V012-N01500.pinyinNames: jia chi jin zhen fa; jiā chí jìn zhēn fǎ
V012-N01500.definitions: A method to hold the lower part of the needle body with a piece of sterile cotton or gauze between the thumb and index finger of the pressing hand while inserting a needle. (Press the skin adjacent to the the needling site with the nail of thumb or index finger of the pressing hand, while inserting a needle against the fingernail with the needling hand).

V012-N01501.label: 奇经八脉
V012-N01501.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01501.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01501.definitions: The eight extraordinary meridians are different from the twelve regular meridians with special distributions and functions. They are Du meridian, Ren meridian, Chong meridian, Dai meridian, Yinwei meridian, Yangwei meridian, Yinqiao meridian and Yangqiao meridian.

V012-N01502.label: 子午流注
V012-N01502.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01502.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01502.definitions: A special theory for point selection. The midnight-noon (Zi Wu) represents the time. The ebb-flow (Liu Zhu) refers to the circulation of qi and blood. This theory believes the meridian qi and blood flows from Zi time (11:00 pm–1:00 am) to Wu time (11:00 am–1:00 pm) and then from the Wu time to the Zi time. The yin qi becomes most abundant at the Zi time and then begins to decline, and the Yang qi starts to increase and becomes most abundant at the Wu time. Based on this theory, the five-Shu points located below the knee and elbow joints should be selected according to the designated hours.

V012-N01503.label: 子母补泻法
V012-N01503.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01503.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01503.englishNames: Reinforcing the mother or reducing the son
V012-N01503.definitions: One of the reinforcing or reducing manipulations. Manipulations of reinforcing the mother or reducing the son are selected according to deficiency or excess of the zang–fu organs or meridian problems.

V012-N01504.label: 孙络
V012-N01504.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01504.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01505.label: 实按灸
V012-N01505.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01505.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01505.englishNames: pressing moxibustion; paper or clothseparated moxibustion; Mild-warm moxibustion
V012-N01505.pinyinNames: shí àn jiǔ; shi an jiu
V012-N01505.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to place several layers of cloth or cotton paper on the selected area and then press the ignited end of a moxa stick to allow the heat to penetrate the skin.

V012-N01506.label: 左右配穴法
V012-N01506.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01506.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01506.englishNames: leftright points combination; leftright points association; left-right points combination; Left and right point combination; left-right point combination
V012-N01506.pinyinNames: zuo you pei xue fa; zuǒ yòu pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01506.definitions: A method to combine points on the left and right side of the body.

V012-N01507.label: 巨刺
V012-N01507.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01507.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01507.englishNames: Contralateral needling; contralateral channel needling; Ju needling
V012-N01507.definitions: A technique to puncture points on the left side if the right side is affected, or vice versa.

V012-N01508.label: 带脉
V012-N01508.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01508.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01508.definitions: The Dai meridian is mainly distributed over the waist, subcostal area and abdomen. It encircles the waist like a girdle.

V012-N01509.label: 平刺
V012-N01509.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01509.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01509.englishNames: horizontal insertion of needle; Subcutaneous needle insertion; transverse insertion
V012-N01509.definitions: Needle insertion at a 15° angle to the skin.

V012-N01510.label: 平补平泻
V012-N01510.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01510.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01510.englishNames: neutral supplementation and draining method; even reinforcingreducing method; uniform reinforcingreducing method; Balanced reinforcing and reducing
V012-N01510.definitions: Needling manipulations to lift, thrust, or twirl evenly.

V012-N01511.label: 开阖补泻
V012-N01511.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01511.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01511.aliases: Tonifying and sedating by opening and closing
V012-N01511.englishNames: Reinforcing and reducing by opening and closing; open-closed supplementation and draining method; reinforcing and reducing method by keeping hole opened or closed
V012-N01511.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce by opening or closing the needle holes.

V012-N01512.label: 弯针
V012-N01512.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01512.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01512.englishNames: bending of needle; bending of the needle; Bent needle
V012-N01512.definitions: The needle body becomes bent upon insertion or retention.

V012-N01513.label: 弹（柄）法
V012-N01513.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01513.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01513.definitions: A needling manipulation to flick the handle of the needle to promote the flow of qi.

V012-N01514.label: 徐疾补泻
V012-N01514.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01514.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01514.aliases: Tonifying and sedating by slowness and rapidness
V012-N01514.englishNames: slow-rapid reinforcing-re ducing method; Reinforcing and reducing by slowness and rapidness
V012-N01514.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce according to the slow or rapid needle insertion and withdrawal.

V012-N01515.label: 得气
V012-N01515.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01515.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01515.englishNames: Arrival of qi; atrival of qi; obtaining qi
V012-N01515.definitions: The arrival of qi is signalled by particular sensations felt by the patient and the practitioner. For the patient, it typically refers to soreness, numbness, distension and heaviness felt around the needle (but may move up or down along the meridian). For the practitioner, it refers to a gripping sensation or a feeling of pressure underneath the needle.

V012-N01516.label: 循法
V012-N01516.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01516.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01517.label: 循经感传
V012-N01517.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01517.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01517.englishNames: Transmission of sensation along meridians; propagated sensation along channel; transmission of sensation along meridian
V012-N01517.definitions: Sensations radiating along the pathways of meridians.

V012-N01518.label: 微针疗法
V012-N01518.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01518.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01518.definitions: A collective term for acupuncture in a specific local area, such as ear, scalp, or face, etc.

V012-N01519.label: 心系[针灸]
V012-N01519.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01519.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01519.definitions: The structures connecting with the heart, such as blood vessels.

V012-N01520.label: 恢刺
V012-N01520.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01520.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01520.englishNames: relaxing needling; Relaxing needling; Hui needling
V012-N01520.definitions: A technique to insert the needle from the side of the tendon and then puncture the contracted muscle in different directions to induce relaxation.

V012-N01521.label: 悬灸
V012-N01521.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01521.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01521.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to hold a moxa stick above the skin during the treatment.

V012-N01522.label: 手三阳经
V012-N01522.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01522.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01522.definitions: The three hand-yang meridians are large intestine meridian, small intestine meridian and Sanjiao meridian.

V012-N01523.label: 手三阴经
V012-N01523.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01523.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01523.definitions: The three hand-yin meridians are lung meridian, heart meridian and pericardium meridian.

V012-N01524.label: 手厥阴心包经
V012-N01524.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01524.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01524.pinyinNames: shǒu jué yīn xīn bāo jīng
V012-N01524.definitions: The pericardium meridian pertains to the pericardium and connects with the Sanjiao. It mainly travels along the palmar aspect of the upper arm and moves towards the forearm running between the tendons of the m. palmaris longus and m. flexor carpi radialis.

V012-N01525.label: 手太阳小肠经
V012-N01525.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01525.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01525.englishNames: The small intestine meridian of hand-Taiyang
V012-N01525.pinyinNames: shǒu tài yáng xiǎo cháng jīng
V012-N01525.definitions: The small intestine meridian pertains to the small intestine and connects with the heart. It mainly travels along the posterior border of the dorsal ulnar aspect of the arm, passes through the shoulder and reaches the face.

V012-N01526.label: 手太阴肺经
V012-N01526.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01526.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01526.definitions: The lung meridian pertains to the lung and connects with the large intestine. It mainly travels along the anterior border of the medial aspect of the arm.

V012-N01527.label: 手少阳三焦经
V012-N01527.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01527.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01527.aliases: Triple-burner meridian； Triple warmer meridian； Triple energizer meridian of hand-Shaoyang
V012-N01527.pinyinNames: shǒu shào yáng sān jiāo jīng
V012-N01527.definitions: The Sanjiao meridian pertains to Sanjiao and connects with the pericardium. It is mainly distributed along the dorsal aspect of the forearm between the radius and ulna and reaches the ears and face.

V012-N01528.label: 手少阴心经
V012-N01528.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01528.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01528.definitions: The heart meridian pertains to the heart and connects with the small intestine. It mainly travels along the posterior border of the ulnar palmar aspect of the arm.

V012-N01529.label: 手指同身寸
V012-N01529.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01529.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01529.definitions: A method to locate acupuncture points based on the finger sizes of the patient.

V012-N01530.label: 手针疗法
V012-N01530.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01530.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01530.englishNames: hand needle therapy; Hand acupuncture; hand acupuncture therapy
V012-N01530.pinyinNames: shou zhen liao fa; shǒu zhēn liáo fǎ
V012-N01530.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the hand with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01531.label: 手阳明大肠经
V012-N01531.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01531.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01531.englishNames: The large intestine meridian of hand-Yangming
V012-N01531.pinyinNames: shǒu yáng míng dà cháng jīng
V012-N01531.definitions: The large intestine meridian pertains to the large intestine and connects with the lung. It mainly travels along the anterior border of the radial aspect of the arm, passes through the cheeks, enters the lower teeth, and then over the upper lip and terminates beside the opposite nostril.

V012-N01532.label: 扬刺
V012-N01532.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01532.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01532.englishNames: shallow surround needling; central-square needling; Quintuple needling; Yang needling
V012-N01532.definitions: A technique to insert one needle perpendicularly in the centre, coupled with four more needles anterior, posterior, right, and left to the perpendicular needle. Used to remove cold in a large, shallow area.

V012-N01533.label: 投火法
V012-N01533.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01533.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01533.definitions: A method whereby an ignited paper is quickly flung into a cup to create a negative pressure on the selected area.

V012-N01534.label: 报刺
V012-N01534.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01534.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01534.englishNames: successive trigger needling; Bao needling; Successive trigger needling
V012-N01534.definitions: A technique to puncture migratory Ashi points: while needling directly at the tender point, searching for other tender points over the surrounding area and needling in succession.

V012-N01535.label: 押手
V012-N01535.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01535.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01535.definitions: The hand that presses the puncturing area with one or more fingers, to facilitate needle insertion.

V012-N01536.label: 拇指同身寸
V012-N01536.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01536.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01536.englishNames: thumb body-cun; thumb body-inch; thumb cun; proportional unit of thumb; Thumb measurement
V012-N01536.pinyinNames: mu zhi tong shen cun; mǔ zhǐ tóng shēn cùn
V012-N01536.definitions: A method to take the width of the interphalangeal joint of the thumb as 1 cun.

V012-N01537.label: 拔罐法
V012-N01537.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01537.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01537.definitions: A therapeutic method to create negative pressure by consuming the air inside the cup with fire or other methods and then place the cup on the skin over the selected area.

V012-N01538.label: 拔罐类
V012-N01538.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01538.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01539.label: 指切进针法
V012-N01539.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01539.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01539.englishNames: fingernail-pressing needle insertion; fingernailpressure nee dle inseyting; Nail-pressing insertion
V012-N01539.pinyinNames: zhǐ qiē jìn zhēn fǎ; zhi qie jin zhen fa
V012-N01539.definitions: A method to press the skin of puncturing area with the nail of thumb or index finger of the pressing hand and insert the needle against the fingernail with the needling hand.

V012-N01540.label: 挑刺法（挑治法）
V012-N01540.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01540.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01540.definitions: A needling technique to gently prick using a three-edged needle and let out a small amount of blood or tissue fluid.

V012-N01541.label: 捻转法
V012-N01541.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01541.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01541.englishNames: twirling method; Twirling or rotating method
V012-N01541.pinyinNames: nian zhuan fa; niǎn zhuǎn fǎ
V012-N01541.definitions: A needling manipulation to twirl (turn) or rotate a needle.

V012-N01542.label: 捻转补泻
V012-N01542.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01542.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01542.aliases: Tonifying and sedating by twirling or rotating
V012-N01542.englishNames: twirling reinforcement and reduction; reinforcingreducing method by twirling; twirling supplementation and draining; Reinforcing and reducing by twirling or rotating
V012-N01542.pinyinNames: nian zhuan bu xie; niǎn zhuǎn bǔ xiè
V012-N01542.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce according to the twirling/rotating direction, angle, frequency and duration.

V012-N01543.label: 提捏进针法
V012-N01543.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01543.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01543.englishNames: pinching needle insertion; Skin-pinching insertion; skinpinching up needle inserting
V012-N01543.pinyinNames: tí niē jìn zhēn fǎ; ti nie jin zhen fa
V012-N01543.definitions: A method to pinch and lift the skin of the puncturing area with the thumb and index finger of the pressing hand while inserting a needle.

V012-N01544.label: 提插法
V012-N01544.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01544.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01544.englishNames: lifting and thrusting method; method of lifting-thrusting needle; lifting-thrusting method; Lifting-thrusting method
V012-N01544.pinyinNames: ti cha fa; tí chā fǎ
V012-N01544.definitions: A needling manipulation to lift and thrust a needle.

V012-N01545.label: 提插补泻
V012-N01545.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01545.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01545.aliases: Tonifying and sedating by lifting and thrusting
V012-N01545.englishNames: Reinforcing and reducing by lifting and thrusting; lifting-thrusting reinforcement and reduction; lifting-thrusting supplementation and draining; liftingthrusting reinforcing reducing method
V012-N01545.pinyinNames: tí chā bǔ xiè; ti cha bu xie
V012-N01545.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce according to the forcefulness, speed, amplitude and duration of the lifting and thrusting.

V012-N01546.label: 揿针
V012-N01546.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01546.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01546.definitions: An intradermal needle that is shaped like a thumbtack used for extended auricular therapy, approximately 0.2 mm–1.5 mm in length.

V012-N01547.label: 摇（柄）法
V012-N01547.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01547.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01547.definitions: A needling manipulation to shake the needle handle to promote the flow of qi.

V012-N01548.label: 散刺法
V012-N01548.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01548.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01548.definitions: A needling technique to prick a point and its surrounding area.

V012-N01549.label: 斜刺
V012-N01549.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01549.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01549.englishNames: oblique insertion; oblique insertion of needle; Oblique needle insertion
V012-N01549.definitions: The needle insertion at a 45° angle to the skin.

V012-N01550.label: 断针
V012-N01550.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01550.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01550.definitions: The needle body is broken and the broken part is on or below the skin surface.

V012-N01551.label: 无瘢痕灸
V012-N01551.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01551.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01551.pinyinNames: wú bān hén jiǔ; wu ban hen jiu
V012-N01551.definitions: A method to place a small moxa cone directly on the skin, then ignite and remove the burning moxa before the skin burns enough to scar.

V012-N01552.label: 晕针
V012-N01552.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01552.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01552.englishNames: faint during acupuncture treatment; fainting during acupuncture treatment; fainting during acupuncture; Needle shock
V012-N01552.definitions: An adverse event during an acupuncture session: Patients may present with a pale complexion or experience dizziness, nausea and chest tightness. In severe cases, cold limbs, cold sweats, drop in blood pressure, syncope or shock may be present.

V012-N01553.label: 本经配穴法
V012-N01553.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01553.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01553.englishNames: point combination of the same meridian; same meridian points combination; combination of affected channel; association of affected channel; combination of affected meridian; association of affected meridian; Point combination of the affected meridian; point combination on the same meridian/channel
V012-N01553.pinyinNames: ben jing pei xue fa; běn jīng pèi xué fǎ
V012-N01553.definitions: A method to combine points along the affected meridian.

V012-N01554.label: 标本
V012-N01554.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01554.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01554.definitions: The word “biao” literally means the tip of tree branches. Here it refers to the head, face and body trunk where meridian qi diffuses. The word “ben” literally means the trunk of a tree. Here it refers to the four limbs where meridian qi gathers.

V012-N01555.label: 根结
V012-N01555.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01555.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01555.definitions: The word “Gen” literally means root. It shares the similar meaning with “Ben”. The word “Jie” literally means knot. It shares the similar meaning with “Biao” (see Biao and Ben).

V012-N01556.label: 梅花针
V012-N01556.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01556.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01556.englishNames: plum-blossom needle; The plum-blossom needle; plumblossom needle
V012-N01556.pinyinNames: mei hua zhen; méi huā zhēn
V012-N01556.definitions: A dermal needle that has a bundle of five short, embedded needles resembling a plum blossom. This needle cannot be reused without sterilization.

V012-N01557.label: 横指同身寸
V012-N01557.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01557.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01557.englishNames: finger-breadth cun; 4-finger-breadth meas urement; Four-finger measurement
V012-N01557.definitions: A method to take the width of the patients’ four fingers (index, middle, ring and little) close together at the level of the dorsal skin crease of the proximal interphalangeal joint of the middle finger as three cun. It is also known as “Yi Fu Fa” in Chinese.

V012-N01558.label: 毛刺
V012-N01558.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01558.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01558.englishNames: skin needling; Skin needling; Mao needling
V012-N01558.definitions: A technique to puncture shallowly into the skin.

V012-N01559.label: 毫针
V012-N01559.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01559.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01559.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It is now the most common needle in acupuncture treatment.

V012-N01560.label: 毫针刺法
V012-N01560.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01560.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01560.englishNames: filiform needling method; technique of filiform needle acupuncture; Filiform needling methods
V012-N01560.pinyinNames: hao zhen ci fa; háo zhēn cì fǎ
V012-N01560.definitions: Basic techniques in using filiform needles.

V012-N01561.label: 气海
V012-N01561.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01561.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01561.definitions: The chest is known as “the sea of qi”.

V012-N01562.label: 气至病所
V012-N01562.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01562.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01562.englishNames: Qi reaches the affected area; qi arrival at disease area
V012-N01562.pinyinNames: qì zhì bìng suǒ; qi zhi bing suo
V012-N01562.definitions: The effect of needling sensation reaching an affected area.

V012-N01563.label: 气街
V012-N01563.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01563.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01563.definitions: Qi streets are the pathways for meridian qi to flow over the head and body trunk. There is a total of four qi streets, respectively on the head, chest, abdomen and lower leg.

V012-N01564.label: 水罐法
V012-N01564.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01564.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01564.aliases: 水煮法； 水吸法； Water suction
V012-N01564.englishNames: boiling water cupping method; Boiling cup method
V012-N01564.pinyinNames: shui guan fa; shuǐ guàn fǎ
V012-N01564.definitions: A method to boil a cup in water to create negative pressure: to hold the boiled bamboo cup (the mouth facing downwards) with a forceps, cover the cup mouth with a towel to absorb the water in the cup, and then quickly place the cup over the selected area.

V012-N01565.label: 水谷之海
V012-N01565.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01565.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01565.englishNames: The sea of water and food
V012-N01565.definitions: The stomach is known as “the sea of water and food”.

V012-N01566.label: 浮刺
V012-N01566.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01566.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01566.englishNames: superficial needling; Fu needling; Superficial needling
V012-N01566.definitions: A technique to puncture the superficial area obliquely. Used indicated for muscle pain with a cold sensation.

V012-N01567.label: 浮络
V012-N01567.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01567.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01567.definitions: Collaterals on the surface of the body.

V012-N01568.label: 温和灸
V012-N01568.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01568.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01568.englishNames: gentle moxibustion; Gentle moxibustion; mildwarm moxibustion
V012-N01568.pinyinNames: wen he jiu; wēn hé jiǔ
V012-N01568.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to keep the end of an ignited moxa stick at a fixed distance (2–3 cm) from the selected area, enabling a mild warm sensation.

V012-N01569.label: 温灸器灸
V012-N01569.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01569.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01569.englishNames: Moxibustion with a moxa burner; moxa burner moxibustion; moxibustion with moxibustioner
V012-N01569.pinyinNames: wēn jiǔ qì jiǔ; wen jiu qi jiu
V012-N01569.definitions: A method to put moxa wool or stick into a metal box or canister, ignite and then place the box or canister over the selected area.

V012-N01570.label: 温针灸
V012-N01570.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01570.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01570.aliases: 温针、温针疗法； Needle-warming moxibustion
V012-N01570.englishNames: warming needle moxibustion; needle warming through moxibustion; warming needling; Warming needle
V012-N01570.pinyinNames: wen zhen jiu; wēn zhēn jiǔ
V012-N01570.definitions: A method to fix a small section of moxa stick or cone onto the handle of an inserted needle prior to igniting.

V012-N01571.label: 滞针
V012-N01571.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01571.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01571.englishNames: stucking of needle; Stuck needle; stuck needle
V012-N01571.definitions: Difficulty or impossible to rotate, lift or thrust a needle.

V012-N01572.label: 激光针
V012-N01572.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01572.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01572.definitions: A therapeutic method to use a low-energy laser beam to stimulate acupuncture points.

V012-N01573.label: 火罐法
V012-N01573.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01573.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01573.englishNames: Fire cupping; fire cupping; fire cupping method
V012-N01573.pinyinNames: huǒ guàn fǎ; huo guan fa
V012-N01573.definitions: A method to create negative pressure by introducing an ignited material inside the cup to consume the air, thereby enabling the cup to suck itself onto the skin. There are three different approaches: fire twinkling; fire throwing; and cotton sticking.

V012-N01574.label: 火针
V012-N01574.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01574.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01574.aliases: 燔针； Cauterized needle； 燔针、煨针
V012-N01574.englishNames: Fire needling; fire needling; fire needle
V012-N01574.definitions: A therapeutic method to insert a hot needle to the affected area and remove immediately afterwards.

V012-N01575.label: 灯火灸
V012-N01575.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01575.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01575.englishNames: rushfire cauterization; burning rush moxibustion; juncibustion; Juncus moxibustion
V012-N01575.pinyinNames: deng huo jiu; dēng huǒ jiǔ
V012-N01575.definitions: A method to apply a quick momentary touch to the selected area with an ignited oiled rush of Juncus.

V012-N01576.label: 灸法
V012-N01576.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01576.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01576.definitions: A therapeutic method to apply burning mugwort (moxa) or other substances near or to particular points or areas of the body to relax, warm and tonify.

V012-N01577.label: 灸法类
V012-N01577.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01577.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01578.label: 点刺法
V012-N01578.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01578.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01578.englishNames: swift pricking blood therapy; point-pricking method; Point-pricking method
V012-N01578.pinyinNames: dian ci fa; diǎn cì fǎ
V012-N01578.definitions: A needling technique to prick superficially and then remove immediately. It is often used for Jing-well points or blood-letting.

V012-N01579.label: 烧山火
V012-N01579.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01579.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01579.definitions: A complex reinforcing manipulation to induce local or general warming sensation. The point to be needled is divided into three levels: shallow (heaven), intermediate (man) and deep (earth). Insert the needle into the shallow level first and then down to the intermediate and deep levels, coupled with rapid thrusting and slow lifting for a total of nine times in each level. Repeat the procedure three times and leave the needle in the deep level.

V012-N01580.label: 焠刺
V012-N01580.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01580.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01580.englishNames: red-hot needling; Cauterized needling; Cui needling
V012-N01580.definitions: A technique to prick swiftly with a cauterized needle. Indicated for cold bi-impediment pattern.

V012-N01581.label: 特定穴
V012-N01581.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01581.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01581.definitions: The meridians points that have special names and functions.

V012-N01582.label: 电针
V012-N01582.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01582.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01582.definitions: A therapeutic method to connect pulses of electrical current to inserted acupuncture needles.

V012-N01583.label: 留罐法
V012-N01583.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01583.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01583.pinyinNames: liú guàn fǎ; liu guan fa
V012-N01583.definitions: Leave the cup in place after suction.

V012-N01584.label: 留针拔罐
V012-N01584.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01584.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01584.englishNames: cupping with retaining of needle; Needle-retention cupping
V012-N01584.pinyinNames: liu zhen ba guan; liú zhēn bá guàn
V012-N01584.definitions: A method to apply cupping to the centre of the site where a needle is retained.

V012-N01585.label: 留针（置针）
V012-N01585.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01585.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01585.definitions: To retain a needle in place after the presence of needling sensation (arrival of qi).

V012-N01586.label: 瘢痕灸
V012-N01586.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01586.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01586.aliases: 有痕灸； 化脓灸； 打脓灸
V012-N01586.pinyinNames: ban hen jiu; bān hén jiǔ
V012-N01586.definitions: A method to place a small moxa cone directly on the skin, then ignite until the skin blisters and then scars after it heals.

V012-N01587.label: 皮内针
V012-N01587.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01587.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01587.pinyinNames: pi nei zhen; pí nèi zhēn
V012-N01587.definitions: A small needling instrument for embedding at a certain point for an extended period.

V012-N01588.label: 皮肤针
V012-N01588.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01588.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01588.englishNames: cutaneous needle; dermal needle; The dermal needle
V012-N01588.pinyinNames: pí fū zhēn; pi fu zhen
V012-N01588.definitions: Needling instrument composed of several short needles used for tapping the points, such as the plum-blossom needle or seven-star needle. These needles cannot be reused without sterilization.

V012-N01589.label: 目系[针灸]
V012-N01589.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01589.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01589.definitions: The posterior intraocular structures connecting with the brain.

V012-N01590.label: 直刺
V012-N01590.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01590.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01590.englishNames: perpendicular insertion; Perpendicular needle insertion; perpendicular insertion of nee dle
V012-N01590.definitions: The needle insertion at a 90° angle to the skin.

V012-N01591.label: 直接灸
V012-N01591.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01591.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01591.pinyinNames: zhijie jiu; zhi jie jiu; zhí jiē jiǔ
V012-N01591.definitions: A method to place a moxa cone directly on the selected area and then ignite to create a superficial burn.

V012-N01592.label: 直针刺
V012-N01592.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01592.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01592.englishNames: Straight needling; perpendicular needling; Zhizhen needling; direct subcutaneous needling
V012-N01592.pinyinNames: zhí zhēn cì; zhi zhen ci
V012-N01592.definitions: A technique to pinch the skin and insert the needle subcutaneously (The word “straight” here means towards the affected shallow area). Used to remove cold in the superficial area.

V012-N01593.label: 眼针疗法
V012-N01593.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01593.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01593.englishNames: Eye acupuncture; ocular acupuncture therapy; eye acupuncture therapy
V012-N01593.pinyinNames: yǎn zhēn liáo fǎ; yan zhen liao fa
V012-N01593.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points around the eye socket with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01594.label: 督脉
V012-N01594.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01594.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01594.definitions: The Du meridian is mainly distributed over the head, face and along the midline of the back.

V012-N01595.label: 短刺
V012-N01595.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01595.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01595.englishNames: short thrust needling; Short needling; short needling; Duan needling
V012-N01595.pinyinNames: duǎn cì; duan ci; dong ci
V012-N01595.definitions: A technique to insert the needle slowly while gently shaking the needle, followed by mild rotation when the needle is close to the bone. Used to treat bone pain or tenderness.

V012-N01596.label: 砭石
V012-N01596.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01596.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01596.definitions: Pointed or wedge-shaped stones historically used to stimulate the surface of the body, drain pus or let blood out.

V012-N01597.label: 穴位埋线
V012-N01597.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01597.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01597.englishNames: catgut embedment in acupoint; Catgut-embedding therapy
V012-N01597.pinyinNames: xue wei mai xian; xué wèi mái xiàn
V012-N01597.definitions: A therapeutic method to implant catgut or surgical suture at a selected point.

V012-N01598.label: 穴位敷贴
V012-N01598.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01598.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01598.definitions: A method to apply warm or hot medicinal substances to acupuncture points.

V012-N01599.label: 穴位注射
V012-N01599.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01599.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01599.definitions: A therapeutic method to inject fluid, sometimes medicated, into acupuncture points.

V012-N01600.label: 第二掌骨侧针法
V012-N01600.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01600.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01600.pinyinNames: dì èr zhǎng gǔ cè zhēn fǎ
V012-N01600.definitions: A method to stimulate the area lateral to the second metacarpal bone with an acupuncture needle. This method is based on micro-system theory that regards the second metacarpal bone as the whole body.

V012-N01601.label: 管针进针法
V012-N01601.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01601.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01601.englishNames: insertion of needle with tube; Guide tube-needle insertion
V012-N01601.pinyinNames: guǎn zhēn jìn zhēn fǎ; guan zhen jin zhen fa
V012-N01601.definitions: A method to insert a needle using a fine tube as a guide.

V012-N01602.label: 经刺
V012-N01602.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01602.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01602.englishNames: meridian needling; Jing needling; Meridian needling
V012-N01602.definitions: A technique to acupuncture stasis/tenderness points along the pathways of meridians.

V012-N01603.label: 经外奇穴
V012-N01603.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01603.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01603.definitions: The points that are not contained in the above-mentioned fourteen meridians; however, these points have specific names and locations.

V012-N01604.label: 经穴
V012-N01604.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01604.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01604.englishNames: channel point; meridian point; Meridian points
V012-N01604.definitions: Acupuncture points of the fourteen meridians (i.e. twelve regular meridians + Du meridian + Ren meridian).

V012-N01605.label: 经穴[经络穴位]
V012-N01605.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01605.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01605.definitions: The point at which the qi flows is known as the Jing-river. The Jing-river points are located around the joints of the wrists or ankles.

V012-N01606.label: 经络
V012-N01606.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01606.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01606.definitions: Meridians and collaterals are pathways for qi and blood to flow throughout the body. They form a comprehensive network linking the tissues and internal organs into an organic whole.

V012-N01607.label: 经络现象
V012-N01607.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01607.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01607.englishNames: meridian phenomenon; channel phenomenon; me ridian phenomenon; The meridian phenomena
V012-N01607.definitions: Specific disturbance of sensation, transmission of sensation and visible changes in skin colour and quality along the pathways of meridians.

V012-N01608.label: 经络腧穴类
V012-N01608.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01608.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01609.label: 络刺
V012-N01609.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01609.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01609.englishNames: Luo needling; Collateral needling; collateral needling
V012-N01609.definitions: A technique to prick small vessels with a three-edged needle for bloodletting.

V012-N01610.label: 络穴
V012-N01610.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01610.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01610.definitions: The points where a meridian exits off from the main flow and connects with the Yin/Yang paired meridian.

V012-N01611.label: 络脉
V012-N01611.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01611.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01611.definitions: Collaterals are branches of meridians, including minute collaterals, blood collaterals and superficial collaterals.

V012-N01612.label: 缪刺
V012-N01612.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01612.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01612.englishNames: contralateral collateral needling; Miu needling; Contralateral blood-letting
V012-N01612.definitions: A technique to conduct blood-letting on the left side if the right side is affected, or vice versa.

V012-N01613.label: 耳穴
V012-N01613.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01613.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01613.definitions: Specific stimulation areas distributed on the auricles.

V012-N01614.label: 耳针疗法
V012-N01614.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01614.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01614.englishNames: ear acupuncture therapy; earacupuncture therapy; Ear acupuncture
V012-N01614.pinyinNames: dong zhen liao fa; ěr zhēn liáo fǎ
V012-N01614.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the ear with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01615.label: 肺系[针灸]
V012-N01615.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01615.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01615.definitions: The trachea and throat. The word “xi” means connecting tissues or structures.

V012-N01616.label: 胃之大络
V012-N01616.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01616.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01616.englishNames: The great collateral of the stomach
V012-N01616.definitions: The major collateral of the stomach begins from Xuli (the pulsation at the apex of the heart), passes through the diaphragm, connects with the lung and emerges from the left breast.

V012-N01617.label: 背俞穴
V012-N01617.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01617.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01617.definitions: The points on the back where qi of the respective zang–fu organs is infused.

V012-N01618.label: 脊背针疗法
V012-N01618.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01618.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01618.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the back with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01619.label: 脾之大络
V012-N01619.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01619.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01619.englishNames: The great collateral of the spleen
V012-N01619.definitions: The major collateral of the spleen begins from Dabao (SP 21), emerges at 3 cun below Yuanye (GB 22) and spreads over the chest and subcostal area.

V012-N01620.label: 腕踝针疗法
V012-N01620.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01620.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01620.englishNames: Wrist-ankle acupuncture; wrist-ankle needle therapy; wrist-ankle acupuncture therapy
V012-N01620.pinyinNames: wàn huái zhēn liáo fǎ; wan huai zhen liao fa
V012-N01620.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the wrist and ankle subcutaneously with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01621.label: 腧穴
V012-N01621.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01621.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01621.definitions: The specific sites where the qi and blood of the zang–fu organs and meridians are transported to the surface of the body. They are pathological reaction points and, at the same time, acupuncture stimulation points.

V012-N01622.label: 腧穴定位法
V012-N01622.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01622.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01622.englishNames: Methods of locating acupuncture points; method of point location
V012-N01622.definitions: The acupuncture points can be located according to the anatomical landmarks, proportional bone measurement, and finger measurement, etc.

V012-N01623.label: 腹针疗法
V012-N01623.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01623.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01623.englishNames: abdomen needle therapy; abdominal acupuncture therapy; Abdominal acupuncture
V012-N01623.pinyinNames: fu zhen liao fa; fù zhēn liáo fǎ
V012-N01623.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate specific abdominal points with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01624.label: 舌针疗法
V012-N01624.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01624.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01625.label: 舒张进针法
V012-N01625.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01625.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01625.englishNames: skin-spreading needle insertion; skin spreading needle insertion; Skin-stretching insertion; skin stretching needle inserting
V012-N01625.pinyinNames: shu zhang jin zhen fa; shū zhāng jìn zhēn fǎ
V012-N01625.definitions: A method to stretch the skin around the puncturing area with the thumb and index finger (or the index and middle fingers) of the pressing hand while inserting a needle.

V012-N01626.label: 艾条
V012-N01626.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01626.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01626.definitions: A cigar-shaped roll of mugwort wrapped in cotton paper.

V012-N01627.label: 艾条灸
V012-N01627.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01627.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01627.englishNames: moxibustion with moxa stick; Moxa stick moxibustion; moxa stick moxibustion
V012-N01627.pinyinNames: ai tiao jiu; ài tiáo jiǔ
V012-N01627.definitions: A method of indirect moxibustion where an ignited moxa stick or roll passes over a selected area without directly touching the surface to warm the area.

V012-N01628.label: 艾炷
V012-N01628.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01628.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01628.definitions: Cone-shaped mass made of moxa wool

V012-N01629.label: 艾炷灸
V012-N01629.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01629.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01629.englishNames: moxa cone moxibustion; Moxa cone moxibustion; moxacone moxibustion
V012-N01629.definitions: A method to place a moxa cone directly or indirectly to the selected area and then ignite.

V012-N01630.label: 艾绒
V012-N01630.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01630.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01630.englishNames: moxa floss; Moxa wool; moxa wool
V012-N01630.definitions: Processed moxa leaves that concentrates the white hairs on the underside of the leaf.

V012-N01631.label: 芒针
V012-N01631.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01631.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01631.definitions: A therapeutic method to puncture one point towards another one subcutaneously using a long, thready needle like wheat awn. It derives from the Long Needle (one of the nine ancient needles).

V012-N01632.label: 荥穴
V012-N01632.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01632.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01632.definitions: The point at which the qi glides is known as the Ying-spring. The Ying-spring points are generally located distal to the metacarpophalangeal or metatarsophalangeal joints.

V012-N01633.label: 药罐
V012-N01633.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01633.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01633.definitions: A boiling cup method to boil bamboo cups in medicinal liquid and then apply to the body.

V012-N01634.label: 药艾条
V012-N01634.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01634.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01634.englishNames: medicinal moxa stick; Medicinal moxa stick
V012-N01634.pinyinNames: yao ai tiao; yào ài tiáo

V012-N01635.label: 蜂针疗法
V012-N01635.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01635.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01635.englishNames: Bee-sting therapy; bee sting and verom therapy
V012-N01635.pinyinNames: fēng zhēn liáo fǎ; feng zhen liao fa
V012-N01635.definitions: A therapy to use the sting from a live bee (mechanical stimulation) and venom (pharmacological action) in the treatment of health conditions.

V012-N01636.label: 血海
V012-N01636.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01636.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01636.definitions: The Chong meridian is known as “the sea of blood (sea of the twelve meridians)”.

V012-N01637.label: 血络
V012-N01637.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01637.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01637.definitions: Superficial vessels visible to the naked eye.

V012-N01638.label: 行针
V012-N01638.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01638.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01638.englishNames: needle manipulation; manipulating needle; Needling manipulation
V012-N01638.definitions: Manipulating the needle after insertion to produce the desired effect.

V012-N01639.label: 表里（经）配穴法
V012-N01639.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01639.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01639.englishNames: Point combination of the coupled meridians
V012-N01639.definitions: A method to combine points from the internally-externally connected meridians.

V012-N01640.label: 豹文刺
V012-N01640.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01640.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01640.englishNames: Leopard-spot needling; leopard-spot needling; Baowen needling
V012-N01640.pinyinNames: bào wén cì; bao wen ci
V012-N01640.definitions: A technique characterized by pricking with a three-edged needle around the point, causing leopard spot-like haemorrhagic spots. It is often used for heart-related problems.

V012-N01641.label: 贴棉法
V012-N01641.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01641.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01641.englishNames: cotton-burning cupping method; Cotton sticking; burning cotton method; cotton fire cupping
V012-N01641.definitions: A method to stick an alcoholic cotton ball on the inner wall (the lower 1/3), ignite the cotton ball and quickly place the cup over the selected area.

V012-N01642.label: 赞刺
V012-N01642.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01642.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01642.englishNames: Zan needling; Repeated shallow needling; repeated shallow needling
V012-N01642.definitions: A technique to repeatedly puncture the shallow areas to cause bloodletting. Often used for carbuncles or abscesses.

V012-N01643.label: 赤白肉际
V012-N01643.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01643.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01643.aliases: Junction of the change in skin colour
V012-N01643.englishNames: The junction between the red and white skin
V012-N01643.definitions: The boundary between the palmar aspect and dorsal aspect of the hands and feet. The skin on the palm aspect appears white, whereas the skin on the dorsal aspect appears red or brown. It is often used as an anatomical landmark to locate certain points.

V012-N01644.label: 走罐法
V012-N01644.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01644.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01644.aliases: Moving cupping； 推罐法、行罐法
V012-N01644.pinyinNames: zǒu guàn fǎ; zou guan fa
V012-N01644.definitions: A method to lubricate the cup mouth and selected area first and then slide the cup up and down while retaining its suction.

V012-N01645.label: 足三阳经
V012-N01645.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01645.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01645.definitions: The three foot-yang meridians are stomach meridian, bladder meridian and gallbladder meridian.

V012-N01646.label: 足三阴经
V012-N01646.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01646.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01646.definitions: The three foot-yin meridians are spleen meridian, kidney meridian and liver meridian.

V012-N01647.label: 足厥阴肝经
V012-N01647.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01647.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01647.definitions: The liver meridian pertains to the liver and connects with the gallbladder. It mainly travels along the medial aspect of the leg, passes over the chest and abdomen, and its divergent meridian reaches the vertex.

V012-N01648.label: 足太阳膀胱经
V012-N01648.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01648.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01648.pinyinNames: zú tài yáng páng guāng jīng
V012-N01648.definitions: The bladder meridian pertains to the urinary bladder and connects with the kidney. It is mainly distributed over the head, back and along the back of the leg.

V012-N01649.label: 足太阴脾经
V012-N01649.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01649.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01649.definitions: The spleen meridian pertains to the spleen and connects with the stomach. It mainly travels along the anterior border of the medial aspect of the leg and passes through the chest and abdomen.

V012-N01650.label: 足少阳胆经
V012-N01650.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01650.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01650.definitions: The gallbladder meridian pertains to the gallbladder and connects with the liver. It is mainly distributed over the head, sides of the head, chest, and abdomen and descends down to the lateral aspect of the leg.

V012-N01651.label: 足少阴肾经
V012-N01651.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01651.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
