V012-N01651.definitions: The kidney meridian pertains to the kidney and connects with the urinary bladder. It mainly travels along the posterior border of the medial aspect of the leg and passes through the chest and abdomen, finally terminating at the root of the tongue.

V012-N01652.label: 足针疗法
V012-N01652.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01652.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01652.englishNames: Foot acupuncture; foot needle therapy; foot acupuncture therapy
V012-N01652.pinyinNames: zú zhēn liáo fǎ; zu zhen liao fa
V012-N01652.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the foot with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01653.label: 足阳明胃经
V012-N01653.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01653.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01653.definitions: The stomach meridian pertains to the stomach and connects with the spleen. It starts directly below the pupil between the eyeball and the infraorbital ridge. Running downward along the lateral side of the nose, to the lateral corner of the mouth. Curving posterior to the anterior angle of the mandible. Then it travels to the posterior aspect of the mandible ascending in front of the ear and following the anterior hairline, it reaches the forehead. It then runs along the throat and enters the supraclavicular fossa. The straight line of the meridian separates the supraclavicular fossa and runs downward along the middle mammillary line. It travels to the side of the umbilicus and descends to the inguinal groove. Running downward it travels along the anterior aspect of the thigh and reaches the knee. From there is continues further down along the anterior border of the lateral aspect of the tibia to the dorsum of the foot and reaches the lateral side of the tip of the second toe.

V012-N01654.label: 输刺（九刺）
V012-N01654.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01654.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01654.englishNames: Shu needling; Shu needling (one of the nine needling techniques)
V012-N01654.definitions: A technique to puncture the Ying-spring point and Shu-stream point of the meridian involved and the back-Shu points of five zang organs.

V012-N01655.label: 输刺（五刺）
V012-N01655.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01655.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01655.englishNames: Shu needling; Shu needling (one of the five needling techniques)
V012-N01655.definitions: A technique characterized by deep puncture directly to the bone. It is often used for kidney-related problems.

V012-N01656.label: 输刺（十二刺）
V012-N01656.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01656.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01656.englishNames: Shu needling (one of the twelve needling techniques)
V012-N01656.definitions: A technique to insert a needle perpendicularly to the deep area to wait for needling sensation and then slowly withdraw the needle when the needling sensation is present. Used to clear pathogenic heat.

V012-N01657.label: 输穴
V012-N01657.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01657.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01657.definitions: The point at which the qi pours through is known as the Shu-stream. The Shu-stream points are generally located proximal to the metacarpophalangeal joints or metatarsophalangeal joints.

V012-N01658.label: 迎随补泻
V012-N01658.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01658.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01658.englishNames: directional supplementation and draining; Directional reinforcing and reducing
V012-N01658.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce or reduce by going against or following the pathways of meridians.

V012-N01659.label: 近部取穴
V012-N01659.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01659.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01659.englishNames: selection of adjacent points; Selection of local points; neighboring point selection; selection of adjacent point
V012-N01659.pinyinNames: jìn bù qǔ xué; jin bu qu xue
V012-N01659.definitions: A method to choose points at or close to the affected body area.

V012-N01660.label: 远道刺
V012-N01660.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01660.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01660.englishNames: Yuandao needling; distant needling; Distal needling
V012-N01660.pinyinNames: yuan dao ci; yuǎn dào cì
V012-N01660.definitions: A technique to puncture points on the lower body (i.e. the lower He-sea points of the six fu organs) to treat problems of the upper body.

V012-N01661.label: 远部取穴
V012-N01661.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01661.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01661.definitions: A method to choose points away from the affected body area.

V012-N01662.label: 透刺
V012-N01662.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01662.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01662.definitions: A method to insert a needle from one point towards another point (or more points).

V012-N01663.label: 透天凉
V012-N01663.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01663.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01663.definitions: A complex reducing manipulation to induce local or general cool sensation. The point to be needled is divided into three levels: shallow (heaven), intermediate (man) and deep (earth). Insert the needle to the deep level first and then lift to the intermediate and shallow levels, coupled with rapid lifting and slow thrusting for a total of six times in each level. Repeat the procedure three times and leave the needle in the shallow level.

V012-N01664.label: 郄穴
V012-N01664.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01664.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01664.definitions: The points where meridian qi is deeply converged.

V012-N01665.label: 金质针
V012-N01665.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01665.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01666.label: 鍉针
V012-N01666.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01666.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01666.englishNames: spoon needle; spoon-like needle; Spoon-like needle
V012-N01666.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a big, thick needle body and a slightly sharp round tip. It is often used for blood problems and heat pattern.

V012-N01667.label: 针体（针身）
V012-N01667.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01667.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01667.definitions: The part between the tip and the handle.

V012-N01668.label: 针刺异常情况
V012-N01668.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01668.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01668.englishNames: abnormal occurrence during acupuncture; Acupuncture-related adverse events
V012-N01668.pinyinNames: zhen ci yi chang qing kuang; zhēn cì yì cháng qíng kuàng
V012-N01668.definitions: Acupuncture-related adverse events include fainting, stuck needle, bent needle and broken needle. These accidents are often caused by a weak constitution, nervousness, hunger, inappropriate body position or medical negligence.

V012-N01669.label: 针刺性气胸
V012-N01669.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01669.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01669.definitions: Pneumothorax induced by inappropriate acupuncture needling that punctures the lung.

V012-N01670.label: 针刺方法
V012-N01670.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01670.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01671.label: 针刺类
V012-N01671.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01671.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01672.label: 针刺血肿
V012-N01672.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01672.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01672.definitions: Localized swelling, distension and pain from bleeding in the tissue after needle withdrawal.

V012-N01673.label: 针刺补泻
V012-N01673.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01673.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01673.aliases: Tonifying and sedating manipulation； 补泻手法
V012-N01673.englishNames: reinforcing and reducing manipulations of acupuncture therapy; Reinforcing and reducing manipulation; reinforcing and reducing manipulations of needle
V012-N01673.pinyinNames: zhēn cì bǔ xiè; zhen ci bu xie
V012-N01673.definitions: Needling manipulations to reinforce healthy qi for deficiency pattern and to remove pathogenic factors for excess pattern.

V012-N01674.label: 针刺角度
V012-N01674.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01674.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01674.englishNames: angle of needle insertion; Angle of insertion; needling angle
V012-N01674.pinyinNames: zhen ci jiao dong; zhēn cì jiǎo dù; zhen ci jiao du
V012-N01674.definitions: The angle formed between the needle body and the skin while the needle is being inserted.

V012-N01675.label: 针尖
V012-N01675.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01675.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01675.definitions: The sharpest part of a needle.

V012-N01676.label: 针感
V012-N01676.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01676.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01676.englishNames: needle sensation; needling sensation; Needling sensation
V012-N01676.definitions: Typical sensations of soreness, numbness, distension and heaviness felt around the needle by the patient.

V012-N01677.label: 针柄
V012-N01677.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01677.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01677.definitions: The part of the needle held by an acupuncturist.

V012-N01678.label: 针根
V012-N01678.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01678.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01678.definitions: The demarcation line between the handle and the body.

V012-N01679.label: 针灸配穴
V012-N01679.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01679.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01679.definitions: The selection of different points in acupuncture treatment.

V012-N01680.label: 铍针
V012-N01680.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01680.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01680.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a (double-edged) sword-like needle tip and is often used to cut open the abscesses to drain pus and blood.

V012-N01681.label: 银质针
V012-N01681.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01681.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01681.definitions: Acupuncture needles made of silver. They are often used in warming needle because of their good thermal and electrical conduction.

V012-N01682.label: 锋针
V012-N01682.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01682.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01682.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It is also known as the three-edged needle. It is often used to prick subcutaneous veins or small vessels in the treatment of abscess, heat pattern, acute gastroenteritis, etc.

V012-N01683.label: 镵针
V012-N01683.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01683.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01683.englishNames: shear needle; arrowhead-like needle; Shear needle
V012-N01683.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has an arrow-shaped head and often used for superficial insertion in the treatment of heat pattern or skin conditions.

V012-N01684.label: 长针
V012-N01684.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01684.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01684.definitions: One of the nine ancient needles. It has a longer needle body (approximately 20–30 cm or longer) and is often used for deep insertion in the treatment of chronic rheumatism or sciatica.

V012-N01685.label: 闪火法
V012-N01685.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01685.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01685.englishNames: Fire twinkling; flash-fire cupping method; fire twinkling method; flash fire cupping
V012-N01685.definitions: A method to ignite an alcoholic cotton ball held with a forceps, place it inside the cup, quickly turn it around in one to two circles, take it out immediately, and place the cup on the selected area.

V012-N01686.label: 闪罐法
V012-N01686.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01686.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01686.pinyinNames: shan guan fa; shǎn guàn fǎ
V012-N01686.definitions: A method to rapidly place and remove the cup repeatedly over the selected area until the local skin becomes red.

V012-N01687.label: 间接灸
V012-N01687.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01687.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01687.aliases: 间隔灸； 隔物灸； 间隔灸，隔物灸； Insulated moxibustion
V012-N01687.pinyinNames: jian jie jiu; jiàn jiē jiǔ
V012-N01687.definitions: A method to place some insulated materials between the moxa cone and the skin.

V012-N01688.label: 阳维脉
V012-N01688.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01688.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01688.definitions: The Yangwei meridian is mainly distributed below the external malleolus, the lateral aspect of the leg, and over the subcostal area, shoulder and sides of the head. It meets with the Du meridian at Fengfu (Du 16) and Yamen (Du 15).

V012-N01689.label: 阳脉之海
V012-N01689.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01689.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01689.definitions: The Du meridian governs meridian qi of all yang meridians and is therefore known as “the sea of yang meridians”.

V012-N01690.label: 阳蹻脉
V012-N01690.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01690.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01690.definitions: The Yangqiao meridian is mainly distributed over the heel, lateral aspect of the leg, thigh, subcostal area, shoulder, head and eyes.

V012-N01691.label: 阴刺
V012-N01691.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01691.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01691.definitions: A technique to puncture bilateral Taixi (KI 3) (the Yuan-primordial point of the kidney meridian) simultaneously. Used to treat cold limbs.

V012-N01692.label: 阴维脉
V012-N01692.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01692.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01692.definitions: The Yinwei meridian is mainly distributed over the medial aspect of the leg and thigh and ascends along the third lateral line of the abdomen. It reaches the throat and meets with the Ren meridian at Tiantu (Ren 22) and Lianquan (Ren 23).

V012-N01693.label: 阴脉之海
V012-N01693.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01693.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01693.definitions: The Ren meridian governs meridian qi of all yin meridians and is therefore known as “the sea of yin meridians”.

V012-N01694.label: 阴蹻脉
V012-N01694.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01694.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01694.definitions: The Yinqiao meridian is mainly distributed over the heel, the medial aspect of the leg, perineum, abdomen, chest, head, and eyes.

V012-N01695.label: 阿是穴
V012-N01695.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01695.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01695.definitions: The points of sensitivity or tenderness. They are neither meridians points nor extra points, and they do not have specific names or locations.

V012-N01696.label: 随证取穴
V012-N01696.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01696.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01696.definitions: A method to choose points according to differentiated pattern.

V012-N01697.label: 隔姜灸
V012-N01697.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01697.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01697.englishNames: ginger interposed moxibustion; moxibustion on ginger; Ginger-insulated moxibustion
V012-N01697.pinyinNames: ge jiang jiu; gé jiāng jiǔ
V012-N01697.definitions: A method to place a fresh ginger slice between the moxa cone and the skin.

V012-N01698.label: 隔盐灸
V012-N01698.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01698.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01698.englishNames: moxibustion on salt; salt interposed moxibustion; Salt-insulated moxibustion
V012-N01698.pinyinNames: ge yan jiu; gé yán jiǔ
V012-N01698.definitions: A method to place some salt (usually at Shenque (Ren 8)) between the moxa cone and the skin.

V012-N01699.label: 隔蒜灸
V012-N01699.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01699.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01699.englishNames: moxibustion on garlic; garlic interposed moxibustion; Garlic-insulated moxibustion
V012-N01699.pinyinNames: ge suan jiu; gé suàn jiǔ
V012-N01699.definitions: A method to place a fresh garlic slice or mashed garlic between the moxa cone and the skin.

V012-N01700.label: 隔附子饼灸
V012-N01700.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01700.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01700.aliases: 附子灸、隔附子灸； Monkshood moxibustion
V012-N01700.englishNames: monkshood cake interposed moxibustion; Monkshood cake-insulated moxibustion
V012-N01700.pinyinNames: ge fu zi bing jiu; gé fù zǐ bǐng jiǔ
V012-N01700.definitions: A method to place a monkshood cake between the moxa cone and the skin.

V012-N01701.label: 雀啄灸
V012-N01701.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01701.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01701.englishNames: pecking sparrow moxibustion; sparrow pecking-like moxibustion; Sparrow-pecking moxibustion
V012-N01701.pinyinNames: que zhuo jiu; què zhuó jiǔ
V012-N01701.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to place an ignited moxa stick near the selected area and move it up and down like a pecking bird.

V012-N01702.label: 雷火神针
V012-N01702.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01702.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01702.englishNames: thunderfire miraculous moxa roll; thunder-fire wonder moxibustion; Thunder-fire moxa stick
V012-N01702.pinyinNames: lei huo shen zhen; léi huǒ shén zhēn
V012-N01702.definitions: An indirect moxibustion method to apply an ignited moxa stick to the selected area. The moxa stick is made of fine powder of Chen Xiang (Lignum Aquilariae Resinatum), Mu Xiang (Radix Aucklandiae), Ru Xiang (Olibanum), Yin Chen (Herba Artemisiae Scopariae), Qiang Huo (Rhizoma et Radix Notopterygii), Gan Jiang (Rhizoma Zingiberis), Chuan Shan Jia (Squama Manitis), and She Xiang(Moschus).

V012-N01703.label: 震颤法
V012-N01703.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01703.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01703.englishNames: needlebody trembling; trembling method; Vibrating method
V012-N01703.pinyinNames: zhen chan fa; zhèn chàn fǎ
V012-N01703.definitions: A needling manipulation to hold the handle of the needle and make a small-amplitude and rapid lifting and thrusting to cause the needle to vibrate up and down to promote the flow of qi.

V012-N01704.label: 面针疗法
V012-N01704.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01704.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01704.englishNames: Facial acupuncture; face acupuncture therapy; facial acupuncture therapy
V012-N01704.pinyinNames: miàn zhēn liáo fǎ; mian zhen liao fa
V012-N01704.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the face with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01705.label: 飞法
V012-N01705.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01705.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01705.englishNames: needlehandle twisting; needle-handle twisting method; Flying method
V012-N01705.definitions: A needling manipulation to twist the needle handle with the thumb and index finger for a couple of times and release the fingers. The repeated twisting and releasing resembles a bird spreading its wings.

V012-N01706.label: 骨度分寸法
V012-N01706.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01706.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01706.definitions: A method to use landmarks on the body surface, primarily joints, to measure the length and width of various parts of the body. Then the width or length is divided respectively into definite numbers of equal units as the standards for the proportional measurement.

V012-N01707.label: 髓海
V012-N01707.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01707.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01707.definitions: The brain is known as “the sea of marrow”.

V012-N01708.label: 麦粒灸
V012-N01708.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01708.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01708.englishNames: Wheat-grain sized cone moxibustion; wheat-grain size cone moxibustion; moxibustion with seedsized moxa cone
V012-N01708.pinyinNames: mài lì jiǔ; mai li jiu
V012-N01708.definitions: A direct moxibustion method using a wheat grain size moxa cone.

V012-N01709.label: 鼻针疗法
V012-N01709.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01709.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01709.englishNames: Nose acupuncture; nose acupuncture therapy; nose needle therapy
V012-N01709.pinyinNames: bí zhēn liáo fǎ; bi zhen liao fa
V012-N01709.definitions: A therapeutic method to stimulate points on the nose with an acupuncture needle.

V012-N01710.label: 齐刺
V012-N01710.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜治则、治法与疗法｜针灸类（含拔罐、刮痧）
V012-N01710.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01710.englishNames: triple needling; Triple needling; Qi needling
V012-N01710.definitions: A technique to insert one needle perpendicularly, coupled with two more needles by its sides. Used to remove cold in a small, deeper area.

V012-N01711.label: 诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N01711.parentName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01711.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01711.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01711.englishNames: Terminologies on diagnosis, patterns and constitution

V012-N01712.label: 体质
V012-N01712.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N01712.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01712.definitions: Relatively stable structural, physiological and psychological characteristics of a human individual.

V012-N01713.label: 土形人
V012-N01713.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01713.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01713.definitions: According to the classification of five-element personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with an earth personality shows more characteristics of earth.

V012-N01714.label: 太阳人
V012-N01714.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01714.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01714.definitions: According to the classification of yin and yang personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a Taiyang personality shows more yang characteristics.

V012-N01715.label: 太阴人
V012-N01715.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01715.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01715.definitions: According to the classification of yin and yang personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a Taiyin personality shows more yin characteristics.

V012-N01716.label: 少阳人
V012-N01716.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01716.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01716.definitions: According to the classification of yin and yang personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a Shaoyang personality shows less yang characteristics.

V012-N01717.label: 少阴人
V012-N01717.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01717.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01717.definitions: According to the classification of yin and yang personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a Shaoyin personality shows less yin characteristics.

V012-N01718.label: 木形人
V012-N01718.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01718.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01718.definitions: According to the classification of five-element personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a wood personality shows more characteristics of wood.

V012-N01719.label: 水形人
V012-N01719.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01719.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01719.definitions: According to the classification of five-element personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a water personality shows more characteristics of water.

V012-N01720.label: 火形人
V012-N01720.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01720.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01720.definitions: According to the classification of five-element personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a fire personality shows more characteristics of fire.

V012-N01721.label: 金形人
V012-N01721.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01721.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01721.definitions: According to the classification of five-element personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a metal personality shows more characteristics of metal.

V012-N01722.label: 阴阳平和人
V012-N01722.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜体质
V012-N01722.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01722.definitions: According to the classification of yin and yang personality in Huangdi Neijing, an individual with a yin-yang harmony personality shows balanced yin and yang.

V012-N01723.label: 四诊
V012-N01723.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N01723.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01723.englishNames: Four Diagnostic Methods; Four diagnostic methods; four diagnostic methods; four examinations

V012-N01724.label: 三部九候诊法
V012-N01724.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01724.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01724.englishNames: Nine Reading on Three Sections Method; Overall pulse diagnosis
V012-N01724.pinyinNames: san bu jiu hou zhen fa
V012-N01724.definitions: A diagnostic method to feel three parts and nine depths. The three parts are head, hand and feet. Each part has three depths (heaven, earth and man), and thus there are a total of nine divisions.

V012-N01725.label: 三部九候遍诊法
V012-N01725.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01725.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01725.englishNames: Diagnosis methods using the three positions and nine indicators
V012-N01725.pinyinNames: sān bù jiǔ hòu biàn zhěn fǎ
V012-N01725.definitions: Pulse examination on three regions of the body: the head, upper limbs and lower limbs. Each region is sub-divided into upper, middle and lower sections. Altogether they form nine pulse-taking positions.

V012-N01726.label: 三部诊法
V012-N01726.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01726.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01726.definitions: A diagnostic method used to feel three arteries: carotid artery at Renyin (ST 9), radial artery at Cunkou and dorsal artery of the foot at Chongyang (ST 42). The Cunkou area is felt for conditions of the twelve regular meridians; the Renyin (ST 9) and Chongyang (ST 42) are felt for stomach qi. Alternatively, Taixi (KI 3) is felt for kidney qi.

V012-N01727.label: 中指定关
V012-N01727.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01727.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01727.englishNames: Locate the Guan position with the middle finger
V012-N01727.definitions: A doctor places the middle finger on the Guan position (the medial side of the styloid process) for pulse examination.

V012-N01728.label: 主色
V012-N01728.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01728.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01728.definitions: The colour one was born with. It remains throughout life. It is determined by race and prenatal factors.

V012-N01729.label: 举、按、寻
V012-N01729.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01729.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01729.definitions: A method of moving and exerting different finger pressures on positions for pulse examination. Lifting is to place the fingers gently on the Cunkou artery pulsation. Pressing is to exert finger pressure to reach deeper area. Searching is to gradually increase or decrease the finger pressure, moving finger contact areas to locate the best position with obvious pulsation.

V012-N01730.label: 乏力
V012-N01730.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01730.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01730.definitions: Weariness of the body. In severe cases, individuals may experience difficulty lifting things or walking, coupled with drowsiness or lack of energy. Often results from qi deficiency or dampness.

V012-N01731.label: 二十八脉
V012-N01731.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01731.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01731.englishNames: twenty-eight pulses; twenty-eight channels; Twenty-eight pulse conditions
V012-N01731.pinyinNames: shi ba mai; èr shí bā mài
V012-N01731.definitions: 1) The 28 pulse conditions, namely, superficial (floating in other texts), deep, slow, rapid, slippery, hesitant, deficient, excessive, long, short, surging, feeble, tight, delayed, wiry, hollow, drumskin, soft, weak, scattered, thready, hidden, stirring, abrupt, knotted, intermittent and large (or swift). 2) The 28 meridians, including the 24 meridians of the both hands and feet, plus the Ren meridian, Du meridian, Yinqiao meridian and Yangqiao meridian.

V012-N01732.label: 五十动
V012-N01732.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01732.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01732.pinyinNames: wu shi dong; wǔ shí dòng
V012-N01732.definitions: When feeling the pulse, it is advisable to measure at least 50 beats to identify an abrupt or regularly/irregularly intermittent pulse.

V012-N01733.label: 五心烦热
V012-N01733.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01733.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01733.englishNames: Feverish sensations in palms, soles and chest; dysphoria with feverish sensation in chest，palms and soles
V012-N01733.definitions: A feeling of restlessness and heat sensation in the chest, along with feverish sensations in the palms and soles. This is most commonly caused by heart blood deficiency, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency or internal retention of fire heat after recovery from medical conditions.

V012-N01734.label: 五更泄泻
V012-N01734.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01734.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01734.definitions: A type of diarrhoea that occurs before dawn. It is mainly caused by kidney yang deficiency. Food retention, alcohol drinking or liver fire may also play a role.

V012-N01735.label: 人迎脉
V012-N01735.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01735.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01735.definitions: The pulsation of the carotid artery, easily felt on both sides of the laryngeal prominence.

V012-N01736.label: 代脉
V012-N01736.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01736.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01737.label: 伏脉
V012-N01737.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01737.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01737.definitions: A pulse that is difficult to feel and requires heavy pressure down to the bone. It indicates severe stagnation of pathogenic factors.

V012-N01738.label: 伪膜
V012-N01738.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01738.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01738.definitions: A yellowish or grey/white patch develops on the ulcerative surface of the throat.

V012-N01739.label: 但寒不热
V012-N01739.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01739.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01739.definitions: Aversion to cold alone without fever is mostly seen in interior cold pattern that results from yang deficiency, cold binding the Wei-defensive qi or cold directly affecting the internal organs.

V012-N01740.label: 余沥不尽
V012-N01740.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01740.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01740.definitions: A feeling of needing to pass urine after urination, a sense of incomplete emptying or urinary dribbling. Often results from cold retention in the urinary bladder due to kidney deficiency.

V012-N01741.label: 促脉
V012-N01741.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01741.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01741.definitions: A pulse that arrives as urgent and forceful but irregularly interrupted. It indicates excess heat, stagnation of qi, blood, phlegm-fluid retention or swelling/pain.

V012-N01742.label: 假神
V012-N01742.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01742.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01742.aliases: 残灯复明； 回光返照； The last radiance of the setting sun/the last flicker of life in a dying person
V012-N01742.definitions: Sudden ‘better’ signs and symptoms in patients with chronic critical conditions: a sudden change from dull eyes with sluggish eyeballs to floating brightness in the eyes; a sudden change from mental unconsciousness or listlessness to clear consciousness and mental restlessness; a sudden change from reluctance to talk and low weak voice into a talkative, clear voice; a sudden change from a grey/dark complexion to flushed cheeks; and a sudden change from no appetite and low food intake into increased appetite and hunger. The false spirit indicates collapse of healthy qi, extreme essential qi failure of zang–fu organs or imminent separation between yin and yang. This is seen as an omen for a dying person.

V012-N01743.label: 全苔
V012-N01743.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01743.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01743.definitions: A tongue coating that covers the full surface of the tongue, often indicating phlegm dampness.

V012-N01744.label: 六阳脉
V012-N01744.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01744.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01744.definitions: One of the normal pulses: constitutional surging and large on Cun, Guan and Chi positions of both hands.

V012-N01745.label: 六阴脉
V012-N01745.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01745.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01745.definitions: One of the normal pulses: constitutional deep and thready on Cun, Guan and Chi positions of both hands.

V012-N01746.label: 关脉
V012-N01746.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01746.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01747.label: 冬脉如营
V012-N01747.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01747.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01747.definitions: In winter, yang qi hides inside, and the skin pore is closed. As a result, the pulse is deep.

V012-N01748.label: 冷汗
V012-N01748.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01748.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01748.definitions: A feeling of coldness after sweating. Most commonly caused by severe deficiency of yang qi.

V012-N01749.label: 冷痛
V012-N01749.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01749.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01749.definitions: Pain with a cold sensation that alleviates with warmth and pressure but aggravates with cold. It is most commonly caused by cold affecting the meridians or yang qi failing to warm the zang–fu organs and limbs.

V012-N01750.label: 切诊
V012-N01750.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01750.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01750.englishNames: Pulse Feeling and Palpation; Palpation; pulse taking and palpation; palpation and pulse taking
V012-N01750.pinyinNames: qie zhen; qiè zhěn; qie mai
V012-N01750.definitions: A diagnostic method to feel, touch, palpate and press certain areas of the body.

V012-N01751.label: 切诊类
V012-N01751.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01751.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01752.label: 刺痛
V012-N01752.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01752.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01752.definitions: Pain as if pricked by a needle. It is most commonly caused by stagnant blood.

V012-N01753.label: 十问歌
V012-N01753.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01753.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01753.definitions: The ten questions include cold/heat, sweating, pains on the head and body, bladder/bowel movements, diet/appetite, chest, hearing (tinnitus/deafness), thirst, previous illnesses and etiological factors.

V012-N01754.label: 半身不遂
V012-N01754.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01754.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01754.definitions: An inability of one side of the body to move voluntarily. Often results from age-related weakness of the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence coupled with contraction of exogenous pathogens, blood stasis due to qi deficiency, or essential qi deficiency.

V012-N01755.label: 半身麻木
V012-N01755.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01755.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01755.definitions: Numbness or tingling sensation on one side of the body. Often results from spleen qi deficiency, qi deficiency of the spleen and lung, or obstructed flow of qi.

V012-N01756.label: 单按
V012-N01756.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01756.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01756.englishNames: Pressing with one finger; single finger palpation; pulse-taking with one finger
V012-N01756.definitions: A pulse diagnosis method to feel the pulse on one hand with just one finger to differentiate the conditions of Cun, Guan and Chi pulses. For example, to lift the index and ring fingers while feeling the Guan position with the middle finger.

V012-N01757.label: 厌食
V012-N01757.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01757.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01757.definitions: A symptom of aversion to food or the smell of food. Often results from damp heat in the spleen and stomach or in the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N01758.label: 厚苔
V012-N01758.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01758.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01758.definitions: A tongue coating through which the underlying tongue surface is not visible, often indicating turbid dampness, phlegm, food retention or heat.

V012-N01759.label: 厥阴头痛
V012-N01759.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01759.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01759.definitions: Headache that is located in the parietal region. It is caused by pathogenic factors affecting the Jueyin meridian, which meets with the Du meridian at the vertex of the head.

V012-N01760.label: 反关脉
V012-N01760.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01760.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01760.englishNames: ectopic radial pulse; Pulse on the back of the wrist
V012-N01760.definitions: A physiological variation of a normal pulse: the changes of the anatomic position of the radial artery shifts the pulse normally at the Cunkou region to the back of the wrist from the Chi position.

V012-N01761.label: 口咸
V012-N01761.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01761.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01761.englishNames: A salty taste; salty taste in mouth
V012-N01761.definitions: A subjective salty sensation in the mouth. Sometimes individuals may experience salty salivation. Often results from deficiency cold or kidney heat.

V012-N01762.label: 口唇淡白
V012-N01762.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01762.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01762.definitions: The lips appear pale and have little redness. Often results from deficiency cold or blood deficiency.

V012-N01763.label: 口唇焦裂
V012-N01763.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01763.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01763.englishNames: Chapped lips; dry and withered lips
V012-N01763.definitions: The lips appear dry, peeling or chapped. In severe cases, the lips may turn red, swollen or bleeding. Often results from heat accumulating in the spleen and stomach, insufficiency of yin fluids or dry heat consuming bodily fluids.

V012-N01764.label: 口唇红肿
V012-N01764.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01764.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01764.englishNames: reddened and swollen lips; Red, swollen lips
V012-N01764.definitions: The lips appear red and swollen. Often results from fire heat scorching the lips.

V012-N01765.label: 口唇青紫
V012-N01765.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01765.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01765.definitions: The lips appear bluish purple, pale purple or dark purple, without lustre or moisture. Often results from blood stasis.

V012-N01766.label: 口干
V012-N01766.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01766.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01766.definitions: A subjective feeling of insufficient saliva in the mouth. Often occurs when exuberant heat consumes or scorches bodily fluids.

V012-N01767.label: 口涩
V012-N01767.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01767.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01767.definitions: A subjective dry, puckering feeling in the mouth. Often results from stomach heat consuming fluids or wind phlegm obstructing the meridians.

V012-N01768.label: 口淡
V012-N01768.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01768.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01768.definitions: A diminished sensitivity of taste, with no pleasant flavour left after eating. Often occurs as a result of qi deficiency of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N01769.label: 口渴
V012-N01769.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01769.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01769.definitions: A subjective feeling of dry mouth and thirst. Often occurs as a result of exuberant heat entering the Ying nutrients and blood, internal retention of water fluids, lung dryness consuming fluids, or fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N01770.label: 口渴欲饮
V012-N01770.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01770.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01770.definitions: A dry mouth with desire to drink water often occurs when insufficient bodily fluids impair qi transformation and fluid distribution.

V012-N01771.label: 口甜
V012-N01771.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01771.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01771.englishNames: A sweet taste; sweet taste in mouth
V012-N01771.definitions: A subjective sugary or honey-like sensation. Often results from damp heat in the spleen and stomach, damage to qi and yin or deficiency heat scorching spleen fluids.

V012-N01772.label: 口疮
V012-N01772.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01772.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01772.definitions: A mouth ulcer is often painful. Often results from ascending of accumulated heat in the heart and spleen or fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N01773.label: 口眼喎斜
V012-N01773.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01773.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01773.englishNames: wry eye and mouth; Deviation of the mouth and eyes
V012-N01773.definitions: Deviation of the mouth and eyes is characterized by the deviation of the mouth corner towards the healthy side, facial muscle flaccidity on the affected side and muscle tension on the healthy side, and an inability to completely close the eye and mouth, frown or blow on the affected side. Often results from wind attacking the meridians or wind phlegm obstructing the meridians.

V012-N01774.label: 口臭
V012-N01774.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01774.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01774.definitions: A noticeably unpleasant odour is present on the breath. Often occurs when deficiency fire retains in the stomach or when one overeats greasy food.

V012-N01775.label: 口舌糜烂
V012-N01775.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01775.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01775.definitions: White ulcerations that occur on the mucous membrane of the oral cavity. Often occurs when damp heat and toxic fire in the spleen and stomach scorches the mouth.

V012-N01776.label: 口苦
V012-N01776.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01776.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01776.englishNames: A bitter taste; bitter taste in mouth
V012-N01776.definitions: A subjective bitter sensation in the mouth. Often occurs when internal heat causes the bile to ascend.

V012-N01777.label: 口辛
V012-N01777.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01777.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01777.definitions: A subjective strong hot, spicy sensation in the mouth. Often results from lung heat.

V012-N01778.label: 口酸
V012-N01778.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01778.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01778.englishNames: sour taste in mouth; A sour taste
V012-N01778.definitions: A subjective sour, vinegar-like sensation in the mouth. In severe cases, individuals may have a sour breath. Often results from ascending of liver heat, liver heat affecting the spleen or food retention.

V012-N01779.label: 口麻
V012-N01779.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01779.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01779.definitions: A subjective feeling of numbness inside the mouth. It is often caused by wind transformed from liver yang.

V012-N01780.label: 口黏腻
V012-N01780.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01780.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01780.englishNames: sticky and greasy in mouth; A sticky, greasy taste
V012-N01780.definitions: A subjective sticky, greasy sensation in the mouth. In severe cases, individuals may feel that the food has no taste. Often occurs when damp heat in the spleen and stomach or internal phlegm heat affect qi transformation of the spleen and stomach and causes the turbid qi to ascend to the mouth.

V012-N01781.label: 吐舌
V012-N01781.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01781.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01781.definitions: A tongue that hangs out of the mouth repeatedly and tip is extended fast from and retracted slowly back into the mouth, often indicating heat in the heart and spleen, epidemic toxin attacking the heart or exhaustion of anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N01782.label: 吐血
V012-N01782.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01782.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01782.definitions: Blood ejection out of the mouth without nausea, vomiting or cough. Often results from exuberant stomach heat, liver qi stagnation transforming into heat or deficiency of spleen qi and heart blood. Contributing factors may include alcohol drinking, ingestion of spicy, oily food, anger, worries or over exertion.

V012-N01783.label: 呃逆
V012-N01783.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01783.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01783.definitions: A brief, involuntary release of qi out of the throat. Often results from stomach qi failing to descend.

V012-N01784.label: 呵欠
V012-N01784.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01784.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01784.definitions: A response to fatigue or sleepiness.

V012-N01785.label: 命关
V012-N01785.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01785.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01785.definitions: One of the diagnostic sites for infantile venule of the index finger. The life gate is the area between the third transverse crease and terminal segment of the index finger. It indicates a severe medical condition.

V012-N01786.label: 咽干
V012-N01786.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01786.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01786.definitions: A subjective feeling of dryness in the throat. Often results from deficiency fire (due to yin deficiency of the liver and kidney) ascends to scorch the throat, the failure of lung yin to nourish and moisten the throat, or upward fire flaming of the lung and stomach.

V012-N01787.label: 唇反
V012-N01787.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01787.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01787.definitions: The lips become everted. Often indicates approaching exhaustion of spleen qi.

V012-N01788.label: 善色
V012-N01788.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01788.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01788.definitions: An abnormal but lustrous complexion, indicating that essential qi of zang–fu organs remain sufficient and stomach qi can ascend to the face. It is often seen in acute, mild conditions or yang pattern that can be easily treated and have a favourable prognosis.

V012-N01789.label: 嗳气
V012-N01789.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01789.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01789.definitions: The release of gas from the stomach through the mouth, often accompanied with burp sounds. Often results from weakness of the spleen and stomach or retention of phlegm, fire or food retention in the stomach, which causes stomach qi to ascend.

V012-N01790.label: 嗳腐
V012-N01790.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01790.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01790.definitions: A symptom of expelling air from the stomach through the mouth, coupled with a foul breath. Often results from weakness of the spleen and stomach or food retention due to an improper diet.

V012-N01791.label: 四肢拘急
V012-N01791.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01791.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01791.englishNames: Contracture of the four extremities; spasm of limbs
V012-N01791.definitions: Spasticity of the hands and feet with impaired flexion and extension. Often results from cold affecting the meridians or malnourishment of the extremities due to heat scorching yin fluids and resultant blood dryness.

V012-N01792.label: 囟填
V012-N01792.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01792.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01792.definitions: A noticeable outward curve of the fontanel. Often occurs when pathogenic fire in febrile diseases ascends or when wind heat or damp heat affects the brain.

V012-N01793.label: 囟陷
V012-N01793.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01793.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01793.definitions: A sunken fontanel is characterised by a noticeable inward curve. It is often caused by vomiting, diarrhoea, deficiency of qi and blood, deficiency cold of the spleen and stomach, congenital maldevelopment. It is normal for an infant under 6 months of age to have a sunken fontanel.

V012-N01794.label: 地图舌
V012-N01794.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01794.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01794.definitions: A tongue with irregular shedding of the coating and distinct demarcation between the shed and unshed areas, looking like a map, often indicating phlegm dampness due to qi deficiency or spleen deficiency coupled with parasitic worms.

V012-N01795.label: 壮热
V012-N01795.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01795.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01795.definitions: Persistent high-grade fever (>39℃) often results from internal exuberant yang heat steaming the surface of the body upon pathogenic factors entering the interior.

V012-N01796.label: 声嘶
V012-N01796.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01796.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01796.definitions: A low harsh or husky quality of the voice, often resulting from external contraction of wind cold/heat, qi and yin deficiency of the lung and kidney or impaired lung qi.

V012-N01797.label: 声怯
V012-N01797.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01797.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01797.definitions: A voice that is faint and low, scarcely audible, often resulting from deficiency of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N01798.label: 夏脉如钩
V012-N01798.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01798.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01798.definitions: In summer, yang qi flourishes and everything grows. As a result, the pulse is surging, large, and forceful, like roaring waves that crash onto the shore powerfully and slowly fade away.

V012-N01799.label: 外关内格
V012-N01799.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01799.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01799.definitions: Refers to radial pulsation felt at Yuji (LU 10), indicating that yang qi is blocked outside, whereas yin qi is rejected inside.

V012-N01800.label: 多梦
V012-N01800.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01800.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01800.definitions: Interrupted sleep by strange dreams or frightening nightmares. Individuals often have a clear memory of the dreams. Often results from hyperactivity of liver yang, deficiency of qi and blood or yin deficiency of the liver and kidney.

V012-N01801.label: 大脉
V012-N01801.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01801.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01801.definitions: A pulse that is broad and can be felt on all three fingers. It indicates pathogenic factors prevailing over anti-pathogenic qi. A large, forceful pulse indicates an excess heat pattern; whereas a large, weak pulse indicates a deficiency pattern or loss of qi.

V012-N01802.label: 太息
V012-N01802.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01802.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01802.definitions: An act of exhaling audibly in a long deep breath, as in weariness or relief. Often results from emotional disturbance and liver qi stagnation.

V012-N01803.label: 太阳头痛
V012-N01803.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01803.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01803.definitions: Headache that radiates to the occipital region. It is caused by pathogenic factors affecting the Taiyang meridian, which enters the brain from the vertex and comes out of the back of the neck.

V012-N01804.label: 太阴头痛
V012-N01804.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01804.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01804.definitions: Headache that often combines with drowsiness, abdominal diarrhoea and spontaneous sweating. It is caused by failure of spleen yang to ascend due to pathogenic factors affecting the Taiyin meridian.

V012-N01805.label: 失神
V012-N01805.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01805.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01805.englishNames: Loss of spirit; loss of vitality
V012-N01805.definitions: Dull eyes with sluggish eyeballs, listlessness or unconsciousness, apathetic expression, slow response, a grey/dark complexion lacking lustre, weight loss and difficult body movements. The loss of spirit indicates severe damage to healthy qi and essential qi failure of zang–fu organs.

V012-N01806.label: 头摇
V012-N01806.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01806.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01806.englishNames: head tremor; Shaking of the head
V012-N01806.definitions: Uncontrolled, involuntary shaking or tremor of the head. Often results from internal wind or deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N01807.label: 头汗
V012-N01807.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01807.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01807.definitions: Excessive sweating on the face and head. Often occurs when retained pathogenic heat ascends to heat the head and face.

V012-N01808.label: 头胀
V012-N01808.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01808.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01808.definitions: A subjective sensation of distending or splitting headache. It is most commonly caused by external damp heat or internal dysfunctions.

V012-N01809.label: 头重
V012-N01809.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01809.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01809.englishNames: Heavy headache; heavy sensation of head
V012-N01809.definitions: Headache with a subjective heavy, bearing down sensation or feeling like being wrapped up in a towel. It is most commonly caused by external dampness, phlegm dampness, pestilence qi, or kidney qi deficiency.

V012-N01810.label: 头重如裹
V012-N01810.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01810.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01810.englishNames: Head heaviness as if being wrapped up
V012-N01810.definitions: A subjective feeling of a bearing down sensation of the head or feeling like a tight band around the head. Often results from phlegm dampness obstructing the head or external contraction of dampness or pestilence.

V012-N01811.label: 头项强痛
V012-N01811.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01811.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01811.englishNames: Headache with neck rigidity; rigidity of nape and headache
V012-N01811.definitions: Pain, contracture and discomfort in the head and neck. Often occurs when wind cold affecting the surface of the body or impaired qi flow of Taiyang meridian.

V012-N01812.label: 妊娠脉
V012-N01812.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01812.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01812.definitions: A pulse characterized by slippery and rapid on the Chi positions, because the Chi position corresponds to the kidney and the uterus is associated with the kidney. A slippery, rapid pulse results from moving of fetal qi.

V012-N01813.label: 嫩舌
V012-N01813.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01813.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01813.definitions: A tongue that is soft and delicate with fine texture and pale in colour, often indicating deficiency of qi and blood or internal cold dampness due to yang qi deficiency.

V012-N01814.label: 完谷不化
V012-N01814.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01814.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01814.englishNames: diarrhea with undigested food; Indigestion of food
V012-N01814.definitions: Presence of undigested food in stools. Often results from deficiency cold of the spleen and stomach or vital fire decline due to kidney deficiency.

V012-N01815.label: 实脉
V012-N01815.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01815.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01815.definitions: A pulse that is forceful at all three regions using different pressure. It indicates an excess pattern.

V012-N01816.label: 客色
V012-N01816.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01816.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01816.definitions: The colour one takes on when physiological, seasonal, climactic, geographic, diet and emotions change the colouring.

V012-N01817.label: 寒战
V012-N01817.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01817.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01817.definitions: A feeling of coldness or shivering. Often results from extreme cold or blocked heat.

V012-N01818.label: 寒热往来
V012-N01818.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01818.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N01819.label: 寸关尺
V012-N01819.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01819.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01819.englishNames: Cun, Guan and Chi; cunguanchi; inch，bar and cubit
V012-N01819.definitions: The three sections over the radial artery for feeling the pulse. Specifically, the Guan position is central to the styloid process at the wrist, where the tip of the physician’s middle finger is placed; the Chi position is next to it on the distal aspect where the tip of the physicians’ index finger is placed; and the Cun position is on the proximal aspect where the tip of the physicians’ ring finger is placed.

V012-N01820.label: 寸口诊法
V012-N01820.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01820.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01820.englishNames: Diagnosis in Cunkou area; Wrist Pulse-taking Method; wrist pulse-taking method; cun pulse-taking method
V012-N01820.pinyinNames: cùn kǒu zhěn fǎ; cun kou zhen fa
V012-N01820.definitions: A diagnostic method used to feel the pulse at the Cunkou area located at the medial aspect of the styloid process where the radial artery pulsates. The Cunkou area is divided into Cun, Guan and Chi positions. Each position is further divided into superficial, intermediate and deep depths. This method is known as three positions and three regions and nine positions in Cunkou area.

V012-N01821.label: 寸脉
V012-N01821.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01821.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01821.definitions: One of the three positions at the Cunkou area: the pulsation of the radial artery felt at the radial styloid process.

V012-N01822.label: 小便涩痛
V012-N01822.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01822.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01822.englishNames: difficulty and pain in micturition; Difficult, painful urination
V012-N01822.definitions: Difficulty passing urine accompanied with a painful sensation. Often occurs when damp heat flows downward to the urinary bladder.

V012-N01823.label: 小便频数
V012-N01823.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01823.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01823.definitions: Increased frequency in passing of urine. If often occurs when 1) kidney yang fails to perform qi transformation of the urinary bladder; 2) the spleen fails to transport and transform water; 3) the lung fails to regulate water passage; and/or 4) liver qi stagnation affects the urinary bladder.

V012-N01824.label: 小便黄赤
V012-N01824.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01824.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01824.definitions: Urine that is more yellow than usual and sometimes appears reddish. Often results from internal heat or damp heat.

V012-N01825.label: 小腹痛
V012-N01825.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01825.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01825.englishNames: lower abdominal pain; Lower abdominal pain
V012-N01825.definitions: Pain in the central area of the lower abdomen. Often results from damp heat, stagnant blood, obstructed flow of abdominal qi, or kidney deficiency.

V012-N01826.label: 少气
V012-N01826.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01826.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01826.definitions: A weak, shallow breathing and a low voice. Often results from constitutional weakness or qi deficiency of the lung and kidney due to chronic conditions.

V012-N01827.label: 少神
V012-N01827.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01827.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01827.aliases: 神气不足； Lack of spirit
V012-N01827.definitions: Dull eyes with sluggish eyeballs, poor spirit, slow response, a complexion lacking lustre, flabby muscle and slow body movements. The insufficiency of spirit indicates insufficiency of healthy qi or mild impairment to essential qi of zang–fu organs.

V012-N01828.label: 少腹拘急
V012-N01828.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01828.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01828.definitions: A subjective sensation of contracture or tension below the umbilicus. Often results from deficiency cold of kidney qi and impaired qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N01829.label: 少腹痛
V012-N01829.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01829.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01829.englishNames: Bilateral lower abdominal pain; pain in lateral lower abdomen
V012-N01829.definitions: Pain on both sides of the lower abdomen. Often occurs when the liver fails to maintain free flow of qi.

V012-N01830.label: 少阳头痛
V012-N01830.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01830.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01830.definitions: Headache that is located in the temples on both sides. It is caused by pathogenic factors affecting the Shaoyang meridian, which travels along the bilateral sides of the head.

V012-N01831.label: 少阴头痛
V012-N01831.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01831.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01831.definitions: Headache that often combines with toothache. It is caused by pathogenic factors affecting the Shaoyin meridian. In Chinese medicine, the kidney dominates bones and generates marrow to fill up the brain.

V012-N01832.label: 尺脉
V012-N01832.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01832.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01832.definitions: One of the three positions at the Cunkou area: the pulsation of the radial artery felt at the cubital side of the radial styloid process.

V012-N01833.label: 布指
V012-N01833.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01833.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01833.englishNames: arranging fingers in pulse taking; Finger positioning
V012-N01833.definitions: Positioning of fingers in pulse examination. Generally, a doctor places the middle finger of the right hand to feel the Guan position of a patient’s left hand, the index finger to feel the Cun position, the ring finger to feel the Chi position, and adjust the distances between the fingers according to the patient’s height.

V012-N01834.label: 常色
V012-N01834.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01834.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01834.definitions: With abundant qi and blood as well as functioning of zang–fu organs, a normal facial colour is moist, lustrous and non-exposed red.

V012-N01835.label: 干咳
V012-N01835.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01835.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01835.definitions: A cough with no or extremely scanty sputum. Often results from fire, dryness or lung yin deficiency.

V012-N01836.label: 平息
V012-N01836.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01836.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01836.definitions: Normal breathing frequency used in ancient times as a reference in measuring patients’ breathing frequencies.

V012-N01837.label: 平脉
V012-N01837.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01837.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01837.definitions: A pulse that can be felt at all three regions, with a frequency of 4–5 beats (60–75 beats per minute (bpm)) in one breath. It is neither superficial nor deep. It is relaxed, smooth, soft, forceful and rhythmic. It is marked by the three essential elements: stomach qi, spirit and root.

V012-N01838.label: 弄舌
V012-N01838.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01838.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01838.englishNames: frequent protrusion of tongue; Wagging tongue
V012-N01838.definitions: A tongue that frequently extends from and retracts back into the mouth and moves in a circular motion, often indicating extreme heat stirring wind.

V012-N01839.label: 弦脉
V012-N01839.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01839.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01839.definitions: A pulse that feels straight, long and tense, like the feeling of pressing a tight string of a musical instrument. It indicates liver/gallbladder problems, pain, wind, or phlegm-fluid retention.

V012-N01840.label: 弱脉
V012-N01840.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01840.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01840.definitions: A pulse that feels thready, soft, deep and weak. It indicates deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N01841.label: 得神
V012-N01841.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01841.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01841.englishNames: fullness of vitality; Presence of spirit
V012-N01841.definitions: Bright eyes with flexible eyeballs, clear consciousness, natural facial expressions, a moist lustrous face, a fit body, smooth body movements, and even breathing. The presence of spirit indicates abundant essential qi or a mild medical condition.

V012-N01842.label: 循衣摸床
V012-N01842.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01842.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01842.definitions: The actions of picking or grasping at imaginary objects, as well as the patient’s own clothes or bed linens. Often seen in delirious or semiconscious states, it can be a grave symptom in cases of extreme qi deficiency or approaching qi exhaustion.

V012-N01843.label: 微脉
V012-N01843.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01843.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01843.definitions: A pulse that is extremely thin, soft and barely palpable. It may be felt and then sometimes is lost. It indicates deficiency of yin, yang, qi and blood.

V012-N01844.label: 心下痛
V012-N01844.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01844.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N01844.definitions: Epigastric pain that is especially located in the pit of the stomach. It is often caused by internal build up of yin cold due to severe deficiency of yang qi.

V012-N01845.label: 心烦
V012-N01845.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N01845.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
