V012-N02044.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02044.definitions: Unconscious murmuring haltingly with frequent repetitions and a low, faint voice, often indicating great damage to heart qi in late-stage medical conditions.

V012-N02045.label: 酸痛
V012-N02045.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02045.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02045.definitions: A pain accompanied by a feeling of soreness and weakness. Often occurs as a result of wind cold obstructing the flow of qi and blood, kidney deficiency, or failure of qi and blood to nourish the tissues.

V012-N02046.label: 里急后重
V012-N02046.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02046.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02046.definitions: Tenesmus is characterized by abdominal pain, a feeling of constantly needing to pass stools, weight-bearing sensation of the anus and a sense of incomplete evacuation after bowel movements. Often results from damp heat obstructing intestinal qi.

V012-N02047.label: 重听
V012-N02047.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02047.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02047.definitions: A symptom of decreased sense of hearing or distorted/repeated hearing. Often results from hyperactivity of deficiency fire or pathogenic factors affecting the meridians around the ears.

V012-N02048.label: 重痛
V012-N02048.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02048.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02048.definitions: Pain with a sensation of heaviness. It is most commonly caused by dampness obstructing the flow of qi.

V012-N02049.label: 金实不鸣
V012-N02049.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02049.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02049.definitions: An expression figuratively referring to sudden hoarseness or loss of voice due to exogenous wind cold or wind heat impairing the dispersing of lung qi.

V012-N02050.label: 金破不鸣
V012-N02050.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02050.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02050.definitions: An expression figuratively referring to hoarseness in chronic or severe medical conditions due to essential qi deficiency of the lung and kidney.

V012-N02051.label: 钩脉
V012-N02051.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02051.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02051.definitions: A pulse that feels firm, surging and large. It comes powerfully and fades away slowly. It is normal in summer.

V012-N02052.label: 错语
V012-N02052.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02052.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02052.definitions: A type of dysphasia that the patient frequently employs wrong words but realizes the mistake afterwards, often resulting from heart qi deficiency, malnourishment of the heart mind or kidney essence failing to nourish the brain.

V012-N02053.label: 镜面舌
V012-N02053.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02053.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02053.definitions: A completely smooth tongue free of coating, like a mirror, often indicating stomach yin exhaustion or collapse of yang qi due to heavy loss of Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N02054.label: 长脉
V012-N02054.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02054.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02054.definitions: A pulse that can be felt beyond the Cun, Guan and Chi positions. It is straight from head to tail. It is straight and long like a pole. It indicates yang excess with internal heat.

V012-N02055.label: 问诊
V012-N02055.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02055.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02055.definitions: A method to collect information regarding the disease occurrence, progression, previous diagnosis and treatment and present symptoms by talking to the patient or the person who accompanies the patient.

V012-N02056.label: 问诊类
V012-N02056.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02056.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02057.label: 闷痛
V012-N02057.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02057.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02057.definitions: A pain accompanied by feeling of oppression. Oppressive pain is often felt in the chest. Often occurs when phlegm turbidity or damp heat obstructs the flow of qi in the upper jiao. Alternatively, it may be associated with emotions.

V012-N02058.label: 闻诊
V012-N02058.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02058.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02058.englishNames: Auscultation; listening and smelling; Listening and Smelling Examination
V012-N02058.definitions: A diagnostic method using listening to the changes in: patients’ speech, breathing, cough, internal sounds and smelling the patients’ breath and noticing body odours and discharges.

V012-N02059.label: 闻诊类
V012-N02059.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02059.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02060.label: 阳微阴弦
V012-N02060.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02060.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02060.englishNames: Feeble yang and wiry yin; weak pulse at yang and wiry pulse at yin
V012-N02060.pinyinNames: yáng wēi yīn xián; yang wei yin xian
V012-N02060.definitions: Feeble yang results from yang qi deficiency of the upper jiao. Wiry yin results from excess yin cold in the lower jiao. Yin cold obstructing chest yang may cause chest bi-impediment or cardiac pain.

V012-N02061.label: 阳斑
V012-N02061.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02061.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02061.definitions: Yang macules appear red or purple and resemble fabric lines. Individuals may have a red face and fever. They often occur when excessive heat affects the Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N02062.label: 阳明头痛
V012-N02062.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02062.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02062.definitions: Frontal headache that radiates to the supraorbital bone. It is caused by pathogenic factors affecting the Yangming meridian, which follows the hairline from the temporal region to the corner of the forehead.

V012-N02063.label: 阴斑
V012-N02063.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02063.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02063.definitions: Yin macules appear bluish purple underneath the skin. Individuals may have a pale complexion, a thready, weak pulse and cold limbs. They often result from the spleen failing to control blood within the vessels or cold retention due to yang deficiency.

V012-N02064.label: 阴汗
V012-N02064.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02064.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02064.englishNames: perineal sweating; Sweats in the genital area
V012-N02064.definitions: Excessive sweating on and around the external genitalia. Often results from damp heat in the lower jiao.

V012-N02065.label: 阴虚潮热
V012-N02065.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02065.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02065.englishNames: Tidal fever due to yin deficiency
V012-N02065.definitions: Low-grade fever in the afternoon or at night (<38℃) or bone-steaming tidal fever. Most commonly seen in yin fluids failing to contain yang and subsequently, floating of deficiency yang.

V012-N02066.label: 除中
V012-N02066.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02066.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02066.englishNames: Sudden spurt of appetitie prior to collapse
V012-N02066.definitions: A sudden increase in food intake in patients with critical conditions who had no appetite previously. Often occurs as a result of qi exhaustion of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02067.label: 隐痛
V012-N02067.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02067.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02067.definitions: Persistent, tolerable and less pronounced pain. It is most commonly caused by yang, qi, essence and blood failing to nourish the zang–fu organs and meridians.

V012-N02068.label: 霉酱苔
V012-N02068.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02068.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02068.definitions: A reddish thick tongue coating with black and yellow tinge, often indicating food retention in the stomach and intestines transforming into heat.

V012-N02069.label: 青舌
V012-N02069.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02069.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02069.definitions: A tongue of bluish colour that resembles the exposed blue veins on the skin, indicating blood stagnation due to yin cold obstructing yang qi.

V012-N02070.label: 面焦
V012-N02070.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02070.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02070.definitions: A withered, brown/dark facial complexion. Often results from stomach qi failing to ascend and nourish the face.

V012-N02071.label: 面白
V012-N02071.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02071.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02071.definitions: A white facial complexion often indicates yang qi failing to circulate blood, consumption of qi and blood or cold contracting the blood vessels.

V012-N02072.label: 面色苍白
V012-N02072.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02072.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02072.definitions: A pale facial complexion is often mixed with bluish or grey. Often results from severe abdominal pain in an interior cold pattern or insufficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02073.label: 面色萎黄
V012-N02073.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02073.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02073.definitions: A pale, yellow and withered facial complexion lacking lustre. Often results from deficiency of qi and blood due to weakness of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02074.label: 面色黧黑
V012-N02074.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02074.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02074.definitions: A dark facial complexion. Often results from kidney yang failing to transform water, internal yin cold, blood failing to nourish the body or stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02075.label: 面赤
V012-N02075.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02075.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02075.definitions: A red facial complexion often indicates heat or hyperactivity of fire due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02076.label: 面青
V012-N02076.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02076.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02076.definitions: A bluish or bluish-purple facial complexion often indicates cold, pain, qi stagnation, blood stasis or convulsion.

V012-N02077.label: 面黄
V012-N02077.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02077.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02077.definitions: A yellow complexion can be sallow or yellow coupled with puffiness. Often indicates internal water retention due to spleen deficiency or insufficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02078.label: 面黄胖
V012-N02078.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02078.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02078.definitions: A yellowish facial complexion coupled with puffiness. Often results from internal dampness due to spleen qi deficiency.

V012-N02079.label: 面黑
V012-N02079.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02079.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02079.definitions: A black facial complexion often indicates kidney yang failing to transform water or chronic blood stasis.

V012-N02080.label: 革脉
V012-N02080.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02080.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02080.definitions: A pulse that can be easily felt with gentle touch. It is superficial with an empty centre, feeling like the head of a drum. It indicates loss of blood/essence and insecurity of healthy qi.

V012-N02081.label: 项强
V012-N02081.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02081.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02081.definitions: Also known as neck rigidity. It is most commonly caused by wind, cold and dampness affecting the Taiyang meridian, external summer heat, and malnourishment of local muscles due to consumption of fluids and blood.

V012-N02082.label: 颤动舌
V012-N02082.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02082.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02082.definitions: A tongue that involuntarily trembles as it moves; in severe cases, the tongue trembles even when it does not move, often indicating internal stirring of liver wind.

V012-N02083.label: 颧红
V012-N02083.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02083.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02083.definitions: Reddening of the cheeks only. Often results from fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02084.label: 风关
V012-N02084.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02084.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02084.aliases: 上关； 初关； Upper gate； the first gate
V012-N02084.definitions: One of the diagnostic sites for infantile venule of the index finger. The wind gate is the area between the metacarpophalangeal transverse crease and the second transverse crease.

V012-N02085.label: 饥不欲食
V012-N02085.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02085.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02085.englishNames: Hungry but with no appetite; hunger without desire to eat
V012-N02085.definitions: A sensation of feeling hunger but no desire to eat food. Often results from stomach yin deficiency.

V012-N02086.label: 骨蒸
V012-N02086.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02086.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02086.englishNames: bone steaming; hectic fever; Bone-steaming tidal fever
V012-N02086.definitions: A subjective feverish sensation that the heat is felt to emanate from the bones. It is most commonly caused by fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02087.label: 鸡胸
V012-N02087.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02087.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02087.definitions: A malformation of the chest characterized by a protrusion of the sternum and ribs giving the appearance like the chest of a pigeon. Often results from weak bones due to congenital or acquired deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02088.label: 黄带
V012-N02088.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02088.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02088.definitions: Pale yellow, sticky vaginal discharge. In some cases, the discharge may have a colour that resembles thick tea and a strong odour. Often occurs when dampness transforms into heat and affects the Ren and Dai meridians.

V012-N02089.label: 黄苔
V012-N02089.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02089.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02089.definitions: A tongue coating yellow in colour, often indicating exuberant heat. A darker yellow colour indicates a more intense heat.

V012-N02090.label: 黑苔
V012-N02090.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02090.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02090.definitions: A tongue coating black in colour, indicating either excessive yin cold or extreme heat in the interior.

V012-N02091.label: 鼻痔
V012-N02091.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02091.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02091.definitions: A nasal polyp is a growth within the nose. Often occurs when toxic damp heat accumulates in the nose.

V012-N02092.label: 鼻鼾
V012-N02092.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02092.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02092.definitions: The sound due to obstructed air movement during breathing while sleeping. Often results from obstructed airway.

V012-N02093.label: 齿焦
V012-N02093.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02093.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02093.definitions: The teeth appear dry and withered. Often results from damage to yin fluids.

V012-N02094.label: 齿痕舌
V012-N02094.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02094.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02094.aliases: Teeth-marked Tongue with dental impressions； Scalloped tongue
V012-N02094.englishNames: Tongue with teeth marks; teethprinted tongue
V012-N02094.definitions: A tongue with dental indentations on its margin. It is often seen in an enlarged tongue.

V012-N02095.label: 龟背
V012-N02095.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜四诊
V012-N02095.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02095.definitions: An abnormally excessive convex kyphotic curvature of the spine, resembling the back of a tortoise. Often results from congenital deficiency, postnatal malnutrition and deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02096.label: 疾病
V012-N02096.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N02096.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02097.label: 上消
V012-N02097.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02097.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02097.englishNames: wasting of the upper jiao; upper consumption; Wasting of the upper jiao; upper wasting-thirst
V012-N02097.definitions: Characterized by excessive thirst, excessive drinking, and clear urine with a sweet or fruit-like smell. Individuals often have normal bowel movements. Often occurs when lung heat consumes yin fluids.

V012-N02098.label: 上石疽
V012-N02098.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02098.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02098.englishNames: upper stony nodule; Upper hard nodules
V012-N02098.pinyinNames: shang shi ju; shàng shí jū
V012-N02098.definitions: Characterized by solid, painless, and smooth-surfaced nodules behind the ear or in the neck.

V012-N02099.label: 上腭痈
V012-N02099.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02099.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02099.englishNames: abscess of upper palate; palate abscess; upper palate abscess; Mandibular abscess
V012-N02099.pinyinNames: shang e yong; shàng è yōng
V012-N02099.definitions: Characterized by intense sore throat, difficulty swallowing and red, swollen palate. It is often caused by exuberant toxin affecting the palate.

V012-N02100.label: 下消
V012-N02100.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02100.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02100.aliases: 肾消； 痟肾； 消肾； Kidney wasting
V012-N02100.englishNames: lower consumption; Wasting of the lower jiao; lower wasting-thirst; wasting of the lower jiao
V012-N02100.definitions: Characterized by thirst, excessive drinking, profuse urination with turbid, oily and sweet smelling urine, a dark complexion, and weight loss. Often occurs when hyperactive heart fire consumes kidney yin or disturbs the qi transformation.

V012-N02101.label: 下疳
V012-N02101.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02101.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02101.definitions: A sexually transmitted disease characterized by round or oval-shaped painful, elevated sores on the shaft of penis, glans penis, prepuce, labia minora or vagina, coupled with palpable painless, cartilage-like nodules. Often occurs when Haemophilus ducreyi enters the skin and affects the genitalia and anus.

V012-N02102.label: 下石疽
V012-N02102.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02102.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02102.englishNames: lower stony nodule; Lower hard nodules
V012-N02102.pinyinNames: xia shi ju; xià shí jū
V012-N02102.definitions: Characterized by nodules in the knee that causes dull pain and delayed pus formation.

V012-N02103.label: 不孕
V012-N02103.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02103.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02103.aliases: Female infertility disorder； Female sterility disorder； Infertility disorder
V012-N02103.definitions: Characterized by the inability of women of childbearing age to become pregnant after one year (or longer) of unprotected sex. Often results from dysfunctions of the uterus and Chong and Ren meridian. Contributing factors may include kidney deficiency, liver qi stagnation, phlegm dampness, and blood stasis.

V012-N02104.label: 不寐
V012-N02104.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02104.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02104.aliases: 不寐病； Insomnia disorder； Sleeplessness； 失眠
V012-N02104.definitions: Characterized by an inability to obtain normal sleep and drowsiness and low energy during the day. Associated symptoms may include dizziness, tinnitus and poor memory. Often results from emotional disturbances, mental exhaustion and, subsequently, the disharmony between the Ying-nutrients and Wei-defence.

V012-N02105.label: 不育类病
V012-N02105.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02105.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02106.label: 丝虫病
V012-N02106.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02106.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02106.pinyinNames: si chong bing; sī chóng bìng
V012-N02106.definitions: A parasitic condition mainly found in children caused by an infection with roundworms of the Filarioidea family. It is characterized by fever, skin redness, oedema and burning pain in the lower extremities, testicular pain and swelling, turbid urine, scrotal swelling and identification of microfilariae. This condition is often spread by blood-feeding black flies and mosquitoes. As a result, the microfilariae enter the body and causes damp heat to affect the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02107.label: 中暑
V012-N02107.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02107.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02107.englishNames: sunstroke; heat stroke and sunstroke; summerheat stroke; Summer heat stroke; heat stroke
V012-N02107.definitions: A condition characterized by high fever, excessive sweating or dry skin, restlessness, thirst, nausea, vomiting and abdominal pain. In severe cases, patients may lose consciousness or experience convulsions. Contributing factors may include working in high temperatures during summer, summer heat or summer dampness suddenly blocking the heart mind, exuberant heat consuming fluids and stirring up liver wind, or summer heat blocking the qi activity.

V012-N02108.label: 中毒及意外伤害类
V012-N02108.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02108.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02108.pinyinNames: zhòng dú jí yì wài shāng hài lèi
V012-N02108.definitions: Acute/chronic poisoning or injuries due to physical, chemical accidents.

V012-N02109.label: 中消
V012-N02109.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02109.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02109.aliases: 消中； 痟中； 消脾； 胃消； Spleen/stomach wasting
V012-N02109.englishNames: middle consumption; wasting of the middle jiao; middle wasting-thirst; Wasting of the middle jiao
V012-N02109.definitions: Characterized by increased appetite but rapid hunger after eating food, weight loss, thirst, excessive drinking and constipation. Often occurs when dry heat in the middle jiao consumes stomach yin.

V012-N02110.label: 中石疽
V012-N02110.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02110.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02110.englishNames: middle stony nodule; Middle hard nodules
V012-N02110.pinyinNames: zhong shi ju; zhōng shí jū
V012-N02110.definitions: Characterized by solid nodules on one side of the groin. Pain occurs on hip flexion.

V012-N02111.label: 中风病
V012-N02111.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02111.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02111.aliases: 中风； 卒中； Wind stroke disorders； Apoplexy
V012-N02111.pinyinNames: zhong feng bing; zhòng fēng bìng
V012-N02111.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting, hemiplegia or deviation of the eyes and mouth corner. Often results from wind phlegm attacking the meridians, disorder of qi and blood, or bleeding in the brain. It includes trismus, haemorrhagic stroke, ischemic stroke, facial palsy and hemiplegia.

V012-N02112.label: 临时诊断用症候术语
V012-N02112.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02112.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02112.pinyinNames: lín shí zhěn duàn yòng zhèng hòu shù yǔ
V012-N02112.definitions: Common signs and symptoms that can be used for provisional diagnosis.

V012-N02113.label: 丹毒
V012-N02113.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02113.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02113.aliases: 火丹； 丹熛； Erysipelas disorder
V012-N02113.englishNames: erysipelas; e	canula drainagerysipelas; Erysipelas disorder; Erysipelas
V012-N02113.pinyinNames: dan dong; dan du; dan er; dān dú
V012-N02113.definitions: Characterized by sudden onset of bright red, swollen and warm skin lesions that enlarge rapidly. Individuals may also experience chills and high fever. Often occurs when toxic fire and blood heat affects the skin.

V012-N02114.label: 久咳病
V012-N02114.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02114.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02114.pinyinNames: jiǔ ké bìng; jiu ke bing
V012-N02114.definitions: Characterized by persistent, recurrent coughing with a prolonged period.

V012-N02115.label: 久泄
V012-N02115.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02115.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02115.definitions: Diarrhoea that recurs or lasts longer than two months. It is often caused by deficiency of the spleen and kidney or disharmony between the liver and spleen.

V012-N02116.label: 久疟
V012-N02116.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02116.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02116.englishNames: chronic malaria; Chronic malaria; Chronic Malaria
V012-N02116.pinyinNames: jiu nue; jiǔ nüè; jiu nve
V012-N02116.definitions: Characterized by intermittent and prolonged episodes. Often results from deficiency of zang–fu organs, damage to Ying nutrients and Wei-defence and retention of Plasmodium parasites.

V012-N02117.label: 久瘖
V012-N02117.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02117.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02117.definitions: Characterized by hoarseness or loss of voice with persistent throat discomfort. It is often caused by malnourishment of the throat or lingering of the pathogenic factors in the throat area.

V012-N02118.label: 乌风内障
V012-N02118.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02118.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02118.englishNames: dark wind glaucoma; black wind glaucoma; Dark glaucoma
V012-N02118.pinyinNames: wu feng nei zhang; wū fēng nèi zhàng
V012-N02118.definitions: Characterized by distension and pain of the eyeball, blurred vision, appearance of opaque specks in the lens, occasional headache, and gradually progressive vision loss. Often results from ascending of hyperactive deficiency fire that carries phlegm.

V012-N02119.label: 乳发
V012-N02119.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02119.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02119.aliases: 脱壳乳痈； Shell-bursting pyogenic abscess
V012-N02119.englishNames: suppurative mastitis; mammary cellulitis; phlegmonous mastitis; Acute septic mastitis
V012-N02119.definitions: Characterized by redness, pain and a burning sensation of the breast, coupled with dark skin pigmentation after abscess rupture. In severe cases, restlessness, unconsciousness and delirium may be present. Often occurs when exogenous damp heat and toxic fire affects the liver and stomach meridians and remains in the skin around the breasts.

V012-N02120.label: 乳头风
V012-N02120.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02120.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02120.englishNames: nipple wind; rhagadia mammae; cracked nipple; Nipple eczema
V012-N02120.pinyinNames: ru tou feng; rǔ tóu fēng
V012-N02120.definitions: Characterized by sharp pain, followed by bleeding, oozing and crusting. Painful fissuring or abscess may be present. It is commonly seen in breast-feeding women. If often occurs when liver fire or damp heat accumulates in the nipples, areolae, or the surrounding skin.

V012-N02121.label: 乳岩
V012-N02121.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02121.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02121.englishNames: breast cancer; Rocky mass in the breast; rocky mass in the breast/breast cancer; carcinoma of breast
V012-N02121.definitions: Characterized by solid, immobile breast lumps, coupled with skin changes on the breast with quality resembling the peel of an orange, followed by discharge of pus and blood, increased pain and weight loss. Often results from emotional disturbance, disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians and qi stagnation intertwined with phlegm stasis.

V012-N02122.label: 乳悬
V012-N02122.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02122.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02122.definitions: Characterized by the breast or nipple falling down, drooping, or downward to the front of the abdomen, coupled with intolerable pain. Contributing factors may include sudden rage damaging qi or blood dryness due to stomach deficiency.

V012-N02123.label: 乳房类病
V012-N02123.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02123.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02123.englishNames: Breast diseases; breast diseases; category of breast disease
V012-N02123.pinyinNames: ru fang lei bing; rǔ fáng lèi bìng
V012-N02123.definitions: A group of breast problems due to congenital weakness, disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians, invasion by exogenous pathogens, emotions, overeat of oily food or milk accumulation.

V012-N02124.label: 乳核
V012-N02124.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02124.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02124.englishNames: mammary nodule; fibroadenoma of breast; breast nodule; Breast nodules; nodule in breast; breast nodule (fibroadenoma of breast)
V012-N02124.definitions: Characterized by hard, mobile, and often pain-free breast nodule (often singular) with a smooth surface and a clear margin that changes with emotions or menstrual cycle. Contributing factors may include emotional disturbance and disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians. These factors may cause phlegm stasis in the breast.

V012-N02125.label: 乳泣
V012-N02125.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02125.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02125.definitions: Characterized by spontaneous flow of milk from the breast that is not associated with childbirth or nursing. Often occurs when qi fails to hold milk (fluids) or when liver qi stagnation transforms into fire to move the milk (fluids).

V012-N02126.label: 乳漏
V012-N02126.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02126.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02126.englishNames: mammary fistula; Mammary duct fistula; breast fistula
V012-N02126.definitions: Characterized by an abnormal passage between a sub-areolar duct and skin that exudates pus or milk. Often occurs spontaneously following acute mastitis, acute septic mastitis, comedomastitis, tuberculosis of the breast, or breast surgery.

V012-N02127.label: 乳疬
V012-N02127.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02127.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02127.englishNames: Gynecomastia; gynecomastia; hypertrophy of breast; mastauxy in children
V012-N02127.definitions: Characterized by oval or round, tender lumps on one or both sides of the sub-areolar region in children or male adults. Contributing factors may include congenital kidney qi deficiency, postnatal deficiency of the liver and kidney, disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridian, malnourishment of the liver, and phlegm qi stagnation.

V012-N02128.label: 乳疳
V012-N02128.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02128.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02128.englishNames: mammary necrosis; erosive mammary ulceration; Eczematous carcinoma of nipple
V012-N02128.definitions: Characterized by nipple ulceration or oozing. Alternatively, necrosis of the nipple with intolerable pain may occur. It is often caused by liver qi stagnation transforming into fire and accumulation of damp heat. The symptoms usually affect the nipple and then spread to the areola and then the breast. It is common for the symptoms to wax and wane.

V012-N02129.label: 乳疽
V012-N02129.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02129.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02129.englishNames: breast gangrene; mammary deep abscess; Breast abscess
V012-N02129.definitions: Characterized by lumps located deep within the breast with mild pain and no skin discolouration at first, followed by a gradual increase in lump size, a slow formation of pus, discharge of yellow pus after rupture, coupled with a deep ulcerative opening and prolonged healing. Contributing factors may include liver blood stagnation, stomach heat accumulation, blocked milk ducts, or toxin entering the interior.

V012-N02130.label: 乳痈
V012-N02130.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02130.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02130.aliases: Deep Rooted Breast Carbuncles； 乳吹
V012-N02130.englishNames: Acute Mastitis; breast abscess; acute mastitis; Acute mastitis
V012-N02130.definitions: Characterized by distending, painful nodules in varying sizes and problems suckling the milk out of the breast, followed by increased nodules with redness, tenderness and discharge of yellow, thick pus. In severe cases, chills and a high-grade fever may be present. The onset is typically rapid and commonly seen within the first few months of delivery. Contributing factors may include cracks on the nipples, contraction of external damp heat, blocked milk ducts or milk accumulation, stomach heat, and liver qi stagnation transforming into heat.

V012-N02131.label: 乳痨
V012-N02131.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02131.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02131.englishNames: tuberculosis of breast; mammary phthisis; Breast tuberculosis; mammary tuberculosis
V012-N02131.definitions: Characterized by (plum-sized) painless breast lumps with unclear boundaries, discharge of thin, clear pus upon disruption of breast abscess, and the formation of breast sinuses or ulcers. Individuals may also experience enlargement of the axillary lymph nodes. This condition often results from infection of bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis upon skin lesion or secondary to pulmonary tuberculosis (or scrofula).

V012-N02132.label: 乳癖
V012-N02132.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02132.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02132.englishNames: breast lump; lump in breast; mammary hyperplasia; Breast lumps; Breast lump disorder; breast lump(hyperplasia of mammary gland)
V012-N02132.pinyinNames: ru xian; ru pi; rǔ pǐ
V012-N02132.definitions: Characterized by breast lumps with or without pain that change with emotions or menstrual cycles. Contributing factors may include emotional disturbance and disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians. These factors may cause stagnant qi, blood and phlegm to remain in the mammary gland.

V012-N02133.label: 乳积
V012-N02133.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02133.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02133.englishNames: infantile dyspepsia; Milk accumulation; milk dyspepsia
V012-N02133.definitions: Characterized by frequent foul breath or vomiting of undigested milk. Associated symptoms may include abdominal distension, diarrhoea, crying for no reason, poor sleep, hot breath and dry stools. Often results from improper breast feeding that damages the spleen and stomach and causes milk to stagnate in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02134.label: 乳蛾
V012-N02134.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02134.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02134.englishNames: tonsillitis; nippled moth; Nippled Moth; Tonsillitis; Tonsillitis disorder
V012-N02134.pinyinNames: ru dong; ru e; rǔ é
V012-N02134.definitions: Characterized by fever, sore throat, and enlargement of the tonsils that resembles a nipple or silkworm moth. Individuals may also present with white pus-filled spots on the surface of the tonsil or swollen, hard and dark red tonsil. Often results from exogenous pathogens affecting the tonsil, ascending of deficiency fire, or stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02135.label: 乳衄
V012-N02135.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02135.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02135.englishNames: thelorrhagia; nipple bleeding; Nipple discharge; bleeding of nipple (thelorrhagia)
V012-N02135.definitions: Characterized by discharge of bloody fluids from the nipple of the breast or palpable, soft, mobile and painless lumps. Contributing factors may include anger or worries damaging the liver and spleen or stagnant fire in liver meridian affecting the spleen’s function in controlling blood within the small vessels.

V012-N02136.label: 五更泻
V012-N02136.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02136.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02136.aliases: 晨泄； 鸡鸣泄； 肾泄； 五更泄； Dawn Diarrhea
V012-N02136.englishNames: diarrhea before dawn; Early morning (Cock’s crow) diarrhoea; Enterorrhea before Dawn
V012-N02136.pinyinNames: wu geng xie; wǔ gēng xiè; wu jing xie
V012-N02136.definitions: A type of diarrhoea characterized by diarrhoea upon abdominal pain before dawn and the pain alleviates after bowel movements. Associated symptoms include cold intolerance, cold limbs, low back/knee soreness with a cold sensation and fatigue. Often results from congenital deficiency, kidney deficiency, or internal yin cold due to decline of the vital fire.

V012-N02137.label: 五软
V012-N02137.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02137.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02137.englishNames: five limpnesses/ flaccidity; five infantile flaccidity; five infant flaccidity; five infant limpness; five kinds of flaccidity; Five flaccidities; Five Kinds of Flaccidity
V012-N02137.pinyinNames: wu ruan; wu luan; wǔ ruǎn
V012-N02137.definitions: Characterized by flaccidity of the neck, mouth, hands, feet and muscles in infants. Often results from congenital qi deficiency or postnatal malnutrition.

V012-N02138.label: 五迟
V012-N02138.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02138.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02138.englishNames: five kinds of retardation; five retardations; five developmental delays; five retardation; Five Kinds of Retardation; Five retardations; five kinds of retadations
V012-N02138.definitions: Characterized by retarded growth and development in standing, walking, hair growth, tooth eruption and speaking. Often results from congenital kidney deficiency or deficiency of qi and blood due to postnatal malnutrition.

V012-N02139.label: 五风内障
V012-N02139.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02139.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02139.englishNames: five kinds of wind glaucoma; five wind glaucoma; Wind glaucoma disorder; Glaucoma
V012-N02139.pinyinNames: wu feng nei zhang; wǔ fēng nèi zhàng
V012-N02139.definitions: Characterized by increased pressure in the eye, headache, eye distension/pain, blurred vision, red eyes and dilation of the pupil. Often results from emotional stress, qi stagnation and blood stasis, and exuberant fire in the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N02140.label: 交肠病
V012-N02140.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02140.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02140.aliases: 产后交肠病； 阴道直肠瘘
V012-N02140.pinyinNames: jiao chang bing; jiāo cháng bìng
V012-N02140.definitions: Characterized by the passing of urine through the anus or passing of faeces through the urethra. It is often caused by a trauma-induced fistula or abnormal connection between the rectum and the vagina during childbirth.

V012-N02141.label: 产后发热
V012-N02141.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02141.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02141.englishNames: postpartum fever; puerperal fever; Postpartum fever
V012-N02141.pinyinNames: chan hou fa re; chǎn hòu fā rè
V012-N02141.definitions: Characterized by high-grade fever and chills in the postpartum period. In some cases, individuals may experience persistent high fever and other systemic symptoms. Often results from postpartum infection, blood deficiency, blood stasis or contraction of exogenous pathogens.

V012-N02142.label: 产后恶血冲心
V012-N02142.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02142.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02142.aliases: 产后败血冲心； 产后恶血入心； 恶血冲心； 败血冲心； 产后血气冲心
V012-N02142.englishNames: postpartum lochia ramming the heart; Lochia disturbing the heart mind
V012-N02142.pinyinNames: chan hou dong xue chong xin; chan hou er xue chong xin; chǎn hòu è xuè chōng xīn
V012-N02142.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, insomnia, mental confusion and incoherent speech after childbirth. In severe cases, loss of consciousness may be present. Often results from deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi and retained lochia disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N02143.label: 产后恶血冲肺
V012-N02143.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02143.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02143.englishNames: postpartum lochia ramming the lung; Lochia affecting the lung
V012-N02143.pinyinNames: chan hou er xue chong fei; chan hou dong xue chong fei; chǎn hòu è xuè chōng fèi
V012-N02143.definitions: Characterized by cough, rapid breathing, chest tightness, restlessness, a red or dark complexion and nosebleed after childbirth. In severe cases, panting or a feeling of impending death may be present. Often occurs when retained lochia affects the lung.

V012-N02144.label: 产后恶血冲胃
V012-N02144.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02144.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02144.englishNames: postpartum lochia ramming the stomach; Lochia affecting the stomach
V012-N02144.pinyinNames: chan hou er xue chong wei; chan hou dong xue chong wei; chǎn hòu è xuè chōng wèi
V012-N02144.definitions: Characterized by colicky abdominal pain that radiates to the waist, coupled with nausea, vomiting, abdominal stuffiness and a poor appetite after childbirth. Often results from deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi and retained lochia affecting the stomach.

V012-N02145.label: 产后恶露不下
V012-N02145.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02145.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02145.englishNames: postpartum lochiostasis; Retention of the lochia
V012-N02145.pinyinNames: chan hou dong lu bu xia; chǎn hòu è lù bú xià; chan hou er lu bu xia
V012-N02145.definitions: Caharaterized by retained vaginal discharge during the postpartum period. Lower abdominal pain may also be present. Often results from build up of lochia in the uterus.

V012-N02146.label: 产后恶露不绝
V012-N02146.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02146.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02146.aliases: 恶露不绝； 恶露不净； 产后恶露不净； 恶露不尽
V012-N02146.pinyinNames: chǎn hòu è lù bù jué; chan hou er lu bu jue; chan hou dong lu bu jue
V012-N02146.definitions: Characterized by abnormally prolonged discharge of lochia for more than 10 days after childbirth. Often results from blood heat, blood stasis, qi deficiency, or contraction of external pathogens.

V012-N02147.label: 产后汗病
V012-N02147.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02147.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02147.pinyinNames: chan hou han bing; chǎn hòu hàn bìng
V012-N02147.definitions: Characterized by spontaneous sweating or night sweats after childbirth.

V012-N02148.label: 产后痉病
V012-N02148.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02148.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02148.englishNames: postpartum convulsion; postpartum convulsion disease; Postpartum tetanus
V012-N02148.pinyinNames: chan hou jing bing; chǎn hòu jìng bìng
V012-N02148.definitions: Characterized by muscle contractions, body rigidity, lockjaw or opisthotonos after childbirth. Often results from blood deficiency, yin deficiency or obstetric wound infection.

V012-N02149.label: 产后类病
V012-N02149.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02149.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02149.englishNames: puerperal diseases; puerperal disease; Postpartum disorders
V012-N02149.pinyinNames: chan hou lei bing; chǎn hòu lèi bìng
V012-N02149.definitions: A group of disorders occurring during or after childbirth.

V012-N02150.label: 产后缺乳
V012-N02150.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02150.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02150.pinyinNames: chan hou que ru; chǎn hòu quē rǔ
V012-N02150.definitions: Characterized by insufficient or absent breastmilk during the lactation period. Often results from deficiency of qi and blood or liver qi stagnation.

V012-N02151.label: 产后腹痛
V012-N02151.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02151.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02151.aliases: 儿枕痛； Postpartum abdominal pain disorder； Puerperal abdominal pain disorder
V012-N02151.englishNames: postpartum abdominal pain; Postpartum abdominal pain
V012-N02151.pinyinNames: chan hou fu tong; chǎn hòu fù tòng
V012-N02151.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal pain after childbirth. Often results from uterine contraction due to obstructed flow of qi and blood.

V012-N02152.label: 产后血崩
V012-N02152.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02152.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02152.englishNames: massive pospartum hemorrhage; postpartum metrorrhagia; postpartum haemorrhage; Postpartum bleeding
V012-N02152.pinyinNames: chan hou xue beng; chǎn hòu xuè bēng
V012-N02152.definitions: Characterized by excessive vaginal bleeding following the birth of a baby. Often results from damage to the Chong and Ren meridians, retained placental or sex following childbirth.

V012-N02153.label: 产后血晕
V012-N02153.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02153.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02153.englishNames: postpartum fainting due to hemorrhage; postpartum hemorrhagic syncope; postpartum anemic fainting; postpartum faint due to hemorrhage; Postpartum fainting
V012-N02153.pinyinNames: chan hou xue yun; chǎn hòu xuè yūn
V012-N02153.definitions: Characterized by sudden onset of dizziness, blurred vision, an inability to sit up, chest fullness and stuffiness, nausea, vomiting, and profuse phlegm. In severe cases, unconsciousness and lockjaw may be present. Often results from qi collapse due to heavy blood loss or stasis obstructing the flow of qi.

V012-N02154.label: 产后身痛
V012-N02154.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02154.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02154.englishNames: postpartum body pain; Postpartum Pantalgia; postpartum unilateral pain; Postpartum body ache; postpartum pain of body
V012-N02154.pinyinNames: chan hou shen tong; chǎn hòu shēn tòng
V012-N02154.definitions: Characterized by joint pain, numbness and heaviness after childbirth. Often results from blood deficiency, kidney deficiency, malnourishment of the tendons and joints, or obstructed flow of qi and blood due to contraction of external pathogens.

V012-N02155.label: 产科类病
V012-N02155.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02155.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02155.englishNames: Obstetric disorders; obstetrical diseases; category of obstetrical disease
V012-N02155.pinyinNames: chǎn kē lèi bìng; chan ke lei bing
V012-N02155.definitions: A group of disorders before or during the childbirth.

V012-N02156.label: 伏兔疽
V012-N02156.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02156.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02156.englishNames: Futu abscess; anterior thigh gangrene; Abscess around Futu (ST 32)
V012-N02156.pinyinNames: fu tu ju; fú tù jū
V012-N02156.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation the point Futu on the front side of the thigh. Often results from stomach fire.

V012-N02157.label: 伏暑
V012-N02157.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02157.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02157.englishNames: latent summerheat; Latent Summer Heat Diseases; Latent summer heat pestilence
V012-N02157.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by a sudden onset, alternating chills and fever or fever without chills that aggravates at night and alleviates during the day, especially after sweating. Associated symptoms may include a burning sensation in the chest and abdomen, skin rashes, and loose stools. In severe cases, restlessness or delirium may be present. Often occurs when seasonal pathogenic factors or epidemic toxin in autumn or winter triggers and causes the latent summer heat and dampness to retain in the qi/Ying nutrient phases, Shaoyang or stomach and intestines.

V012-N02158.label: 伏梁
V012-N02158.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02158.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02158.aliases: 伏梁积气； 心积伏梁； 伏梁气； Masses accumulating below the cardiac region (Crohn’s disease or regional enteritis)
V012-N02158.englishNames: disease due to latent stagnation of qi and blood; Masses in the upper abdomen; heart amassment
V012-N02158.definitions: Masses in the upper abdomen can cause abdominal pain and diarrhoea with stools containing pus and blood. They often result from accumulation of foul turbidity and stagnant qi and blood.

V012-N02159.label: 伏气温病
V012-N02159.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02159.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02159.englishNames: Latent warm diseases; latent warm diseases; warm disease with latent pathogenic factors
V012-N02159.pinyinNames: fú qì wēn bìng; fu qi wen bing
V012-N02159.definitions: A group of diseases initially characterized by exuberant interior heat and associated symptoms due to dry heat damaging yin. They are often caused by latent pathogenic factors in the interior or pre-existing heat coupled with contraction of new pathogenic factors.

V012-N02160.label: 休息痢
V012-N02160.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02160.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02160.englishNames: intermittent dysentery; Intermittent dysentery; Chronic Intermittent Dysentery; recurrent dysentery
V012-N02160.pinyinNames: xiu xi li; xiū xi lì
V012-N02160.definitions: Characterized by intermittent diarrhoea containing mucus or blood and dull abdominal pain. Patients present with tenesmus during acute episodes. Abdominal distension, a low food intake and lassitude are present during intermittence. Often occurs when chronic dysentery damages the spleen and stomach, coupled with retention of pathogenic toxin.

V012-N02161.label: 伤寒类病
V012-N02161.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02161.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02161.aliases: 伤寒； Cold injury； Exogenous febrile disorders
V012-N02161.englishNames: cold damage diseases; Cold damage; category of cold damage disease
V012-N02161.pinyinNames: shang han lei bing; shāng hán lèi bìng
V012-N02161.definitions: A group of disorders caused by external wind cold. It is initially characterized by cold-induced symptoms and cold damaging yang qi. These include Taiyang disorders, Yangming disorders, Shaoyang disorders, Taiyin disorders, Shaoyin disorders and Jueyin disorders.

V012-N02162.label: 伤筋病
V012-N02162.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02162.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02162.definitions: A group of conditions caused by damage to the structure and function of tendons by acute trauma or chronic strain. These include tendon dislocation/subluxation, bone dislocation/subluxation, neck stiffness, cervical spondylosis, frozen shoulder, lumbar strain and lumbar intervertebral disc herniation.

V012-N02163.label: 伤风鼻塞
V012-N02163.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02163.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02163.englishNames: nasal congestion due to wind attack; nasal obstruction due to mild cold; acute rhinitis; Acute rhinitis
V012-N02163.pinyinNames: shang feng bi sai; shāng fēng bí sāi
V012-N02163.definitions: Characterized by stuffy nose, runny nose (clear or thick nasal discharges), sneezing or other common cold symptoms. It is often caused by wind cold or wind heat affecting the lung and obstructing the nasal cavity.

V012-N02164.label: 伤食
V012-N02164.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02164.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02164.englishNames: food damage; Food damage; food damage (dyspepsia)
V012-N02164.definitions: Characterized by nausea, aversion to food, belching, a foul breath, gastric stuffiness and abdominal distension. Often occurs when binge eating damages the spleen and stomach or food retention coupled with external contraction of wind cold impairs the transportation and transformation of the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02165.label: 侏儒
V012-N02165.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02165.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02165.pinyinNames: zhu ru; zhū rú; zhu  ru
V012-N02165.definitions: Characterized by unusually short limbs/torso, lack of secondary sexual characteristics, and normal intelligence. It is often caused by intracranial tumour or growth hormone deficiency.

V012-N02166.label: 侠瘿瘤
V012-N02166.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02166.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02166.pinyinNames: xia ying liu; xiá yǐng liú
V012-N02166.definitions: Characterized by thyroid nodules, fatigue, slow reactions, decreased appetite, constipation, polyuria or bloody urine, bone pain, malformation or even fracture. Often occurs when disordered qi, blood, yin and yang cause phlegm qi and stagnant blood to remain beside the Adam’s apple.

V012-N02167.label: 便秘
V012-N02167.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02167.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02167.definitions: Less than two bowel movements per week or difficulty passing hard stools. Often results from heat accumulating in the stomach and intestines, cold retention due to yang deficiency, deficiency of qi, blood and yin fluids or abdominal masses. It is commonly seen in intestinal disorders, anal lesions, muscle flaccidity, post-stroke hemiplegia, Parkinson’s disease, parenteral lump compression, overuse of dry, warm or astringent medicines, abdominal surgery, the aged or general debilitation.

V012-N02168.label: 便秘病
V012-N02168.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02168.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02168.pinyinNames: bian mi bing; biàn mì bìng
V012-N02168.definitions: Characterized by hard stools and difficult, infrequent bowel movements, usually less than three stools per week. Patients may also experience straining with bowel movements. Contributing factors may include heat accumulating in the stomach and intestines, deficiency cold, consumption of qi, blood and yin fluids, abdominal masses or abdominal surgery.

V012-N02169.label: 便血
V012-N02169.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02169.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02169.definitions: The passage of fresh blood through the anus, usually in or with stools. Alternatively, individuals may have black “tarry” faeces or expulsion of fresh bright red blood without stools. Contributing factors may include qi deficiency of the spleen and stomach, damp heat accumulating in the stomach and intestines, or stagnation of qi and blood in the stomach and intestines. It is often seen in anal disorders, gastrointestinal diseases, epidemic haemorrhagic fever, bleeding disorders, ancylostomiasis, schistosomiasis or food/drug poisoning.

V012-N02170.label: 偏头风
V012-N02170.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02170.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02170.aliases: 偏头痛； Migraine disorder； Migraine pain disorder； Migraine； 偏正头风
V012-N02170.englishNames: hemilateral head wind; Migraine disorder; migraine; Hemilateral head wind
V012-N02170.pinyinNames: pian tou feng; piān tóu fēng
V012-N02170.definitions: A condition characterized by sudden, recurrent and intense headache occurring on one side of the head. Contributing factors may include wind cold, wind fire that carries phlegm, liver yang transforming into wind or stagnant blood obstructing the meridians. These factors may cause disordered qi and blood to affect the head.

V012-N02171.label: 偏痹
V012-N02171.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02171.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02171.definitions: Characterized by pain radiating along the back or outside of one leg from the lower back, accompanied by numbness or pain that aggravates with increased intra-abdominal pressure, and restricted movement. Contributing factors may include acute lumbar sprain, chronic lumbar strain, or cold dampness obstructing the meridians.

V012-N02172.label: 健忘
V012-N02172.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02172.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02172.definitions: A noticeable memory decrease that does not conform to age-related effects. Often results from deficiency of the heart and spleen, disharmony between the heart and kidney, ageing, or phlegm stasis affecting the heart mind. It is commonly seen in mental over-exertion, cerebral atrophy, insomnia, emotional disorder, dementia, head trauma or poisoning.

V012-N02173.label: 关格
V012-N02173.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02173.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02173.englishNames: block and repulsion (disease); anuria and vomiting; urinary retention and vomiting; obstruction and rejection; Block and repulsion disorder; dysuria and frequent vomiting; block and repulsion disease/disorder; Anuria and Vomiting; Urinary block and vomiting
V012-N02173.definitions: A critical condition characterized by urinary retention and persistent vomiting, coupled with skin itching, and a strong-odoured breath. In severe cases, convulsions of the hands and feet, drowsiness and mental cloudiness may be present. Often occurs in late-stage oedema, urinary retention or strangury. Contributing factors may include impaired qi transformation due to yin/yang failure of the spleen and kidney and toxic, turbid dampness attacking the stomach. Alternatively, it may also occur when dampness transforms into heat and stirs wind, or in severe cases, to disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02174.label: 关节痛
V012-N02174.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02174.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02174.pinyinNames: guan jie tong; guān jié tòng
V012-N02174.definitions: A subjective feeling of pain in the joints. Contributing factors may include traumatic injuries or wind, cold, damp or heat impairing the flow of meridian qi. It is often seen in joint subluxation, bone injury, bi-impediment, or osteoarticular tuberculosis.

V012-N02175.label: 内伤发热病
V012-N02175.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02175.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02175.englishNames: fever due to internal dysfunctions; fever disease due to internal damage; Fever due to internal dysfunctions
V012-N02175.pinyinNames: nei shang fa re bing; nèi shāng fā rè bìng
V012-N02175.definitions: Characterized by a low-grade fever, tidal fever, bone-steaming sensation, hot flashes and feverish sensations of the palms and soles. Individuals may also present with symptoms indicative of deficiency of yin, yang, qi, blood and the zang–fu organs. Contributing factors may include over-exertion, emotional disturbance, improper diet or dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs. These factors may cause deficiency of qi and blood and disharmony between yin and yang.

V012-N02176.label: 内伤咳嗽
V012-N02176.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02176.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02176.englishNames: Cough due to internal dysfunctions; endogenous cough; cough due to internal damage
V012-N02176.pinyinNames: nei shang ke su; nèi shāng ké sou
V012-N02176.definitions: Characterized by intermittent coughs varying in intensity. Associated symptoms may include fatigue, frequent common colds, chest tightness, panting, pain in the subcostal region, haemoptysis and puffy face or eyes. Contributing factors may include chronic cough damaging lung qi, phlegm dampness due to spleen deficiency, liver fire affecting the lung, water retention due to kidney deficiency or deficiency of qi and yin.

V012-N02177.label: 内发丹毒
V012-N02177.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02177.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02177.englishNames: Erysipelas on the chest, back/low back and hip; internal erysipelas; endogenous erysipelas
V012-N02177.pinyinNames: nei fa dan dong; nèi fā dān dú; nei fa dan er
V012-N02177.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, warm and painful skin lesions on the chest, back/low back and hip, with a sharply demarcated edge and discolouration upon pressure. Individuals may experience chills and high fever. Often results from liver fire and extreme heat in the liver and spleen meridians.

V012-N02178.label: 内痔
V012-N02178.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02178.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02178.englishNames: internal hemorrhoid; internal hemorroid; Internal haemorrhoids; Interior haemorroid disorder
V012-N02178.definitions: Characterized by varicose dilatation of a vein above the dentate line, bloody stools and prolapsed haemorrhoids. Internal haemorrhoids often result from deficiency of the zang–fu organs, an improper diet, sitting or standing for an extended period of time, weight-bearing or constipation.

V012-N02179.label: 内障类病
V012-N02179.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02179.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02179.englishNames: category of endogenous ocular diseases; endogenous ocular diseases; Eye disorders involving the pupil, eyeball, retina or other eye tissues
V012-N02179.pinyinNames: nei zhang lei bing; nèi zhàng lèi bìng
V012-N02179.definitions: Conditions of the pupil, eyeball, retina, vitreous humour or other eye tissues caused by deficiency of the liver and kidney, deficiency of qi and blood, emotional stress, or ascending of wind fire that carries phlegm dampness.

V012-N02180.label: 冬温
V012-N02180.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02180.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02180.aliases: 冬瘟； 冬温病； 冬瘟病； Winter febrile disorders； Winter pestilence disorders
V012-N02180.englishNames: winter warmth; warm disease in winter; Winter pestilence
V012-N02180.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by headache, fever, cough, sore throat, sweating and excessive thirst. In severe cases, a persistent high-grade fever, unconsciousness, delirium, spasms and convulsions may be present. Often occurs when pathogenic winter warmth or epidemic toxin attacks the Wei-defence or disturbs the mind.

V012-N02181.label: 冷泪
V012-N02181.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02181.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02181.englishNames: cold tearing; cold tear; cold lacrimation; Cold tears; epiphora
V012-N02181.definitions: Characterized by excess clear, thin tears, especially when exposed to wind. Individuals do not have eye redness, swelling or pain. Often results from deficiency of the liver and kidney, deficiency of qi and blood, or invasion by wind.

V012-N02182.label: 冻僵
V012-N02182.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02182.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02182.pinyinNames: dong jiang; er jiang; dòng jiāng
V012-N02182.definitions: A condition caused by exposure to cold environment for prolonged periods. Individuals may experience pale skin, a cold feeling, chills, fever, excitation, elevated blood pressure and faster heart rate. When the core body temperature has decreased below 32°C, an indifferent expression, confusion, decreased breathing/heart rate, and muscle rigidity may occur. When the core body temperature has decreased below 29°C, slow reactions, loss of consciousness, and low blood pressure may be present. When the core body temperature has decreased below 26°C, ventricular fibrillation or respiratory/cardiac arrest may occur.

V012-N02183.label: 冻疮
V012-N02183.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02183.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02183.pinyinNames: dong chuang; er chuang; dòng chuāng
V012-N02183.definitions: Characterized by redness, swelling, a cold feeling, itching and pain upon sudden exposure to low temperatures. Areas that are usually affected include the back of the hand/foot, ears and cheeks. In severe cases, blood blisters, blue-grey discolouration of the skin, ulceration or autoamputation may be present.

V012-N02184.label: 凉燥病
V012-N02184.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02184.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02184.pinyinNames: liang zao bing; liáng zào bìng
V012-N02184.definitions: A condition characterized by fever, chills, headache, absence of sweating, unproductive cough or difficulty expectoration of sputum, and a dry throat and lips. Often occurs in autumn when cool dryness affects the lung and consumes bodily fluids.

V012-N02185.label: 出疹
V012-N02185.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02185.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02185.definitions: Raised red or white spots on the skin. It is often caused by wind heat, wind dampness or pestilence. It can be seen in urticaria, measles, roseola infantum, eczema, dermatitis medicamentosa, sweat rash, scabies, pigmented purpuric lichenoid dermatitis, pityriasis rosea, skin rashes during menstruation/pregnancy, or scarlet fever.

V012-N02186.label: 创伤类病
V012-N02186.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02186.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02186.englishNames: traumatic diseases; category of traumatic disease; Traumatic injuries
V012-N02186.pinyinNames: chuang shang lei bing; chuāng shāng lèi bìng
V012-N02186.definitions: Injuries caused by violent forces, metallic tools, firearms or animal bites. They vary from bone/tendon injuries to tissue or organ ruptures.

V012-N02187.label: 劳淋
V012-N02187.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02187.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02187.englishNames: overstrain strangury; fatigue strangury; stranguria due to overstrain; overstrain stranguria; Fatigue strangury
V012-N02187.definitions: Characterized by recurrent, fatigue-induced frequent, urgent, painful and dripping urination, lower back painlower back pain, and lassitude. Associated symptoms may include tidal, low-grade fever and weight loss. Contributing factors may include retention of toxins due to chronic strangury, excessive worries or mental exertion damaging the heart and spleen, sexual indulgence damaging the kidney or tuberculosis affecting the opening and closing of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02188.label: 包虫病
V012-N02188.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02188.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02188.pinyinNames: bāo chóng bìng; bao chong bing
V012-N02188.definitions: A zoonotic parasitosis contracted by infection with the eggs of the parasites (hydatid cyst). It is characterized by painless, movable nodules or cysts with normal skin colour. Associated symptoms may include fever, cough, haemoptysis, abdominal pain and bloody stools. The nodules occur when hydatid cysts remain in the liver, lung and intestines and bind with phlegm, stasis and water dampness.

V012-N02189.label: 卑惵
V012-N02189.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02189.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02189.englishNames: guilt complex; Low self-esteem; upset with timidity; "bi die" syndrome
V012-N02189.pinyinNames: bei die; bēi dié; bi die
V012-N02189.definitions: Characterized by depression, timidity, self-inferiority and panic. In severe cases, emotional detachment may be present. Often results from gallbladder deficiency, heart qi insufficiency or disquieted gallbladder qi.

V012-N02190.label: 卒心痛
V012-N02190.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02190.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02190.englishNames: sudden heart pain; Sudden cardiac pain; sudden cardiac pain; sudden precordial pain
V012-N02190.pinyinNames: zu xin tong; zú xīn tòng
V012-N02190.definitions: Characterized by sudden, stabbing cardiac pain, angina and shortness of breath. In severe cases, feeling of impending death may be present. Often results from deficiency of zang–fu organs, wind cold directly attacking the heart or phlegm stasis obstructing the heart vessels.

V012-N02191.label: 卒死
V012-N02191.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02191.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02191.pinyinNames: zu si; zú sǐ; cu si
V012-N02191.definitions: Characterized by unexpected critical signs that occur within six hours. These include loss of consciousness, absence of pulsations at the radial, carotid and anterior tibial arteries, respiratory/cardiac arrest, cyanosis, pupil dilation and cold limbs. These signs are caused by separation between yin and yang.

V012-N02192.label: 卒腹痛
V012-N02192.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02192.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02192.englishNames: sudden abdominal pain; Sudden abdominal pain
V012-N02192.pinyinNames: zu fu tong; cù fù tòng
V012-N02192.definitions: Characterized by sudden intolerable and progressively aggravated abdominal pain or colic. Contributing factors may include stagnant qi and blood in the stomach and intestines due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, food poisoning, parasites, stagnant food, stones or dry faeces.

V012-N02193.label: 历节风
V012-N02193.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02193.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02193.englishNames: multiple arthralgia; Joint wind; joint-running wind
V012-N02193.pinyinNames: li jie feng; lì jié fēng
V012-N02193.definitions: Characterized by severe, migratory joint pain, swelling, redness and hotness, coupled with impaired movement. The symptoms are aggravated at night and alleviate during the day. Often occurs when wind, cold or dampness affect the meridians or, over time, transform into heat to affect the joints.

V012-N02194.label: 厌食病
V012-N02194.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02194.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02194.englishNames: Loss of appetite; anorexia disease; anorexia
V012-N02194.pinyinNames: yàn shí bìng; yan shi bing
V012-N02194.definitions: Characterized by ingestion of small amount of food, aversion to food, a poor appetite, weight loss, fatigue, and a body weight that is 75–85% below the standard weight. In severe cases, malnutrition and anaemia may also be present. Often results from qi deficiency of the spleen and stomach and stomach yin deficiency due to poor eating habits, dieting or acute/chronic diseases.

V012-N02195.label: 厥头痛
V012-N02195.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02195.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02195.pinyinNames: jue tou tong; jué tóu tòng
V012-N02195.definitions: Characterized by intense headache, a red face and restlessness. Nausea, vomiting, cold hands/feet and significantly elevated blood pressure may be present. Often occurs when wind yang ascends and disordered qi and blood affect the head.

V012-N02196.label: 厥心痛
V012-N02196.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02196.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
