V012-N02196.englishNames: precordial pain; Cardiac pain with cold limbs; true heart pain with cold limbs; Pericordial Pain with Cold Limbs; precordial pain with cold limbs
V012-N02196.pinyinNames: jué xīn tòng; jue xin tong
V012-N02196.definitions: A paroxysmal heart condition characterized by cardiac pain radiating toward the back and aggravates upon physical exertion and alleviates after rest. Associated symptoms include upward rushing of qi to the throat, chest discomfort, repeated sighing, cold limbs and a dark-bluish facial complexion. Often results from deficiency of the five zang organs, emotional disturbance, and pathogenic factors affecting the pericardium or phlegm stasis obstructing the heart.

V012-N02197.label: 厥病
V012-N02197.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02197.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02197.definitions: A group of emergent conditions characterized by sudden fainting and cold limbs. Contributing factors may include mental stress, eating disorder, six exogenous pathogenic factors, pestilence, phlegm dampness, drug poisoning or food poisoning. These factors may block the flow of qi and cause the disorder of qi and blood.

V012-N02198.label: 厥脱类病
V012-N02198.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02198.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02198.englishNames: syncope and prostration diseases; Syncope/fainting; category of syncope and prostration
V012-N02198.pinyinNames: jue tuo lei bing; jué tuō lèi bìng
V012-N02198.definitions: A group of critical conditions caused by disorder of qi and blood, exhaustion of yang qi or collapse of yin blood. Contributing factors may include mental stress, eating disorder, drug poisoning, food poisoning or external contraction of pathogenic factors.

V012-N02199.label: 反胃病
V012-N02199.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02199.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02199.pinyinNames: fan wei bing; fǎn wèi bìng
V012-N02199.definitions: Characterized by vomiting in the evening of food eaten in the morning and vomiting in the morning of food eaten in the evening. In severe cases, vomiting immediately after eating, gastric stuffiness, abdominal distension and indigestion may be present. Often results from an upward flow of rebellious stomach qi due to chronic gastrointestinal conditions, surgery, abdominal tumour or retention of food and phlegm in the stomach.

V012-N02200.label: 发
V012-N02200.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02200.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02200.englishNames: cellulitis; Effusion; pyogenic carbuncle; phlegmon; effusion
V012-N02200.definitions: Characterized by a sudden onset of large abscesses. Contributing factors may include exogenous fire heat, traumatic wound, internal retention of damp heat and toxic fire. It includes cervical abscess, gluteal abscess, abscess of the calf and effusions on the back of the hands/feet.

V012-N02201.label: 发热
V012-N02201.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02201.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02201.definitions: Having a temperature above the normal range or a subjective feverish sensation. Contributing factors may include exogenous pathogenic factors, pestilence, emotions or over-exertion. It is often seen in externally contracted conditions, sores/ulcers, consumptive conditions, or heat in the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02202.label: 发瘤
V012-N02202.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02202.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02202.definitions: Characterized by cystic tumours in the skin that may contain powder-like stuff or hair growth. They are often caused by fetal heat and retention of stagnant blood or turbid phlegm in the skin.

V012-N02203.label: 发际疮
V012-N02203.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02203.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02203.englishNames: hairline sore; multiple folliculitis of nape; Hairline sore; hairline boil; hairline boils
V012-N02203.pinyinNames: fa ji chuang; fà jì chuāng
V012-N02203.definitions: Small, raised, swollen sores that occur at the back of the neck close to the hairline. They may cause severe pain or itching. These sores are often caused by internal retention of damp heat coupled with external contraction of wind fire.

V012-N02204.label: 口僻
V012-N02204.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02204.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02204.englishNames: Facial paralysis disorder; deviated mouth; wry mouth; Deviation of the mouth corner
V012-N02204.definitions: Characterized by a sudden facial numbness, deviation of the eyes and mouth corner and slurred speech. Often occurs when wind cold or wind heat affects the face or wind phlegm obstructs the meridians.

V012-N02205.label: 口疳
V012-N02205.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02205.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02205.englishNames: oral ulcer; Mouth ulcer; oral gan disease; infantile malnutrition with aphthae
V012-N02205.definitions: Characterized by single/multiple red, painful ulcers that occur on the mucous membrane of the oral cavity. Often results from emotional stagnation transforming into fire, heat accumulated in the heart and spleen, or hyperactive fire (due to yin deficiency) scorching the oral mucosa.

V012-N02206.label: 口糜
V012-N02206.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02206.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02206.englishNames: oral erosion; aphthous stomatitis; coccigenic stomatitis; aphtha; oral erosion (aphtha); Oral erosion; aphthouf stomatitis
V012-N02206.definitions: Characterized by multiple spots of erosion, congestion, oedema and pseudomembrane on the buccal mucosa, and foul breath. Often results from internal build up of damp heat or deficiency fire scorching the oral cavity.

V012-N02207.label: 口舌痰包
V012-N02207.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02207.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02207.englishNames: phlegmatic mass in mouth and tongue; phlegmatic cyst in the mouth and tongue; Sublingual cysts
V012-N02207.pinyinNames: kou she tan bao; kǒu shé tán bāo
V012-N02207.definitions: Characterized by presence of round, smooth and flexible cysts inside the oral cavity or under the tongue. They are often caused by retained phlegm dampness.

V012-N02208.label: 口齿病类
V012-N02208.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02208.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02208.definitions: A group of mouth and tongue disorders caused by damp heat in the spleen and stomach, ascending of deficiency fire, exuberance of stomach fire, ascending of wind heat and phlegm fire, dental caries, or phlegm dampness due to dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02209.label: 合谷疔
V012-N02209.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02209.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02209.englishNames: hegu boil; Hegu ding; hegu（LI 4）ding; Hegu boil
V012-N02209.pinyinNames: he gu ding; hé gǔ dīng
V012-N02209.definitions: Characterized by boils on the arc formed by the thumb and index finger of the hand.

V012-N02210.label: 吐酸
V012-N02210.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02210.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02210.aliases: 吞酸； 泛酸； 噫酸； Acid reflux
V012-N02210.englishNames: acid vomiting; acid regurgitation; Acid regurgitation
V012-N02210.definitions: The swallowing of acid contents regurgitated from the stomach to the throat or vomiting out of the mouth. It is often accompanied by stomach discomfort and belching. Contributing factors may include liver qi stagnation, stomach qi disharmony, liver fire affecting the stomach, food stagnation in the spleen and stomach, or ascending of fluid due to deficiency cold of the spleen and stomach. It is often seen in improper diet, oesophageal heat, stomach ulcer or stomach distension.

V012-N02211.label: 呃逆病
V012-N02211.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02211.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02211.pinyinNames: e ni bing; è nì bìng
V012-N02211.definitions: Characterized by involuntary brief “hic” sounds and, sometimes, gastric discomfort. Often results from an upward flow of rebellious stomach qi due to ingestion of cold, spicy food or intense emotions affecting the diaphragm.

V012-N02212.label: 呕吐
V012-N02212.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02212.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02212.definitions: The involuntary, forceful expulsion of the contents of the stomach through the mouth. Contributing factors may include exogenous/endogenous diseases, head injury, pregnancy and drug poisoning. These factors may affect the descending of stomach qi. It is often seen in gastrointestinal diseases, disorders of the liver, gallbladder and pancreas, motion sickness, morning sickness, intracranial lesion, qi exhaustion, or drug poisoning.

V012-N02213.label: 呕血
V012-N02213.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02213.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02213.definitions: The bleeding from the upper gastrointestinal tract (stomach or oesophagus) out of the mouth. Contributing factors may include heat accumulating in the liver and stomach, spleen qi deficiency or stagnant blood. These factors may affect the descending of stomach qi. It is often seen in stomach heat, stomach ulcer, tympanites, bleeding disorders, epidemic haemorrhagic fever, kidney failure, stomach or liver cancer.

V012-N02214.label: 周痹
V012-N02214.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02214.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02214.englishNames: Generalized impediment; general arthralgia; bi disease with general apin; general impediment; bi disease with general pain
V012-N02214.definitions: Characterized by recurrent, migratory and asymmetrical joint pain. Associated symptoms may include limb heaviness, numbness, joint redness and swelling, and neck stiffness. Often occurs in patients with a weakened immune system when wind, cold, dampness or heat affect the blood flow.

V012-N02215.label: 咯血
V012-N02215.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02215.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02215.definitions: The expectoration of blood from the throat or along the respiratory tract below the throat. Contributing factors may include fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, ascending of heart fire, or pathogenic heat accumulating in the lung. It is often seen in pharyngitis or lung diseases.

V012-N02216.label: 咳嗽
V012-N02216.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02216.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02216.aliases: 暴咳； 内伤咳嗽； 外感咳嗽； 咳逆
V012-N02216.pinyinNames: ké sou; ke sou; ke su
V012-N02216.definitions: The noisy expulsion of “cough” sound from the lungs. Contributing factors may include six exogenous pathogenic factors six exogenous pathogenic factors affecting the lung, exposure to irritant gas, phlegm fluid retention in the lung, or deficiency of qi and yin. These factors may cause lung qi to ascend. It is often seen in early-stage exogenous diseases, lung diseases, or conditions of other zang–fu organs that affect the lung.

V012-N02217.label: 咳血
V012-N02217.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02217.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02217.definitions: The coughing up of blood from the bronchi or lungs. Contributing factors may include traumatic injuries, exogenous pathogenic factors affecting the lung, liver fire affecting the lung, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, or qi failing to keep blood with the vessels. It is often seen in pulmonary tuberculosis, lung cancer or emphysema. It may also occur as a result of bleeding disorders, epidemic haemorrhagic fever or heart failure.

V012-N02218.label: 咽喉病类
V012-N02218.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02218.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02218.definitions: A group of throat disorders caused by wind, cold, dampness or heat affecting the throat; wind, heat or phlegm fire affecting the throat or tonsil; smoking or alcohol drinking consuming yin fluids; phlegm qi stagnation in the throat; foreign bodies trapped in the throat; heat accumulated in the zang–fu organs or malnourishment of the throat due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02219.label: 咽喉菌
V012-N02219.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02219.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02219.aliases: 咽菌； 喉菌； 喉岩
V012-N02219.englishNames: throat cancer; throat carcinoma; Throat cancer
V012-N02219.pinyinNames: yan hou jun; yān hóu jūn
V012-N02219.definitions: Characterized by sore throat, dysphagia, a foreign body sensation in the throat, presence of lumps in the throat with a rough surface. It is often caused by qi stagnation, blood stasis and retention of turbid phlegm in the throat for a long period of time.

V012-N02220.label: 哮喘病
V012-N02220.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02220.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02220.pinyinNames: xiao chuan bing; xiào chuǎn bìng
V012-N02220.definitions: Characterized by recurrent episodes of difficulty breathing, wheezing and shortness of breath with an inability to lie flat. Contributing factors may include congenital deficiency or impaired functions of the lung, spleen and kidney. These factors may generate phlegm to obstruct the airway.

V012-N02221.label: 哮病
V012-N02221.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02221.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02221.aliases: 哮喘； 哮证； Wheezing disorder； Wheezing pattern
V012-N02221.englishNames: Wheezing disorder; asthma; wheezing disease; wheezing; Wheezing; wheezing [disease/disorder]
V012-N02221.definitions: Characterized by sudden, whistling sounds with an inability to lie flat. Precursory symptoms may include an itchy nose, sneezing, cough and chest tightness. Contributing factors may include heredity, exposure to dust or irritant gas, exogenous pathogenic factors, fatigue, improper diet or emotions. These factors, coupled with pre-existing phlegm may cause phlegm qi to stagnate and obstruct the respiratory airways.

V012-N02222.label: 唇湿
V012-N02222.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02222.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02222.definitions: Characterized by lip skin peeling, dryness and rhagadia. It is often caused by damp heat in the spleen and stomach, coupled with exposure to wind.

V012-N02223.label: 唇风
V012-N02223.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02223.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02223.aliases: Lip Exfoliative Inflammation； 驴嘴风； 唇燥裂； Exfoliative cheilitis
V012-N02223.englishNames: exfoliative cheilitis; Exfoliative Cheilitis; lip wind; chronic cheilitis; exfoliating cheilitis; Lip wind
V012-N02223.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, itchy and painful lips with cracks and exudation. Individuals may also experience peeling lips and twitching of lips. Often results from contraction of exogenous wind heat and dampness or ascending of accumulated damp heat in the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02224.label: 喉关痈
V012-N02224.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02224.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02224.pinyinNames: hou guan yong; hóu guān yōng
V012-N02224.definitions: Characterized by intense sore throat, fever, difficulty swallowing and red, swollen epiglottis. It is often caused by retention of pathogenic toxin in the epiglottis.

V012-N02225.label: 喉咳
V012-N02225.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02225.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02225.englishNames: laryngeal cough; throat coughing; throat cough; Laryngitis
V012-N02225.definitions: Characterized by sudden or recurrent dry, scratchy throat, cough with scanty or no sputum, hoarse voice, and mild congestion and swelling of the throat. It is often caused by exposure to pathogenic factors, fume or odour stimuli, chronic cough, or malnourishment of the throat due to yin deficiency of the lung and kidney.

V012-N02226.label: 喉痈
V012-N02226.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02226.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02226.englishNames: throat abscess; Peritonsillar abscess; Throat Abscess
V012-N02226.definitions: Characterized by high fever, red, swollen throat with intense pain and difficulty swallowing. It is often caused by retention of toxic heat coupled with contraction of exogenous pathogens, causing slough and sepsis in the throat area.

V012-N02227.label: 喉痹
V012-N02227.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02227.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02227.aliases: 喉闭； 喉闭疮； Pharyngitis； Sore Throat
V012-N02227.englishNames: throat impediment; throat obstruction; pharyngitis; Throat Obstruction; Throat impediment
V012-N02227.definitions: Characterized by throat redness, swelling and pain. Alternatively, individuals may experience a dry throat, a foreign body sensation or an unpleasant, scratchy throat. Often results from exogenous pathogens affecting the throat, malnourishment of the throat due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs, or localized stagnation of qi and blood due to ascending of deficiency fire.

V012-N02228.label: 喉癣
V012-N02228.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02228.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02228.englishNames: throat tinea; membranous pharyngitis; lichenoid erosion of the throat; lichenoid erosion of throat; throat tuberculosis; tinea-like erosion of throat; Tuberculosis of the throat
V012-N02228.definitions: Characterized by dry, sore throat with a burning sensation, localized ulceration with surrounding redness and lichen-like indentation. Hot flush, night sweats and hoarse voice may also be present. It is often caused by infection with the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis or malnourishment of the throat due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02229.label: 喘病
V012-N02229.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02229.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02229.aliases: 喘证； Dyspnoea disorder； Dyspnoea pattern
V012-N02229.definitions: Characterized by shortness of breath, rapid breathing, and difficulty breathing that worsens upon exertion. In severe cases, breathing with an open mouth and elevated shoulder, nasal flaring and an inability to lie flat may be present. Associated symptoms may include cough with profuse phlegm, chest tightness, cyanosis, raised chest and distension in the subcostal region. Contributing factors may include heredity, exogenous pathogenic wind cold affecting the lung, turbid phlegm accumulating in the lung, chronic cough damaging lung qi or kidney failing to absorb qi.

V012-N02230.label: 嗜睡
V012-N02230.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02230.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02230.definitions: Prolonged night time sleep and excessive daytime sleepiness. Contributing factors may include yang qi deficiency or internal retention of phlegm dampness/stagnant blood. It is often a warning sign of unconsciousness in exogenous diseases and commonly seen in hypersomnia, dementia, obesity, head injuries, poisoning, or consumptive conditions.

V012-N02231.label: 嘈杂
V012-N02231.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02231.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02231.englishNames: stomach upset; Epigastic Upset; gastric upset; Gastric upset; Epigastric upset disorder; epigastric upset
V012-N02231.definitions: A subjective feeling of stomach emptiness, the sensation of fullness after a very small amount of food, abdominal distension or unexplained discomfort. Often results from an improper diet, stomach cold, stomach heat, yin blood deficiency or disharmony between the liver and stomach. It is commonly seen in problems of stomach, oesophagus, gallbladder or pancreas.

V012-N02232.label: 噎膈病
V012-N02232.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02232.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02232.pinyinNames: ye ge bing; yē gé bìng
V012-N02232.definitions: Characterized by difficulty in swallowing, vomiting immediately after eating food or inability to swallow solid food (a feeling of food stuck or held up before it either passes into the stomach or is regurgitated). Often occurs when phlegm qi stagnation (plum pit qi), stagnant fire, blood stasis or cancer toxin obstructs the oesophagus.

V012-N02233.label: 噤口痢
V012-N02233.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02233.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02233.englishNames: anorectic dysentery; food-denying dysentery; Fasting Dysentery; Food-denying dysentery
V012-N02233.pinyinNames: jin  kou li; 噤kou li; jin kou li; jìn kǒu lì
V012-N02233.definitions: Characterized by nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite, frequent, severe dysentery and muscle wasting. Often occurs when epidemic damp heat toxin accumulates in the intestine to affect the stomach and damage qi and yin.

V012-N02234.label: 囊虫病
V012-N02234.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02234.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02234.pinyinNames: nang chong bing; náng chóng bìng
V012-N02234.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by eating food or drinking water contaminated by tapeworm eggs from human faeces. It is mainly found in children and characterized by palpable subcutaneous solid lumps, seizures, visual impairment and presence of tapeworms. The tapeworm eggs enter the intestine and develop into larvae, which binds with phlegm turbidity and affect the skin, brain and eyes.

V012-N02235.label: 圆癣
V012-N02235.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02235.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02235.aliases: 钱癣； 金钱癣； Tinea corporis disorder； Coin-shaped tinea
V012-N02235.englishNames: tinea circinata; round tinea(tinea circinata); Tinea circinate disorder; Tinea corporis
V012-N02235.definitions: Characterized by itchy, red rashes, blisters, crusting and scaling with a ring, especially on glabrous (hairless) skin. Often results from internal build up of damp heat and contraction of exogenous pathogens.

V012-N02236.label: 圆翳内障
V012-N02236.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02236.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02236.aliases: 如银内障； 沉翳； 浮翳； 老年性白内障； 园翳内障； LENS OPACITY INTER OCULOPATHY
V012-N02236.englishNames: round cataract; Cataract; round nebula cataract(senile cataract); cataract; Lens Opacity Internal Oculopathy; senile cataract; round nebular cataract
V012-N02236.pinyinNames: yuan yi nei zhang; yuán yì nèi zhàng
V012-N02236.definitions: Characterized by clouding of the lens in the eye leading to a decrease in vision. Often results from ageing, deficiency of the liver and kidney due to diabetes, spleen deficiency or retained heat in the liver meridian.

V012-N02237.label: 坐板疮
V012-N02237.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02237.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02237.englishNames: seat sore; multiple folliculitis of buttock; bullock sore; furunculosis of buttock; Seat sore; buttock sore
V012-N02237.pinyinNames: zuo ban chuang; zuò bǎn chuāng
V012-N02237.definitions: Sores on the buttock. They are often caused by retention of summer heat and dampness in the skin.

V012-N02238.label: 外感咳嗽
V012-N02238.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02238.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02238.aliases: Cough upon exposure to exogenous factors
V012-N02238.englishNames: exogenous cough; cough due to external contraction; Cough due to external contraction
V012-N02238.pinyinNames: wai gan ke su; wài gǎn ké sou
V012-N02238.definitions: Characterized by a sudden onset, urgent coughing sound, difficult expectoration of sputum, or coughing with scanty, yellow or white sputum. Associated symptoms include fever, headache, stuffy/runny nose, a red, sore and scratchy throat, and a dry mouth. Often occurs when six exogenous pathogenic factors or pestilence attack the lung and causes lung qi to ascend.

V012-N02239.label: 外感时令类病
V012-N02239.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02239.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02239.englishNames: seasonal external contraction diseases; category of seasonal external contraction disease; Seasonal disorders
V012-N02239.pinyinNames: wai gan shi ling lei bing; wài gǎn shí lìng lèi bìng
V012-N02239.definitions: A group of externally contracted disorders caused by fatigue, deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi, or failure to adjust to exogenous wind, cold, summer heat or dampness. They are characterized by seasonal onset and rarely contagious. Examples of seasonal disorders include common cold, dampness obstructing the spleen and stomach, summer heat stroke, autumn dryness, etc.

V012-N02240.label: 外感病类
V012-N02240.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02240.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02240.definitions: A group of disorders caused by external pathogenic factors such as wind, cold, summer heat, dampness, warmth or pestilence. These are further classified into seasonal exogenous disorders, cold damage, warm disease and epidemics from pestilence.

V012-N02241.label: 外感痉病
V012-N02241.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02241.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02241.aliases: 刚痉； 柔痉； Convulsive disorder； Convulsion disorder
V012-N02241.englishNames: exogenous convulsive disease; Convulsive disorders upon exposure to exogenous pathogens
V012-N02241.pinyinNames: wai gan jing bing; wài gǎn jìng bìng
V012-N02241.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to heat (alternatively, aversion to cold may be present), neck rigidity, shaking of the head, lockjaw, and convulsions of the hands/feet. In severe cases, opisthotonos may be present. A hard convulsion has no sweating, whereas a soft convulsion has sweating. Often occurs when pathogenic wind cold, dampness, summer heat, damp heat or warmth affect the meridians or tendons. It may also occur when pathogenic heat enters the interior to stir liver wind, coupled with internal heat consuming yin fluids.

V012-N02242.label: 外痔
V012-N02242.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02242.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02242.englishNames: external hemorrhoids; external hemorrhoid; External haemorrhoids
V012-N02242.definitions: Characterized by varicose dilatation of a vein below the dentate line, a weight-bearing sensation, pain and a foreign body sensation within the anus. External haemorrhoids often results from downward flow of damp heat or skin friction or tear around the anus.

V012-N02243.label: 外阴臊疣
V012-N02243.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02243.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02243.englishNames: vulval verruca acuminata; vulvar condyloma; Genital condyloma
V012-N02243.pinyinNames: wai yin sao you; wài yīn sào yóu
V012-N02243.definitions: Characterized by infectious cauliflower-like warts that commonly affect the genitalia. They have a moist surface and tend to bleed. These warts are often caused by internal retention of damp heat and exposure to toxins.

V012-N02244.label: 外障类病
V012-N02244.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02244.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02244.englishNames: exogenous ocular diseases; Eye disorders involving the eyelid, inner/outer canthus, sclera and iris.
V012-N02244.pinyinNames: wai zhang lei bing; wài zhàng lèi bìng
V012-N02244.definitions: Conditions of the eyelids, inner/outer canthus, sclera and iris caused by six exogenous pathogens, fire heat in the five zang organs, phlegm dampness due to spleen deficiency, ascending of deficiency fire due to yin deficiency of the liver and kidney, or eye injuries.

V012-N02245.label: 多寐病
V012-N02245.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02245.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02245.pinyinNames: er mei bing; dong mei bing; duō mèi bìng
V012-N02245.definitions: Paroxysmal and uncontrollable excessive daytime sleepiness. Often occurs when turbid phlegm obstructs the ascending of clear yang.

V012-N02246.label: 夜啼
V012-N02246.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02246.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02246.englishNames: Night Crying; nocturnal crying; night crying; Night crying disorder; nisht crying; Night crying
V012-N02246.definitions: A condition that often occurs in babies under the age of one year. Characterized by continuous crying during or even through the night but appearing normal at daytime. Often results from spleen cold, heart heat and fear or fright that disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02247.label: 大偻
V012-N02247.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02247.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02247.definitions: Characterized by pain in the lower back or gluteal region, stiffness of the lower back, and impaired movement. Over time, individuals may present with loss of spinal mobility, and limitation of anterior flexion, lateral flexion, and extension of the lumbar spine. Leg weakness, migratory joint pain that aggravates upon cold and feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest may also be present. Often results from kidney deficiency or exposure to wind, cold and damp heat.

V012-N02248.label: 大头瘟
V012-N02248.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02248.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02248.englishNames: swollen-head epidemic; swollen-head infection; fever with swollen head; erysipelas facialis; swollen head due to infection; pestilence with swollen face; Swollen face
V012-N02248.pinyinNames: da tou wen; dà tóu wēn
V012-N02248.definitions: Characterized by fever, chills, and local (face, ear, neck and throat) redness, swelling, hotness and pain. It is more common in winter or spring. This condition often occurs when seasonal wind heat toxin attacks the three yang meridians in the face. It is known as erysipelas facialis in modern medicine.

V012-N02249.label: 大瘕泄
V012-N02249.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02249.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02249.pinyinNames: da xia xie; da jia xie; dà jiǎ xiè
V012-N02249.definitions: Characterized by diarrhoea, abdominal pain, tenesmus and stools that contain mucus or blood. Often occurs when internal build up of damp heat (due to emotions or improper diet) obstructs the flow of intestinal qi and impairs intestinal collaterals. Over time, individuals may develop deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02250.label: 大脚风
V012-N02250.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02250.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02250.englishNames: swollen foot wind wilting; Elephant skin-like leg; swollen foot wind flaccidity; elephantiasis of leg
V012-N02250.pinyinNames: da jiao feng; dà jiǎo fēng
V012-N02250.definitions: Characterized by tight, shiny and swollen skin in the lower leg, followed by thickening and darkening of the skin that resembles elephant skin. It is often caused by wind, dampness and heat obstructing the flow of qi and blood due to filariasis or recurrent erysipelas.

V012-N02251.label: 天疱疮
V012-N02251.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02251.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02251.englishNames: lingering pemphigus; Pemphigus; pemphigus and pemphigoid
V012-N02251.pinyinNames: tian pao chuang; tiān pào chuāng
V012-N02251.definitions: Commonly affects children in summer or autumn. Characterized by initial skin blisters that contain pus and have an inflamed base, which often begin on the scalp and torso. Some individuals may also experience skin lesions that do not cause itch or pain. Often results from summer heat and dampness that affects the lung meridian and scorches the skin.

V012-N02252.label: 天花
V012-N02252.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02252.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02252.aliases: 痘疹； 痘疮； 天行时痘； 天痘； 天行痘
V012-N02252.definitions: An infectious disease caused by the variola virus and often affects children. It is characterized by chills, fever and skin rashes, papules, sores, pustules and scabs. The latter may fall off, leaving marks on the skin. This condition usually passes through six stages: fever, early rashes, raised bumps, pustular rashes, scabs and falling off of the scabs.

V012-N02253.label: 天行赤眼
V012-N02253.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02253.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02253.aliases: 红眼病； Contagious Conjunctivitis； Epidemic Keratoconjunctivitis
V012-N02253.englishNames: epidemic conjunctivitis; epidemic red eye; epidemic keratoconjunctivitis; epidemic red eye(acute contagious conjunctivitis); Epidemic Red Eye; Epidemic conjunctivitis
V012-N02253.pinyinNames: tian xing chi yan; tiān xíng chì yǎn
V012-N02253.definitions: A highly contagious condition characterized by sudden onset of redness of the white of the eye, watery discharge, and sensitivity to bright lights. It can rapidly spread among the community and often results from exogenous wind heat, pestilence or coupled with heat accumulated in the lung and stomach.

V012-N02254.label: 失志
V012-N02254.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02254.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02254.englishNames: loss of will; Manic depression/ bipolar disorder
V012-N02254.definitions: A condition characterized by alternating depression and elevated mood. During manic phases, individuals are often excitable and may speak in a rapid, uninterruptible manner. During depressive phases, individuals often present with a low mood or self-loathing. Forgetfulness, timidity, fear and jumpiness or easily startled may also be present. Often occurs when stagnant qi, phlegm and stasis disturbs the heart mind. Contributing factors may include emotional disturbance, feeling of guilty, improper diet or hereditary factors.

V012-N02255.label: 失眠
V012-N02255.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02255.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02255.definitions: Prolonged inability to get sufficient sleep. Contributing factors may include over-thinking, deficiency of qi and blood, liver qi stagnation, internal fire heat, or retention of turbid phlegm. These factors may cause disharmony between yin and yang and subsequently, mental restlessness. It is often seen in sleeplessness, neurasthenia, depression, mania, hysteria and/or some systemic conditions.

V012-N02256.label: 失荣
V012-N02256.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02256.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02256.englishNames: cervical malignancy with cachexia; cervical carcinoma with cachexia; Cervical malignancy with cachexia
V012-N02256.definitions: This refers to advanced malignant tumour of the cervical lymph node, either primary or metastatic. During the early stage, individuals may present with deep-rooted, solid, immobile nodules in the cervical area or behind the ear, without skin discolouration. Over time, the nodules increased with mild pain and dark purple skin discolouration, flat or sunken lesion with a rough surface resembling a cauliflower, coupled with pain and discharge of strong-odoured pus and blood. Associated symptoms may include restlessness, weight loss and haggardness. Often occurs when phlegm fire stagnates in the cervical area. The phlegm results from worry or anxiety damaging the spleen. The fire is transformed from liver qi stagnation.

V012-N02257.label: 失音
V012-N02257.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02257.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02257.definitions: The occurrence of hoarseness or an inability to make sounds. Contributing factors may include wind cold or wind heat (toxic fire) affecting the throat, dry heat consuming the lung fluids, internal phlegm heat due to over-eating of oily, sweet food, essential qi deficiency due to chronic diseases, or liver qi stagnation. It is often seen in hoarseness, tuberculosis of the throat, qi exhaustion, throat polyps, diphtheria, or loss of voice in pregnancy.

V012-N02258.label: 头痛
V012-N02258.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02258.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02258.definitions: A subjective feeling of pain in the region of the head. Contributing factors may include contraction of wind, cold, damp or heat, emotions, ascending of wind yang and toxic heat, turbid phlegm or stagnant blood impairing the flow of meridian qi and blood, or malnourishment of the brain due to deficiency of qi, blood, Ying nutrients and essence. It is often seen in head wind, migraine, hemilateral head wind, facial wind and pain, cerebral arteriosclerosis, brain tumour, ear, nose and throat problems, exogenous diseases or other systemic conditions.

V012-N02259.label: 头身形体病类
V012-N02259.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02259.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02259.pinyinNames: tóu shēn xíng tǐ bìng lèi
V012-N02259.definitions: A group of disorders of the body tissues caused by six exogenous pathogens, strain, improper diet, phlegm-fluid retention or stagnant blood. These include brain diseases, thyroid disorders, breast diseases, genital diseases, hernia, bi-impediments, flaccidity, beriberi, xerosis and obesity.

V012-N02260.label: 头风病
V012-N02260.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02260.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02260.aliases: 头风； 头痛病； Head wind disorder； Headache
V012-N02260.pinyinNames: tou feng bing; tóu fēng bìng
V012-N02260.definitions: Characterized by chronic, recurrent headache with no fixed positions. Contributing factors may include external contraction of wind heat, wind cold or summer heat and dampness, hyperactive liver yang transforming into wind, or wind phlegm disturbing the mind. This condition is subcategorized into migraine, parietal headache or thunderclap headache.

V012-N02261.label: 奇恒痢
V012-N02261.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02261.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02261.pinyinNames: qi heng li; qí héng lì
V012-N02261.definitions: Characterized by a slow onset, abdominal pain, and mild diarrhoea with dark-red, jelly-like stools. In severe cases, a sudden high-grade fever, chills, a dry, swollen throat, unconsciousness, stools containing blood, tenesmus and severe abdominal pain are present. Other critical symptoms may include vomiting, fainting or sepsis. Often occurs when contaminated food/water, damp heat or cold dampness damage the intestinal collaterals or affect the heart and lung.

V012-N02262.label: 奔豚
V012-N02262.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02262.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02262.aliases: 奔豚气； 贲豚； 贲豚气； 肾积气； Running piglet qi； ascending of yin cold qi from the kidney
V012-N02262.englishNames: running piglet; kidney amassment; Running piglet; running-piglet; Gas Rushing Syndrome
V012-N02262.definitions: Characterized by qi rushing up from the lower abdomen to the chest and throat. Panic, abdominal pain or alternating chills and fever may also be present. Often results from ascending of yin cold qi from the kidney, fear/fright or ascending of liver qi along the meridian pathway.

V012-N02263.label: 女性前阴类病
V012-N02263.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02263.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02263.englishNames: Disorders of the female external genitalia; category of female external genital disease; female external genital diseases
V012-N02263.pinyinNames: nv xing qian yin lei bing; nǚ xìng qián yīn lèi bìng
V012-N02263.definitions: A group of disorders of the vulvae and female external genitalia not related to menstruation, leucorrhoea, pregnancy and childbirth.

V012-N02264.label: 女性生殖病
V012-N02264.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02264.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02264.englishNames: Female genital disorders; category of female genital disease; female genital diseases
V012-N02264.pinyinNames: nǚ xìng shēng zhí bìng; nv xing sheng zhi bing
V012-N02264.definitions: A group of disorders that affect the female reproductive system.

V012-N02265.label: 奶癣
V012-N02265.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02265.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02265.definitions: A condition that commonly affects infants or young children. It is characterized by dry or wet skin rashes on the face, torso, and hands and feet, coupled with exudation, skin tearing due to scratching, restlessness, and night crying. Often results from food or drug allergies or wind, damp or heat affecting the skin.

V012-N02266.label: 奶麻
V012-N02266.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02266.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02266.aliases: 假麻； Pseudorubella； 奶疹； 假诊
V012-N02266.definitions: Characterized by a sudden high fever and sometime with convulsion. Red skin rashes will appear all over the body after the fever disappeared in 3 to 5 days. Subsequently, rashes will resolve after 1 to 2 days. This condition often results from contraction of epidemic toxins.

V012-N02267.label: 妊娠下肢抽筋
V012-N02267.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02267.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02267.englishNames: Leg cramps during pregnancy; lower limbs spasm during pregnancy; lower limb cramp in pregnancy; lower-limb spasm during pregnancy
V012-N02267.pinyinNames: ren shen xia zhi chou jin; rèn shēn xià zhī chōu jīn
V012-N02267.definitions: Characterized by painful spasms during pregnancy that radiate through the calves, especially at night. Often results from the failure of liver blood to nourish the muscles and sinews.

V012-N02268.label: 妊娠偏头痛
V012-N02268.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02268.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02268.englishNames: migraine during pregnancy; Migraine during pregnancy
V012-N02268.pinyinNames: ren shen pian tou tong; rèn shēn piān tóu tòng
V012-N02268.definitions: Characterized by paroxysmal or persistent episodes of headache on one or both sides during pregnancy. Associated symptoms include nausea and vomiting. They often result from liver blood deficiency or yang hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02269.label: 妊娠消渴
V012-N02269.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02269.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02269.englishNames: Diabetes during pregnancy; consumptive thirst during pregnancy; consumptive-thirst disease during pregnancy; wasting and thirsting during pregnancy
V012-N02269.pinyinNames: rèn shēn xiāo kě; ren shen xiao ke
V012-N02269.definitions: Characterized by pre-existing diabetes or progression of latent diabetes upon pregnancy.

V012-N02270.label: 妊娠疱疹
V012-N02270.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02270.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02270.englishNames: gestational bleb; herpes during pregnancy; bleb during pregnancy; gestaional pemphigoid; Herpes simplex during pregnancy
V012-N02270.pinyinNames: ren shen pao zhen; rèn shēn pào zhěn
V012-N02270.definitions: Characterized by red, raised, and swollen or painful herpes zoster on the external genitalia, around the anus or in the vagina during pregnancy. Associated symptoms include increased vaginal discharge, painful urination, fatigue, low fever and tender, enlarged inguinal lymph node. Often results from infection of herpes virus.

V012-N02271.label: 妊娠瘙痒症
V012-N02271.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02271.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02271.englishNames: gestational pruritus; pruritus during pregnancy; Skin itching during pregnancy
V012-N02271.pinyinNames: ren shen sao yang zheng; rèn shēn sào yǎng zhèng
V012-N02271.definitions: Characterized by itchy skin or in severe cases, generalized itching all over the body during pregnancy. Often results from stagnant heat in the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N02272.label: 妊娠转胞
V012-N02272.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02272.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02272.aliases: 转胞； 妊娠小便不通
V012-N02272.englishNames: bladder colic during pregnancy; Urine retention in pregnancy
V012-N02272.pinyinNames: ren shen zhuan bao; rèn shēn zhuǎn bāo
V012-N02272.definitions: Characterized by urine retention and, in severe cases, sudden onset of lower abdominal distension and pain. Often occurs when downbearing fetal qi compresses the urinary bladder due to kidney deficiency or qi deficiency.

V012-N02273.label: 妊娠风疹
V012-N02273.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02273.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02273.englishNames: gestational rubella; rubella during pregnancy; Rubella during pregnancy
V012-N02273.pinyinNames: ren shen feng zhen; rèn shēn fēng zhěn
V012-N02273.definitions: Characterized by red skin rashes, maculae or papules during pregnancy, coupled with fever and headache. They often result from infection of rubella virus.

V012-N02274.label: 姜片虫病
V012-N02274.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02274.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02274.aliases: 小儿姜片虫病； 赤虫病； 扁虫病； paediatric fasciolopsiasis
V012-N02274.pinyinNames: jiang pian chong bing; jiāng piàn chóng bìng
V012-N02274.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by infection with the intestinal fluke Fasciolopsis buski. It is characterized by presence of flukes or their eggs in the faeces, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, a sallow complexion and weight loss. In children, this condition may cause poor growth and development. It occurs when people eat aquatic plants that have Fasciolopsis sp. encysted on them, which remain in the small intestine to obstruct the flow of qi and affect the transportation and transformation of the spleen.

V012-N02275.label: 子喑
V012-N02275.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02275.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02275.englishNames: Loss of voice during pregnancy; gestational aphonia
V012-N02275.definitions: Characterized by hoarseness or loss of voice during pregnancy. Often results from failure of kidney yin to ascend to nourish the throat.

V012-N02276.label: 子嗽
V012-N02276.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02276.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02276.englishNames: cough during pregnancy; gestational cough; Cough during pregnancy
V012-N02276.pinyinNames: zi su; zi sou; zǐ sòu
V012-N02276.definitions: Characterized by persistent cough during pregnancy. Often occurs when lung qi fails to descend. Contributing factors may include lung dryness due to yin deficiency, exposure to wind cold, or phlegm-fluid retention due to spleen deficiency.

V012-N02277.label: 子岩
V012-N02277.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02277.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02277.englishNames: testicle cancer; testiculus cancer; Testicular cancer
V012-N02277.definitions: Characterized by solid, rough-surfaced lumps in the testicle, which may or may not cause pain. The lump may grow rapidly and become attached to the scrotum. It is often caused by undescended testis, inflammation or retention of stagnant blood or turbid phlegm in the testicle.

V012-N02278.label: 子悬
V012-N02278.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02278.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02278.englishNames: pregnancy suspension; chest fullness during pregnancy; Floating sensation pregnancy disorder; distention in chest during pregnancy; gestational suspension; chest distention during pregnancy; floating sensation pregnancy disease; Ascending of fetal qi
V012-N02278.definitions: Characterized by a feeling of distension and fullness in the chest. Often results from disharmony between qi and blood due to liver qi stagnation or spleen deficiency.

V012-N02279.label: 子淋
V012-N02279.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02279.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02279.englishNames: stranguria during pregnancy; gestational stranguria; strangury of pregnancy; Stranguria during pregnancy; strangury during pregnancy
V012-N02279.definitions: Characterized by frequent, urgent, hesitant and painful urination during pregnancy. Often results from damp heat or deficiency fire impairing the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02280.label: 子满
V012-N02280.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02280.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02280.aliases: 妊娠肿满； 胎水胀满
V012-N02280.englishNames: polyhydramnios; hydramnios; Hydramnios during pregnancy; gestational edema and panting
V012-N02280.definitions: Characterized by the build up of too much amniotic fluid during pregnancy that causes abnormally enlarged abdomen, sensation of fullness and dyspnoea. Often results from yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney, coupled with internal retention of water dampness.

V012-N02281.label: 子烦
V012-N02281.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02281.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02281.englishNames: dysphoria during pregnancy; gestational dysphoria; pregnancy vexation; Vexation during pregnancy
V012-N02281.definitions: Characterized by vexation or feeling depressed during pregnancy. Alternatively, restlessness and irritability may be present. Often results from yin deficiency, phlegm fire or liver qi stagnation.

V012-N02282.label: 子痈
V012-N02282.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02282.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02282.englishNames: testicle carbuncle; epididymitis and orchitis; testicular abscess; epididy mitis and orchitis; Inflammatory infection of the testis and epididymis
V012-N02282.definitions: Characterized by testicle swelling and tenderness. Alternatively, individuals may experience dull pain in the scrotum of the affected side that radiates to the groin, coupled with a weight-bearing sensation or fever. Often occurs when damp heat, phlegm and stagnant blood retain in the testis or epididymis. Contributing factors may include damp heat or cold dampness affecting the testicle, improper diet (e.g. overeating of hot, spicy or oily food), sexual indulgence, liver qi stagnation transforming into fire or trauma.

V012-N02283.label: 子痫
V012-N02283.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02283.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02283.aliases: 妊娠痫证； 子冒； Eclampsia disorder
V012-N02283.englishNames: eclampsia; Eclampsia of pregnancy; Eclampsia disorder; Eclampsia; eclampsia of pregnancy
V012-N02283.definitions: Characterized by sudden vertigo, fainting, loss of consciousness, eyes fixed upward, trismus, vomiting white foam, convulsions of the limbs, and rigidity during the late pregnancy or before, during or after delivery. Individuals may return to normal after a while; however, some may develop into coma. Often results from internal stirring of liver wind or ascending of phlegm fire due to yin deficiency of the liver and kidney or consumption of yin blood.

V012-N02284.label: 子痰
V012-N02284.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02284.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02284.englishNames: tuberculosis of epididymis; Tuberculosis of the testicle
V012-N02284.definitions: Characterized by slowly progressive and painless lumps on the testicle. Over time, pus discharge upon rupture may be present. Often results from deficiency of healthy qi, insufficiency of the liver and kidney and retention of turbid phlegm in the testicle.

V012-N02285.label: 子眩
V012-N02285.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02285.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02285.englishNames: dizziness during pregnancy; pregnancy vertigo; Dizziness during pregnancy
V012-N02285.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, facial puffiness and limb oedema in middle or late stages of pregnancy. Often results from hyperactivity of liver yang, deficiency of qi and blood, or exuberance of phlegm turbidity.

V012-N02286.label: 子肿
V012-N02286.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02286.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02286.englishNames: gestational edema; gestational anasarca; Oedema during pregnancy; pregnancy swelling; edema in pregnant; edema during pregnancy
V012-N02286.definitions: Characterized by facial puffiness and swollen feet and ankles during the middle or late stages of pregnancy. Often results from water damp retention due to qi stagnation or yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02287.label: 子隐
V012-N02287.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02287.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02287.definitions: A condition that testis on one or both sides is/are not palpable. Often occurs as a result of congenital anomaly.

V012-N02288.label: 孕痈
V012-N02288.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02288.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02288.englishNames: intestinal abscess during pregnancy; abdominal carbuncle during pregnancy; Acute appendicitis during pregnancy
V012-N02288.definitions: Characterized by acute intestinal abscess (appendicitis) during pregnancy. Often results from accumulation of toxic heat.

V012-N02289.label: 客忤
V012-N02289.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02289.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02289.aliases: 小儿客忤； Fright seizure disorder； Infantile fright seizure
V012-N02289.englishNames: sudden frighten in infant; sudden frightening in infant; fright seizure; infantile fright; Fright seizure disorder; Fright seizure
V012-N02289.definitions: Characterized by sudden screaming or crying, vomiting, facial colour change, diarrhoea and abdominal pain. Often results from fright upon exposure to strangers or unknown events.

V012-N02290.label: 寄生虫病类
V012-N02290.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02290.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02290.definitions: Parasitic diseases are infectious diseases caused or transmitted by a parasite.

V012-N02291.label: 寒厥
V012-N02291.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02291.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02291.aliases: 阴厥； 冷厥； Cold syncope disorder； Yin syncope
V012-N02291.englishNames: cold syncope; cold type of cold limbs; Cold syncope
V012-N02291.definitions: Characterized by hypothermia, cold limbs and mental cloudiness. Chilblains may also be present. Often results from exposure to cold environment for a prolonged period or severe cold consuming yang qi.

V012-N02292.label: 寒湿痢
V012-N02292.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02292.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02292.englishNames: Cold dampness dysentery; cold-dampness dysentery; cold-damp dysentery
V012-N02292.pinyinNames: han shi li; hán shī lì
V012-N02292.definitions: Characterized by abdominal pain/spasm, paroxysmal abdominal pain that alleviates after bowel movements, tenesmus, body heaviness, and chronic diarrhoea with stools containing more mucus than blood or mucus alone. Often results from direct contraction of epidemic cold dampness toxin, contact with dysentery patients or pathogenic factors transforming into cold due to constitutional yang deficiency.

V012-N02293.label: 射工伤
V012-N02293.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02293.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02293.englishNames: caterpillar sting; pricked injury; Caterpillar stings
V012-N02293.pinyinNames: she gong shang; shè gōng shāng
V012-N02293.definitions: Exposure to toxic hair of caterpillars may cause intense itching, redness, swelling, blisters or ulcers.

V012-N02294.label: 小便不禁
V012-N02294.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02294.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02295.label: 小儿乳蛾
V012-N02295.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02295.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02295.englishNames: infantile tonsillitis; infantile nippled moth; Paediatric tonsillitis
V012-N02295.pinyinNames: xiao dong ru dong; xiao er ru e; xiǎo ér rǔ é
V012-N02295.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen tonsil with yellow and white pus-filled spots. Over time, the tonsil may become enlarged and hard on palpation. High fever, sore throat, swollen lymph nodes (glands) in the neck, difficulty swallowing and body aches may be present. It is often caused by pathogenic factors affecting the tonsil, ascending of toxic heat in the lung and stomach, fire (due to yin deficiency of the lung and kidney) scorching the tonsil, malnourishment of the tonsil due to weakness of the spleen and stomach, or retention of phlegm stasis in the throat.

V012-N02296.label: 小儿厌食
V012-N02296.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02296.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02296.pinyinNames: xiao er yan shi; xiao dong yan shi; xiǎo ér yàn shí
V012-N02296.definitions: Characterized by persistent poor appetite, aversion to food, and significant weight loss, but with normal spirit and physical activities. Alternatively, abdominal distension, belching, and irregular bowel movements may be present. It is often caused by spleen deficiency or damage to stomach yin due to inappropriate feeding or fright.

V012-N02297.label: 小儿呕吐
V012-N02297.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02297.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02297.pinyinNames: xiǎo ér ǒu tù; xiao er ou tu; xiao dong ou tu
V012-N02297.definitions: Characterized by vomiting of breast milk or food after feeding. It is often caused by overfeeding or damage to stomach qi.

V012-N02298.label: 小儿天花
V012-N02298.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02298.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02298.aliases: 小儿痘疹； 百岁疮； 天行痘
V012-N02298.pinyinNames: xiao dong tian hua; xiǎo ér tiān huā; xiao er tian hua
V012-N02298.definitions: An epidemic condition characterized by fever, cough, yawning, a red face, cold hands/feet/ears, followed by skin rashes. It usually lasts through six stages: fever, early rash, pustular rash, pustules, scabs, and resolving scabs. It is often caused by exposure to the smallpox virus.

V012-N02299.label: 小儿尿频
V012-N02299.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02299.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02299.englishNames: infantile frequen urination; infantile frequent urination; Paediatric frequent urination
V012-N02299.pinyinNames: xiao dong niao pin; xiao er niao pin; xiǎo ér niào pín
V012-N02299.definitions: Characterized by increased urination frequency with small amount of urine volume. Urgent, painful urination may also occur. It is often caused by impaired qi transformation of the urinary bladder due to retention of damp heat in the lower jiao, pinworm, stones or emotional disturbance.

V012-N02300.label: 小儿异食
V012-N02300.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02300.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02300.pinyinNames: xiao dong yi shi; xiao er yi shi; xiǎo ér yì shí
V012-N02300.definitions: An abnormal craving for objects not normally considered food, including fingernails, paintings on toys, mud, paper, hair, dirt, soil block, coal cinder or rocks. Ingestion of these objects may damage the body. It is often caused by spleen-related malnutrition or intestinal parasites.

V012-N02301.label: 小儿惊风
V012-N02301.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02301.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02301.pinyinNames: xiǎo ér jīng fēng; xiao dong jing feng; xiao er jing feng
V012-N02301.definitions: A collective term of infantile unconsciousness, convulsions or seizures due to external contraction or internal dysfunctions. It includes acute infantile convulsion, chronic infantile convulsion and chronic spleen wind.

V012-N02302.label: 小儿时令类病
V012-N02302.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02302.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02302.englishNames: seasonal epidemic diseases in pediatrics; Epidemic diseases in children
V012-N02302.pinyinNames: xiao er shi ling lei bing; xiao dong shi ling lei bing; xiǎo ér shí lìng lèi bìng
V012-N02302.definitions: A collective term of epidemic or seasonal paediatric conditions, including common cold, summer non-acclimatization, summer fever or chicken pox.

V012-N02303.label: 小儿温疫类病
V012-N02303.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02303.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02303.englishNames: infantile pestilence; infantile pestilence diseases; Contagious diseases in children
V012-N02303.pinyinNames: xiao er wen yi lei bing; xiǎo ér wēn yì lèi bìng; xiao dong wen yi lei bing
V012-N02303.definitions: A collective term of contagious paediatric diseases due to contraction of warm epidemic or pestilence.

V012-N02304.label: 小儿湮尻疮
V012-N02304.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02304.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02304.aliases: 臀红； Nappy/diaper dermatitis disorder； Red skin around the nappy/diaper
V012-N02304.pinyinNames: xiao dong yan kao chuang; xiǎo ér yān kāo chuāng
V012-N02304.definitions: Characterized by sore, red and irritated skin on the convex surfaces of the buttocks and the bulge of the groin. Often occurs when the baby’s skin become exposed to urine and faeces in a nappy/diaper.

V012-N02305.label: 小儿疫毒痢
V012-N02305.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02305.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02305.englishNames: infantile pestilent dysentery; fulminant dysentery in children; Paediatric bacillary dysentery; infantile fulminant dysentery
V012-N02305.pinyinNames: xiao dong yi dong li; xiǎo ér yì dú lì; xiao er yi er li; xiao er yi du li
V012-N02305.definitions: An epidemic condition characterized by sudden high fever, restlessness, intense abdominal pain, and stools containing pus and blood. In severe cases, convulsions, shock and cold limbs may be present. Often occurs when epidemic damp heat damages the intestines or enters the pericardium and triggers liver wind.

V012-N02306.label: 小儿痴呆
V012-N02306.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02306.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02306.pinyinNames: xiao er chi dai; xiao dong chi dai; xiǎo ér chī dāi
V012-N02306.definitions: Characterized by delayed movement or language development, dull expression, and learning disability. Vacant look, head shaking or tongue wagging may be present. It is often caused by genetic deficiency, malnutrition, brain injury, high fever and/or drug poisoning affecting brain development.

V012-N02307.label: 小儿痿病
V012-N02307.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02307.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02307.aliases: Infant flaccid disorder； Infant flaccidity disorder； Infant limpness disorder
V012-N02307.englishNames: infantile flaccidity; infantile flaccidity disease; flaccidity disease in children; infantile wilting disease; Paediatric flaccidity
V012-N02307.pinyinNames: xiao dong wei bing; xiao er wei bing; xiǎo ér wěi bìng
V012-N02307.definitions: Characterized by muscle flaccidity and impaired voluntary movement control. In severe cases, muscular atrophy or paralysis may be present. This condition is often caused by damp heat, emotional disturbance, genetic deficiency or qi deficiency of the lung and spleen that result in malnourishment of the bones, tendons and muscles.

V012-N02308.label: 小儿白喉
V012-N02308.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02308.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02308.pinyinNames: xiao dong bai hou; xiǎo ér bái hóu; xiao er bai hou
V012-N02308.definitions: An epidemic condition characterized by fever, sore throat, white film (pseudo-membrane) build up in the throat or nose. The layer of pseudo-film coating is hard to crack, and can cause bleeding after peeling off. In severe cases, difficulty in swallowing, breathing or shock may be present. The condition often begins with lung and stomach bacterial infection spreading to the throat area.

V012-N02309.label: 小儿相关病类
V012-N02309.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02309.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02309.aliases: Childhood and adolescence associated disorders； Child related diseases
V012-N02309.pinyinNames: xiǎo ér xiāng guān bìng lèi
V012-N02309.definitions: A collective term of paediatric conditions caused by congenital weakness, abnormal childbirth, external contraction of pathogenic factors, improper feeding, fear/fright or deficiencies of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02310.label: 小儿肺风痰喘
V012-N02310.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02310.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02310.aliases: 肺风痰喘； 肺炎喘嗽
V012-N02310.pinyinNames: xiǎo ér fèi fēng tán chuǎn
V012-N02310.definitions: Characterized by fever, cough, panting, nasal flaring and profuse phlegm. In severe cases, consistent high fever, unconsciousness, convulsions and seizures might occur. Often occurs when wind cold or warmth affects the descending and dispersing of lung qi.

V012-N02311.label: 小儿赤游丹
V012-N02311.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02311.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02311.pinyinNames: xiao dong chi you dan; xiǎo ér chì yóu dān; xiao er chi you dan
V012-N02311.definitions: Characterized by rapidly enlarged erythematous skin lesion, accompanied by fever and chills. In severe cases, high fever, unconsciousness and convulsions may be present. It is often caused by inappropriate care of skin injury or fetal toxin affecting the skin.

V012-N02312.label: 小儿软脚瘟
V012-N02312.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02312.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02312.englishNames: infantile pestilent flaccidity of foot; Infantile paralysis
V012-N02312.pinyinNames: xiao er ruan jiao wen; xiao dong ruan jiao wen; xiǎo ér ruǎn jiǎo wēn
V012-N02312.definitions: An infectious condition that starts with fever, sweating, headache, nausea, vomiting and diarrhoea. The fever may subdue after a few days and reappear, accompanied by neck stiffness, bulging fontanelle, hyperesthesia, refusing to be touched or hugged, and restlessness or drowsiness. Over time, the muscles become weak, steps floppy and poorly controlled, and can even end with physical handicap. Often occurs when epidemic summer heat and dampness enters the gastrointestinal tract thereafter affecting muscles or obstructing meridians. Alternatively, it may occur due to lack of yin fluids after a long period of illness and malnourishment of sinews.

V012-N02313.label: 小儿遗尿
V012-N02313.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02313.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02313.pinyinNames: xiao er yi niao; xiao dong yi niao; xiǎo ér yí niào
V012-N02313.definitions: Characterized by an inability to control urination during sleep after the age of five years. It is often caused by incomplete development of kidney qi resulting in bladder function failure.

V012-N02314.label: 小肠瘅
V012-N02314.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02314.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02314.englishNames: small intestinal heat disease; Acute haemorrhagic necrotizing enterocolitis
V012-N02314.pinyinNames: xiao chang dan; xiǎo cháng dān
V012-N02314.definitions: An acute condition characterized by sudden abdominal pain, diarrhoea, fever and blood in stools. Often results from internal damp heat, stagnation of qi and blood and ingestion of contaminated food.

V012-N02315.label: 尘肺
V012-N02315.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02315.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02315.definitions: A chronic condition characterized by persistent coughing, chest tightness/pain, and over time, panting upon physical exertion. Often occurs when inhaled dust in working or living environment obstructs lung collaterals and impairs the lung’s function in dispersing and descending.

V012-N02316.label: 尪痹
V012-N02316.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02316.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02316.englishNames: aggrevated bi; Joint impediment; arthral impediment
V012-N02316.definitions: Characterized by morning (joint) stiffness, multiple symmetrical pain of smaller joints, restricted movement or joint deformity. Often occurs when wind, cold, dampness or heat remain in the sinews, bones or joints, and over time, damages the yin blood of the liver and kidney.

V012-N02317.label: 尸厥
V012-N02317.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02317.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02317.englishNames: corpse-like syncope; cadaverous syncope; Sudden syncope
V012-N02317.definitions: A severe condition characterized by loss of consciousness, impaired ability to speak, faint breathing, body rigor, and bowel and bladder incontinence. Contributing factors may include medical conditions, poisoning or traumatic injuries. These factors may cause separation between yin and yang and damage to the Yuan-primordial qi.

V012-N02318.label: 尿崩
V012-N02318.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02318.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02318.englishNames: flooding urination disease/disorder; flooding urination; profuse urination; diabetes insipidus; Flooding urine disorder; Profuse urination (diabetes insipidus); profuse urine
V012-N02318.definitions: Characterized by large amounts of dilute urine and excessive thirst. Often occurs when the kidney fails to perform the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02319.label: 尿浊
V012-N02319.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02319.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02319.englishNames: turbid urine; Cloudy urine; turbid urine (disease); Turbid urine disorder
V012-N02319.definitions: Turbid, white urine (like rice-washed water). Often results from downward flow of damp heat or deficiency of the spleen and kidney. It is commonly seen in filariasis, renal tuberculosis, chronic prostatitis, kidney cancer, and chest or abdominal trauma or surgeries. It may also be seen in children with external contraction of pathogenic factors or internal dysfunctions.

V012-N02320.label: 尿血
V012-N02320.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02320.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02320.definitions: The brown or red discolouration of the urine. Contributing factors may include damp heat in the urinary bladder, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, exuberance of heart fire, pestilence or toxic drug damaging the kidney and urinary bladder. It is often seen in stony stranguria, heat stranguria, pregnancy strangury, kidney heat, renal tuberculosis, kidney/bladder cancer, blood disorders, or contagious diseases.

V012-N02321.label: 岩类病
V012-N02321.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02321.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02321.aliases: 癌症； 岩病； Carcinoma； Cancer
V012-N02321.definitions: Any solid lumps on the body surface, internal organs and tissues that look and feel like rocks, coupled with obstruction, pain, bleeding, rapid weight loss as well as positive findings of tumour markers, gene detection or biopsy. Contributing factors may include irregular lifestyle, emotional stagnation, family history, contaminated food or environment or chronic inflammation. These factors may cause qi and blood to stagnate and intertwine with toxic phlegm.

V012-N02322.label: 崩中
V012-N02322.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02322.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02322.definitions: Characterized by irregular menstrual cycle and sudden, profuse uterine bleeding. Often occurs when heat forces the blood to move recklessly. Alternatively, it may occur as a result of stagnant blood or qi failing to secure the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02323.label: 崩漏
V012-N02323.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02323.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02323.aliases: 暴崩； 崩中； 漏下； 血崩； Uterine Bleeding
V012-N02323.englishNames: metrorrhagia; metrostaxis; Metrorrhagia and metrostaxis; metrorrhagia and metrostaxis; Metrorrhagia disorder; metrostaxis and metrorrhagia; Metrorrhagia; flooding and spotting
V012-N02323.definitions: Characterized by a sudden onset of excessive uterine bleeding or small but continuous uterine bleeding. It includes metrorrhagia and metrostaxis, often resulting from insecurity of the Chong and Ren meridians. Contributing factors may include kidney deficiency, blood heat, damp heat, qi deficiency, blood stasis, or trauma, etc.

V012-N02324.label: 带下病类
V012-N02324.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02324.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02324.definitions: Characterized by abnormal vaginal discharges in amount, colour, quality and odour. It may appear whitish, yellowish or reddish. In some cases, individuals may have mixed-colour vaginal discharge or persistent, profuse and whitish vaginal discharges. This condition often results from downward flow of damp heat or damp toxin, unsafe sex, or deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02325.label: 干呕
