V012-N02325.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02325.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02325.definitions: The reverse movement of the stomach and oesophagus without vomiting of food. Often results from failure of stomach qi to descend due to stomach deficiency or liver qi stagnation.

V012-N02326.label: 干燥病
V012-N02326.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02326.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02326.pinyinNames: gan zao bing; gān zào bìng
V012-N02326.definitions: Characterized by dryness in mouth, nose or eyes. It can be accompanied by no sweating, dry skin and bone joint pain. It is often caused by yin deficiency or toxin infection, leading to yin consumption and thus malnourishment of skin, orifices and joints.

V012-N02327.label: 干疳
V012-N02327.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02327.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02327.englishNames: dry malnutrition; severe gan disease; dryness (infantile) malnutrition; dryness infantile malnutrition; Severe infantile malnutrition; chronic infantile malnutrition
V012-N02327.definitions: Characterized by withered skin, dry, thin and brittle hair, emaciation, oedema, purpura, nosebleed or gum bleeding. It is often caused by exhaustion of qi and blood due to extreme deficiency of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02328.label: 干脚气
V012-N02328.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02328.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02328.englishNames: weak foot due to dryness; Dry beriberi; dry beriberi
V012-N02328.pinyinNames: gan jiao qi; gān jiǎo qì
V012-N02328.definitions: Characterized by tingling or pain in legs and feet, and muscular atrophy and contracture. Associated symptoms may include mental confusion, delirium, foot/toe drop, abnormal gait and absence of knee reflex.

V012-N02329.label: 干霍乱
V012-N02329.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02329.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02329.aliases: 搅肠痧； 斑痧； 乌痧胀； Abdominal colic
V012-N02329.pinyinNames: gàn huò luàn; gan huo luan
V012-N02329.definitions: An infectious disease characterized by sudden onset of epigastric discomfort, abdominal colic, and an urge to but failure to vomit or defaecate. In severe cases, bluish complexion or generalized dark purple patches may be present. Contributing factors may include infection with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae or cold retention in the stomach or intestines. These factors may cause qi to stagnate in the chest and abdomen, and obstruct the qi flow of the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02330.label: 强中
V012-N02330.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02330.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02330.aliases: 阳强； Persistent erection disorder； 强中病
V012-N02330.definitions: Characterized by abnormally prolonged erection of the penis. Contributing factors may include liver fire, stasis, damp heat, overuse of aphrodisiac drugs or sexual indulgence. These factors may cause internal toxic fire and trigger ministerial fire to consume kidney yang and result in hyperactivity of deficiency yang.

V012-N02331.label: 心厥
V012-N02331.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02331.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02331.definitions: A severe condition characterized by a feeble pulse, cold limbs, low blood pressure and fainting or loss of consciousness. It is often caused by heart yang deficiency and floating of yang qi.

V012-N02332.label: 心悸
V012-N02332.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02332.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02332.definitions: Unpleasant, involuntary sensations of rapid, irregular and/or forceful beating of the heart. Often results from failure of qi and blood to nourish the heart, emotions, exogenous pathogens affecting the heart or phlegm stasis obstructing the heart vessels. It is commonly seen in heart impediment, heart heat, lung conditions, emotional disorders, chronic debility, or thyroid problems.

V012-N02333.label: 心水病
V012-N02333.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02333.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02333.pinyinNames: xīn shuǐ bìng; xin shui bing
V012-N02333.definitions: A severe heart condition characterized by palpitations, rapid breathing upon physical exertion, an inability to lie flat, coughing with foamy sputum, oedema, scanty urine or urine retention. It is often caused by heart qi deficiency, water retention due to heart yang deficiency or heart blood stasis.

V012-N02334.label: 心痛
V012-N02334.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02334.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02334.englishNames: heart pain; Cardiac pain; heart pain [disease/disorder]
V012-N02334.definitions: A subjective feeling of pain around the Danzhong point (Ren 17) and on the left side of the chest. Contributing factors may include internal phlegm turbidity and stagnant blood, cold retention due to yang deficiency, or yin blood deficiency. It is often seen in cardiac pain in chest bi-impediment, angina pectoris, heart bi-impediment, or heart heat.

V012-N02335.label: 心痹
V012-N02335.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02335.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02335.englishNames: heart impediment; heart bi; Heart bi-impediment
V012-N02335.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, chest tightness, shortness of breath, and flushed cheeks. In severe cases, cardiac murmurs may be present. Often occurs when wind, cold and damp heat affect the heart.

V012-N02336.label: 心瘅
V012-N02336.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02336.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02336.definitions: A condition characterized by fever, palpitations, chest tightness and a rapid, abrupt or regularly/irregularly intermittent pulse. Often occurs when exogenous warm heat or warm toxin due to surgical trauma affect the endocardium or cardiac muscle.

V012-N02337.label: 心系病
V012-N02337.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02337.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02337.englishNames: category of heart system disease; heart system diseases; Heart system disorders
V012-N02337.pinyinNames: xin xi bing; xīn xì bìng
V012-N02337.definitions: Dysfunctions of the heart system and meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, over-exertion, water retention and stagnant blood.

V012-N02338.label: 怔忡病
V012-N02338.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02338.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02338.englishNames: severe palpitations; severe palpitation; Fearful throbbing
V012-N02338.pinyinNames: zheng zhong bing; zhēng chōng bìng; zheng chong bing
V012-N02338.definitions: Characterized by irregular, paroxysmal and involuntary palpitations. Associated symptoms include chest tightness, shortness of breath, visible pulsations on the chest at the apex, umbilical venous pulsations, and a regularly/irregularly intermittent or abrupt and rapid pulse. Often results from heart yin/blood failing to nourish the heart, pathogenic factors obstructing the heart vessels or water retention (due to yang deficiency) affecting the heart.

V012-N02339.label: 急乳蛾
V012-N02339.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02339.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02339.englishNames: Acute tonsillitis; acute nippled moth; acute tonsillitis; acute tonsilitis
V012-N02339.pinyinNames: jí rǔ é; ji ru dong; ji ru e
V012-N02339.definitions: Characterized by fever and red, swollen and painful tonsil that resembles silkworm moth. It is often caused by wind heat affecting the tonsil.

V012-N02340.label: 急喉痹
V012-N02340.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02340.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02340.englishNames: Acute pharyngitis; acute throat obstruction; acute pharyngitis; acute throat impediment
V012-N02340.pinyinNames: jí hóu bì; ji hou bi
V012-N02340.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen and sore throat. It is often caused by pathogenic factors affecting the lung, stomach and throat.

V012-N02341.label: 急喉风
V012-N02341.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02341.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02341.aliases: 喉风； 锁喉风； 走马喉风
V012-N02341.englishNames: acute throat wind; acute laryngemphraxis; acute larynx wind; acute laryngeal infection; Acute laryngeal obstruction
V012-N02341.pinyinNames: ji hou feng; jí hóu fēng
V012-N02341.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen and sore throat, followed by profuse sputum and difficulty swallowing and breathing. It is often caused by ascending of wind heat and phlegm fire to the throat.

V012-N02342.label: 急惊风
V012-N02342.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02342.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02342.englishNames: acute infantile convulsion; Acute convulsion disorder; Acute infantile convulsion
V012-N02342.pinyinNames: ji jing feng; jí jīng fēng
V012-N02342.definitions: Characterized by a sudden onset of fever, loss of consciousness, blank staring, trismus, neck stiffness and convulsions of the limbs. Often results from seasonal pathogens, heat or indigestion in the stomach, fear/fright and phlegm accumulation that affects the heart and liver.

V012-N02343.label: 急风病
V012-N02343.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02343.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02343.englishNames: Acute wind stroke; acute wind disease
V012-N02343.pinyinNames: ji feng bing; jí fēng bìng
V012-N02343.definitions: Characterized by sudden collapse, loss of unconsciousness, lockjaw, upward fixation of the eyes, neck rigidity, and phlegm sounds in the throat. In severe cases, opisthotonos, loss of consciousness and convulsions may be present.Often occurs when wind phlegm mists the mind or epidemic pathogenic qi directly attacks the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02344.label: 性传播类病
V012-N02344.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02344.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02344.englishNames: Sexually transmitted diseases; category of sexually transmitted disease; sexually transmitted diseases
V012-N02344.pinyinNames: xìng chuán bō lèi bìng; xing chuan bo lei bing
V012-N02344.definitions: Sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) are infections that are spread through sexual contact.

V012-N02345.label: 恶核
V012-N02345.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02345.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02345.pinyinNames: dong he; e he; è hé
V012-N02345.definitions: Characterized by painless scrofula lumps, coupled with fever and weight loss. Often results from qi stagnation, essential qi deficiency or retention of cancer toxin and phlegm stasis in the surface of the body.

V012-N02346.label: 恶阻
V012-N02346.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02346.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02346.aliases: 妊娠呕吐； Morning sickness disorder； Vomiting during pregnancy disorder； Pregnancy vomiting； 妊娠恶阻
V012-N02346.englishNames: Morning sickness; Morning sickness disorder; morning sickness
V012-N02346.pinyinNames: dong zu; è zǔ; e zu; er zu
V012-N02346.definitions: Characterized by nausea, vomiting and aversion to food and smelling of oily food after one to two months of pregnancy. In severe cases, individuals may vomit immediately after eating. Often results from ascending of foetal qi and failure of stomach qi to descend.

V012-N02347.label: 悬痈
V012-N02347.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02347.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02347.englishNames: Huiyin abscess; Perineal abscess; vulva carbuncle; huiyin（CV 1）abscess
V012-N02347.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation in the perineum. Often results from deficiency of the liver, kidney and spleen, coupled with damp heat retention.

V012-N02348.label: 悬饮
V012-N02348.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02348.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02348.englishNames: Pleural fluid retention disorder; suspending fluid retention; pleural fluid retention; pleural fluid retention [disease/disorder]; Pleural fluid retention; suspending fluid
V012-N02348.definitions: Characterized by fullness, distension and tightness in the chest or subcostal region and cough-induced pain. Often occurs when fluid retention in the chest cavity (due to lung tuberculosis/cancer or other systemic conditions) obstructs the flow of qi.

V012-N02349.label: 情志病类
V012-N02349.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02349.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02349.definitions: Emotional disorders include plum pit qi, hysteria or depression. These disorders result from disorders of qi, blood, yin and yang as well as dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs and are often caused by intense mental stress or persistent emotional depression.

V012-N02350.label: 惊悸病
V012-N02350.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02350.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02350.englishNames: palpitation due to fright; Fright palpitations; fright-induced palpitations
V012-N02350.pinyinNames: jing ji bing; jīng jì bìng
V012-N02350.definitions: Characterized by fright-induced palpitations, panic, jumpiness or easily startled, insomnia or dream-disturbed sleep. Often results from heart qi deficiency, fright, or phlegm heat disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N02351.label: 感冒
V012-N02351.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02351.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02351.englishNames: common cold; Common Cold; Common cold disorder; common cold disease; Common cold
V012-N02351.definitions: Characterized by fever, chills, headache, body ache, nasal obstruction, sneezing, sore/scratchy throat and cough. In mild cases, common cold is known as wind injury (shang feng). An epidemic common cold, or influenza, often occurs when exogenous pathogenic factors affect the lung exterior.

V012-N02352.label: 慢乳蛾
V012-N02352.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02352.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02352.englishNames: Chronic tonsillitis; chronic tonsillitis; chronic tonsilitis; chronic nippled moth
V012-N02352.pinyinNames: màn rǔ é; man ru e; man ru dong
V012-N02352.definitions: Characterized by frequent discharge of pus from the tonsil with mild redness, swelling and throat discomfort. It is often caused by dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs and ascending of deficiency fire due to persistent tonsillitis.

V012-N02353.label: 慢喉痹
V012-N02353.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02353.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02353.englishNames: chronic throat obstruction; chronic pharyngitis; chronic throat impediment; Chronic pharyngitis
V012-N02353.pinyinNames: man hou bi; màn hóu bì
V012-N02353.definitions: Characterized by dry, sore throat, a foreign body sensation and discomfort in the throat. It is often caused by malnourishment of the throat, ascending of deficiency fire to the throat or retention of pathogenic factors in the throat.

V012-N02354.label: 慢惊风
V012-N02354.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02354.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02354.englishNames: chronic infantile convulsion; Recurrent convulsion disorder; Chronic infantile convulsion
V012-N02354.pinyinNames: man jing feng; màn jīng fēng
V012-N02354.definitions: Characterized by repeated twitching of hands and feet, intermittent convulsions, fatigue, a pale face, sleepiness with eyes unclosed, vomiting and diarrhoea of bluish stools. Often results from healthy qi deficiency, damage to spleen yang or internal stirring of liver wind. It may also develop from acute infantile convulsion.

V012-N02355.label: 慢脾风
V012-N02355.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02355.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02355.englishNames: Chronic spleen wind; chronic convulsion due to spleen disorder; chronic spleen wind
V012-N02355.pinyinNames: màn pí fēng; man pi feng; man jing feng
V012-N02355.definitions: A critical condition characterized by head shaking with closed eyes, dark bluish face and lips, sweats on the forehead, drowsiness, cold limbs, twitching or convulsion of the limbs, and vomiting of clear water. Often results from excessive or chronic vomiting or diarrhoea damaging spleen yang or surge of internal liver wind.

V012-N02356.label: 房事类病
V012-N02356.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02356.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02356.definitions: A collective term of sexual dysfunctions caused by decline of the vital gate. Contributing factors may include emotions, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency and sexual indulgence.

V012-N02357.label: 扁瘊
V012-N02357.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02357.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02357.englishNames: Flat warts; verruca plana; flat wart
V012-N02357.definitions: Characterized by small, smooth flattened and slightly raised warts on the face or dorsum of the hands. They are often caused by damp heat affecting the skin, coupled with exposure to toxins.

V012-N02358.label: 手足部疔疮
V012-N02358.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02358.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02358.englishNames: Boils on the hand/foot; hand-foot boil and sore
V012-N02358.pinyinNames: shou zu bu ding chuang; shǒu zú bù dīng chuāng
V012-N02358.definitions: Red, swollen boils on the hands/feet that may cause intense pain or damage the bones or sinews. They often result from localized injury coupled with contraction of toxic fire.

V012-N02359.label: 抱头火丹
V012-N02359.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02359.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02359.englishNames: head erysipelas; Erysipelas on the head
V012-N02359.pinyinNames: bao tou huo dan; bào tóu huǒ dān
V012-N02359.definitions: Characterized by facial redness, warmth, swelling and pain. In severe cases, blisters and eyelid swelling may be present. Often results from wind heat transforming into fire.

V012-N02360.label: 抽搐
V012-N02360.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02360.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02360.definitions: Uncontrolled shaking of the limbs, mouth corner or head. Contributing factors may include internal exuberance of toxic heat, stirring of wind yang, toxic wind affecting the meridians, or consumption of yin blood. It is often seen in late-stage exogenous diseases, exhaustion of Yuan-primordial qi, epileptic seizures, infantile convulsions, poisoning or accidental injuries.

V012-N02361.label: 损伤内证病
V012-N02361.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02361.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02361.englishNames: Internal injuries; internal damage diseases; category of internal damage diseases
V012-N02361.pinyinNames: sǔn shāng nèi zhèng bìng; sun shang nei zheng bing
V012-N02361.definitions: A group of disorders caused by damage to bones, tendons, qi, blood, meridians and zang–fu organs by direct or indirect trauma on the body. These include trauma of the head, chest and abdomen, coupled with traumatic bleeding, pain, fever, fainting, urine retention, flaccidity, numbness, dizziness, panting and cough.

V012-N02362.label: 摄领疮
V012-N02362.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02362.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02362.aliases: 牛皮癣； 顽癣； Oxhide lichen
V012-N02362.englishNames: Collar sore; neurodermatitis; cervical neurodermatitis; nape sores; neurodermatitis of nape
V012-N02362.pinyinNames: she ling chuang; shè lǐng chuāng
V012-N02362.definitions: Characterized by severe painful or itchy, symmetric scaly plaques. The itch-scratch cycle causes the skin to become thick and leathery, typically on the neck, elbow, arms, legs and lumbosacral region. Often results from wind dampness affecting the skin, liver hyperactivity due to blood deficiency or malnourishment of the skin due to fluids consumption.

V012-N02363.label: 支饮
V012-N02363.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02363.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02363.englishNames: thoracic fluid retention; Thoracic fluid retention
V012-N02363.definitions: Characterized by coughing, panting, chest fullness with an inability to lie flat, shortness of breath and foamy sputum. Associated symptoms may include fever, chills, a cold sensation in the back, back pain, facial puffiness, and leg oedema. Often occurs when retained thoracic fluid affects the lung. In addition, it is commonly triggered by external wind cold or eating cold/cool food.

V012-N02364.label: 新感温病
V012-N02364.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02364.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02364.englishNames: newly contracted warm disease; new contraction of warm disease; newly acquired warm disease; Acute warm diseases
V012-N02364.pinyinNames: xin gan wen bing; xīn gǎn wēn bìng
V012-N02364.definitions: A group of diseases characterized by a sudden fever, mild or no aversion to wind cold, headache, and sore throat, followed by a high-grade fever, persistent sweating and thirst with a desire to drink water. Associated symptoms may include constipation, dysentery, unconsciousness, delirium, maculae, bleeding and convulsions. Contributing factors may include external contraction of pathogenic warmth, warmth transmitting to the stomach/intestine, warmth affecting the heart, warmth damaging blood, or warmth stirring up wind.

V012-N02365.label: 旋耳疮
V012-N02365.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02365.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02365.englishNames: eczema of external ear; ear eczema; Ear sore; Ear Eczema; eczema of ear
V012-N02365.pinyinNames: xuan dong chuang; xuan er chuang; xuán ěr chuāng
V012-N02365.definitions: Characterized by skin redness, oozing, ulceration and discharge of bloody pus. In severe cases, it may cause breaking of the ear crease with sharp pain. It is often caused by otitis media or ascending of damp heat along the pathways of the liver, gallbladder and spleen meridians.

V012-N02366.label: 无头疽
V012-N02366.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02366.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02366.aliases: 阴疽； Yin abscess； Blind Deep Rooted Carbuncles
V012-N02366.englishNames: headless abscess; suppurative osteomyelitis and arthritis; deep carbuncle; Headless abscess; Deep Carbuncle; headless gangrene; headless abscess (suppurative osteomyelitis; arthritis)
V012-N02366.pinyinNames: wu tou ju; wú tóu jū
V012-N02366.definitions: Characterized by severe, persistent pain and diffuse swelling, with no skin colour changes. They may rupture and take a long period time to heal. Often result from retained toxins or traumatic injuries.

V012-N02367.label: 早泄
V012-N02367.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02367.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02367.aliases: 早泄病； Premature ejaculation disorder； Premature Ejaculation
V012-N02367.englishNames: premature ejaculation; prospermia; Premature ejaculation; Prospermia; premature ejaculation (disease); Premature ejaculation disorder
V012-N02367.definitions: A condition that ejaculation always or nearly always occurs prior to sexual intercourse or within about one minute. Often results from fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency or deficiency of the heart and kidney.

V012-N02368.label: 时行感冒
V012-N02368.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02368.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02368.aliases: 时邪感冒； Seasonal cold disorder
V012-N02368.englishNames: influenza; Seasonal common cold; Seasonal cold disorder
V012-N02368.pinyinNames: shi xing gan mao; shí xíng gǎn mào
V012-N02368.definitions: A condition characterized by sudden fever, chills or mild aversion to wind or cold, sore, swollen and congested throat, headache, and muscle/joint soreness and pain. Often occurs when pathogenic toxins attack the Wei-defence, and is more common in spring and winter.

V012-N02369.label: 昏迷
V012-N02369.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02369.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02369.definitions: An inability to maintain an awareness of self and environment coupled with a complete (or near-complete) lack of responsiveness to people and other environmental stimuli. Contributing factors may include phlegm turbidity, toxic heat, traumatic injuries, disorder of qi and blood, exhaustion of yin and yang, or other intense stimuli. It is often seen in terminal stage of exogenous diseases or late-stage endogenous diseases.

V012-N02370.label: 春温
V012-N02370.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02370.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02370.aliases: spring warm disorder； 春温病； 春瘟病； Spring febrile disorder； Spring pestilence disease
V012-N02370.englishNames: spring warm disorder; Spring warmth disorder; Spring Warm Syndrome; spring warmth; Spring pestilence
V012-N02370.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by a sudden high-grade fever, headache, neck rigidity, vomiting, macular eruption, restlessness, followed by unconsciousness and convulsions. Often occurs when pathogenic warm heat or epidemic toxin enters the body through the mouth or nose to affect the Ying nutrients, blood and brain.

V012-N02371.label: 晕动病
V012-N02371.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02371.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02371.aliases: 注车； 注船； Car sickness； Seasickness
V012-N02371.pinyinNames: yun dong bing; yūn dòng bìng; yun er bing
V012-N02371.definitions: Characterized by a pale face, dizziness, nausea and vomiting. The symptoms slowly decrease and then disappear once the motion has stopped. Contributing factors may include emotions, hunger/binge eating or disagreement between visually perceived movement and the vestibular system’s sense of movement. These factors may cause disordered qi and blood, allowing phlegm turbidity to ascend and disturb the mind.

V012-N02372.label: 晚期血吸虫病
V012-N02372.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02372.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02372.aliases: 晚期蛊虫病； Advanced snail fever
V012-N02372.englishNames: Advanced schistosomiasis; advanced schistosomiasis; advanced stage of schistosomiasis
V012-N02372.pinyinNames: wan qi xue xi chong bing; wǎn qī xuè xī chóng bìng
V012-N02372.definitions: Late-stage schistosomiasis characterized by extreme weight loss, positive finding of the rectal mucosa biopsy, ascites, varicose abdominal veins, liver cirrhosis and splenomegaly. Often occurs when prolonged schistosomiasis causes qi and blood to stagnate in the liver and intestines.

V012-N02373.label: 暑厥
V012-N02373.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02373.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02373.englishNames: summerheat syncope; summer-heat syncope; Summer heat syncope
V012-N02373.definitions: A condition characterized by sunstroke, high fever, unconsciousness, and cold limbs. Alternatively, nausea, vomiting and abdominal pain may be present. Often occurs when summer heat disturbs the heart mind or blocks the qi activity.

V012-N02374.label: 暑温
V012-N02374.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02374.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02374.aliases: Summer Febrile Rreval Dis； warm disease in summer； 暑瘟； 暑温病； 暑瘟病； Summer heat febrile disorders； Summer heat pestilence disorders
V012-N02374.englishNames: warm disease in summer; summerheat-warmth; Summer-heat disorder; Summer Febrile Prevalent Diseases; Summer heat pestilence; summerheat warmth
V012-N02374.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by a sudden high-grade fever, headache, vomiting, and neck rigidity. In severe cases, unconsciousness and convulsions may be present. Often occurs when pathogenic summer heat or epidemic toxin enters the body through mosquito bites to disturb the mind or affect the heart and liver.

V012-N02375.label: 暑疖
V012-N02375.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02375.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02375.englishNames: summer furuncle; summer boil; Heat boils
V012-N02375.definitions: Boils on the head and face due to toxic summer heat affecting the skin.

V012-N02376.label: 暑病
V012-N02376.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02376.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02376.englishNames: summerheat disease; summer-heat disease; Summer disorders
V012-N02376.definitions: A group of disorders caused by external contraction of summer heat or summer dampness. They often occur when summer heat or dampness remain in the Wei-defence, affect the stomach and intestines, or in severe cases, block the flow of qi.

V012-N02377.label: 暑秽病
V012-N02377.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02377.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02377.englishNames: Summer heat turbidity; summer filthy disease; summer filth disease
V012-N02377.pinyinNames: shu hui bing; shǔ huì bìng
V012-N02377.definitions: A condition characterized by distending headache, gastric stuffiness, chest tightness, restlessness, nausea, a hot sensation of the skin, and sweating. In severe cases, unconsciousness and deafness may be present. Often occurs when summer heat and foul turbidity impair the qi flow of the lung and stomach or disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02378.label: 暑脱
V012-N02378.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02378.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02378.definitions: A condition characterized by heat stroke, with a sudden pale complexion, cold sweats, a drop in blood pressure, and a feeble, thready and extremely faint pulse. Often occurs when summer heat or summer heat dampness directly affect the zang–fu organs and cause a drastic deprivation of qi, blood, yin and yang.

V012-N02379.label: 暑风
V012-N02379.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02379.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02379.englishNames: summerheat-wind; Summer heat convulsion; summer-heat convulsion
V012-N02379.definitions: A condition characterized by heat stroke, high fever, unconsciousness and convulsions. Often occurs when summer heat disturbs the heart mind or stirs liver wind.

V012-N02380.label: 暴吐
V012-N02380.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02380.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02380.definitions: The occurrence of sudden, severe involuntary expulsion of food, phlegm or bile through the mouth. Contributing factors may include binge eating, pestilence or food/drug poisoning. These factors may cause fulminant ascending of stomach qi.

V012-N02381.label: 暴咳病
V012-N02381.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02381.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02381.pinyinNames: bao ke bing; bào ké bìng
V012-N02381.definitions: Characterized by severe coughing with an acute attack and a short duration.

V012-N02382.label: 暴喘
V012-N02382.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02382.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02382.englishNames: sudden dyspnea; Fulminant wheezing; fulminant dyspnea
V012-N02382.definitions: An acute, severe condition characterized by sudden wheezing, shortness of breath, sweating, an inability to lie flat, bulging chest, subcostal distension, a dark complexion, and cyanosis of the lips. Contributing factors include a history of chronic asthma, exposure to exogenous pathogens or dysfunction of the internal organs. These factors may cause lung qi to ascend and impair the function of the kidney to absorb qi.

V012-N02383.label: 暴泻
V012-N02383.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02383.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02383.definitions: An acute condition characterized by sudden abdominal pain and diarrhoea. Vomiting and fever may also be present. Often results from improper diet or exposure to wind, cold or damp heat.

V012-N02384.label: 暴瘖
V012-N02384.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02384.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02384.definitions: Characterized by acute hoarseness or loss of voice and sore throat. It is often caused by pathogenic factors affecting the throat.

V012-N02385.label: 暴盲
V012-N02385.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02385.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02385.englishNames: sudden blindness; sudden vissal loss; Sudden blindness; Sudden Vissal Loss
V012-N02385.definitions: Characterized by sudden visual impairment or vision loss of one or both eyes. Often results from blockage of eye tissues, blood heat (due to yang hyperactivity) damaging eye collaterals, or retinal detachment.

V012-N02386.label: 暴聋
V012-N02386.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02386.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02386.englishNames: sudden deafness; Sudden deafness disorder; Sudden deafness
V012-N02386.definitions: Characterized by sudden onset of hearing impairment in one or both ears. Individuals may also experience vertigo and tinnitus. Often results from stagnation of qi and blood due to dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs, or pathogenic factors affecting the ear.

V012-N02387.label: 暴风客热
V012-N02387.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02387.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02387.englishNames: sudden wind and heat attack of eyes; Sudden attack of wind heat on the eye; sudden attack of wind-heat on the eye; sudden wind and invading fever; acute catarrhal and allergic con junctivitis; fulminant wind-heat invasion; acute catarrhal conjunctivitis; fulminant wind and invading fever
V012-N02387.pinyinNames: bao feng ke re; bào fēng kè rè
V012-N02387.definitions: A condition characterized by sudden onset of redness, swelling and pain of the white of the eye, sensitivity to bright lights, and watery discharge. Often results from exuberant fire of the lung and stomach or exogenous wind heat affecting the eye.

V012-N02388.label: 月经先后无定期
V012-N02388.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02388.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02388.englishNames: irregular menstrual cycle; irrgular menstrual cycle; menstruation at irregular intervals; Irregular menstruation disorders; irregular menstruation cycle; Irregular menstrual period
V012-N02388.pinyinNames: yue jing xian hou wu ding qi; yuè jīng xiān hòu wú dìng qī
V012-N02388.definitions: A menstrual cycle that begins more than one week early or late for three consecutive months. Often results from liver qi stagnation, kidney deficiency or disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02389.label: 月经先期
V012-N02389.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02389.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02389.aliases: Advanced menstruation disorder； 经行先期； 经早； precocious menstruation
V012-N02389.englishNames: advanced menstruation; Early menstrual period; Advanced menstruation disorder
V012-N02389.pinyinNames: yue jing xian qi; yuè jīng xiān qī
V012-N02389.definitions: A menstrual cycle that begins at least one week early for three consecutive months, with a normal menstrual duration. Often results from insecurity of Chong and Ren meridians (due to qi deficiency) or heat disturbing the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02390.label: 月经前后诸症
V012-N02390.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02390.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02390.aliases: 月经前后诸证； 经行前后诸证
V012-N02390.pinyinNames: yuè jīng qián hòu zhū zhèng
V012-N02390.definitions: A group of menstrual disorders that occurs days before or during menstruation.

V012-N02391.label: 月经后期
V012-N02391.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02391.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02391.aliases: 经行后期； 经迟； Delayed menstruation disorder
V012-N02391.englishNames: delayed menstruation; retarded menstruation; Delayed menstruation disorder; Delayed menstrual period
V012-N02391.pinyinNames: yue jing hou qi; yuè jīng hòu qī
V012-N02391.definitions: A menstrual cycle that begins at least one week or up to three months late for three consecutive cycles, with a normal menstrual duration. Often results from insufficiency of the Chong and Ren meridians (due to kidney deficiency and blood deficiency) or phlegm dampness obstructing the Chong and Ren meridians (due to blood cold and qi stagnation).

V012-N02392.label: 月经类病
V012-N02392.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02392.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02392.aliases: 月经病； 月经不调； 月经周期病； Menstruation associated disorders； Menstrual diseases
V012-N02392.englishNames: category of menstrual disease; Menstrual disorders; menstrual diseases
V012-N02392.pinyinNames: yue jing lei bing; yuè jīng lèi bìng
V012-N02392.definitions: A group of disorders characterized by abnormal changes in menstrual cycle, duration, volume, colour, and quality of menses, coupled with menstrual complaints. They often result from disharmony between the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02393.label: 月经过多
V012-N02393.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02393.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02393.aliases: 经水过多； 月水过多
V012-N02393.englishNames: profuse menstruation; Menorrhagia; menorrhagia; menorrhagin; hypermenorrhea; Heavy menstruation; Menorrhagia disorder; heavy menstrual bleeding
V012-N02393.pinyinNames: yue jing guo duo; yue jing guo dong; yue jing guo er; yuè jīng guò duō
V012-N02393.definitions: An abnormally heavy menstrual volume (>80 ml), with a normal menstrual cycle and duration. Often results from insecurity of Chong and Ren meridians (due to qi deficiency) or heat forcing the blood to move recklessly.

V012-N02394.label: 月经过少
V012-N02394.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02394.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02394.aliases: Scanty menstruation disorder； 月经涩少
V012-N02394.englishNames: hypomenorrhea; Scanty menstruation; Hypomenorrhea; Decreased menstruation disorder; scant menstruation; scanty menstruation; hypermenorrhea; decreased menstruation; haypomenorrhea
V012-N02394.pinyinNames: yue jing guo shao; yuè jīng guò shǎo
V012-N02394.definitions: An abnormally scanty menstrual volume (less than 1/2 of the normal menstrual volume, <20 ml, less than two days of menstrual duration, or just drops of menstrual blood). Often results from essence/blood deficiency or blood stagnation.

V012-N02395.label: 有头疽
V012-N02395.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02395.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02395.englishNames: [headed]carbuncle; headed carbuncle (carbuncle); headed gangrene; carbuncle; Carbuncle; ［headed］ carbuncle
V012-N02395.pinyinNames: you tou ju; yǒu tóu jū
V012-N02395.definitions: Characterized by a cluster of red, swollen, warm and painful boils filled with purulent exudate. They may spread to deeper and surrounding areas that resemble a honeycomb (>9cm or even >30cm in diameter). In severe cases, it may cause inward collapse of abscess toxins. Carbuncles often result from stagnation of qi and blood in the skin due to external contraction of wind heat, damp heat and toxic fire.

V012-N02396.label: 杨梅疮
V012-N02396.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02396.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02396.englishNames: syphilitic skin lesion; red bayberry sore; Syphilitic skin lesion
V012-N02396.pinyinNames: yang mei chuang; yáng méi chuāng
V012-N02396.definitions: Highly contagious red skin lesions, sores, papules or nodules seen in patients with syphilis. They often occur when syphilis affects the skin.

V012-N02397.label: 杨梅结毒
V012-N02397.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02397.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02397.englishNames: Advanced syphilis; late syphilis; advanced syphilis; syphilis of pharynx
V012-N02397.pinyinNames: yang mei jie dong; yáng méi jié dú; yang mei jie er
V012-N02397.definitions: Late-stage syphilis, characterized by nodules, open sores, ulcers on the gum or atrophic scars on the head, face and four extremities. They often occur when syphilis affects the bones, joints and zang–fu organs.

V012-N02398.label: 松毛虫伤
V012-N02398.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02398.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02398.englishNames: pine caterpillar sting; Pine caterpillar injuries; pine caterpillar injury
V012-N02398.pinyinNames: song mao chong shang; sōng máo chóng shāng
V012-N02398.definitions: Exposure to toxic hairs of pine caterpillars may cause intense itching, redness, swelling or blisters at the affected sites. Individuals may also experience joint redness, swelling, pain and restricted movement.

V012-N02399.label: 松皮癣
V012-N02399.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02399.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02399.englishNames: pine bark plaque; skin amyloidosis; Loose skin tinea; pine-bark psoriasis
V012-N02399.pinyinNames: song pi xuan; sōng pí xuǎn
V012-N02399.definitions: Characterized by itchy, thickened and solid papules or nodules with a dark, brown colour that resemble the bark of a pine tree. Often results from damp heat retained in the skin.

V012-N02400.label: 梅核气
V012-N02400.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02400.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02400.englishNames: plum-stone qi(globus hystericus); Globus Hystericus; globus hystericus; plum-stone qi; plum-pit qi; Plum pit qi
V012-N02400.pinyinNames: mei he qi; méi hé qì
V012-N02400.definitions: A sensation of a foreign body trapped in the throat that can neither be swallowed nor expectorated. Often occurs when phlegm and qi stagnate in the throat due to emotional disturbance.

V012-N02401.label: 梅毒
V012-N02401.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02401.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02401.pinyinNames: mei dong; méi dú; mei er
V012-N02401.definitions: A sexually transmitted disease characterized by sores on the genitals, vagina or skin, bone pain and dull expression. Often occurs when the bacterium Treponema pallidum enters the skin, muscles, bones, zang–fu organs, meridians or even the brain.

V012-N02402.label: 梅毒攻心
V012-N02402.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02402.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02402.englishNames: syphilis attacking the heart; Syphilis affecting the heart
V012-N02402.pinyinNames: mei er gong xin; mei dong gong xin; méi dú gōng xīn
V012-N02402.definitions: Late-stage syphilis characterized by palpitations, chest pain, heart murmurs and tremor. In severe cases, heart failure may occur. They often occur when syphilis affects the heart and blood vessels.

V012-N02403.label: 梅毒致痿
V012-N02403.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02403.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02403.englishNames: syphilis causing flaccidity; syphilis-induced flaccidity; Syphilis affecting the spinal cord
V012-N02403.pinyinNames: mei er zhi wei; méi dú zhì wěi; mei dong zhi wei
V012-N02403.definitions: Late-stage syphilis characterized by shooting (electric shock-like) pain, an abnormal or disturbed sensation and unsteady gait. Often occurs when syphilis affects the spinal cord.

V012-N02404.label: 梦交
V012-N02404.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02404.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02404.aliases: Sexosomnia； Coitus in Dream； Sexual Intercourse in Dream
V012-N02404.englishNames: sexual intercourse in dream; wet dream; dreaming of intercourse; Sleep sex; Wet Dream
V012-N02404.definitions: Characterized by sexual intercourse during dreams. Individuals may have creamy, white vaginal discharges. Often results from emotional stagnation transforming into fire or hyperactivity of ministerial fire due to yin blood deficiency.

V012-N02405.label: 梦魇
V012-N02405.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02405.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02405.definitions: An unpleasant dream that may contain screaming, a feeling of oppressed by heavy objects or a sense of suffocation. Individuals often wake up in a state of distress but able to return to sleep. Often occurs when fear or fright damages heart qi or when pathogenic factors disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02406.label: 椒疮
V012-N02406.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02406.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02406.englishNames: trachoma; prickly-ash-like sore(trachoma); prickly-ash-like sore; tracoma; Pricklyash-like sores on the eyelid
V012-N02406.definitions: Characterized by red, solid granulations in the undersurface of the upper eyelid that resemble wild pepper. Individuals may experience a feeling of pain and itching, sensitivity to bright lights, and watery discharge. Often results from stagnation of qi and blood in the local area due to exogenous wind heat and heat accumulated in the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02407.label: 横痃
V012-N02407.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02407.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02407.definitions: A sexually transmitted disease characterized by swelling of the lymph nodes in the groin. It appears as small as an almond at first and then becomes a pebble-sized, fish mouth-shaped blister, followed by pain, redness, a burning sensation and discharge of pus. They often occur when sexually transmitted infections affect the groin.

V012-N02408.label: 正水
V012-N02408.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02408.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02408.englishNames: regular edema; Generalized oedema; typical anasarca; typical edema
V012-N02408.definitions: Characterized by progressive oedema of the whole body, scanty urine or urine retention, abdominal fullness and panting. Often occurs when retained water (due to yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney) affects the lung.

V012-N02409.label: 正疟
V012-N02409.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02409.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02409.aliases: 间日疟； 三日疟； Quartan malaria
V012-N02409.englishNames: regular malaria; normal malaria; Regular malaria
V012-N02409.definitions: Characterized by shivering and then fever and sweating, occurring every other day or three days. Patients with warm malaria present with more fever than shivering, whereas patients with cold malaria present with more shivering than fever. Often occurs when anti-pathogenic qi struggles with pathogenic factors in between the exterior and interior.

V012-N02410.label: 毒蕈中毒
V012-N02410.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02410.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02410.englishNames: poisonous mushroom poisoning; mushroom poisoning; Mushroom poisoning
V012-N02410.pinyinNames: dong xun zhong dong; du xun zhong er; dú xùn zhòng dú
V012-N02410.definitions: Food poisoning caused by ingestion of toxic substances present in mushrooms. Symptoms of mushroom poisoning may vary from headache, vomiting, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, jaundice, drowsiness and visual hallucination to mental confusion or sudden death, depending on how severe the toxin affects the gastrointestinal tract, heart, brain, liver or kidney.

V012-N02411.label: 毒蛇咬伤
V012-N02411.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02411.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02411.englishNames: venomous snake bite; poisonous snake bite; Venomous snake bites
V012-N02411.pinyinNames: dong she yao shang; dú shé yǎo shāng; er she yao shang
V012-N02411.definitions: An injury caused by the bite of a venomous snake. The toxins may enter the Ying nutrients and blood and affect the zang–fu organs, resulting in chills, fever, nausea, vomiting, headache and dizziness. In severe cases, individuals may experience bleeding, loss of consciousness and convulsions.

V012-N02412.label: 气厥
V012-N02412.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02412.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02412.aliases: 郁厥； Qi Syncope Syndrome； Syncope due to Disorder of Qi； Qi syncope disorder
V012-N02412.englishNames: Qi Syncope; qi syncope; Qi syncope; syncope due to disorder of qi
V012-N02412.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting (patients recover after some time). Some may also experience sensory or motor disturbance but no organic pathological changes. Often occurs when emotional disturbance causes qi to stagnate or when clear yang fails to ascend due to spleen qi deficiency.

V012-N02413.label: 气喘
V012-N02413.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02413.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02413.definitions: The occurrence of difficulty or rapid breathing. Contributing factors may include exogenous wind cold or wind heat affecting the lung, exuberant pathogenic heat affecting the lung, phlegm-fluid retention in the lung, or qi deficiency of the lung and kidney. It is often seen in exogenous diseases, lung diseases, heart diseases, disorders of the throat or thoracic cage, debilitation, qi collapse, or abdominal tumour/fluid.

V012-N02414.label: 气淋
V012-N02414.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02414.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02414.englishNames: Qi strangury; qi strangury; strangurla due to disturbance of qi; qi stranguria
V012-N02414.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal distension and fullness, frequent, hesitant, painful or dripping urination. Contributing factors may include liver qi stagnation or age-related qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney. These factors may impair the qi transformation or opening and closing of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02415.label: 气瘤
V012-N02415.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02415.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02415.englishNames: qi tumor(subcutaneous neurofibroma); qi tumor; tumor due to disorder of qi; Qi tumour
V012-N02415.definitions: Characterized by single or multiple raised soft tumours that are indented upon pressure but bulging again when the pressure is released. The skin is either normal or with brown spots. They are often caused by over-exertion consuming lung qi or phlegm qi stagnation due to exposure to pathogenic factors.

V012-N02416.label: 气瘿
V012-N02416.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02416.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02416.englishNames: qi goiter; Qi goiter disorder; simple goiter; endemic goiter; Qi goiter
V012-N02416.definitions: Characterized by diffuse swelling in the front of the neck with a normal skin colour. It may feel soft or nodular and its size changes with emotions. Contributing factors may include diet, iodine deficiency, emotions or heredity. These factors may cause phlegm qi stagnation in the front of the neck.

V012-N02417.label: 气腹痛
V012-N02417.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02417.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02417.englishNames: abdominal pain due to qi stagnation; Qi stagnation-induced abdominal pain
V012-N02417.pinyinNames: qi fu tong; qì fù tòng
V012-N02417.definitions: A paroxysmal abdominal pain with no physical changes. Often results from stomach/intestine qi stagnation due to improper diet, exposure to cold, emotional disturbance.

V012-N02418.label: 气臌
V012-N02418.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02418.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02418.englishNames: Qi Tympanites; qi tympanites; Qi tympanites
V012-N02418.definitions: A type of tympanites characterized by increased abdominal size, tight abdominal skin and a sallow skin colour. Often results from qi stagnation in the abdomen due to emotional disturbance.

V012-N02419.label: 气血津液病类
V012-N02419.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02419.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02419.englishNames: Disorders of qi, blood and bodily fluids
V012-N02419.pinyinNames: qì xuè jīn yè bìng lèi
V012-N02419.definitions: A collective term for disorders of qi, blood, bodily fluids, essence and spirit caused by exogenous pathogenic factors, emotions, an improper diet, over-exertion, water-fluid retention or stagnant blood.

V012-N02420.label: 水毒
V012-N02420.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02420.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02420.englishNames: Cutaneous schistosomiasis; schistosome cercarial dermatitis; water poison
V012-N02420.pinyinNames: shuǐ dú; shui dong; shui er
V012-N02420.definitions: Exposure to cercariae of schistosoma in water may cause itchy skin wheals or rashes that resolve spontaneously after a couple of days.

V012-N02421.label: 水火烫伤
V012-N02421.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02421.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02421.englishNames: scald or burn due to hot water or fire; Burns and scalds
V012-N02421.pinyinNames: shui huo tang shang; shuǐ huǒ tàng shāng
V012-N02421.definitions: Damage to the skin by heat: a burn is caused by dry heat such as fire, whereas a scald is caused by something wet, such as hot water or steam. In addition to skin damage (redness, swelling, pain, blisters or scabs), fire/heat may affect the zang–fu organs, resulting in fever, restlessness, a dry mouth, and yellow urine. In severe cases, loss of consciousness and delirium may be present.

V012-N02422.label: 水疝
V012-N02422.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02422.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02422.definitions: Characterized by a painless enlargement in the scrotum on one or both sides, accompanied by a bearing-down sensation, impalpable testis, absence of redness or pain and positive transillumination test. Often results from an accumulation of water dampness inside the scrotum.

V012-N02423.label: 水痘
V012-N02423.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02423.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02423.aliases: 水疱； 水花； 水疮； varicella； 小儿水痘
V012-N02423.pinyinNames: shui dong; shui dou; shui er; shuǐ dòu
V012-N02423.definitions: Characterized by fever, cough and skin rashes that turn into fluid-filled blisters, which then crust over to form scabs. This condition often occurs when epidemic wind and internal damp heat affect the surface of the body.

V012-N02424.label: 水白疔
V012-N02424.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02424.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02424.englishNames: Palmar boil; palmar boil; palmar blister; palmar blister ding
V012-N02424.pinyinNames: shui bai ding; shuǐ bái dīng
V012-N02424.definitions: Characterized by bright, yellow blisters on the palmar aspect of the hand. They can be cured by piercing to allow the pus to drain.

V012-N02425.label: 水肿类病
V012-N02425.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02425.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02425.englishNames: category of disease with edema; Oedema; edema diseases
V012-N02425.pinyinNames: shui zhong lei bing; shuǐ zhǒng lèi bìng
V012-N02425.definitions: A group of conditions caused by impaired qi transformation and retained water flowing over the skin. Contributing factors may include external pathogenic factors, over-exertion or improper diet.

V012-N02426.label: 水臌
V012-N02426.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02426.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02426.englishNames: water tympanites; Fluid Tympanites; Water tympanites
V012-N02426.definitions: A type of tympanites characterized by increased abdominal size, abdominal distension, varicose abdominal veins, a sallow skin colour, oedema and enlarged spleen. Contributing factors may include water retention in the abdomen due to spleen deficiency, prolonged alcohol drinking or kidney deficiency.

V012-N02427.label: 汗类病
V012-N02427.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02427.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02427.englishNames: category of sweating disease; Sweating disorders; sweating diseases
V012-N02427.pinyinNames: han lei bing; hàn lèi bìng
V012-N02427.definitions: A group of conditions caused by disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence upon invasion by exogenous pathogenic wind, cold, and summer heat and dampness. Alternatively, they may also result from insecurity of the skin pores due to deficiency of the zang–-fu organs. These include spontaneous sweating, night sweats, yellow sweating and bloody sweating.

V012-N02428.label: 沙虱病
V012-N02428.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02428.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02428.pinyinNames: sha shi bing; shā shī bìng
V012-N02428.definitions: An infectious form of typhus caused by the intracellular parasitic bacterium Orientia tsutsugamushi. The bite of an infected mite may not cause pain but a prickly feeling. However, after two or three days, individuals may experience chills, fever, skin rashes, ulcerations and scabbing. Often results from the bite of trombiculid mite. The toxin of the mite then damages the blood in the localized area.

V012-N02429.label: 河豚中毒
V012-N02429.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02429.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02429.englishNames: balloon fish poisoning; Puffer fish poisoning; globefish poisoning
V012-N02429.pinyinNames: he tun zhong dong; hé tún zhòng dú; he tun zhong er
V012-N02429.definitions: Food poisoning caused by ingestion of inappropriately cooked puffer fish. It is characterized by sudden vomiting, abdominal pain, diarrhoea and bloody stools, followed by a tingling sensation in the lips, tongue and extremities, eyelid ptosis, limb weakness/paralysis, and cardiac arrhythmias. In severe cases, respiratory or circulatory failure may be present.

V012-N02430.label: 泄泻病
V012-N02430.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02430.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02430.pinyinNames: xie xie bing; xiè xiè bìng
V012-N02430.definitions: Characterized by increased frequency of bowel movements, loose stools, stools containing undigested food or watery stools. Contributing factors may include external contraction of wind, cold, damp or heat, an improper diet, emotional disturbance or yang qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02431.label: 流注
V012-N02431.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02431.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02431.aliases: 流注； 母子发； Multiple Metastatic Abscess
V012-N02431.englishNames: Deep multiple abscess disorder; Gravitational Abscess; deep multiple abscess; multiple abscess; Deep multiple abscess; gravitational abscess
V012-N02431.definitions: Characterized by multiple, deeply located and painful abscesses with diffuse swelling but no skin colour changes.

V012-N02432.label: 流火
V012-N02432.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02432.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02432.englishNames: shank erysipelas; Erysipelas of the leg; fire flow (erysipelas of shank)
V012-N02432.definitions: Characterized by red, swelling, warmth and pain in the lower leg, with a demarcated edge and discolouration upon pressure. Individuals may experience chills and high fever. Often results from downward flow of damp heat.

V012-N02433.label: 流痰
V012-N02433.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02433.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02433.aliases: Osteoarticular Tuberculosis； Pyogenous Chronic Arthrosis； 穿骨流注； 骨痨； Flowing phlegm disorder； Bone and joint tuberculosis disorder； Bone tuberculosis
V012-N02433.englishNames: flowing phlegm; tuberculosis of bone and joint; Chronic Suppurative Abscess of Bones and Joints; tuberculosis of bone andjoint; flowing  phlegm(tuberculosis of bone and joint); Flowing phlegm disorder; Flowing phlegm; chronic suppurative abscess of bones and joints
V012-N02433.definitions: A condition initially characterized by dull soreness and pain upon physical exertion, followed by local swelling, pain and discharge of thin, clear pus or pus that resembles rotten cotton threads, delayed healing, and formation of a sinus tract. If the spine is affected, limb rigidity, impaired movement or paralysis may be present. This condition is known as osteoarticular tuberculosis in modern medicine. Often occurs when Mycobacterium tuberculosis and turbid phlegm flow into bones and joints due to congenital kidney deficiency coupled with contraction of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

V012-N02434.label: 消渴类病
V012-N02434.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02434.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02434.englishNames: category of wasting and thirst disease; Wasting and thirst disorders
V012-N02434.pinyinNames: xiao ke lei bing; xiāo kě lèi bìng
V012-N02434.definitions: A group of disorders characterized by polydipsia, polyphagia and polyuria. Contributing factors include overeating of oily, sweet food, emotions, sexual indulgence, exposure to pathogenic warm heat or abuse of medicinal minerals. These factors may cause stomach heat, lung dryness, hyperactive heart fire to consume yin and disturb qi transformation.

V012-N02435.label: 淋证
V012-N02435.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02435.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02435.definitions: Characterized by frequent, urgent, hesitant and painful urination. The pain during urination may radiate to the low back and abdomen. Contributing factors may include damp heat affecting the lower jiao, deficiency of the spleen and kidney, liver qi stagnation, tuberculosis, stones or tumours. These factors may impair the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02436.label: 混合痔
V012-N02436.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02436.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02436.englishNames: mixed hemorrhoid; mixed hemorrhoids; Mixed haemorrhoids
V012-N02436.pinyinNames: hun he zhi; hùn hé zhì
V012-N02436.definitions: Characterized by varicose dilatation of a vein both above and below the dentate line. Often results from downward flow of damp heat to the anus, deficiency of the zang–fu organs, defaecation, weight-bearing, or straining during childbirth.

V012-N02437.label: 混睛障
V012-N02437.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02437.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02437.englishNames: Corneal opacity disorder; murky-eye nebula; stromal keratitis; interstitial keratitis; corneal opacity; mixed nebula; luterstitial keratitis; murky eye nebula(interstitial keratitis); Interstitial keratitis
V012-N02437.pinyinNames: hun jing zhang; hún jīng zhàng
V012-N02437.definitions: Characterized by corneal opacity and vision blurring. Individuals may experience eye pain and sensitivity to light. Often results from local stagnation of qi and blood due to wind heat in the liver meridian or toxic heat in the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N02438.label: 温燥病
V012-N02438.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02438.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02438.pinyinNames: wen zao bing; wēn zào bìng
V012-N02438.definitions: A condition characterized by fever, headache, cough with scanty sputum, dry nose and throat, thirst, and scanty, yellow urine. Often occurs in autumn when dryness and heat affect the lung and consumes yin fluids.

V012-N02439.label: 温疫类病
V012-N02439.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02439.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02439.englishNames: category of warm epidemic disease; warm epidemic diseases; Epidemic febrile disorders
V012-N02439.pinyinNames: wen yi lei bing; wēn yì lèi bìng
V012-N02439.definitions: A group of febrile disorders caused by external contraction of pathogenic warmth or pestilence. Endemic febrile disorders are prevalent and infectious within a geographic area, whereas pandemic febrile disorders are strongly contagious across the globe.

V012-N02440.label: 温病
V012-N02440.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02440.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02440.englishNames: warm disease; Seasonal Prevalent Diseases; Warmth disorders; warmth disease; Warm diseases
V012-N02440.definitions: A group of disorders caused by external contraction of pathogenic warm/heat. It is initially characterized by typical heat-induced symptoms or heat transforming into dryness after entering the interior. In severe cases, heat may transmit to the pericardium, damage yin or stir up internal wind. They are known as endemic warm diseases when spreading through population within a geographic area.

V012-N02441.label: 湿温
V012-N02441.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02441.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02441.aliases: 湿瘟； 湿温病； 湿瘟病； Dampness febrile disorders； Dampness pestilence disorders； damp warm
V012-N02441.englishNames: damp warm; damp-warm disease; Dampness pestilence; Dampness Prevalent Diseases; Dampness and warmth disorder; dampness-warmth
V012-N02441.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by a persistent high-grade fever, gastric discomfort, abdominal distension, indifferent expression, a greasy tongue coating, a delayed pulse, pink or white skin rashes, masses in the left subcostal region, and low white blood cell count. Often occurs when damp heat enters the body through the mouth and nose to accumulate in the spleen and stomach and obstruct the qi activity.

V012-N02442.label: 湿热痢
V012-N02442.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02442.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02442.englishNames: damp-heat dysentery; dampness-heat dysentery; Damp heat dysentery
V012-N02442.pinyinNames: shi re li; shī rè lì
V012-N02442.definitions: Characterized by a sudden fever, abdominal pain, tenesmus and diarrhoea with stool containing mucus and blood. Often results from ingestion of contaminated food/water or contact with dysentery patients.

V012-N02443.label: 湿脚气
V012-N02443.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02443.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02443.englishNames: Wet beriberi; weak foot due to dampness; damp beriberi; wet beriberi
V012-N02443.pinyinNames: shi jiao qi; shī jiǎo qì
V012-N02443.definitions: Characterized by swelling, soreness and weakness of lower legs. Associated symptoms include palpitations, cyanosis, oedema and cardiac dilatation.

V012-N02444.label: 湿阻病
V012-N02444.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02444.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02444.aliases: 湿阻； 冒湿； 伤湿
V012-N02444.pinyinNames: shi zu bing; shī zǔ bìng
V012-N02444.definitions: A condition characterized by body heaviness, limb soreness, a poor appetite, gastric discomfort, abdominal distension and lassitude. Often occurs when dampness affects the qi flow of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02445.label: 湿霍乱
V012-N02445.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02445.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02445.pinyinNames: shi huo luan; shī huò luàn
V012-N02445.definitions: An infectious disease characterized by sudden onset of abdominal colic, severe vomiting and diarrhoea, and leg cramps. In severe cases, sunken eyes, dry, wrinkled skin, and cold hands/feet. Often occurs when people eat too much cold food, summer heat and dampness or the bacterium Vibrio cholerae directly affects the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02446.label: 溢饮
V012-N02446.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02446.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02446.englishNames: anasarcous fluid retention; subcutaneous fluid retention; Subcutaneous fluid retention
V012-N02446.definitions: Characterized by facial puffiness and leg or generalized pitting oedema, and body heaviness. Associated symptoms may include cold intolerance, panting, coughing and a dry mouth with no desire to drink water. Often occurs when water retention (due to spleen deficiency) flows over the surface of the body or when exogenous wind cold blocks the flow of lung qi.

V012-N02447.label: 滑精
V012-N02447.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02447.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02447.definitions: Characterized by more than four times of involuntary discharge of semen in one month. Often results from kidney qi failing to secure the essence gate due to sexual indulgence.

V012-N02448.label: 滑胎
V012-N02448.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02448.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02448.englishNames: habitual abortion; Habitual miscarriage; recurrent spontaneous abortion; RSA
V012-N02448.definitions: Characterized by spontaneous death of an embryo or fetus for three or more consecutive pregnancies. Often results from kidney deficiency and insecurity of the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02449.label: 滞颐
V012-N02449.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02449.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02449.englishNames: dribbling; infantile slobbering; Drooling in babies
V012-N02449.definitions: Characterized by saliva flowing outside of the mouth unintentionally that may wet the cheeks and chest. Often results from deficiency cold of the spleen or heat accumulating in the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02450.label: 漆疮
V012-N02450.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02450.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02450.englishNames: lacquer sore; contact dermatitis; Lacquer sore
V012-N02450.definitions: Characterized by skin redness, swelling, itching, a burning sensation and blisters after contact to various lacquers. It is often caused by congenital weakness coupled with exposure to lacquers.

V012-N02451.label: 漏下
V012-N02451.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02451.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02451.definitions: Characterized by irregular menstrual cycle and small but continuous uterine bleeding. Often occurs as a result of kidney deficiency, blood stasis and insecurity of the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02452.label: 漏肩风
V012-N02452.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02452.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02452.aliases: 冻结肩； 肩痹； 肩周炎； 冷凝肩； Adhesive capsulitis of shoulder
V012-N02452.pinyinNames: lou jian feng; lòu jiān fēng
V012-N02452.definitions: A condition characterized by shoulder soreness, heaviness and pain that aggravate at night, and impaired movement of the affected side. Alternatively, atrophy of shoulder muscles may be present. Often results from chronic strain, contraction of external wind, cold or dampness, trauma or degenerative changes.

V012-N02453.label: 火赤疮
V012-N02453.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02453.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02453.englishNames: redfire ulcers; dermatitis herpetiformis; red-fire sore; itching pemphigus; Fire red sore
V012-N02453.pinyinNames: huo chi chuang; huǒ chì chuāng
V012-N02453.definitions: Characterized by intolerably itchy blisters filled with a watery fluid. Often results from exuberant heart fire or internal dampness due to spleen deficiency.

V012-N02454.label: 炭疽
V012-N02454.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02454.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02454.definitions: An infectious disease characterized by skin lesion that eventually forms an ulcer with an eschar and surrounding swelling. This condition may also affect the lungs, intestines, brain and other parts of the body. The bacterium Bacillus anthracis may enter the body via the skin, mouth or nose and cause qi and blood to stagnate. Often results from contact with infected animals, handling infectious animal products or ingestion of undercooked foods of (infectious) animal origin.

V012-N02455.label: 烂喉丹痧
V012-N02455.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02455.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02455.aliases: 丹痧； 喉痧； 烂喉痧； 小儿烂喉丹痧； 疫喉痧； 猩红热； Scarlet fever
V012-N02455.englishNames: scarlet fever involving the throat; scarlet fever; scarlatina; Ulcerated throat with red exanthem
V012-N02455.pinyinNames: lan hou dan sha; làn hóu dān shā
V012-N02455.definitions: Characterized by fever, sore, ulcerative and swollen throat, characteristic diffuse red skin rashes, a “strawberry” tongue with a spreading pattern and desquamating process. In severe cases, a high-grade fever, unconsciousness and shock may be present. Often affects children and occurs when epidemic warm heat affects the throat and skin through the mouth, nose or skin lesion.

V012-N02456.label: 烂疔
V012-N02456.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02456.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02456.englishNames: ulcerated gangrene; gas gangrene; ulcerated boil; Necrotic boil; ulcerated ding
V012-N02456.definitions: Characterized by boils that can cause necrotic damage to muscle tissues, discharge of strong-odoured pus and blood. In severe cases, they may rapidly progress to toxaemia and shock. Often results from skin lesion coupled with exposure to toxins.

V012-N02457.label: 热厥
V012-N02457.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
