V012-N02457.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02457.aliases: 阳厥； Heat Syncope； Yang syncope； 煎厥
V012-N02457.englishNames: heat syncope; Heat Syncope Syndrome; Heat syncope
V012-N02457.definitions: Characterized by cold extremities up to the elbows and knees or beyond, body fever without sweating, a flushed face, a dry tongue and lips, scanty, dark yellow urine, and dry stools. In severe cases, unconsciousness may be present. Often results from exposure to hot environment for a prolonged period or severe fire heat/summer heat damaging yin or disturbing the heart mind.

V012-N02458.label: 热淋
V012-N02458.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02458.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02458.aliases: 急淋； 湿热淋； Heat stranguria disorder； Damp heat stranguria； acute stranguria
V012-N02458.englishNames: heat strangury; heat stranguria; Heat stranguria disorder; Heat strangury
V012-N02458.definitions: Characterized by frequent, urgent and painful urination with scanty, red urine and a burning sensation. Associated symptoms may include fever, chills, lower back painlower back pain, lower abdominal cramps, distension and pain, a yellow, greasy tongue coating and a slippery, rapid pulse. Often occurs when damp heat affects the kidney and urinary bladder.

V012-N02459.label: 热痹
V012-N02459.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02459.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02459.englishNames: heat impediment; heat bi; heat arthralgia; Heat impediment; arthralgia due to heattoxicity; Arthralgia due to Heattoxicity; arthralgia caused by heat pathogen
V012-N02459.definitions: Characterized by joint redness, swelling, pain, hotness and impaired movement that is alleviated with cold. Associated symptoms may include fever, thirst and scanty, dark yellow urine. Often occurs when toxic heat or damp heat flows into the joints or when combined internal heat and exogenous pathogenic heat affect the joints.

V012-N02460.label: 燕口疮
V012-N02460.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02460.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02460.pinyinNames: yan kou chuang; yàn kǒu chuāng
V012-N02460.definitions: Characterized by small, aphthous sores or erosions on the mouth corners in children. They often result from contraction of exogenous pathogens, heat accumulation in the heart and spleen, or ascending of internal fire due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02461.label: 燥痹
V012-N02461.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02461.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02461.englishNames: dry impediment; dry bi; Dryness-related impediment
V012-N02461.definitions: Characterized by dry mouth, eyes, nose and throat, scanty or absent saliva/nasal discharge/tears, dryness or mild pain of the eyes, dry skin, scanty or absent sweating, bone/joint pain, joint rigidity/deformation, and impaired joint flexion and extension. In severe cases, withered, scaly skin and damage to multiple organs and systems. Often results from exposure to dry heat or internal cold dampness transforming into dryness to consume yin fluids. As a result, qi and blood fail to nourish the zang–fu organs, orifices, tissues, skins, bones and sinews.

V012-N02462.label: 牙咬痈
V012-N02462.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02462.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02462.englishNames: acute wisdom tooth pericoronitis; pericoronitis of wisdom tooth; wisdom tooth pericoronitis; Pericoronitis of wisdom tooth
V012-N02462.pinyinNames: ya yao yong; yá yǎo yōng
V012-N02462.definitions: Characterized by fever, foul breath, swollen gum tissue, pain, and pus formation. In severe cases, swollen cheeks, difficulty opening mouth and discharge of pus after rupture. It is often caused by retention of toxic heat in the wisdom teeth.

V012-N02463.label: 牙宣
V012-N02463.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02463.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02463.aliases: 食床； 齿挺； 齿龈宣露
V012-N02463.englishNames: periodontal disease; gingival atrophy; Gingival Atrophy; gum atrophy; Gingival recession
V012-N02463.definitions: Characterized by the exposure in the roots of the teeth caused by a loss of gum tissue and/or retraction of the gingival margin from the crown of the teeth. Individuals may also experience loose teeth and gum bleeding. Often occurs when stomach fire ascends to scorch the gum or malnourishment of the gum due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02464.label: 牙漏
V012-N02464.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02464.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02464.aliases: 齿漏； 齿漏疳； 漏疳
V012-N02464.definitions: Characterized by an abnormal passage in the oral cavity on the gingiva. Often results from retained toxins affecting the alveolar periosteum, gum or adjacent skin due to untreated or inappropriately treated tooth problems.

V012-N02465.label: 牙疳
V012-N02465.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02465.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02465.englishNames: ulcerative gingivitis; gingival gan disease; gingival malnutrition; Ulcerative gingivitis; gum gan disease
V012-N02465.definitions: Characterized by gingival redness, ulceration and bleeding with a foetid odour. Often results from wind heat or cold dampness affecting the gum.

V012-N02466.label: 牙痈
V012-N02466.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02466.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02466.englishNames: gingival abscess; Periodontal abscess; gum abscess; periapical periodontitis
V012-N02466.definitions: Characterized by persistent intense toothache, red swollen gum and alveolar mucosa, discharge of pus, loose teeth and aggravated pain on chewing. Often occurs when internal fire heat ascends to the gum.

V012-N02467.label: 牙痛
V012-N02467.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02467.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02467.definitions: Pain in the teeth and/or gum. Contributing factors may include exogenous wind, ascending of stomach fire, deficiency fire due to kidney deficiency, spleen qi deficiency, or tooth erosion. It is often seen in dental caries, gingival abscess, pericoronitis of wisdom tooth, or maxillary osteomyelitis.

V012-N02468.label: 狂犬病
V012-N02468.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02468.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02468.pinyinNames: kuang quan bing; kuáng quǎn bìng
V012-N02468.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, aversion to wind, hydrophobia, photophobia and spasms/convulsions. In severe cases, agitation, loss of consciousness, paralysis or death may occur. This condition can affect humans and other mammals. It is caused by bites from infected dogs. The toxin then enters the bloodstream and triggers the liver wind.

V012-N02469.label: 狂病
V012-N02469.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02469.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02469.englishNames: manic psychosis; Manic psychosis; manic disease
V012-N02469.definitions: A major manic episode characterized by elevated mood, mental/physical hyperactivity, disorganized behaviour, restlessness, and irritability. Contributing factors may include mental stress-induced imbalance between yin and yang, phlegm fire ascending to disturb the mind or stagnant blood obstructing the heart mind.

V012-N02470.label: 狐惑
V012-N02470.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02470.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02470.englishNames: fox-creeper disease; Behcet’s syndrome; erosion of throat, anus and genitalia; erosion of throat,anus and genitalia; huhuo disease; Behcet's syndrome; Hu Huo
V012-N02470.definitions: Characterized by mind wandering, drowsiness, a poor appetite, restlessness, red eyes and sores on the throat and sex organs. Often results from retained damp heat.

V012-N02471.label: 狐疝
V012-N02471.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02471.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02471.englishNames: inguinal hernia; indirect hernia; Inguinal hernia
V012-N02471.definitions: Characterized by a protrusion of abdominal cavity contents through the inguinal canal. It may cause pain and distension that is aggravated when standing and alleviated when lying down. Contributing factors may include failure of the liver to maintain free flow of qi, qi deficiency in infants or the elderly, or increased intra-abdominal pressure.

V012-N02472.label: 狐臭
V012-N02472.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02472.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02472.definitions: Characterized by unpleasant smell due to stale perspiration under the arms (or other body parts such as areola, umbilicus or external genitalia). It can be hereditary or acquired due to disharmony between qi and blood and internal retention of damp heat.

V012-N02473.label: 猫眼疮
V012-N02473.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02473.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02473.englishNames: Cat’s eye-shaped sore; cat eye-like sore(erythema multiforme); cat’s eye sore; erythema multiforme; cat eye’s sore; cat-eye sore; cat's eye sore
V012-N02473.pinyinNames: māo yǎn chuāng; mao yan chuang
V012-N02473.definitions: Characterized by red, cat’s eye-shaped skin rash with rings. It commonly affects the hands and feet. Some individuals may also get it on the lips and genital area. This skin condition often occurs when wind, cold, dampness or heat enters the Ying nutrients and blood phases.

V012-N02474.label: 瓜藤缠
V012-N02474.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02474.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02474.englishNames: vine tangling; erythema nodosum; melon-vine vasculitis nodularis; Melon vine-like nodules
V012-N02474.pinyinNames: gua teng chan; guā téng chán
V012-N02474.definitions: Characterized by tender, red (broad bean-sized) nodules or lumps on both shins that resemble fruits growing on vines. Often results from downward flow of damp heat or chronic stasis transforming into heat.

V012-N02475.label: 生殖病类
V012-N02475.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02475.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02475.definitions: A collective term for male and female reproductive system diseases caused by congenital underdevelopment, contraction of exogenous pathogens, or consumption of qi, blood, yin and yang due to chronic conditions.

V012-N02476.label: 田螺疱
V012-N02476.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02476.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02476.pinyinNames: tian luo pao; tián luó pào
V012-N02476.definitions: Characterized by excess sweating, blisters and oozing on the feet. In severe cases, local swelling and pain may be present. It is often caused by damp heat, sweating and exposure to cold.

V012-N02477.label: 男性前阴类病
V012-N02477.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02477.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02477.englishNames: Disorders of the male external genitalia; male genital diseases; category of genital disease
V012-N02477.pinyinNames: nan xing qian yin lei bing; nán xìng qián yīn lèi bìng
V012-N02477.definitions: A group of disorders of the male external genitalia caused by congenital underdevelopment, trauma, damp heat affecting the testis or scrotum or the liver meridian, or toxic phlegm accumulating in the external genitalia.

V012-N02478.label: 男性生殖病
V012-N02478.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02478.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02478.englishNames: Male genital disorders; male reproductive diseases; category of male reproductive disease
V012-N02478.pinyinNames: nan xing sheng zhi bing; nán xìng shēng zhí bìng
V012-N02478.definitions: A group of male reproductive dysfunctions due to congenital development, postnatal malnutrition, sexual indulgence or consumption of qi, blood, yin and yang due to chronic conditions. These include disorders of the male external genitalia, sterility, and sperm disorders.

V012-N02479.label: 疔疮走黄
V012-N02479.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02479.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02479.englishNames: Septicemic furunculosis disorder; Boil toxins entering the zang–fu organs; Septimic Furunculosis; worsened rooted boil
V012-N02479.pinyinNames: dīng chuāng zǒu huáng; ding chuang zou huang
V012-N02479.definitions: Characterized by swollen sores or ulcers that rapidly spread over the surrounding area. The sores/ulcers appear black on the top and contain no pus. Systemic symptoms include chills, high fever, restlessness, unconsciousness and delirium. Often results from delayed treatment of boils or toxins entering the blood and zang–fu organs upon squeezing.

V012-N02480.label: 疖
V012-N02480.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02480.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02480.aliases: 疖肿； Furuncle disorders； Boil disorders； Boils
V012-N02480.definitions: Characterized by an acute onset of red, swollen area on the skin. Often results from wind fire and summer heat affecting the skin. Furuncles may resolve when pus is drained.

V012-N02481.label: 疖病
V012-N02481.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02481.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02481.englishNames: furunculosis; furuncle disease; Multiple boils; Furunculosis
V012-N02481.definitions: Characterized by recurrent, multiple boils in specific or multiple sites. They often occur when wind fire and damp heat accumulate in the skin.

V012-N02482.label: 疝气
V012-N02482.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02482.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02482.englishNames: hernia; Lower abdominal colic disorder; Hernia
V012-N02482.definitions: Characterized by an abnormal protrusion of abdominal cavity contents into the scrotum or inguinal canal. Contributing factors include maldevelopment, ageing, spleen qi deficiency or stagnation of qi and blood. It may also result from increased negative abdominal pressure due to crying, coughing, emotions or straining due to constipation or problems with urination.

V012-N02483.label: 疟母
V012-N02483.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02483.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02483.englishNames: malaria with splenomegaly; malaria with hypochondriac mass; malaria with abdominal mass; Mother-of-malaria
V012-N02483.definitions: Characterized by distending and painful lumps in the subcostal region, a dark grey complexion and a tongue with stasis. Often occurs when combined phlegm and stasis remain in the subcostal region in chronic malaria.

V012-N02484.label: 疟疾
V012-N02484.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02484.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02484.aliases: Alternating fever and chills disorder； 瘴疟
V012-N02484.englishNames: malaria; Malaria; Alternating fever and chills disorder
V012-N02484.pinyinNames: nue ji; nüè jí; nve ji
V012-N02484.definitions: Characterized by paroxysms of sudden coldness followed by shivering and then fever and sweating. In severe cases, unconsciousness, delirium, and masses in the left subcostal region may be present. Often occurs when toxin of malarial parasites enters the bloodstream and struggles with the Wei-defensive qi between the exterior and interior. It may also occur when toxin of malaria parasites disturbs the heart mind.

V012-N02485.label: 疣目
V012-N02485.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02485.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02485.aliases: 枯筋箭； 千日疣； 枯筋； 箭千日
V012-N02485.englishNames: wart eye (verruca vulgaris); warts; thorny wart; verruca vulgaris; thorny war; Warts
V012-N02485.definitions: Characterized by small (soybean-sized), rough and hard growths that commonly affect the hands, feet and scalp. They are often caused by liver blood failing to nourish the skin coupled with exposure to toxins.

V012-N02486.label: 疥疮
V012-N02486.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02486.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02486.aliases: 疥癞； 疥疮病； 疥癣疮
V012-N02486.definitions: A condition caused by the mite Sarcoptes scabiei. It initially causes tiny, red papules, blisters, pustules or burrows on the wrist, armpit, umbilicus or medial side of the thigh. Scratching-related secondary infection may cause eczematous rash or lichenification. Individuals may experience mild itching during the day but severe itch at night. There are five types of scabies: dry, dampness, mites, sand and suppuration.

V012-N02487.label: 疫斑热
V012-N02487.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02487.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02487.englishNames: epidemic hemorrhagic fever; Epidemic haemorrhagic fever
V012-N02487.pinyinNames: yi ban re; yì bān rè
V012-N02487.definitions: An infectious disease characterized by sudden onset of high fever, macular eruption, headache, pain in the eye socket, lower back painlower back pain, abdominal pain, muscle/joint pain, and redness of the eyes, throat, cheeks and chest. Patients may experience hypotension, shock and oliguria followed by polyuria. Contributing factors may include contact with rodents or field work. The bacterium Yersinia pestis may enter the bloodstream and damage the heart and kidney.

V012-N02488.label: 疫毒痢
V012-N02488.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02488.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02488.englishNames: fulminant dysentery; pestilent dysentery; epidemic toxin dysentery; Epidemic toxin dysentery
V012-N02488.pinyinNames: yi er li; yi dong li; yì dú lì
V012-N02488.definitions: Characterized by frequent stools containing blood and mucus, tenesmus, a high-grade fever, and abdominal pain. In severe cases, loss of consciousness with convulsions, cold limbs and cyanosis may be present. Often occurs when epidemic damp heat toxin (dysentery bacillus) damages the intestine, enters the pericardium or stirs liver wind.

V012-N02489.label: 疫疔
V012-N02489.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02489.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02489.englishNames: cutaneous anthrax; pestilent ding; Cutaneous anthrax; pestilent boil; pestilent furuncle
V012-N02489.definitions: Characterized by skin blisters with eschar (a black centre), surrounding swelling and necrotic tissues. It may also cause systemic symptoms involving the lung, intestines and brain. Contributing factors may include handling contaminated hair products of infected animals and ingestion of undercooked meat of infected animals.

V012-N02490.label: 疫病
V012-N02490.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02490.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02490.definitions: A group of contagious epidemic disorders caused by direct contact with epidemic toxins and subsequent human-to-human or animal-to-human transmission. These include wind pestilence, spring pestilence, summer heat pestilence, winter pestilence, plague, seasonal epidemic cholera, measles, smallpox, etc.

V012-N02491.label: 疫霍乱
V012-N02491.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02491.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02491.aliases: 时疫霍乱； Severe vomiting and diarrhoea disorder
V012-N02491.pinyinNames: yì huò luàn; yi huo luan
V012-N02491.definitions: A fulminating infectious disease characterized by sudden onset of large amounts of watery (like rice water) diarrhoea, sunken eyes and leg cramps. In severe cases, patient may faint or die. It is more commonly seen in summer or autumn. Contributing factors may include ingestion of contaminated food and infection with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae. These factors may damage the stomach and intestines and result in severe exhaustion of qi and bodily fluids.

V012-N02492.label: 疮疡类病
V012-N02492.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02492.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02492.englishNames: category of carbuncle and ulceration disease; carbuncle and ulceration diseases; Sores and ulcers
V012-N02492.pinyinNames: chuang yang lei bing; chuāng yáng lèi bìng
V012-N02492.definitions: A collective term for acute or chronic suppurative skin conditions caused by pathogenic fire heat scorching the blood or wind, cold and dampness obstructing the flow of qi and blood. These include furuncles, deep-rooted boils, abscesses, effusion, gangrene, deep abscesses, chalazion, scrofula, shank sores, fistulas and ulcerations.

V012-N02493.label: 疰夏
V012-N02493.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02493.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02493.englishNames: summer non-acclimatization; Summer non-acclimatization; summer nonacclimation; Summer Fever; summer nonacclimatization; summer consumptive disease
V012-N02493.definitions: A condition characterized by a decreased appetite and lassitude. Alternatively, a low-grade fever may be present. Often occurs when summer dampness affects the spleen and stomach or when summer heat consumes qi and affects the spleen function of transportation and transformation.

V012-N02494.label: 疳气
V012-N02494.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02494.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02494.englishNames: mild gan disease; mild infantile malnutrition; gan qi; mild (infantile) malnutrition; Mild infantile malnutrition
V012-N02494.definitions: Characterized by a sallow complexion, emaciation, a poor appetite, alternating constipation and diarrhoea, poor spirit and irritability. It is often caused by inappropriate feeding or spleen deficiency.

V012-N02495.label: 疳病
V012-N02495.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02495.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02495.aliases: 疳证； Infantile malnutrition disorder； Malnutrition pattern
V012-N02495.englishNames: Infantile malnutrition disorder; infantile malnutrition; infantile malnutrition disease; gan; Infantile malnutrition
V012-N02495.definitions: Characterized by a sallow complexion, muscle wasting and bulging abdomen. Often results from improper feeding, congenital weakness or deficiency of the spleen and stomach. These factors may cause the failure of qi, blood and nutrients to nourish the zang–fu organs, hence delaying the growth and development of babies.

V012-N02496.label: 疳积
V012-N02496.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02496.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02496.englishNames: infantile malnutrition; infantile malnutrition with accumulation; gan disease with food stagnation; mild (infantile) malnutrition with accumulation; Infantile Malnutrition; Mild malnutrition with food stagnation
V012-N02496.definitions: An intermediate stage of infantile malnutrition characterized by emaciation, a sallow complexion, bulging abdomen, veins showing through the skin, poor spirit, and restlessness. Often results from improper feeding or food stagnation in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02497.label: 疽
V012-N02497.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02497.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02497.englishNames: ju; carbuncle and abscess; Deeprooted Carbuncle; deeprooted carbuncle; gangrene; Deep-rooted ulcer
V012-N02497.definitions: A collective term for ulcers in bones or joints due to exogenous pathogens or stagnation of qi and blood. They may develop into inward collapse of toxins or fistulas. Deep-rooted ulcers include headed or headless carbuncles and abscesses.

V012-N02498.label: 疽毒内陷
V012-N02498.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02498.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02498.englishNames: Inward collapse of abscess toxins; internal sinking of carbuncle toxin; inward collapse of carbuncle toxin; inward sinking of carbuncle toxin
V012-N02498.pinyinNames: ju dong nei xian; jū dú nèi xiàn; ju er nei xian
V012-N02498.definitions: Characterized by flat, dark purple ulcerations. Alternatively, individuals may have retracted ulcers with no pus or with grey, greenish pus. Systemic symptoms may include chills, high fever, restlessness, unconsciousness and delirium. Often occurs when retained toxins of chronic sores or ulcers affect the Ying nutrients, blood and zang–fu organs.

V012-N02499.label: 痄腮
V012-N02499.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02499.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02499.aliases: 腮肿； 蛤蟆瘟； 含腮疮； 腮颌发； 小儿痄腮； 鸬鹚瘟； Parotic Swelling； Parotitis
V012-N02499.definitions: An epidemic disease characterized by fever and diffuse swelling and pain on one or both sides of the parotid glands. In severe cases, unconsciousness, convulsions and painful, swollen testis. Often affects children and results from retained phlegm fire in the cheek following wind heat toxin entering the body via the mouth and nose.

V012-N02500.label: 痈
V012-N02500.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02500.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02500.aliases: 痈病； Abscess disorders； 发； 外痈
V012-N02500.englishNames: Anthracia; carbuncle; abscess; Abscess; abscess [disease]; abscess ［disease］
V012-N02500.definitions: Characterized by localized red, swollen, warm and painful sores/ulcers that are soft and headless. They may form pus that is easy to rupture and easy to heal. Often occurs when toxic heat affects qi and blood. A large abscess is known as an effusion.

V012-N02501.label: 痉病
V012-N02501.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02501.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02501.aliases: 痉证； Tetanus； 发痉； 风痉
V012-N02501.definitions: Characterized by neck rigidity, and involuntary convulsions of the limbs. In severe cases, lockjaw or opisthotonos may also be present. Contributing factors may include six exogenous pathogenic factors or epidemic heat stirring liver wind or deficiency wind due to excessive sweating, loss of blood or chronic diarrhoea. These factors may cause malnourishment of the tendons and muscles and result in involuntary contractions.

V012-N02502.label: 痔疮
V012-N02502.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02502.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02502.definitions: A collective term for swollen veins in the anal canal caused by stagnation of qi and blood around the anus due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs, contraction of external wind dampness and internal build up of toxic heat.

V012-N02503.label: 痛痹
V012-N02503.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02503.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02503.aliases: 寒痹； Cold impediment disorder； Painful impediment disorder； Cold hindrance disorder； Cold impediment； 寒胜痛痹； Arthralgia Aggravated by Cold
V012-N02503.englishNames: painful impediment; Painful impediment; Pain Arthralgia; Cold impediment disorder; arthralgia aggravated by cold; painful bi; cold arthralgia
V012-N02503.definitions: Characterized by joint pain with a fixed position that is exacerbated by cold and alleviated by warmth. Often occurs when cold affects the joints.

V012-N02504.label: 痛经
V012-N02504.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02504.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02504.aliases: 月经痛； 经行腹痛； Dysmenorrhea disorder； Painful periods disorder； Menstrual cramp disorder
V012-N02504.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal cramps or weight-bearing sensation before or during menstruation. The pain may radiate to the lumbosacral region. In severe cases, individuals may faint from severe pain. Often occurs when emotional disturbance or six exogenous pathogens obstruct the Chong and Ren meridians. Alternatively, it may also result from the failure of essence and blood to nourish the uterus.

V012-N02505.label: 痛风
V012-N02505.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02505.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02505.definitions: Characterized by recurrent attacks of a red, tender, hot and swollen joint, coupled with joint deformity and trophi formation. It most commonly affects the metatarsal-phalangeal joint at the base of the big toe. Other joints, such as the heels, ankles, knees, wrists, and fingers, may also be affected. Often occurs with phlegm stasis and trophi deposits in joints due to an improper diet, exposure to pathogenic factors, or damp heat accumulation within the body.

V012-N02506.label: 痞气
V012-N02506.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02506.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02506.englishNames: mass due to qi stagnation; spleen amassment; Masses in the spleen
V012-N02506.definitions: Masses in the spleen manifest as raised lumps in the gastric region. They often result from spleen deficiency coupled with qi stagnation.

V012-N02507.label: 痤疮
V012-N02507.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02507.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02507.aliases: 黑头粉刺； 粉刺； comedo
V012-N02507.definitions: A skin condition characterized by noticeable expansion of the pores on the face, chest and back, coupled with blackheads. Often results from wind heat affecting the lung meridian, damp heat in the stomach and intestines or spleen deficiency.

V012-N02508.label: 痧病
V012-N02508.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02508.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02508.englishNames: filthy-attack eruptive disease; Bumpy sand-like exanthem
V012-N02508.definitions: Characterized by a sudden fever, gastric discomfort, abdominal distension or abdominal colic, nausea without vomiting, diarrhoea urges without bowel movements, concurrent vomiting and diarrhoea, and millet-sized red rashes. In severe cases, subcutaneous bluish petechiae may be present. Associated symptoms may include body ache, sore throat, unconsciousness, a band-tightened sensation around the waist, dark, bluish fingernails, and hands/feet rigidity or numbness. Often occurs when seasonal pathogenic qi or turbid toxin accumulates in the stomach or intestines, affects the skin or disturbs the heart mind.

V012-N02509.label: 痨淋
V012-N02509.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02509.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02509.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal pain, frequent, hesitant urination, and bloody urine. Associated symptoms are persistent low fever and night sweats. This condition often occurs when bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis consumes yin essence and impairs the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02510.label: 痨病
V012-N02510.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02510.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02510.englishNames: pulmonary tuberculosis; Tuberculosis; consumptive disease; phthisis; tuberculosis
V012-N02510.definitions: An epidemic condition caused by over-exertion or contraction of the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis. It includes lung tuberculosis, brain tuberculosis, breast tuberculosis, liver tuberculosis, kidney tuberculosis, intestine tuberculosis, flowing phlegm, etc.

V012-N02511.label: 痫病
V012-N02511.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02511.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02511.definitions: A condition characterized by a long-term risk of recurrent seizures. Clinical signs and symptoms include sudden unconsciousness, drooling, convulsions of the limbs, upward staring of the eyes often preceded by vocalizations. Contributing factors may include heredity, fear/fright, emotional disturbance, or improper diet. It may occur secondary to brain diseases, high fever, poisoning or head trauma. These factors can cause wind phlegm or stagnant blood to cloud the heart mind.

V012-N02512.label: 痰厥
V012-N02512.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02512.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02512.englishNames: Phlegm Syncope; phlegm syncope; Phlegm syncope
V012-N02512.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting or unconsciousness, phlegm sounds in the throat, cold limbs, and a deep, excessive, wiry and slippery pulse. Often occurs when emotional disturbance causes qi to stagnate in the throat or when wind phlegm obstructs the airways adn blocks the heart mind.

V012-N02513.label: 痰核
V012-N02513.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02513.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02513.definitions: Characterized by soft, mobile and painless subcutaneous nodules on the neck, arms, legs and back. They vary in size and may not cause skin colour changes. These nodules often result from turbid phlegm retention in the skin due to spleen deficiency.

V012-N02514.label: 痰毒
V012-N02514.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02514.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02514.englishNames: phlegmatic toxin; phlegm toxin; Acute suppurative lymphadenitis
V012-N02514.pinyinNames: tan dong; tan er; tán dú
V012-N02514.definitions: Characterized by painful lumps on the neck, armpit and groin with no skin colour changes at first but later become reddish and cause yellow, thick pus. Often results from external contraction of wind, heat and dampness or in combination with phlegm dampness.

V012-N02515.label: 痰饮
V012-N02515.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02515.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02515.englishNames: phlegm fluid retention; phlegm-fluid retention; Phlegm-fluid retention
V012-N02515.definitions: Characterized by obesity with recent weight loss and a decreased appetite, bowel sounds, and loose stools. Associated symptoms may include palpitations, shortness of breath and vomiting of foamy saliva. Often results from fluid retention in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02516.label: 痰饮类病
V012-N02516.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02516.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02516.englishNames: category of phlegm fluid retention disease; Phlegm-fluid disorders; phlegm fluid diseases
V012-N02516.pinyinNames: tan yin lei bing; tán yǐn lèi bìng
V012-N02516.definitions: Water retention in certain body parts due to dysfunction of the lung, spleen and kidney.

V012-N02517.label: 痴呆病
V012-N02517.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02517.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02517.pinyinNames: chi dai bing; chī dāi bìng
V012-N02517.definitions: A progressive condition characterized by loss of memory, slow reactions and a decline or eventually loss of cognitive and intellectual functions. Contributing factors include ageing-related deficiency of essence blood, congenital deficiency, or phlegm stasis obstructing the mind.

V012-N02518.label: 痹证类病
V012-N02518.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02518.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02518.aliases: 风湿病； 风湿痹病； 痹证； 痹病； Impediment disorders； Joint impediment disorders； Bi-impediment disease/pattern
V012-N02518.englishNames: bi-impediment diseases; category of impediment disease; Bi-impediment disorders
V012-N02518.pinyinNames: bi zheng lei bing; bì zhèng lèi bìng
V012-N02518.definitions: A group of disorders characterized by joint pain, numbness and impaired movement due to wind, cold and dampness or heat attacking the meridians.

V012-N02519.label: 痿痹
V012-N02519.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02519.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02519.definitions: Characterized by symmetric sensory and motor disorders, muscular atrophy, dry, thin and delicate skin and abnormal sweating. Often occurs upon exposure to cold, dampness, toxic heat or poisons. It may also result from an improper diet, phlegm stasis obstructing the meridians or malnourishment of muscles or sinews due to essence-blood deficiency.

V012-N02520.label: 痿证类病
V012-N02520.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02520.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02520.aliases: 痿病； 痿证； Flaccidity disorder； Wilting disorder； Wei-flaccidity pattern
V012-N02520.englishNames: category of wilting disease; Wei-flaccidity disorders; flaccidity diseases
V012-N02520.pinyinNames: wei zheng lei bing; wěi zhèng lèi bìng
V012-N02520.definitions: A group of disorders characterized by limb weakness and muscle atrophy caused by malnourishment of the muscles or sinews, resulting from damp heat, emotions, congenital weakness, age-related deficiency of the liver and kidney, or qi deficiency of the lung and spleen.

V012-N02521.label: 痿躄
V012-N02521.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02521.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02521.englishNames: atrophic wilting; atrophic flaccidity; leg flaccidity; Lower limb weakness and flaccidity
V012-N02521.definitions: Characterized by progressive lower leg paralysis, weakness, atrophy and absence of voluntary movement. Often results from malnourishment of the leg muscles due to congenital weakness, postnatal malnutrition, or retention of damp heat and turbid phlegm.

V012-N02522.label: 瘘病
V012-N02522.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02522.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02522.definitions: A group of disorders characterized by delayed wound healing, dribbling of pus or recurrent ulcerations. They often result from deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02523.label: 瘘管
V012-N02523.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02523.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02523.definitions: Characterized by an abnormal ulcerative opening that forms from a deep abscess to the surface of the body. Often results from a deficiency of qi and blood, coupled with retention of toxins.

V012-N02524.label: 瘙痒
V012-N02524.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02524.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02524.definitions: A sensation that causes the desire or reflex to scratch. It is often caused by exposure to pathogenic wind, blood heat or blood deficiency. It can be seen in disorders of the skin and mucous membrane or food/drug allergy

V012-N02525.label: 瘟毒
V012-N02525.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02525.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02525.aliases: 温毒； 温毒发斑； 瘟毒发斑； Pestilence eruptions
V012-N02525.definitions: Characterized by high fever, headache, and presence of maculae or sores/ulcers. In severe cases, clouding of consciousness may be present. This condition often occurs when pestilence toxin enters the bloodstream with lice/fleas to damage the Ying nutrients or disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02526.label: 瘤癌病类
V012-N02526.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02526.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02526.definitions: A group of disorders involving abnormal retention of qi, blood and toxic phlegm within the zang–fu organs or other parts of the body that cause gradual debility. They include benign and malignant tumours.

V012-N02527.label: 瘤类病
V012-N02527.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02527.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02527.englishNames: Neoplasms; tumor diseases; category of tumor diseases
V012-N02527.pinyinNames: liú lèi bìng; liu lei bing
V012-N02527.definitions: Any abnormal growths of tissue on the surface of the body or within internal organs. These include polyps, cysts or tumours and often result from stagnant blood, phlegm and turbid qi.

V012-N02528.label: 瘫痪
V012-N02528.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02528.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02528.definitions: Muscle flaccidity or disuse of the limbs. Contributing factors may include cold, dampness, toxic heat or stasis obstructing the meridians, malnourishment of the muscles or sinews due to essence-blood deficiency, intracranial lesion or impaired flow of meridian qi. It is often seen in stroke, hemiplegia, qi collapse, brain tumour, polio, muscular atrophy, athlete’s foot, foot weakness, limb weakness, cranial or spinal injury, or surgical wound.

V012-N02529.label: 瘰疬
V012-N02529.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02529.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02529.aliases: 马刀夹瘿； 马刀瘰疬； 湿瘰疬； 鼠疮； 重台瘰疬
V012-N02529.definitions: Characterized by localized smooth, bead-like lumps that are neither reddish nor painful. These lumps may cause discharge of thin, clear pus and form sinuses or fistulas. Often results from yin deficiency of the lung and kidney, liver qi stagnation transforming into fire, or retention of turbid phlegm. Alternatively, it may also occur as a result of fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, tuberculosis and phlegm fire retention in the neck, armpit and hip.

V012-N02530.label: 瘴疟
V012-N02530.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02530.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02530.englishNames: Miasmic malaria; miasmic malaria; miasmatic malaria; malignant malaria
V012-N02530.definitions: Characterized by sudden episodes occurring at irregular periods, unconsciousness, delirium, jaundice and severe anaemia. Often occurs when mosquito bites in damp heat environment enter the bloodstream to disturb the heart mind or causes the bile to overflow.

V012-N02531.label: 瘴病
V012-N02531.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02531.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02531.definitions: Characterized by sudden onset of alternating chill and fever, headache, aversion to cold, upper and lower back stiffness, hoarse voice, abdominal distention, body heaviness, bones and joints pain. In severe case, high fever and unconsciousness may be present. They are commonly seen in tropical or subtropical forests. Often results from exposure to miasma in hot, humid mountainous regions.

V012-N02532.label: 瘾疹
V012-N02532.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02532.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02532.aliases: 风瘾疹； 风疹块； Hives； 风？（病晶）； Urticant Eruptions； 风㾦癗
V012-N02532.englishNames: urticaria; Urticaria; hidden rashes; hidden rash
V012-N02532.definitions: Characterized by rashes with red, raised, itchy bumps that may also burn or sting. These rashes come and go, and do not leave any skin changes. Often results from contraction of exogenous wind, non-acclimatization, or an allergic reaction.

V012-N02533.label: 瘿劳
V012-N02533.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02533.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02533.englishNames: goiter exhaustion; hypothyrea; Hypothyroidism; hypothyroidism; overstrained goiter
V012-N02533.definitions: Characterized by fatigue, feeling cold, delayed relaxation of tendon reflexes, sleepiness and swelling of the limbs. Hair loss, low libido and a slow pulse may be present. Contributing factors may include delayed treatment of goitre, inappropriate use of medication, unsuccessful surgery or brain tumour. These factors may cause yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney and generate phlegm dampness.

V012-N02534.label: 瘿气
V012-N02534.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02534.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02534.definitions: Characterized by enlargement of the thyroid gland, rapid hunger, weight loss, restlessness, palpitations, irritability, heat intolerance and sweating. Tremor of the hands and exophthalmos may also be present. Often occurs when qi stagnation transforms into fire to consume qi and yin and causes phlegm qi to stagnate in the front of the neck.

V012-N02535.label: 瘿痈
V012-N02535.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02535.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02535.definitions: Characterized by sudden presence of painful thyroid nodules with no discolouration of the skin or mild redness, fatigue, and muscle pain. Associated symptoms include fever, sweating, palpitations, tremor of the hands and a foreign body sensation in the throat. Contributing factors may include exogenous wind heat affecting the lung and stomach or stagnant heat in the liver and stomach. The combined external and internal heat may consume fluids and form into phlegm, resulting in phlegm fire stagnation in the neck.

V012-N02536.label: 瘿类病
V012-N02536.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02536.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02536.englishNames: goitered carbuncle diseases; Thyroid disorders; goitered carbuncle
V012-N02536.pinyinNames: ying lei bing; yǐng lèi bìng
V012-N02536.definitions: A group of disorders involving enlargement of the thyroid gland, causing a swelling in the front part of the neck. Contributing factors may include deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi, invasion of exogenous pathogens, emotional disturbance or non-acclimatization.

V012-N02537.label: 瘿肿
V012-N02537.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02537.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02537.definitions: Characterized by an enlargement or solid lump below the Adam’s apple in the front part of the neck. Contributing factors may include age-related qi deficiency, liver qi stagnation and blood stasis, or phlegm turbidity due to spleen deficiency. These factors may cause phlegm qi and stagnant blood in front of the neck.

V012-N02538.label: 癃闭
V012-N02538.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02538.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02538.englishNames: Urinary retention; dribbling urinary block (disease); dribbing urinary block; retention of urine; Dribbling urinary block disorder; dribbling and retention of urine; dribbling urinary block disease/disorder
V012-N02538.definitions: Characterized by scanty urine, dripping urination and lower abdominal distension/pain. In severe cases, individuals may experience an inability to empty the bladder. Contributing factors may include damp heat in the urinary bladder, stagnant blood, stones or tumours obstructing the urethra, kidney yang failing to perform qi transformation, kidney yin deficiency or surgery.

V012-N02539.label: 癣
V012-N02539.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02539.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02539.definitions: Characterized by skin thickening coupled with scaling and exudation.

V012-N02540.label: 癫病
V012-N02540.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02540.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02540.pinyinNames: diān bìng; dian bing; dian  bing
V012-N02540.definitions: A major depressive episode characterized by mental confusion, low mood, dull or indifferent expression, incoherent speech and aversion to activity. Contributing factors may include heredity, personality defects or emotional disturbance. These factors may cause qi to stagnate and cloud the mind. It may also be secondary to brain diseases or poisoning.

V012-N02541.label: 白喉
V012-N02541.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02541.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02541.definitions: Characterized by fever, sore throat, red, swollen tonsils, the formation of white false membrane attached firmly to the pharynx, larynx and nose, difficulty swallowing, difficulty breathing, rapid breathing, restlessness, palpitations and shock. Often affects children in autumn and winter and occurs when bacterium Corynebacterium diphtheria affects the throat and consumes yin fluids.

V012-N02542.label: 白崩
V012-N02542.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02542.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02542.englishNames: acute discharge of leukorrhea; Profuse, whitish vaginal discharge
V012-N02542.definitions: Characterized by persistent, profuse and whitish vaginal discharges that resemble rice-washed water or transparent mucus. Often results from yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney, unsafe sex, or contraction of cold dampness or damp heat. This condition is commonly seen in the elderly women or women with a weakened immune system.

V012-N02543.label: 白疕
V012-N02543.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02543.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02543.definitions: Characterized by red, scaling plaques on the skin, often covered with loose, silver-coloured scales. These lesions have clear boundaries and commonly affect the scalp, back and the extensor surface of the arms and legs. They sometimes crack and bleed. Often results from congenital weakness or malnourishment of the skin due to blood deficiency, invasion of wind dryness, dampness retention or blood stasis.

V012-N02544.label: 白秃疮
V012-N02544.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02544.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02544.englishNames: white bald scalp sore(tinea alba); tinea blanca; tinea capitis; White ringworm
V012-N02544.pinyinNames: bai tu chuang; bái tū chuāng
V012-N02544.definitions: Characterized by the presence of dandruff on the scalp. Over time, lustreless, broken hair with itching may occur. It is often caused by wind heat retention after infection during hair cutting.

V012-N02545.label: 白驳风
V012-N02545.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02545.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02545.definitions: Characterized by pale plaques of depigmented skin that do not cause itch or pain. Often results from disharmony between qi and blood, coupled with contraction of exogenous wind.

V012-N02546.label: 百合病
V012-N02546.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02546.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02546.englishNames: lily disease; Lily bulb disease; Baihe Disease; Lily disorder; disease of all vessels
V012-N02546.pinyinNames: bai he bing; bǎi hé bìng
V012-N02546.definitions: Characterized by mental strain, listlessness, insomnia, and a poor appetite. Often results from disordered qi and blood, deficiency of yin fluids, malnourishment of meridians and disturbance of the heart mind after acute febrile diseases, poisoning or brain trauma.

V012-N02547.label: 百晬嗽
V012-N02547.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02547.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02547.aliases: 乳嗽； 胎嗽； 百晬咳； 百晬内嗽
V012-N02547.englishNames: hundred-day cough; Neonatal cough; hundry-day cough
V012-N02547.pinyinNames: bai zui su; bai 晬su; bǎi zuì sòu
V012-N02547.definitions: Characterized by cough with profuse sputum and rapid breathing. Often occurs in a newborn within one hundred days after birth. This condition often occurs when wind attacks the lung, causing heat and phlegm to obstruct the free flow of lung qi.

V012-N02548.label: 皮水
V012-N02548.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02548.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02548.definitions: Characterized by generalized pitting oedema. In severe cases, bulging abdomen may be present. Associated signs and symptoms may include limb swelling or pain, chest tightness, a poor appetite, no aversion to wind, a white, greasy coating and a superficial pulse. Often occurs when turbid dampness obstructs the flow of spleen qi and causes water dampness to flow over the surface of the body.

V012-N02549.label: 皮痹
V012-N02549.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02549.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02549.englishNames: skin bi; scleroderma; skin impediment; Skin impediment; skin numbness; dermatosclerosis
V012-N02549.definitions: Characterized by progressive local or systemic skin swelling, hardness or atrophy. In severe cases, the zang–fu organs may be affected. Often occurs when wind, cold, dampness or heat remain in the skin or reside in the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02550.label: 皮肤类病
V012-N02550.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02550.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02550.englishNames: skin diseases; Skin conditions; category of skin disease
V012-N02550.pinyinNames: pi fu lei bing; pí fū lèi bìng
V012-N02550.definitions: A group of acute or chronic skin conditions caused by exposure to sunlight, rain, wind cold, wind heat, summer heat and dampness and water dampness, insect bites/stings, allergic reactions to pollen, dust or drugs, or fetal toxin retention.

V012-N02551.label: 皮肤粘膜病类
V012-N02551.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02551.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02551.englishNames: Disorders of the skin and mucous membrane
V012-N02551.pinyinNames: pí fū nián mó bìng lèi
V012-N02551.definitions: A group of disorders caused by six exogenous pathogens, strain, improper diet, phlegm fluid and stagnant blood.

V012-N02552.label: 盗汗
V012-N02552.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02552.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02552.englishNames: Night sweats; night sweating; night sweats
V012-N02552.definitions: Characterized by episodes of sweating during sleep that stop after waking up and are not related to external environmental factors. Most commonly caused by internal heat due to yin deficiency or damp heat opening the skin pores.

V012-N02553.label: 目盲
V012-N02553.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02553.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02553.definitions: Severe impairment or loss of eyesight. Contributing factors may include stagnation of qi and blood, ascending of phlegm heat, hyperactivity of liver fire, or malnourishment of the eyes due to yin blood deficiency of the liver and kidney. It is often seen in cataract or emotional disturbance.

V012-N02554.label: 眉疮
V012-N02554.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02554.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02554.aliases: 恋眉疮； 眉（疒敛）疮； 眉？？疮； 眉lian疮
V012-N02554.definitions: Characterized by itchy, persistent skin lesion, oozing, scab and skin peeling. It is often seen in infants and caused by fetal infection.

V012-N02555.label: 真心痛
V012-N02555.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02555.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02555.englishNames: real heart pain; angina pectoris; true heart pain; true cardiac pain; True heart pain disorder; True cardiac pain
V012-N02555.pinyinNames: zhen xin tong; zhēn xīn tòng
V012-N02555.definitions: A critical heart condition characterized by sudden, severe or persistent cardiac pain. Associated symptoms include sweats, cold limbs, a pale face, bluish lips and an extremely feeble or regularly/irregularly intermittent pulse. Often results from heart yang deficiency, insufficiency of qi and yin, or phlegm-stasis obstructing the heart vessels.

V012-N02556.label: 眩晕
V012-N02556.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02556.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02556.definitions: Subjective dizziness, blurred vision and feeling like a spinning or swaying movement. Contributing factors may include failure of qi, blood and essence to nourish the head, wind phlegm or fire heat disturbing the head or stagnant blood obstructing clear yang. It is commonly seen in wind dizziness, cerebral arteriosclerosis, deficiency dizziness, aural vertigo, pregnancy dizziness, eclampsia, postpartum blood dizziness, motion sickness, cerebral atrophy or mental over-exertion. It may also be secondary to head trauma, neck pain, drug poisoning, brain tumour or ear, nose and throat conditions.

V012-N02557.label: 眼丹
V012-N02557.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02557.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02557.englishNames: eyelid cellulitis; erysipelas of eyelid; reddened eyelid; Blepharitis
V012-N02557.definitions: Characterized by redness, swelling and tenderness of the eyelid. Individuals may also experience fever and headache. Often results from accumulated heat in the spleen and stomach, coupled with contraction of exogenous wind fire.

V012-N02558.label: 眼病类
V012-N02558.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02558.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02558.definitions: A group of disorders of the eye tissue or visual impairment caused by invasion of exogenous pathogens, ascending of wind, fire and phlegm dampness due to dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs or eye injuries.

V012-N02559.label: 眼科杂病类
V012-N02559.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02559.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02559.definitions: Eye disorders that do not fall under the above categories. These include (nutritional) keratomalacia, paralytic strabismus, sudden protrusion of the eyeball, night blindness, myopia and hyperopia.

V012-N02560.label: 着痹
V012-N02560.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02560.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02560.aliases: 湿痹； 湿胜着痹； Dampness impediment disorder； Fixed impediment disorder； Dampness hindrance disorder； Dampness impediment
V012-N02560.englishNames: stationary bi; arthralgia caused by damp pathogen; fixed impediment; Dampness impediment disorder; Fixed Arthralgia; fixed arthralgia; Fixed impediment
V012-N02560.definitions: Characterized by joint or muscle soreness, pain in a fixed position. Alternatively, joint swelling may be present. Often occurs when dampness affects the joints.

V012-N02561.label: 睑弦赤烂
V012-N02561.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02561.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02561.aliases: Blepharitis Marginalis； Eyelid Marginal Ulceration； 风弦赤烂； Marginal blepharitis
V012-N02561.englishNames: Red Ulcerated Eyelid; red ulceration of palpebral margin(marginal blepharitis); red ulcerated eyelid; blepharitis marginalis; marginal blepharitis; Red, wet and ulcerative eyelid; ulcerous eyelid margin
V012-N02561.pinyinNames: jian xian chi lan; jian xuan chi lan; jiǎn xián chì làn
V012-N02561.definitions: Characterized by red, wet and ulcers on the eyelid. Individuals may experience itching and a burning pain. Often results from water/dampness retention in the eyelid due to yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney, or due to qi deficiency of the heart and lung.

V012-N02562.label: 石水
V012-N02562.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02562.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02562.definitions: Characterized by recurrent oedema, hardness of the lower abdomen, distending pain in the subcostal region, abdominal fullness, absence of panting, a bright pale complexion and a deep pulse. Individuals may also have persistent proteinuria and hypertension. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic factors trigger the retained water dampness in the lower jiao (due to chronic wind oedema and skin oedema).

V012-N02563.label: 石淋
V012-N02563.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02563.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02563.englishNames: Stony stranguria disorder; stony strangury; urolithic stranguria; stony stranguria; Stone strangury; stone strangury
V012-N02563.definitions: Characterized by lower back painlower back pain, haematuria or discharge of stones, coupled with positive imaging findings of urinary stones. Often occurs when damp heat scorches the urine into stones.

V012-N02564.label: 石疖
V012-N02564.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02564.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02564.englishNames: stone furuncle; stony furuncle; Headed boils
V012-N02564.definitions: Hard boils caused by internal build up of toxic heat or contraction of summer heat.

V012-N02565.label: 石疽
V012-N02565.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02565.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02565.definitions: Characterized by solid, persistent lumps on the skin with presence or absence of dull pain. Often results from chronic accumulation of phlegm and damp heat as well as stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02566.label: 石瘕
V012-N02566.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02566.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02566.englishNames: stony conglomeration; stony uterine mass; Stony masses; Uterine mass disorder
V012-N02566.pinyinNames: shi xia; shi jia; shí jiǎ
V012-N02566.definitions: A condition that may cause earlier period, prolonged period, and heavy menstrual volume. In severe cases, individual may have solid, bulky masses that can enlarge the uterus. They often occur as a result of stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02567.label: 石瘿
V012-N02567.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02567.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02567.englishNames: stony goiter(thyroid carcinoma); stony goiter; Stony goitre (thyroid cancer); thyroid carcinoma
V012-N02567.definitions: Characterized by solid, rock-like, immobile and irregular lumps in front of the neck. Often results from qi stagnation of the liver and spleen and intertwined stasis and phlegm over a long period of time.

V012-N02568.label: 石蛾
V012-N02568.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02568.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02568.englishNames: tonsil hypertrophy; hypertrophy of tonsils; chronic tonsillitis; stony moth; Stony tonsillitis
V012-N02568.pinyinNames: shi dong; shi e; shí é
V012-N02568.definitions: Characterized by a hard enlarged tonsil. It is often seen in children and caused by stagnant qi and blood due to weakness of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02569.label: 破伤风
V012-N02569.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02569.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02569.aliases: 伤痉； 金疮痉； Tetanus from incised wound
V012-N02569.pinyinNames: po shang feng; pò shāng fēng
V012-N02569.definitions: Characterized by muscle spasm, paroxysmal convulsions, trismus and difficulty breathing. Often occurs when pathogenic wind toxin enters the body through a puncture wound.

V012-N02570.label: 秋燥病
V012-N02570.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02570.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02570.englishNames: autumn dryness disease; Autumn dryness; autumn-dryness disease
V012-N02570.pinyinNames: qiu zao bing; qiū zào bìng
V012-N02570.definitions: A condition characterized by fever, headache, dry cough or difficulty in expectoration of sputum, dry nose and throat, and thirst. Often occurs when dryness affects the exterior and consumes yin fluids.

V012-N02571.label: 积滞
V012-N02571.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02571.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02571.englishNames: indigestion; retention of food; Paediatric indigestion; stagnation of food
V012-N02571.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension, fullness and pain, accompanied by vomiting, acid reflux, diarrhoea or constipation. Some children may also present with disturbed sleep, teeth grinding and finger-sucking. Over time, indigestion may develop into malnutrition. It is often caused by milk stagnation due to inappropriate feeding or food stagnation in the spleen and stomach due to improper diet.

V012-N02572.label: 积病
V012-N02572.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02572.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02572.englishNames: abdominal mass disease; immobile abdominal mass disease; Tangible masses
V012-N02572.definitions: Masses that are immobile and can cause abdominal distension and pain in fixed positions. They often result from blood stasis. Contributing factors may include parasitic worms, food retention, dry faeces or phlegm retention.

V012-N02573.label: 积聚类病
V012-N02573.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02573.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02573.aliases: 癥瘕； 积聚； 痃癖
V012-N02573.englishNames: abdominal mass diseases; category of abdominal mass accumulation diseases; Abdominal masses
V012-N02573.pinyinNames: ji ju lei bing; jī jù lèi bìng
V012-N02573.definitions: Tangible or intangible abdominal masses that can cause abdominal distension and pain. Contributing factors include emotional disturbance, stagnation of qi and blood, food retention, or qi deficiency coupled with external contraction of pathogenic factors.

V012-N02574.label: 稻瘟病
V012-N02574.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02574.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02574.pinyinNames: dào wēn bìng; dao wen bing
V012-N02574.definitions: An infectious disease characterized by sudden onset of high fever, headache, muscle pain (especially the lower leg), eye redness and swollen groin lymph nodes. Associated symptoms include unconsciousness, convulsions, jaundice, haemorrhage, hepatosplenomegaly or hepatic/renal insufficiency. It is commonly seen in summer or autumn. This condition often results from contact with water contaminated with the corkscrew-shaped bacteria Leptospira sp. The bacterium enters the body via skin, mucous membrane or digestive tract to affect meridians or zang–fu organs and damage the Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N02575.label: 穿拐痰
V012-N02575.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02575.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02575.englishNames: ankle penetrating phlegm; Carbuncle of the ankle joint; tuberculous arthritis of the ankle joint
V012-N02575.pinyinNames: chuan guai tan; chuān guǎi tán
V012-N02575.definitions: Characterized by tuberculous arthritis of ankle joint. This condition often results from downward flow of damp heat along the pathways of the three foot yin meridians.

V012-N02576.label: 窦道
V012-N02576.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02576.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02576.definitions: Characterized by a deep, complicated ulcerative channel that connects with an abscess or suppurating area due to delayed healing of thoracic/abdominal surgical wounds. Often results from a deficiency of qi and blood, coupled with retention of toxins.

V012-N02577.label: 筋出槽
V012-N02577.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02577.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02577.pinyinNames: jin chu cao; jīn chū cáo
V012-N02577.definitions: A condition characterized by local pain, impaired movement, hypertonia, nodules, ropiness, and tenderness. Often occurs when indirect or chronic external force impact damages the structure, function and position of the tendons.

V012-N02578.label: 筋疝
V012-N02578.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02578.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02578.definitions: Characterized by an enlargement of the veins within the scrotum (that resemble earthworms), coupled with a bearing-down sensation. Often results from stagnation of qi and blood within the scrotum.

V012-N02579.label: 筋痹
V012-N02579.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02579.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02579.englishNames: muscular rheumatism; tendon bi; impediment of the sinew; Sinew impediment; sinew impediment
V012-N02579.definitions: Characterized by swelling and pain of the tendons, muscles and joints with a dark red skin discolouration and impaired joint movement. Often results from over-exertion, tendon injury or cold-induced stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02580.label: 筋痿
V012-N02580.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02580.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02580.englishNames: sinew wilting; Limb flaccidity; sinew flaccidity
V012-N02580.definitions: Characterized by limb contracture, impaired extension and gradual disuse. Often results from essence-qi deficiency and malnourishment of muscles or sinews due to bed-ridden rheumatoid arthritis or prolonged bone impediment.

V012-N02581.label: 筋瘤
V012-N02581.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02581.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02581.englishNames: nodular varicosity; tendon tumor; sinew tumor; varix; varicosity; Sinew tumour
V012-N02581.definitions: Characterized by varicose veins that resemble earthworms, usually in the lower leg. The dilated veins often occur when standing for a long period of time causes qi and blood to stagnate or when anger damages the liver and liver fire consumes blood.

V012-N02582.label: 筋瘿
V012-N02582.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02582.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02582.definitions: Characterized by varicose jugular veins on the goiter that resemble an earthworm. Often occurs when hyperactive liver fire consumes yin blood and causes jugular vein distention.

V012-N02583.label: 类霍乱
V012-N02583.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02583.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02583.pinyinNames: lei huo luan; lèi huò luàn
V012-N02583.definitions: An acute condition characterized by sudden abdominal pain, vomiting and diarrhoea. Often results from stomach/intestine qi disorder due to ingestion of contaminated food or exposure to summer (damp) heat or cold dampness.

V012-N02584.label: 粟疮
V012-N02584.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02584.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02584.englishNames: conjunctival folliculitis; millet-shaped follicle; millet sore(conjunctival folliculitis); conjunctival follicle; millet sore; follicular conjunctivitis; Millet-like sores on the eyelid
V012-N02584.definitions: Characterized by yellow, soft granulations in the undersurface of the upper eyelid that resemble millet. Individuals may experience a feeling of itching and discomfort. Often results from damp heat in the spleen and stomach, coupled with attack from exogenous wind in the eyelid.

V012-N02585.label: 精冷
V012-N02585.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02585.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02585.englishNames: cold sperm; Cold semen; seminal cold
V012-N02585.definitions: Characterized by a subjective feeling of ejaculating clear, thin semen. This condition may affect the chance of conception. Often results from cold retention in the essence chamber due to kidney yang deficiency.

V012-N02586.label: 精浊
V012-N02586.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02586.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02586.englishNames: turbid sperm; chronic prostatitis; turbid essence; seminal turbidity (disease); Turbid semen; turbid semen; turbid essence(chronic prostatitis)
V012-N02586.definitions: Characterized by white discharge after urination, hesitant urination, and weight-bearing sensation of the lower abdomen. Alternatively, individuals may experience itchy or painful penis but have no cloudy urine. Often results from downward flow of damp heat, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency or blood stasis in the essence chamber.

V012-N02587.label: 精液类病
V012-N02587.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02587.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02587.englishNames: category of semen disease; Sperm disorders; semen diseases
V012-N02587.pinyinNames: jing ye lei bing; jīng yè lèi bìng
V012-N02587.definitions: A group of disorders involving abnormal changes in quality or quantity of sperm due to downward flow of damp heat, hyperactivity of fire due to yin deficiency, age-related kidney deficiency, or mental over-exertion.

V012-N02588.label: 精癃
V012-N02588.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02588.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02588.englishNames: prostatic hypertrophy; hypertrophy of prostate; urethral block; Prostate enlargement
V012-N02588.definitions: Characterized by frequent urination, trouble starting to urinate, weak stream, or inability to urinate. If often occurs when age-related kidney qi deficiency and qi weakness of the spleen and stomach cause phlegm and stasis to impair the qi transformation of Sanjiao.

V012-N02589.label: 糙皮病
V012-N02589.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02589.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02589.pinyinNames: cao pi bing; cāo pí bìng
V012-N02589.definitions: Characterized by inflamed skin that may progress into skin lesion(s) and desquamation. Associated symptoms may include mouth/tongue ulceration, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, and hypoesthesia of the extremities. In severe cases, restlessness, depression, dementia, delirium or unconsciousness may be present. It is often caused by dietary lack of niacin, weakness of the spleen and stomach and subsequently, malnourishment of the skin and zang–fu organs.

V012-N02590.label: 紫癜病
V012-N02590.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02590.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02590.pinyinNames: zi dian bing; zǐ diàn bìng
V012-N02590.definitions: A condition characterized by red or purple discoloured (grape-shaped) spots on the skin or mucosa that do not blanch on applying pressure, especially on the leg. In severe cases, it may affect the zang–fu organs. Contributing factors may include congenital weakness, heat accumulating in the zang–fu organs, allergic reactions to food or medicine, blood heat damaging blood collaterals, or the spleen failing to control blood within the small vessels.

V012-N02591.label: 紫癜风
V012-N02591.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02591.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02591.englishNames: tinea versicolor; lichen planus; Allergic purpura
V012-N02591.pinyinNames: zi dian feng; zǐ diàn fēng
