V012-N02591.definitions: Characterized by itchy, flat purple red rashes that can occur in any body parts, including the oral cavity. Often results from internal heat due to yin deficiency or retention of damp heat, coupled with contraction of exogenous wind.

V012-N02592.label: 紫白癜风
V012-N02592.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02592.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02592.englishNames: sweat strain; tinea versicolor; purple vitiligo; Tinea versicolour
V012-N02592.pinyinNames: zi bai dian feng; zǐ bái diàn fēng
V012-N02592.definitions: Characterized by scattered or merged hypopigmentation macules on the neck, shoulder, chest, back and upper arms, coupled with chaff-like scaling. Often results from excess sweating.

V012-N02593.label: 红丝疔
V012-N02593.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02593.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02593.englishNames: red filament; acute lymphangitis; Red thread-like boil; Acute Lymphangitis; red streaked boil; red-streaked furuncle cutaneous anthrax
V012-N02593.pinyinNames: hong si ding; hóng sī dīng
V012-N02593.definitions: Characterized by warm, painful red streaks below the skin surface on the arms or lower legs. Often results from skin lesion on the hands/feet or pathogenic fire heat affecting the meridians.

V012-N02594.label: 红蝴蝶疮
V012-N02594.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02594.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02594.englishNames: red butterfly sore; lupus erythematosus; red-butterfly lupus erythematosus; Red butterfly-like sore
V012-N02594.pinyinNames: hong hu die chuang; hóng hú dié chuāng
V012-N02594.definitions: Characterized by red rashes on the face or hands with scaling and atrophy that resemble a butterfly. Individuals may also experience joint pain. Often results from qi stagnation and blood stasis due to yin–yang disorder or wind heat accumulating in the skin.

V012-N02595.label: 线瘊
V012-N02595.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02595.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02595.englishNames: verruca filiformis; filiform wart; thready wart; Thread-like warts
V012-N02595.definitions: Characterized by filiform or finger-like warts that commonly affect the eyelids, neck and chin. They do not cause pain or itching. They are often caused by wind affecting the skin, coupled with exposure to toxins.

V012-N02596.label: 经期延长
V012-N02596.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02596.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02596.englishNames: menostaxis; prolonged menstruation; Prolonged menstruation; Prolonged menstruation disorder
V012-N02596.pinyinNames: jing qi yan chang; jīng qī yán cháng
V012-N02596.definitions: A menstrual duration that lasts more than a week or up to two weeks long, with a normal menstrual cycle. Often results from internal heat due to yin deficiency, qi failing to keep blood within the vessels, or stagnant blood obstructing the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02597.label: 经行乳房胀痛
V012-N02597.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02597.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02597.englishNames: distending pain of breasts during menstruation; distending pain in the breasts during menstruation; menstrual distending pain of breasts; Breast distension/pain during menstruation
V012-N02597.pinyinNames: jing xing ru fang zhang tong; jīng xíng rǔ fáng zhàng tòng
V012-N02597.definitions: Characterized by breast distension or nipple itch/pain before or during menstruation. Often occurs as a result of liver qi stagnation or phlegm dampness obstructing the meridians.

V012-N02598.label: 经行发热
V012-N02598.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02598.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02598.pinyinNames: jing xing fa re; jīng xíng fā rè
V012-N02598.definitions: Characterized by fever before or during menstruation. Often results from disorder of qi and blood and disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence.

V012-N02599.label: 经行口糜
V012-N02599.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02599.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02599.englishNames: menstrual aphthous stomatitis; menstrual oral ulcer; menstrual aphtha; Oral ulceration during menstruation
V012-N02599.pinyinNames: jing xing kou mi; jīng xíng kǒu mí
V012-N02599.definitions: Characterized by congestion, oedema and ulceration on the oral mucosa before or during menstruation but heals spontaneously after menstruation. Often occurs as a result of stomach heat or fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02600.label: 经行吐衄
V012-N02600.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02600.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02600.aliases: 倒经； 逆经； Reversed menstruation
V012-N02600.englishNames: menstrual hematemesis and epistaxis(vicarious menstruation); hematemesis and／or epistaxis during menstruation; hematemesis and/or epistaxis during menstruation; menstrual hematemesis and epistaxis; Vomiting of blood or nosebleed during menstruation
V012-N02600.pinyinNames: jing xing tu nv; jīng xíng tù nǜ
V012-N02600.definitions: Characterized by vomiting of blood or nosebleed before or during menstruation. Individuals may experience decreased or little menstrual volume. Often occurs when heat forces the blood to ascend along the pathway of Chong meridian.

V012-N02601.label: 经行失眠
V012-N02601.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02601.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02601.englishNames: insomnia during menstruation; Insomnia during menstruation
V012-N02601.pinyinNames: jing xing shi mian; jīng xíng shī mián
V012-N02601.definitions: Characterized by insomnia or sleepless throughout the night before or during menstruation but stops spontaneously after menstruation. Contributing factors may include excessive worries, over-exertion, blood deficiency, liver qi stagnation transforming into fire, or fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02602.label: 经行头痛
V012-N02602.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02602.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02602.pinyinNames: jing xing tou tong; jīng xíng tóu tòng
V012-N02602.definitions: Characterized by headache before or during menstruation. Often results from deficiency of qi and blood, stagnation of qi and blood, or hyperactivity of liver fire due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02603.label: 经行情志异常
V012-N02603.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02603.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02603.englishNames: menstrual mental disorder; moody state duringmenstruation; menstrual mental disorders; Moodiness during menstruation; moodiness during menstruation
V012-N02603.pinyinNames: jing xing qing zhi yi chang; jīng xíng qíng zhì yì cháng
V012-N02603.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, irritability, sadness or weeping before or during menstruation. Alternatively, depression and sleepless throughout the night may be present. Often occurs as a result of liver qi stagnation or ascending of phlegm fire.

V012-N02604.label: 经行泄泻
V012-N02604.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02604.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02604.englishNames: menstrual diarrhea; diarrhea during menstruation; Diarrhoea during menstruation
V012-N02604.pinyinNames: jing xing xie xie; jīng xíng xiè xiè
V012-N02604.definitions: Characterized by diarrhoea before or during menstruation but stops spontaneously after menstruation. Often occurs as a result of yang qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02605.label: 经行浮肿
V012-N02605.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02605.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02605.englishNames: menstrual edema; edema during menstruation; Puffiness and swelling during menstruation
V012-N02605.pinyinNames: jing xing fu zhong; jīng xíng fú zhǒng
V012-N02605.definitions: Characterized by facial puffiness and generalized oedema before or during menstruation. Often occurs as a result of yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney or qi stagnation and dampness retention.

V012-N02606.label: 经行眩晕
V012-N02606.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02606.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02606.englishNames: menstrual vertigo; Dizziness during menstruation; menstrual dizziness
V012-N02606.pinyinNames: jing xing xuan yun; jīng xíng xuàn yūn
V012-N02606.definitions: Characterized by dizziness and blurred vision before or during menstruation. Often results from hyperactivity of liver yang due to yin blood deficiency or phlegm dampness obstructing the clean yang.

V012-N02607.label: 经行身痛
V012-N02607.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02607.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02607.englishNames: menstrual body pain; Generalized pain during menstruation
V012-N02607.pinyinNames: jing xing shen tong; jīng xíng shēn tòng
V012-N02607.definitions: Characterized by generalized pain before or during menstruation. Often occurs when blood fails to nourish the meridians or cold causes the qi and blood to stagnate.

V012-N02608.label: 经行风疹块
V012-N02608.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02608.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02608.englishNames: menstrual wheal; Wind wheals during menstruation; rubella during menstruation; menstrual urticaria
V012-N02608.pinyinNames: jing xing feng zhen kuai; jīng xíng fēng zhěn kuài
V012-N02608.definitions: Characterized by sudden occurrence of intensely itchy red skin rashes or wind wheals before or during menstruation but stops spontaneously after menstruation. Often occurs as a result of yin deficiency or blood dryness generating wind.

V012-N02609.label: 经间期出血
V012-N02609.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02609.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02609.englishNames: intermenstrual bleeding; hemorrhage during menstruation; Bleeding between periods
V012-N02609.pinyinNames: jing jian qi chu xue; jīng jiān qī chū xiě
V012-N02609.definitions: Characterized by bleeding between the two menstrual periods (ovulatory phase), with a normal menstrual cycle. Often results from blood heat due to yin deficiency, liver qi stagnation transforming into fire or damp heat disturbing the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02610.label: 结舌
V012-N02610.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02610.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02610.definitions: Characterized by tongue stiffness that affects eating or speaking. Often occurs when an unusually short, thick lingual frenulum decreases the mobility of the tongue tip.

V012-N02611.label: 绝经前后诸症
V012-N02611.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02611.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02611.aliases: 绝经前后诸病； 更年期综合征； Menopausal disorder； Menopausal pattern
V012-N02611.englishNames: Perimenopausal disorders; premenopausal and postmenopausal syndrome; Menopausal disorder
V012-N02611.pinyinNames: jue jing qian hou zhu zheng; jué jīng qián hòu zhū zhèng
V012-N02611.definitions: A group of disorders involving irregular menstruation and gradual menopause. Individuals may experience tidal fever, hot flashes, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, flushed face, sweating, restlessness, palpitations, insomnia, poor memory, dizziness, tinnitus, and low back soreness. These conditions often result from kidney qi failure and imbalance between yin and yang.

V012-N02612.label: 绦虫病
V012-N02612.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02612.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02612.aliases: 寸白虫病； 小儿绦虫病； Paediatric taeniasis
V012-N02612.pinyinNames: tao chong bing; tāo chóng bìng
V012-N02612.definitions: A parasitic condition contracted after eating undercooked pork, beef or fish infected with tapeworms belonging to the genus Taenia. It is mainly found in children and characterized by abdominal pain, diarrhoea, weight loss, or presence of tapeworms or larvae in the faeces. The tapeworms remain in the small intestine, obstruct spleen qi and prevent the absorption of nutrients.

V012-N02613.label: 绿风内障
V012-N02613.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02613.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02613.aliases: Acute Angleclosure Glaucoma； Green Pupil Inter Oculopathy； Green Pupil Internal Oculopathy
V012-N02613.englishNames: green wind glaucoma; acute angle-closure glaucoma; green glaucoma(acute angle-closure glaucoma); Green Wind Glaucoma; greenish wind glaucoma; greenish glaucoma; Greenish glaucoma; acute angleclosure glaucoma
V012-N02613.pinyinNames: lv feng nei zhang; lǜ fēng nèi zhàng
V012-N02613.definitions: Characterized by hardening of the eyeball, dilation of the pupil with greenish colour, and drastic impairment of vision. Splitting headache, eye distension and pain, nausea and vomiting may also be present. Often results from liver qi stagnation transforming into fire, ascending of phlegm fire due to spleen deficiency, or disharmony between qi and blood due to yin deficiency and yang hyperactivity.

V012-N02614.label: 缩阴病
V012-N02614.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02614.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02614.pinyinNames: suo yin bing; suō yīn bìng
V012-N02614.definitions: Characterized by intense lower abdominal pain, a subjective feeling of genital (penis, testis, scrotum, vulva or breasts) shrinking. Often occurs when cold obstructs the liver meridian or when yang fails to warm the external genitalia.

V012-N02615.label: 翻花疮
V012-N02615.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02615.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02615.englishNames: proliferative sore; Squamous cell carcinoma; cauliflower-like sore; squamous cell carcinoma
V012-N02615.pinyinNames: fan hua chuang; fān huā chuāng
V012-N02615.definitions: Characterized by rapid growth of pterygium and persistent bleeding upon rupture. It is often caused by liver deficiency and blood dryness, and over time, build up of toxins under the skin.

V012-N02616.label: 耳病类
V012-N02616.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02616.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02616.definitions: A group of ear disorders caused by exogenous pathogenic heat, phlegm dampness, ascending of toxic fire, foreign bodies obstructing the ear canal, malnourishment of the ear due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs, or eardrum injuries.

V012-N02617.label: 耳聋
V012-N02617.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02617.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02617.pinyinNames: dong long; er long; ěr lóng
V012-N02617.definitions: Hearing loss in one or both ears (also known as severe hearing impairment). Often results from internal phlegm fire, deficiency of qi and blood, trauma or drug side-effects/poisoning. It is commonly seen in ear problems or other systemic conditions that may affect the hearing.

V012-N02618.label: 耳闭
V012-N02618.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02618.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02618.englishNames: ear lock; chronic nonsuppurative otitis media; blocked ear; deafness; Ear block
V012-N02618.pinyinNames: dong bi; er bi; ěr bì
V012-N02618.definitions: Characterized by a feeling of ear distension, stuffiness and obstruction, tinnitus, hearing impairment (conductive hearing loss), and retracted eardrum with pus discharge. Often results from obstructed flow of qi and blood within the ear.

V012-N02619.label: 耳鸣
V012-N02619.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02619.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02620.label: 聚星障
V012-N02620.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02620.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02620.englishNames: Superficial punctate keratitis; superficial punctate keratitis; starred nebula; keratitis due to herpes simplex; clustered stars nebula (herpes simplex keratitis); clustered-star nebula; herpes simplex keratitis
V012-N02620.pinyinNames: jù xīng zhàng; ju xing zhang
V012-N02620.definitions: Characterized by presence of multiple fine drops of opacity on the cornea. Individuals may experience pain, red, watery and sensitivity to bright lights. Often results from contraction of exogenous wind fire/heat, internal retention of damp heat, or ascending of fire due to yin deficiency of the liver and kidney.

V012-N02621.label: 聚病
V012-N02621.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02621.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02621.englishNames: mobile abdominal mass disease; Intangible masses; abdominal accumulation disease
V012-N02621.definitions: Masses that are mobile and can cause migratory pain. They often result from qi stagnation. Contributing factors may include emotional disturbance, an improper diet or irregular lifestyle.

V012-N02622.label: 肉痹
V012-N02622.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02622.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02622.definitions: Characterized by weakness, pain, numbness or atrophy in the proximal musculature, coupled with dark red maculae on the eyelids. Often occurs when toxic heat and phlegm dampness remain in the skin.

V012-N02623.label: 肉瘤
V012-N02623.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02623.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02623.englishNames: flesh tumor; lipoma; fleshy tumor; Fleshy tumour
V012-N02623.definitions: Characterized by soft subcutaneous lumps with no skin discolouration or tenderness. Often caused by phlegm and qi retention due to improper diet damaging the spleen.

V012-N02624.label: 肉瘿
V012-N02624.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02624.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02624.englishNames: fleshy goiter; Fleshy goiter; flesh goiter; thyroid adenoma and cyst; adenoma of thyroid; thyroid adenoma
V012-N02624.definitions: Characterized by single or multiple soft, round lumps that move with swallowing. Often occurs when emotion-related phlegm dampness accumulates in the front of the neck or when qi stagnation transforms into fire to consume qi and yin.

V012-N02625.label: 肌痿
V012-N02625.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02625.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02625.englishNames: muscular flaccidity; muscular wilting; Muscle flaccidity
V012-N02625.definitions: Characterized by muscular atrophy, numbness, weakness and disuse. Often occurs when the spleen fails to nourish muscles or turbid dampness affects the meridians or muscles.

V012-N02626.label: 肛漏
V012-N02626.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02626.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02626.definitions: Characterized by discharge of pus, serous fluid or faeces around the anus, a subcutaneous cord-like structure and a palpable narrow tunnel with its internal opening in the anal canal and its external opening in the skin near the anus. Often forms when anal abscesses do not heal properly due to retention of toxins or deficiency of the lung and spleen.

V012-N02627.label: 肛痈
V012-N02627.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02627.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02627.englishNames: Anorectal abscess; anal abcess; anal abscess; anorectal abscess; perianal or perirectal abscess; Anal abscess disorder
V012-N02627.definitions: Characterized by fever, chills, redness, swelling, hotness and pain around the anus. In some cases, it can result in painful anal fistulas upon pus rupture. Often results from overeating of oily, sweet, hot and spicy food, excessive alcohol drinking, anal tears, deficiency of the lung and spleen, or downward flow of damp heat to affect the anus.

V012-N02628.label: 肛肠类病
V012-N02628.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02628.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02628.englishNames: Anorectal diseases; category of anal and intestinal disease; anorectal diseases
V012-N02628.pinyinNames: gāng cháng lèi bìng; gang chang lei bing
V012-N02628.definitions: A group of anorectal disorders caused by wind, dampness, heat, dryness, qi deficiency, blood deficiency, or blood stasis.

V012-N02629.label: 肛裂
V012-N02629.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02629.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02629.aliases: 裂痔； 钩肠痔； 裂肛
V012-N02629.englishNames: anal fissure; Anal fissure; fissured anus; Fissured anus disorder
V012-N02629.definitions: Characterized by a break or tear in the skin of the anal canal. It may cause pain during or after defaecation and bleeding. Often results from heat in the intestine, insufficiency of yin fluids or downward flow of damp heat.

V012-N02630.label: 肛门臊疣
V012-N02630.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02630.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02630.englishNames: Perianal condyloma; anal verruca acuminata; anal condyloma
V012-N02630.pinyinNames: gang men sao you; gāng mén sào yóu
V012-N02630.definitions: Characterized by infectious cauliflower-like warts that commonly affect the perianal area. They have a moist surface and tend to bleed. These warts are often caused by internal retention of damp heat and exposure to toxins.

V012-N02631.label: 肝吸虫病
V012-N02631.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02631.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02631.pinyinNames: gān xī chóng bìng; gan xi chong bing
V012-N02631.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by Fasciola hepatica. It is characterized by pain in the subcostal region, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, a poor appetite, jaundice, masses in the subcostal region and the presence of the eggs of the liver fluke. This condition is often contracted by ingestion of undercooked fish or shrimps. The liver flukes lodge in the bile duct and bind with damp heat to affect the normal functioning of the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N02632.label: 肝热病
V012-N02632.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02632.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02632.englishNames: liver heat disease; Liver heat disease
V012-N02632.pinyinNames: gan re bing; gān rè bìng
V012-N02632.definitions: Characterized by subcostal and abdominal distension and fullness, nausea, aversion to oily food, a poor appetite, and yellow urine. Associated symptoms include fever, jaundice, distending pain in the right subcostal region and enlarged liver. This condition often occurs when damp-heat toxin affects the liver/gallbladder and spleen and causes the bile to overflow.

V012-N02633.label: 肝痈
V012-N02633.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02633.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02633.englishNames: liver abscess; Liver abscess disorder; Liver abscess; Liver Abscess
V012-N02633.definitions: Characterized by a sudden fever, gradually increased distending pain in the right subcostal region, and gradual weight loss. In severe cases, patients may experience severe abdominal pain following abscess rupture, coughing with phlegm that contains pus and blood, diarrhoea with stools that contain pus and blood and fainting. Often occurs when damp heat or parasitic toxin retains in the liver and form pus or abscess.

V012-N02634.label: 肝痨
V012-N02634.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02634.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02634.englishNames: liver consumption; liver tuberculosis; Liver tuberculosis
V012-N02634.definitions: Characterized by pain and mass in the right subcostal region. Associated symptoms include tidal fever and night sweats. This condition often occurs when bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis affects the liver and consumes liver yin.

V012-N02635.label: 肝瘟
V012-N02635.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02635.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02635.aliases: 急黄； 瘟黄； 疫黄； Jaundice disorder； Pestilential jaundice； Acute jaundice
V012-N02635.englishNames: liver-pestilence disease; liver pestilence; Liver pestilence; pestilent jaundice
V012-N02635.definitions: Characterized by a persistent high-grade fever, severe jaundice, pain in the subcostal region, abdominal distension, followed by somnolence, unconsciousness, delirium and mania. Associated symptoms may include bleeding, scanty urine or urine retention. Often occurs when damp heat toxin attacks the spleen and liver to scorch Ying nutrients and blood, damage the liver and, in severe cases, blocks the heart mind.

V012-N02636.label: 肝癖
V012-N02636.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02636.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02636.pinyinNames: gan xian; gān pǐ; gan pi
V012-N02636.definitions: Characterized by distension/pain in the subcostal region, abdominal bloating, palpable lumps in the right subcostal region, obesity and lassitude. Often results from disharmony between the liver and spleen and turbid phlegm accumulating in the liver due to over ingestion of sweet, fatty food and physical inactivity.

V012-N02637.label: 肝系病
V012-N02637.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02637.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02637.englishNames: Liver system diseases; category of liver system disease; liver system diseases
V012-N02637.pinyinNames: gan xi bing; gān xì bìng
V012-N02637.definitions: Dysfunctions of the liver system and meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, overexertion, water retention and stagnant blood.

V012-N02638.label: 肝胀
V012-N02638.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02638.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02638.aliases: 肝着； 肝著； Liver distension disorder； Liver fixity
V012-N02638.definitions: Characterized by pain and lumps in the right subcostal region that alleviate upon pressing or patting. Often occurs as a result of liver qi/blood stagnation.

V012-N02639.label: 肝衰病
V012-N02639.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02639.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02639.pinyinNames: gan shuai bing; gān shuāi bìng
V012-N02639.definitions: A severe liver condition caused by wind fire misting the mind due to viral infection, chronic diseases or excessive alcohol drinking. It is characterized by rapid-onset jaundice and changes in mental status such as mental confusion, hallucination, mania, fear, agitation or indifference with a reluctance to talk. In severe cases, coma, flapping tremor or paroxysmal convulsions may be present.

V012-N02640.label: 肝黄
V012-N02640.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02640.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02640.definitions: A type of jaundice caused by retention of damp heat and stasis in liver meridians due to excessive alcohol drinking, over-exertion or prolonged liver diseases. It is characterized by bluish or dark yellow complexion, weight loss, spasm of the extremities, and a dry tongue. Associated symptoms include bluish fingernails, eye redness or ascites with varicose abdominal veins.

V012-N02641.label: 肠痈
V012-N02641.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02641.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02641.englishNames: intestinal abscess; Intestinal abscess; Intestinal abscess disorder; Acute Appendicitis; acute appendicitis
V012-N02641.definitions: Characterized by fever, right lower abdominal pain and contracture, abdominal tenderness or palpable lumps. Often results from toxic heat accumulating in the intestines.

V012-N02642.label: 肠痨
V012-N02642.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02642.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02642.definitions: Characterized by abdominal pain, diarrhoea, or alternating constipation and diarrhoea. Associated symptoms are persistent low fever and night sweats. This condition often occurs when Mycobacterium tuberculosis consumes qi and yin and phlegm turbidity obstructs the intestines.

V012-N02643.label: 肠痹
V012-N02643.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02643.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02643.englishNames: intestinal impediment; Functional intestinal obstruction; intestinal painful impediment
V012-N02643.definitions: Characterized by polydipsia, dysuria, diarrhoea and abdominal distension/pain. Often occurs when wind, cold and dampness obstructs the flow of intestinal qi and water.

V012-N02644.label: 肠结
V012-N02644.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02644.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02644.englishNames: intestinal painful impediment; intestinal binding; Intestinal obstruction
V012-N02644.definitions: Characterized by paroxysmal abdominal colic, abdominal bloating, absence of bowel sounds, flatus, and constipation. Often occurs when abdominal surgery, tumour compression or carcinoma toxins obstruct the flow of intestinal qi.

V012-N02645.label: 肠覃
V012-N02645.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02645.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02645.englishNames: lower abdominal mass (in woman); ovarian cyst; female abdominal mass; lower abdominal mass[in woman]; lower abdominal mass in woman(ovarian cyst); Ovarian cysts
V012-N02645.definitions: Soft, round and smooth lumps that generally do not affect menstruation. They often result from retention of stagnant blood and turbid phlegm.

V012-N02646.label: 肠郁
V012-N02646.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02646.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02646.definitions: Characterized by abdominal pain and alternating diarrohea with constipation. The symptoms are closely associated with fluctuating emotions. Often results from qi stagnation due to emotional disturbance.

V012-N02647.label: 肠风
V012-N02647.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02647.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02647.englishNames: bloody stools; blood stool; Intestinal wind (bloody stools)
V012-N02647.definitions: Characterized by fresh blood in stools. Individuals may also have haemorrhoids. Contributing factors may include retained wind cold or toxic heat in the large intestine, damp heat accumulating in the stomach and intestines or qi failing to keep blood within the vessels.

V012-N02648.label: 股疽
V012-N02648.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02648.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02648.englishNames: thigh abscess; Thigh abscess; medial-lateral thigh gangrene
V012-N02648.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation in the lateral sides of the thigh. Often results from stagnation of toxins and emotional disturbances.

V012-N02649.label: 肥气
V012-N02649.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02649.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02649.englishNames: liver amassment; lump at the left hypochondrium; Masses in the liver
V012-N02649.definitions: Masses in the liver (right subcostal region) can cause abdominal distension, bulging and bleeding. They often result from accumulation of stagnant blood due to malaria, schistosomiasis, etc.

V012-N02650.label: 肥疮
V012-N02650.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02650.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02650.englishNames: fat sore; fat sore (tinea favosa); Scalp ringworm; favus; tinea favosa
V012-N02650.definitions: Characterized by itchy (painless) red papules with yellowish discharge and a hair projecting in the centre. A shining bare patch (scar) lacking any hair may occur after the scab and scutulum have dropped off. It is often caused by ascending damp heat in the spleen and stomach or getting an infection as a result of a hair cut.

V012-N02651.label: 肥胖病
V012-N02651.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02651.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02651.pinyinNames: fei pang bing; féi pàng bìng
V012-N02651.definitions: Characterized by evenly distributed excess body fat and body weight exceeding the standard weight of the same sex, age and height by more than 20%. Individuals may experience fatigue and slow movement. It is often caused by ingestion of oily, fatty food, physical inactivity, spleen deficiency and subsequently, accumulation of phlegm dampness.

V012-N02652.label: 肺厥
V012-N02652.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02652.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02652.definitions: Characterized by sudden rapid breathing, mental cloudiness and cold limbs. Often occurs when prolonged lung disorders cause lung qi exhaustion or turbid phlegm to ascend to mist the mind.

V012-N02653.label: 肺吸虫病
V012-N02653.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02653.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02653.pinyinNames: fei xi chong bing; fèi xī chóng bìng
V012-N02653.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by an infection with the lung fluke. It is characterized by cough, chest pain, and expectoration of discoloured sputum containing clumps of eggs. Epilepsy, paralysis or subcutaneous nodules may also be present. This condition often results from ingestion of river crabs, crayfish or amphibious crabs contaminated with larvae of Paragonimiasis sp. As a result, the adult worms remain in the lung or affect the brain or subcutaneous tissues.

V012-N02654.label: 肺水病
V012-N02654.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02654.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02654.pinyinNames: fèi shuǐ bìng; fei shui bing
V012-N02654.definitions: An acute, severe condition characterized by sudden rapid breathing, coughing with a large amount of bloody, foamy sputum, oedema, scanty urine, severe palpitations, a pale face, and cyanosis of the tongue and lips. Often occurs when heart yang deficiency or too fast infusion rates cause excess fluids to accumulate in the lung.

V012-N02655.label: 肺痈
V012-N02655.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02655.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02655.englishNames: abscess of lung; lung abscess; Lung abscess; Pulmonary Abscess; lund abscess
V012-N02655.definitions: Characterized by a sudden fever, cough, chest pain and expectoration of strong-odoured pus/blood-stained sputum. Often occurs when toxic heat accumulates in the lung, causes sores or ulcers in the lung lobes and over time, forms abscesses.

V012-N02656.label: 肺痨
V012-N02656.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02656.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02656.englishNames: lung consumption; pulmonary tuberculosis; Pulmonary Tuberculosis; lung tuberculosis; Lung tuberculosis
V012-N02656.definitions: Characterized by cough, haemoptysis, tidal fever in the afternoon, night sweats, weight loss and fatigue. Often results from deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi and Mycobacterium tuberculosis affecting the lobes of lung.

V012-N02657.label: 肺痿
V012-N02657.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02657.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02657.englishNames: lung wilting; lund withering disease; atrophic lung disease; Atrophy of the lung lobes; lung atrophy; lung flaccidity; Atrophic Lung Disease; Lung withering disorder
V012-N02657.definitions: Characterized by shortness of breath and cough with expectoration of turbid sputum. Often occurs when chronic cough damages lung qi, consumes fluids and results in withering or disuse of the lung lobes.

V012-N02658.label: 肺瘅
V012-N02658.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02658.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02658.definitions: Characterized by cough with sticky, yellow phlegm or blood-stained sputum and thirst. Associated symptoms may include tidal fever, chills, pain in the subcostal region, constipation and yellow urine. Often occurs when pathogenic heat, heat of zang–fu organs or phlegm heat affect the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N02659.label: 肺积
V012-N02659.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02659.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02659.englishNames: Masses in the lung; abdominal mass due to lung disorder
V012-N02659.definitions: Characterized by rapid, rushing breathing and masses in the right subcostal region. Associated symptoms may include fever, chills, chest tightness, hiccups and coughing with expectoration of pus and blood. They often result from dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs or stagnation of qi and blood. They can also be triggered by exogenous pathogenic factors.

V012-N02660.label: 肺系病
V012-N02660.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02660.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02660.englishNames: category of lung system disease; Lung system diseases; lung system diseases
V012-N02660.pinyinNames: fei xi bing; fèi xì bìng
V012-N02660.definitions: Dysfunctions of the lung system and meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, over-exertion, water retention and stagnant blood.

V012-N02661.label: 肺胀
V012-N02661.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02661.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02661.englishNames: lung distention; lung distension; Lung distension disorder; lungdistention; Lung distension; Lung Distention
V012-N02661.definitions: Characterized by chest stuffiness or distension, cough with expectoration of sputum, shortness of breath and panting upon exertion. In severe cases, a raised chest, distention in the subcostal region and cyanosis of the lips and tongue may be present. Often occurs when chronic cough or wheezing causes lung qi to stagnate.

V012-N02662.label: 肾俞漏
V012-N02662.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02662.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02662.englishNames: shenshu（BL 23）fistula; Shenshu (BL 23) fistula; fistula at Shenshu (BL 23); Shenshu fistula; Fistula at Shenshu (BL 23)
V012-N02662.pinyinNames: shen yu lou; shèn shū lòu
V012-N02662.definitions: Characterized by sinus around the point Shenshu (BL 23) upon rupture of cold abscess in spinal (lumbar vertebrae) tuberculosis.

V012-N02663.label: 肾俞虚痰
V012-N02663.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02663.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02663.englishNames: deficiency phlegm at Shenshu (BL 23); Deficiency phlegm at Shenshu (BL 23); spinal tuberculosis in Shenshu; Shenshu (BL 23) deficiency-phlegm; spinal tuberculosis in shenshu（BL 23）
V012-N02663.pinyinNames: shen yu xu tan; shèn shū xū tán
V012-N02663.definitions: Characterized by an insidious onset, long-continued pus formation, discharge of thin, clear pus containing rotten cotton thread-like substance, delayed healing, and damage to the sinews or bones. It manifests as tuberculous abscess or sinus around the point Shenshu (BL 23).

V012-N02664.label: 肾厥
V012-N02664.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02664.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02664.definitions: A severe condition characterized by mental cloudiness in patients with chronic kidney diseases. Often results from kidney qi exhaustion and turbid dampness misting the mind.

V012-N02665.label: 肾囊风
V012-N02665.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02665.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02665.englishNames: scrotum eczema; scrotal wind pattern; Scrotal eczema
V012-N02665.pinyinNames: shen nang feng; shèn náng fēng
V012-N02665.definitions: Characterized by severe itching (sometimes pain), erythema, scaling, skin lesions with sometimes oozing rash on the scrotum. Often results from downward flow of damp heat along the liver meridian or wind affecting the scrotal region.

V012-N02666.label: 肾垂
V012-N02666.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02666.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02666.definitions: Characterized by a bearing-down sensation and lower back pain. Alternatively, recurrent bloody urine may be present. Often occurs when constitutional weakness or overexertion causes qi to sink.

V012-N02667.label: 肾岩翻花
V012-N02667.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02667.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02667.aliases: 肾癌翻花； 阴茎癌； 阴茎岩
V012-N02667.pinyinNames: shen yan fan hua; shèn yán fān huā
V012-N02667.definitions: Characterized by papulae or solid nodules on the skin or in the tissues of the penis, accompanied by foul smelling discharge from the penis. It is often caused by constitutional deficiency of the liver and kidney, exuberant ministerial fire due to worry or anger, blood dryness in liver meridian and stagnant pathogenic fire.

V012-N02668.label: 肾水病
V012-N02668.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02668.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02668.englishNames: renal edema; kidney edema; Kidney water
V012-N02668.pinyinNames: shen shui bing; shèn shuǐ bìng
V012-N02668.definitions: Characterized by persistent oedema, belly bulging, scanty urine, lower back painlower back pain, and a dark facial complexion, coupled with persistent proteinuria, haematuria, hypoproteinaemia and hyperlipidaemia. During an acute attack, kidney water is known as kidney wind. Often occurs when kidney yang fails to transform qi and circulate water due to recurrent oedema, coupled with wind heat or drug-related toxins damage the kidney.

V012-N02669.label: 肾痈
V012-N02669.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02669.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02669.englishNames: kidney abscess; renal carbuncle; Renal abscess
V012-N02669.definitions: A condition (secondary to abscess in Jingmen (GB 25) or other body parts) characterized by sudden chills, fever, and severe lower back pain. Weak, hesitant urine stream, painful urination and pain in the renal area and costovertebral angle to percussion may also be present. Often occurs when toxins from the abscess affects the kidney.

V012-N02670.label: 肾痨
V012-N02670.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02670.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02670.englishNames: renal tuberculosis; kidney consumption; Kidney tuberculosis
V012-N02670.definitions: Characterized by frequent, painful urination, low back soreness, and bloody urine. Associated symptoms include tidal fever, night sweats and flushed cheeks. This condition often occurs when bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis affects the kidney through bloodstream and consumes qi and yin.

V012-N02671.label: 肾瘅
V012-N02671.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02671.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02671.definitions: Characterized by fever, lower back painlower back pain and abnormal urination. Often occurs when damp heat or toxic warmth affects the kidney.

V012-N02672.label: 肾着
V012-N02672.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02672.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02672.englishNames: low back pain due to cold dampness; kidney affection by cold dampness; Lower back pain due to cold dampness
V012-N02672.definitions: Characterized by lower back pain and heaviness with a cold sensation and difficulty turning over. Often occurs when cold dampness obstructs the meridians in the low back.

V012-N02673.label: 肾系病
V012-N02673.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02673.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02673.englishNames: category of kidney system disease; kidney system diseases; Kidney system disorders
V012-N02673.pinyinNames: shen xi bing; shèn xì bìng
V012-N02673.definitions: Dysfunctions of the kidney system and meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, over-exertion, water retention and stagnant blood.

V012-N02674.label: 胁痛
V012-N02674.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02674.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02674.englishNames: Hypochondriac Pain; hypochondriac pain; Pain in the subcostal region; Hypochondrium pain disorder
V012-N02674.definitions: Pain on one or both sides of the subcostal region often results from qi stagnation or bile accumulation. It is commonly seen in liver cancer, liver abscess, liver heat, liver distension, ascites, gallbladder heat, gallbladder distension, pleural effusion, masses below the left subcostal region or fibrinous pleurisy.

V012-N02675.label: 胃疡
V012-N02675.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02675.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02675.definitions: Characterized by frequent gastric pain/discomfort, acid reflux or black stools. Contributing factors may include liver qi stagnation, improper diet, exposure to external pathogenic factors or drug-related side-effects. These factors may affect the functions of the spleen and stomach and impair stomach collaterals.

V012-N02676.label: 胃痛
V012-N02676.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02676.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02676.aliases: 胃脘痛； 脘痛； Gastric pain
V012-N02676.definitions: A subjective pain in the upper abdominal below the xiphoid process. Often results from failure to stomach qi to descend due to cold/heat, improper diet, deficiency of qi, blood, yin and yang, and stagnation of qi and blood. It is commonly seen in stomach problems or problems of the intestines, pancreas, gallbladder, liver and spleen. It may also be seen in severe cardiac pain.

V012-N02677.label: 胃痞病
V012-N02677.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02677.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02677.aliases: 胃痞； 痞满； Gastric fullness
V012-N02677.pinyinNames: wei pi bing; wèi pǐ bìng
V012-N02677.definitions: Characterized by stomach stuffiness and distension without tenderness. Associated symptoms include a low food intake, abdominal diarrhoea, weight loss and weakness. Often results from qi deficiency of the spleen and stomach and malnourishment/atrophy of stomach collaterals.

V012-N02678.label: 胃瘅
V012-N02678.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02678.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02678.definitions: An acute condition characterized by sudden stomach ache, vomiting of blood, and black stools. Often results from irritation to the stomach and damage to stomach collaterals.

V012-N02679.label: 胃石病
V012-N02679.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02679.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02679.englishNames: mobile stomach lumps; stomach stone disease; Gastroliths
V012-N02679.pinyinNames: wei shi bing; wèi shí bìng
V012-N02679.definitions: Characterized by palpable lumps in the upper abdomen, accompanied by stomach ache, nausea, vomiting, and a poor appetite. Often results from swallowing of hair, ingestion of minerals or excessive ingestion of food rich in tannic acid such as persimmon, hawthorn or date plum.

V012-N02680.label: 胃络痛
V012-N02680.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02680.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02680.pinyinNames: wèi luò tòng; wei luo tong
V012-N02680.definitions: Characterized by irregular stomach ache, stuffiness and distension. Often results from stomach qi disorder and disharmony of stomach collaterals.

V012-N02681.label: 胃缓
V012-N02681.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02681.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02681.englishNames: stomach prolapse; down-bearing stomachache; Stomach prolapse
V012-N02681.definitions: Characterized by a painful, downbearing sensation of the abdomen that aggravates after eating food or in a standing position. Muscle wasting may also be present. Often results from spleen qi sinking due to improper diet or overexertion.

V012-N02682.label: 胃胀病
V012-N02682.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02682.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02682.pinyinNames: wei zhang bing; wèi zhàng bìng
V012-N02682.definitions: Characterized by persistent stomach stuffiness, distension, pain and discomfort. Associated symptoms include acid reflux and oedema. Often results from dysfunctional spleen and stomach or internal turbid phlegm obstructing the flow of qi.

V012-N02683.label: 胃饮
V012-N02683.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02683.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02683.englishNames: stomach fluid retention; Fluid retention in the stomach
V012-N02683.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension, splashing sound in the abdomen and vomiting of watery fluids. Often results from water/fluid retention in the stomach and intestines due to abdominal surgery or acid reflux.

V012-N02684.label: 胆疸
V012-N02684.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02684.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02684.definitions: Characterized by right subcostal pain and jaundice. Often results from the inability of the bile to flow from the liver to the duodenum. Contributing factors may include gallstones, roundworms, tumour or surgeries.

V012-N02685.label: 胆瘅
V012-N02685.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02685.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02685.definitions: Characterized by right upper abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and occasionally fever. Often occurs when damp heat retains in the gallbladder or stagnant bile, qi and blood transform into heat.

V012-N02686.label: 胆石病
V012-N02686.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02686.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02686.pinyinNames: dan shi bing; dǎn shí bìng
V012-N02686.definitions: Characterized by right upper abdominal distension/oppression or intolerable pain. Often results from excessive intake of fatty, oily food, retention of damp heat or parasites in the gallbladder. Associated symptoms include fever and obstructive jaundice.

V012-N02687.label: 胆胀
V012-N02687.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02687.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02687.englishNames: gallbladder distention; Gallbladder distension disorder; Gallbladder distention
V012-N02687.definitions: Characterized by recurrent right upper abdominal pain with stuffiness and distension. Often results from retention of damp heat or phlegm stasis in the gallbladder or anger-related gallbladder qi stagnation.

V012-N02688.label: 胎传梅毒
V012-N02688.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02688.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02688.pinyinNames: tai chuan mei er; tāi chuán méi dú; tai chuan mei dong
V012-N02688.definitions: A congenital condition characterized by neonatal failure to gain weight, dark red skin with eruptions of macular rashes, and petechial lesions. It occurs when a mother with syphilis passes the infection on to her baby during pregnancy.

V012-N02689.label: 胎孕类病
V012-N02689.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02689.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02689.englishNames: pregnancy diseases; category of pregnancy disease; Disorders of pregnancy and childbirth
V012-N02689.pinyinNames: tai yun lei bing; tāi yùn lèi bìng
V012-N02689.definitions: A group of disorders involving pregnancy, childbirth, and the postpartum period.

V012-N02690.label: 胎萎不长
V012-N02690.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02690.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02690.englishNames: retarded growth of fetus; Slow foetal growth
V012-N02690.pinyinNames: tāi wěi bù cháng; tai wei bu chang; tai wei bu zhang
V012-N02690.definitions: Characterized by a baby growing slowly than a normal baby in the uterus during pregnancy. Often results from deficiency of qi and blood, deficiency of the spleen and kidney or malnutrition after conception.

V012-N02691.label: 胎黄
V012-N02691.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02691.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02691.englishNames: fetal jaundice; Neonatal jaundice; neonatal jaundice; Neonatal Jaundice
V012-N02691.definitions: Characterized by yellowish colouration of the skin, mucosa, and sclera of a newborn baby which lasts more than two weeks after birth. Often results from contraction of damp heat during pregnancy, biliary track deformation, or gall bile blockage.

V012-N02692.label: 胞生痰核
V012-N02692.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02692.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02692.englishNames: phlegm nodule in eyelid; chalazion; phlegm node in the eyelid; phlegm node of the eyelid; phlegmatic nodule in eyelid; phlegm node in eyelid(chalazion); Phlegm node of the eyelid
V012-N02692.pinyinNames: bao sheng tan he; bāo shēng tán hé
V012-N02692.definitions: Characterized by hard lumps in the eyelid that are neither reddish nor painful. Often results from phlegm dampness due to spleen deficiency or damp heat in the spleen and stomach, hence causing blockage in the associated meridians.

V012-N02693.label: 胞肿如桃
V012-N02693.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02693.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02693.englishNames: peach-like swelling of eyelid; inflammatory eyelid disorder; inflammatory edema of eyelid; peach-like swelling of the eyelid; Inflammatory swelling of the eyelid; inflammatory swelling of the eyelid; severe inflammatory edema of eyelid; Inflammatory eyelid disorder
V012-N02693.pinyinNames: bao zhong ru tao; bāo zhǒng rú táo
V012-N02693.definitions: Characterized by severe pain, swelling and redness of the eyelid that resembles a ripe peach. Often results from exuberant heat obstructing the flow of qi and blood.

V012-N02694.label: 胞虚如球
V012-N02694.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02694.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02694.aliases: 睥虚如球； Non-inflammatory eyelid disorder
V012-N02694.englishNames: Non-inflammatory eyelid disorder; ball-like edema of eyelid; non-inflammatory eyelid disorder; non-inflammatory edema of the eyelid; ball-like edema of the eyelid; puffiness of eyelid; Non-inflammatory oedema of the eyelid; ball-like swelling of eyelid; non-inflammatory edema of eyelid
V012-N02694.pinyinNames: bao xu ru qiu; bāo xū rú qiú
V012-N02694.definitions: Characterized by non-painful swelling of the eyelid that feels soft and hollow, with no change in the local skin colour. Often results from water/dampness retention in the eyelid due to yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney, or due to qi deficiency of the heart and lung.

V012-N02695.label: 胞衣不下
V012-N02695.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02695.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02695.aliases: 胞衣不出； 胎衣不出； 息胞； 息胎
V012-N02695.pinyinNames: bao yi bu xia; bāo yī bú xià
V012-N02695.definitions: Characterized by a failure of placental expulsion within 30 minutes of the baby’s birth. Often results from severe deficiency of qi and blood or stagnation of qi and blood upon contraction of exogenous pathogens.

V012-N02696.label: 胞阻
V012-N02696.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02696.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02696.englishNames: abdominal pain during pregnancy; uterine obstruction; Abdominal pain in pregnancy
V012-N02696.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal pain during pregnancy. Often results from stagnation of qi and blood in the uterus.

V012-N02697.label: 胬肉攀睛
V012-N02697.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02697.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02697.pinyinNames: nu rou pan jing; nǔ ròu pān jīng
V012-N02697.definitions: Characterized by triangular tissue growth on the cornea of the eye that may slowly grow and affect the pupil. Often results from wind heat affecting the heart and lung meridians, qi stagnation and blood stasis, excess heat in the spleen and stomach, or fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02698.label: 胯腹痈
V012-N02698.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02698.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02698.englishNames: inguinal abscess; acute pyogenic inguinal lymphadenitis; inguinal carbuncle (acute pyogenic inguinal lymphadenitis; inguinal-abdominal carbuncle; Inguinal abscess; acute inguinal pyogenic car buncle
V012-N02698.pinyinNames: kua fu yong; kuà fù yōng
V012-N02698.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation in the inguinal region. It is often caused by internal retention of damp heat, phlegm qi stagnation, or infection on abrasion.

V012-N02699.label: 胰痈
V012-N02699.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02699.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02699.englishNames: pancreatic abscess; Pancreatic abscess; pancreas abscess
V012-N02699.definitions: Characterized by a history of severe pancreas heat for 2–3 weeks, coupled with high fever, persistent upper abdominal pain that radiates to the back and left lower back, and a palpable cystic lump in the upper abdomen with a fixed location, a sense of mobility and tenderness. Jaundice may also be present. Often secondary to pancreas heat, it develops when retained toxins in the pancreas cause decay of qi and blood.

V012-N02700.label: 胰瘅
V012-N02700.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02700.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02700.englishNames: pancreas heat disease; Pancreas heat; pancreatic fever
V012-N02700.definitions: Characterized by sudden, severe upper abdominal pain, coupled with nausea, vomiting, fever and increased amylase in urine. Often results from excessive alcohol use, binge eating, or extreme emotions. It may also be secondary to gallstones or ascaris-induced colic, which results in retention of damp heat in the pancreas.

V012-N02701.label: 胰胀
V012-N02701.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02701.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02701.englishNames: pancreatic distension; Pancreatic distension; pancreas distension
V012-N02701.definitions: Characterized by weight loss and repeated abdominal pain, distension and diarrhoea. Often results from toxic pathogenic factors affecting the pancreas and spleen.

V012-N02702.label: 胶瘤
V012-N02702.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02702.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02702.definitions: A condition characterized by round, smooth, hard cysts around the finger/wrist joints or tendons. They are often caused by phlegm and fluid retention.

V012-N02703.label: 胸痛
V012-N02703.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02703.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02703.definitions: A subjective feeling of pain in the chest area, including cardiac/chest pain or pain in the subcostal region. Contributing factors may include chest injury, internal fire heat, phlegm-fluid retention, or stagnation of qi and blood. It is often seen in chest injury, pneumothorax, lung abscess, lung heat, pleural effusion, whooping cough, lung cancer or cardiac pain in chest bi-impediment.

V012-N02704.label: 胸痹心痛
V012-N02704.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02704.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02704.englishNames: Chest bi-impediment /cardiac pain; heartache due to chest impediment
V012-N02704.pinyinNames: xiong bi xin tong; xiōng bì xīn tòng
V012-N02704.definitions: Characterized by chest tightness, shortness of breath and paroxysmal cardiac pain. Often occurs when heart qi fails to pump blood and results in malnourishment of the heart.

V012-N02705.label: 胼胝
V012-N02705.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02705.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02705.definitions: Characterized by a relatively thick and hard skin area due to repeated friction or pressure. Calluses are most often found on feet or hands.

V012-N02706.label: 脂瘤
V012-N02706.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02706.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02706.englishNames: atheroma; sebaceous cyst; Skin tumour; adipose tumor
V012-N02706.definitions: A condition characterized by round, soft tumours on the skin that may discharge powder-like stuff upon rupture. They are often caused by retention of phlegm and qi in the skin.

V012-N02707.label: 脉痹
V012-N02707.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02707.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02707.englishNames: vessel impediment; vessel bidisease; vessel bi; Vessel impediment
V012-N02707.definitions: Characterized by a deep pulse in the radial or anterior tibial arteries, asymmetrical blood pressure, and weakness, numbness or pain of the affected limb, coupled with intermittent claudication and skin discolouration. Often occurs when wind, cold and dampness affect the blood vessels.

V012-N02708.label: 脉痿
V012-N02708.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02708.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02708.englishNames: vessel flaccidity; Heart vessel flaccidity; vessel wilting
V012-N02708.definitions: Characterized by muscular atrophy in the lower legs, foot weakness, inability to stand and impaired lifting and flexion of the knee and ankle joints. It is often caused by ascending of heart blood/qi or blood failing to nourish the vessels upon heavy loss of blood.

V012-N02709.label: 脏毒
V012-N02709.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02709.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02709.englishNames: perianal abscess; anal cryptitis; Anal abscess
V012-N02709.pinyinNames: zang er; zang dong; zàng dú
V012-N02709.definitions: Characterized by pain, hot and a weight-bearing sensation within the anus that radiates toward the perineum or buttocks after bowel movements, red, swollen anal sinus, and purulent discharge. Often results from dry faeces, diarrhoea, retained faeces in the anal sinus, or downward flow of damp heat.

V012-N02710.label: 脏腑病及相关病类
V012-N02710.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02710.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02710.englishNames: Diseases of zang–fu organs and associated meridians
V012-N02710.pinyinNames: zàng fǔ bìng jí xiāng guān bìng lèi
V012-N02710.definitions: Dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs and associated meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, over-exertion, water retention or stagnant blood.

V012-N02711.label: 脏躁
V012-N02711.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02711.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02711.englishNames: hysteria; visceral agitation(hysteria); Hysteriform Diseases; Uneasiness disorder; Hysteria
V012-N02711.definitions: A paroxysmal mental disorder in women characterized by mood swings, mind wandering, sadness with no apparent reason, restlessness, crying or laughing with unexplained reason and frequent sighing. Often occurs when emotional disturbance causes qi disorder and consumes heart blood and spleen qi. It may also occur as a result of yin–yang disorder due to menopause.

V012-N02712.label: 脐痈
V012-N02712.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02712.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02712.englishNames: umbilical carbuncle; umbilical abscess; Umbilical Carbuncle; umbilical carbuncle (omphalitis); Umbilical abscess
V012-N02712.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation in the umbilical region. Often occurs when damp heat and toxic fire remain in the umbilicus. It may also be secondary to infection upon scratch.

V012-N02713.label: 脐风
V012-N02713.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02713.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02713.englishNames: tetanus neonatorum; neonatal tetanus; neonnatal tetanus; Umbilical wind (neonatal tetanus); umbilical wind
V012-N02713.definitions: Characterized by cyanotic lips, lockjaw, and facial expression with an unnatural smile. In severe cases, tetanic convulsions and opisthotonus may be present. Often results from infection of the unhealed umbilical stump from wind-related pathogen.

V012-N02714.label: 脑痨
V012-N02714.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02714.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02714.definitions: A condition characterized by tidal fever, night sweats, headache, vomiting, neck stiffness, weight loss, and fatigue. In severe cases, drowsiness or convulsions may be present. This condition occurs upon exposure to patients infected with tuberculosis or when the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis affects the brain.

V012-N02715.label: 脑萎
V012-N02715.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02715.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02715.definitions: Characterized by progressive forgetfulness, and decreased intelligence. Some individuals may present with personality disorder, dementia or tremor. Often results from deficiency of the five zang organs or age-related deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02716.label: 脑鸣
V012-N02716.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02716.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02716.englishNames: ringing in the brain; Ringing in the brain; cerebral tinnitus; intracranial tinnitus; intracranial tunnitus; Cerebral tinnitus disorder
V012-N02716.definitions: Characterized by a subjective sensation of ringing in the brain instead of the ear. Associated symptoms may include depression, anxiety, insomnia, restlessness and poor memory. Often results from aged-related kidney deficiency. Alternatively, it may also occur when stagnant fire (due to stress or anxiety) ascends to affect the head.

V012-N02717.label: 脚气冲心
V012-N02717.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02717.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02717.englishNames: Cardiac beriberi; disease of weak foot affecting heart; beriberi ramming the heart; beriberi involving heart
V012-N02717.pinyinNames: jiao qi chong xin; jiǎo qì chōng xīn
V012-N02717.definitions: Characterized by leg weakness, palpitations, orthopnoea, face and lips cyanosis, mental confusion, nausea, vomiting and oedema due to long-term beriberi. It is often caused by weakened body after long-term illness, leading to damp toxins attacking the heart.

V012-N02718.label: 脚气疮
V012-N02718.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02718.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02718.englishNames: beriberi sore; Ringworm of the leg
V012-N02718.pinyinNames: jiao qi chuang; jiǎo qì chuāng
V012-N02718.definitions: Characterized by symmetrical itchy skin lesions with thickened skin on the leg. It commonly affects the anterior or lateral aspects of the shin. Often results from retention of wind, damp and heat in the leg.

V012-N02719.label: 脚气病
V012-N02719.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02719.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02719.pinyinNames: jiao qi bing; jiǎo qì bìng
V012-N02719.definitions: Characterized by leg weakness, painful calf muscles, poor coordination, tingling, a burning sensation and muscle tenderness, spasm and swelling. Muscle wasting and foot/toe drop may also be present. Often results from spleen deficiency or downward flowing of damp heat (due to improper diet with vitamin B1 deficiency).

V012-N02720.label: 脚湿气
V012-N02720.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02720.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02720.aliases: 臭田螺； Athlete’s foot； 田螺疱
V012-N02720.englishNames: foot dampness qi(tinea pedis); tinea pedis; Foot dampness itch disorder; Tinea pedis
V012-N02720.pinyinNames: jiao shi qi; jiǎo shī qì
V012-N02720.definitions: Characterized by intensely itchy blisters with ulceration, oozing and scaling on the feet. Often results from downward flow of damp heat or living in humid places.

V012-N02721.label: 脚蚓
V012-N02721.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02721.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02721.englishNames: earthworm tinea pedis; Keratotic tinea pedis
V012-N02721.definitions: Characterized by plantar keratinization, dryness and patchy skin peeling, coupled with intense itching and susceptibility to painful rhagadia in winter. It is often caused by internal damp heat coupled with exposure to wind.

V012-N02722.label: 脱位病
V012-N02722.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02722.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02722.pinyinNames: tuo wei bing; tuō wèi bìng
V012-N02722.definitions: A condition caused by sudden trauma on the joint. It may cause functional impairment and failure in spontaneous reduction.

V012-N02723.label: 脱疽
V012-N02723.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02723.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02723.englishNames: Digital gangrene; Gangrene disorder; digital gangrene; Gangrene of Digit; toe or finger gangrene; gangrene; gangrene of digit
V012-N02723.definitions: Characterized by limb coldness, numbness and sudden pain during walking, followed by intermittent claudication, severe pain, a change in skin colour to red or black, and eventually toe/finger necrosis. Often results from deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi, cold retention and blood stasis due to yang deficiency, toxic heat retention due to yin deficiency, or stagnation of qi and blood.

V012-N02724.label: 脾水
V012-N02724.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02724.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02724.englishNames: Oedema due to spleen deficiency; spleen edema; edema due to dysfunction of spleen
V012-N02724.definitions: Characterized by increased abdominal size, oedema, scanty urine, and heaviness of the limbs. A poor appetite and a sallow complexion may be present. Often results from spleen yang deficiency and water retention due to chronic stomach, intestine, liver or gallbladder problems.

V012-N02725.label: 脾消
V012-N02725.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02725.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02725.englishNames: spleen flaccidity; Wasting of the spleen
V012-N02725.definitions: A chronic condition characterized by wasting of the limbs, fatigue, diarrhoea, and strong-odoured oily or foamy stools. A poor appetite, abdominal distension, muscle pain, oedema, spoon nails and clubbed fingers/toes may be present. Often results from poor digestion and absorption (malnourishment of the body) due to chronic problems of the stomach, intestine or other organs.

V012-N02726.label: 脾瘅
V012-N02726.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02726.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02726.definitions: Characterized by overeating, overweight, belly fat and a sweet, greasy taste in the mouth. Often results from overeating of oily, sweet food and physical inactivity.

V012-N02727.label: 脾系病
V012-N02727.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02727.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02727.englishNames: spleen system diseases; category of spleen system disease; Spleen system diseases
V012-N02727.pinyinNames: pi xi bing; pí xì bìng
V012-N02727.definitions: Dysfunctions of the spleen system and meridians due to six exogenous pathogenic factors, emotional disturbance, over-exertion, water retention and stagnant blood.

V012-N02728.label: 脾约
V012-N02728.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02728.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02728.englishNames: splenic constipation; spleen constipation; Splenic constipation
V012-N02728.definitions: Characterized by dry stools, difficult, and infrequent bowel movements. Associated symptoms include abdominal distension, a dry mouth, and frequent urination. Contributing factors may include internal dry heat, age-related deficiency of yin fluids or yin cold retention due to spleen deficiency.

V012-N02729.label: 脾胀
V012-N02729.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02729.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02729.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension, discomfort of the four limbs, body heaviness, and splashing sound in the abdomen. A poor appetite, hiccups, restlessness and insomnia may also be present. Often occurs when cold damp affects the transportation and transformation of the spleen.

V012-N02730.label: 腋痈
V012-N02730.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02730.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02730.englishNames: axillary abscess; carbuncle of axilla; armpit carbuncle; acute pyogenic axillary lymphadenitis; Axillary abscess
V012-N02730.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, warm and painful abscess in the axillary region. Individuals may also experience chills and high fever. Often results from retained heat in the liver and spleen or infection by traumatic injuries.

V012-N02731.label: 腕疽
V012-N02731.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02731.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02731.englishNames: tuberculous arthritis of wrist; carpal carbuncle; Carbuncle of the wrist
V012-N02731.definitions: Characterized by tuberculous arthritis of the wrist.

V012-N02732.label: 腰痛
V012-N02732.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02732.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
