V012-N02732.englishNames: Lower back pain; lumbago; Lumbago disorder; Lumbago
V012-N02732.definitions: Pain on one or both sides of the lumbar vertebrae. Often results from kidney problems, low back trauma/strain or cold damp/damp heat affecting the lumbar region. It is commonly seen in kidney diseases, impediment, strangury, gynaecological conditions, acute lumbar sprain or sciatica.

V012-N02733.label: 腰痹
V012-N02733.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02733.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02733.aliases: Lower back pain disorder； Lumbago disorder
V012-N02733.definitions: Characterized by chronic or recurrent lower back pain that aggravates upon bending, exposure to cold or fatigue. In severe cases, impaired movement may be present. Often results from trauma, strain or exposure to cold dampness.

V012-N02734.label: 腹水
V012-N02734.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02734.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02734.definitions: Abnormal build up of fluid in the abdomen. Contributing factors may include qi stagnation, blood stasis, spleen deficiency, or internal accumulation of damp heat. It is often seen in tympanites, masses or tuberculosis.

V012-N02735.label: 腹痛
V012-N02735.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02735.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02735.englishNames: abdominal pain; Abdominal pain disorder; Abdominal pain
V012-N02735.definitions: Pain that occurs between the chest and pelvic regions. It can be acute or chronic, often resulting from external contraction of six pathogenic factors, parasitic worms, improper diet, stones obstructing the flow of qi and blood or deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02736.label: 腹胀病
V012-N02736.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02736.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02736.pinyinNames: fu zhang bing; fù zhàng bìng
V012-N02736.definitions: Characterized by abdominal fullness and pain, along with increased abdominal size. Contributing factors may include damp heat in the liver and gallbladder, spleen deficiency with qi stagnation, and cold dampness affecting the spleen.

V012-N02737.label: 腿风
V012-N02737.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02737.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02737.englishNames: restless legs syndrome; Restless legs pattern
V012-N02737.definitions: Characterized by intolerable soreness, numbness and a burning pain felt deep in the calf muscle. Individuals may experience a crawling feeling, itching, pricking pain that may occasionally affect the arms. The symptoms are alleviated by moving the affected leg, change of body position, walking or tapping the affected area. However, the symptoms recur after relaxation and can severely disturb the sleep. Often occurs when pathogenic wind retains in the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence and causes meridian qi and blood to stagnate.

V012-N02738.label: 膈疝
V012-N02738.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02738.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02738.englishNames: diaphragm hernia; disphragmatic hernia; diaphragmatic hernia; Diaphragmatic hernia
V012-N02738.definitions: Characterized by sudden distension and pain in the diaphragmatic area and severe vomiting. It results either from congenital defect or postnatal injuries.

V012-N02739.label: 膏淋
V012-N02739.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02739.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02739.englishNames: unctuous stranguria; Unctuous strangury; chylous stranguria; unctuous strangury; stranguria due to chyluria
V012-N02739.definitions: Characterized by recurrent painful discharge of turbid, milky urine like rice water, cream or sauce, and back pain/soreness. Associated symptoms may include hesitant urination, oedema, fatigue, or anaemia. Symptoms can be induced or exacerbated by high-fat diet, fatigue or exposure to cold. Contributing factors may include damp heat due to deficiency of the spleen and kidney, filariasis, tuberculosis or tumours. These factors may impair the qi transformation of the lower jiao.

V012-N02740.label: 膏药风
V012-N02740.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02740.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02740.pinyinNames: gao yao feng; gāo yào fēng
V012-N02740.definitions: Characterized by itchy red spots and blisters with a sharply demarcated edge under adhesive plaster.

V012-N02741.label: 膝弯痈
V012-N02741.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02741.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02741.englishNames: popliteal carbuncle; poples abscess; Abscess around Weizhong (BL40)
V012-N02741.pinyinNames: xi wan yong; xī wān yōng
V012-N02741.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation around the point Weizhong (BL 40). Often results from downward flow of damp heat or infection on abrasion.

V012-N02742.label: 膝痈
V012-N02742.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02742.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02742.englishNames: Knee abscess; patellar carbuncle; knee abscess
V012-N02742.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation around the knees. Often occurs when toxins affect the liver, spleen and kidney meridians.

V012-N02743.label: 膝痹
V012-N02743.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02743.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02743.definitions: Characterized by knee pain (in a fixed position), swelling, joint stiffness and impaired movement, coupled with bony crepitus. Contributing factors may include age-related malnourishment of the knee joints, impaired meridian qi, or long-term standing and/or weight bearing.

V012-N02744.label: 臀痈
V012-N02744.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02744.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02744.englishNames: Pyogenic Carbuncle of Buttock; gluteal abscess; pyogenic carbuncle of buttock; gluteal cellulitis; buttock carbuncle; Gluteal abscess; armpit carbuncle; acute pyogenic axillary
V012-N02744.definitions: Characterized by a sudden onset, deep location and large area of abscess formation in the gluteal region. It tends to form pus and takes a long period of time to heal. This condition often results from damp heat accumulating in the bladder meridian or infection by intramuscular injection.

V012-N02745.label: 臁疮
V012-N02745.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02745.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02745.englishNames: shank sore; chronic shank ulcer; ecthyma; chronic ulcer of shank; Chronic Ulcer of Shank; Shank sore
V012-N02745.definitions: Characterized by chronic, recurrent sores or ulcers on the lower leg. They often result from infection via skin lesions, downward flow of damp heat, or stagnation of qi and blood transforming into heat.

V012-N02746.label: 臊疣
V012-N02746.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02746.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02746.definitions: Characterized by infectious, cauliflower-like warts that commonly affect the external genitalia and perianal area. They have a moist surface and tend to bleed. These warts are often caused by internal retention of damp heat and exposure to toxins.

V012-N02747.label: 臌胀病
V012-N02747.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02747.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02747.aliases: 臌胀； 鼓胀； 蛊胀； 单腹胀； Tympanites disorder
V012-N02747.pinyinNames: gu zhang bing; gǔ zhàng bìng
V012-N02747.definitions: Characterized by severe abdominal bulging (like a drum). Contributing factors may include excessive alcohol drinking, liver disease, tuberculosis, abdominal masses or schistosomiasis. This condition is subdivided into alcohol tympanites, water tympanites, qi tympanites, blood tympanites and parasitic tympanites.

V012-N02748.label: 自汗
V012-N02748.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02748.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02748.definitions: Characterized by persistent, spontaneous day sweating that is not attributed to physical exertion, hot weather, wearing too many clothes or taking diaphoretics. It is mostly commonly caused by disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence or qi failing to secure the yin fluids.

V012-N02749.label: 舌痈
V012-N02749.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02749.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02749.definitions: Characterized by localized redness, swelling and pain with a burning sensation. Alternatively, discharge of pus, fever, thirst and constipation may be present. It is often caused by ascending of heart fire or stomach fire, or deficiency fire scorching the tongue.

V012-N02750.label: 花柳毒淋
V012-N02750.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02750.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02750.pinyinNames: hua liu dong lin; huā liǔ dú lìn; hua liu er lin
V012-N02750.definitions: A sexually transmitted disease characterized by difficult, frequent and painful urination, red, swollen urethral meatus and discharge of pus or blood. This condition results from sexual contact with an infected person. Then, the bacterium Neisseria gonorrhoeae affects the urethra, urinary bladder and essence chamber.

V012-N02751.label: 花癫
V012-N02751.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02751.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02751.definitions: A condition characterized by uncontrolled obsessions or abnormal behaviours about non-specific members of the opposite sex. It is often caused by liver qi stagnation transforming into fire or hyperactivity of ministerial fire.

V012-N02752.label: 茧唇
V012-N02752.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02752.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02752.aliases: 唇癌； 唇菌； 唇岩
V012-N02752.englishNames: lip cancer; Lip ulcer; cocoon lip; carcinoma of lips
V012-N02752.definitions: Characterized by presence of outgrowths, oozing, scabbing and thickening in the lips. Over time, everted lower lip, skin peeling and distending pain may occur. Often occurs when damp heat of the spleen and stomach ascends to the lips.

V012-N02753.label: 药毒
V012-N02753.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02753.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02753.aliases: 药疹； Drug eruption； 膏药风
V012-N02753.englishNames: Drug-induced dermatitis; dermatitis medicamentosa; medicinal toxicity
V012-N02753.definitions: Characterized by an adverse drug reaction of the skin and mucous membranes. With a latent period, drug eruption may cause extensive, symmetrical itchy maculae or blisters. Some may also have fever. It is often caused by oral administration, injection or topical application of medications.

V012-N02754.label: 药毒类病
V012-N02754.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02754.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02754.englishNames: Drug poisoning; drug poisoning diseases; category of drug poisoning disease
V012-N02754.pinyinNames: yào dú lèi bìng; yao dong lei bing; yao er lei bing
V012-N02754.definitions: A group of disorders caused by overdose, incorrect use, administration of inappropriately prepared or contaminated drugs or incompatible drug combination. Examples include gelsemium poisoning, Tripterygium wilfordii poisoning, aconite poisoning, almond poisoning, croton poisoning and arsenic poisoning.

V012-N02755.label: 菜乌紫病
V012-N02755.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02755.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02755.englishNames: pickle disease; pickles cyanosis; Pickles cyanosis
V012-N02755.pinyinNames: cai wu zi bing; cài wū zǐ bìng
V012-N02755.definitions: Food poisoning caused by ingestion of newly pickled vegetables.

V012-N02756.label: 萎黄病
V012-N02756.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02756.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02756.pinyinNames: wei huang bing; wěi huáng bìng
V012-N02756.definitions: Characterized by dry, sallow complexion, pale eyelids and finger/toe nails, dizziness and fatigue. Chapped lips, poor memory and difficulty concentrating may be present. Contributing factors may include malnutrition, weakness of the spleen and stomach, food retention, parasites or heavy menstruation.

V012-N02757.label: 落枕
V012-N02757.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02757.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02757.aliases: 外伤性斜颈； 急性斜颈
V012-N02757.englishNames: stiff neck; Neck stiffness; Stiff Neck
V012-N02757.pinyinNames: lào zhěn; luo zhen; lao zhen
V012-N02757.definitions: A condition characterized by pain or soreness that limits neck movement. Often results from inappropriate pillow height, poor sleep posture or external contraction of wind cold.

V012-N02758.label: 薄厥
V012-N02758.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02758.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02758.englishNames: flopping syncope; emotional syncope; sudden syncope; Flopping syncope
V012-N02758.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting, intense headache, high blood pressure, restlessness, a red face, numbness on one side of the body, and tremor of the limbs. In severe cases, vomiting of blood, mental cloudiness, sweating and rapid breathing may be present. Often occurs when emotions like sudden rage cause hyperactivity of liver yang and disordered qi and blood.

V012-N02759.label: 虚劳类病
V012-N02759.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02759.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02759.englishNames: consumptive diseases diseases; category of consumptive disease; Deficiency/consumption
V012-N02759.pinyinNames: xu lao lei bing; xū láo lèi bìng
V012-N02759.definitions: A collective term for chronic deficiency or consumption of qi, blood, yin, yang and the five zang organs. Contributing factors may include over-exertion, an improper diet, emotions, alcohol consumption, sexual indulgence or major medical conditions.

V012-N02760.label: 虚损
V012-N02760.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02760.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02760.definitions: A group of chronic consumptive conditions characterized by a lustreless complexion, lassitude, shortness of breath, a low voice, dizziness, blurred vision and poor appetite. Associated symptoms include abdominal distension, loose stools, palpitations, forgetfulness, nocturnal emissions, irregular menstruation, spontaneous sweating, night sweats, weight loss, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, cold intolerance and cold limbs. Contributing factors may include major or chronic disease, heavy blood loss during childbirth or surgery, congenital weakness or ageing. These factors may consume qi, blood, yin, yang and the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02761.label: 虚痉
V012-N02761.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02761.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02761.englishNames: deficiency convulsion; Convulsions due to deficiency
V012-N02761.definitions: Characterized by tremor of the hands/feet, and muscle spasm/pain. In severe cases, shaking of the head, convulsions, lassitude, fatigue, spontaneous sweating and dizziness may be present. Often occurs when chronic diseases consume yin fluids and result in internal stirring of deficiency wind or failure of qi and blood to nourish muscles or tendons.

V012-N02762.label: 虚眩
V012-N02762.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02762.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02762.englishNames: deficiency dizziness; Dizziness due to deficiency
V012-N02762.definitions: Characterized by dizziness that aggravates upon postural changes, fatigue, low blood pressure and a weak pulse. It is often caused by failure of qi and blood to nourish the heart and mind due to congenital deficiency, postnatal malnutrition or over-exertion.

V012-N02763.label: 虫兽咬伤病
V012-N02763.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02763.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02763.englishNames: Insect or animal bites; insect or beast bite; insect or beast bites
V012-N02763.pinyinNames: chóng shòu yǎo shāng bìng; chong shou yao shang bing
V012-N02763.definitions: A group of injuries caused by snakes, dogs or other animals. The toxins from the bites enter the bloodstream and may cause localized redness, swelling, pain or numbness, accompanied by mild or severe systemic symptoms.

V012-N02764.label: 虫臌
V012-N02764.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02764.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02764.englishNames: Parasitic tympanites; parasitic tympanites; Parasitic Tympanites
V012-N02764.definitions: A type of tympanites characterized by frequent abdominal distension and pain, increased abdominal size, varicose abdominal veins, weight loss, fatigue, palpable subcostal masses, a sallow or dark grey facial complexion. Often results from qi stagnation, blood stasis and water retention due to parasitic toxins affecting the liver and spleen.

V012-N02765.label: 虫螫伤类病
V012-N02765.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02765.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02765.aliases: 虫螫； 恶虫叮咬伤； Insect stings/bites
V012-N02765.englishNames: category of insect bite disease; insect bite diseases; Insect stings
V012-N02765.pinyinNames: chong zhe shang lei bing; chóng shì shāng lèi bìng
V012-N02765.definitions: A group of disorders caused by insect stings and characterized by local itching, pain, redness and rashes/papulae.

V012-N02766.label: 蚂蝗咬伤
V012-N02766.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02766.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02766.englishNames: leech sting; leech bite; Leech bites
V012-N02766.pinyinNames: ma huang yao shang; mǎ huáng yǎo shāng
V012-N02766.definitions: Leech bites may cause persistent bleeding at the site of bites with mild itching and pain.

V012-N02767.label: 蚕豆黄
V012-N02767.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02767.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02767.pinyinNames: cán dòu huáng; can dong huang; can er huang
V012-N02767.definitions: A type of haemolytic jaundice caused by ingestion of fresh fava beans. It is characterized by jaundice, pale face, body ache, abdominal pain, and urine in a colour resembling thick black tea or soy sauce. Associated symptoms include fever, nausea, vomiting, hypersomnia, palpitations, and scanty urine or urine retention.

V012-N02768.label: 蛀节疔
V012-N02768.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02768.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02768.englishNames: ding of finger condyle; interphalangeal boil; Boils on the knuckle; interphalangeal ding
V012-N02768.pinyinNames: zhu jie ding; zhù jié dīng
V012-N02768.definitions: Characterized by furuncles on the knuckles of the hand.

V012-N02769.label: 蛇串疮
V012-N02769.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02769.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02769.aliases: 蛇丹； 缠腰火丹； 缠腰龙； 带状疱疹； Shingles； Herpes zoster
V012-N02769.englishNames: snakelike sores; herpes zoster; Herpes zoster disorder; snake-like sore; Snake-shaped sore; snake-clustered sores
V012-N02769.pinyinNames: she chuan chuang; shé chuàn chuāng
V012-N02769.definitions: Characterized by severely painful, bean-sized skin rash with a single stripe (snake-shaped) of blisters that wraps around one side of the torso. Often results from liver qi stagnation transforming into fire, damp heat retention in the spleen meridian or stagnation of damp heat and toxic fire in the skin.

V012-N02770.label: 蛇头疔
V012-N02770.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02770.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02770.englishNames: snake-head ding; whitlow; snake-head boil; snake-head whitlow; felon; snakehead furuncle; Snake head-like whitlow
V012-N02770.pinyinNames: she tou ding; shé tóu dīng
V012-N02770.definitions: Characterized by a swollen fingertip that resembles the head of a snake. It is known as digital pyogenic inflammation in Western medicine.

V012-N02771.label: 蛇皮癣
V012-N02771.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02771.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02771.englishNames: ichthyosis; Dry skin disorder; Snake skin-like tinea
V012-N02771.pinyinNames: she pi xuan; shé pí xuǎn
V012-N02771.definitions: Characterized by dry, thickened, scaly skin that resembles snake skin. Often results from genetic factors or malnourishment of the skin due to blood deficiency and invasion of wind dryness.

V012-N02772.label: 蛇节疔
V012-N02772.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02772.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02772.pinyinNames: shé jié dīng; she jie ding
V012-N02772.definitions: Characterized by boils on the middle segment of the fingers that may cause swelling of the whole finger.

V012-N02773.label: 蛔厥
V012-N02773.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02773.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02773.englishNames: cold limbs syndrome due to ascariasis; Biliary ascariasis; syncope due to ascariasis; cold limbs due to ascariasis
V012-N02773.definitions: Characterized by a history of ascariasis, a sudden drilling upper abdominal colic pain, and vomiting. In severe cases, patients may present with vomiting of roundworms, cold sweats, cold limbs and fever. Contributing factors may include intestinal cold with stomach heat, reversed qi flow of the liver and gallbladder or roundworms entering the biliary tract due to incorrect roundworm-repelling methods.

V012-N02774.label: 蛔虫病
V012-N02774.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02774.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02774.pinyinNames: huí chóng bìng; hui chong bing
V012-N02774.definitions: A parasitic condition mainly found in children and characterized by a poor appetite, periumbilical pain, underweight, swelling of the abdomen, and worms in stools. The roundworm may infest the bile ducts, pancreas or appendix and cause severe complications. The parasite is acquired through ingestion of embryonated eggs in contaminated food. After the eggs are swallowed, they live and remain in the intestines, where they hatch into larvae. Over time, they transform into damp heat to damage the spleen and stomach and absorb nutrients.

V012-N02775.label: 蛲虫病
V012-N02775.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02775.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02775.aliases: 小儿蛲虫病； Paediatric pinworm infection
V012-N02775.pinyinNames: nao chong bing; náo chóng bìng
V012-N02775.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by ingesting the eggs of pinworms. It is mainly found in children and characterized by severe itching in the anal or vaginal area. Secondary anal erosion, eczema or bleeding may develop in the areas that are constantly scratched. This condition is usually first identified when live, thin, white pinworms are noticed in the faeces.

V012-N02776.label: 蜂螫伤
V012-N02776.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02776.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02776.pinyinNames: feng zhe shang; fēng shì shāng
V012-N02776.definitions: Bee/wasp stings may cause the venom or toxin to enter the body or remain in the skin. Individuals may experience pricking pain, scratching pain, a burning sensation, skin redness and oedema, papules or blisters at the sting sites. In severe cases, fever, dizziness, nausea, vomiting and low blood pressure may be present.

V012-N02777.label: 蜈蚣螫伤
V012-N02777.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02777.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02777.pinyinNames: wú gōng shì shāng; wu gong zhe shang
V012-N02777.definitions: An injury resulting from the action of a centipede’s forcipules, pincer-like appendages that pierce the skin and inject venom into the wound. It is characterized by stasis spots, redness, swelling and pain and swelling in the area of the bite. Associated symptoms may include swollen, painful lymph nodes in the regions of the bitten limb, chills and fever.

V012-N02778.label: 蜘蛛咬伤
V012-N02778.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02778.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02778.pinyinNames: zhi zhu yao shang; zhī zhū yǎo shāng
V012-N02778.definitions: Spider bites may cause the venom to enter the body. Individuals may experience pricking pain, itching or oedema at the bite sites, accompanied by abdominal spasm/pain, muscle rigidity, and eyelid ptosis. Chills, vomiting, salivation or difficulty breathing may also be present.

V012-N02779.label: 蜣螂蛀
V012-N02779.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02779.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02779.englishNames: dung beetle erosion; tuberculosis of finger joint; Dung beetle-shaped joints; interphalangeal putrefaction
V012-N02779.pinyinNames: qiang lang zhu; qiāng láng zhù
V012-N02779.definitions: Characterized by swollen (without redness, pain or hot sensation) metacarpophalangeal joints that resemble a dung beetle, impaired flexion and extension, and a gradual sensation of numbness or pain. It is known as tuberculosis of the metacarpophalangeal joints in modern medicine. This condition often results from deficiency-related phlegm dampness and prolonged cold retention.

V012-N02780.label: 蝎螫伤
V012-N02780.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02780.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02780.englishNames: Scorpion sting; scorpion sting; scorpian sting
V012-N02780.pinyinNames: xiē shì shāng; xie zhe shang
V012-N02780.definitions: Scorpion sting may cause the venom or toxin to enter the body. Individuals may experience severe pain, scratching pain, a burning sensation, skin redness/swelling or blisters as the bite sites. In severe cases, red streaks on the skin, enlarged lymph nodes, chills, fever, nausea, vomiting or respiratory paralysis may be present.

V012-N02781.label: 蠼螋伤
V012-N02781.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02781.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02781.englishNames: staphylinid injury; Rove beetle injuries; anisolobis sting
V012-N02781.pinyinNames: qu sou shang; qú sōu shāng
V012-N02781.definitions: Exposure to pederin, a strong toxin in the blood of rove beetle, may cause a burning pain, itching, linear red spots or pustules.

V012-N02782.label: 血厥
V012-N02782.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02782.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02782.aliases: 脉厥； Blood Syncope； Syncope due to Excessive Bleeding； Blood syncope disorder
V012-N02782.englishNames: blood syncope; Bleeding Syncope; Blood syncope; syncope due to excessive bleeding
V012-N02782.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting, a pale face, cold limbs and transient mental cloudiness. Contributing factors may include nervousness, fear, intense traumatic pain, ageing or a sudden change in posture. These factors may disturb qi activity and result in the failure of blood to nourish the brain.

V012-N02783.label: 血吸虫病
V012-N02783.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02783.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02783.aliases: 小儿血吸虫病； 蛊虫病； Snail fever； Paediatric schistosomiasis
V012-N02783.pinyinNames: xue xi chong bing; xuè xī chóng bìng
V012-N02783.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by schistosomes. It is characterized by skin itching, cough, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, masses in the subcostal region, weight loss, ascites, and presence of schistosome eggs in stools. This condition is contracted by contact with fresh water organisms contaminated with the parasites. Once the parasites enter the body, they may affect the flow of qi, blood and body fluids in the lung, intestines or liver.

V012-N02784.label: 血汗
V012-N02784.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02784.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02784.definitions: Characterized by red coloured sweats, along with symptoms due to fire-heat or deficiency of qi and blood. Contributing factors may include exogenous fire heat affecting the surface of the body, fire hyperactivity of the heart, liver and stomach, or deficiency of qi and blood.

V012-N02785.label: 血淋
V012-N02785.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02785.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02785.englishNames: Blood strangury; stranguria due to hematuria; hematuric strangury; blood stranguria; blood strangury
V012-N02785.definitions: Characterized by painful urination with blood in urine. Often occurs when damp heat or cold damp affects the bladder and enters the bloodstream to cause the blood to move recklessly. It may also occur when qi fails to control blood within the vessels or when qi deficiency results in blood stagnation.

V012-N02786.label: 血溢类病
V012-N02786.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02786.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02786.englishNames: category of hemorrhagic disease; hemorrhagic diseases; Bleeding disorders
V012-N02786.pinyinNames: xue yi lei bing; xuè yì lèi bìng
V012-N02786.definitions: A group of conditions caused by the failure of qi to hold blood within the vessels or hyperactive fire (due to yin deficiency) forces blood to move recklessly. They include haemoptysis, nosebleed, bloody stools or purpura.

V012-N02787.label: 血疝
V012-N02787.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02787.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02787.englishNames: hernia due to blood stasis; blood hernia; Haematocele
V012-N02787.definitions: Characterized by enlargement of the scrotum with a dark purple skin discolouration. Often occurs when traumatic injuries or surgery cause stagnation of qi and blood in the scrotum.

V012-N02788.label: 血疸
V012-N02788.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02788.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02788.definitions: A type of jaundice caused by medications, snake venom or malaria. It is characterized by jaundice, pale face and dizziness.

V012-N02789.label: 血痣
V012-N02789.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02789.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02789.definitions: Characterized by enlarged superficial blood vessels in bright red or dark red colour that do not blanch on applying pressure. They are of variable size and have a smooth surface. Naevi are often caused by prolonged liver qi stagnation or blood heat.

V012-N02790.label: 血痹
V012-N02790.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02790.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02790.definitions: Characterized by paroxysmal paleness, dark purple or reddish extremities (fingers/toes). Red patches, sensation of finger cold and/or numbness may be present. Often occurs when wind cold affects the blood vessels. Emotional stress may also disturb the harmony between Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N02791.label: 血瘤
V012-N02791.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02791.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02791.englishNames: angioma; hemangioma; blood tumor; Blood tumour
V012-N02791.definitions: Characterized by a rubbery, bright red or dark purple soft nodule of extra blood vessels in the skin in neonates. It is often caused by hyperactivity of fetal fire and disordered blood flow.

V012-N02792.label: 血臌
V012-N02792.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02792.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02792.englishNames: Blood tympanites; blood tympanites; Blood Tympanites
V012-N02792.definitions: A type of tympanites characterized by increased abdominal size, varicose abdominal veins, and scanty urine. Associated symptoms may include presence of vascular naevi on the face or chest, bleeding, vomiting of blood, and black stools. Often occurs when prolonged liver diseases, oedema, tuberculosis or abdominal masses cause qi, blood and water to stagnate in the abdomen.

V012-N02793.label: 血风劳
V012-N02793.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02793.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02793.englishNames: Postpartum exhaustion; consumptive disease due to blood coagulation
V012-N02793.pinyinNames: xue feng lao; xuè fēng láo
V012-N02793.definitions: Characterized by weight loss, tidal fever, flushed cheeks, night sweats and unproductive coughing. Often results from yin deficiency due to puerperal malnutrition coupled with contraction of external pathogens.

V012-N02794.label: 行痹
V012-N02794.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02794.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02794.aliases: 风痹； 风胜行痹； Wind impediment disorder； Moving impediment disorder； Migrating painful movement disorder； Migratory impediment； Wind hindrance disorder； Wind impediment
V012-N02794.englishNames: Wind impediment disorder; migratory bi; migratory arthralgia; migratory impediment; Migratory Arthralgia; moving impediment; arthralgia caused by wind pathogen; Migratory impediment
V012-N02794.definitions: Characterized by migratory joint or muscle pain. Often occurs when wind affects the joints or muscles.

V012-N02795.label: 褥疮
V012-N02795.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02795.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02795.aliases: 席疮； Bed sore disorder； Pressure ulcer
V012-N02795.englishNames: bedsore; bed sore; Bedsores; decubital ulcer; decubitus; Bed sore disorder
V012-N02795.definitions: Characterized by localized, superficial and persistent skin lesion or necrosis that usually occurs on the sacrum, elbows, ankles, or the hips. Often results from malnourishment of the skin due to obstructed flow of qi and blood, or an extended period of friction.

V012-N02796.label: 解颅
V012-N02796.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02796.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02796.englishNames: hydrocephalus due to nonclosure of fontanel; metopism; non-closure of fontanel; Hydrocephalus due to Nonclosure of Fontanel; ununited skull(hydrocephalus); ununited skull; Hydrocephalus
V012-N02796.pinyinNames: xie lu; jie lu; jiě lú
V012-N02796.definitions: Characterized by an enlargement of the head and retarded closure of the cranial sutures. Often results from congenital defect or retention of excess fluid within the skull due to accumulated toxic heat.

V012-N02797.label: 谷劳
V012-N02797.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02797.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02797.englishNames: Fatigue after eating food; cereal consumptive disease
V012-N02797.definitions: Characterized by drowsiness after eating food and heaviness of the limbs. Abdominal fullness, rapid hunger and a poor appetite may also be present. Often results from deficiency of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02798.label: 走马牙疳
V012-N02798.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02798.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02798.englishNames: acute gangrenous stomatitis; Acute ulcerative gingivitis; noma
V012-N02798.pinyinNames: zou ma ya gan; zǒu mǎ yá gān
V012-N02798.definitions: Characterized by sudden onset of gum or buccal mucosa necrosis, bleeding (dark, purple blood), and a foul breath. In severe cases, individuals may experience paresthesia of the lips, infection of the mandible, or loose teeth. Often results from contraction of seasonal pathogens or retained toxins of smallpox.

V012-N02799.label: 足底疔
V012-N02799.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02799.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02799.englishNames: plantar boil; ding of sole; Boils on the sole
V012-N02799.pinyinNames: zu di ding; zú dǐ dīng
V012-N02799.definitions: Characterized by boils on the sole of the foot.

V012-N02800.label: 足跟疽
V012-N02800.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02800.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02800.englishNames: tuberculosis of heel; heel carbuncle; Carbuncle of the heel
V012-N02800.pinyinNames: zu gen ju; zú gēn jū
V012-N02800.definitions: Characterized by tuberculosis of the heel. This condition often results from downward flow of damp heat to the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02801.label: 足跟痹
V012-N02801.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02801.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02801.pinyinNames: zú gēn bì; zu gen bi
V012-N02801.definitions: Characterized by heel pain or numbness that is aggravated on walking. It is often caused by wear and tear of bone, delayed healing of trauma, age-related deficiency of the liver and kidney, bone deformation or bone spurs.

V012-N02802.label: 转筋
V012-N02802.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02802.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02802.aliases: 腓腨转筋； （腓腨）转筋； Muscle cramp disorder； Muscle spasm disorder； 腓臑转筋
V012-N02802.englishNames: Muscle spasm disorder; muscle cramp; Leg cramp
V012-N02802.definitions: Characterized by a sudden, uncontrolled muscle contraction in the lower leg, coupled with stiffness and impaired flexion and extension. The symptoms can be alleviated by pulling, twisting or hot compression on the affected limb. Often results from ageing or exposure to cold.

V012-N02803.label: 软疖
V012-N02803.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02803.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02803.definitions: Soft boils caused by prolonged retention of toxic heat.

V012-N02804.label: 软脚瘟
V012-N02804.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02804.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02804.englishNames: Flaccid foot pestilence; pestilence with flaccidity of foot; pestilence with flaccid leg; pestilent flaccidity of foot
V012-N02804.pinyinNames: ruǎn jiǎo wēn; ruan jiao wen
V012-N02804.definitions: Characterized by double-peaked fever, headache, abdominal pain, diarrhoea, pain in the surface of the body, muscle flaccidity/weakness that develops into atrophy, unstable walking, and malformation of the affected limbs. It commonly affects children with pestilence. This condition often occurs when summer dampness affect the intestines and stomach, accumulates in the muscles and obstructs meridians. Alternatively, it may occur when heat damages yin fluids and results in malnourishment of the sinews.

V012-N02805.label: 过期不产
V012-N02805.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02805.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02805.pinyinNames: guo qi bu chan; guò qī bù chǎn
V012-N02805.definitions: Characterized by pregnancy extended more than two weeks beyond the expected date of delivery. Alternatively, it refers to pregnancy extended to 42 weeks calculated to the first day of the last menstrual period.

V012-N02806.label: 遗尿
V012-N02806.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02806.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02806.aliases: Enuresis disorder； 遗溺； 遗尿病
V012-N02806.definitions: Characterized by repeated inability to control urination during sleep. Contributing factors may include congenital weakness, kidney qi deficiency or internal retention of damp heat and stagnant blood. These factors may impair the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N02807.label: 遗精
V012-N02807.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02807.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02807.englishNames: Nocturnal emissions; seminal emission (disease); Involuntary ejaculation disorder; spermatorrhea; seminal emission; Spermatorrhea
V012-N02807.definitions: Characterized by more than four times of involuntary ejaculations experienced during sleep in one month. Often results from fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, mental over-exertion, downward flow of damp heat, kidney qi failing to secure the essence gate.

V012-N02808.label: 邪祟病
V012-N02808.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02808.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02808.englishNames: delusional disorders; Paranoia disorders; illusion due to evil disease
V012-N02808.pinyinNames: xié suì bìng; xie sui bing
V012-N02808.definitions: A group of paranoid or delusional symptoms that include seeing things or hearing voices that others do not, smelling something that does not exist, disorganized thinking and speech or hallucinations of sudden death. It is often caused by deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi coupled with pathogenic factors disturbing the mind or phlegm stasis misting the heart mind.

V012-N02809.label: 郁病
V012-N02809.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02809.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02809.aliases: 郁证； Depression disorder； Depressive disorder； Melancholy disorder
V012-N02809.englishNames: depression disease; Depression; depressive disease; depression
V012-N02809.definitions: Characterized by a low mood, lack of interest, fullness and stuffiness in the chest and subcostal region, and a poor appetite. In severe cases, a sense of guilt, dejection, paranoia, self-harm and tendency to commit suicide. Often occurs when long-term emotional disturbance or character defects causes qi to stagnate. It is first associated with the liver and later with the heart and spleen.

V012-N02810.label: 酒厥
V012-N02810.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02810.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02810.englishNames: alcoholic syncope; syncope due to immoderate drinking; alcoholism syncope; Alcoholic syncope
V012-N02810.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, vomiting, sleepiness, and panting. In severe cases, fainting and cold limbs may be present. It occurs when excessive alcohol drinking affects the heart mind.

V012-N02811.label: 酒癖
V012-N02811.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02811.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02811.englishNames: alcoholophilia; Alcohol-induced masses; alcohol-induced masses; alcohol-induced mass
V012-N02811.pinyinNames: jiu pi; jiu xian; jiǔ pǐ
V012-N02811.definitions: Characterized by pain and masses in the subcostal region, thirst, restlessness, and gradual weight loss. Red spots on the face, red palms, spider angioma and jaundice may be present. Contributing factors may include retention of alcoholic toxin, phlegm and stasis in the subcostal region due to prolonged alcohol drinking, damp heat accumulating in the spleen and stomach and disharmony between qi and blood.

V012-N02812.label: 酒臌
V012-N02812.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02812.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02812.definitions: A type of tympanites characterized by increased abdominal size, general oedema, varicose abdominal veins, weight loss, fatigue, a poor appetite, and gum bleeding or nosebleed. Often results from retention of stagnant qi, blood and phlegm dampness in the subcostal region due to prolonged or excessive alcohol drinking.

V012-N02813.label: 里喉痈
V012-N02813.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02813.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02813.englishNames: retropharyngeal abscess; retrophayngeal abscess; peritonsillar abscess; Posterior pharyngeal abscess
V012-N02813.pinyinNames: li hou yong; lǐ hóu yōng
V012-N02813.definitions: Characterized by intense sore throat, high fever, impaired neck movement and redness and swelling of the posterior pharyngeal wall. It is often caused by retention of pathogenic toxins in the base of the throat.

V012-N02814.label: 重舌
V012-N02814.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02814.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02814.englishNames: double tongue(sublingual swelling); sublingual cyst; double tongue; sublingual swollen tongue; Lotus tongue
V012-N02814.pinyinNames: chong she; zhong she; chóng shé
V012-N02814.definitions: Characterized by sublingual swelling in red or purple colour (resemble the shape of a lotus). It is often caused by heat accumulating in the heart and spleen, deficiency fire scorching the tongue or retention of damp heat and blood stasis in the tongue.

V012-N02815.label: 野屎风
V012-N02815.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02815.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02815.englishNames: fecal contact dermatosis; hookworm dermatitis; Ancylostoma dermatitis
V012-N02815.pinyinNames: ye shi feng; yě shǐ fēng
V012-N02815.definitions: A condition caused by exposure to the infestation of larvae of hookworms, especially Ancylostoma braziliense, when they penetrate the skin of their host. Individuals may experience extremely itchy skin wheals or rashes between the toes or fingers that resolve spontaneously after a couple of days.

V012-N02816.label: 针眼
V012-N02816.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02816.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02816.aliases: 麦粒肿； 土疳； 偷针眼； Stye
V012-N02816.definitions: Characterized by red, tender bump at the edge of the eyelid that may contain pus formation and appear in the shape and size of a wheat grain. Often results from invasion of wind heat, heat accumulating in the spleen and stomach, or retained toxic heat coupled with contraction of wind.

V012-N02817.label: 钩虫病
V012-N02817.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02817.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02817.aliases: 小儿钩虫病； 黄胖病； 黄肿病； Paediatric ancylostomiasis
V012-N02817.pinyinNames: gou chong bing; gōu chóng bìng
V012-N02817.definitions: A parasitic condition caused by infection with Ancylostoma sp. hookworms. It is characterized by fast hunger after eating food, fatigue, sallow skin, oedema and presence of hookworm larvae in the stools. Alternatively, anaemia or abnormal food preference may be present. In children, this condition may cause poor growth and development. The hookworms enter the body through contact with contaminated faeces or soil, remain in the small intestine and bind with damp heat to consume qi and blood.

V012-N02818.label: 锁喉痈
V012-N02818.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02818.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02818.englishNames: throat-locking cellulitis; throat-locking carbuncle; Abscess in the tonsil; throatblocking carbuncle; throat-blocking carbuncle; cellulitis of floor of mouth
V012-N02818.pinyinNames: suo hou yong; suǒ hóu yōng
V012-N02818.definitions: Characterized by sudden, fulminant abscess formation in the laryngeal prominence. In severe cases, acute laryngeal obstruction may occur. It is often caused by exposure to wind warmth or toxin retention of carbuncles/ulcers in the local area.

V012-N02819.label: 闭经
V012-N02819.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02819.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02819.aliases: 经闭； 避年居经； Amenorrhoea disorder； No periods disorder
V012-N02819.definitions: An absence of menarche by the age of 16 with normal secondary sexual characteristics, absence of menarche or secondary sexual characteristics by the age of 14, an abnormal stoppage of the menses for more than six months after an established menstruation, or a stoppage of menses for three consecutive cycles. Contributing factors may include deficiency of the liver and kidney, deficiency of qi and blood, blood dryness due to yin deficiency, tuberculosis, qi stagnation and blood stasis, or phlegm dampness. These factors may affect the normal functioning of the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02820.label: 阳水
V012-N02820.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02820.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02820.definitions: Characterized by an acute onset of pitting swelling of the face and eyelid. Associated symptoms may include fever and aversion to wind, with no body weakness. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic factors block the skin pores, obstruct the flow of qi and result in water dampness.

V012-N02821.label: 阳痿
V012-N02821.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02821.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02821.definitions: Characterized by the inability to develop or maintain an erection of the penis during sexual activity. Often results from decline of the vital gate, deficiency of the liver and kidney, fear/fright or depression.

V012-N02822.label: 阳黄病
V012-N02822.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02822.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02822.pinyinNames: yang huang bing; yáng huáng bìng
V012-N02822.definitions: An acute condition characterized by bright yellow discolouration of the skin and sclera, dark, tea-like urine, and a decreased appetite. Associated symptoms may include fever, thirst, nausea, vomiting, distending pain in the subcostal region and white/light-coloured stools. Contributing factors may include external contraction of epidemic damp heat from improper diet, alcohol drinking, or stones/lumps forcing the bile to flow superficially.

V012-N02823.label: 阴冷
V012-N02823.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02823.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02823.aliases: 阴寒； Coldness of Genitalis； Cold uterus
V012-N02823.englishNames: pudendal coldness; vulval coldness; Coldness of External Genitals; Uterine cold
V012-N02823.definitions: Characterized by a subjective feeling of cold in the external genitalia. Individuals may also have low sexual desire. Often results from retained cold qi in the uterus due to kidney deficiency.

V012-N02824.label: 阴吹
V012-N02824.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02824.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02824.englishNames: vaginal flatus; flatus vaginalis; Vaginal flatus disorder; Vaginal flatulence
V012-N02824.pinyinNames: yin chui; yin chu; yīn chuī
V012-N02824.definitions: Characterized by an emission or expulsion of air from the vagina. The sound is somewhat comparable to flatulence from the anus. Often results from qi disorder of the fu organs or qi stagnation and phlegm affecting the uterus.

V012-N02825.label: 阴挺
V012-N02825.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02825.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02825.aliases: 子宫脱垂； Uterine prolapse； 阴脱
V012-N02825.englishNames: prolapse of uterus; metroptosis; uterine prolapse; Prolapsed uterus; prolapse of the uterus; uterine or vaginal prolapse
V012-N02825.definitions: Characterized by uterus slipping out of place, or sometimes protruding down through the vagina. Often results from qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N02826.label: 阴水
V012-N02826.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02826.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02826.definitions: Characterized by a gradual onset of facial puffiness, and pitting foot or leg swelling. Often occurs when water dampness (due to deficiency of the five zang organs) flow over the surface of the body.

V012-N02827.label: 阴疮
V012-N02827.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02827.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02827.aliases: 阴肿； 阴蚀； 阴茧； Vulvar swelling； Vulvar erosion
V012-N02827.englishNames: sore of vulvae; vulval sore; pudendal sore; Vulvar ulceration
V012-N02827.definitions: Characterized by painful, swollen ulcerations on the female external genital region. In severe cases, individuals may experience pus discharge or small, elevated lumps. Often occurs when toxic heat or cold dampness remains in the vulva.

V012-N02828.label: 阴癣
V012-N02828.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02828.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02828.englishNames: inguinal tinea; tinea inguinalis; Tinea cruris
V012-N02828.definitions: Characterized by itchy skin papules, blisters and crusting on the groin, buttocks and medial aspects of the thigh. Often results from damp heat affecting the groin area.

V012-N02829.label: 阴黄病
V012-N02829.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02829.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02829.pinyinNames: yin huang bing; yīn huáng bìng
V012-N02829.definitions: A chronic condition characterized by dim yellow discolouration of the skin and sclera, dull pain in the subcostal region, lassitude, a poor appetite, abdominal distension or loose stools. Contributing factors may include persistent yang jaundice damaging anti-pathogenic qi, dampness retention or cold dampness affecting the spleen.

V012-N02830.label: 附骨疽
V012-N02830.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02830.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02830.aliases: 多骨疽； 咬骨疽； 朽骨疽
V012-N02830.englishNames: suppurative osteomyelitis; bone-attaching abscess; bone-attaching carbuncle(suppurative osteomyelitis); Suppurative osteomyelitis; Suppurative Osteomyelitis
V012-N02830.pinyinNames: fu gu ju; fù gǔ jū
V012-N02830.definitions: Characterized by pain in the long bones of the arms and legs with diffuse swelling and pus discharge that may form fistulas or damage the tendons and bones. Often results from deeply located toxins damaging the tendons or bones or localized blood stagnation.

V012-N02831.label: 附骨痰
V012-N02831.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02831.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02831.englishNames: bone-attaching phlegm; tuberculosis of hip joint; lumbar vertebrae phlegm; Bone phlegm; phlegmatic osteomyelitis
V012-N02831.pinyinNames: fu gu tan; fù gǔ tán
V012-N02831.definitions: Characterized by hip joint swelling and pain, difficulty walking or limping, discharge of thin, clear pus or pus that resembles rotten cotton threads, formation of a sinus tract, and delayed healing. Often occurs when Mycobacterium tuberculosis affects the hip joint.

V012-N02832.label: 难产
V012-N02832.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02832.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02832.englishNames: difficult delivery; Difficult delivery; dystocia; Dystocia
V012-N02832.definitions: A condition that a baby has difficulty passing through the birth canal in a vaginal delivery. Contributing factors may include pelvic abnormalities, large baby, abnormal position and presentation of the fetus, or uterine/vulvar diseases.

V012-N02833.label: 雀目
V012-N02833.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02833.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02833.aliases: 肝虚雀目； Night blindness disorder； Night blindness due to liver deficiency； 雀盲
V012-N02833.englishNames: sparrow vision (night blindness); Night blindness; night blindness
V012-N02833.definitions: Characterized by difficult or impossible vision/sight in relatively low light. Individuals may experience sensitivity to light. Often results from insufficient generation of qi and blood (due to spleen deficiency) or liver blood failing to nourish the eyes. It also occurs at the early stage of keratomalacia.

V012-N02834.label: 雷头风
V012-N02834.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02834.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02834.englishNames: thunder head wind; thunder headache; thunder-like headache; Thunder head wind
V012-N02834.pinyinNames: lei tou feng; léi tóu fēng
V012-N02834.definitions: Characterized by severe, sudden onset of headache like a clap of thunder and a storm raging in your head. Associated symptoms may include chills, fever and referred eye pain. Often occurs when wind fire carries phlegm or when phlegm heat generates wind to affect the head.

V012-N02835.label: 震颤
V012-N02835.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02835.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02835.definitions: Involuntary shaking of the hand, feet or head. Contributing factors may include malnourishment of the muscles or sinews due to insufficiency of liver blood and kidney essence and deficiency of qi and yin, internal stirring of deficiency wind, or wind phlegm obstructing the meridians. It is often seen in Parkinson’s disease, brain atrophy, or qi collapse.

V012-N02836.label: 青盲
V012-N02836.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02836.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02836.englishNames: blue blindness; optic atrophy; Blue Blindness; bluish blindness; Bluish blindness; bluish blindness (optic atrophy)
V012-N02836.definitions: A condition characterized by gradually progressive visual impairment to complete vision loss. Often results from deficiency of liver blood and kidney essence, yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney, liver qi stagnation, eye injuries or eye tumour.

V012-N02837.label: 青筋腿
V012-N02837.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02837.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02837.englishNames: varicose vein of leg; legs with varicosis; Varicose veins of the leg
V012-N02837.pinyinNames: qing jin tui; qīng jīn tuǐ
V012-N02837.definitions: Characterized by enlarged and twisted (like an earthworm) veins when standing. Often results from standing for an extended period of time, blood stasis due to qi deficiency, or stagnation of qi and blood due to external contraction of cold dampness.

V012-N02838.label: 青风内障
V012-N02838.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02838.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02838.englishNames: bluish wind glaucoma(angle-opening glaucoma); blue wind glaucoma; blu ish glaucoma; bluish wind glaucoma; Bluish glaucoma
V012-N02838.pinyinNames: qing feng nei zhang; qīng fēng nèi zhàng
V012-N02838.definitions: Characterized by mild dilation of the pupil with a bluish colour, increased pressure in the eye, gradual visual field constriction and, eventually vision loss. Often results from liver qi stagnation, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency or ascending of phlegm fire.

V012-N02839.label: 面风痛
V012-N02839.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02839.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02839.pinyinNames: mian feng tong; miàn fēng tòng
V012-N02839.definitions: Characterized by episodes of severe, sudden pain or spasm in one side of the face, often accompanied by facial spasm. Often occurs when wind cold or wind heat attacks the meridians on the face or phlegm stasis affects the meridians.

V012-N02840.label: 鞭虫病
V012-N02840.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02840.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02841.label: 项痹
V012-N02841.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02841.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02841.definitions: Characterized by neck rigidity, stiffness and impaired movement, and soreness and pain in the neck, shoulder and arm. Associated symptoms may include dizziness, headache, arm numbness, muscular atrophy, leg weakness, unstable walking, disturbed bladder/bowel functions and paralysis. Often occurs when tendon/bone injury or degenerative changes irritate or compress the spinal cord, nerves and vessels. Contributing factors include ageing, chronic strain/injury, and contraction of external pathogenic factors.

V012-N02842.label: 顽痹
V012-N02842.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02842.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02842.definitions: Characterized by recurrent joint pain, numbness, deformity and impaired movement that aggravate upon exposure to wind cold. Contributing factors may include a history of wind-cold-dampness bi-impediment, over-exertion, or age-related malnourishment of the joints.

V012-N02843.label: 顿咳
V012-N02843.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02843.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02843.aliases: 百日咳； 小儿顿嗽； 天哮； 鹭鸶咳； Pertussis
V012-N02843.pinyinNames: dùn ké; dong ke; dun ke; du ke
V012-N02843.definitions: A highly contagious disease affecting children. Initially, symptoms are typically similar to those of a common cold. This is then followed by a paroxysmal cough with a high-pitched whoop sound or gasp. Vomiting of saliva or phlegm may also occur. Often occurs when the bacterium Bordetella pertussis attacks the lung, obstructs the airway and causes lung qi to ascend.

V012-N02844.label: 颅脑类病
V012-N02844.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02844.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02844.englishNames: Brain diseases; cranial diseases; category of cranial disease
V012-N02844.pinyinNames: lú nǎo lèi bìng; lu nao lei bing
V012-N02844.definitions: Any disorders involving the head or brain due to invasion of exogenous pathogens, ascending of wind yang, ascending of qi and blood, wind phlegm disturbing the mind or stagnant blood obstructing the brain collaterals.

V012-N02845.label: 颈痈
V012-N02845.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02845.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02845.englishNames: cervical carbuncle; acute pyogenic lymphadenitis of neck; cervical abscess; Cervical abscess
V012-N02845.definitions: Characterized by warm, swollen and painful abscesses on both sides of the neck with clear boundaries and no skin colour changes. Often results from contraction of wind warmth and retention of toxic phlegm in the skin.

V012-N02846.label: 颌下痈
V012-N02846.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02846.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02846.pinyinNames: ge xia yong; he xia yong; hé xià yōng
V012-N02846.definitions: Characterized by intense sore throat, fever, difficulty swallowing, red, swollen jaw and the tonsil and pharyngeal wall being pulled to the opposite side. It is often caused by pathogenic toxin affecting the submandibular region.

V012-N02847.label: 颜面部疔疮
V012-N02847.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02847.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02847.englishNames: facial boil and sore; deep-rooted facial boil; Facial boils
V012-N02847.pinyinNames: yan mian bu ding chuang; yán miàn bù dīng chuāng
V012-N02847.definitions: Characterized by rapid occurrence of solid, deep-rooted boils on the face that are shaped like nails and the same size as millets. They often result from exposure to toxic fire heat, insect bites, skin lesion or localized stagnation of qi and blood. Squeezing a boil may cause serious complications.

V012-N02848.label: 颤病
V012-N02848.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02848.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02848.aliases: 脑风； 颤症； Parkinson’s disease
V012-N02848.definitions: Characterized by tremor of hands and feet, involuntary shaking of the head or twitching of the mouth and eyes. Contributing factors may include medical conditions, poisoning, electrical shock, or age-related deficiency of essence and blood. It may also occur when phlegm or stagnant blood obstructs the brain collaterals or meridian qi.

V012-N02849.label: 风厥
V012-N02849.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02849.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02849.definitions: Characterized by chest tightness, palpitations, difficulty breathing, cold limbs, a feeble pulse, skin itching, low blood pressure and mental cloudiness. Often results from wind disturbing or blocking qi and blood or yang qi collapse.

V012-N02850.label: 风寒湿痹
V012-N02850.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02850.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02850.englishNames: wind-cold-dampness bi; wind-cold-dampness arthralgia; Wind-cold-dampness impediment; wind-cold-damp bi; arthralgia caused by wind-cold-damp pathogens; wind-cold-dampness impediment; windcolddampness arthralgia
V012-N02850.pinyinNames: feng han shi bi; fēng hán shī bì
V012-N02850.definitions: Characterized by migratory muscle or joint soreness, heaviness and pain. Often occurs when wind, cold and dampness affect the joints and muscles and causes qi and blood to stagnate.

V012-N02851.label: 风水
V012-N02851.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02851.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02851.englishNames: wind edema; Wind edema disorder; Wind oedema
V012-N02851.definitions: Characterized by a sudden fever, aversion to wind, sore, swollen throat, joint pain, and swelling of the face and eyelid. Over time, individuals may experience generalized oedema. Associated signs and symptoms may include shortness of breath, scanty urine, proteinuria and elevated blood pressure. If often occurs when pathogenic wind affects the lung’s functions in dispersing and descending qi and regulating water passage.

V012-N02852.label: 风温病
V012-N02852.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02852.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02852.aliases: 风瘟； 风瘟病； 风温； 风温肺热病； Wind febrile disorders； Wind pestilence with lung heat
V012-N02852.pinyinNames: feng wen bing; fēng wēn bìng
V012-N02852.definitions: A type of pestilence characterized by a sudden fever, mild aversion to wind cold, cough with yellow phlegm or haemoptysis, sore throat, followed by a persistent high-grade fever, sweating, excessive thirst, chest tightness and panting. In severe cases, unconsciousness, delirium and convulsions may be present. It is often seen in spring and winter. Contributing factors include pathogenic wind warmth attacking the Wei-defence, heat accumulating in the lung or heat transmitting to the pericardium.

V012-N02853.label: 风疹
V012-N02853.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02853.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02853.aliases: 风痧； 小儿风痧； 风痧(病)
V012-N02853.definitions: Characterized by fever, cough and pink rashes (resembling fine sand). The rashes start on the face and spread down to the trunk, arms and legs. The rashes may combine into patches. They come and go and do not cause skin peeling or discolouration. Alternatively, swollen lymph node behind the ear may be present. This condition often occurs when epidemic wind heat or virus affects the lung and the surface of the body.

V012-N02854.label: 风痱
V012-N02854.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02854.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02854.aliases: 中风后遗症； 喑痱； Post-stroke disability
V012-N02854.definitions: Characterized by post-stroke tongue stiffness and disuse muscle atrophy. Contributing factors may include wind phlegm disturbing the heart mind, stagnant blood obstructing the meridians, or deficiency of essence and marrow.

V012-N02855.label: 风眩
V012-N02855.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02855.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02855.englishNames: wind vertigo; wind dizziness; Wind dizziness
V012-N02855.definitions: Characterized by vertigo, headache, elevated blood pressure and a wiry pulse. Often results from yang hyperactivity due to liver/kidney yin deficiency, which further causes wind yang to ascend to disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02856.label: 飞扬喉
V012-N02856.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02856.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02856.englishNames: hematoma of uvula; hematoma of upper palate; Uvula haematoma
V012-N02856.pinyinNames: fei yang hou; fēi yáng hóu
V012-N02856.definitions: Characterized by sudden blood blisters in the palate that have a purple colour, thin wall and tend to rupture. It is often caused by internal heat or rupture to the oral cavity during eating.

V012-N02857.label: 食亦
V012-N02857.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02857.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02857.englishNames: weight loss depsite excessive appetite; emaciation due to improper food; Excessive hunger
V012-N02857.definitions: Characterized by increased appetite, rapid hunger after eating food, weight loss, lassitude and dry stools. Contributing factors may include an improper diet, over ingestion of oily, sweet food, or excessive alcohol drinking. These factors may cause damp heat to accumulate in the stomach, intestines and gallbladder, which in turn affects the normal conduction of the gallbladder and stomach.

V012-N02858.label: 食厥
V012-N02858.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02858.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02858.englishNames: crapulent syncope; Over-eating-induced syncope; Crapulent Syncope
V012-N02858.definitions: Characterized by fainting after over-eating, abdominal distension and fullness, belching and a slippery, forceful pulse. Often results from binge eating. Alternatively, it may occur when wind cold or anger triggers the stagnant food in the spleen and stomach and causes qi to ascend to disturb the heart mind.

V012-N02859.label: 食毒类病
V012-N02859.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02859.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02859.englishNames: food poisoning; food poisoning diseases; Food poisoning
V012-N02859.pinyinNames: shi er lei bing; shi dong lei bing; shí dú lèi bìng
V012-N02859.definitions: A group of disorders caused by ingestion of toxic/contaminated food or inappropriately processed food.

V012-N02860.label: 食瘕
V012-N02860.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02860.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02860.englishNames: abdominal mass due to improper diet; Stomach masses due to food retention; abdominal mass due to improper food
V012-N02860.pinyinNames: shí jiǎ; shi jia; shi xia
V012-N02860.definitions: Characterized by palpable lumps in the gastric region, abdominal pain and vomiting. Often results from food retention and failure of stomach qi to descend. Contributing factors may include general weakness, over-exertion, abdominal surgery, binge eating or abdominal masses.

V012-N02861.label: 食积
V012-N02861.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02861.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02861.englishNames: food dyspepsia; food accumulation; Food retention disorder; Spleen system disorders; dyspepsia; Food stagnation
V012-N02861.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension and fullness, belching, acid reflux, decreased appetite, and strong-odoured stools. Associated symptoms may include abdominal pain, crying and irregular bowel movements. Often occurs when improper diet damages the spleen and stomach and food is retained in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02862.label: 食管痹
V012-N02862.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02862.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02862.pinyinNames: shi guan bi; shí guǎn bì
V012-N02862.definitions: Characterized by intermittent dysphagia and vomiting. Contributing factors may include improper diet, emotional disturbance or damage to the oesophagus. These factors cause qi stagnation and ascending of stomach qi.

V012-N02863.label: 食管瘅
V012-N02863.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02863.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02863.pinyinNames: shi guan dan; shí guǎn dān
V012-N02863.definitions: Characterized by chest pain behind the sternum with a burning sensation, stomach discomfort and acid reflux. Contributing factors may include exposure to pathogenic factors/ irritating/drugs, internal heat or a prolonged ascending of rebellious stomach qi.

V012-N02864.label: 飧泄
V012-N02864.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02864.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02864.englishNames: diarrhea with undigested food; Diarrhoea with undigested food
V012-N02864.definitions: A type of diarrhoea characterized by repeated, food-related diarrhoea that contains undigested food. Contributing factors may include yang qi deficiency of the spleen and stomach. an improper diet or external wind, cold, damp or heat affecting the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02865.label: 饥厥
V012-N02865.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02865.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02865.definitions: Characterized by sudden palpitations, weakness, cold sweats, a pale face and, in severe cases, sudden fainting may be present. Often results from failure of Ying nutrients to nourish the brain due to inability to eat food or hunger.

V012-N02866.label: 饥疝
V012-N02866.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02866.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02866.definitions: Characterized by rapid hunger after eating food, followed by stomach ache that alleviates with eating food or warmth. Often results from cold qi retention in the stomach and heart meridians due to constitutional yang deficiency or ingestion of raw, cold food.

V012-N02867.label: 骨折病
V012-N02867.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02867.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02867.englishNames: fracture diseases; Fracture; category of fracture
V012-N02867.pinyinNames: gu zhe bing; gǔ zhé bìng
V012-N02867.definitions: A condition in which there is damage in the continuity of the bone due to external force impact or stress.

V012-N02868.label: 骨痹
V012-N02868.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02868.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02868.englishNames: bone bi; Bone impediment; bone bidisease; bone impediment
V012-N02868.definitions: Characterized by bone pain, joint stiffness, deformity or impaired movement, and limb numbness and weakness. In severe cases, kyphosis of the lumbar spine, impaired extension of the leg, leg weakness or lower back pain may be present. Often results from retention of wind, cold and dampness.
