It may also result from age-related malnourishment of the bones.

V012-N02869.label: 骨痿
V012-N02869.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02869.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02869.englishNames: bone wilting; Bone flaccidity; bone flaccidity
V012-N02869.definitions: Characterized by low back soreness, difficulty in maintaining a standing position, bowed legs, muscular atrophy in the lower legs, and inability to walk. A dark complexion and withered gum may also be present. It is often caused by pathogenic heat damaging yin, long-term over-exertion, and hyperactivity of fire due to kidney essence deficiency.

V012-N02870.label: 骨蚀
V012-N02870.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02870.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02870.definitions: Characterized by bone pain, muscular atrophy, limping and shortening of the affected leg without suppuration. It is often caused by pathogenic factors penetrating the bone or traumatic injuries to sinews or bones.

V012-N02871.label: 骨错缝
V012-N02871.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02871.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02871.englishNames: dislocation of bone; joint subluxation; Bone/joint dislocation/subluxation; mild malposition of bone and joint; bone dislocation
V012-N02871.pinyinNames: gu cuo feng; gǔ cuò fèng
V012-N02871.definitions: A condition characterized by local pain, impaired movement and palpable enhancement of motion end feel, decreased relaxation, and tenderness. Often occurs when indirect or chronic force causes the bones/joints to subluxate.

V012-N02872.label: 髓劳
V012-N02872.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02872.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02872.definitions: Characterized by haemorrhage, anaemia and deficiency of whole blood cells. Contributing factors may include heredity, postnatal malnutrition or exposure to drugs or certain chemicals.

V012-N02873.label: 高风内障
V012-N02873.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02873.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02873.englishNames: Retinopathy pigmentosa; retinopathy pigmentosa; high-wind internal visual obstruction (pigmentary retinopathy)
V012-N02873.pinyinNames: gāo fēng nèi zhàng; gao feng nei zhang
V012-N02873.definitions: A progressive degenerative eye condition characterized by night blindness, gradual visual field constriction, and eventual blindness. Often results from congenital deficiency or insufficiency of liver blood and kidney essence.

V012-N02874.label: 鱼蟹类中毒
V012-N02874.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02874.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02874.englishNames: fish and crab poisoning; Fish and crab poisoning
V012-N02874.pinyinNames: yu xie lei zhong er; yu xie lei zhong dong; yú xiè lèi zhòng dú
V012-N02874.definitions: Food poisoning caused by ingestion of contaminated or inappropriately cooked fish or crab. It is characterized by sudden cardiac/abdominal tightness and fullness, restlessness, vomiting, abdominal pain, and diarrhoea. Associated symptoms include dizziness, facial puffiness, red skin rashes and itching. In severe cases, palpitations, shortness of breath, rapid breathing or death may occur.

V012-N02875.label: 鸡眼
V012-N02875.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02875.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02875.englishNames: Chicken eye-shaped skin bump; corn; clavus
V012-N02875.definitions: Characterized by thick, hardened and elevated skin bump that develop in response to repetitive friction and pressure. Corns can be painful when pressed and often develop on the feet and toes (occasionally on the hands and fingers).

V012-N02876.label: 鹅掌风
V012-N02876.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02876.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02876.englishNames: tinea manuum; Gooseweb Wind; goose-web wind; Tinea manuum; goose-web wind(tinea manuum); Hand dampness itch disorder; goose web wind; gooseweb wind
V012-N02876.pinyinNames: e zhang feng; dong zhang feng; é zhǎng fēng; er zhang feng
V012-N02876.definitions: Characterized by blisters on the palm with scaling, roughness, thickening and cracking. Often results from external contraction of damp heat or get from others who have the infection.

V012-N02877.label: 鹤膝风
V012-N02877.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02877.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02877.englishNames: arthrosis like crane knee; Crane Like Arthropathy; crane knee phlegm; Tuberculosis of the knee joint (crane’s-knee wind)
V012-N02877.pinyinNames: he xi feng; hè xī fēng
V012-N02877.definitions: Characterized by swelling of the knee joint and the wasting of quadriceps muscle, resembling a crane’s knee.

V012-N02878.label: 麻疹
V012-N02878.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02878.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02878.definitions: A condition characterized by fever, cough, runny nose, watery, inflamed eyes, presence of small white spots inside the mouth, and generalized maculopapular rash, followed by desquamation and pigmentation. In severe cases, complications involving the lung, throat, heart and liver may be present, which are often seen in infants. Often results from toxin (measles virus) affects the lung and spleen and spreads to the bloodstream and skin.

V012-N02879.label: 麻风病
V012-N02879.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02879.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02879.pinyinNames: ma feng bing; má fēng bìng
V012-N02879.definitions: A long-term infectious disease characterized by generalized numbness and skin lesions with purple-red patches that resemble snake skin. Alternatively, nodules or raised bumps may be present. Often results from close contact with patients infected with leprosy. The bacterium Mycobacterium leprae enters the bloodstream and damages the skin, meridians and the five zang organs.

V012-N02880.label: 黄水疮
V012-N02880.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02880.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02880.aliases: 浸淫疮； 滴脓疮； 脓窝疮； Impetigo disorder； 脓疱疮； Impetigo
V012-N02880.englishNames: Yellow-water sore; impetigo; Impetigo disorder; yellow-water sore（impetigo); yellow fluid ulcers; Yellow Fluid Ulcers
V012-N02880.pinyinNames: huáng shuǐ chuāng; huang shui chuang
V012-N02880.definitions: Characterized by itchy or painful small vesicles that may develop into scab-like, yellowish crust. Often occurs when exogenous summer heat and dampness obstructs the flow of qi and fumigates the skin.

V012-N02881.label: 黄汗
V012-N02881.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02881.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02881.englishNames: yellowish sweating; yellow sweat; Yellow sweating
V012-N02881.definitions: Characterized by yellow-coloured sweating that contaminates cloth. It is often caused by disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence or external contraction of damp heat.

V012-N02882.label: 黄瓜痈
V012-N02882.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02882.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02882.englishNames: paravertebral abscess; cucumber-like carbuncle; Paravertebral abscess
V012-N02882.pinyinNames: huang gua yong; huáng guā yōng
V012-N02882.definitions: Characterized by abscess formation on bilateral sides of the spine. Often results from spleen fire and toxin accumulation.

V012-N02883.label: 黄疸病
V012-N02883.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02883.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02883.pinyinNames: huang dan bing; huáng dǎn bìng
V012-N02883.definitions: Characterized by yellow discolouration of the skin, sclera and urine. Contributing factors may include external contraction of epidemic damp heat, alcohol-related internal build up of damp heat, cold dampness affecting the spleen, or stones/lumps/stasis forcing the bile to flow superficially.

V012-N02884.label: 黄风内障
V012-N02884.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02884.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02884.pinyinNames: huang feng nei zhang; huáng fēng nèi zhàng
V012-N02884.definitions: Characterized by dilation of the pupil with a pale, yellowish colour, and vision loss. Often results from ascending of liver fire that carries phlegm. It may also occur as a result of untreated greenish glaucoma, bluish glaucoma or black glaucoma.

V012-N02885.label: 黑疸
V012-N02885.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02885.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02885.englishNames: black jaundice; Darkening of the skin
V012-N02885.definitions: Characterized by fatigue, weight loss, dark sallow discolouration of the skin and face, darkening of the forehead, black stools, itchy skin, and abdominal distension (as if there is water inside). Contributing factors may include deficiency of the liver and kidney due to chronic jaundice, internal phlegm, sexual indulgence, tuberculosis or tumour that consumes essential qi and obstructs blood vessels.

V012-N02886.label: 黑风内障
V012-N02886.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02886.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02886.englishNames: Black glaucoma; dark wind glaucoma; black wind glaucoma
V012-N02886.pinyinNames: hēi fēng nèi zhàng; hei feng nei zhang
V012-N02886.definitions: Characterized by headache, eye distension and pain, blurred vision, dilated pupil with a black colour, and visual impairment. Often results from liver wind that carries phlegm or disharmony between qi and blood due to kidney deficiency.

V012-N02887.label: 鼠乳
V012-N02887.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02887.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02887.englishNames: mouse nipple; molluscum  contagiosum; mouse nipple(molluscum contagiosum); rat-nipple wart; water wart; molluscum contagiosum; Infectious warts
V012-N02887.definitions: Characterized by small (millet/mung bean-sized), raised warts that commonly affect the torso and limbs. These warts have a black centre with wax-like lustre and may discharge white, milky fluids. They are often caused by wind affecting the skin, coupled with exposure to toxins.

V012-N02888.label: 鼠疫
V012-N02888.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02888.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02888.definitions: A fulminating infectious disease characterized by a sudden onset, high fever, bleeding, and swelling of lymph nodes. Alternatively, coughing with phlegm or haemoptysis may be present. In severe cases, patients may faint or die. This condition occurs when the bacterium Yersinia pestis enters the lung or bloodstream following flea bites or handling an infected animal.

V012-N02889.label: 鼻槁
V012-N02889.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02889.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02889.englishNames: Rhinitis sicca; withered nose; atrophic rhinitis
V012-N02889.definitions: Characterized by dryness inside the nose, turbinate hypertrophy, atrophy of the mucous membrane, foul-smelling crusts, and decreased sensitivity to smell. Often results from malnourishment of the nose due to deficiency of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02890.label: 鼻渊
V012-N02890.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02890.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02890.aliases: Thick Rhinorrhea； 脑漏； Nasal sinusitis disorder
V012-N02890.englishNames: nasal sinusitis; Acute and Chronic Sinusitis; sinusitis; acute and chronic sinusitis; Nasal sinusitis disorder; Profuse nasal discharge
V012-N02890.definitions: Characterized by thick nasal mucus, nasal obstruction, decreased sensitivity to smell. Individuals may also experience pus discharge, dizziness and head distension. If often results from contraction of exogenous pathogens, retained heat in the zang–fu organs, or deficiency of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N02891.label: 鼻疳
V012-N02891.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02891.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02891.englishNames: nasal vestibulitis; nasal malnutrition; lingering nasal-sore; Nasal sore; nasal gan disease; nasal vestibulitits and eczema
V012-N02891.definitions: Characterized by recurrent red, swollen, itchy and painful sores on the nose. Often occurs when wind, heat or dampness steam the nasal cavity.

V012-N02892.label: 鼻病类
V012-N02892.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02892.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02892.definitions: A group of nasal disorders caused by wind, cold, dampness or heat affecting the nose, accumulation of damp heat and turbid phlegm, ascending of toxic fire, heat accumulated in the zang–fu organs, malnourishment of the nose due to deficiency of qi and blood, nasal trauma, or foreign body trapped in the nasal cavity.

V012-N02893.label: 鼻窦痰包
V012-N02893.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02893.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02893.englishNames: phlegmatic mass in nasal sinus; phlegmatic mass of nasal sinus; Sinusitis
V012-N02893.pinyinNames: bi dou tan bao; bí dòu tán bāo
V012-N02893.definitions: Characterized by stuffy nose, poor sense of smell, drowsiness, and thick nasal discharge that is usually green in colour (like egg white). Alternatively, local distension, pain or discomfort may be present. It is often caused by turbid phlegm and damp heat affecting the head, face and nose.

V012-N02894.label: 鼻血瘤
V012-N02894.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02894.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02894.englishNames: Nasal cavity haemangioma; hematoma of nose; nasal blood tumor
V012-N02894.pinyinNames: bí xuè liú; bi xue liu
V012-N02894.definitions: Characterized by red, flat haemangioma inside the nasal cavity that may cause recurrent bleeding from the nose. It can be congenital or acquired through damage to the nasal cavity.

V012-N02895.label: 鼻衄
V012-N02895.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02895.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02895.definitions: The occurrence of bleeding from the nose. Contributing factors may include nasal problems, trauma, ascending of fire heat along the lung, stomach and liver meridians, or spleen deficiency. It is often seen in nasal trauma, bleeding disorders, measles, erysipelas, scarlet fever, flu, dizziness, aplastic anaemia, tympanites or severe headache due to ascending of kidney qi.

V012-N02896.label: 鼻鼽
V012-N02896.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02896.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02896.aliases: Allergic rhinitis disorder； 鼽嚏； Mucous Snivel
V012-N02896.englishNames: atrophic rhinitis; allergic rhinitis; Allergic rhinitis; Allergic Rhinitis; Allergic rhinitis disorder
V012-N02896.definitions: Characterized by sudden or recurrent nasal itching, sneezing, and runny or stuffy nose. Often results from genetic factors, deficiency of the zang–fu organs coupled with contraction of exogenous pathogens, or exposure to pollen, dust or polluted air.

V012-N02897.label: 齿槽风
V012-N02897.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02897.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02897.aliases: 穿腮毒； 牙槽风； 骨槽风； Osteomyelitis of the maxilla
V012-N02897.englishNames: maxillary osteomyelitis; jaw wind; Dental abscess
V012-N02897.pinyinNames: chi cao feng; chǐ cáo fēng
V012-N02897.definitions: Characterized by pain of the alveolar bone, coupled with pus formation and discharge of dead bones. Often results from exuberant phlegm fire.

V012-N02898.label: 齿衄
V012-N02898.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02898.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02898.englishNames: gum bleeding; gingival hemorrhage; Gum bleeding
V012-N02898.definitions: The occurrence of bleeding from the gum. Contributing factors may include heat accumulating in the stomach meridian, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency, or deficiency of the heart and spleen. It is often seen in periodontal atrophy, purpura, aplastic anaemia, liver masses, bleeding disorders, or epidemic haemorrhagic fever.

V012-N02899.label: 龋齿
V012-N02899.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02899.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02899.definitions: Characterized by erosions of the surface of the tooth or a breakdown of teeth. It is often caused by exposure to pathogenic factors, fire hyperactivity due to yin deficiency or poor dental hygiene.

V012-N02900.label: 龟背痰
V012-N02900.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜疾病
V012-N02900.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02900.englishNames: tortoise-back phlegm; thoracic spine tuberculosis; Tortoise-back phlegm; chronic vertebral suppurative abscess
V012-N02900.pinyinNames: gui bei tan; guī bèi tán
V012-N02900.definitions: Characterized by swelling and pain of the lower thoracic and upper lumbar vertebrae with an abnormally excessive convex kyphotic curvature of the spine that resembles the back of a tortoise. Often occurs when Mycobacterium tuberculosis affects the spine and damages the bones and sinews.

V012-N02901.label: 证候
V012-N02901.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N02901.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02902.label: 三焦证类
V012-N02902.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02902.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02902.aliases: Three region patterns； Triple burner patterns； Triple energizer patterns； Triple energizer stage patterns； Wenbing Sanjiao Patterns

V012-N02903.label: 上寒下热证
V012-N02903.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02903.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02903.englishNames: upper cold and lower heat pattern/ syndrome; Upper cold with lower heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of cold in the upper and heat in the lower
V012-N02903.pinyinNames: shang han xia re zheng; shàng hán xià rè zhèng
V012-N02903.definitions: Characterized by cold retention in the upper body but heat retention in the lower body.

V012-N02904.label: 上热下寒证
V012-N02904.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02904.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02904.englishNames: upper heat and lower cold pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of heat in the upper and cold in the lower; Upper heat with lower cold pattern
V012-N02904.pinyinNames: shang re xia han zheng; shàng rè xià hán zhèng
V012-N02904.definitions: Characterized by heat retention in the upper body but cold retention in the lower body.

V012-N02905.label: 上焦湿热证
V012-N02905.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02905.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02905.aliases: Dampness and heat in the upper energizer pattern
V012-N02905.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in upper energizer; Damp heat in the upper jiao pattern; upper energizer dampness-heat pattern/syndrome
V012-N02905.pinyinNames: shang jiao shi re zheng; shàng jiāo shī rè zhèng
V012-N02905.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to cold, body heaviness, cough, chest tightness and absence of sweating. The tongue coating is yellow and white. The pulse is soft and delayed. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the upper jiao.

V012-N02906.label: 上焦燥热证
V012-N02906.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02906.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02906.aliases: Upper energizer dryness and heat pattern； Upper energizer dryness heat pattern
V012-N02906.englishNames: Dry heat in the upper jiao pattern; syndrome/pattern of dryness and heat in upper energizer
V012-N02906.pinyinNames: shang jiao zao re zheng; shàng jiāo zào rè zhèng
V012-N02906.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to wind cold, thirst, unproductive cough, headache, scanty sweating, dry lips, a dry, sore throat, tinnitus, red eyes, gum swelling and dry skin. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is rapid and large. This pattern often occurs when dry heat affects the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N02907.label: 上盛下虚证
V012-N02907.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02907.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02907.englishNames: upper exuberance and lower deficiency pattern/syndrome; Upper excess with lower deficiency pattern
V012-N02907.definitions: Characterized by deficiency of the liver and kidney coupled with accumulation of phlegm heat in the upper body.

V012-N02908.label: 下焦湿热证
V012-N02908.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02908.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02908.aliases: 湿热蕴结下焦证； Dampness and heat in the lower energizer pattern
V012-N02908.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the lower energizer; Damp heat in the lower jiao pattern; lower energizer dampness-heat pattern/syndrome
V012-N02908.pinyinNames: xia jiao shi re zheng; xià jiāo shī rè zhèng
V012-N02908.definitions: Characterized by urinary dribbling with a painful urination, loose stools with a strong odour, and lower abdominal distension and pain. Alternatively, urine retention, constipation, yellow, white and smelly vaginal discharge, fever and thirst may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the large intestine or urinary bladder.

V012-N02909.label: 中焦湿热证
V012-N02909.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02909.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02909.aliases: Dampness and heat in the middle energizer pattern； Middle energizer dampness and heat pattern
V012-N02909.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the middle energizer; Damp heat in the middle jiao pattern
V012-N02909.pinyinNames: zhong jiao shi re zheng; zhōng jiāo shī rè zhèng
V012-N02909.definitions: Characterized by fever that cannot be resolved after sweating and become more pronounced in the afternoon, abdominal masses and distension, nausea, vomiting, a poor appetite, thirst with little water intake, yellow urine and loose stools. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the spleen and stomach.

V012-N02910.label: 中焦积热证
V012-N02910.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02910.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02910.aliases: 中焦实热证； Excess heat in the middle jiao
V012-N02910.englishNames: Heat accumulating in the middle jiao pattern
V012-N02910.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, abdominal distension and pain, constipation and scanty, yellow urine. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is rapid and forceful. This pattern often occurs when heat accumulates in the middle jiao.

V012-N02911.label: 亡阳证
V012-N02911.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02911.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02911.aliases: 阳脱证； 阳气暴脱证； 元阳虚脱证； 元阳外脱证； Yang exhaustion pattern/Yang collapse pattern
V012-N02911.englishNames: yang collapse pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of yang prostration; Yang depletion pattern
V012-N02911.pinyinNames: wang yang zheng; wáng yáng zhèng
V012-N02911.definitions: Characterized by cold sweats, cold body/limbs, lassitude, faint breathing, and a pale complexion. The tongue is pale with a moist coating. The pulse is extremely feeble. Often results from yang qi exhaustion or collapse.

V012-N02912.label: 亡阴证
V012-N02912.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02912.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02913.label: 任脉证
V012-N02913.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02913.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02913.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of conception vessel; Ren meridian pattern
V012-N02913.pinyinNames: ren mai zheng; rèn mài zhèng
V012-N02913.definitions: Characterized by hernia, vaginal discharge and abdominal masses. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Ren meridian.

V012-N02914.label: 余毒未清证
V012-N02914.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02914.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02914.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of remnant toxin failing to be cleared; Retained toxin pattern
V012-N02914.pinyinNames: yu dong wei qing zheng; yú dú wèi qīng zhèng
V012-N02914.definitions: Characterized by a persistent low-grade fever and toxin-based symptoms. Often occurs when residual toxin remains in the body.

V012-N02915.label: 余热未清证
V012-N02915.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02915.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02915.aliases: 余热未尽证； Lingering of residual heat pattern
V012-N02915.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of remnant heat failing to be cleared; Residual heat pattern; residual heat pattern/ syndrome
V012-N02915.pinyinNames: yu re wei qing zheng; yú rè wèi qīng zhèng
V012-N02915.definitions: Characterized by persistent low fever or fever at night, restlessness, thirst, constipation, yellow urine, and fatigue. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when pathogenic heat is not completely removed.

V012-N02916.label: 八纲证类
V012-N02916.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02916.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02916.englishNames: Patterns according to the eight principles

V012-N02917.label: 六经病证
V012-N02917.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02917.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02917.definitions: A group of disorders of the six meridians according to the cold damage theory.

V012-N02918.label: 六经证类
V012-N02918.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02918.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02919.label: 其他证类
V012-N02919.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02919.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N02920.label: 内闭外脱证
V012-N02920.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02920.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02920.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of internal block and external collapse; Interior blockage and exterior collapse pattern; pattern/syndrome of internal block and external collapse
V012-N02920.pinyinNames: nei bi wai tuo zheng; nèi bì wài tuō zhèng
V012-N02920.definitions: Characterized by fever, cough, and panting. Alternatively, tenesmus, urine retention, constipation, cardiac or abdominal colic, a pale face, cold limbs, cold sweats, and faint breathing may be present. The pulse is extremely feeble. Often occurs when excessive pathogenic factors block the interior and anti-pathogenic qi becomes extremely deficient.

V012-N02921.label: 冲任不固证
V012-N02921.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02921.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02921.englishNames: Insecurity of the Chong and Ren pattern; syndrome/pattern of thoroughfare vessel and conception insecurity
V012-N02921.pinyinNames: chōng rèn bú gù zhèng; chong ren bu gu zheng
V012-N02921.definitions: Characterized by dribbling menses. Alternatively, uterine bleeding, profuse vaginal discharge or miscarriage may be present. This pattern often occurs when qi of the Chong and Ren fails to secure the uterus.

V012-N02922.label: 冲任失调证
V012-N02922.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02922.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02922.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of thoroughfare vessel and conception vessel disharmony; Disharmony between the Chong and Ren pattern
V012-N02922.pinyinNames: chong ren shi diao zheng; chōng rèn shī tiáo zhèng
V012-N02922.definitions: Characterized by irregular menstruation and lower abdominal distension/pain. This pattern often results from functional disorder of the Chong and Ren meridians.

V012-N02923.label: 冲任瘀阻证
V012-N02923.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02923.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02923.aliases: 冲任瘀滞证； 瘀滞冲任证； 瘀阻冲任证
V012-N02923.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of thoroughfare vessel and conception vessel obstruction; Blood stasis obstructing the Chong and Ren pattern
V012-N02923.pinyinNames: chong ren yu zu zheng; chōng rèn yū zǔ zhèng
V012-N02923.definitions: Characterized by dark purple menstrual colour with clots. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is deep and wiry. This pattern often occurs when stasis obstructs the uterus. Contributing factors may include inappropriate care during period or after childbirth, contraction of external pathogenic qi or emotional disturbance. It may also develop from further progression of qi stagnation of Chong and Ren.

V012-N02924.label: 冲脉证
V012-N02924.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02924.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02924.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of thoroughfare vessel; Chong meridian pattern
V012-N02924.pinyinNames: chong mai zheng; chōng mài zhèng
V012-N02924.definitions: Characterized by abdominal contracture due to qi counterflow. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Chong meridian.

V012-N02925.label: 凉燥证
V012-N02925.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02925.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02925.aliases: 寒燥证； 凉燥袭肺证； Cool dryness attacking the lung pattern
V012-N02925.englishNames: cool dryness pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of cool dryness; Cool dryness pattern
V012-N02925.pinyinNames: liang zao zheng; liáng zào zhèng
V012-N02925.definitions: Characterized by severe aversion to cold, mild fever, headache, absence of sweating, a dry mouth/nose/throat, and cough with scanty phlegm. The tongue coating is thin, white and dry. The pulse is superficial and tense. This pattern often occurs when cool dryness affects the lung.

V012-N02926.label: 十二正经证类
V012-N02926.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02926.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02926.englishNames: Patterns of the twelve regular meridians
V012-N02926.pinyinNames: shí èr zhèng jīng zhèng lèi

V012-N02927.label: 半表半里证
V012-N02927.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02927.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02927.aliases: Half exterior and half interior (commonly used but wrong in meaning)
V012-N02927.englishNames: half-exterior halfinterior pattern/ syndrome; Neither exterior nor interior pattern
V012-N02927.definitions: Characterized by alternating fever and aversion to cold, fullness in the chest and sub-costal area, vexation, vomiting, poor appetite, a bitter mouth, a dry throat and blurred vision. The pulse is wiry. Often occurs when anti-pathogenic qi fights with pathogenic factors between the exterior and interior: exogenous pathogenic factors have not entered the interior or internal pathogenic factors have not reached the exterior.

V012-N02928.label: 卫分证
V012-N02928.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02928.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02928.aliases: 卫分病； 卫分证候； Defence phase pattern； Wei aspect pattern
V012-N02928.englishNames: Wei Fen Symptom Complex; Wei-defence phase pattern; defense aspect pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of defense phase; Defense phase patterns
V012-N02928.pinyinNames: wei fen zheng; wèi fèn zhèng
V012-N02928.definitions: Characterized by fever and mild aversion to wind cold. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic warm heat affects the normal functioning of Wei-defensive qi and disturbs the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N02929.label: 卫气同病证
V012-N02929.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02929.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02929.englishNames: Pattern involving both Wei-defence and qi phases; pattern/syndrome of both defense-qi aspects disease; syndrome/pattern of disease involving both defense phase and qi phase
V012-N02929.pinyinNames: wèi qì tóng bìng zhèng; wei qi tong bing zheng
V012-N02929.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, headache, and mild aversion to wind cold. Alternatively, body ache/heaviness, nasal obstruction, cough, thirst, restlessness, sweating, dark yellow urine, a sore throat and a bitter taste in the mouth may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic factors in febrile conditions enter the qi phase and transform into heat, while pathogenic factors in the Wei-defence phase remain unresolved.

V012-N02930.label: 卫气营血证类
V012-N02930.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02930.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02930.aliases: Four phase patterns； Wenbing aspect patterns
V012-N02930.englishNames: Patterns of Wei-defence, qi, Ying nutrients and blood
V012-N02930.pinyinNames: wèi qì yíng xuè zhèng lèi

V012-N02931.label: 厥阴病证
V012-N02931.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02931.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02931.aliases: 厥阴病； Late Yin stage Pattern； Reverting yin pattern
V012-N02931.englishNames: reverting yin disease pattern/syndrome; Late Yin stage Patterns; Jueyin pattern
V012-N02931.definitions: Characterized by wasting, extreme thirst, qi surging up to the heart, pain and feverish sensation in the heart, hunger but no desire to eat food and vomiting of roundworm after eating. This pattern often occurs in the advanced stage of six meridian transmission and manifests as extreme heat or cold, or alternating cold and heat.

V012-N02932.label: 大肠热结证
V012-N02932.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02932.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02932.aliases: 肠道热结证； 肠道实热证； 大肠实热证； Large intestine excess heat pattern； Excess heat in the large intestine； 肠热腑实证； 热结肠燥证
V012-N02932.englishNames: large intestinal heat bind pattern/ syndrome; Heat stagnation in the large intestine pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat binding in the large intestine
V012-N02932.pinyinNames: dà cháng rè jié zhèng; da chang re jie zheng
V012-N02932.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, constipation, abdominal distension/hardness/fullness and abdominal pain with tenderness. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is deep and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant interior heat blocks the intestinal qi.

V012-N02933.label: 大肠虚寒证
V012-N02933.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02933.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02933.aliases: 大肠虚冷证； Large intestine deficiency cold pattern
V012-N02933.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of deficiency-cold in the large intestine; Large intestine deficiency cold pattern; Deficiency cold in the large intestine pattern
V012-N02933.pinyinNames: da chang xu han zheng; dà cháng xū hán zhèng
V012-N02933.definitions: Characterized by chronic diarrhoea/dysentery, dull abdominal pain that alleviates upon warmth and pressure, cold intolerance and cold limbs. The tongue is pale with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is deep, slow and weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi fails to secure the large intestine.

V012-N02934.label: 太阳中风证
V012-N02934.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02934.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02934.englishNames: Taiyang wind attack pattern; syndrome/pattern of taiyang wind stroke; Taiyang disease with wind affection
V012-N02934.pinyinNames: tài yáng zhòng fēng zhèng; tai yang zhong feng zheng
V012-N02934.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to cold/wind and sweating. The pulse is superficial and delayed. Alternatively, headache, neck rigidity, body ache, lower back pain, nasal obstruction with clear discharge and a thin, white tongue coating may be present. This pattern often occurs when wind cold attacks the Taiyang meridians and disturbs the harmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence.

V012-N02935.label: 太阳伤寒证
V012-N02935.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02935.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02935.aliases: 太阳寒证； 太阳伤寒
V012-N02935.englishNames: Taiyang cold attack pattern; syndrome/pattern of taiyang cold damage; greater yang cold damage pattern/ syndrome
V012-N02935.pinyinNames: tai yang shang han zheng; tài yáng shāng hán zhèng
V012-N02935.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, headache, neck pain and rigidity, absence of sweating, and panting. The pulse is superficial and tight. This pattern often occurs when external wind cold obstructs Wei-defensive yang and closes the skin striae.

V012-N02936.label: 太阳病证
V012-N02936.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02936.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02936.aliases: Early yang stage pattern； Greater yang pattern； 太阴病； 太阳病
V012-N02936.englishNames: Early yang stage pattern; Taiyang pattern; greater yin disease pattern/syndrome; greater yang disease pattern/syndrome
V012-N02936.definitions: Characterized by manifestations of Taiyang meridian pattern and Taiyang–fu organ pattern. This pattern often occurs when the struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors affects the normal functioning of the Wei-defence and Ying nutrients.

V012-N02937.label: 太阳经证
V012-N02937.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02937.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02937.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of taiyang meridian; Taiyang meridian pattern; greater yang meridian pattern/syndrome
V012-N02937.pinyinNames: tai yang jing zheng; tài yáng jīng zhèng
V012-N02937.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to wind/cold, headache, body ache, lower back pain, nasal obstruction with clear discharge, and sweating. The pulse is superficial and delayed or superficial and tight. This pattern often occurs when wind cold affects the Taiyang meridian and disturbs the normal functioning of the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence.

V012-N02938.label: 太阳腑证
V012-N02938.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02938.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02938.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of taiyang fu-organs; Taiyang–fu organ pattern; greater yang bowel pattern/syndrome
V012-N02938.pinyinNames: tai yang fu zheng; tài yáng fǔ zhèng
V012-N02938.definitions: Characterized by thirst, restlessness, insomnia, mania, lower abdominal hardness/fullness, inhibited urination and vomiting immediately after drinking water. The pulse is deep and hesitant or deep and knotted. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat enters the fu organs and affects the qi transformation of the urinary bladder. It may also occur when heat and stagnant blood affects the lower abdomen.

V012-N02939.label: 太阳蓄水证
V012-N02939.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02939.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02939.aliases: 太阳蓄水； 膀胱蓄水证； Bladder water accumulation pattern； Water amassment in the bladder pattern； Water retention in the urinary bladder
V012-N02939.englishNames: greater yang waterretention pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of taiyang amassment of water; Taiyang water retention pattern
V012-N02939.pinyinNames: tai yang xu shui zheng; tài yáng xù shuǐ zhèng
V012-N02939.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal bulging, distension and pain and inhibited urination. This pattern often occurs when the bladder fails to perform qi transformation, resulting in water retention.

V012-N02940.label: 太阳蓄血证
V012-N02940.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02940.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02940.aliases: 太阳蓄血； 膀胱蓄血证； Blood accumulation in the bladder pattern； Blood and heat build up in the bladder pattern； Blood retention in the urinary bladder
V012-N02940.englishNames: greater yang blood amassment pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/ pattern of taiyang amassment of blood; Taiyang disease with stagnated blood syndrome; syndrome/pattern of taiyang amassment of blood; Taiyang blood retention pattern
V012-N02940.pinyinNames: tai yang xu xue zheng; tài yáng xù xuè zhèng
V012-N02940.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal distension and stabbing pain with a fixed location, and normal urination. The tongue is purple with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often results from abdominal injury or occurs when heat affects the urinary bladder, causing blood accumulating in the urinary bladder.

V012-N02941.label: 太阴病证
V012-N02941.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02941.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02941.aliases: Early yin stage pattern； Greater yin pattern
V012-N02941.englishNames: Early yin stage pattern; Taiyin pattern
V012-N02941.definitions: Characterized by abdominal fullness, vomiting, a poor appetite, diarrhoea, absence of thirst and occasional abdominal pain. The pulse is deep and delayed or weak. This pattern often results from exposure to wind cold and retention of cold dampness due to spleen yang deficiency.

V012-N02942.label: 奇经八脉证类
V012-N02942.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02942.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02942.englishNames: Patterns of the eight extra meridians
V012-N02942.pinyinNames: qí jīng bā mài zhèng lèi

V012-N02943.label: 实寒证
V012-N02943.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02943.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02943.englishNames: excess cold pattern/ syndrome; Excess cold pattern
V012-N02943.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, cold limbs, abdominal pain with tenderness, bowel sounds, diarrhoea, loss of sense of taste with profuse salivation, and clear, profuse urine. The tongue coating is white and moist. The pulse is slow or tight. Often occurs when pathogenic cold attacks the body.

V012-N02944.label: 实证
V012-N02944.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02944.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02944.englishNames: excess pattern/ syndrome; Excess pattern; excess syndrome/pattern; Sthenic Syndrome
V012-N02944.definitions: Characterized by high fever, thirst, chest tightness, panting, rapid breathing, phlegm sounds in the throat, restlessness, delirium, abdominal distension/pain/tenderness, and scanty, dark-yellow urine or hesitant, painful urination. The tongue is rough with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is replete and forceful. Contributing factors may include six pathogenic factors, parasites or dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs (subsequent food stagnation, retention of phlegm/fluid/water/dampness and blood stasis).

V012-N02945.label: 寒凝气滞证
V012-N02945.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02945.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02945.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of congealing cold with qi stagnation; syndrome/pattern of cold congealing and qi stagnation; Cold induced qi stagnation pattern
V012-N02945.pinyinNames: han ning qi zhi zheng; hán níng qì zhì zhèng
V012-N02945.definitions: Characterized by headache, body aches, and joint pain with a cold sensation, and contracture of hands/feet. Alternatively, abdominal distension, fullness and pain with a cold sensation, vomiting of clear saliva, bowel sounds, and diarrhoea may be present. The tongue coating is white and moist. The pulse is wiry and tight. Often occurs when retained cold blocks the free flow of qi.

V012-N02946.label: 寒凝胞宫证
V012-N02946.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02946.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02946.aliases: 胞宫寒滞证； 寒滞胞宫证； Cold congealment in the uterus pattern； Cold stagnation in the uterus pattern
V012-N02946.englishNames: Cold stagnation in the uterus pattern; Cold retention in the uterus pattern
V012-N02946.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal pain with a cold sensation that alleviates upon warmth. Dysmenorrhoea, delayed period with dark purple colour, white, thin and clear vaginal discharge and infertility may also be present. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is deep and tight. This pattern often occurs when cold remains in the uterus.

V012-N02947.label: 寒凝血瘀证
V012-N02947.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02947.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02947.aliases: 寒凝血涩证； 寒凝瘀结证； 寒邪内积证
V012-N02947.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold congealing and blood stasis; Cold induced blood stasis pattern; pattern/syndrome of congealing cold with blood stasis
V012-N02947.pinyinNames: han ning xue yu zheng; hán níng xuè yū zhèng
V012-N02947.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold pain that alleviates with warmth, bluish, cold limbs, delayed periods, and dysmenorrhea with dark, purple blood and clots. The tongue is dark purple with a white coating. The pulse is deep, slow and hesitant. Often occurs when cold blocks the circulation of blood.

V012-N02948.label: 寒实结胸证
V012-N02948.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02948.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02948.englishNames: Retained excess cold in the chest pattern; chest binding syndrome with cold fluid; syndrome/pattern of cold excess binding in the chest
V012-N02948.pinyinNames: hán shí jié xiōng zhèng; han shi jie xiong zheng
V012-N02948.definitions: Characterized by pain below the heart and cold limbs. Alternatively, chest pain, abdominal distension and pain with tenderness and cough with white, thin phlegm may be present. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is deep, wiry and tight. Often occurs when pathogenic cold enters the interior or retains in the chest area due to the inappropriate use of purging therapy for Taiyang disease. It may also result from cold phlegm retention in the thoracic diaphragm.

V012-N02949.label: 寒湿困脾证
V012-N02949.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02949.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02949.aliases: 湿困脾阳证； 寒湿偏胜，中阳被困证； 寒湿蕴脾证； Cold dampness encumbering the spleen system pattern； Cold dampness accumulating in the spleen pattern
V012-N02949.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of cold-dampness encumbering the spleen; syndrome/pattern of cold and dampness encumbering the spleen; Cold dampness affecting the spleen pattern; Cold-dampness encumbering the spleen system pattern
V012-N02949.pinyinNames: han shi kun pi zheng; hán shī kùn pí zhèng
V012-N02949.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension/tightness, a greasy taste, a poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, tastelessness, abdominal pain, loose stools, and heaviness of the head and body. Dark yellow eyes/skin, profuse leucorrhoea, and obesity may also be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and delayed. This pattern often occurs when internal cold dampness obstructs spleen yang.

V012-N02950.label: 寒湿着腰证
V012-N02950.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02950.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02950.englishNames: Cold dampness affecting the low back pattern
V012-N02950.definitions: Characterized by a cold sensation and pain in the low back that worsens with cold. This pattern often occurs when cold dampness affects the lumbar region.

V012-N02951.label: 寒湿蕴肤证
V012-N02951.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02951.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02951.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold and dampness retaining in the skin; Retained cold dampness in the skin pattern
V012-N02951.pinyinNames: han shi yun fu zheng; hán shī yùn fū zhèng
V012-N02951.definitions: Characterized by skin numbness, stiffness, swelling, insensitivity and cold upon palpation. Often occurs when cold dampness retains in the skin.

V012-N02952.label: 寒湿阻络证
V012-N02952.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02952.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02952.aliases: 寒湿入络证； Cold dampness entering the meridians pattern； 寒湿侵袭，痹阻经络证
V012-N02952.englishNames: Cold dampness obstructing the meridians pattern; syndrome/pattern of cold and dampness obstructing collaterals
V012-N02952.pinyinNames: hán shī zǔ luò zhèng; han shi zu luo zheng
V012-N02952.definitions: Characterized by limb or localized heaviness, coldness, pain, numbness or swelling, aversion to cold and cold limbs. The tongue coating is white and slippery. This pattern often occurs when cold damp affects the meridians.

V012-N02953.label: 寒滞经络证
V012-N02953.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02953.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02953.englishNames: Cold retention in the meridians pattern
V012-N02953.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, limb coldness, pain, contracture or numbness and a dark purple or pale complexion. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is wiry and tight. This pattern often occurs when retained cold affects the flow of blood within meridians.

V012-N02954.label: 寒滞肝脉证
V012-N02954.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02954.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02954.aliases: 寒滞肝经证； Cold stagnation in liver meridian pattern； Liver cold pattern； Liver meridian cold stagnation pattern； Liver meridian excess cold pattern； 肝经实寒证； 肝寒证
V012-N02954.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold stagnation in the liver meridian; Liver meridian cold stagnation pattern; Cold retention in the liver meridian pattern; pattern/syndrome of cold stagnating in the liver meridian
V012-N02954.pinyinNames: han zhi gan mai zheng; hán zhì gān mài zhèng
V012-N02954.definitions: Characterized by pain in the lateral lower abdomen with a cold sensation, aversion to cold and cold limbs. Alternatively, genital pain with contraction or parietal headache may be present, which aggravates with cold and alleviates with warmth. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is wiry and tight. This pattern often occurs when cold remains in the liver meridian.

V012-N02955.label: 寒滞胃肠证
V012-N02955.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02955.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02955.englishNames: Cold retention in the stomach and intestines pattern; pattern/syndrome of cold stagnating in stomach and intestines; syndrome/pattern of cold stagnation in the stomach and intestines
V012-N02955.pinyinNames: han zhi wei chang zheng; hán zhì wèi cháng zhèng
V012-N02955.definitions: Characterized by cold, intense pain in the stomach/abdomen that alleviates upon warmth, vomiting, diarrhoea of thin, clear stools, aversion to cold and cold limbs. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when cold obstructs the qi flow of the stomach and intestines.

V012-N02956.label: 寒热错杂证
V012-N02956.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02956.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02956.aliases: 寒热夹杂证； 寒热挟杂证
V012-N02956.englishNames: Tangled cold and heat pattern; Combined cold and heat pattern; cold-heat complex pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of cold and heat complex
V012-N02956.pinyinNames: han re cuo za zheng; hán rè cuò zá zhèng
V012-N02956.definitions: Characterized by simultaneous occurrence of cold pattern and heat pattern in one individual patient at a certain pathological stage.

V012-N02957.label: 寒痰证
V012-N02957.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02957.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02957.englishNames: cold-phlegm pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of cold phlegm; Cold phlegm pattern
V012-N02957.pinyinNames: han tan zheng; hán tán zhèng
V012-N02957.definitions: Characterized by coughing with white phlegm, chest tightness, gastric stuffiness, panting, wheezing sounds, cold intolerance, and cold limbs. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry, slippery or wiry and tense. Often occurs when pathogenic cold binds with turbid phlegm.

V012-N02958.label: 寒痰阻肺证
V012-N02958.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02958.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02958.aliases: 寒痰停肺证； Cold phlegm obstructing the lung pattern； Retention of cold phlegm in the lung
V012-N02958.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold phlegm obstructing the lung; Cold phlegm obstructing the lung pattern; pattern/syndrome of cold- phlegm obstructing the lung
V012-N02958.pinyinNames: han tan zu fei zheng; hán tán zǔ fèi zhèng
V012-N02958.definitions: Characterized by cough, panting, phlegm sounds in the throat, chest fullness/tightness, white, sticky or thin/clear phlegm, shortness of breath upon physical exertion, an inability to lie flat, cold limbs, and cold intolerance. Fever and aversion to cold may also be present. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is tense. This pattern often occurs when retained phlegm and external cold affect the descending of lung qi.

V012-N02959.label: 寒证
V012-N02959.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02959.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02959.englishNames: cold pattern/ syndrome; cold syndrome/pattern; Cold pattern; Cold Syndrome
V012-N02959.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, cold intolerance, cold pain with preference for warmth, absence of thirst, thin, clear phlegm and nasal discharge, clear, profuse urine, loose stools and a pale complexion. The tongue is purple with a white coating. The pulse is tight or slow. Often results from excess of yin qi or cold attacking the interior.

V012-N02960.label: 寒邪犯胃证
V012-N02960.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02960.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02960.aliases: 寒邪客胃证； 胃中寒冷证； 寒滞胃脘证； 胃实寒证； 胃寒证
V012-N02960.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of pathogenic cold invading the stomach; Cold invading the stomach system pattern; Cold attacking the stomach pattern
V012-N02960.pinyinNames: han xie fan wei zheng; hán xié fàn wèi zhèng
V012-N02960.definitions: Characterized by cold, intense stomach ache that alleviates upon warmth, vomiting of clear saliva, aversion to cold, and cold limbs. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when cold affects the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N02961.label: 寒饮伏肺证
V012-N02961.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02961.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02961.aliases: 寒饮停肺证； Retention of cold fluids in the lung pattern
V012-N02961.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold fluid retention in lung; Cold fluids affecting the lung pattern
V012-N02961.pinyinNames: han yin fu fei zheng; hán yǐn fú fèi zhèng
V012-N02961.definitions: Characterized by frequent, violent coughs with profuse, thin and white phlegm, rapid breathing and chest tightness. In severe cases, panting, an inability to lie flat, fever and aversion to cold may also be present. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is superficial and tense. This pattern often occurs when retained fluid combined with external cold affect the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N02962.label: 寒饮停胃证
V012-N02962.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02962.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02962.aliases: 胃寒饮停证； Stomach cold with fluids retention pattern； 痰饮停胃证
V012-N02962.englishNames: Cold fluids retention in the stomach pattern; syndrome/pattern of cold fluid retention in the stomach
V012-N02962.pinyinNames: hán yǐn tíng wèi zhèng; han yin ting wei zheng
V012-N02962.definitions: Characterized by gastric masses/distension, splashing sounds in the stomach, and vomiting of thin, clear saliva. The tongue coating is white and slippery. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when cold fluids remain in the stomach.

V012-N02963.label: 小肠实热证
V012-N02963.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02963.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02963.aliases: Small intestine excess heat pattern； 心移热膀胱证
V012-N02963.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of excess-heat in the small intestine; Excess heat in the small intestine pattern; Small intestine excess heat pattern
V012-N02963.pinyinNames: xiao chang shi re zheng; xiǎo cháng shí rè zhèng
V012-N02963.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, restlessness, insomnia, mouth/tongue ulcerations, scanty, yellow urine and painful urination with a burning sensation. In severe cases, bloody urine may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when heart fire flows downward to the small intestine or when damp heat retains in the small intestine.

V012-N02964.label: 小肠气滞证
V012-N02964.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02964.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02964.aliases: 小肠气痛证； Small intestine qi stagnation pattern
V012-N02964.englishNames: small intestinal qi stagnation pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation in the small intestine; Small intestine qi stagnation pattern
V012-N02964.pinyinNames: xiao chang qi zhi zheng; xiǎo cháng qì zhì zhèng
V012-N02964.definitions: Characterized by abdominal fullness/distension/pain that aggravates upon emotional disturbance and is alleviated upon belching or flatus. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often results from qi stagnation in the small intestine.

V012-N02965.label: 小肠虚寒证
V012-N02965.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02965.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02965.englishNames: Deficiency cold in the small intestine pattern; syndrome/pattern of deficiency cold in the small intestine; Small intestine deficiency cold pattern
V012-N02965.pinyinNames: xiao chang xu han zheng; xiǎo cháng xū hán zhèng
V012-N02965.definitions: Characterized by undigested food in stools, dull abdominal pain that alleviates upon warmth, thirst with a desire to drink warm water, cold limbs, inhibited urination and loose stools. The tongue is pale with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is deep, weak and slack. This pattern often occurs when internal cold (due to yang qi deficiency) affects the functions of the small intestine in separating the clear from turbid.

V012-N02966.label: 少阳病证
V012-N02966.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02966.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02966.aliases: Late yang stage pattern； Lesser yang pattern； 少阳病
V012-N02966.englishNames: Late yang stage pattern; Shaoyang pattern; lesser yang disease pattern/syndrome
V012-N02966.definitions: Characterized by a bitter mouth, a dry throat, dizziness, blurred vision, fever alternating with aversion to cold, fullness in the chest and subcostal region, no desire to eat or drink, restlessness, and vomiting. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic factors affect the qi flow of Shaoyang meridians.

V012-N02967.label: 少阴寒化证
V012-N02967.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02967.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02967.englishNames: Shaoyin cold transformation pattern; syndrome/pattern of shaoyin transforming into cold; syndrome/ pattern of shaoyin transforming into cold; Shaoyin disease with cold transformation syndrome; lesser yin cold transformation pattern/syndrome
V012-N02967.pinyinNames: shào yīn hán huà zhèng; shao yin han hua zheng
V012-N02967.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold without fever, diarrhoea with undigested food in stools, and cold limbs. The pulse is feeble and thready. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic factors enter the Shaoyin meridians and transform into cold, coupled with yang qi failure of the heart and kidney.

V012-N02968.label: 少阴热化证
V012-N02968.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02968.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02968.englishNames: Shaoyin disease with heat transformation syndrome; lesser yin heat transformation pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of shaoyin heat transforming into heat; Shaoyin heat transformation pattern
V012-N02968.pinyinNames: shao yin re hua zheng; shào yīn rè huà zhèng
V012-N02968.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, insomnia, and a dry mouth/throat. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic factors enter the Shao meridian and transform into heat, coupled with yang hyperactivity due to yin deficiency.

V012-N02969.label: 少阴病证
V012-N02969.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02969.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02969.aliases: Lesser yin pattern； Middle yin stage pattern； 少阴病
V012-N02969.englishNames: Shaoyin pattern; Middle yin stage Pattern (TM), Middle yin stage pattern; lesser yin disease pattern/syndrome
V012-N02969.definitions: Characterized by manifestations of Shaoyin cold transformation pattern and Shaoyin heat transformation. This pattern often results from hypofunction of the heart and kidney as well as general deficiency of yin and yang.

V012-N02970.label: 带脉证
V012-N02970.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02970.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02970.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of belt vessel; Dai meridian pattern
V012-N02970.pinyinNames: dai mai zheng; dài mài zhèng
V012-N02970.definitions: Characterized by abdominal fullness and a cold sensation in the lower back (like sitting in water). This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Dai meridian.

V012-N02971.label: 心气虚证
V012-N02971.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02971.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02971.aliases: 心气不足证； 心气亏虚证； 心气虚弱证
V012-N02971.englishNames: heart qi deficiency pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of heart qi deficiency; Heart qi deficiency pattern
V012-N02971.pinyinNames: xin qi xu zheng; xīn qì xū zhèng
V012-N02971.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, shortness of breath and lassitude that aggravate upon physical exertion. Spontaneous sweating and a pale face may also be present. The tongue is pale. The pulse is deficient. This pattern often occusr when heart qi fails to help with the heart to pump blood.

V012-N02972.label: 心气血两虚证
V012-N02972.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02972.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02972.aliases: Dual deficiency of heart qi and blood pattern
V012-N02972.englishNames: Dual deficiency of heart qi and blood pattern; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of heart qi and blood; Deficiency of heart qi and blood pattern; pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of heart qi and blood
V012-N02972.pinyinNames: xin qi xue liang xu zheng; xīn qì xuè liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N02972.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, shortness of breath, lassitude, fatigue, dizziness, forgetfulness, dream-disturbed sleep and a pale face. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready and weak. This pattern often occurs when qi and blood fail to nourish the heart and heart mind.

V012-N02973.label: 心气阴两虚证
V012-N02973.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02973.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02973.englishNames: Deficiency of heart qi and yin pattern; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of heart qi and yin
V012-N02973.pinyinNames: xin qi yin liang xu zheng; xīn qì yīn liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N02973.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, shortness of breath, lassitude, dizziness, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep and flushed cheeks. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is rapid or weak. This pattern often occurs when qi and yin fail to nourish the heart and heart mind.

V012-N02974.label: 心火上炎证
V012-N02974.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02974.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02974.aliases: 心火偏亢证； Heart fire flaming upward pattern
V012-N02974.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart fire flaming up; pattern/syndrome of heart fire flaming upward; Heart fire flaming upward pattern; Upward flaming of heart fire pattern
V012-N02974.pinyinNames: xin huo shang yan zheng; xīn huǒ shàng yán zhèng
V012-N02974.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, restlessness, a red face and red and painful mouth/tongue ulcerations. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when hyperactive fire ascends to the mouth/tongue along the heart meridian.

V012-N02975.label: 心神不宁证
V012-N02975.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02975.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02975.aliases: 心神不安证； Heart spirit restlessness pattern
V012-N02975.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart spirit inquietude; Disquieted heart spirit pattern; Heart spirit restlessness pattern
V012-N02975.pinyinNames: xin shen bu ning zheng; xīn shén bù níng zhèng
V012-N02975.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, panic, restlessness, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, and jumpy or easily startled. This pattern often results from emotional disturbance or medical conditions.

V012-N02976.label: 心系证类
V012-N02976.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02976.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02976.englishNames: category of heart system syndromes/patterns; Patterns of the heart system; Heart system patterns
V012-N02976.pinyinNames: xin xi zheng lei; xīn xì zhèng lèi

V012-N02977.label: 心肝火旺证
V012-N02977.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02977.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02977.aliases: 心肝实热证； 心肝热盛证； Excess heat in the heart and liver pattern
V012-N02977.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of effulgent fire in the heart and liver; Fire hyperactivity of the heart and liver pattern
V012-N02977.pinyinNames: xin gan huo wang zheng; xīn gān huǒ wàng zhèng
V012-N02977.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, restlessness, irritability, red face and eyes, pain in the subcostal region, a bitter mouth, insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid and forceful. This pattern often occurs when exuberant fire/heat affects the heart and liver.

V012-N02978.label: 心肝血瘀证
V012-N02978.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02978.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02978.englishNames: Blood stasis in the heart and liver pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood stasis in the heart and liver
V012-N02978.pinyinNames: xīn gān xuè yū zhèng; xin gan xue yu zheng
V012-N02978.definitions: Characterized by headache, stabbing pain in the subcostal region, and palpitations. Alternatively, amenorrhoea may be present. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when blood of the heart and liver becomes stagnant.

V012-N02979.label: 心肝血虚证
V012-N02979.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02979.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02979.aliases: Heart and liver blood deficiency pattern
V012-N02979.englishNames: heart-liver blood deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Heart and liver blood deficiency pattern; Blood deficiency of the heart and liver pattern; syndrome/pattern of heart and liver blood deficiency
V012-N02979.pinyinNames: xin gan xue xu zheng; xīn gān xuè xū zhèng
V012-N02979.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, dream-disturbed sleep, poor memory, dizziness, blurred vision, dull pain in the subcostal region, scanty menstrual volume and pale face/tongue/fingernails. The pulse is thready. This pattern often occurs when blood fails to nourish the heart and liver.

V012-N02980.label: 心肝阴虚证
V012-N02980.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02980.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02980.aliases: Heart and liver yin deficiency pattern
V012-N02980.englishNames: Yin deficiency of the heart and liver pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in heart and liver
V012-N02980.pinyinNames: xin gan yin xu zheng; xīn gān yīn xū zhèng
V012-N02980.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, insomnia, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, a low-grade fever, flushed cheeks, dizziness and blurred vision. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids deficiency of the heart and liver causes deficiency heat.

V012-N02981.label: 心肺气虚证
V012-N02981.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02981.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02981.aliases: Heart and lung qi deficiency pattern
V012-N02981.englishNames: Heart and lung qi deficiency pattern; heart-lung qi deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Qi deficiency of the heart and lung pattern; syndrome/pattern of heart and lung qi deficiency
V012-N02981.pinyinNames: xin fei qi xu zheng; xīn fèi qì xū zhèng
V012-N02981.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, cough, shortness of breath, panting, chest tightness and lassitude. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from qi deficiency of the heart and lung.

V012-N02982.label: 心肺热盛证
V012-N02982.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02982.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02982.aliases: 心肺实热证； 心肺火旺证； Excess heat/fire hyperactivity in the heart and lung pattern； 热炽心肺证
V012-N02982.englishNames: Exuberant heat in the heart and lung pattern
V012-N02982.definitions: Characterized by panting, cough with yellow, sticky sputum, restlessness and insomnia. In severe cases, unconsciousness and delirium may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid and forceful. This pattern often occurs when exuberant fire/heat disturbs the heart and lung.

V012-N02983.label: 心肺阳虚证
V012-N02983.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02983.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02983.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart and lung yang deficiency; Yang deficiency of the heart and lung pattern
V012-N02983.pinyinNames: xin fei yang xu zheng; xīn fèi yáng xū zhèng
V012-N02983.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, cough, cold intolerance, cold limbs, chest tightness and cough with thin, white sputum. The tongue is pale purple. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from yang qi deficiency of the heart and lung.

V012-N02984.label: 心肺阴虚证
V012-N02984.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02984.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02984.aliases: Heart and lung yin deficiency pattern
V012-N02984.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart and lung yin deficiency; Yin deficiency of the heart and lung pattern
V012-N02984.pinyinNames: xin fei yin xu zheng; xīn fèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N02984.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, cough, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, flushed cheeks and night sweats. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids of the heart and lung become deficient.

V012-N02985.label: 心肾不交证
V012-N02985.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02985.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02985.aliases: 心肾阴虚阳亢证； 心肾阴虚火旺证； 心肾阴虚证； Heart and kidney systems disharmony pattern； Yin deficiency of the heart and kidney
V012-N02985.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart and kidney failure in interaction; heart-kidney noninteraction pattern/ syndrome; Disharmony between the heart and kidney pattern; Heart and kidney systems disharmony pattern
V012-N02985.pinyinNames: xin shen bu jiao zheng; xīn shèn bù jiāo zhèng
V012-N02985.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, restlessness, insomnia, tinnitus, low back/knee and soreness and weakness. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready, rapid and weak. This pattern often results from lack of coordination between heart fire and kidney water.

V012-N02986.label: 心肾气虚证
V012-N02986.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02986.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02986.aliases: Heart and kidney qi deficiency pattern
V012-N02986.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart and kidney qi deficiency; Qi deficiency of the heart and kidney pattern
V012-N02986.pinyinNames: xin shen qi xu zheng; xīn shèn qì xū zhèng
V012-N02986.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, shortness of breath, low back/knee soreness and weakness, frequent urination and urinary dribbling. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from qi deficiency of the heart and kidney.

V012-N02987.label: 心肾阳虚证
V012-N02987.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02987.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02987.aliases: 心肾虚寒证； Heart and kidney yang deficiency pattern； Deficiency cold of the heart and kidney
V012-N02987.englishNames: heart-kidney yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Heart and kidney yang deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency in heart and kidney; Yang deficiency of the heart and kidney pattern
V012-N02987.pinyinNames: xin shen yang xu zheng; xīn shèn yáng xū zhèng
V012-N02987.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, mild to severe palpitations, dysuria, leg oedema and low back/knee soreness and coldness. The tongue is pale purple with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi of the heart and kidney fails to warm the body.

V012-N02988.label: 心胆气虚证
V012-N02988.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02988.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02988.aliases: Heart and gallbladder qi deficiency pattern
V012-N02988.englishNames: Heart and gallbladder qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency in heart and gallbladder; Qi deficiency of the heart and gallbladder pattern
V012-N02988.pinyinNames: xin dan qi xu zheng; xīn dǎn qì xū zhèng
V012-N02988.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, insomnia, timidity, jumpiness or easily startled, dizziness and chest tightness. The pale is tongue. This pattern often occurs as a result of heart qi deficiency and gallbladder qi uneasiness.

V012-N02989.label: 心脉瘀阻证
V012-N02989.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02989.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02989.aliases: 心血瘀滞证； 心脉痹阻证； Heart blood stagnation pattern； heart vessel obstruction pattern
V012-N02989.definitions: Characterized by chest tightness, palpitations, and stabbing cardiac pain that radiates to the shoulder and back. The tongue is dark purple.
