The pulse is thready and hesitant or regularly/irregularly intermittent. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the heart vessels.

V012-N02990.label: 心脾两虚证
V012-N02990.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02990.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02990.aliases: 心脾亏虚证； Heart and spleen systems deficiency pattern
V012-N02990.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of the heart and spleen; Deficiency of the heart and spleen pattern; pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of the heart and spleen; Heart and spleen systems deficiency pattern
V012-N02990.pinyinNames: xin pi liang xu zheng; xīn pí liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N02990.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, lassitude, low food intake, abdominal distension and loose stools. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when heart blood and spleen (yang) qi become deficient.

V012-N02991.label: 心脾气虚证
V012-N02991.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02991.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02991.aliases: Heart and spleen qi deficiency pattern
V012-N02991.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart and spleen qi deficiency; Qi deficiency of the heart and spleen pattern
V012-N02991.pinyinNames: xin pi qi xu zheng; xīn pí qì xū zhèng
V012-N02991.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, lassitude, dizziness, poor memory, low food intake, abdominal distension and loose stools. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs as a result of qi deficiency of the heart and spleen.

V012-N02992.label: 心脾积热证
V012-N02992.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02992.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02992.aliases: 心脾实热证； Excess heat in the heart and spleen pattern
V012-N02992.englishNames: Heat accumulating in the heart and spleen pattern
V012-N02992.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst and painful mouth/tongue ulcerations. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid and forceful. This pattern often occurs as a result of pathogenic heat accumulating in the heart and spleen.

V012-N02993.label: 心脾阳虚证
V012-N02993.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02993.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02993.aliases: 心脾虚寒证； Deficiency cold of the heart and spleen/Heart and spleen yang deficiency pattern
V012-N02993.englishNames: syndrome/ pattern of yang deficiency in the heart and spleen; Yang deficiency of the heart and spleen pattern; syndrome/pattern of  yang deficiency in the heart and spleen
V012-N02993.pinyinNames: xin pi yang xu zheng; xīn pí yáng xū zhèng
V012-N02993.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, palpitations, lassitude, low food intake, abdominal distension and loose stools. The tongue is pale purple with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi of the heart and spleen fails to warm the body and transport the water and food.

V012-N02994.label: 心血虚证
V012-N02994.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02994.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02994.aliases: 心血亏虚证； 心虚血少证； 心血不足证
V012-N02994.englishNames: Heart blood deficiency pattern; heart blood deficiency pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of heart blood deficiency
V012-N02994.pinyinNames: xīn xuè xū zhèng; xin xue xu zheng
V012-N02994.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, dizziness, dream-disturbed sleep, forgetfulness, a pale white or sallow complexion and pale lips. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready. This pattern often occurs when heart blood fails to nourish the heart and heart spirit.

V012-N02995.label: 心阳暴脱证
V012-N02995.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02995.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02995.aliases: 心阳欲脱证； Heart yang collapse pattern
V012-N02995.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of sudden collapse of heart yang; Heart yang collapse pattern; Sudden collapse of heart yang pattern
V012-N02995.pinyinNames: xin yang bao tuo zheng; xīn yáng bào tuō zhèng
V012-N02995.definitions: Characterized by cold sweats, cold limbs, faint breathing, mild to severe palpitations, a pale complexion and mental confusion or unconsciousness. The pulse is extremely weak. This pattern can be a further progression of heart yang deficiency. It may also occur as a result of sudden blockage or attack by excess pathogens.

V012-N02996.label: 心阳虚证
V012-N02996.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02996.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02996.aliases: 心阳亏虚证； 心阳气虚证； 心阳不足证
V012-N02996.englishNames: syndrome/pattern heart yang deficiency; syndrome/ pattern heart yang deficiency; Heart yang deficiency pattern; heart yang deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N02996.pinyinNames: xin yang xu zheng; xin yin xu zheng; xīn yáng xū zhèng
V012-N02996.definitions: Characterized by mild to severe palpitations, chest tightness, cold intolerance, cold limbs, a bright pale face, shortness of breath, and spontaneous sweating. Oedema in the leg and dark lips may also be present. The tongue is purple with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak or regularly/irregularly intermittent. This pattern can be a further progression of heart qi deficiency. It may also occur when heart yang fails to warm the body.

V012-N02997.label: 心阴虚证
V012-N02997.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02997.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02997.aliases: 心阴亏虚证； 心阴不足证
V012-N02997.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart yin deficiency; heart yin deficiency pattern/syndrome; Heart yin deficiency pattern
V012-N02997.pinyinNames: xin yin xu zheng; xīn yīn xū zhèng
V012-N02997.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, restlessness, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, dizziness, forgetfulness, hot flushes and night sweats. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when heart yin fails to nourish the heart and heart spirit.

V012-N02998.label: 思虑伤脾证
V012-N02998.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02998.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02998.aliases: 思伤心脾证； 思伤脾气证； Anxiety damaging the spleen system pattern； Overthinking damaging spleen qi
V012-N02998.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of excessive contemplation damaging the spleen; Worry damaging the spleen pattern
V012-N02998.pinyinNames: si lv shang pi zheng; sī lǜ shāng pí zhèng
V012-N02998.definitions: Characterized by worries, suspiciousness, dizziness, lassitude, palpitations, timidity, insomnia, forgetfulness, a poor appetite, and a lustreless complexion. The tongue is pale with a thin, white coating. The pulse is thready. This pattern often results from qi stagnation due to thinking too much, mental over-exertion or emotional disturbance.

V012-N02999.label: 情志郁结证
V012-N02999.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N02999.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N02999.aliases: 忧伤神气证； Excessive worry damaging spirit qi pattern
V012-N02999.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of emotional stagnation; Emotional stagnation pattern
V012-N02999.pinyinNames: qing zhi yu jie zheng; qíng zhì yù jié zhèng
V012-N02999.definitions: Characterized by depression, apathetic facial expression, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, dizziness, lassitude, and a poor appetite. The pulse is wiry. Often occurs when excessive worry damages the mind.

V012-N03000.label: 惊恐伤肾证
V012-N03000.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03000.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03000.aliases: 恐惧伤肾证； 恐伤肾气证
V012-N03000.englishNames: Fear damaging the kidney system pattern; syndrome/pattern of sudden fright and terror damaging the kidney; Fear/fright damaging the kidney pattern
V012-N03000.pinyinNames: jing kong shang shen zheng; jīng kǒng shāng shèn zhèng
V012-N03000.definitions: Characterized by panic, impotence, nocturnal emissions, timidity, paranoia, and bladder/bowel incontinence. Often occurs when severe fear or fright damages kidney qi.

V012-N03001.label: 手厥阴心包经证
V012-N03001.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03001.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03001.aliases: Heart governor meridian pattern； Heart ruler meridian pattern
V012-N03001.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of pericardium meridian of hand-juejin; syndrome/pattern of pericardium meridian of hand-jueyin; Pericardium meridian pattern
V012-N03001.pinyinNames: shou jue yin xin bao jing zheng; shǒu jué yīn xīn bāo jīng zhèng
V012-N03001.definitions: Characterized by cardiac pain, chest tightness, restlessness, mania, armpit swelling, arm contracture and a feverish sensation in the palms. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the pericardium meridian.

V012-N03002.label: 手太阳小肠经证
V012-N03002.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03002.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03002.englishNames: Small intestine meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of small intestine meridian of hand-taiyang
V012-N03002.pinyinNames: shou tai yang xiao chang jing zheng; shǒu tài yáng xiǎo cháng jīng zhèng
V012-N03002.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal pain, lower back painlower back pain that radiates towards the testis, deafness, yellowish eyes, swollen cheek, a sore, swollen throat and pain along the posterior border of the lateral aspect of the shoulder and arm. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the small intestine meridian.

V012-N03003.label: 手太阴肺经证
V012-N03003.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03003.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03003.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of lung meridian of hand-taiyin; Lung meridian pattern
V012-N03003.pinyinNames: shou tai yin fei jing zheng; shǒu tài yīn fèi jīng zhèng
V012-N03003.definitions: Characterized by cough, panting, shortness of breath, haemoptysis, chest fullness/distension, a sore, swollen throat, pain in the clavicle and along the anterior border of the medial aspect of the arm, and pain with a cold sensation in the shoulder and back. This pattern often results from impaired flow of lung meridian qi.

V012-N03004.label: 手少阳三焦经证
V012-N03004.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03004.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03004.aliases: Triple energizer meridian pattern； Triple burner meridian pattern； Triple warmer meridian pattern
V012-N03004.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of triple energizer meridian of hand-shaoyang; Sanjiao meridian pattern
V012-N03004.pinyinNames: shou shao yang san jiao jing zheng; shǒu shào yáng sān jiāo jīng zhèng
V012-N03004.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension, oedema, enuresis, inhibited urination, deafness, tinnitus, a sore throat, red, swollen and painful eyes, swollen cheeks, and pain behind the ears and along the lateral aspect of the shoulder, arm and elbow. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Sanjiao meridian.

V012-N03005.label: 手少阴心经证
V012-N03005.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03005.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03005.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heart meridian of hand-shaoyin; Heart meridian pattern
V012-N03005.pinyinNames: shou shao yin xin jing zheng; shǒu shào yīn xīn jīng zhèng
V012-N03005.definitions: Characterized by cardiac pain, a dry throat, thirst, yellowish eyes, pain in the subcostal region and along the medial aspect of the arm, and a feverish sensation in the palms. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the heart meridian.

V012-N03006.label: 手阳明大肠经证
V012-N03006.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03006.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03006.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of large intestine meridian of hand-yangming; Large intestine meridian pattern
V012-N03006.pinyinNames: shou yang ming da chang jing zheng; shǒu yáng míng dà cháng jīng zhèng
V012-N03006.definitions: Characterized by abdominal pain, bowel sounds, diarrhoea, constipation, dysentery, a sore, swollen throat, toothache, clear nasal discharge, nosebleed, and pain, hot or cold sensation along the meridian pathway. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the large intestine meridian.

V012-N03007.label: 暑湿困脾证
V012-N03007.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03007.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03007.aliases: 暑湿困阻中焦证； 暑湿伤脾证
V012-N03007.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of summer-heat and dampness encumbering the spleen; Summer heat and dampness obstructed spleen pattern
V012-N03007.pinyinNames: shu shi kun pi zheng; shǔ shī kùn pí zhèng
V012-N03007.definitions: Characterized by abdominal discomfort, poor appetite, a sticky, greasy taste in the mouth, thirst with little water intake, limb soreness/heaviness/pain, dizziness and headache with a distending sensation. Alternatively, waves of fever in the afternoon, limb oedema, loose stools, and scanty urine may be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when summer heat and dampness obstruct the qi activities of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03008.label: 暑湿袭表证
V012-N03008.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03008.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03008.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of summer and heat and dampness attacking the exterior; Summer heat and dampness attacking the exterior pattern; pattern/syndrome of summerheatdampness assailing the exterior
V012-N03008.pinyinNames: shu shi xi biao zheng; shǔ shī xí biǎo zhèng
V012-N03008.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to wind cold, body heaviness, sweating/absence of sweating, and thirst. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is soft and rapid. Often occurs when summer heat and dampness attack the surface of the body and affect the normal functioning of the Wei-defensive qi.

V012-N03009.label: 暑湿证
V012-N03009.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03009.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03009.aliases: 暑湿内蕴证； Internal retention of summer heat and dampness
V012-N03009.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of summer-heat and dampness; Summer heat and dampness pattern; summerheatdampness pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03009.pinyinNames: shu shi zheng; shǔ shī zhèng
V012-N03009.definitions: Characterized by alternating chills and fever, inhibited sweating, limb heaviness, thirst, restlessness, dusty complexion, chest tightness, nausea, vomiting, loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation, and scanty, hesitant urination. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when summer heat and dampness or pestilence enter and remain in the body.

V012-N03010.label: 暑热动风证
V012-N03010.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03010.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03010.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of summerheat-heat stirring wind; Summer heat stirring wind pattern; syndrome/pattern of summer-heat stirring wind
V012-N03010.pinyinNames: shu re dong feng zheng; shǔ rè dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03010.definitions: Characterized by persistent high fever, unconsciousness, mania, delirium, and convulsions of the limbs. Alternatively, opisthotonus and trismus may be present. Often occurs when summer heat/dampness or pestilence stir the liver wind.

V012-N03011.label: 暑热证
V012-N03011.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03011.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03011.aliases: 暑温证； 暑热动风； 暑热内郁证； Internal retention of summer heat pattern
V012-N03011.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of summer-heat; Summer Heat Syndrome; summerheat-heat pattern/syndrome; Summer heat pattern
V012-N03011.pinyinNames: shu re zheng; shǔ rè zhèng
V012-N03011.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, dizziness, sweating, lassitude, chest tightness, and scanty, yellow urine. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when summer heat or pestilence attacks the body to cause a severe struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors or a depletion of qi and fluids.

V012-N03012.label: 暑耗气阴证
V012-N03012.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03012.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03012.aliases: 暑耗肺胃气阴证； 暑伤津气证； 暑伤气津证； Summer heat consuming qi and fluids pattern
V012-N03012.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of summer-heat consuming qi and yin; Summer heat consuming qi and yin pattern
V012-N03012.pinyinNames: shu hao qi yin zheng; shǔ hào qì yīn zhèng
V012-N03012.definitions: Characterized by persistent high fever, thirst with a desire to drink water, dry skin, lassitude, and shortness of breath. Alternatively, gastric discomfort, abdominal distension, frequent vomiting, and diarrhoea may be present. The tongue is dry and red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when summer heat or pestilence consumes qi and damages yin.

V012-N03013.label: 毒入营血证
V012-N03013.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03013.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03013.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of toxin entering the nutrient and blood; Toxin entering the Ying-nutrients blood pattern
V012-N03013.pinyinNames: dong ru ying xue zheng; dú rù yíng xuè zhèng
V012-N03013.definitions: Characterized by a high fever, excessive thirst, unconsciousness, delirium and dark purple maculae or bleeding of dark red blood. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when toxic fire/heat enters the Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N03014.label: 毒陷心肝证
V012-N03014.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03014.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03014.englishNames: Toxin entering the heart and liver pattern; syndrome/pattern of toxin sinking into the heart and liver
V012-N03014.pinyinNames: dú xiàn xīn gān zhèng; dong xian xin gan zheng
V012-N03014.definitions: Characterized by fever, unconsciousness, delirium, convulsions of the limbs, and dark purple skin rashes. Alternatively, nosebleed or a dark purple complexion may be present. The tongue is dark red. The pulse is deep, thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when toxin enters the heart and liver.

V012-N03015.label: 气不摄血证
V012-N03015.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03015.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03015.aliases: 气不统血证； 气虚血溢证； Qi failing to control blood pattern
V012-N03015.englishNames: Qi failing to hold blood pattern; pattern/syndrome of qi failing to control the blood; syndrome/pattern of qi failing to control blood
V012-N03015.pinyinNames: qi bu she xue zheng; qì bù shè xuè zhèng
V012-N03015.definitions: Characterized by bloody stools, subcutaneous haemorrhage, nosebleed, gum bleed, uterine bleeding, heavy menstruation, lassitude, shortness of breath, reluctance to talk and a lustreless complexion. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. Often occurs when qi fails to control blood within the vessels.

V012-N03016.label: 气分湿热证
V012-N03016.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03016.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03016.aliases: Qi phase dampness and heat pattern
V012-N03016.englishNames: qi aspect dampnessheat pattern/ syndrome; Qi phase dampness and heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in qi phase; Damp heat in the qi phase
V012-N03016.pinyinNames: qi fen shi re zheng; qì fèn shī rè zhèng
V012-N03016.definitions: Characterized by unresolved fever after sweating, chest tightness, abdominal distension, yellowish pigmentation of the skin and eyes, limb weakness, nausea, vomiting and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and rapid or slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the qi phase.

V012-N03017.label: 气分证
V012-N03017.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03017.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03017.aliases: 气分热盛证； 气分热盛； 热炽气分
V012-N03017.englishNames: Qi phase pattern; Qi phase patterns; syndrome/pattern of qi phase; qi aspect pattern/ syndrome; Qifen Syndrome
V012-N03017.pinyinNames: qì fèn zhèng; qi fen zheng
V012-N03017.definitions: Characterized by fever without aversion to cold. The tongue coating turns yellow. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic warm transforms into heat.

V012-N03018.label: 气滞水停证
V012-N03018.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03018.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03019.label: 气滞湿阻证
V012-N03019.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03019.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03019.aliases: 气滞湿困证； 湿阻气滞证
V012-N03019.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation and dampness obstruction; Qi stagnation with dampness retention pattern
V012-N03019.pinyinNames: qi zhi shi zu zheng; qì zhì shī zǔ zhèng
V012-N03019.definitions: Characterized by distension, tightness and migratory pain in the chest, subcostal region and abdomen, nausea, vomiting, body heaviness, dizziness and drowsiness. Oedema may also be present. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery, or soft and delayed. Often results from qi stagnation coupled with internal retention of turbid dampness.

V012-N03020.label: 气滞痰凝证
V012-N03020.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03020.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03020.aliases: 气郁痰凝证； 气滞郁痰阻证； 气滞痰阻证； 气郁痰阻证
V012-N03020.englishNames: Qi stagnation with phlegm retention pattern; syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation with phlegm congealation
V012-N03020.pinyinNames: qi zhi tan ning zheng; qì zhì tán níng zhèng
V012-N03020.definitions: Characterized by distension, tightness and migratory pain in the chest, subcostal region, and abdomen. Alternatively, masses, skin hardness/numbness, depression and white phlegm may be present. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery. Often results from qi stagnation coupled with internal retention of turbid phlegm.

V012-N03021.label: 气滞血瘀证
V012-N03021.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03021.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03021.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation and blood stasis; Qi stagnation and blood stasis pattern; pattern/syndrome of qi stagnation and blood stasis
V012-N03021.pinyinNames: qi zhi xue yu zheng; qì zhì xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03021.definitions: Characterized by stabbing or migratorymigratory pain in a fixed location. Movable masses may be present. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when qi stagnation affects the flow of blood.

V012-N03022.label: 气滞证
V012-N03022.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03022.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03022.aliases: 气机郁结证； 气结证； 气郁证； 气机阻滞证
V012-N03022.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation; qi stagnation pattern/ syndrome; Qi stagnation pattern
V012-N03022.pinyinNames: qi zhi zheng; qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03022.definitions: Characterized by migratory distension, fullness and pain in the subcostal region and abdomen that alleviate after sighing, belching, bowel sounds and flatus. The pulse is wiry. Often results from qi stagnation of the zang–fu organs or in the localized area.

V012-N03023.label: 气脱证
V012-N03023.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03023.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03023.aliases: 元气虚脱证； 元气衰败证
V012-N03023.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi collapse; Qi collapse pattern; qi collapse pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03023.pinyinNames: qi tuo zheng; qì tuō zhèng
V012-N03023.definitions: Characterized by a sudden pale complexion, cyanotic lips, sweating, cold limbs and faint breathing. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often results from sudden and rapid leaking of qi.

V012-N03024.label: 气营两燔证
V012-N03024.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03024.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03024.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of dual blaze of qinutrient aspects; syndrome/pattern of blazing fire in the qi phase and nutrient phase; Heat blazing in both qi and Ying phases pattern
V012-N03024.pinyinNames: qi ying liang fan zheng; qì yíng liǎng fán zhèng
V012-N03024.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, restlessness, and headache. Alternatively, unconsciousness, maculae, constipation, and yellow urine may be present. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when qi phase pattern co-exists with Ying nutrients phase pattern in febrile conditions.

V012-N03025.label: 气虚发热证
V012-N03025.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03025.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03025.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency with fever; Qi deficiency induced fever pattern; qi deficiency fever pattern/syndrome
V012-N03025.pinyinNames: qi xu fa re zheng; qì xū fā rè zhèng
V012-N03025.definitions: Characterized by a persistent low-grade fever that aggravates upon fatigue, low food intake, fatigue, shortness of breath, and reluctance to talk. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. Often results from qi deficiency and floating of yang qi.

V012-N03026.label: 气虚外感证
V012-N03026.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03026.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03026.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of qi deficiency with external contraction; Qi deficiency with external contraction pattern
V012-N03026.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, spontaneous sweating, headache, nasal obstruction, a low, weak voice, shortness of breath and lassitude. The pulse is superficial and weak. Often results from constitutional deficiency, insecure Wei-defence and exposure to external pathogenic factors.

V012-N03027.label: 气虚气滞证
V012-N03027.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03027.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03027.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency and stagnation; Qi deficiency induced qi stagnation pattern
V012-N03027.pinyinNames: qi xu qi zhi zheng; qì xū qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03027.definitions: Characterized by lassitude, shortness of breath, fatigue, and distension, tightness and migratory pain in the chest or abdomen. The tongue is pale. The pulse is wiry and delayed. Often occurs when qi fails to move and causes qi stagnation.

V012-N03028.label: 气虚湿困证
V012-N03028.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03028.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03028.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency with dampness encumbrance; Qi deficiency with dampness retention pattern
V012-N03028.pinyinNames: qi xu shi kun zheng; qì xū shī kùn zhèng
V012-N03028.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, fatigue, body heaviness and chest tightness. Abdominal distension and diarrhoea may also be present. The tongue is enlarged with a greasy coating. The pulse is soft. Often results from qi deficiency coupled with dampness retention.

V012-N03029.label: 气虚痰结证
V012-N03029.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03029.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03029.aliases: 气虚痰阻证； 气虚痰滞证
V012-N03029.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency with phlegm binding; Qi deficiency with phlegm retention pattern
V012-N03029.pinyinNames: qi xu tan jie zheng; qì xū tán jié zhèng
V012-N03029.definitions: Characterized by shortness of breath, fatigue, cough with phlegm, mental confusion, and skin numbness. The tongue coating is greasy. The pulse is slippery. Alternatively, goiter or scrofula may be present. Often results from qi deficiency coupled with internal retention of turbid phlegm.

V012-N03030.label: 气虚血瘀证
V012-N03030.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03030.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03030.aliases: 气虚血凝证； 气虚血滞证； 气虚血涩证； 气虚瘀滞证
V012-N03030.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of qi deficiency with blood stasis; syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency with blood stasis; Qi deficiency induced blood stasis pattern
V012-N03030.pinyinNames: qi xu xue yu zheng; qì xū xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03030.definitions: Characterized by a pale, dark-grey complexion, fatigue, shallow breathing, reluctance to talk, and severe pain with a fixed location. The tongue is pale purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is deep and hesitant. Often occurs when qi fails to circulate blood.

V012-N03031.label: 气虚证
V012-N03031.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03031.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03031.englishNames: qi deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency
V012-N03031.pinyinNames: qi xu zheng; qì xū zhèng
V012-N03031.definitions: Characterized by shortness of breath, fatigue, lassitude, reluctance to talk, and spontaneous sweating. The tongue is pale. The pulse is deficient. Often results from hypofunctions of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03032.label: 气血两燔证
V012-N03032.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03032.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03032.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exuberant fire in qi phase and blood phase; pattern/syndrome of dual blaze of qi-blood aspects; Heat blazing in both qi and blood phases pattern
V012-N03032.pinyinNames: qi xue liang fan zheng; qì xuè liǎng fán zhèng
V012-N03032.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, headache, restlessness, maculae, hematemesis, nosebleed and bloody stools. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when heat in the qi phase remains unresolved, while toxic heat stays in the Ying nutrients or blood phases.

V012-N03033.label: 气血两虚证
V012-N03033.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03033.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03033.englishNames: Qi and blood deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of qi and blood; pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of qi and blood
V012-N03033.pinyinNames: qì xuè liǎng xū zhèng; qi xue liang xu zheng
V012-N03033.definitions: Characterized by lassitude, shortness of breath, reluctance to talk, a pale white or sallow complexion, dizziness, blurred vision, pale lips and fingernails, palpitations and insomnia. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. Often occurs when qi and blood fail to nourish the body.

V012-N03034.label: 气逆证
V012-N03034.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03034.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03034.aliases: 气机上逆证； 气机横逆证
V012-N03034.englishNames: Qi uprising pattern; Qi counterflow pattern; qi counterflow pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of qi counterflow
V012-N03034.pinyinNames: qì nì zhèng; qi ni zheng
V012-N03034.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, vomiting, belching, hiccups, and acid reflux. In severe cases, sudden fainting or panting may be present. Often results from counterflow of qi or disordered qi activity.

V012-N03035.label: 气郁化火证
V012-N03035.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03035.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03035.aliases: 气滞化火证； 气滞化热证； 气郁化热证
V012-N03035.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi depression transforming into fire; pattern/syndrome of depressed qi transforming into fire; Qi stagnation transformed fire pattern
V012-N03035.pinyinNames: qi yu hua huo zheng; qì yù huà huǒ zhèng
V012-N03035.definitions: Characterized by depression, restlessness, irritability, distension, tightness and burning pain in the subcostal region, and a dry, bitter mouth. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. Often occurs when qi stagnation transforms into fire.

V012-N03036.label: 气闭证
V012-N03036.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03036.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03036.aliases: 气机壅闭证； 气机闭塞证
V012-N03036.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi block; Qi blockage pattern; qi block pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03036.pinyinNames: qi bi zheng; qì bì zhèng
V012-N03036.definitions: Characterized by colic or paroxysmal abdominal pain, absence of bowel sounds or flatus, and obstruction of the bladder and bowel movements. Alternatively, sudden fainting, trismus and limb rigidity may be present. Often results from blockage of qi activity.

V012-N03037.label: 气阴两虚证
V012-N03037.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03037.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03037.aliases: 气阴亏虚证； 气阴两亏证； Insufficiency of qi and yin pattern
V012-N03037.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of qi and yin; syndrome/pattern of qi and yin deficiency; Qi and yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03037.pinyinNames: qi yin liang xu zheng; qì yīn liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03037.definitions: Characterized by lassitude, shortness of breath, reluctance to talk, a dry mouth and throat, tidal fever, spontaneous sweating, night sweats, scanty urine, and constipation. The tongue is red or enlarged with a dry, scanty coating. The pulse is thready, weak or deficient and rapid. Often results from qi deficiency coupled with consumption of yin fluids.

V012-N03038.label: 气陷证
V012-N03038.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03038.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03038.englishNames: Qi sinking pattern; qi sinking pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of qi sinking
V012-N03038.pinyinNames: qi xian zheng; qì xiàn zhèng
V012-N03038.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, lassitude, a bearing-down sensation in the abdomen, rectal prolapse, and prolapsed internal organs or uterus. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is weak. Often occurs when qi fails to ascend.

V012-N03039.label: 气随血脱证
V012-N03039.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03039.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03039.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi collapse following blood loss; pattern/syndrome of qi collapse following bleeding; Blood loss-induced qi collapse pattern
V012-N03039.pinyinNames: qi sui xue tuo zheng; qì suí xuè tuō zhèng
V012-N03039.definitions: Characterized by a pale complexion, cold limbs, profuse sweating, and weak breathing. In severe cases, fainting may be present. The pulse is extremely feeble, or deficient and large. Often occurs when heavy blood loss causes qi to collapse.

V012-N03040.label: 水气凌心证
V012-N03040.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03040.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03040.aliases: 肾水凌心证； 心肾阳虚水泛证
V012-N03040.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of water qi intimidating the heart; pattern/syndrome of water qi intimidating the heart; Water retention affecting the heart pattern; Water qi intimidating the heart system pattern
V012-N03040.pinyinNames: shui qi ling xin zheng; shuǐ qì líng xīn zhèng
V012-N03040.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, leg oedema, palpitations, an inability to lie flat, and cough with thin, white sputum. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when water retention (due to yang qi deficiency of the heart and kidney) affects the heart.

V012-N03041.label: 水饮证
V012-N03041.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03041.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03041.aliases: 水饮内停证； Internal retention of water fluids pattern
V012-N03041.definitions: Characterized by inhibited urination, lower abdominal distension/fullness, thirst, palpitations, dizziness, cough, panting, oedema, cold limbs and diarrhoea. The tongue is enlarged with a slippery coating. Often occurs when abnormal distribution of bodily fluids causes water–damp retention.

V012-N03042.label: 津亏热结证
V012-N03042.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03042.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03042.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fluid depletion and heat binding; Fluids deficiency with heat accumulation pattern
V012-N03042.pinyinNames: jin kui re jie zheng; jīn kuī rè jié zhèng
V012-N03042.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, dry lips and tongue, inhibited urination, constipation and restlessness. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. Often results from fluids insufficiency coupled with internal retention of heat.

V012-N03043.label: 津液亏虚证
V012-N03043.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03043.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03043.aliases: 津液亏损证； 伤津证； Fluid deficiency pattern
V012-N03043.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fluid and humor depletion; Fluid deficiency pattern; fluid-humor deficiency pattern/syndrome; Fluids deficiency pattern
V012-N03043.pinyinNames: jin ye kui xu zheng; jīn yè kuī xū zhèng
V012-N03043.definitions: Characterized by dry mouth, nose, throat and lips, thirst with a desire to drink water, scanty urine and constipation. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready, rapid and weak. Often occurs when bodily fluids fail to nourish the zang–fu organs and tissues.

V012-N03044.label: 液脱证
V012-N03044.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03044.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03044.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of humor collapse; humor collapse pattern/syndrome; Fluids loss pattern
V012-N03044.pinyinNames: ye tuo zheng; yè tuō zhèng
V012-N03044.definitions: Characterized by weight loss, dry, cracked lips, dry, withered skin, sunken eyes, impaired joint movement, scanty urination and constipation. The tongue is dry. The pulse is thready and weak. Often results from a severe deficiency of bodily fluids.

V012-N03045.label: 温毒袭表证
V012-N03045.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03045.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03045.aliases: 热毒袭表证； 火毒袭表证
V012-N03045.englishNames: Warm toxin attacking the exterior pattern
V012-N03045.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to cold, body aches (like being beaten with a stick), and splitting headache. The tongue is red with a yellow, white coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid. Often occurs when fire/heat or pestilence attacks the Wei-defensive exterior.

V012-N03046.label: 温燥证
V012-N03046.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03046.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03046.aliases: 燥热证； 温燥伤肺证； 温燥袭肺证； Warm dryness damaging/attacking the lung pattern
V012-N03046.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of warm dryness; warm dryness pattern/syndrome; Warm dryness pattern
V012-N03046.pinyinNames: wen zao zheng; wēn zào zhèng
V012-N03046.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to wind cold, unproductive cough, thirst, dry skin/nose/throat, and scanty, yellow urine. The tongue coating is thin and yellow. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when warm dryness consumes yin fluids.

V012-N03047.label: 温邪侵袭肺卫证
V012-N03047.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03047.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03047.aliases: 毒侵肺卫证； Heat attacking the lung defence pattern； Warm attacking the lung defence pattern； Pathogenic warmth affecting the Wei-defence
V012-N03047.englishNames: Heat attacking the lung defense pattern; Toxin attacking the lung defence
V012-N03047.pinyinNames: wēn xié qīn xí fèi wèi zhèng
V012-N03047.definitions: Characterized by fever, headache, mild aversion to wind cold, mild/no sweating, cough, mild thirst and sore throat. The tongue coating is thin white or yellow. The pulse is floating and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic warm heat attacks the Wei-defence and affects the dispersing of lung qi.

V012-N03048.label: 湿伤脾阳证
V012-N03048.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03048.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03048.aliases: Dampness affecting the middle jiao pattern
V012-N03048.definitions: Characterized by gastric discomfort, abdominal distension, a greasy taste in the mouth, nausea, vomiting, loss of sense of taste, absence of thirst, abdominal pain, loose stools, and head/body heaviness. Alternatively, dark, yellowish eyes and body skin may be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and delayed. Often occurs when retained dampness impairs spleen yang.

V012-N03049.label: 湿困脾胃证
V012-N03049.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03049.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03049.aliases: Dampness weakening the spleen and stomach； 湿阻脾胃证
V012-N03049.englishNames: Dampness affecting the spleen and stomach pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness impacting the spleen and stomach
V012-N03049.pinyinNames: shī kùn pí wèi zhèng; shi kun pi wei zheng
V012-N03049.definitions: Characterized by stomach stuffiness, abdominal distension, a greasy taste, poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, tastelessness, absence of thirst, abdominal pain, loose stools and a heavy sensation of the head and body. Yellow eyes and body skin may also be present. The tongue is pale and swollen with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and delayed. This pattern often occurs when internal turbid dampness obstructs the middle jiao.

V012-N03050.label: 湿毒证
V012-N03050.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03050.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03050.aliases: 湿毒蕴结证； Accumulation of damp toxin pattern
V012-N03050.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness toxin; Damp toxin pattern; dampness toxin pattern/syndrome
V012-N03050.pinyinNames: shi dong zheng; shī dú zhèng
V012-N03050.definitions: Characterized by chronic, progressive development of ulcerative oozing that is difficult to heal. Often occurs when long-term dampness transforms into toxins.

V012-N03051.label: 湿热下注证
V012-N03051.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03051.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03051.englishNames: Damp heat downflow pattern; pattern/syndrome of dampness-heat pouring downward; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat pouring downwards
V012-N03051.pinyinNames: shi re xia zhu zheng; shī rè xià zhù zhèng
V012-N03051.definitions: Characterized by frequent, urgent, dribbling or painful urination. Alternatively, strong-smelling, loose stools, yellow, smelly vaginal discharge, eczema and itching of the external genitalia, and leg sores/ulcers may be present. The tongue coating is yellow and greasy. Often occurs when damp heat flows downward to affect the intestines, urinary bladder, uterus, external genitalia, and legs.

V012-N03052.label: 湿热困脾证
V012-N03052.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03052.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03052.aliases: 湿热蕴脾证； Damp heat accumulating in the spleen pattern
V012-N03052.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of damp-heat encumbering the  spleen; Damp heat affecting the spleen pattern; syndrome/pattern of damp-heat encumbering the spleen
V012-N03052.pinyinNames: shi re kun pi zheng; shī rè kùn pí zhèng
V012-N03052.definitions: Characterized by abdominal fullness/upset, a poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation, body heaviness, thirst but drink a small amount of water, feverish sensation (but no fever) that cannot be resolved after sweating. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and rapid. This pattern often occurs when internal damp heat affects the functioning of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03053.label: 湿热弥漫三焦证
V012-N03053.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03053.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03053.aliases: 三焦湿热证； Damp heat affecting triple energizer pattern
V012-N03053.englishNames: Damp heat affecting the three jiao pattern
V012-N03053.pinyinNames: shī rè mí màn sān jiāo zhèng
V012-N03053.definitions: Characterized by fever that cannot be resolved after sweating, body heaviness, gastric discomfort and masses, absence of thirst or thirst with little water intake, scanty, yellow urine and a sensation of incomplete evacuation after bowel movements. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp is mixed with heat.

V012-N03054.label: 湿热蕴肤证
V012-N03054.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03054.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03054.aliases: 肌肤湿热证； 湿热蕴结肌肤证
V012-N03054.englishNames: Retained damp heat in the skin pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat retaining in the skin
V012-N03054.pinyinNames: shī rè yùn fū zhèng; shi re yun fu zheng
V012-N03054.definitions: Characterized by skin redness, swelling, ulcers, itching, and oozing. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when damp heat affects the surface of the body.

V012-N03055.label: 湿热蕴肾证
V012-N03055.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03055.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03055.englishNames: Damp heat accumulating in the kidney pattern
V012-N03055.definitions: Characterized by a burning distension/pain in the low back, hesitant, painful urination, bloody urine (possibly pyuria), fever, thirst or yellow, sticky vaginal discharge. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat retains in the kidney.

V012-N03056.label: 湿热证
V012-N03056.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03056.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03056.aliases: 湿热蕴结证； Damp heat accumulation pattern
V012-N03056.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat; dampness-heat pattern/syndrome; Damp heat pattern
V012-N03056.pinyinNames: shi re zheng; shī rè zhèng
V012-N03056.definitions: Characterized by inhibited body fever, body heaviness, gastric discomfort, thirst with little water intake/absence of thirst, scanty, yellow urine and loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when damp binds with heat, resulting in difficulty clearing heat and resolving dampness.

V012-N03057.label: 湿热阻痹证
V012-N03057.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03057.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03057.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat obstruction and impediment; Damp heat affecting the bones/joints pattern
V012-N03057.pinyinNames: shi re zu bi zheng; shī rè zǔ bì zhèng
V012-N03057.definitions: Characterized by fever and swollen, heavy, hot and painful joints. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat accumulates in tendons, bones and joints.

V012-N03058.label: 湿热阻络证
V012-N03058.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03058.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03058.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat obstructing collaterals; Damp heat obstructing the meridians pattern
V012-N03058.pinyinNames: shi re zu luo zheng; shī rè zǔ luò zhèng
V012-N03058.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild thirst, limb heaviness, pain and numbness, and localized ulceration and itching. The tongue coating is yellow and greasy. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the meridians.

V012-N03059.label: 湿痰证
V012-N03059.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03059.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03059.aliases: 痰湿证； 痰湿阻滞内阻证； 痰湿阻滞证； 痰湿内阻证
V012-N03059.definitions: Characterized by cough with easy expectoration of profuse, sticky phlegm, limb heaviness, dizziness, tinnitus, chest stuffiness, and tightness, a low food intake, a greasy taste and a foreign body sensation in the throat. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is soft and slippery. Often results from the internal build up of phlegm dampness.

V012-N03060.label: 湿遏卫阳证
V012-N03060.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03060.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03060.aliases: Dampness obstructing the defence yang pattern
V012-N03060.englishNames: Dampness obstructing the defense yang pattern; Dampness blocking Wei-defensive yang pattern; pattern/syndrome of dampness obstructing defense yang
V012-N03060.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to cold, mild sweating, headache, lassitude, chest tightness, a poor appetite and absence of thirst. The tongue coating is thin, white or greasy. The pulse is delayed. This pattern often occurs when dampness blocks the flow of Wei-defensive qi.

V012-N03061.label: 湿阻气分证
V012-N03061.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03061.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03061.aliases: 湿阻证； 湿邪阻滞证； Dampness obstructing the qi phase pattern
V012-N03061.englishNames: Dampness obstructing the qi phase pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness obstruction in qi phase; Dampness obstructing the qi phase
V012-N03061.pinyinNames: shi zu qi fen zheng; shī zǔ qì fèn zhèng
V012-N03061.definitions: Characterized by fever that cannot be resolved by sweating, thirst with no desire to drink water, chest or gastric discomfort, nausea, vomiting, and a sensation of incomplete evacuation after bowel movements. Associated symptoms may include diarrhoea, body heaviness and fatigue. The tongue is red with a slightly yellow, slippery and greasy coating. The pulse is soft and rapid. This pattern often occurs when dampness affects the qi phase.

V012-N03062.label: 湿阻肠道证
V012-N03062.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03062.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03062.aliases: 湿滞肠道证； 湿蕴肠道证
V012-N03062.englishNames: Dampness in the intestines pattern; Dampness obstructing the large intestine pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness obstructing the intestines
V012-N03062.pinyinNames: shī zǔ cháng dào zhèng; shi zu chang dao zheng
V012-N03062.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension/dull pain, and thin, clear and loose stools or sticky, strong-odoured stools. The tongue coating is white and slippery. The pulse is soft and slack. This pattern often occurs when dampness affects the conduction of the large intestine.

V012-N03063.label: 火毒证
V012-N03063.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03063.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03063.aliases: 热毒证； 温毒证； 热毒炽盛证； 邪热炽盛证； 火热炽盛证； Exuberance of toxic/pathogenic/fire heat
V012-N03063.englishNames: Fire toxin pattern; fire toxin pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of fire toxin
V012-N03063.pinyinNames: huo dong zheng; huǒ dú zhèng
V012-N03063.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, ulcerative and painful skin boils with a burning sensation, fever, and thirst. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when fire heat transforms into toxins.

V012-N03064.label: 热入气分证
V012-N03064.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03064.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03064.aliases: Heat entering the qi phase pattern
V012-N03064.englishNames: Heat entering the qi phase pattern; Heat entering the qi phase
V012-N03064.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, dark yellow urine, and constipation. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when toxic fire/heat struggles with anti-pathogenic qi in the qi phase.

V012-N03065.label: 热入营分证
V012-N03065.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03065.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03065.aliases: Heat in the nutrient phase pattern
V012-N03065.englishNames: Heat in the nutrient phase pattern; Heat entering the Ying nutrients phase pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat entering the nutrient phase
V012-N03065.pinyinNames: rè rù yíng fèn zhèng; re ru ying fen zheng
V012-N03065.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates at night, restlessness, insomnia, unconsciousness, delirium and maculae. The tongue is deep red. This pattern often occurs when toxic fire/heat enters the Ying nutrients phase and disturbs the heart mind.

V012-N03066.label: 热入营血证
V012-N03066.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03066.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03066.aliases: 热灼营血证； Heat entering the nutrient and blood phase pattern
V012-N03066.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of heat entering the nutrient and blood phases; Heat entering the nutrient and blood phase pattern; pattern/syndrome of heat entering nutrient-blood aspects; Heat entering the Ying nutrients and blood phases pattern
V012-N03066.pinyinNames: re ru ying xue zheng; rè rù yíng xuè zhèng
V012-N03066.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates at night, restlessness and insomnia. Alternatively, unconsciousness, thirst with little water intake, maculae, bleeding, constipation and yellow urine may be present. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic warm/heat consumes yin blood and disturbs the heart mind.

V012-N03067.label: 热入血分证
V012-N03067.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03067.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03067.aliases: 热炽血分证； 热在血分证； Excess heat in the blood aspect pattern； Blood and heat pattern
V012-N03067.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of heat entering the blood aspect; Heat entering the blood phase pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat entering the blood phase
V012-N03067.pinyinNames: re ru xue fen zheng; rè rù xuè fèn zhèng
V012-N03067.definitions: Characterized by haemorrhagic symptoms such as haemoptysis, nosebleed, bloody urine and bloody stools. Alternatively, a high-grade fever, unconsciousness and twitching of hands/feet may be present. The tongue is dark purple. This pattern often occurs when fire/heat consumes Ying nutrients and blood.

V012-N03068.label: 热入血室证
V012-N03068.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03068.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03068.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of heat entering blood chamber; Heat entering the blood chamber pattern
V012-N03068.definitions: Characterized by a burning pain in the lower abdomen, tenderness, and profuse menstrual volume. Alternatively, amenorrhoea, fever, thirst and restlessness may be present. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when heat affects the blood chamber.

V012-N03069.label: 热实结胸证
V012-N03069.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03069.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03069.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of excess-heat binding in the chest; Exuberant heat affecting the chest pattern
V012-N03069.pinyinNames: re shi jie xiong zheng; rè shí jié xiōng zhèng
V012-N03069.definitions: Characterized by pain below the heart with tenderness, constipation, and fever or waves of fever. Alternatively, chest or abdominal distension/pain and cough may be present. The tongue coating is yellow. The pulse is deep and wiry. Often occurs when pathogenic factors affect the heart/chest due to inappropriate treatment of Taiyang disease. It may also occur as a result of phlegm-fluid retention in the thoracic diaphragm.

V012-N03070.label: 热扰心神证
V012-N03070.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03070.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03070.aliases: 火扰心神证； Fire harassing heart spirit pattern； Fire disturbing the heart mind
V012-N03070.englishNames: Fire harassing heart spirit pattern; Heat disturbing the heart spirit pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat disturbing the heart spirit; pattern/syndrome of heat harassing the heart spirit
V012-N03070.pinyinNames: re rao xin shen zheng; rè rǎo xīn shén zhèng
V012-N03070.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, restlessness, insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep. Delirium, fever, thirst and a red face may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant excessive heat disturbs the heart spirit.

V012-N03071.label: 热毒入营证
V012-N03071.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03071.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03071.aliases: 邪毒炽盛证； Exuberance of toxic fire/heat pattern； 热毒陷营证； 火毒入营证； 火毒陷营证
V012-N03071.englishNames: Heat toxin entering the Ying nutrients phase pattern
V012-N03071.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates at night, maculae, unconsciousness, delirium, a dry mouth, thirst and constipation. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when toxic fire/heat enters the Ying nutrients and blood phases.

V012-N03072.label: 热毒内陷证
V012-N03072.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03072.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03072.aliases: 热毒内闭证； 火毒内陷证； 火毒内闭证； Interior fire toxin pattern； fire/heat toxin blocking the interior pattern
V012-N03072.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, unconsciousness, delirium, a dark red complexion, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is dark red with a yellow coating. The pulse is deep and rapid. Often occurs when exuberant fire/heat toxins enters the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03073.label: 热毒淤肝证
V012-N03073.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03073.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03073.aliases: 热毒瘀肝证； Heat toxin retention in the liver pattern
V012-N03073.englishNames: Heat toxin affecting the liver pattern
V012-N03073.definitions: Characterized by burning pain or masses in the subcostal region, a high-grade fever, thirst, yellowish skin and eyes, and a red face. In severe cases, mental unconsciousness may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when toxic fire/heat remains in the liver.

V012-N03074.label: 热毒蕴肤证
V012-N03074.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03074.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03074.aliases: 火毒蕴结肌肤证； Fire toxin accumulating in the skin pattern
V012-N03074.englishNames: Retained heat toxin in the skin pattern
V012-N03074.definitions: Characterized by red, swelling and painful skin sores/ulcers/boils with a burning sensation. Alternatively, thirst and constipation may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when toxic fire heat accumulates or remains in the skin.

V012-N03075.label: 热痰证
V012-N03075.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03075.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03076.label: 热盛伤津证
V012-N03076.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03076.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03076.aliases: 热伤津液证； 火热伤津证； 热盛津枯证； 热盛耗液证； Exuberant heat consuming fluids pattern
V012-N03076.englishNames: Exuberant heat damaging fluids pattern; syndrome/pattern of exuberant heat damaging fluid
V012-N03076.pinyinNames: re sheng shang jin zheng; rè shèng shāng jīn zhèng
V012-N03076.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst with a desire to drink water, dry, withered skin, sunken eyes, dry stools, and scanty, yellow urine. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when exuberant fire/heat consumes or damages bodily fluids.

V012-N03077.label: 热盛伤阴证
V012-N03077.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03077.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03077.aliases: 火热伤阴证； 邪热伤阴证； Pathogenic heat damaging yin pattern
V012-N03077.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exuberant heat damaging yin; Exuberant heat damaging yin pattern
V012-N03077.pinyinNames: re sheng shang yin zheng; rè shèng shāng yīn zhèng
V012-N03077.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst with a desire to drink cold water, dry stools, and scanty, dark yellow urine. The tongue is dry and red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when exuberant fire/heat damages yin fluids.

V012-N03078.label: 热盛动血证
V012-N03078.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03078.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03078.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exuberant heat stirring blood; Exuberant heat moving blood pattern; pattern/syndrome of exuberant heat with bleeding
V012-N03078.pinyinNames: re sheng dong xue zheng; rè shèng dòng xuè zhèng
V012-N03078.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, and red face and eyes. Alternatively, bloody urine, bloody stools, nosebleed or maculae may be present. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is surging and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat causes the blood to flow recklessly.

V012-N03079.label: 热盛动风证
V012-N03079.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03079.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03079.aliases: 热极动风证； 热极生风证； 热盛生风证
V012-N03079.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exuberant heat stirring wind; Exuberant heat stirring wind pattern; pattern/syndrome of exuberant heat stirring wind
V012-N03079.pinyinNames: re sheng dong feng zheng; rè shèng dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03079.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, unconsciousness, convulsions of hands/feet, neck rigidity, opisthotonos and trismus. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat stirs liver wind.

V012-N03080.label: 热证
V012-N03080.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03080.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03080.aliases: 热毒蕴结肌肤； 热扰胸膈； 热盛动风； 热盛动血； 热盛酿脓； 热盛气滞； 热实结胸； 热重于风； 热重于湿； 少腹热滞
V012-N03080.englishNames: Heat Syndrome; heat syndrome/pattern; heat pattern/ syndrome; Heat pattern
V012-N03080.definitions: Characterized by fever, a red face, thirst, constipation and yellow or red urine. Alternatively, malar flush, night sweats, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, a dry, red tongue and a thready, rapid pulse may be present. It is sub-categorized into patterns of exterior heat, interior heat, deficiency heat and excess heat. Often results from contracting pathogenic heat or hyperactivity of yang due to yin deficiency.

V012-N03081.label: 热邪阻痹证
V012-N03081.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03081.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03081.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, hot and painful joints. This pattern often occurs when wind, dampness and heat, especially heat, affect the tendons, bones and joints.

V012-N03082.label: 热闭心包证
V012-N03082.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03082.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03082.aliases: 热闭心神证； Heat blocking the heart mind pattern； 热陷心包证； 热入心包证
V012-N03082.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, and mental unconsciousness. Delirium, mania, a red face and rapid breathing may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant excessive heat disturbs the heart spirit.

V012-N03083.label: 燥毒证
V012-N03083.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03083.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03083.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dryness toxin; Dryness toxin pattern
V012-N03083.pinyinNames: zao dong zheng; zào dú zhèng
V012-N03083.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, sore and ulcerative throat, constipation, dark yellow urine, thirst with a desire to drink water and dry nose and skin. Alternatively, red, ulcerative eyes may be present. The tongue is dry. The pulse is thready and hesitant. Often occurs when toxic dry heat attacks the body.

V012-N03084.label: 燥痰证
V012-N03084.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03084.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03084.aliases: 燥痰蕴结证； Accumulation of dry phlegm
V012-N03084.englishNames: Dry phlegm pattern; dryness-phlegm pattern/syndrome; Dry-phlegm pattern
V012-N03084.definitions: Characterized by cough with difficult expectoration of sticky or blood-stained phlegm, chest tightness/pain, and a dry mouth and nose. The tongue is dry with a greasy coating. The pulse is hesitant. Often results from internal build up of dry heat and turbid phlegm.

V012-N03085.label: 疫毒内闭证
V012-N03085.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03085.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03085.englishNames: Epidemic toxin blocking the heart spirit pattern; syndrome/pattern of pestilent toxin blocking the internal
V012-N03085.pinyinNames: yi dong nei bi zheng; yì dú nèi bì zhèng
V012-N03085.definitions: Characterized by unconsciousness, delirium, sound of phlegm in the throat, difficulty breathing and constipation. Often occurs when epidemic toxin blocks the heart spirit.

V012-N03086.label: 病因证类
V012-N03086.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03086.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03087.label: 痰凝胞宫证
V012-N03087.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03087.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03087.aliases: 痰湿凝结胞宫证； 痰阻胞宫证； Phlegm obstructing the uterus pattern； Phlegm-dampness obstructing the uterus pattern； Phlegm congealment in the uterus pattern； Phlegm obstructing the uterus
V012-N03087.englishNames: Phlegm retention in the uterus pattern; Phlegm obstructing the uterus pattern
V012-N03087.definitions: Characterized by profuse, white vaginal discharge or amenorrhoea/infertility, obesity and fatigue. The tongue is pale with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery or soft and delayed. This pattern often occurs when phlegm-dampness obstructs the uterus.

V012-N03088.label: 痰核留结证
V012-N03088.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03088.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03088.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm nodule retention; pattern/syndrome of lingering phlegm nodule; Retained phlegm nodules pattern
V012-N03088.pinyinNames: tan he liu jie zheng; tán hé liú jié zhèng
V012-N03088.definitions: Characterized by painless, subcutaneous nodules on the neck and limbs. These nodules are hard, smooth and mobile. They do not appear red or feel warm. The tongue coating is greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery. Often occurs when turbid phlegm remains in the body.

V012-N03089.label: 痰气互结证
V012-N03089.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03089.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03089.aliases: 气滞痰结证； 气郁痰结证； 痰气郁结证； Intermingled phlegm and qi pattern
V012-N03089.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of mutual binding of phlegm and qi; pattern/syndrome of binding of phlegm and qi; Phlegm qi stagnation pattern
V012-N03089.pinyinNames: tan qi hu jie zheng; tán qì hù jié zhèng
V012-N03089.definitions: Characterized by emotional depression, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, a foreign body sensation in the throat, fullness, and tightness in the sub-costal region and profuse phlegm. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery. Often results from phlegm binding with qi.

V012-N03090.label: 痰气阻膈证
V012-N03090.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03090.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03090.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and qi obstructing the diaphragm; Phlegm qi obstructing the diaphragm pattern
V012-N03090.pinyinNames: tan qi zu ge zheng; tán qì zǔ gé zhèng
V012-N03090.definitions: Characterized by diaphragm fullness and tightness, a sensation of food being stuck or held up, hiccups, and vomiting of phlegm or saliva and the sensation is alleviated after belching. The tongue coating is greasy or slippery. The pulse is wiry. Often occurs when turbid phlegm obstructs the flow of qi in the diaphragm.

V012-N03091.label: 痰湿中阻证
V012-N03091.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03091.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03091.aliases: 痰浊中阻证； 痰饮中阻证； 痰湿蕴脾证； Phlegm turbidity obstructing the middle jiao； Phlegm fluid obstructing the middle jiao； Phelgm-dampness accumulating in the spleen pattern
V012-N03091.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and dampness obstructing the middle; Phlegm dampness accumulating in the spleen pattern
V012-N03091.pinyinNames: tan shi zhong zu zheng; tán shī zhōng zǔ zhèng
V012-N03091.definitions: Characterized by a greasy taste, a poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, abdominal masses/distension, splashing sounds in the stomach and loose stools. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and slack. This pattern often occurs when phlegm dampness obstructs the stomach and intestines.

V012-N03092.label: 痰湿蕴肺证
V012-N03092.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03092.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03092.aliases: 痰湿壅肺证； 痰湿阻肺证； 痰浊蕴肺证； 痰浊阻肺证； Turbid phlegm accumulation in the lung pattern； Phlegm-dampness obstructing the lung； phlegm dampness accumulating in the lung
V012-N03092.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and dampness accumulating in the lung 痰湿壅肺证; syndrome/pattern of phlegm and dampness accumulating in the lung; Phlegm dampness accumulating in the lung pattern
V012-N03092.pinyinNames: tan shi yun fei zheng; tán shī yùn fèi zhèng
V012-N03092.definitions: Characterized by cough with profuse phlegm (white and sticky/thick/thin and clear, especially in the morning, cough alleviates upon expectoration of phlegm), heavy, turbid coughing sounds, chest tightness, abdominal fullness/distension, and a poor appetite. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is soft and slippery. This pattern often occurs when phlegm-dampness affects the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N03093.label: 痰湿阻络证
V012-N03093.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03093.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03093.aliases: 痰湿阻痹证； Phlegm-dampness affecting the limbs/joints pattern
V012-N03093.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and dampness obstructing collaterals; Phlegm-dampness obstructing the meridians pattern
V012-N03093.pinyinNames: tan shi zu luo zheng; tán shī zǔ luò zhèng
V012-N03093.definitions: Characterized by limb or joint numbness and swelling, and skin swelling, hardness, numbness and itching. The tongue coating is white and greasy. This pattern often occurs when turbid phlegm-dampness obstructs the meridians.

V012-N03094.label: 痰火扰心证
V012-N03094.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03094.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03094.aliases: 痰火扰神证； 痰热扰神证； 痰热扰心证； Phlegm-fire harassing the heart system pattern； Phlegm fire/heat disturbing the mind； phlegm heat disturbing the heart
V012-N03094.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of phlegm-fire harassing the heart; Phlegm fire disturbing the heart pattern; Phlegm-fire harassing the heart system pattern
V012-N03094.definitions: Characterized by hyperactivity, talkativeness, restlessness, impulsiveness, uncontrollable behaviour, inattention, feverish sensation in the chest, vexation, a poor appetite, a bitter mouth, constipation, and dark-yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs as a result of an internal build-up of phlegm fire.

V012-N03095.label: 痰火闭窍证
V012-N03095.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03095.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03095.aliases: 痰热内闭证； Phlegm heat blocked the interior pattern
V012-N03095.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and fire blocking the orifices; Phlegm fire blocked the heart spirit pattern
V012-N03095.pinyinNames: tan huo bi qiao zheng; tán huǒ bì qiào zhèng
V012-N03095.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, a red face, rapid breathing, restlessness, mania, unconsciousness, delirium, sound of phlegm in the throat, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when intertwined fire heat and turbid phlegm blocks the heart mind.

V012-N03096.label: 痰热动风证
V012-N03096.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03096.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03096.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of phlegm- heat stirring wind; syndrome/pattern of phlegm and heat stirring wind; Phlegm heat stirring wind pattern
V012-N03096.pinyinNames: tan re dong feng zheng; tán rè dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03096.definitions: Characterized by distension and tightness in the subcostal region, cough, panting, fever, thirst, expectoration of yellow, sticky phlegm, sound of phlegm in the throat, convulsions of the limbs, dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and wiry. Often occurs when internal phlegm heat stirs liver wind.

V012-N03097.label: 痰热壅肺证
V012-N03097.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03097.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03097.aliases: 痰热蕴肺证； 痰火壅肺证； 痰火蕴肺证； 痰热闭肺证； Phlegm heat obstructing the lung pattern； Phlegm fire/heat accumulating in the lung； phlegm heat obstructing the lung
V012-N03097.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and heat congesting in the lung; Phlegm heat obstructing the lung pattern; Phlegm heat accumulating in the lung pattern
V012-N03097.pinyinNames: tan re yong fei zheng; tán rè yōng fèi zhèng
V012-N03097.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, cough, panting, expectoration of yellow, sticky phlegm, and chest tightness. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when phlegm heat accumulates in the lung.

V012-N03098.label: 痰热气滞证
V012-N03098.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03098.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03098.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation due to phlegm and heat; Phlegm heat stagnated qi pattern
V012-N03098.pinyinNames: tan re qi zhi zheng; tán rè qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03098.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, irritability, distension and tightness in the subcostal region, a burning wandering pain, coughing, panting, expectoration of yellow phlegm, fever, and thirst. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. Often occurs when phlegm heat obstructs the flow of qi.

V012-N03099.label: 痰热结胸证
V012-N03099.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03099.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03099.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and heat binding in the chest; Phlegm heat affecting the chest pattern
