V012-N03099.pinyinNames: tan re jie xiong zheng; tán rè jié xiōng zhèng
V012-N03099.definitions: Characterized by a feverish sensation in the chest, chest stuffiness, distension and pain, coughing with yellow phlegm, abdominal hardness and fullness with tenderness. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when turbid phlegm and heat remain in the chest.

V012-N03100.label: 痰瘀互结证
V012-N03100.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03100.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03100.aliases: 痰瘀内阻证； 瘀痰内阻证； 瘀痰互阻证； 血瘀痰凝证； 痰瘀搏结证
V012-N03100.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and stasis obstructing the internal; Intertwined phlegm and blood stasis pattern
V012-N03100.pinyinNames: tan yu hu jie zheng; tán yū hù jié zhèng
V012-N03100.definitions: Characterized by local lumps with stabbing pain, limb numbness or flaccidity, chest tightness, and profuse phlegm that may contain dark purple clots. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis and a greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when turbid phlegm intertwines with stagnant blood.

V012-N03101.label: 痰瘀化热证
V012-N03101.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03101.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03101.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and stasis transforming into heat; Phlegm and blood stasis transformed heat pattern
V012-N03101.pinyinNames: tan yu hua re zheng; tán yū huà rè zhèng
V012-N03101.definitions: Characterized by local swelling and hardness coupled with a burning sensation and stabbing pain, chest tightness, and expectoration of yellow phlegm that may contain blood. The tongue is dark red or with ecchymosis and a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when retained phlegm and blood stasis transform into heat.

V012-N03102.label: 痰瘀阻肺证
V012-N03102.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03102.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03102.aliases: 瘀痰阻肺证； Phlegm stasis obstructing the lung pattern
V012-N03102.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm and stasis obstructing the lung; Phlegm and blood stasis obstructing the lung pattern
V012-N03102.pinyinNames: tan yu zu fei zheng; tán yū zǔ fèi zhèng
V012-N03102.definitions: Characterized by cough, panting, chest tightness coupled with a stabbing pain, and profuse or blood-stained phlegm. The tongue is pale purple with a greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and slippery or wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood and turbid phlegm accumulate in the lung.

V012-N03103.label: 痰瘀阻膈证
V012-N03103.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03103.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03104.label: 痰迷心窍证
V012-N03104.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03104.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03104.aliases: 痰迷心神证； 痰蒙心窍证； 痰阻心窍证； 痰迷闭心窍证； 痰蒙心神证； 痰阻心神证； 痰闭心神证； 痰闭心窍证； Phlegm obstructing the heart； phlegm blocking the heart； phlegm blocking the heart spirit
V012-N03104.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm confusing the heart; Phlegm misting the heart pattern
V012-N03104.pinyinNames: tan mi xin qiao zheng; tán mí xīn qiào zhèng
V012-N03104.definitions: Characterized by dull expression, clouding of consciousness, depression, weird behaviour, an inability to recognize people, phlegm sounds in the throat, chest tightness, profuse phlegm, and a dark, grey complexion. The tongue coating is greasy. The pulse is slippery. This pattern often occurs when phlegm turbidity mists the heart spirit.

V012-N03105.label: 痰阻心脉证
V012-N03105.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03105.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03105.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm obstructing the heart vessels; Phlegm obstructing the heart vessels pattern
V012-N03105.pinyinNames: tan zu xin mai zheng; tán zǔ xīn mài zhèng
V012-N03105.definitions: Characterized by chest tightness, chest pain, obesity, profuse phlegm, a heavy sensation of the body, and a dark complexion. The tongue is pale purple with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery. This pattern often occurs when phlegm turbidity obstructs the flow of heart blood.

V012-N03106.label: 痰阻精室
V012-N03106.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03106.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03106.englishNames: Phlegm obstructing the essence chamber pattern
V012-N03106.definitions: Characterized by impotence, low sperm count, low libido, obesity, and fatigue. The tongue is pale with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery. This pattern often occurs when phlegm dampness affects the essence chamber.

V012-N03107.label: 痰食互结证
V012-N03107.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03107.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03107.englishNames: Intermingled phlegm and food stagnation pattern; syndrome/pattern of mutual binding of phlegm and retained food
V012-N03107.pinyinNames: tan shi hu jie zheng; tán shí hù jié zhèng
V012-N03107.definitions: Characterized by chest stuffiness, tightness, distension and pain, coughing with phlegm, a low food intake, abdominal distension, and vomiting of phlegm and undigested food. The tongue coating is putrid and greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery. Often occurs when turbid phlegm and undigested food obstruct the flow of qi.

V012-N03108.label: 瘀滞肌肤证
V012-N03108.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03108.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03108.englishNames: Stasis affecting the skin pattern; syndrome/pattern of stasis and stagnation in the muscles and skin
V012-N03108.pinyinNames: yū zhì jī fū zhèng; yu zhi ji fu zheng
V012-N03108.definitions: Characterized by dry, rough, scaly or numb skin, scratchy or painful blood streaks or dark purple ecchymosis in the skin. The pulse is superficial, thready and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood affects the skin.

V012-N03109.label: 瘀热入络证
V012-N03109.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03109.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03109.aliases: 热蕴络瘀证； 瘀热阻络证； 血热伤络证
V012-N03109.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis and heat entering collaterals; Stasis and heat entering the meridians pattern
V012-N03109.pinyinNames: yu re ru luo zheng; yū rè rù luò zhèng
V012-N03109.definitions: Characterized by a low-grade fever and redness of the affected area with a burning pain. The tongue is deep red or purple. The pulse is thready, hesitant and rapid. This pattern often occurs when intertwined heat and stagnant blood obstruct the meridians.

V012-N03110.label: 瘀血阻络证
V012-N03110.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03110.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03110.aliases: 瘀血伤络证； 瘀阻脉络证
V012-N03110.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing collaterals; Stagnant blood obstructing the meridians pattern
V012-N03110.pinyinNames: yu xue zu luo zheng; yū xuè zǔ luò zhèng
V012-N03110.definitions: Characterized by a stabbing pain with a fixed location. Alternatively, purple macules, lumps or bleeding of dark blood may be present. The tongue is purple or with petechiae. The pulse is hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the meridians.

V012-N03111.label: 瘀血阻膈证
V012-N03111.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03111.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03111.aliases: 膈下瘀阻证； 瘀阻胸膈证
V012-N03111.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood stasis obstructing the diaphragm; Stagnant blood obstructed the diaphragm pattern
V012-N03111.pinyinNames: yu xue zu ge zheng; yū xuè zǔ gé zhèng
V012-N03111.definitions: Characterized by stabbing pain in the diaphragm or epigastric area with a fixed location or difficulty in swallowing food. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when stagnant blood retains in the thoracic diaphragm.

V012-N03112.label: 瘀阻下焦证
V012-N03112.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03112.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03112.aliases: 瘀滞下焦证； 下焦瘀滞证； Stasis obstructing the lower energizer pattern
V012-N03112.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing the lower energizer; Stasis obstructing the lower jiao pattern
V012-N03112.pinyinNames: yu zu xia jiao zheng; yū zǔ xià jiāo zhèng
V012-N03112.definitions: Characterized by stabbing or distending pain in the lower abdomen, tenderness. Alternatively, palpable masses and bloody urine/stools may be present. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood affects the intestines, urinary bladder and uterus.

V012-N03113.label: 瘀阻肺络证
V012-N03113.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03113.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03113.aliases: 瘀血乘肺证； 瘀血停肺证
V012-N03113.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing the lung collaterals; Stasis obstructing the lung collaterals pattern
V012-N03113.pinyinNames: yu zu fei luo zheng; yū zǔ fèi luò zhèng
V012-N03113.definitions: Characterized by stabbing chest pain, and cough with haemoptysis (coughing up of dark red blood or clots). The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the lung collaterals.

V012-N03114.label: 瘀阻胃络证
V012-N03114.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03114.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03114.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of (blood) stasis in the stomach collateral; syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing the stomach collaterals; Stasis obstructing the stomach collaterals pattern
V012-N03114.pinyinNames: yu zu wei luo zheng; yū zǔ wèi luò zhèng
V012-N03114.definitions: Characterized by a stabbing stomach ache, tenderness or palpable stomach masses. Some patients may experience vomiting of dark-coloured blood or blood clots. The tongue has ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the stomach collaterals.

V012-N03115.label: 瘀阻胸胁证
V012-N03115.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03115.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03115.aliases: 胸络不和证； 瘀滞胸胁证； 瘀停胸胁证； Stasis retention in the chest/subcostal region pattern
V012-N03115.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing the chest and hypochondrium; Chest/subcostal stasis pattern
V012-N03115.pinyinNames: yu zu xiong xie zheng; yū zǔ xiōng xié zhèng
V012-N03115.definitions: Characterized by pain in the chest/subcostal region with a fixed location, tenderness, chest tightness, and cyanosis of the lips. The tongue is dark or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the chest and subcostal region.

V012-N03116.label: 瘀阻脑络证
V012-N03116.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03116.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03116.aliases: 瘀阻脑窍证； 瘀血犯头证； Stasis obstructing the brain pattern
V012-N03116.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of (blood) stasis obstructing the brain collateral; Blood stasis obstructing the brain collaterals pattern; syndrome/pattern of stasis obstructing the brain collaterals
V012-N03116.pinyinNames: yu zu nao luo zheng; yū zǔ nǎo luò zhèng
V012-N03116.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, persistent stabbing headache in a fixed location, and a dark grey complexion. Some patients may experience transient unconsciousness or poor memory after head trauma. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis/petechiae. The pulse is thready and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood blocks the brain collaterals.

V012-N03117.label: 真实假虚证
V012-N03117.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03117.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03117.englishNames: true excess with false deficiency pattern/ syndrome; True excess with false deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of genuine excess and false deficiency
V012-N03117.pinyinNames: zhen shi jia xu zheng; zhēn shí jiǎ xū zhèng
V012-N03117.definitions: An excess pattern that displays false deficiency symptoms.

V012-N03118.label: 真寒假热证
V012-N03118.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03118.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03118.englishNames: true cold with false heat pattern/ syndrome; True cold with false heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of genuine cold and false heat
V012-N03118.pinyinNames: zhen han jia re zheng; zhēn hán jiǎ rè zhèng
V012-N03118.definitions: Characterized by true-cold signs and symptoms such as cold hands/feet, profuse, clear urine, a pale tongue with a white coating, coupled with false-heat signs and symptoms such as fever but with a desire to put on more clothes, flushed cheeks and thirst with a desire for hot water. Often results from blockage of external yang by severe interior yin and cold.

V012-N03119.label: 督脉证
V012-N03119.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03119.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03119.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of governor vessel; Du meridian pattern
V012-N03119.pinyinNames: dong mai zheng; dū mài zhèng
V012-N03119.definitions: Characterized by spinal pain/stiffness and opisthotonos. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Du meridian.

V012-N03120.label: 精室瘀阻证
V012-N03120.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03120.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03120.aliases: 瘀阻精室证； 瘀血阻滞精室证； Stasis/stagnant blood obstructing the essence chamber pattern
V012-N03120.englishNames: Stasis obstructing the essence chamber pattern; syndrome/pattern  stasis and obstruction in essence chamber; syndrome/pattern stasis and obstruction in essence chamber
V012-N03120.pinyinNames: jīng shì yū zǔ zhèng; jing shi yu zu zheng
V012-N03120.definitions: Characterized by stabbing pain in the perineum with a fixed location and tenderness. Alternatively, lumps, low sperm count, impotence or pain during ejaculation may be present. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood affects the essence chamber.

V012-N03121.label: 精室证类
V012-N03121.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03121.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03121.englishNames: category of essence chamber syndromes/patterns; Patterns involving the essence chamber; category of essence chamber syndrome/pattern
V012-N03121.pinyinNames: jing shi zheng lei; jīng shì zhèng lèi

V012-N03122.label: 精气亏虚证
V012-N03122.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03122.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03122.aliases: 精气不足证； 精虚气弱证； 精亏气虚证； 精亏证； Essence deficiency pattern； Essential qi insufficiency
V012-N03122.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of essence and qi depletion; essential qi deficiency pattern/syndrome; Essence and qi deficiency pattern
V012-N03122.pinyinNames: jing qi kui xu zheng; jīng qì kuī xū zhèng
V012-N03122.definitions: Characterized by weight loss, dizziness, tinnitus cranii, short stature, slow movements and mental retardation. Alternatively, low sperm count, impotence or premature ejaculation may be present. Often results from essential qi deficiency.

V012-N03123.label: 精血亏虚证
V012-N03123.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03123.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03123.aliases: 精血不足证； 精亏血弱证； 精虚血少证
V012-N03123.englishNames: Essence and blood deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of essence and blood depletion
V012-N03123.pinyinNames: jing xue kui xu zheng; jīng xuè kuī xū zhèng
V012-N03123.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, poor memory, tinnitus, palpitations, a pale face and delayed wound healing. Often results from a deficiency of essence and blood due to chronic diseases or insufficient generation of qi and blood.

V012-N03124.label: 经络证类
V012-N03124.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03124.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03125.label: 肌肤筋骨证类
V012-N03125.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03125.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03125.englishNames: Patterns involving the skin, tendons and bones
V012-N03125.pinyinNames: jī fū jīn gǔ zhèng lèi

V012-N03126.label: 肝旺脾虚证
V012-N03126.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03126.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03126.aliases: 肝木侮土证； 脾虚肝旺证； 肝气乘脾证； 肝旺脾困证； 肝滞脾虚证； 肝郁脾虚证； Liver stagnation with spleen deficiency pattern
V012-N03126.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver effulgence and spleen deficiency; Liver hyperactivity with spleen deficiency pattern
V012-N03126.pinyinNames: gan wang pi xu zheng; gān wàng pí xū zhèng
V012-N03126.definitions: Characterized by distension pain in the subcostal region, emotional depression, a poor appetite, low food intake, abdominal distension and loose stools. This pattern often occurs as a result of liver qi stagnation coupled with spleen deficiency.

V012-N03127.label: 肝气上逆证
V012-N03127.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03127.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03127.definitions: Characterized by vertigo, headache, chest tightness, flushed face, tinnitus, deafness and migratory pain in the subcostal region. The symptoms are associated with emotions. In severe cases, patients may experience vomiting of blood or irregular menstruation. This pattern often occurs when emotional disturbance causes liver qi to stagnate and ascend.

V012-N03128.label: 肝气犯胃证
V012-N03128.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03128.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03128.aliases: 肝胃气滞证； Qi stagnation of the liver and stomach pattern； 肝胃不和证； 肝木犯胃证
V012-N03128.englishNames: Liver qi affecting the stomach pattern; pattern/syndrome of liver qi invading the stomach; syndrome/pattern of liver qi invading the stomach
V012-N03128.pinyinNames: gān qì fàn wèi zhèng; gan qi fan wei zheng
V012-N03128.definitions: Characterized by vomiting, acid reflux, repeated belching, gastric discomfort, and distending pain in the chest and subcostal region that aggravate upon emotional disturbance. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when liver qi stagnation affects the stomach.

V012-N03129.label: 肝气犯脾证
V012-N03129.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03129.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03129.englishNames: Liver qi affecting the spleen pattern
V012-N03129.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, irritability, a bitter mouth, chest tightness, pain in the subcostal region, abdominal stuffiness/fullness after eating food and loose stools. The pulse is wiry and delayed. This pattern often occurs when hyperactive liver qi affects the spleen.

V012-N03130.label: 肝气虚证
V012-N03130.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03130.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03130.aliases: 肝阳亏虚证； Liver yang insufficiency pattern； 肝气亏虚证
V012-N03130.englishNames: Liver qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of liver qi deficiency
V012-N03130.pinyinNames: gān qì xū zhèng; gan qi xu zheng
V012-N03130.definitions: Characterized by distension/tightness in the subcostal region, low mood, fatigue, shortness of breath, dizziness and blurred vision. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi becomes deficient, and the liver fails to maintain the free flow of qi.

V012-N03131.label: 肝火上炎证
V012-N03131.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03131.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03131.aliases: 肝火上扰清窍证； 肝火上扰证； 肝热上扰证； Liver fire flaming upward pattern； Hyperactivity of liver fire pattern
V012-N03131.englishNames: Liver fire flaming upward pattern; pattern/syndrome of liver fire flaming upward; syndrome/pattern of liver fire flaming up; Upward flaming of liver fire pattern
V012-N03131.pinyinNames: gan huo shang yan zheng; gān huǒ shàng yán zhèng
V012-N03131.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, restlessness, insomnia, and headache. Alternatively, eye redness, swelling and pain, sudden tinnitus/deafness, haematemesis, nosebleed and a red face may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberance liver fire flows upward.

V012-N03132.label: 肝火犯肺证
V012-N03132.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03132.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03132.aliases: 木火刑金证； Liver fire invading the lung system pattern； Wood fire tormenting metal pattern
V012-N03132.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of liver fire invading the lung; Liver fire invading the lung system pattern; syndrome/pattern of liver fire invading the lung; Liver fire affecting the lung pattern
V012-N03132.pinyinNames: gan huo fan fei zheng; gān huǒ fàn fèi zhèng
V012-N03132.definitions: Characterized by a burning pain in the chest and subcostal region, restlessness, irritability, a dry, bitter mouth and paroxysmal coughs. In severe cases, haemoptysis may be present. The tongue is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant liver fire affects the descending of lung qi.

V012-N03133.label: 肝火犯胃证
V012-N03133.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03133.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03133.aliases: Liver fire invading the stomach system pattern
V012-N03133.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver fire invading the stomach; Liver fire invading the stomach system pattern; Liver fire affecting the stomach pattern
V012-N03133.pinyinNames: gan huo fan wei zheng; gān huǒ fàn wèi zhèng
V012-N03133.definitions: Characterized by a burning pain in the stomach and subcostal region, a dry, bitter mouth, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when liver fire affects the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N03134.label: 肝热动风证
V012-N03134.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03134.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03134.aliases: 热动肝风证； Liver heat stirring wind pattern； Pattern of heat stirring liver wind
V012-N03134.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver heat stirring wind; Liver heat stirring wind pattern
V012-N03134.pinyinNames: gan re dong feng zheng; gān rè dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03134.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, thirst, unconsciousness, delirium, convulsions of the limbs, and opisthotonos. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant heat generates wind.

V012-N03135.label: 肝热血瘀证
V012-N03135.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03135.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03135.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood stasis due to liver heat; Liver heat with blood stagnation pattern
V012-N03135.pinyinNames: gan re xue yu zheng; gān rè xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03135.definitions: Characterized by a stabbing pain in the subcostal region with a burning sensation, and a dry, bitter mouth. The tongue is deep red with a yellow coating. The pulse is hesitant and rapid. This pattern often results from internal build up of heat and liver blood stagnation.

V012-N03136.label: 肝热阴虚证
V012-N03136.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03136.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03136.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver heat and yin deficiency; Liver heat with yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03136.pinyinNames: gan re yin xu zheng; gān rè yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03136.definitions: Characterized by a dull pain in the subcostal region with a burning sensation, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, restlessness, irritability and a dry, bitter mouth. The tongue is red with a scanty, yellow coating. The pulse is wiry, thready and rapid. This pattern often results from internal build up of heat and liver yin deficiency.

V012-N03137.label: 肝瘀化热证
V012-N03137.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03137.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03137.englishNames: Liver qi stagnation transforming into heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of liver stasis transforming into heat
V012-N03137.pinyinNames: gan yu hua re zheng; gān yū huà rè zhèng
V012-N03137.definitions: Characterized by a burning sensation and stabbing pain/masses in the subcostal region with tenderness, and a dry, bitter mouth. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis, and a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when prolonged liver qi stagnation and liver blood stasis transform into heat.

V012-N03138.label: 肝瘀痰结证
V012-N03138.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03138.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03138.englishNames: Liver stagnation with phlegm retention pattern; syndrome/pattern of phlegm binding and blood stasis in liver
V012-N03138.pinyinNames: gan yu tan jie zheng; gān yū tán jié zhèng
V012-N03138.definitions: Characterized by masses, distending or stabbing pain and tenderness in the subcostal region, along with phlegm in the throat. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis and a greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant qi and phlegm accumulate in the liver.

V012-N03139.label: 肝系证类
V012-N03139.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03139.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03139.englishNames: category of liver system syndromes/patterns; Patterns of the liver system; Liver system patterns
V012-N03139.pinyinNames: gan xi zheng lei; gān xì zhèng lèi

V012-N03140.label: 肝经湿热证
V012-N03140.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03140.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03140.aliases: Liver meridian damp heat pattern； 肝经湿毒证
V012-N03140.englishNames: Liver meridian dampness-heat pattern; pattern/syndrome of dampness-heat in the liver meridian; Damp heat affecting the liver meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the liver meridian
V012-N03140.pinyinNames: gān jīng shī rè zhèng; gan jing shi re zheng
V012-N03140.definitions: Characterized by distending pain in the subcostal region, itching, swelling and pain of the external genitalia or yellow, sticky vaginal discharge. Earache with discharge may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the liver meridian.

V012-N03141.label: 肝经风热证
V012-N03141.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03141.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03141.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and heat in liver meridian; Wind heat affecting the liver meridian pattern
V012-N03141.pinyinNames: gan jing feng re zheng; gān jīng fēng rè zhèng
V012-N03141.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to wind and/or cold, vertex headache. Red, swollen and painful eyes may be present. The tongue coating is thin and yellow. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when wind heat attacks the liver meridian.

V012-N03142.label: 肝肠气滞证
V012-N03142.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03142.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03142.englishNames: Qi stagnation of the liver and large intestine pattern; syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation in the liver and intestines
V012-N03142.pinyinNames: gan chang qi zhi zheng; gān cháng qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03142.definitions: Characterized by distending pain in the subcostal region and abdomen, bowel sounds, flatus, diarrhoea upon abdominal pain and a bearing-down sensation of the anus. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when liver qi stagnation affects the large intestine.

V012-N03143.label: 肝肾两虚证
V012-N03143.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03143.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03143.aliases: 肝肾亏虚证； 肝肾精血亏损证； Deficiency of liver blood and kidney essence； Liver and kidney yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03143.englishNames: Deficiency of the liver and kidney pattern; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of liver and kidney
V012-N03143.pinyinNames: gān shèn liǎng xū zhèng; gan shen liang xu zheng
V012-N03143.definitions: Characterized by pale, lustreless complexion, pale lips and fingernails, dizziness, tinnitus, dry eyes, palpitations, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, jumpiness or easily startled, irregular menstruation, scanty menstrual volume, amenorrhoea and low back soreness. The tongue is red. The pulse is thready. This pattern often results from chronic diseases or age-related liver blood/kidney essence deficiency.

V012-N03144.label: 肝肾阴虚证
V012-N03144.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03144.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03144.aliases: 肝肾虚火证； 肝肾阴亏证； Liver and kidney yin deficiency pattern； Deficiency fire of the liver and kidney
V012-N03144.englishNames: Liver and kidney yin deficiency pattern; Yin deficiency of the liver and kidney pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in liver and kidney; liver-kidney yin deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03144.pinyinNames: gan shen yin xu zheng; gān shèn yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03144.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, tinnitus, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, a low-grade fever, flushed cheeks, pain in the subcostal region, and low back/knee soreness and weakness. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin deficiency of the liver and kidney causes deficiency heat.

V012-N03145.label: 肝胃不和证
V012-N03145.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03145.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03145.aliases: 肝气犯胃证； 肝胃不调证； Disharmony of liver and stomach systems pattern； Liver qi invading the stomach pattern； Liver–stomach disharmony pattern； Incoordination between the liver and stomach
V012-N03145.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of disharmony of liver and stomach; Disharmony of liver and stomach systems pattern; Disharmony between the liver and stomach pattern
V012-N03145.pinyinNames: gan wei bu he zheng; gān wèi bù hé zhèng
V012-N03145.definitions: Characterized by gastric distension, fullness and pain, a poor appetite, belching, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, pain in the subcostal region, and restlessness. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when liver qi stagnation affects the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N03146.label: 肝胃热盛证
V012-N03146.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03146.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03146.aliases: 肝胃积热证； Heat accumulating in the liver and stomach pattern
V012-N03146.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exuberant heat in the liver and stomach; Exuberant heat in the liver and stomach pattern
V012-N03146.pinyinNames: gan wei re sheng zheng; gān wèi rè shèng zhèng
V012-N03146.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, restlessness, irritability, pain in the subcostal region, a bitter mouth and a burning pain in the stomach. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid and forceful. This pattern often occurs when exuberant fire/heat affects the liver and stomach.

V012-N03147.label: 肝胃虚寒证
V012-N03147.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03147.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03147.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of deficiency-cold in the liver and stomach; Deficiency cold of the liver and stomach pattern
V012-N03147.pinyinNames: gan wei xu han zheng; gān wèi xū hán zhèng
V012-N03147.definitions: Characterized by distension in the subcostal region, gastric discomfort and pain with a cold sensation that alleviates upon pressure and a low food intake. The tongue is pale. The pulse is deep and slow. This pattern often occurs as a result of yang qi deficiency and disharmony between the liver and stomach.

V012-N03148.label: 肝胃阴虚证
V012-N03148.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03148.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03148.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in liver and stomach; Yin deficiency of the liver and stomach pattern
V012-N03148.pinyinNames: gan wei yin xu zheng; gān wèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03148.definitions: Characterized by a dry, bitter mouth, dull pain in the subcostal region and stomach, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is wiry, thready and rapid. This pattern often results from yin fluid deficiency of the liver and stomach.

V012-N03149.label: 肝胆湿热证
V012-N03149.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03149.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03149.englishNames: liver-gallbladder dampness-heat pattern/syndrome; Damp heat in the liver and gallbladder pattern; Liver-gallbladder dampness-heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the liver and gallbladder
V012-N03149.pinyinNames: gan dan shi re zheng; gān dǎn shī rè zhèng
V012-N03149.definitions: Characterized by yellowish skin and eyes, fever, a bitter mouth, distending pain or masses in the subcostal region, poor appetite, nausea, vomiting, aversion to oily food, and yellowish urine. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when internal damp heat affects the qi flow of the liver and gallbladder.

V012-N03150.label: 肝脾不和证
V012-N03150.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03150.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03150.aliases: 肝脾不和证； Disharmony of liver and spleen systems pattern； Liver depression and spleen deficiency pattern； Imbalance between liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03150.englishNames: Disharmony of liver and spleen systems pattern; Disharmony between the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03150.definitions: Characterized by distension and pain in the subcostal region, emotional depression, a poor appetite, abdominal distension and loose stools. The pulse is wiry and delayed. This pattern often occurs when liver qi stagnation affects the transportation and transformation of the spleen.

V012-N03151.label: 肝脾两虚证
V012-N03151.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03151.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03151.aliases: 肝脾不足证； 肝脾亏虚证； 肝脾两亏证
V012-N03151.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of liver and spleen; Deficiency of the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03151.pinyinNames: gan pi liang xu zheng; gān pí liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03151.definitions: Characterized by dull pain in the subcostal region, dizziness, blurred vision, a low food intake, abdominal distension and loose stools. This pattern often results from deficiency of the liver and spleen.

V012-N03152.label: 肝脾气滞证
V012-N03152.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03152.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03152.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver and spleen qi stagnation; Qi stagnation of the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03152.pinyinNames: gan pi qi zhi zheng; gān pí qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03152.definitions: Characterized by distension and pain in the subcostal region, abdominal bloating, bowel sounds, and a sensation of incomplete evacuation after bowel movements. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often results from the qi stagnation of the liver and spleen.

V012-N03153.label: 肝脾湿热证
V012-N03153.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03153.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03153.aliases: 中焦湿热证； Damp heat in the middle jiao pattern
V012-N03153.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the liver and spleen; Damp heat in the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03153.pinyinNames: gan pi shi re zheng; gān pí shī rè zhèng
V012-N03153.definitions: Characterized by distension in the subcostal region, abdominal bloating, nausea and aversion to oil. Alternatively, jaundice and a sensation of incomplete evacuation after bowel movements. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is wiry, slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the free flow of liver qi and transportation and transformation of the spleen.

V012-N03154.label: 肝脾血瘀证
V012-N03154.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03154.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03154.aliases: 肝脾瘀滞证； Stasis of the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03154.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood stasis in the liver and spleen; Blood stasis of the liver and spleen pattern
V012-N03154.pinyinNames: gan pi xue yu zheng; gān pí xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03154.definitions: Characterized by abdominal pain, fullness and distension, skin ecchymosis or petechiae, masses in the subcostal region or stabbing pain with tenderness. The tongue is dark or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood affects the liver and spleen.

V012-N03155.label: 肝血瘀滞证
V012-N03155.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03155.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03155.definitions: Characterized by stabbing pain/masses with a fixed location in the subcostal region, and tenderness. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when blood stasis obstructs the liver collaterals.

V012-N03156.label: 肝血虚证
V012-N03156.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03156.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03156.aliases: 肝血亏虚证； Liver blood deficiency pattern； Insufficiency of liver blood
V012-N03156.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver blood deficiency; Liver blood deficiency pattern; liver blood deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03156.pinyinNames: gan xue xu zheng; gān xuè xū zhèng
V012-N03156.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, dry eyes, poor eyesight, limb numbness, dry fingernails and toenails, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, scanty menstrual volume with a pale menstrual colour, a pale complexion and lips. In severe cases, amenorrhoea may be present. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready. This pattern often occurs when blood fails to nourish the liver.

V012-N03157.label: 肝郁化火证
V012-N03157.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03157.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03157.aliases: 肝气化火证； 气火郁结证； 肝经郁热证； 气郁化火证； Qi stagnation transforming into fire pattern
V012-N03157.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of depressed liver qi transforming into fire; syndrome/pattern of stagnated liver qi transforming into fire; Liver qi stagnation transforming into fire pattern
V012-N03157.pinyinNames: gan yu hua huo zheng; gān yù huà huǒ zhèng
V012-N03157.definitions: Characterized by distending pain in the subcostal region, restlessness, irritability, a dry, bitter mouth and dark yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when prolonged liver qi stagnation transforms into heat/fire.

V012-N03158.label: 肝郁气滞证
V012-N03158.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03158.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03158.aliases: 肝郁不舒证； 肝气郁滞证； 肝郁证； Liver qi stagnation pattern； Liver depression and qi stagnation pattern； Liver meridian stagnated heat pattern； Liver stagnation； 肝气郁结证
V012-N03158.englishNames: Liver qi stagnation pattern; syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation due to liver depression; pattern/syndrome of liver depression and qi stagnation
V012-N03158.pinyinNames: gan yu qi zhi zheng; gān yù qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03158.definitions: Characterized by emotional depression, frequent sighing, distension/tightness/migratory pain in the chest, subcostal region or bilateral lower abdomen, breast distension/pain in women and irregular menstruation. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when the liver fails to maintain the free flow of qi.

V012-N03159.label: 肝郁湿热证
V012-N03159.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03159.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03159.aliases: 肝滞湿热证； Liver stagnation with damp heat pattern
V012-N03159.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat due to liver depression; Liver qi stagnation with damp heat retention pattern
V012-N03159.pinyinNames: gan yu shi re zheng; gān yù shī rè zhèng
V012-N03159.definitions: Characterized by distending pain/masses in the subcostal region, thirst, and a bitter mouth. Yellowish skin and eyes may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often results from internal accumulation of damp heat, coupled with liver qi stagnation.

V012-N03160.label: 肝郁痰火证
V012-N03160.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03160.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03160.aliases: 痰热郁肝证； 肝郁痰热证
V012-N03160.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm-fire due to liver depression; Liver stagnation with phlegm fire pattern
V012-N03160.pinyinNames: gan yu tan huo zheng; gān yù tán huǒ zhèng
V012-N03160.definitions: Characterized by a distending/burning pain in the subcostal region, restlessness, irritability, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, vertigo, headache and expectoration of yellow phlegm. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs as a result of liver qi stagnation and internal build up of phlegm heat.

V012-N03161.label: 肝郁血瘀证
V012-N03161.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03161.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03161.aliases: 肝瘀气滞证； 肝血瘀滞证； Pattern of liver blood stasis and stagnation； Pattern of liver stasis with qi stagnation； Liver depression and blood stasis pattern
V012-N03161.englishNames: Liver depression and blood stasis pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood stasis due to liver depression; pattern/syndrome of liver depression and blood stasis; Liver qi stagnation with blood stasis pattern
V012-N03161.pinyinNames: gan yu xue yu zheng; gān yù xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03161.definitions: Characterized by distending or stabbing pain in the rib area, subcostal or lower abdominal masses and emotional depression. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when liver qi stagnation causes blood stasis.

V012-N03162.label: 肝郁血虚证
V012-N03162.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03162.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03162.aliases: 血虚肝郁证； Liver stagnation pattern
V012-N03162.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood deficiency due to liver depression; Liver qi stagnation with blood deficiency pattern
V012-N03162.pinyinNames: gan yu xue xu zheng; gān yù xuè xū zhèng
V012-N03162.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, distension in the subcostal region, emotional depression, dream-disturbed sleep, poor memory, and a pale complexion. The pale is pale purple. The pulse is wiry and thready. This pattern often occurs as a result of blood deficiency and liver qi stagnation.

V012-N03163.label: 肝郁阴虚证
V012-N03163.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03163.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03163.englishNames: Liver qi stagnation with yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency due to liver depression
V012-N03163.pinyinNames: gan yu yin xu zheng; gān yù yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03163.definitions: Characterized by distending or burning pain in the subcostal region, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, dizziness, blurred vision, and a dry, bitter mouth. The tongue is dark red with a scanty coating. The pulse is wiry and thready. This pattern often occurs as a result of liver qi stagnation and liver yin deficiency.

V012-N03164.label: 肝阳上亢证
V012-N03164.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03164.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03164.aliases: Liver yang ascendant hyperactivity pattern； Liver yang disturbing the mind； 肝阳上扰证； 肝阳亢盛证
V012-N03164.englishNames: Liver yang ascendant hyperactivity pattern; pattern/syndrome of ascendant hyperactivity of liver yang; Hyperactivity of liver yang pattern; syndrome/pattern of liver yang hyperactivity; syndrome/ pattern of liver yang hyperactivity
V012-N03164.pinyinNames: gān yáng shàng kàng zhèng; gan yang shang kang zheng
V012-N03164.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, tinnitus, lower back pain, limb numbness, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, flushed cheeks, restlessness, irritability, and a dry and bitter mouth. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when liver yin fails to control liver yang.

V012-N03165.label: 肝阳化风证
V012-N03165.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03165.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03165.aliases: Liver yang transforming into wind pattern
V012-N03165.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of liver yang transforming into wind; syndrome/pattern of liver yang transforming into wind; Liver yang transforming into wind pattern
V012-N03165.pinyinNames: gan yang hua feng zheng; gān yáng huà fēng zhèng
V012-N03165.definitions: Characterized by vertigo, headache with a distending sensation, limb numbness, tinnitus, restlessness, irritability and a red face. The tongue is red. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when hyperactive liver yang stirs liver wind.

V012-N03166.label: 肝阴虚证
V012-N03166.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03166.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03166.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver yin deficiency; liver yin deficiency pattern/syndrome; Liver yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03166.pinyinNames: gan yin xu zheng; gān yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03166.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, dry eyes, poor eyesight, flushed cheeks, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest. A burning pain in the subcostal region may be present. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluid fails to nourish the liver.

V012-N03167.label: 肝风内动证
V012-N03167.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03167.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03167.aliases: 肝风证； Internal stirring of liver wind； Liver wind stirring the interior pattern
V012-N03167.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver wind stirring inside; Liver wind stirring the interior pattern; Internal stirring of liver wind pattern; pattern/syndrome of internal stirring of liver wind
V012-N03167.pinyinNames: gan feng nei dong zheng; gān fēng nèi dòng zhèng
V012-N03167.definitions: Characterized by convulsions of the four limbs, vertigo and tremor. This pattern often results from wind yang, fire heat and yin blood deficiency.

V012-N03168.label: 肠燥津亏证
V012-N03168.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03168.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03168.aliases: 大肠液亏证； 大肠津亏证； Large intestine fluid deficiency pattern； Fluid insufficiency in the large intestine； 肠燥津伤证
V012-N03168.englishNames: Intestinal dryness pattern; pattern/syndrome of intestinal dryness and fluid depletion
V012-N03168.definitions: Characterized by extremely dry stools (like sheep droppings), one bowel movement in several days, abdominal distension/pain, palpable abdominal masses, and thirst. The tongue is dry. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when body fluids fail to nourish the large intestine.

V012-N03169.label: 肠道湿热证
V012-N03169.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03169.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03169.aliases: Large intestine dampness heat pattern； 大肠湿热证
V012-N03169.englishNames: Damp heat in the intestine pattern; intestinal dampnessheat pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03169.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension/pain, sudden, fulminant diarrhoea, diarrhoea with blood, tenesmus, a sensation of incomplete evacuation, sticky, strong-odoured stools, a burning sensation around the anus, fever, thirst, and scanty urine. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when internal damp heat obstructs the intestine.

V012-N03170.label: 肠风伤络证
V012-N03170.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03170.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03170.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of intestine wind with collateral damage; Wind damaging intestinal collaterals pattern
V012-N03170.pinyinNames: chang feng shang luo zheng; cháng fēng shāng luò zhèng
V012-N03170.definitions: Characterized by bright red stool, dry stools and itching around the anus. In severe cases, the passing of pure blood may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when wind fire damages the intestinal collaterals, resulting in intestinal bleeding.

V012-N03171.label: 肺实热证
V012-N03171.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03171.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03171.aliases: 肺热壅盛证； 肺热炽盛证； Excess lung heat pattern； exuberance of lung heat pattern
V012-N03171.englishNames: Excess heat in the lung pattern
V012-N03171.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, cough, rapid breathing and panting. Chest pain, sore throat, nasal flaring, constipation and yellow urine may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat affects the lung.

V012-N03172.label: 肺气虚证
V012-N03172.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03172.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03172.aliases: 肺气亏虚证； 肺卫气虚不固证； 肺卫气虚证； Lung qi deficiency pattern； Defense qi deficiency pattern； Lung deficiency with exterior weakness pattern； Lung and defense qi insecurity pattern； Insecurity of lung Wei-defence
V012-N03172.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of lung qi deficiency; lung qi deficiency pattern/syndrome; Lung qi deficiency pattern
V012-N03172.pinyinNames: fei qi xu zheng; fèi qì xū zhèng
V012-N03172.definitions: Characterized by weak coughing and panting, and shortness of breath that aggravate upon physical exertion, expectoration of thin clear phlegm, a low voice, spontaneous sweating, wind intolerance and susceptibility to common colds. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from lung qi deficiency and decreased lung function.

V012-N03173.label: 肺气阴两虚证
V012-N03173.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03173.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03173.aliases: Lung qi and yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03173.englishNames: Lung qi/yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of lung qi and yin; Lung qi and yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03173.pinyinNames: fèi qì yīn liǎng xū zhèng; fei qi yin liang xu zheng
V012-N03173.definitions: Characterized by weak, unproductive coughs, shortness of breath, panting, a low/hoarse voice and feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest. The pulse is thready and weak. This pattern often results from a deficiency of lung qi and yin.

V012-N03174.label: 肺热移肠证
V012-N03174.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03174.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03174.englishNames: Lung heat transmitting into the intestine pattern; syndrome/pattern of lung heat transmitted to the intestine; Lung heat transmitting to the large intestine pattern
V012-N03174.pinyinNames: fei re yi chang zheng; fèi rè yí cháng zhèng
V012-N03174.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, cough, panting, abdominal bloating and constipation. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid or replenish. This pattern often occurs when exuberant lung heat transmits downward to the large intestine.

V012-N03175.label: 肺热阴虚证
V012-N03175.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03175.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03175.aliases: 阴虚肺热证； 肺热津伤证； 阴虚肺燥证； 肺燥津亏证； Lung heat damaging fluids pattern； lung dryness due to yin/fluids deficiency pattern
V012-N03175.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency due to lung heat; Lung heat and yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of  yin deficiency due to lung heat
V012-N03175.pinyinNames: fei re yin xu zheng; fèi rè yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03175.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, cough with scanty phlegm, panting, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant lung heat consumes yin fluids.

V012-N03176.label: 肺燥肠闭证
V012-N03176.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03176.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03176.aliases: Lung dryness with intestinal obstruction pattern
V012-N03176.englishNames: Lung dryness with intestinal obstruction pattern; pattern/syndrome of lung dryness with intestinal obstruction; syndrome/pattern of lung dryness and intestinal block; Lung dryness affecting the large intestine pattern
V012-N03176.pinyinNames: fei zao chang bi zheng; fèi zào cháng bì zhèng
V012-N03176.definitions: Characterized by cough, thirst, panting, constipation, and abdominal fullness/distension/pain. The tongue coating is yellow and dry. The pulse is deep and excessive. This pattern often occurs when lung dryness consumes body fluids and blocks intestinal qi.

V012-N03177.label: 肺系证类
V012-N03177.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03177.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03177.englishNames: category of lung system syndromes/patterns; Patterns of the lung system
V012-N03177.pinyinNames: fei xi zheng lei; fèi xì zhèng lèi

V012-N03178.label: 肺肾气虚证
V012-N03178.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03178.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03178.aliases: Insufficient qi of the lung and kidney pattern； Kidney failing to receive qi pattern； Lung–kidney deficiency pattern； 肾不纳气证； 肾失摄纳证
V012-N03178.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency in the lung and kidney; Qi deficiency of the lung and kidney pattern; lung-kidney qi deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03178.pinyinNames: fei shen qi xu zheng; fèi shèn qì xū zhèng
V012-N03178.definitions: Characterized by more exhalation than inhalation, weak coughs that aggravate upon physical exertion, expectoration of thin, clear sputum, a low voice and spontaneous sweating. Cough-induced urine leaking may also be present. The tongue is pale purple. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from qi deficiency of the lung and kidney.

V012-N03179.label: 肺肾阳虚证
V012-N03179.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03179.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03179.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency in the lung and kidney; Yang deficiency of the lung and kidney pattern; lung-kidney yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03179.pinyinNames: fei shen yang xu zheng; fèi shèn yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03179.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, cough with profuse, thin and clear sputum, panting, leg oedema and scanty urine. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when water retention (due to kidney yang deficiency) affects the lung.

V012-N03180.label: 肺肾阴虚证
V012-N03180.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03180.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03180.aliases: Lung and kidney yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03180.englishNames: lung-kidney yin deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Lung and kidney yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in the lung and kidney; Yin deficiency of the lung and kidney pattern
V012-N03180.pinyinNames: fei shen yin xu zheng; fèi shèn yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03180.definitions: Characterized by cough with scanty phlegm or blood-stained sputum, a dry throat or hoarseness, low back/knee soreness and weakness, weight loss, bone-steaming tidal fever, night sweats and flushed cheeks. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids deficiency of the lung and kidney causes deficiency heat.

V012-N03181.label: 肺胃火热证
V012-N03181.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03181.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03181.aliases: 肺胃热盛证； Exuberant heat in the lung and stomach pattern
V012-N03181.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fire-heat in the lung and stomach; Fire heat in the lung and stomach pattern
V012-N03181.pinyinNames: fei wei huo re zheng; fèi wèi huǒ rè zhèng
V012-N03181.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, profuse sweating, cough, panting, a burning pain in the stomach, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant heat affects the lung and stomach.

V012-N03182.label: 肺胃阴虚证
V012-N03182.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03182.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03182.aliases: 肺胃津亏证； Fluids insufficiency of the lung and stomach pattern
V012-N03182.englishNames: syndrome/ pattern of yin deficiency in the lung and stomach; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in the lung and stomach; Yin deficiency of the lung and stomach pattern
V012-N03182.pinyinNames: fei wei yin xu zheng; fèi wèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03182.definitions: Characterized by thirst with a desire to drink water, unproductive cough, gastric discomfort and rapid hunger after eating food. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often results from yin fluids deficiency of the lung and stomach.

V012-N03183.label: 肺胃风热证
V012-N03183.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03183.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03183.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and heat in the lung and stomach; Wind heat affecting the lung and stomach pattern
V012-N03183.pinyinNames: fei wei feng re zheng; fèi wèi fēng rè zhèng
V012-N03183.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, cough and skin rashes. The tongue is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when wind heat affects the lung and stomach.

V012-N03184.label: 肺阳虚证
V012-N03184.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03184.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03184.aliases: 肺虚寒证； 肺阳亏虚证； Lung yang deficiency pattern
V012-N03184.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of lung yang deficiency; Lung yang deficiency pattern; lung yang deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03184.pinyinNames: fei yang xu zheng; fèi yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03184.definitions: Characterized by panting, weak coughs with thin, white phlegm, chest tightness, a low voice, shallow breathing, shortness of breath, and cold limbs. The tongue is pale, purple, enlarged and tender with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is deep, slow and weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi fails to warm the lung.

V012-N03185.label: 肺阴虚证
V012-N03185.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03185.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03185.aliases: 肺阴亏虚证； 肺虚热证； Lung yin deficiency pattern； Insufficiency of lung yin pattern
V012-N03185.englishNames: lung yin deficiency pattern/syndrome; Lung yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of lung yin deficiency; syndrome/ pattern of lung yin deficiency
V012-N03185.pinyinNames: fei yin xu zheng; fèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03185.definitions: Characterized by unproductive coughs or coughing with scanty, sticky phlegm or blood-stained phlegm, a dry mouth and throat, a hoarse voice, hot flushes, flushed cheeks, and night sweats. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when lung yin deficiency generates internal heat.

V012-N03186.label: 肾不纳气证
V012-N03186.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03186.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03186.aliases: Kidney failing to receive qi pattern； 肾气失纳证
V012-N03186.englishNames: Kidney failing to receive qi pattern; Kidney failing to absorb qi pattern; syndrome/pattern of kidney failing to receive qi; pattern/syndrome of kidney failing to receive qi
V012-N03186.pinyinNames: shèn bú nà qì zhèng; shen bu na qi zheng
V012-N03186.definitions: Characterized by shortness of breath, dyspnoea, panting or sweating upon physical exertion, and more exhalation than inhalation. In severe cases, facial puffiness and leg oedema may be present. The pulse is thready or deficient, floating and rootless. This pattern often occurs when kidney qi fails to absorb lung qi.

V012-N03187.label: 肾气不固证
V012-N03187.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03187.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03187.englishNames: kidney qi insecurity pattern/syndrome; Kidney qi insecurity pattern
V012-N03187.definitions: Characterized by urgent, frequent urination with clear, dribbling urine, bladder or bowel incontinence, impotence, premature ejaculation, dribbling menstruation, threatened miscarriage, tinnitus, and low back/knee soreness and weakness. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when kidney qi fails to seal and store.

V012-N03188.label: 肾气虚水泛证
V012-N03188.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03188.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03188.aliases: 肾气虚水停证； Kidney qi deficiency with water retention pattern； Kidney qi deficiency with water flooding pattern
V012-N03188.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of kidney qi deficiency and water flooding; Kidney qi deficiency with water retention pattern; Water retention due to kidney qi deficiency pattern
V012-N03188.pinyinNames: shen qi xu shui fan zheng; shèn qì xū shuǐ fàn zhèng
V012-N03188.definitions: Characterized by leg oedema, scanty urine, and low back/knee soreness and weakness. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when kidney qi fails to perform qi transformation, resulting in water retention.

V012-N03189.label: 肾气虚证
V012-N03189.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03189.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03189.aliases: 肾气亏虚证； Kidney qi depletion pattern； Kidney qi insufficiency
V012-N03189.englishNames: Kidney qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of kidney qi deficiency; kidney qi deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03189.pinyinNames: shèn qì xū zhèng; shen qi xu zheng
V012-N03189.definitions: Characterized by low back soreness, tinnitus, low libido, dizziness, and poor memory. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from kidney qi deficiency.

V012-N03190.label: 肾精亏虚证
V012-N03190.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03190.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03190.aliases: 肾精不足证； 肾精亏损证； 肾虚精亏证； 肾精亏耗证； 肾气亏虚证； Kidney qi/essence deficiency
V012-N03190.englishNames: Kidney essence deficiency pattern; syndrome/ pattern of kidney essence depletion; syndrome/pattern of kidney essence depletion
V012-N03190.pinyinNames: shen jing kui xu zheng; shèn jīng kuī xū zhèng
V012-N03190.definitions: Characterized by delayed growth in children, decreased reproductive function, premature ageing, tinnitus, hair loss, loose teeth, and poor memory. This pattern often results from kidney essence deficiency.

V012-N03191.label: 肾系证类
V012-N03191.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03191.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03191.englishNames: Patterns of the kidney system; category of kidney system syndromes/patterns
V012-N03191.pinyinNames: shen xi zheng lei; shèn xì zhèng lèi

V012-N03192.label: 肾虚寒湿证
V012-N03192.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03192.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03192.aliases: 肾经寒湿证； Cold dampness in the kidney meridian pattern
V012-N03192.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of cold-dampness due to kidney deficiency; Cold dampness due to kidney deficiency pattern
V012-N03192.pinyinNames: shen xu han shi zheng; shèn xū hán shī zhèng
V012-N03192.definitions: Characterized by low back/knee heaviness and pain with a cold sensation, limited movement, cold intolerance and cold limbs. The tongue coating is white and slippery. The pulse is soft and delayed. This pattern often occurs when cold dampness remains in the kidney meridian.

V012-N03193.label: 肾虚水泛证
V012-N03193.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03193.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03194.label: 肾虚证
V012-N03194.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03194.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03194.englishNames: Kidney deficiency pattern; kidney deficiency pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of kidney deficiency
V012-N03194.pinyinNames: shèn xū zhèng; shen xu zheng
V012-N03194.definitions: Characterized by low back soreness, back pain, dizziness, insomnia, a poor memory, hearing decline, decreased libido and reproductive function. This pattern often results from congenital deficiency, postnatal over-exertion or chronic diseases.

V012-N03195.label: 肾虚髓亏证
V012-N03195.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03195.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03195.aliases: 肾虚髓减证； Kidney deficiency with marrow depletion pattern
V012-N03195.englishNames: Kidney deficiency with marrow depletion pattern; Kidney marrow deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of kidney deficiency and marrow depletion
V012-N03195.pinyinNames: shen xu sui kui zheng; shèn xū suǐ kuī zhèng
V012-N03195.definitions: Characterized by delayed growth and development, delayed healing of bone fracture, low back/knee soreness and weakness, dizziness, tinnitus, poor memory and dementia. This pattern often results from kidney essence/marrow deficiency.

V012-N03196.label: 肾阳虚证
V012-N03196.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03196.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03196.aliases: Kidney yang deficiency with water flooding pattern； Kidney yang deficiency with water retention pattern； Life-gate fire depletion pattern； Primordial yang deficiency pattern； 肾阳亏虚证； 肾阳虚损证； 肾虚寒证； 元阳亏虚证； 元阳虚衰证； 命门火衰证
V012-N03196.englishNames: Kidney yang deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of kidney yang deficiency; kidney yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03196.pinyinNames: shèn yáng xū zhèng; shen yang xu zheng
V012-N03196.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs (especially below the lower back and knee joints), a bright, pale or dark complexion, profuse, clear urine and frequent urination at night. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when kidney yang fails to warm the body.

V012-N03197.label: 肾阴虚证
V012-N03197.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03197.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03197.aliases: 阴虚肾亏证； 肾阴亏虚证； 肾阴虚热证； 肾阴虚损证； Genuine yin deficiency pattern； Kidney water depletion and deficiency pattern； Primordial yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03197.englishNames: Kidney yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of kidney yin deficiency; kidney yin deficiency pattern/syndrome; syndrome/ pattern of kidney yin deficiency
V012-N03197.pinyinNames: shen yin xu zheng; shèn yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03197.definitions: Characterized by low back/knee soreness and weakness, lassitude, dizziness, blurred vision, deafness, tinnitus, nocturnal emissions, a dry mouth, sore throat, flushed cheeks, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, and tidal fever in the afternoon. The tongue is red with no or scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often results from kidney yin deficiency.

V012-N03198.label: 肾阴阳两虚证
V012-N03198.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03198.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03198.aliases: Kidney yin and yang deficiency pattern
V012-N03198.englishNames: syndrome/ pattern of dual deficiency of kidney yin and yang; syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of kidney yin and yang; Deficiency of kidney yin and yang pattern; Kidney yin and yang deficiency pattern
V012-N03198.pinyinNames: shen yin yang liang xu zheng; shèn yīn yáng liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03198.definitions: Characterized by low back soreness, back pain, dizziness, insomnia, poor memory, hearing decline, low libido and decreased reproductive function. This pattern often results from congenital deficiency, over-exertion or chronic diseases.

V012-N03199.label: 胃寒气逆证
V012-N03199.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03199.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03199.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi counterflow due to stomach cold; Cold-induced ascending of stomach qi pattern
V012-N03199.pinyinNames: wei han qi ni zheng; wèi hán qì nì zhèng
V012-N03199.definitions: Characterized by vomiting, hiccups, belching, and cold stomach ache. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is wiry and tense. This pattern often occurs when cold affects the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N03200.label: 胃气上逆证
V012-N03200.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03200.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03200.aliases: Stomach qi reverse flow pattern； Stomach qi uprising pattern
V012-N03200.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of ascending counterflow of stomach qi; Ascending of stomach qi pattern; Stomach qi uprising pattern
V012-N03200.pinyinNames: wei qi shang ni zheng; wèi qì shàng nì zhèng
V012-N03200.definitions: Characterized by vomiting, hiccups, and belching. This pattern often occurs when cold, heat, diet or emotions causes stomach qi to ascend.

V012-N03201.label: 胃气虚证
V012-N03201.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03201.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03201.aliases: 胃气亏证； 胃气亏虚证
V012-N03201.englishNames: Stomach qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of stomach qi deficiency; stomach qi deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03201.pinyinNames: wei qi xu zheng; wèi qì xū zhèng
V012-N03201.definitions: Characterized by gastric distension/tightness and dull pain that alleviates upon pressure or after eating food, a poor appetite, and fatigue. The tongue is pale and tender. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when stomach qi fails to perform absorption and transportation.

V012-N03202.label: 胃火证
V012-N03202.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03202.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03202.aliases: 胃热炽盛证； 胃火炽盛证； 胃实热证； 胃热证； Stomach heat exuberance pattern； stomach fire exuberance pattern； Excess stomach heat pattern； Stomach heat pattern
V012-N03202.definitions: Characterized by a burning stomach pain with tenderness, thirst with a preference for cold drinks, gum swelling and pain, gum bleeding, constipation and scanty, yellow urine. Hunger after eating food and a foul breath may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when accumulated fire heat in the stomach affects the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N03203.label: 胃热气逆证
V012-N03203.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03203.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03203.aliases: 胃火气逆证； Fire-induced ascending of stomach qi pattern
V012-N03203.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi counterflow due to stomach heat; Heat-induced ascending of stomach qi pattern
V012-N03203.pinyinNames: wei re qi ni zheng; wèi rè qì nì zhèng
V012-N03203.definitions: Characterized by vomiting, hiccups, belching, a burning stomach ache and thirst. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when fire/heat or hot, spicy food disturbs the descending of stomach qi.

V012-N03204.label: 胃热阴虚证
V012-N03204.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03204.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03204.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency due to stomach heat; Stomach heat with yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03204.pinyinNames: wei re yin xu zheng; wèi rè yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03204.definitions: Characterized by a burning stomach ache, thirst, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, and constipation. The tongue is dry and red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant stomach heat consumes yin fluids.

V012-N03205.label: 胃燥津伤证
V012-N03205.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03205.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03205.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fluid damage due to stomach dryness; syndrome/pattern of fluid damage clue to stomach dryness; Stomach dryness due to loss of fluids pattern
V012-N03205.pinyinNames: wei zao jin shang zheng; wèi zào jīn shāng zhèng
V012-N03205.definitions: Characterized by stomach upset and stuffiness, hunger but no appetite, thirst, constipation, and a dry tongue. This pattern often occurs when fluids fail to moisten the stomach.

V012-N03206.label: 胃肠实热证
V012-N03206.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03206.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03206.aliases: 胃肠积热证； Heat accumulating in the stomach and intestines pattern
V012-N03206.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of excess-heat in the stomach and intestine; Excess heat in the stomach and intestines pattern
V012-N03206.pinyinNames: wei chang shi re zheng; wèi cháng shí rè zhèng
V012-N03206.definitions: Characterized by a high fever or severe feverish sensation in the afternoon, a burning stomach ache that alleviates after eating cool-property food, thirst, sweating, abdominal pain/hardness/distension with tenderness, constipation, strong-odoured stools, and scanty, yellow urine. In severe cases, unconsciousness, delirium, mania and faecal impaction with watery diarrhoea may also be present. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. Alternatively, the tongue may be dark brown with thorns. The pulse is rapid or deep, replete and forceful. This pattern often occurs when exuberant heat accumulates in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N03207.label: 胃肠气滞证
V012-N03207.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03207.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03207.englishNames: Qi stagnation of the stomach and intestines pattern; syndrome/pattern of  qi stagnation in stomach and intestine; syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation in stomach and intestine; gastrointestinal qi stagnation pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03207.pinyinNames: wèi cháng qì zhì zhèng; wei chang qi zhi zheng
