V012-N03207.definitions: Characterized by gastric or abdominal masses/distension/(migratory) pain that alleviates upon belching or flatus. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when qi of the stomach and intestines becomes stagnant.

V012-N03208.label: 胃肠湿热证
V012-N03208.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03208.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03208.englishNames: Damp heat in the stomach and intestines pattern
V012-N03208.definitions: Characterized by abdominal masses/distension, vomiting, nausea, a poor appetite, loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation, fever and thirst. Stools containing pus or blood, tenesmus, vomiting and violent diarrhoea may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat accumulates in the stomach and intestines.

V012-N03209.label: 胃肠瘀滞证
V012-N03209.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03209.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03209.aliases: 肠胃瘀滞证； 瘀滞胃肠证； Stasis obstructing the stomach and intestines pattern
V012-N03209.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stasis and stagnation in the intestine and stomach; Blood stasis in the stomach and intestines pattern
V012-N03209.pinyinNames: wei chang yu zhi zheng; wèi cháng yū zhì zhèng
V012-N03209.definitions: Characterized by stabbing gastric or abdominal pain with tenderness, palpable masses, haematemesis, and stools containing dark blood clots. The tongue has ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the stomach and intestines.

V012-N03210.label: 胃阴虚证
V012-N03210.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03210.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03210.aliases: 胃阴亏虚证； 胃阴不足证
V012-N03210.englishNames: Stomach yin deficiency pattern; stomach yin deficiency pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of stomach yin deficiency; syndrome/ pattern of stomach yin deficiency
V012-N03210.pinyinNames: wei yin xu zheng; wèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03210.definitions: Characterized by a dry mouth and throat, hunger but no desire to eat food, and a dull stomach ache with a burning sensation. Gastric upset/distension, retching, hiccups and constipation may also be present. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids fail to nourish the stomach.

V012-N03211.label: 胆实热证
V012-N03211.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03211.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03211.englishNames: Excess heat in the gallbladder pattern
V012-N03211.definitions: Characterized by yellowish sclera, skin and urine, distending pain in the subcostal region, vomiting of bile, headache, dizziness, a bitter mouth and a dry, sore throat. This pattern often occurs when exuberant damp heat accumulates in the gallbladder.

V012-N03212.label: 胆气虚证
V012-N03212.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03212.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03212.aliases: 胆气亏虚证； 胆虚气怯证
V012-N03212.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of gallbladder qi deficiency; gallbladder qi deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Gallbladder qi deficiency pattern
V012-N03212.pinyinNames: dan qi xu zheng; dǎn qì xū zhèng
V012-N03212.definitions: Characterized by jumpiness or easily startled, palpitations, insomnia, nightmares, shortness of breath, spontaneous sweating, tinnitus, blurred vision, limb oedema, loss of taste during eating, and restlessness. The tongue is pale with a greasy coating. The pulse is deep and delayed. This pattern often results from gallbladder qi deficiency and dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03213.label: 胆热痰扰证
V012-N03213.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03213.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03213.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of gallbladder heat and phlegm  harassment; Gallbladder heat with phlegm disturbance pattern; syndrome/pattern of gallbladder heat and phlegm harassment
V012-N03213.pinyinNames: dan re tan rao zheng; dǎn rè tán rǎo zhèng
V012-N03213.definitions: Characterized by a bitter mouth, restlessness, timidity, jumpiness or easily startled, distension and fullness in the subcostal region, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, vertigo and a bitter mouth. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often results from gallbladder heat coupled with internal phlegm retention.

V012-N03214.label: 胆经郁热证
V012-N03214.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03214.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03214.aliases: 胆经实热证； 胆火证； 胆热证
V012-N03214.englishNames: Stagnant heat in the gallbladder meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of stagnated heat in the gallbladder meridian
V012-N03214.pinyinNames: dǎn jīng yù rè zhèng; dan jing yu re zheng
V012-N03214.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, irritability, distension in the subcostal region, a bitter mouth, ear distension/pain, tinnitus, headache on one side, insomnia and dream-disturbed sleep. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is wiry and rapid. This pattern often occurs when internal fire/heat remains in the gallbladder meridian.

V012-N03215.label: 胆腑虫扰证
V012-N03215.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03215.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03215.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of gallbladder worms harassment; Parasitic roundworms disturbing the gallbladder pattern
V012-N03215.pinyinNames: dan fu chong rao zheng; dǎn fǔ chóng rǎo zhèng
V012-N03215.definitions: Characterized by paroxysmal drilling pain in the upper abdomen coupled with a pale complexion, cold limbs, vomiting of bitter saliva or roundworms. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when roundworms disturb the gallbladder.

V012-N03216.label: 胆郁痰扰证
V012-N03216.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03216.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03216.englishNames: Gallbladder depression with phlegm harassment pattern; syndrome/pattern of gallbladder depression and phlegm harassment; Gallbladder stagnation with phlegm disturbance pattern; pattern/syndrome of depressed gallbladder with harassing phlegm
V012-N03216.pinyinNames: dan yu tan rao zheng; dǎn yù tán rǎo zhèng
V012-N03216.definitions: Characterized by restlessness, timidity, jumpiness or easily startled, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, distension and fullness in the subcostal region, repeated sighing, vertigo, nausea, vomiting and vomiting of phlegm and salivation. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry and delayed. This pattern often occurs when internal turbid phlegm disturbs the dispersal of gallbladder qi.

V012-N03217.label: 胎毒蕴热证
V012-N03217.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03217.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03217.aliases: 胎毒蕴结证； 胎毒内蕴证； 胎毒证； Accumulation of fetal toxin pattern
V012-N03217.englishNames: Fetal toxin and heat accumulation pattern; syndrome/pattern of fetal toxin accumulating into heat
V012-N03217.pinyinNames: tai dong yun re zheng; tāi dú yùn rè zhèng
V012-N03217.definitions: Characterized by skin ulceration and peeling. Symptoms may include mouth ulceration, ulceration of the eyes, or stools with a strong odour (that may contain blood). Often results from contraction of toxic heat as a fetus.

V012-N03218.label: 胞宫湿热证
V012-N03218.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03218.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03218.aliases: Dampness heat in the uterus pattern； 湿热蕴胞证
V012-N03218.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of uterine dampness and heat; Damp heat in the uterus pattern; Dampness-heat in the uterus pattern
V012-N03218.pinyinNames: bao gong shi re zheng; bāo gōng shī rè zhèng
V012-N03218.definitions: Characterized by profuse, yellow, sticky and smelly vaginal discharge, and itching and ulceration of the vulva. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat remains in the uterus.

V012-N03219.label: 胞宫瘀阻证
V012-N03219.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03219.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03219.aliases: 瘀滞胞宫证； 瘀阻胞宫证
V012-N03219.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of uterine obstruction; Blood stasis obstructing the uterus pattern
V012-N03219.pinyinNames: bao gong yu zu zheng; bāo gōng yū zǔ zhèng
V012-N03219.definitions: Characterized by stabbing pain in the lower abdomen with a fixed location and tenderness, possibly abdominal lumps, and delayed period with a scanty volume, dark purple colour and clots. Amenorrhoea or uterine bleeding may also be present. The tongue is dark purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when stagnant blood affects the uterus.

V012-N03220.label: 胞宫虚寒证
V012-N03220.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03220.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03220.aliases: 胞宫阳虚证； Uterine deficiency cold pattern； Uterine yang deficiency pattern； Yang deficiency of the uterus
V012-N03220.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of uterine deficiency-cold; Deficiency cold in the uterus pattern; Uterine deficiency cold pattern
V012-N03220.pinyinNames: bao gong xu han zheng; bāo gōng xū hán zhèng
V012-N03220.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, lower abdominal pain with a cold sensation that alleviates upon warmth and pressure and a pale menstrual colour and thin quality of menses. Alternatively, thin, clear vaginal discharge, infertility, miscarriage and a pale complexion may be present. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is deep and weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi fails to warm the uterus.

V012-N03221.label: 胞宫血热证
V012-N03221.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03221.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03221.aliases: 胞宫郁热证； 胞宫积热证； Blood and heat accumulation in the uterus pattern； Blood heat build up in the uterus pattern； Heat accumulating in the uterus
V012-N03221.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood-heat in the uterus; Blood heat in the uterus pattern; Blood and heat accumulation in the uterus pattern
V012-N03221.pinyinNames: bao gong xue re zheng; bāo gōng xuè rè zhèng
V012-N03221.definitions: Characterized by a burning pain in the lower abdomen, and earlier period with profuse volume and bright red colour. Yellow, sticky and smelly vagina discharge may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when heat accumulates in the uterus.

V012-N03222.label: 胞宫证类
V012-N03222.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03222.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03222.englishNames: category of uterus syndromes/ patterns; Patterns involving the uterus; category of uterus syndrome/pattern
V012-N03222.pinyinNames: bao gong zheng lei; bāo gōng zhèng lèi

V012-N03223.label: 脏腑兼证类
V012-N03223.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03223.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03224.label: 脏腑经络证类
V012-N03224.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03224.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03224.englishNames: Patterns of the zang–fu organs and meridians
V012-N03224.pinyinNames: zàng fǔ jīng luò zhèng lèi

V012-N03225.label: 脓毒证
V012-N03225.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03225.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03225.aliases: 脓毒蕴积证； Accumulation of toxic pus pattern
V012-N03225.englishNames: Toxic pus pattern; pus toxin syndrome/pattern
V012-N03225.pinyinNames: nóng dú zhèng; nong dong zheng
V012-N03225.definitions: Characterized by sores/ulcers with the discharge of pus with a strong odour, fever and thirst. The tongue coating is putrid and greasy. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when toxic pus accumulates in the body.

V012-N03226.label: 脱证
V012-N03226.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03226.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03226.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of collapse; Collapse pattern; Desertion Syndrome
V012-N03226.definitions: Characterized by sudden fainting or clouding of consciousness, limb flaccidity, open hands, cold limbs and profuse sweating. In severe cases, body sweats with a cold sensation, bowel and bladder incontinence and a limp tongue may be present. The tongue is dark purple with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is deep and delayed or deep and feeble. This pattern often results from exhaustion of Yuan-primordial qi and mental cloudiness.

V012-N03227.label: 脾不统血证
V012-N03227.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03227.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03227.aliases: 脾不摄血证； Spleen failing to hold blood within the vessels
V012-N03227.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of spleen failing to govern the blood; pattern/syndrome of spleen failing to control the blood; Spleen failing to control blood pattern; Spleen failing to control the blood pattern
V012-N03227.pinyinNames: pi bu tong xue zheng; pí bù tǒng xuè zhèng
V012-N03227.definitions: Characterized by chronic bleeding, purpura, uterine bleeding, heavy menstrual flow, early menstruation, poor appetite, abdominal distension, loose stools and fatigue. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when spleen qi fails to keep the blood within the vessels.

V012-N03228.label: 脾气下陷证
V012-N03228.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03228.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03228.aliases: 脾虚气陷证； 中气下陷证； Spleen qi sinking pattern； Middle qi sinking pattern； Qi sinking of the middle jiao
V012-N03228.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi collapse due to spleen deficiency; Spleen qi sinking pattern
V012-N03228.pinyinNames: pi qi xia xian zheng; pí qì xià xiàn zhèng
V012-N03228.definitions: Characterized by a bearing-down sensation in the abdomen that aggravates after eating food. Frequent urges to defaecate with a bearing-down sensation of the anus, persistent diarrhoea, rectal prolapse, prolapsed uterus or cloudy urine may be present. Associated symptoms may include shortness of breath, fatigue, lassitude, reluctance to talk, dizziness, blurred vision, a pale, lustreless face, poor appetite and loose stools. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is delayed and weak. This pattern often develops from severe spleen qi deficiency and occurs when spleen qi fails to ascend.

V012-N03229.label: 脾气不固证
V012-N03229.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03229.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03229.definitions: Characterized by persistent diarrhoea, shortness of breath, a bearing-down sensation, a poor appetite, and abdominal distension. In severe cases, faecal incontinence may also be present. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often occurs when spleen qi fails to secure the intestines.

V012-N03230.label: 脾气虚证
V012-N03230.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03230.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03230.aliases: 脾气亏虚证； Spleen qi deficiency pattern； Spleen qi depletion pattern； Spleen qi insecurity pattern； Insufficiency of spleen
V012-N03230.englishNames: Spleen qi deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen qi deficiency; spleen qi deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03230.pinyinNames: pi qi xu zheng; pí qì xū zhèng
V012-N03230.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension that aggravates after eating food, loose stools, and lassitude. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is slack and weak. This pattern often occurs when spleen qi fails to transport and transform.

V012-N03231.label: 脾系证类
V012-N03231.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03231.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03231.englishNames: category of spleen system syndromes/patterns; Spleen system patterns; Patterns of the spleen system
V012-N03231.pinyinNames: pi xi zheng lei; pí xì zhèng lèi

V012-N03232.label: 脾肺两虚证
V012-N03232.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03232.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03232.aliases: 土不生金证； 脾肺气虚证； Lung and spleen deficiency pattern； Qi deficiency of the spleen and lung
V012-N03232.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of spleen and lung; Deficiency of the spleen and lung pattern
V012-N03232.pinyinNames: pi fei liang xu zheng; pí fèi liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03232.definitions: Characterized by low-pitched cough with thin, clear sputum, shortness of breath, panting, a low food intake, abdominal bloating and loose stools. The tongue is pale with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from qi deficiency of the lung and spleen.

V012-N03233.label: 脾肾不固证
V012-N03233.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03233.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03233.aliases: 脾肾气虚不固证； 脾肾气虚下陷证； Qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney pattern
V012-N03233.englishNames: syndrome/ pattern of insecurity of spleen and kidney; syndrome/pattern of insecurity of spleen and kidney; Insecurity of the spleen and kidney pattern
V012-N03233.pinyinNames: pi shen bu gu zheng; pí shèn bú gù zhèng
V012-N03233.definitions: Characterized by turbid urine or urinary incontinence, shortness of breath, and a bearing-down sensation. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from sinking of spleen qi and insecurity of kidney qi.

V012-N03234.label: 脾肾两虚证
V012-N03234.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03234.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03234.aliases: 脾肾不足证； 脾肾两亏证； 脾肾亏虚证； Insufficiency of the spleen and kidney pattern
V012-N03234.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of spleen and kidney; Deficiency of the spleen and kidney pattern
V012-N03234.pinyinNames: pi shen liang xu zheng; pí shèn liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03234.definitions: Characterized by a low food intake, abdominal bloating, loose stools, low back soreness/pain, and tinnitus. This pattern often occurs as a result of deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N03235.label: 脾肾气虚证
V012-N03235.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03235.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03235.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi deficiency in spleen and kidney; Qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney pattern
V012-N03235.pinyinNames: pi shen qi xu zheng; pí shèn qì xū zhèng
V012-N03235.definitions: Characterized by lassitude, shortness of breath, a low food intake, abdominal bloating, loose stools, chronic diarrhoea, low back soreness/pain, and tinnitus. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. This pattern often results from qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney.

V012-N03236.label: 脾肾阳虚证
V012-N03236.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03236.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03236.aliases: 脾肾虚寒证； Spleen and kidney yang deficiency pattern； Deficiency cold of the spleen and kidney
V012-N03236.englishNames: Spleen and kidney yang deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency in spleen and kidney; Yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney pattern; spleen-kidney yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03236.pinyinNames: pi shen yang xu zheng; pí shèn yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03236.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, a bright, pale complexion, low back soreness, abdominal pain with a cold sensation, chronic diarrhoea with stools that contain undigested food. Alternatively, oedema and scanty urine may be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is deep, slow and weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi deficiency of the spleen and kidney causes internal deficiency cold.

V012-N03237.label: 脾胃不和证
V012-N03237.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03237.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03237.aliases: 脾胃气滞证； 中焦不和证； 中焦气滞证； Disharmony of the middle jiao pattern； Qi stagnation of the spleen and stomach pattern； Qi stagnation of the middle jiao pattern
V012-N03237.englishNames: spleen-stomach disharmony pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of spleen and stomach disharmony; Disharmony between the spleen and stomach pattern
V012-N03237.pinyinNames: pi wei bu he zheng; pí wèi bù hé zhèng
V012-N03237.definitions: Characterized by gastric or abdominal stuffiness, distension and discomfort, and poor appetite. Alternatively, abdominal distension after eating food, loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation, belching and bowel sounds may be present. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often occurs when qi of the spleen and stomach becomes stagnant.

V012-N03238.label: 脾胃实热证
V012-N03238.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03238.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03238.aliases: 脾胃积热证； 脾胃热盛证； 中焦实热证； 中焦积热证； 中焦热盛证； Heat accumulating in the spleen and stomach/middle jiao pattern； Excess heat in the spleen and stomach/middle jiao pattern
V012-N03238.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of excess-heat in the spleen and stomach; Excess heat in the spleen and stomach pattern
V012-N03238.pinyinNames: pi wei shi re zheng; pí wèi shí rè zhèng
V012-N03238.definitions: Characterized by a burning stomach ache that alleviates after eating cool-property food, fever, thirst, abdominal pain/distension, and constipation. A foul breath, red, painful mouth ulcerations, gum swelling, pain, and bleeding may also be present. The tongue is red with a yellow coating. The pulse is rapid. This pattern often occurs when exuberant fire heat accumulates in the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03239.label: 脾胃湿热证
V012-N03239.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03239.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03239.aliases: 中焦湿热证； 湿热中阻证； Damp heat in the middle jiao pattern
V012-N03239.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the spleen and stomach; pattern/syndrome of dampness-heat in the spleen and stomach; Damp heat in the spleen and stomach pattern
V012-N03239.pinyinNames: pi wei shi re zheng; pí wèi shī rè zhèng
V012-N03239.definitions: Characterized by feverish sensation (but no fever) that worsens in the afternoon, abdominal masses/distension, nausea, vomiting, a poor appetite, thirst but drink only a small amount of water, yellow urine, and loose stools. This pattern often occurs when damp heat affects the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03240.label: 脾胃阳虚证
V012-N03240.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03240.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03240.aliases: 脾胃虚寒证； 中焦虚寒证； 中焦阳虚证； Deficiency cold of the spleen and stomach pattern； Yang deficiency/deficiency cold of the middle jiao pattern
V012-N03240.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency in spleen and stomach; Yang deficiency of the spleen and stomach pattern; syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency in  spleen and stomach
V012-N03240.pinyinNames: pi wei yang xu zheng; pí wèi yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03240.definitions: Characterized by abdominal distension, low food intake, cold abdominal pain that alleviates with warmth and pressure, cold intolerance, cold limbs, and loose stools. The tongue is pale with a white, moist coating. The pulse is deep, slow and weak. This pattern often occurs when yang qi fails to warm the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03241.label: 脾胃阴虚证
V012-N03241.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03241.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03241.aliases: 脾胃虚热证； 中焦阴虚证； 中焦虚热症； Yin deficiency/deficiency heat of the middle jiao pattern； Deficiency heat of the spleen and stomach pattern； 中焦虚热证
V012-N03241.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in spleen and stomach; Yin deficiency of the spleen and stomach pattern; spleen-stomach yin deficiency pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency in  spleen and stomach
V012-N03241.pinyinNames: pi wei yin xu zheng; pí wèi yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03241.definitions: Characterized by a dry mouth and throat, hunger but no desire to eat food, weight loss, and constipation. Gastric upset, dull abdominal pain/distension, retching, and hiccups may also be present. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids fail to nourish the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03242.label: 脾虚气滞证
V012-N03242.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03242.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03242.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of qi stagnation due to spleen deficiency; Spleen deficiency with qi stagnation pattern
V012-N03242.pinyinNames: pi xu qi zhi zheng; pí xū qì zhì zhèng
V012-N03242.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension/pain, loose stools with a sensation of incomplete evacuation, bowel sounds, flatus and lassitude. The pulse is wiry. This pattern often results from spleen qi deficiency with an obstructed flow of qi.

V012-N03243.label: 脾虚水泛证
V012-N03243.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03243.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03243.aliases: 脾虚水停证； 脾虚水困证； 脾气虚水停证； 脾气虚水湿证； Spleen deficiency with qi stagnation pattern
V012-N03243.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of spleen deficiency with water flood; syndrome/pattern of water flooding due to spleen deficiency; Spleen deficiency with water retention pattern; Spleen deficiency with water flooding pattern
V012-N03243.pinyinNames: pi xu shui fan zheng; pí xū shuǐ fàn zhèng
V012-N03243.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, lassitude, a pale complexion, and facial puffiness. Ascites may also be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is soft. This pattern often occurs when spleen qi fails to transport and transform, resulting in water retention.

V012-N03244.label: 脾虚湿困证
V012-N03244.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03244.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03244.aliases: 脾虚夹湿证； 脾虚湿蕴证； 脾虚湿盛证； 脾虚湿泛证； Spleen deficiency with dampness accumulation pattern； Spleen deficiency with dampness encumbrance pattern； Spleen dampness with dampness accumulation pattern； 脾虚湿滞证
V012-N03244.englishNames: Spleen deficiency with dampness pattern; Spleen deficiency with dampness accumulation pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen deficiency with dampness encumbrance; pattern/syndrome of spleen deficiency with dampness encumbrance
V012-N03244.pinyinNames: pí xū shī kùn zhèng; pi xu shi kun zheng
V012-N03244.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, and body heaviness or mild oedema. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, moist or greasy coating. The pulse is soft and slack. This pattern often results from internal retention of turbid dampness due to spleen qi deficiency.

V012-N03245.label: 脾虚湿热证
V012-N03245.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03245.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03245.englishNames: Spleen deficiency with damp heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen deficiency with dampness and heat
V012-N03245.pinyinNames: pi xu shi re zheng; pí xū shī rè zhèng
V012-N03245.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, feverish sensation but no fever, and body heaviness. The tongue is red and enlarged with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often results from spleen qi deficiency coupled with internal build-up of damp heat.

V012-N03246.label: 脾虚生风证
V012-N03246.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03246.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03246.definitions: Characterized by mild twitching of the hands/feet, cold limbs, faint breathing from the nose and mouth and sleep with eyes open. Often occurs when chronic vomiting/diarrhoea or overuse of purgative medicine damage spleen qi. In children, this pattern occurs when congenital spleen and kidney deficiency generates internal wind.

V012-N03247.label: 脾虚痰湿证
V012-N03247.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03247.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03247.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of phlegm dampness due to spleen deficiency; Spleen deficiency with phlegm dampness pattern
V012-N03247.pinyinNames: pi xu tan shi zheng; pí xū tán shī zhèng
V012-N03247.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, obesity, body heaviness, fatigue, and drowsiness. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, greasy coating. The pulse is soft and slack. This pattern often results from spleen qi deficiency coupled with internal build up of phlegm dampness.

V012-N03248.label: 脾虚血亏证
V012-N03248.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03248.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03248.aliases: Spleen deficiency with blood insufficiency pattern
V012-N03248.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood depletion due to spleen deficiency; Spleen deficiency and blood depletion pattern; Spleen deficiency with blood deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood depletion clue to spleen deficiency
V012-N03248.pinyinNames: pi xu xue kui zheng; pí xū xuè kuī zhèng
V012-N03248.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, dizziness, fatigue, amenorrhea, delayed period, scanty menstruation, and a pale face. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready and weak. This pattern often occurs when spleen qi fails to generate sufficient blood.

V012-N03249.label: 脾虚食积证
V012-N03249.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03249.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03249.aliases: 脾虚夹食证； Spleen deficiency with ingestion of food pattern
V012-N03249.englishNames: Spleen deficiency with food retention pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen deficiency with food retention
V012-N03249.pinyinNames: pi xu shi ji zheng; pí xū shí jī zhèng
V012-N03249.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, frequent diarrhoea, frequent abdominal distension/pain, acid reflux, a sensation of incomplete evacuation after diarrhoea, and strong-odoured stools. The tongue is pale with a greasy coating. This pattern often results from food retention in the stomach and intestines due to spleen failing to transport and transform.

V012-N03250.label: 脾阳虚水泛证
V012-N03250.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03250.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03250.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of water flooding due to spleen yang deficiency; Spleen yang deficiency with water retention pattern
V012-N03250.pinyinNames: pi yang xu shui fan zheng; pí yáng xū shuǐ fàn zhèng
V012-N03250.definitions: Characterized by low food intake, abdominal distension, loose stools, cold intolerance, facial puffiness, and leg oedema. Ascites may also be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is soft or weak. This pattern often occurs when spleen yang fails to warm and transport, resulting in water retention.

V012-N03251.label: 脾阳虚证
V012-N03251.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03251.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03251.aliases: 脾虚寒证； 脾阳亏虚证； 脾阳不足证； Spleen yang insufficiency pattern
V012-N03251.englishNames: Spleen yang deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen yang deficiency; spleen yang deficiency pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03251.pinyinNames: pi yang xu zheng; pí yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03251.definitions: Characterized by cold abdominal pain that alleviates upon warmth, vomiting of clear water, cold limbs, cold intolerance, loose stools, and clear, profuse urine. The tongue is pale, enlarged and tender with a white, moist coating. The pulse is deep and slow. This pattern often results from spleen yang deficiency coupled with internal cold.

V012-N03252.label: 脾阴虚证
V012-N03252.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03252.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03252.aliases: 脾阴亏虚证； Spleen yin depletion pattern； Insufficiency of spleen yin； 脾燥证
V012-N03252.englishNames: Spleen yin deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen yin  deficiency; syndrome/pattern of spleen yin deficiency; spleen yin deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03252.pinyinNames: pí yīn xū zhèng; pi yin xu zheng
V012-N03252.definitions: Characterized by a poor appetite, abdominal distension, constipation, weight loss, lassitude, scanty saliva, dry lips, and a low-grade fever. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when spleen yin deficiency affects the transportation and transformation of the spleen and stomach.

V012-N03253.label: 膀胱失约证
V012-N03253.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03253.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03253.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of bladder failing to control; Qi failing to secure the urinary bladder pattern
V012-N03253.pinyinNames: bang guang shi yue zheng; páng guāng shī yuē zhèng
V012-N03253.definitions: Characterized by frequent urge to urinate but unable to pass, white urine, lower abdominal tightness and distension, a bearing-down sensation of the anus, body heaviness, lassitude, reluctance to talk and shortness of breath. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a thin, white coating. The pulse is thready, weak or soft. This pattern often occurs when kidney qi fails to secure the urinary bladder.

V012-N03254.label: 膀胱湿热证
V012-N03254.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03254.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03254.englishNames: Bladder dampness-heat pattern; Damp heat in the urinary bladder pattern; bladder dampnessheat pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of dampness and heat in the bladder
V012-N03254.pinyinNames: páng guāng shī rè zhèng; bang guang shi re zheng
V012-N03254.definitions: Characterized by urgent, frequent and painful urination with a burning sensation, fever and thirst. Alternatively, the urine may be turbid or contain stones. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. The pulse is slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when damp heat accumulates in the urinary bladder.

V012-N03255.label: 膀胱蕴热证
V012-N03255.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03255.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03255.aliases: 膀胱实热证； 膀胱积热证； 膀胱蓄热证； Bladder heat accumulation pattern； Bladder heat excess pattern； Bladder heat retention pattern； Excess heat in the urinary bladder； 热迫膀胱证； 热结膀胱证； 热积膀胱证
V012-N03255.englishNames: Heat accumulating in the urinary bladder pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat accumulation in the baldder; Bladder heat accumulation pattern; syndrome/pattern of heat accumulation in the bladder
V012-N03255.pinyinNames: páng guāng yùn rè zhèng; bang guang yun re zheng
V012-N03255.definitions: Characterized by lower abdominal hardness, fullness and contracture, hot, painful urination, fever without aversion to cold, and mania. This pattern often occurs when heat enters the urinary bladder.

V012-N03256.label: 膀胱虚寒证
V012-N03256.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03256.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03256.aliases: 膀胱虚冷证； Bladder deficiency cold pattern
V012-N03256.englishNames: bladder deficiency cold pattern/ syndrome; Bladder deficiency cold pattern; syndrome/pattern of deficiency-cold in bladder; Deficiency cold of the urinary bladder pattern
V012-N03256.pinyinNames: bang guang xu han zheng; páng guāng xū hán zhèng
V012-N03256.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, lower abdominal pain with a cold sensation, urinary incontinence, inhibited urination, frequent urination at night with clear, profuse urine. The tongue coating is white and slippery. This pattern often occurs when kidney yang fails to secure the qi transformation of the urinary bladder.

V012-N03257.label: 营分证
V012-N03257.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03257.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03257.aliases: 热炽营分； 营分病； 营分热盛； 营分证候； 营热炽盛； 营分热盛证； Nutrient phase patterns； Ying aspect patterns； Exuberant heat in the Ying nutrients phase
V012-N03257.englishNames: Yingfen Syndrome; nutrient aspect pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of nutrient phase; Nutrient phase patterns; Ying nutrients phase pattern
V012-N03257.pinyinNames: ying fen zheng; yíng fèn zhèng
V012-N03257.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates at night, restlessness and insomnia. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat consumes the Ying nutrient yin and disturbs the heart mind.

V012-N03258.label: 营卫不和证
V012-N03258.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03258.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03258.aliases: Nutrient qi and defence qi disharmony pattern
V012-N03258.englishNames: Nutrient qi and defense qi disharmony pattern; syndrome/pattern of nutrient qi and defense qi disharmony; Disharmony between the Ying nutrients and Wei-defence pattern
V012-N03258.pinyinNames: ying wei bu he zheng; yíng wèi bù hé zhèng
V012-N03258.definitions: Characterized by spontaneous sweating without fever. Alternatively, patients may present with fever and spontaneous sweating, or neither. This pattern often occurs when yang qi becomes deficient or when stagnant yang qi in the surface of the body affects Ying nutrients and yin fluids.

V012-N03259.label: 虚实夹杂证
V012-N03259.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03259.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03259.englishNames: Combined deficiency and excess pattern; syndrome/pattern of deficiency and excess complex
V012-N03259.pinyinNames: xū shí jiā zá zhèng; xu shi jia za zheng
V012-N03259.definitions: Characterized by coexistence of excess of pathogenic factors and deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi. It may present as a deficiency pattern coupled with excess, an excess pattern coupled with deficiency, or mixed deficiency and excess.

V012-N03260.label: 虚寒证
V012-N03260.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03260.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03260.aliases: 里虚寒证； 脏腑虚寒证； 脏腑虚冷证
V012-N03260.englishNames: Deficiency cold pattern; deficiency cold pattern/syndrome; syndrome/pattern of deficiency cold
V012-N03260.pinyinNames: xu han zheng; xū hán zhèng
V012-N03260.definitions: Characterized by a bright, pale complexion, cold intolerance, and clear, profuse urine. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is deep, slow or deep, thready and weak. Contributing factors may include constitutional yang qi deficiency, chronic internal dysfunctions or inappropriate treatment damaging yang qi.

V012-N03261.label: 虚证
V012-N03261.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03261.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03261.englishNames: deficiency pattern/ syndrome; deficiency syndrome/pattern; Deficiency Symptom Complex; Deficiency pattern
V012-N03261.definitions: Characterized by general weakness, a weak pulse and abdominal softness. Often results from insufficiency of qi, yin, yang or blood.

V012-N03262.label: 虚阳外越证
V012-N03262.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03262.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03262.aliases: 虚阳上浮证； 虚阳浮越证
V012-N03262.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of external floating of yang deficiency; Floating of deficiency yang pattern
V012-N03262.pinyinNames: xu yang wai yue zheng; xū yáng wài yuè zhèng
V012-N03262.definitions: Characterized by toothache, mouth ulceration, insomnia, dizziness, hot flushes, feverish sensations in the palms and soles, red, swollen lips, profuse, clear urine, and loose or dry stools. Often occurs when internal cold retention (due to kidney yang deficiency) causes the deficiency yang qi to float outward.

V012-N03263.label: 血分证
V012-N03263.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03263.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03263.aliases: 血热内扰证； 热炽血分证； 血热证； 热炽血分； 血热内扰； 温邪侵袭血分证
V012-N03263.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood phase; Xuefen Syndrome; blood aspect pattern/ syndrome; Blood phase patterns; Blood phase pattern
V012-N03263.pinyinNames: xue fen zheng; xuè fèn zhèng
V012-N03263.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates at night, restlessness, insomnia, unconsciousness, delirium, a dark purple tongue and a wiry, rapid pulse. Alternatively, dark purple maculae, haematemesis, nosebleed, bloody stools, bloody urine, a dry tongue with a scanty coating and a thready, deficient pulse may be present. This pattern often occurs when heat in febrile conditions moves blood, stirs wind and consumes yin.

V012-N03264.label: 血寒证
V012-N03264.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03264.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03264.englishNames: blood cold pattern/ syndrome; Blood cold pattern; syndrome/pattern ofblood cold; syndrome/pattern of blood cold
V012-N03264.pinyinNames: xue han zheng; xuè hán zhèng
V012-N03264.definitions: Characterized by pain in the hands/feet with a dark purple colour and cold sensation, the pain alleviates with warmth and aggravates with cold. Alternatively, lower abdominal pain, cold limbs and delayed period with a dark purple menstrual colour and clots may be present. The tongue is pale dark with a white coating. The pulse is deep, slow and hesitant. Often occurs when cold causes the qi and blood to stagnate.

V012-N03265.label: 血热证
V012-N03265.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03265.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03265.englishNames: Blood heat pattern; blood heat pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of blood heat
V012-N03265.pinyinNames: xue re zheng; xuè rè zhèng
V012-N03265.definitions: Characterized by haemoptysis, haematemesis, nosebleed, bloody urine, bloody stools with a bright red, sticky colour, and earlier period with heavy menstruation. Alternatively, red, swollen and painful sores/boils, restlessness, thirst, and fever may be present. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often occurs when exuberant fire heat in the zang–fu organs causes the blood to flow recklessly.

V012-N03266.label: 血燥证
V012-N03266.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03266.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03266.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood dryness; Blood dryness pattern
V012-N03266.pinyinNames: xue zao zheng; xuè zào zhèng
V012-N03266.definitions: Characterized by flaking of grey-white dandruff and dry scalp. Often results from blood/fluid deficiency, spleen deficiency or heat-damaging yin.

V012-N03267.label: 血瘀动血证
V012-N03267.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03267.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03267.englishNames: Blood stasis stirring blood pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood stasis stirring the blood
V012-N03267.pinyinNames: xuè yū dòng xuè zhèng; xue yu dong xue zheng
V012-N03267.definitions: Characterized by bleeding of dark purple blood with clots, localized stabbing pain with a fixed position or bluish lumps. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is hesitant. Often occurs when stagnant blood causes the blood to flow out of the vessels.

V012-N03268.label: 血瘀化热证
V012-N03268.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03268.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03268.aliases: 瘀血化热证； 血瘀化火证； 瘀热内郁证； 瘀滞化热证； Stasis transforming into heat pattern
V012-N03268.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood stasis transforming into heat; Blood stasis transformed heat pattern
V012-N03268.pinyinNames: xue yu hua re zheng; xuè yū huà rè zhèng
V012-N03268.definitions: Characterized by stabbing pain with a burning sensation, bluish lumps, afternoon or night fever, and a dry mouth with no desire to drink water. The tongue is dark red or with ecchymosis. The pulse is hesitant and rapid. Often occurs when prolonged blood stasis transforms into heat.

V012-N03269.label: 血瘀水停证
V012-N03269.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03269.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03269.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood stasis and water retention; Blood stasis induced water retention pattern; pattern/syndrome of blood stasis with water retention
V012-N03269.pinyinNames: xue yu shui ting zheng; xuè yū shuǐ tíng zhèng
V012-N03269.definitions: Characterized by abdominal masses with a stabbing pain, abdominal bulging, inhibited urination, amenorrhoea or oedema. Alternatively, bluish, diffuse swelling and distending pain may be present. The tongue is pale purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is hesitant. Often occurs when stagnant blood causes water retention.

V012-N03270.label: 血瘀证
V012-N03270.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03270.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03270.aliases: 瘀血内阻证； 瘀血阻滞证
V012-N03270.englishNames: Blood stasis pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood stasis; blood stasis pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03270.pinyinNames: xue yu zheng; xuè yū zhèng
V012-N03270.definitions: Characterized by local bluish lumps with tenderness. Alternatively, abdominal masses with stabbing pain in a fixed location or dark purple haemorrhagic spots may be present. The tongue is purple or with ecchymosis. The pulse is wiry and hesitant. Often occurs when stagnant blood obstructs the circulation of blood.

V012-N03271.label: 血脱证
V012-N03271.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03271.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03271.englishNames: Blood collapse pattern; blood collapse pattern/syndrome
V012-N03271.definitions: Characterized by a pale face, dizziness, blurred vision, mild to severe palpitations, shortness of breath, and cold limbs. The tongue is pale. The pulse feels like a scallion stalk. Often results from sudden or chronic blood loss.

V012-N03272.label: 血虚内热证
V012-N03272.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03272.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03272.englishNames: Blood deficiency induced internal heat pattern; syndrome/pattern of internal heat due to blood deficiency
V012-N03272.pinyinNames: xuè xū nèi rè zhèng; xue xu nei re zheng
V012-N03272.definitions: Characterized by a lustreless complexion and fever. The tongue is pale. The pulse is weak. Often results from blood deficiency generating internal heat.

V012-N03273.label: 血虚动风证
V012-N03273.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03273.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03273.aliases: 血虚生风证； 血虚风动证； Blood deficiency generating wind pattern
V012-N03273.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood deficiency stirring wind; Blood deficiency stirring wind pattern
V012-N03273.pinyinNames: xue xu dong feng zheng; xuè xū dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03273.definitions: Characterized by a lustreless complexion, dry fingernails, dream-disturbed sleep, blurred vision, limb numbness, and skin itching. Often occurs when blood fails to nourish the body and causes stirring of internal wind.

V012-N03274.label: 血虚寒凝证
V012-N03274.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03274.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03274.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of blood deficiency and congealing cold; syndrome/pattern of cold congealing due to blood deficiency; Cold retention with blood deficiency pattern
V012-N03274.pinyinNames: xue xu han ning zheng; xuè xū hán níng zhèng
V012-N03274.definitions: Characterized by a pale dark complexion, dizziness, blurred vision, dark purple lips and tongue, cold hands/feet, and localized coldness, pain and numbness. Alternatively, delayed period, scanty menstrual volume in purple colour, clots, dysmenorrhea or amenorrhoea may be present. The tongue coating is white. The pulse is deep, thready and hesitant. Often results from blood deficiency coupled with cold retention.

V012-N03275.label: 血虚津亏证
V012-N03275.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03275.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03275.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood deficiency and fluid depletion; Blood and fluids deficiency pattern
V012-N03275.pinyinNames: xue xu jin kui zheng; xuè xū jīn kuī zhèng
V012-N03275.definitions: Characterized by a pale, lustreless complexion, withered skin, pale white lips and fingernails, dry nose, throat and eyes, scanty urination and constipation. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and hesitant. Often occurs when blood and body fluids fail to nourish and moisten the body.

V012-N03276.label: 血虚肠燥证
V012-N03276.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03276.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03276.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of blood deficiency and intestinal dryness; Intestinal dryness due to blood deficiency pattern; syndrome/pattern of blood deficiency with intestinal dryness
V012-N03276.pinyinNames: xuè xū cháng zào zhèng; xue xu chang zao zheng
V012-N03276.definitions: Characterized by difficulty passing of dry stools, one bowel movement in several days, and a pale face. Bloody stools may also be present. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when blood fails to nourish the large intestine.

V012-N03277.label: 血虚证
V012-N03277.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03277.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03277.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of blood deficiency; Blood deficiency pattern; blood deficiency pattern/syndrome
V012-N03277.pinyinNames: xue xu zheng; xuè xū zhèng
V012-N03277.definitions: Characterized by a pale or sallow complexion, pale lips, tongue and fingernails, dizziness, blurred vision, palpitations, dream-disturbed sleep, hands/feet numbness, scanty menstruation with a pale menstrual colour, and delayed period or amenorrhea. The pulse is thready. Often occurs when blood fails to nourish the zang–fu organs, meridians and other body tissues.

V012-N03278.label: 血虚风燥证
V012-N03278.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03278.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03278.aliases: 血虚风盛证； 血虚肤燥生风证
V012-N03278.englishNames: Blood deficiency-induced wind dryness pattern; pattern/syndrome of blood deficiency and wind-dryness; syndrome/pattern of wind dryness due to blood deficiency
V012-N03278.pinyinNames: xuè xū fēng zào zhèng; xue xu feng zao zheng
V012-N03278.definitions: Characterized by dry, rough, itchy and scaly skin, malnourishment and loss of hair, skin numbness, contraction of the hands/feet, a pale, lustreless complexion, pale white fingernails, dizziness, and blurred vision. The tongue is pale. The pulse is thready. Often occurs when blood fails to nourish the skin, tendons and muscles.

V012-N03279.label: 表实证
V012-N03279.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03279.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03279.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of external excess; exterior excess pattern/syndrome; Exterior excess pattern
V012-N03279.pinyinNames: biao shi zheng; biǎo shí zhèng
V012-N03279.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, absence of sweating, headache and body aches. The pulse is superficial and tense. Often occurs when the struggle between yang qi and pathogenic factors closes the skin striae.

V012-N03280.label: 表实里虚证
V012-N03280.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03280.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03280.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of exterior excess and interior deficiency; Exterior excess with interior deficiency pattern
V012-N03280.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, absence of sweating, and fever. Associated symptoms include lassitude, shortness of breath, a poor appetite, fatigue, palpitations and lower back pain. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is superficial. Often occurs as a result of the struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors coupled with deficiency of anti-pathogenic qi. Alternatively, it may result from deficiency of the heart, spleen and kidney coupled with external contraction of wind cold. It may also result from inappropriate use of purgative therapy for external contraction of wind cold.

V012-N03281.label: 表寒肺热证
V012-N03281.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03281.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03281.aliases: 外寒肺热证； 肺热表寒证； Exterior cold with lung heat pattern
V012-N03281.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exterior cold with lung heat; Exterior cold with lung heat pattern
V012-N03281.pinyinNames: biao han fei re zheng; biǎo hán fèi rè zhèng
V012-N03281.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, thirst, absence of sweating, restlessness, cough, panting and chest tightness. The tongue coating is yellow mixed with white. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when cold binds the Wei-defence and causes internal lung heat.

V012-N03282.label: 表寒证
V012-N03282.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03282.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03282.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of external cold; Exterior cold pattern; exterior cold pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03282.pinyinNames: biao han zheng; biǎo hán zhèng
V012-N03282.definitions: Characterized by severe aversion to cold, mild fever, headache, and joint soreness/pain. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is superficial and tight. Often occurs when wind cold attacks the surface of the body.

V012-N03283.label: 表寒里热证
V012-N03283.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03283.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03283.aliases: 表寒内热证； 外寒里热证
V012-N03283.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exterior cold and interior heat; Exterior cold with interior heat pattern; pattern/syndrome of exterior cold and interior heat
V012-N03283.pinyinNames: biao han li re zheng; biǎo hán lǐ rè zhèng
V012-N03283.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, body aches, absence of sweating, vexation, panting, and thirst. The tongue is red with a yellow, white coating. The pulse is floating and rapid. Often occurs when pathogenic cold binds the exterior and blocks the heat inside.

V012-N03284.label: 表热证
V012-N03284.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03284.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03284.aliases: 风热犯表证； 风热袭表证； Exterior heat pattern
V012-N03284.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of external heat; exterior heat pattern/ syndrome; Exterior heat pattern
V012-N03284.pinyinNames: biao re zheng; biǎo rè zhèng
V012-N03284.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to wind, headache, thirst, sore throat, and cough with yellow phlegm. Sores, ulcers or boils may also be present. The tongue coating is thin and white or slightly yellow. The pulse is superficial and rapid. Often occurs when wind, heat or summer dryness attacks the surface of the body.

V012-N03285.label: 表热里寒证
V012-N03285.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03285.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03285.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of exterior heat and interior cold; pattern/syndrome of exterior heat and interior cold; Exterior heat with interior cold pattern
V012-N03285.pinyinNames: biao re li han zheng; biǎo rè lǐ hán zhèng
V012-N03285.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, sweating, headache, sore throat, clear urine, loose stools, and abdominal fullness and distension. Often caused by constitutional yang deficiency coupled with contraction of external heat.

V012-N03286.label: 表虚里实证
V012-N03286.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03286.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03286.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of exterior deficiency and interior excess; Exterior deficiency with interior excess pattern
V012-N03286.definitions: Characterized by aversion to wind, sweating, coughing with profuse phlegm, and abdominal distension/pain with tenderness, and constipation. The tongue is dark with a yellow, thick coating. Often results from deficiency of Wei-defensive qi coupled with build up of internal heat. Alternatively, it may occur when retained food in the stomach and intestines transform into heat, along with external contraction of wind. It may also result from inappropriate treatment of an exterior pattern or inappropriate use of sweating therapy for an interior excess pattern.

V012-N03287.label: 表证
V012-N03287.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03287.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03287.englishNames: external syndrome/pattern; Exterior pattern; Exterior Syndrome; exterior pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03287.definitions: Characterized by aversion to wind/cold, fever, headache, and body aches. The tongue coating is thin. The pulse is floating. These signs and symptoms are often seen in the early stage of externally contracted conditions that have a sudden onset, superficial location and a short duration. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic factors affect the interstices, joints and head.

V012-N03288.label: 表里俱实证
V012-N03288.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03288.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03288.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of dual exterior and interior excess; syndrome/pattern of dual internal and external excess; Exterior–interior excess pattern
V012-N03288.pinyinNames: biao li ju shi zheng; biǎo lǐ jù shí zhèng
V012-N03288.definitions: Characterized by fever, aversion to heat, absence of sweating, headache, neck stiffness, restlessness, abdominal distension and constipation. In severe cases, mania may be present. The tongue coating is dry and thick. The pulse is slippery and rapid. Often results from unresolved pathogenic factors in the exterior coupled with internal retention of undigested food, accumulated heat, phlegm fluid, water dampness, stagnant blood, dry faeces, pus, parasites and stones.

V012-N03289.label: 表里实寒证
V012-N03289.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03289.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03289.englishNames: Exterior–interior excess cold pattern; syndrome/pattern of internal and external excess-cold
V012-N03289.pinyinNames: biǎo lǐ shí hán zhèng; biao li shi han zheng
V012-N03289.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, cold limbs, headache, body aches, and absence of sweating. Alternatively, coughing with white phlegm, cold pain in the stomach and abdomen, vomiting and diarrhoea with clear and watery discharge may also be present. Often occurs when pathogenic cold affects both the exterior and interior.

V012-N03290.label: 表里实热证
V012-N03290.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03290.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03290.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual external and internal heat; Exterior–interior excess heat pattern
V012-N03290.pinyinNames: biao li shi re zheng; biǎo lǐ shí rè zhèng
V012-N03290.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to wind/cold, headache, body aches, vexation, panting, thirst, constipation and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is floating and rapid. Often occurs when pathogenic wind heat or pestilence affects both the exterior and interior.

V012-N03291.label: 足厥阴肝经证
V012-N03291.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03291.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03291.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of liver meridian of foot-jueyin; Liver meridian pattern
V012-N03291.pinyinNames: zu jue yin gan jing zheng; zú jué yīn gān jīng zhèng
V012-N03291.definitions: Characterized by lower back painlower back pain, chest fullness, hiccups, enuresis, inhibited urination, hernia and lower abdominal swelling. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the liver meridian.

V012-N03292.label: 足太阳膀胱经证
V012-N03292.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03292.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03292.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of bladder meridian of foot-taiyang; Bladder meridian pattern
V012-N03292.pinyinNames: zu tai yang bang guang jing zheng; zú tài yáng páng guāng jīng zhèng
V012-N03292.definitions: Characterized by inhibited urination, enuresis, mania, alternating fever and chills, eye pain, lacrimation with exposure to wind, nasal obstruction with discharge, nosebleed, headache and pain along the neck, back, low back, buttocks and posterior aspect of the leg. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the bladder meridian.

V012-N03293.label: 足太阴脾经证
V012-N03293.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03293.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03293.englishNames: Spleen meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of spleen meridian of foot-taiyin
V012-N03293.pinyinNames: zú tài yīn pí jīng zhèng; zu tai yin pi jing zheng
V012-N03293.definitions: Characterized by gastric pain, vomiting immediately after eating food, belching, abdominal distension, loose stools, jaundice, body heaviness and weakness, pain or stiffness of the base of the tongue, swelling along the medial side of the leg and cold limbs. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the spleen meridian.

V012-N03294.label: 足少阳胆经证
V012-N03294.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03294.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03294.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of gallbladder meridian of foot-shaoyang; Gallbladder meridian pattern
V012-N03294.pinyinNames: zu shao yang dan jing zheng; zú shào yáng dǎn jīng zhèng
V012-N03294.definitions: Characterized by a bitter mouth, blurred vision, alternating fever and chills, headache, pain in the chin, outer canthus and clavicle, armpit swelling, pain along the chest, subcostal region, thigh and lateral aspect of the leg and foot, and a feverish sensation along the lateral aspect of the foot. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the gallbladder meridian.

V012-N03295.label: 足少阴肾经证
V012-N03295.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03295.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03295.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of kidney meridian of foot-shaoyin; Kidney meridian pattern
V012-N03295.pinyinNames: zu shao yin shen jing zheng; zú shào yīn shèn jīng zhèng
V012-N03295.definitions: Characterized by haemoptysis, panting, a dry tongue, a sore, swollen throat, oedema, constipation, diarrhoea, lower back painlower back pain, pain along the posterior of the spine and thigh, flaccidity and a feverish sensation in the soles. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the kidney meridian.

V012-N03296.label: 足阳明胃经证
V012-N03296.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03296.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03296.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of stomach meridian of foot-yangming; Stomach meridian pattern
V012-N03296.pinyinNames: zu yang ming wei jing zheng; zú yáng míng wèi jīng zhèng
V012-N03296.definitions: Characterized by bowel sounds, abdominal distension, oedema, stomach ache, vomiting, fast hunger after eating food, thirst, a sore, swollen throat, nosebleed, pain in the chest or knee, fever and mania. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the stomach meridian.

V012-N03297.label: 邪伏膜原证
V012-N03297.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03297.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03297.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of pathogen hidden in the pleurodiaphragmatic interspace; Pathogenic factors hidden in the pleurodiaphragmatic interspace pattern; syndrome/pattern of pathogenic factors hidden in the pleurodiaphragmatic interspace
V012-N03297.pinyinNames: xié fú mó yuán zhèng; xie fu mo yuan zheng
V012-N03297.definitions: Characterized by alternating chills and fever at fixed hours, a severe, splitting headache, body pain, distension and tightness in the sub-costal region, and vomiting of phlegm and saliva. The tongue coating appears like white powder. Often occurs when epidemic pathogens hide in between the exterior and interior.

V012-N03298.label: 里热证
V012-N03298.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03298.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03298.aliases: 里热炽盛证； 实热证； Excess heat pattern
V012-N03298.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of internal heat; interior heat pattern/ syndrome; Interior heat pattern
V012-N03298.pinyinNames: li re zheng; lǐ rè zhèng
V012-N03298.definitions: Characterized by persistent high fever, red face and eyes, restlessness, thirst with a desire to drink cold water, constipation and scanty, dark yellow urine. In severe cases, unconsciousness and delirium may be present. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow coating. The pulse is deep and excessive. Often occurs when external pathogenic warm heat enters the interior.

V012-N03299.label: 里证
V012-N03299.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03299.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03299.englishNames: interior pattern/ syndrome; internal syndrome/pattern; Interior pattern; Interior Syndrome
V012-N03299.definitions: A group of patterns caused by entering of external pathogens into the interior, improper diet, over exhaustion, emotional disturbance or phlegm stasis, resulting in disorders and dysfunctions in qi, blood, yin, yang, zang–fu organs and meridian systems.

V012-N03300.label: 闭证
V012-N03300.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03300.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03300.aliases: 气闭； 中风闭证； blockage syndrome
V012-N03300.englishNames: Blockage pattern; syndrome/pattern of obstruction; Excess Syndrome of Stroke
V012-N03300.definitions: Characterized by trismus, closed hands, unconsciousness, fever or cold limbs. This pattern often results from wind stroke or heat entering the Ying nutrients and blood phases.

V012-N03301.label: 阳明病证
V012-N03301.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03301.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03301.aliases: 阳明病； Middle yang stage pattern； Yang brightness pattern
V012-N03301.englishNames: yang brightness disease pattern/ syndrome; Middle yang stage pattern; Yangming disorders
V012-N03301.definitions: A group of disorders caused by hyperactive yang heat in Yangming meridians or fu organs.

V012-N03302.label: 阳明经证
V012-N03302.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03302.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03302.aliases: 阳明气热证，阳明热证； 阳明热盛证； 阳明气热证； 阳明热证
V012-N03302.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yangming meridian; yang brightness meridian pattern/ syndrome; Yangming meridian pattern
V012-N03302.pinyinNames: yang ming jing zheng; yáng míng jīng zhèng
V012-N03302.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, aversion to heat instead of cold, profuse sweating, and excessive thirst with a strong desire to drink water. Alternatively, restlessness, panting, and a red face may be present. The tongue coating is dry and yellow. The pulse is surging and large. This pattern often occurs when hyperactive heat affects the Yangming meridians and the entire body.

V012-N03303.label: 阳明腑实证
V012-N03303.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03303.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03303.aliases: 阳明燥结证； 阳明腑证； 阳明热结证； 阳明实证； Yangming–fu organ pattern
V012-N03303.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yangming fu-organ excess; Excess Yangming–fu organ pattern
V012-N03303.pinyinNames: yang ming fu shi zheng; yáng míng fǔ shí zhèng
V012-N03303.definitions: Characterized by fever that aggravates in the afternoon, sweating of hands/feet, abdominal fullness, distension and pain with tenderness, and constipation. In severe cases, unconsciousness, delirium, restlessness, and insomnia may be present. The tongue coating is dry, yellow and thick or charred black with thorns. The pulse is deep and forceful , or slippery and rapid. This pattern often occurs when pathogenic heat enters the intestine and mixes with dry faeces.

V012-N03304.label: 阳维脉证
V012-N03304.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03304.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03304.englishNames: Yangwei meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of yang link vessel
V012-N03304.pinyinNames: yang wei mai zheng; yáng wéi mài zhèng
V012-N03304.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever and lower back painlower back pain. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Yangwei meridian.

V012-N03305.label: 阳虚水泛证
V012-N03305.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03305.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03305.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of yang deficiency with water flood; syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency with water flooding; Yang deficiency induced water retention pattern
V012-N03305.pinyinNames: yang xu shui fan zheng; yáng xū shuǐ fàn zhèng
V012-N03305.definitions: Characterized by cold limbs, swelling of the limbs, inhibited urination, palpitations, panting, abdominal distension and diarrhoea. The tongue is pale and enlarged with a white, slippery coating. Often occurs when yang qi fails to warm the body and transform water fluids.

V012-N03306.label: 阳虚证
V012-N03306.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03306.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03306.aliases: 阳虚证类； 阳气虚证； 虚寒证； 阳虚内寒证； 阳气亏虚证
V012-N03306.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yang deficiency; yang deficiency pattern/syndrome; Yang deficiency pattern
V012-N03306.pinyinNames: yang xu zheng; yáng xū zhèng
V012-N03306.definitions: Characterized by cold intolerance, cold limbs, lassitude, fatigue, shortness of breath, tastelessness, absence of thirst or preference for hot water, clear urine, and loose stools. Alternatively, scanty urine, oedema and a pale face may be present. The tongue is pale and enlarged. The pulse is deep, slow and weak. Often occurs when yang qi fails to warm and nourish the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03307.label: 阳证
V012-N03307.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03307.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03307.englishNames: Yang Syndrome; yang syndrome/pattern; Yang pattern; yang pattern/ syndrome
V012-N03307.definitions: Causes outward, upward symptoms and manifests as conditions characterized by excitability, hyperactivity, restlessness, and brightness. It encompasses exterior pattern, heat pattern and excess pattern.

V012-N03308.label: 阳蹻脉证
V012-N03308.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03308.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03308.aliases: 阳跷脉证； Yang heel vessel pattern
V012-N03308.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yang heel vessel; Yangqiao meridian pattern; syndrome/ pattern of yang heel vessel
V012-N03308.pinyinNames: yang qiao mai zheng; yáng qiāo mài zhèng
V012-N03308.definitions: Characterized by eye pain (inner canthus) and insomnia. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Yangqiao meridian.

V012-N03309.label: 阴竭阳脱证
V012-N03309.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03309.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03309.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin exhaustion and yang prostration; pattern/syndrome of yin exhaustion and yang collapse; Yin exhaustion and yang collapse pattern
V012-N03309.pinyinNames: yin jie yang tuo zheng; yīn jié yáng tuō zhèng
V012-N03309.definitions: Characterized by panting, more exhalation than inhalation, restlessness, breathing with an open mouth and elevated shoulders and oily sweating. Often results from yang collapse following exhaustion of yin fluids.

V012-N03310.label: 阴维脉证
V012-N03310.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03310.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03310.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin link vessel; Yinwei meridian pattern
V012-N03310.pinyinNames: yin wei mai zheng; yīn wéi mài zhèng
V012-N03310.definitions: Characterized by cardiac pain and melancholy. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Yinwei meridian.

V012-N03311.label: 阴虚内热证
V012-N03311.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03311.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03311.englishNames: Yin deficiency-induced internal heat pattern; pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with internal heat
V012-N03311.definitions: Characterized by a persistent low-grade fever, night sweats, flushed cheeks, a dry mouth with a desire to drink water, scanty, yellow urine and constipation. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when deficiency of yin fluids generates internal heat.

V012-N03312.label: 阴虚动风证
V012-N03312.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03312.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03312.aliases: 阴虚风动证； 阴虚肝风内动证； 虚风内动证
V012-N03312.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and wind stirring; pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with stirring wind; Yin deficiency stirring wind pattern
V012-N03312.pinyinNames: yin xu dong feng zheng; yīn xū dòng fēng zhèng
V012-N03312.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, limb numbness, weight loss, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, a dry mouth and throat, scanty, yellow urine and constipation. Alternatively, convulsions or tremor of the hands/feet may be present. The tongue is red with a scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when deficiency of yin fluids causes malnourishment of meridians and stirring of deficiency wind.

V012-N03313.label: 阴虚津亏证
V012-N03313.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03313.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03313.aliases: 阴津亏虚证； 阴液亏虚证
V012-N03313.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with fluid depletion; Yin fluids deficiency pattern
V012-N03313.definitions: Characterized by a dry mouth, throat and skin, sunken eyes, scanty, yellow urine, constipation, weight loss, and night sweats. The tongue is red with a dry, scanty coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when yin fluids fail to nourish the body.

V012-N03314.label: 阴虚肠燥证
V012-N03314.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03314.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03314.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency with intestinal dryness; Intestinal dryness due to yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03314.pinyinNames: yin xu chang zao zheng; yīn xū cháng zào zhèng
V012-N03314.definitions: Characterized by difficulty passing of dry stools (like sheep droppings), one bowel movement in several days, and a dry mouth/nose/throat/skin. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready, rapid and hesitant. This pattern often occurs when yin fluids fail to nourish the large intestine.
