V012-N03315.label: 阴虚血燥证
V012-N03315.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03315.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03315.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and blood dryness; Yin deficiency-induced blood dryness pattern
V012-N03315.pinyinNames: yin xu xue zao zheng; yīn xū xuè zào zhèng
V012-N03315.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, blurred vision, a dry mouth and throat, dry, itchy skin, dry hair, tidal fever in the afternoon, night sweats and flushed cheeks. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when deficiency of yin fluids generates internal heat to consume blood and bodily fluids.

V012-N03316.label: 阴虚证
V012-N03316.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03316.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03316.aliases: 阴液亏虚证； Insufficiency of yin fluids pattern
V012-N03316.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency; yin deficiency pattern/ syndrome; Yin deficiency pattern
V012-N03316.pinyinNames: yin xu zheng; yīn xū zhèng
V012-N03316.definitions: Characterized by feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, bone-steaming fever, flushed cheeks, dizziness, tinnitus, dry lips, nose, throat and skin, night sweats, nocturnal emissions, and constipation. The tongue is red with a dry, scanty coating. The pulse is thready, rapid and weak. Alternatively, the tongue is deep red with a peeled coating. Often results from consumption of yin fluids and relative hyperactivity of yang qi.

V012-N03317.label: 阴虚阳亢证
V012-N03317.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03317.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03317.aliases: 虚阳偏亢证； 虚阳偏旺证
V012-N03317.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with yang hyperactivity; Yin deficiency-induced yang hyperactivity pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin deficiency and yang hyperactivity
V012-N03317.pinyinNames: yin xu yang kang zheng; yīn xū yáng kàng zhèng
V012-N03317.definitions: Characterized by tidal fever, night sweats, flushed cheeks, dizziness, blurred vision, restlessness, and insomnia. The tongue is dry and red. The pulse is thready and rapid. Often occurs when yin fluids fails to restrain yang.

V012-N03318.label: 阴证
V012-N03318.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03318.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03318.englishNames: Yin Syndrome; yin pattern/syndrome; Yin pattern; yin syndrome/pattern
V012-N03318.definitions: Causes inward, downward symptoms and manifests as conditions characterized by inhibition, listlessness, tranquillity and gloominess. It encompasses interior pattern, cold pattern and deficiency pattern.

V012-N03319.label: 阴蹻脉证
V012-N03319.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03319.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03319.aliases: 阴跷脉证； Yin heel vessel pattern
V012-N03319.englishNames: Yinqiao meridian pattern; syndrome/pattern of yin heel vessel
V012-N03319.pinyinNames: yin qiao mai zheng; yīn qiāo mài zhèng
V012-N03319.definitions: Characterized by hypersomnia and urinary retention. This pattern often results from impaired qi flow of the Yinqiao meridian.

V012-N03320.label: 阴阳两虚证
V012-N03320.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03320.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03320.aliases: 阴阳两亏证； 阴损阳亏证； 阴阳亏虚证
V012-N03320.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of dual deficiency of yin and yang; pattern/syndrome of dual deficiency of yin and yang; Yin and yang deficiency pattern
V012-N03320.pinyinNames: yin yang liang xu zheng; yīn yáng liǎng xū zhèng
V012-N03320.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, tinnitus, cold intolerance, cold limbs, feverish sensations in the palms, soles and chest, palpitations and low back soreness. The tongue is dry and pale. The pulse is weak and rapid. Often results from a deficiency of yin fluids and yang qi of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03321.label: 阴阳气血津液精髓证类
V012-N03321.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03321.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03321.conceptName: 阴阳气血津液精髓证类
V012-N03321.preferredName: 阴阳气血津液精髓证类
V012-N03321.englishNames: Patterns of yin, yang, qi, blood, bodily fluids, essence and marrow
V012-N03321.pinyinNames: yīn yáng qì xuè jīn yè jīng suǐ zhèng lèi

V012-N03322.label: 风中经络证
V012-N03322.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03322.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03322.definitions: Characterized by skin numbness and itching or sudden deviation of the eyes and mouth corner. This pattern often occurs when wind attacks the meridians.

V012-N03323.label: 风寒化热证
V012-N03323.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03323.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03323.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and cold transforming into heat; Wind cold transformed heat pattern
V012-N03323.pinyinNames: feng han hua re zheng; fēng hán huà rè zhèng
V012-N03323.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, headache, body ache and joint soreness/pain. Associated symptoms include cough with thick phlegm, and a sore/scratchy throat. The tongue tip is red with a dry, yellow/white coating. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when retained wind cold transforms into heat and enters the interior.

V012-N03324.label: 风寒表证
V012-N03324.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03324.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03324.aliases: 寒邪束表证； 风寒束表证； Cold/wind cold binding the exterior pattern
V012-N03324.definitions: Characterized by severe aversion to cold, fever, absence of sweating, headache, and body aches. Alternatively, nasal obstruction, clear nasal discharge, and cough may be present. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is superficial and tense. Often occurs when wind cold blocks the skin pores.

V012-N03325.label: 风寒袭肺证
V012-N03325.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03325.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03325.aliases: 风寒束肺证； Wind cold fettering the lung pattern； Wind cold binding the lung
V012-N03325.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and cold attacking the lung; Wind cold attacking the lung pattern; pattern/syndrome of wind-cold assailing the lung
V012-N03325.pinyinNames: feng han xi fei zheng; fēng hán xí fèi zhèng
V012-N03325.definitions: Characterized by aversion to cold, fever, absence of sweating, cough, chest tightness, panting and expectoration of white phlegm. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is superficial and tense. This pattern often occurs when wind cold affects the dispersing of lung qi.

V012-N03326.label: 风毒犯表证
V012-N03326.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03326.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03326.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind toxin invading the exterior; Wind toxin attacking the exterior pattern
V012-N03326.pinyinNames: feng dong fan biao zheng; fēng dú fàn biǎo zhèng
V012-N03326.definitions: Characterized by sudden skin itching, papules or wheals. The pulse is superficial. Often occurs when wind toxin attacks the surface of the body.

V012-N03327.label: 风毒蕴肤证
V012-N03327.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03327.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03327.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind toxin retaining in the skin; Wind toxin accumulating in the skin pattern
V012-N03327.pinyinNames: feng dong yun fu zheng; fēng dú yùn fū zhèng
V012-N03327.definitions: Characterized by skin redness, itching, pain, rashes or wheals. In severe cases, skin ulcer or peeling may be present. Often occurs when wind toxin retains in the skin.

V012-N03328.label: 风毒证
V012-N03328.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03328.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03328.aliases: 风毒炽盛证； Exuberance of wind toxin pattern
V012-N03328.englishNames: wind-toxin pattern/ syndrome; syndrome/pattern of wind toxin; Wind toxin pattern
V012-N03328.pinyinNames: feng dong zheng; fēng dú zhèng
V012-N03328.definitions: Characterized by sudden skin swelling, numbness, itching or pain and redness, swelling and pain of the head/face/mouth/nose and eyes. Alternatively, wheals may be present. The pulse is superficial and rapid. Often occurs when wind toxin attacks the skin.

V012-N03329.label: 风水证
V012-N03329.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03329.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03329.aliases: 风袭水停证； 风水相搏证； Pattern of wind and water contending with each other
V012-N03329.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and water; Wind water pattern
V012-N03329.pinyinNames: feng shui zheng; fēng shuǐ zhèng
V012-N03329.definitions: Characterized by sudden oedema in the eyelids, head, face and four limbs. Alternatively, wheals, mild aversion to wind cold and scanty urine may be present. The pulse is superficial. Often occurs when external wind affects the dispersing of lung qi and results in internal water retention.

V012-N03330.label: 风湿化热证
V012-N03330.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03330.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03330.aliases: 风湿郁热证； 风湿化火证； 风湿热郁证； Wind dampness transformed fire pattern
V012-N03330.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and dampness transforming into heat; Wind dampness transformed heat pattern
V012-N03330.pinyinNames: feng shi hua re zheng; fēng shī huà rè zhèng
V012-N03330.definitions: Characterized by limb soreness/heaviness, and joint swelling, pain and impaired movement. Alternatively, skin itching, oozing, fever, and thirst may be present. The tongue is red with a dry, yellow/white coating. Often occurs when retained wind dampness transforms into heat.

V012-N03331.label: 风湿蕴肤证
V012-N03331.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03331.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03331.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and dampness retaining in the skin; Wind dampness accumulating in the skin pattern
V012-N03331.pinyinNames: feng shi yun fu zheng; fēng shī yùn fū zhèng
V012-N03331.definitions: Characterized by skin itching, ulceration, rashes or oozing. Often occurs when wind dampness retains in the skin.

V012-N03332.label: 风湿袭表证
V012-N03332.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03332.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03332.aliases: 湿郁卫分证； 表湿证； 湿郁卫表证
V012-N03332.englishNames: Wind dampness attacking the exterior pattern; pattern/syndrome of wind-dampness assailing the exterior
V012-N03332.definitions: Characterized by body soreness/heaviness, headache with a heavy sensation, aversion to cold, fever that cannot be resolved by sweating, chest tightness, and absence of thirst. The tongue coating is white, slippery or greasy. The pulse is soft and delayed. Often occurs when wind dampness attacks the surface of the body and obstructs the flow of Wei-defensive qi.

V012-N03333.label: 风火热毒证
V012-N03333.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03333.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03333.aliases: 风火毒证； 风火热毒炽盛证
V012-N03333.englishNames: Wind fire/heat toxin pattern; syndrome/pattern of wind and fire and heat toxin; wind-fire-heat toxin pattern/syndrome
V012-N03333.pinyinNames: fēng huǒ rè dú zhèng; feng huo re dong zheng
V012-N03333.definitions: Characterized by red, swollen, itchy, numb or painful skin sores/boils, coupled with a burning sensation. Alternatively red, swollen and painful face, nose and eyes, fever, thirst, unconsciousness, delirium, constipation and yellow urine may be present. The tongue is deep red with brown/yellow coating. The pulse is surging and rapid. Often occurs when toxic wind-fire/heat accumulates in the skin.

V012-N03334.label: 风热夹湿证
V012-N03334.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03334.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03334.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and heat complicated by dampness; Wind heat and dampness pattern
V012-N03334.pinyinNames: feng re jia shi zheng; fēng rè jiā shī zhèng
V012-N03334.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst with little water intake, and limb heaviness. Alternatively, skin rashes, blisters, itching or oozing may be present. The tongue is red with a yellow, greasy coating. Often occurs when wind heat that carries dampness affects the skin.

V012-N03335.label: 风热犯肺证
V012-N03335.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03335.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03335.aliases: Wind heat invading the lung pattern
V012-N03335.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and heat invading the lung; Wind heat attacking the lung pattern; pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the lung; Wind-heat invading the lung pattern
V012-N03335.pinyinNames: feng re fan fei zheng; fēng rè fàn fèi zhèng
V012-N03335.definitions: Characterized by cough, panting, fever, mild aversion to wind cold, and body ache or sore throat. The tongue tip is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid. This pattern often occurs when wind heat invades the lung defence and affects the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N03336.label: 风热表证
V012-N03336.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03336.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03336.aliases: 风热外侵证； 风热犯表证； Wind heat invading the exterior pattern
V012-N03336.definitions: Characterized by fever, mild aversion to cold/wind, sweating, thirst, and red, sore throat. The tongue tip is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid. Often occurs when wind heat attacks the surface of the body and affects the normal functioning of the Wei-defence.

V012-N03337.label: 风热阻络证
V012-N03337.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03337.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03337.aliases: 风热中络证； Wind heat attacking the meridians pattern
V012-N03337.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and heat obstructing collaterals; Wind heat affecting the meridians pattern
V012-N03337.pinyinNames: feng re zu luo zheng; fēng rè zǔ luò zhèng
V012-N03337.definitions: Characterized by localized numbness, burning pain, itching and redness. This pattern often occurs when wind heat impairs the flow of meridian qi.

V012-N03338.label: 风燥伤肺证
V012-N03338.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03338.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03338.aliases: 风燥犯肺证； 燥邪伤肺证； 燥邪犯肺证； Dryness invading the lung pattern； Dryness damaging/attacking the lung
V012-N03338.englishNames: Wind dryness damaging the lung pattern; syndrome/pattern of wind and dryness damaging the lung
V012-N03338.pinyinNames: feng zao shang fei zheng; fēng zào shāng fèi zhèng
V012-N03338.definitions: Characterized by unproductive coughs or cough with scanty, sticky or blood-stained phlegm, dry nose and throat, and a dry mouth. The tongue tip is red with a dry, thin and yellow coating. The pulse is thready and rapid. This pattern often occurs when wind dryness consumes body fluids and affects the dispersing and descending of lung qi.

V012-N03339.label: 风燥袭表证
V012-N03339.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03339.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03339.aliases: 外燥袭表证； External dryness affecting the exterior pattern
V012-N03339.englishNames: Wind dryness attacking the exterior pattern
V012-N03339.definitions: Characterized by mild fever, aversion to wind cold, headache, absence of sweating, a dry mouth and throat, and thirst. The pulse is superficial. Often occurs when wind dryness blocks the Wei-defensive qi and consumes body fluids.

V012-N03340.label: 风痰入络证
V012-N03340.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03340.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03340.aliases: 风痰阻络证； Wind phlegm obstructing the meridians pattern
V012-N03340.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of wind and phlegm entering collaterals; Wind phlegm entering the meridians pattern
V012-N03340.pinyinNames: feng tan ru luo zheng; fēng tán rù luò zhèng
V012-N03340.definitions: Characterized by limb numbness or paralysis, skin numbness or itching, dizziness and salivation. The tongue coating is greasy. This pattern often occurs when liver wind that carries phlegm affects the meridians.

V012-N03341.label: 风痰证
V012-N03341.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03341.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03341.englishNames: Wind-phlegm pattern; syndrome/pattern of wind and phlegm; wind-phlegm pattern/ syndrome; Wind phlegm pattern
V012-N03341.pinyinNames: feng tan zheng; fēng tán zhèng
V012-N03341.definitions: Characterized by vomiting of foamy phlegm, chest tightness, dizziness, headache, eye distension/pain, sound of phlegm in the throat, and deviation of the eyes and mouth border. The tongue coating is white and greasy. The pulse is wiry and slippery. Often occurs when external wind that carries turbid phlegm attacks the body or liver wind that carries turbid phlegm affects the body.

V012-N03342.label: 风邪袭表证
V012-N03342.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03342.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03342.aliases: 风邪犯表证； 风邪外袭证； Wind invading the exterior pattern
V012-N03342.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of pathogenic wind attacking the exterior; Wind attacking the exterior pattern
V012-N03342.pinyinNames: feng xie xi biao zheng; fēng xié xí biǎo zhèng
V012-N03342.definitions: Characterized by aversion to wind, fever, sweating and a superficial pulse. Alternatively, skin itching, oedema, cough, sore throat, headache or body aches may be present. Often occurs when wind attacks the surface of the body and affects the normal functioning of the Wei-defence.

V012-N03343.label: 饮停心包证
V012-N03343.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03343.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03343.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fluid retention in the pericardium; Fluid retention affecting the pericardium pattern
V012-N03343.pinyinNames: yin ting xin bao zheng; yǐn tíng xīn bāo zhèng
V012-N03343.definitions: Characterized by mild to severe palpitations, chest fullness and tightness, panting, and an inability to lie flat. The tongue is pale purple with a white, slippery coating. The pulse is deep, hidden or weak. This pattern often occurs when retained fluid in the pericardium obstructs the flow of qi and blood.

V012-N03344.label: 饮溢四肢证
V012-N03344.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03344.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03344.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of fluid overflowing in the four limbs; Fluids overflowing into the limbs pattern
V012-N03344.pinyinNames: yin yi si zhi zheng; yǐn yì sì zhī zhèng
V012-N03344.definitions: Characterized by body pain/heaviness, and joint pain. Other associated symptoms include cough, panting, chest tightness, and fatigue. Often occurs when fluid overflows to the limbs and the surface of the body.

V012-N03345.label: 饮溢胸胁证
V012-N03345.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03345.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03345.definitions: Characterized by subcostal distension/fullness, pain in the subcostal region induced by cough, swallowing, breathing or turning around. Retching or shortness of breath may also be present. Often occurs when fluid remains in the subcostal region.

V012-N03346.label: 髓亏证
V012-N03346.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜证候
V012-N03346.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03346.englishNames: syndrome/pattern of marrow deficiency; Marrow deficiency pattern
V012-N03346.pinyinNames: sui kui zheng; suǐ kuī zhèng
V012-N03346.definitions: Characterized by dizziness, tinnitus, headache with a hollow sensation, low back soreness/weakness, slow movements, limb flaccidity. Often occurs when essence and marrow fail to nourish the body.

V012-N03347.label: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03347.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N03347.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03347.conceptName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03347.preferredName: 诊断基本概念与原则类

V012-N03348.label: 体征
V012-N03348.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03348.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03348.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03348.definitions: Abnormal changes that can be objectively measured.

V012-N03349.label: 司外揣内
V012-N03349.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03349.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03349.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03349.englishNames: judging the inside from observation of the outside; Judging the inside from observing the outside
V012-N03349.definitions: A method to understand internal pathological changes through observing the external manifestations.

V012-N03350.label: 四诊合参
V012-N03350.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03350.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03350.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03350.englishNames: Combination of the four diagnostic methods; correlation of all four examinations
V012-N03350.definitions: The process of an overall analysis of clinical data collected through the four diagnostic methods and providing a diagnosis and potential treatment options.

V012-N03351.label: 病
V012-N03351.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03351.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03351.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03351.definitions: The whole process of struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors, yin–yang imbalance and progression, manifesting as specific symptoms and patterns. In a narrow sense, a disease has its specific name.

V012-N03352.label: 病证结合
V012-N03352.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03352.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03352.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03352.definitions: Integrated differentiation of both pattern and disease. This helps to analyse the disease onset and development, and thus provide reliable evidence for practical treatment strategies.

V012-N03353.label: 症状
V012-N03353.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03353.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03353.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03353.definitions: Abnormal functions reflecting the presence of an unusual state, or of a disease. It includes a patient’s subjective feelings or manifestations perceived by a doctor.

V012-N03354.label: 证
V012-N03354.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03354.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03354.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03354.definitions: A pathological summarization on the disease location, etiological factors, nature, severity and prognosis in a certain stage.

V012-N03355.label: 辨病
V012-N03355.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03355.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03355.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03355.definitions: The process of differentiating a disease from its whole process and its characteristics according to the etiology, underlying pathology, progression and prognosis.

V012-N03356.label: 辨证
V012-N03356.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03356.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03356.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03356.englishNames: Pattern identification; pattern identification/ syndrome differentiation
V012-N03356.definitions: The process of an overall analysis of clinical data to determine the location, etiological factors and nature of a disease at a certain stage.

V012-N03357.label: 辨证论治
V012-N03357.parentName: 诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03357.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜诊断基本概念与原则类
V012-N03357.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03357.definitions: The whole process of applying theories, principles, prescriptions and medicines to clinical treatment. Pattern differentiation aims to analyse the signs and symptoms collected by the four examination methods according to the fundamental theories of the eight principles, zang–fu organs, etiology and pathogenesis. Treatment refers to specific therapeutic methods directed at the differentiated pattern.

V012-N03358.label: 辨证方法类
V012-N03358.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语
V012-N03358.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03359.label: 三焦传变
V012-N03359.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03359.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03359.englishNames: Pattern transmission along the three jiao
V012-N03359.definitions: Febrile diseases first affect the upper jiao, then the middle jiao and further to the lower jiao. This is known as the sequential transmission, indicating a mild-to-severe pathological process. Direct transmission from the lung Wei-defence to pericardium is known as nonsequential transmission. It is often a critical condition due to exuberant pathogenic heat.

V012-N03360.label: 三焦辨证
V012-N03360.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03360.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03360.aliases: Pattern differentiation of the triple energizer； Syndrome Differ Trip Energizer； Syndrome Differentiation, Sanjiao； Syndrome Differentiation, Triple Energizer
V012-N03360.englishNames: syndrome differentiation of sanjiao theory; triple-energizer syndrome differentiation/pattern identification; Pattern differentiation of the three jiao; Syndrome Differentiation of Triple Energizer
V012-N03360.pinyinNames: san jiao bian zheng; sān jiāo biàn zhèng
V012-N03360.definitions: A pattern differentiation method based on the three transmission stages (upper jiao, middle jiao and lower jiao) of febrile diseases developed by a Qing dynasty physician Wu Jutong (1758–1836). The upper jiao pattern involves the lung and pericardium, the middle jiao pattern involves the spleen and stomach, and the lower jiao pattern involves the liver and kidney.

V012-N03361.label: 三阳病证
V012-N03361.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03361.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03361.englishNames: Patterns of the three yang meridians
V012-N03361.definitions: The patterns of six meridians are categorized into two types: patterns of the three yang meridians and patterns of the three yin meridians. Patterns of the three yang meridians include Taiyang pattern, Yangming pattern and Shaoyang pattern. They often manifest as heat and excess due to a fierce struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors.

V012-N03362.label: 三阴病证
V012-N03362.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03362.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03362.englishNames: Patterns of the three yin meridians
V012-N03362.definitions: The patterns of six meridians are categorized into two types: patterns of the three yang meridians and patterns of the three yin meridians. Patterns of the three yin meridians include Taiyin pattern, Shaoyin pattern, and Jueyin pattern. They often manifest as cold and deficiency due to weakness of anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N03363.label: 上焦病证
V012-N03363.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03363.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03363.pinyinNames: shàng jiāo bìng zhèng; shang jiao bing zheng
V012-N03363.definitions: Characterized by fever, sweating, coughing and panting. Unconsciousness and delirium may also be present. Often occurs when pathogenic warm heat attacks the lung and pericardium meridians.

V012-N03364.label: 下焦病证
V012-N03364.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03364.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03364.pinyinNames: xià jiāo bìng zhèng; xia jiao bing zheng
V012-N03364.definitions: Characterized by fever, flushed cheeks, and twitching or spasms of hands/feet. The tongue is deep red with a scanty coating. Often occurs when pathogenic warm heat attacks the lower jiao and impairs yin of the liver and kidney.

V012-N03365.label: 中焦病证
V012-N03365.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03365.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03365.pinyinNames: zhong jiao bing zheng; zhōng jiāo bìng zhèng
V012-N03365.definitions: Characterized by fever, thirst, abdominal fullness and constipation. Alternatively, subjective feverish sensation without elevated temperature, nausea, vomiting, stomach stuffiness and loose stools. Often occurs when pathogenic warm heat affects the spleen and stomach and transforms into dryness or dampness.

V012-N03366.label: 中经
V012-N03366.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03366.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03366.definitions: A condition characterized by limb heaviness, hemiplegia and slurred speech. Often occurs when qi and blood fail to circulate through the limbs after wind attacks the meridians.

V012-N03367.label: 中络
V012-N03367.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03367.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03367.definitions: A condition characterized by skin numbness, impaired movement and deviation of eyes and mouth border. Often occurs when wind attacks and blocks the collaterals.

V012-N03368.label: 传经
V012-N03368.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03368.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03368.definitions: Refers to the pattern of one meridian transforming into the pattern of another meridian during pathological processes of cold damage diseases.

V012-N03369.label: 八纲辨证
V012-N03369.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03369.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03369.englishNames: eight-principle syndrom differentiation/pattern identification; Syndrome Differentiation of Eight Principles; Pattern differentiation by the eight principles
V012-N03369.pinyinNames: ba gang bian zheng; bā gāng biàn zhèng
V012-N03369.definitions: A method to differentiate diseases into yin, yang, exterior, interior, cold, heat, deficiency and excess patterns through analysing the data collected by the four diagnostic methods. The analysis focuses on the disease location, nature and strength between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors.

V012-N03370.label: 六经辨证
V012-N03370.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03370.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03370.aliases: Syndrome Differ 6 Meridian； Syndrome Differentiation of Six Meridians
V012-N03370.englishNames: six-meridian/channel syndrome differentiation/pattern identification; Syndrome Differentiation Six Meridians; syndrome differentiation of six channels tneory; Pattern differentiation of six meridians
V012-N03370.pinyinNames: liu jing bian zheng; liù jīng biàn zhèng
V012-N03370.definitions: A pattern differentiation method developed by Zhang Zhongjing for externally contracted conditions according to the six meridians established in the Huang Di Nei Jing: Taiyang meridian, Yangming meridian, Shaoyang meridian, Taiyin meridian, Shaoyin meridian and Jueyin meridian.

V012-N03371.label: 卫气营血辨证
V012-N03371.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03371.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03371.aliases: Syndrome Differ Wqyx； Syndrome Differentiation Wei Qi Ying Xue
V012-N03371.englishNames: defense-qi-nutrient-blood syndrome differentiation/pattern identification; Pattern differentiation of Wei-defence, qi, Ying nutrients and blood; syndrome differentiation of weifen，qifen，yingfen and xuefen; Syndrome Differentiation of Defense Qi，Nutrient and Blood
V012-N03371.pinyinNames: wei qi ying xue bian zheng; wèi qì yíng xuè biàn zhèng
V012-N03371.definitions: A pattern differentiation method for pathological phases of externally contracted febrile diseases. It includes Wei-defence phase pattern, qi phase pattern, Ying nutrients phase pattern and blood phase pattern, indicating the depth, severity and transmission of epidemic febrile diseases.

V012-N03372.label: 卫营同病
V012-N03372.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03372.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03372.englishNames: Pattern involving both the Wei-defence and Ying nutrients
V012-N03372.definitions: Characterized by Ying nutrients phase symptoms including fever that worsens at night, mental cloudiness and a deep red tongue as well as Wei-defence phase symptoms including aversion to cold, cough and a thin, white tongue coating. Often occurs when a Wei-defence phase pattern transmits to the pericardium or to the Ying nutrients phase; however, pathogenic factors still retain in the Wei-defence phase.

V012-N03373.label: 合病
V012-N03373.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03373.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03373.definitions: Refers to pattern involving two or three meridians simultaneously.

V012-N03374.label: 喜证
V012-N03374.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03374.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03374.definitions: Characterized by mental distraction, mild or severe palpitations, limb weakness, incoherent speech, uncontrollable episodes of laughing, and strange behaviour. In severe cases, mental aberration may be present. The tongue is pale with a thin coating. The pulse is delayed. Often occurs when excessive joy causes qi to slack.

V012-N03375.label: 奇经八脉病证
V012-N03375.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03375.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03375.englishNames: Patterns of the eight extra meridians
V012-N03375.pinyinNames: qí jīng bā mài bìng zhèng
V012-N03375.definitions: The eight extra meridians include Chong, Ren, Du, Dai, Yinwei, Yangwei, Yinqiao and Yangqiao meridians. They facilitate the connections among the twelve regular meridians and harmonize yin, yang, qi and blood. Pathologically, the eight extra meridians manifest as problems along their pathways or disorders associated with their special functions.

V012-N03376.label: 寒包火
V012-N03376.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03376.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03376.definitions: A condition characterized by chills, fever, absence of sweating, headache, body ache, stuffy/runny nose, cough with sticky phlegm, asthma, loss of voice, red, swollen and painful eyes, swollen, painful gums, and thirst. The tongue coating is thin and yellow. The pulse is superficial, slippery and rapid. Often occurs when external wind cold envelops internal heat.

V012-N03377.label: 寒淫证
V012-N03377.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03377.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03377.definitions: Characterized by chills, fever, absence of sweating, headache, body ache, rapid breathing, and coughing. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is superficial and tight. Alternatively, contracture of the hands and feet, cold limbs, abdominal pain, bowel sounds, diarrhoea and vomiting may be present. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic cold damages yang qi and obstructs the flow of qi and blood.

V012-N03378.label: 寒热格拒
V012-N03378.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03378.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03378.definitions: 1) A condition when pathogenic cold or heat rejects the normal treatment with cold- or heat-property medicines. For example, exuberant pathogenic heat may reject the cool-/cold-property medicines; likewise, exuberant pathogenic cold may reject warm or pungent medicines. In these cases, it is advisable to add a small dose of warm-heat-property medicines in a formula that mainly consists of cool-/cold-property ingredients to clear exuberant heat, or vice versa. 2) This type of contrasting pathological state could exist when extreme yin cold rejects yang heat or extreme yang heat rejects yin cold.

V012-N03379.label: 寒热真假
V012-N03379.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03379.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03379.definitions: Refers to how extreme cold or heat may cause false symptoms in critical conditions, such as extreme cold manifesting as heat or extreme heat manifesting as cold.

V012-N03380.label: 寒热转化
V012-N03380.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03380.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03380.definitions: Refers to cold pattern transforming into heat or heat pattern transforming into cold due to radical changes in yin, yang and a struggle between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors.

V012-N03381.label: 寒证化热
V012-N03381.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03381.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03381.definitions: This refers to originally a cold pattern followed by presence of heat pattern coupled with absence or insignificant cold pattern. Contributing factors include external contraction of cold or cold dampness coupled with hyperactivity of yang qi or damage to yin fluids by overuse of dry/warm-property medicines for a cold pattern.

V012-N03382.label: 并病
V012-N03382.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03382.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03382.definitions: This means one meridian pattern develops before another meridian pattern resolves.

V012-N03383.label: 循经传
V012-N03383.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03383.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03383.definitions: This means that externally contracted conditions transmit according to the sequence of six meridians.

V012-N03384.label: 忧证
V012-N03384.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03384.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03384.definitions: Characterized by listlessness, lassitude, feeling depressed, a low voice and reluctance to talk, feeling sadness, poor sleep, dream-disturbed sleep and a dull headache. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is weak. Often results from qi stagnation due to excessive worries.

V012-N03385.label: 怒证
V012-N03385.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03385.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03385.definitions: Characterized by chest tightness, frequent sighing, restlessness, irritability, flushed face and ears, distending pain in the sub-costal region, dizziness, blurred vision, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, and aggressiveness. In severe cases, hallucination, fainting or vomiting of blood may be present. The tongue is red with a thin, yellow coating. The pulse is wiry. Often results from liver qi stagnation due to sudden rage or inhibited anger for a long period of time.

V012-N03386.label: 思证
V012-N03386.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03386.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03386.definitions: Characterized by lassitude, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, dizziness, poor memory, a poor appetite, abdominal fullness/distension, mild or severe palpitations, mental distraction, flaccid muscles, and limb weakness. The tongue is pale with a white coating. The pulse is weak and thready. Often occurs when too much thinking or mental exertion causes qi of the spleen and stomach to stagnate.

V012-N03387.label: 恐证
V012-N03387.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03387.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03387.definitions: Characterized by mental stress, jumpiness or easily startled, difficulty sleeping, interrupted sleep, mild or severe palpitations, and uneasiness. Often occurs when shocking and horrifying incidents causes qi to descend.

V012-N03388.label: 悲证
V012-N03388.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03388.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03388.definitions: Characterized by talking to oneself, mental distraction, restlessness, loneliness, chest tightness, sadness, mild or severe palpitations, poor memory, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, a low voice, a pale, lustreless facial complexion, and lying in a curled up position with little or no physical activity. Often occurs when excessive grief consumes lung qi and affects the heart mind.

V012-N03389.label: 惊证
V012-N03389.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03389.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03389.definitions: Characterized by palpitations, poor, interrupted sleep, panic, easily startled, spontaneous sweating, lassitude, and frequent urination with profuse, clear urine. The tongue is pale red with a thin, white coating. The pulse is short or stirring. Often occurs when sudden fright causes qi disorder and mental restlessness.

V012-N03390.label: 暑淫证
V012-N03390.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03390.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03390.definitions: Characterized by aversion to heat, sweating, thirst, fatigue, and yellow urine. The tongue is red with a white or yellow coating. The pulse is deficient and rapid. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic summer heat consumes qi, damages fluids or binds with dampness.

V012-N03391.label: 气血津液辨证
V012-N03391.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03391.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03391.englishNames: Syndrome Differentiation, Qi Blood Jin Ye; Pattern differentiation of qi, blood and bodily fluids
V012-N03391.pinyinNames: qi xue jin ye bian zheng; qì xuè jīn yè biàn zhèng
V012-N03391.definitions: A pattern differentiation method to analyse pathologies of qi, blood and bodily fluids according to the relevant theories of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03392.label: 湿淫证
V012-N03392.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03392.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03392.definitions: Characterized by distending headache, chest stuffiness, no thirst, body heaviness, body ache, fever, fatigue and turbid urine. The tongue coating is white and slippery. The pulse is soft or delayed. Often occurs when pathogenic dampness affects the skin, muscles, sinews or bones.

V012-N03393.label: 火淫证
V012-N03393.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03393.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03393.definitions: Characterized by high fever, thirst, a red face and eyes, restlessness, delirium, nosebleed and hematemesis. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is surging and rapid or thready and rapid. Often occurs when fire, heat or warmth enters the Ying-nutrients and blood phase to consume and stir blood, forcing the blood to move recklessly.

V012-N03394.label: 热证转寒
V012-N03394.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03394.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03394.definitions: This refers to an initial heat pattern followed by presence of cold pattern coupled with absent or insignificant heat. Contributing factors include excessive heat consuming qi or damage to yang qi by overuse of cool/cold-property medicines for a heat pattern.

V012-N03395.label: 燥淫证
V012-N03395.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03395.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03395.definitions: Characterized by mild headache, aversion to cold, absence of sweating, coughing, scratchy throat, and nasal obstruction. The tongue is dry and pale. The pulse is superficial and tight. Alternatively, fever, sweating, thirst, a dry throat, cough-induced chest pain or blood-stained sputum may be present. The tongue is dry with a yellow coating. The pulse is superficial and rapid.

V012-N03396.label: 病因辨证
V012-N03396.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03396.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM

V012-N03397.label: 直中
V012-N03397.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03397.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03397.definitions: This means that instead of transmitting from yang meridians, pathogenic factors directly attack the yin meridians.

V012-N03398.label: 真寒假热
V012-N03398.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03398.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03398.definitions: A yin pattern with apparent yang symptoms. These include fever but prefer more clothes or blanket; thirst but no desire to drink water; and restless hands and feet but mental tranquillity. The tongue coating is black but slippery and moist. The pulse is large and surging, but weak upon pressure. It is commonly seen in people with constitutional deficiency cold coupled with contraction of external pathogens or floating of deficiency yang (interior cold rejecting yang) due to over-exertion or dysfunctions of the zang–fu organs.

V012-N03399.label: 真热假寒
V012-N03399.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03399.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03399.definitions: A yang pattern with apparent yin symptoms. These include aversion to cold but no desire for more clothes or blanket; cold hands and feet but a burning sensation in the chest and abdomen; and watery diarrhoea with dry or strong-smelling stools. The pulse is deep but wiry, slippery and forceful upon pressure. In addition, excessive thirst, a dry mouth, a foul breath, a dry, white tongue coating, and yellow urine may be present. Often occurs when pathogenic factors enter the interior to transform into heat and causes yang excess to reject yin.

V012-N03400.label: 经络辨证
V012-N03400.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03400.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03400.aliases: Syndrome Differ Meridian； Syndrome Differentiation of Meridian
V012-N03400.englishNames: Pattern differentiation of meridians; channel/meridian syndrom differentiation/pattern identification; Syndrome Differentiation Meridian; syndrome differentiation of channel theory
V012-N03400.pinyinNames: jīng luò biàn zhèng; jing luo bian zheng
V012-N03400.definitions: A pattern differentiation method based on meridian theory. It aims to analyse clinical signs and symptoms, identify specific meridians and related zang–fu organs and then conclude the etiological factors, pathological nature and pathological mechanism.

V012-N03401.label: 脏腑辨证
V012-N03401.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03401.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03401.aliases: Syndrome Differ Zang Fu Organ； Syndrome Differentiation，Zangfu Organ
V012-N03401.englishNames: Pattern differentiation of zang–fu organs; Syndrome Differentiation, Zang-Fu Viscera; visceral syndrome differentiation/pattern identification
V012-N03401.pinyinNames: zàng fǔ biàn zhèng; zang fu bian zheng
V012-N03401.definitions: A pattern differentiation method to analyse signs and symptoms according to physiology and pathology of zang–fu organs to deduce the disease location and nature, as well as the strength between anti-pathogenic qi and pathogenic factors.

V012-N03402.label: 虚实
V012-N03402.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03402.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03402.definitions: Two of the eight principles in differentiating the strength between pathogenic factors and anti-pathogenic qi. Excess means exuberant pathogenic factors, and deficiency means insufficiency of anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N03403.label: 虚实辨证
V012-N03403.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03403.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03403.aliases: Syndrome Differ Asthen sthen； Syndrome Differentiation Asthenia Sthenia
V012-N03403.englishNames: Syndrome Differentiation of Excess and Deficiency; deficiency-excess syndrom differentiation/pattern identification; Pattern differentiation of deficiency and excess
V012-N03403.pinyinNames: xu shi bian zheng; xū shí biàn zhèng
V012-N03403.definitions: A method to differentiate deficiency pattern or excess patterns according to the relative strength of pathogenic factors and anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N03404.label: 表邪入里
V012-N03404.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03404.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03404.definitions: Refers to unresolved pathogenic factors in the exterior entering the interior. Contributing factors include weakened anti-pathogenic qi, excessive pathogenic factors, improper care, and wrong or inappropriate treatment.

V012-N03405.label: 表里传
V012-N03405.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03405.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03405.definitions: This means that externally contracted conditions transmit to the exteriorly–interiorly connected meridians.

V012-N03406.label: 表里出入
V012-N03406.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03406.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03406.englishNames: Entering of exterior pattern and exiting of interior pattern
V012-N03406.definitions: The disease progression when pathogenic factors in the exterior enter the interior or when pathogenic factors in the interior move out to the exterior.

V012-N03407.label: 表里同病
V012-N03407.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03407.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03407.definitions: The coexistence of exterior pattern and interior pattern. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic factors enter the interior before exterior pattern is resolved. Alternatively, it may occur when biao (branch) is affected before the ben (root) disorders are resolved.

V012-N03408.label: 证候相兼
V012-N03408.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03408.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03408.englishNames: Concurrent patterns; concurrent syndrome/pattern pattern manifestation; concurrent syndromes
V012-N03408.pinyinNames: zhèng hòu xiāng jiān; zheng hou xiang jian
V012-N03408.definitions: The coexistence of multiple patterns, excluding those with opposite disease nature. Examples include exterior deficiency pattern, exterior cold pattern, interior excess pattern, interior deficiency cold pattern, etc.

V012-N03409.label: 证候转化
V012-N03409.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03409.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03409.definitions: This refers to mutual transformation of patterns with opposite nature under certain conditions.

V012-N03410.label: 越经传
V012-N03410.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03410.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03410.definitions: This means that externally contracted conditions skip one or two meridians and directly transmit to the next meridian.

V012-N03411.label: 逆传
V012-N03411.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03411.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03411.definitions: Refers to pathogenic factors directly entering the Ying nutrients and blood phases from the Wei-defence phase (skipping the qi phase). Often indicates a more severe condition.

V012-N03412.label: 逆传心包证
V012-N03412.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03412.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03412.definitions: Characterized by a high-grade fever, unconsciousness, delirium and restlessness. The tongue is deep red. The pulse is rapid. Often occurs when pathogenic warmth attacks the Wei-defence first and then directly enters the pericardium.

V012-N03413.label: 里邪出表
V012-N03413.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03413.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03413.englishNames: Interior pathogen moving out to the exterior
V012-N03413.definitions: Refers to pathogenic factors in interior pattern moving out to the exterior through proper care and strengthened anti-pathogenic qi.

V012-N03414.label: 阴阳辨证
V012-N03414.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03414.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03414.aliases: Syndrome Differ Yin Yang； Syndrome Differentiation Yin Yang
V012-N03414.englishNames: Syndrome Differentiation of Yin-Yang; yin-yang syndrom differentiation/pattern identification; syndrome diffterentiation ofyin-yang; Pattern differentiation of yin and yang
V012-N03414.pinyinNames: yin yang bian zheng; yīn yáng biàn zhèng
V012-N03414.definitions: A method to differentiate yin pattern and/or yang pattern according to the basic properties of yin and yang and disease nature.

V012-N03415.label: 风淫证
V012-N03415.lineagePath: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM｜诊断、病证和体质术语｜辨证方法类
V012-N03415.vocabularyName: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
V012-N03415.definitions: Characterized by aversion to wind, fever, sweating, headache, stuffy/runny nose, and a dry throat. The tongue coating is thin and white. The pulse is superficial and delayed. Often occurs when exogenous pathogenic wind attacks the exterior.

name: WHO international standard terminologies on TCM
